definitions.meaning: 提出（观点、理论等）（熟词僻义）

advantage.etymology: 源自 avant（在前）+ -age，原指「处于前方的位置」

similar_words.description: disadvantage 是反义词，意为「劣势」

similar_words.description: venture 意为「冒险；风险企业」，是其词根

definitions.meaning: 备用的；替代者（熟词僻义）

similar_words.description: alternative 意为「可供选择的；替代方案」，常与 alternate 混淆
alter 意为「改变」，同源词

definitions.meaning: 可供选择的事物；替代方案；供选择的
definitions.example: Public transport provides an affordable alternative to private cars.

similar_words.description: alternate 强调交替或备用，alternative 强调选择项

definitions.example: Although the task was difficult, the research team completed it on schedule.

similar_words.description: though 与 although 近义，但 though 更口语，也可作副词
however 意为「然而」，连接方式与 although 不同

definitions.example: At high altitude, the air becomes thinner and breathing becomes harder.

altitude.etymology: alt（高，参 alto）+ -itude（名词后缀），表示「高度」

similar_words.description: attitude 意为「态度」，与 altitude 拼写极易混淆
latitude 意为「纬度」，词尾相同，地理语境常见

definitions.example: Altogether, the evidence suggests that early education has long-term benefits.

similar_words.description: all together 意为「全都在一起」，altogether 意为「完全；总共」
overall 意为「总体上」，与 altogether 在总结语境相近

definitions.example: Aluminum is widely used in aircraft because it is light and resistant to corrosion.

aluminum.etymology: 源自 alum（明矾）+ -ium（金属元素后缀）

similar_words.description: aluminium 是英式拼写，与 aluminum 同义
alloy 意为「合金」，铝常用于制造合金

definitions.meaning: 业余爱好者；业余的；不熟练的
definitions.example: The competition is open to both professional and amateur athletes.

definitions.meaning: 外行的；不专业的（熟词僻义）

amateur.etymology: 源自拉丁语 amare（爱），原指「出于爱好而做某事的人」

similar_words.description: professional 是反义词，意为「专业的；职业的」
mature 意为「成熟的」，与 amateur 词形部分相似

definitions.example: The speed of technological change continues to amaze researchers.

amaze.etymology: a-（加强）+ maze（迷惑），原指「使迷失、困惑」

similar_words.description: amazing 是形容词，意为「令人惊奇的」
maze 意为「迷宫；困惑」，是其词根

ambassador.etymology: 源自中世纪拉丁语 ambactus（仆从、使者），后指「外交代表」

similar_words.description: embassy 意为「大使馆」，与 ambassador 语义相关

definitions.meaning: 模棱两可的，含糊不清的

ambiguous.etymology: 前缀ambi-表“两边”，词根ag走，-ous形容词后缀；字面义“走两边的”，引申为“模糊不清的”

similar_words.description: 拼写相近，含义为“有野心的，有雄心的”

definitions.example: She finally achieved her ambition to become a professional researcher in the field.

ambition.etymology: 词根amb走，-ion名词后缀；原指“奔走追求目标”，引申为“野心、志向”

similar_words.description: 拼写相近，含义为“模棱两可的”

definitions.example: The company announced an ambitious plan to cut carbon emissions by 70% within a decade.

ambitious.etymology: 来自名词'ambition'，-ous形容词后缀，表“具有…性质的”

similar_words.description: 拼写相近，含义为“含糊不清的”

definitions.example: The injured climber was rushed to hospital by ambulance shortly after the accident.

ambulance.etymology: 词根ambul表“行走”，-ance名词后缀；原指“流动的野战医院”，后引申为“救护车”

definitions.example: The parliament voted to amend the existing environmental protection law last week.

amend.etymology: 前缀a-表“加强”，词根mend表“改正、修补”，合义为“修正、修改”

similar_words.description: 拼写相近，含义为“使惊奇”

definitions.example: The amiable researcher was always willing to help graduate students with their projects.

definitions.example: The company announced its new product amid growing concerns about market competition.

definitions.example: A large amount of research funding has been allocated to the study of climate change.

amount.etymology: a-前缀表朝向，词根mount表示「上升」，本义为合计上升到某个数值

similar_words.description: 「amount」修饰不可数名词；「number」修饰可数名词复数

similar_words.description: 「ample」指超出需求的充足；「adequate」指刚好满足需求的足够

definitions.example: The electronic amplifier is used to increase the strength of the input signal.

similar_words.description: 「amplifier」是名词，指放大器；「amplify」是动词，意为放大

amplify.etymology: 词根ampl表示「宽大」，-fy动词后缀表「使…」

similar_words.description: 「amplify」多强调放大信号、详述观点；「expand」多侧重扩大范围、尺寸

definitions.example: The writer amuses readers with witty descriptions of daily life in the city.

amuse.etymology: a-前缀表加强，词根mus源自法语muse表娱乐、沉思

similar_words.description: 「amuse」指使开心消遣；「amaze」指使惊讶惊愕

definitions.example: This drug is a synthetic analogue of a natural hormone produced in the human body.

analogue.etymology: 词根ana表示「类似」，log表示「话语、比拟」

similar_words.description: 「analogue」指实体的类似物；「analogy」指逻辑上的类比、类推

definitions.example: The lecturer drew an analogy between the human brain and a modern computer.

analogy.etymology: 词根ana表示「类似」，log表示「说理」，指通过类似事物说理

similar_words.description: 「analogy」指逻辑上的类比方法；「analogue」指具体的类似事物

definitions.example: Scientists analysed 10 years of data to identify patterns in global temperature changes.

analyse.etymology: 词根ana表示「散开」，lys表示「分解」，指将整体分解开研究

similar_words.description: 「analyse」是英式拼写；「analyze」是美式拼写，词义完全相同

similar_words.description: 是动词形式，analysis为名词形式

definitions.example: This study adopts an analytic approach to examine the changing social structure.

analytic.etymology: 来自analysis的形容词形式，词根lys（解开）

similar_words.description: analytic是形容词，对应名词是analysis

definitions.example: Modern humans are believed to share common ancestors in Africa.

similar_words.description: ancestor指祖先，descendant指后代，词义相反

definitions.example: The ship dropped anchor in the quiet bay to avoid the storm.

similar_words.description: 拼写相近，意义完全不同，anchor指锚，angel指天使

definitions.example: Many ancient civilizations developed along large river basins.

definitions.example: The writer illustrated his point with a personal anecdote.

anecdote.etymology: 希腊语anekdota（未发表的故事）派生而来

definitions.example: Iron deficiency is the most common cause of anemia in the world.

anemia.etymology: 前缀an-（无）+词根em（血）+名词后缀-ia，意为“缺血”

definitions.example: In medieval art, an angel is usually depicted with large white wings.

angel.etymology: 希腊语angelos（信使）派生而来，原指神的信使

similar_words.description: 拼写仅差一个字母，angel意为天使，angle意为角度

definitions.example: Public anger over the new policy quickly spread across the country.

anger.etymology: 古挪威语angr（痛苦）派生而来

similar_words.description: anger是名词，angry是对应的形容词形式

definitions.example: Reporters approached the controversial issue from a completely new angle.

similar_words.description: 拼写仅差一个字母，angle意为角度，angel意为天使

definitions.example: The entire community shared in the family's anguish over the loss of their child.

similar_words.description: 拼写接近，词义不同，'angle'表示角度、观点

definitions.example: He twisted his ankle when he fell down the stairs during the hike.

ankle.etymology: 源自古英语，无复杂词根词缀，为基础身体部位名词

similar_words.description: 拼写简单，无相近易混考研核心词

definitions.example: The couple held a small party to celebrate their 25th wedding anniversary.

definitions.example: The journal publishes its annual report on global carbon emissions every spring.
The university's annual includes a list of all graduates from the past academic year.

annual.etymology: 词根ann表示“年”，后缀-ual构成形容词和名词

similar_words.description: 同根词，'anniversary'表示周年纪念日

definitions.example: The police received an anonymous tip that led to the arrest of the suspect.

similar_words.description: 拼写接近，词义不同，'aunt'表示姨母、姑母

definitions.example: The old television required an external antenna to receive clear broadcast signals.

definitions.meaning: 无论如何，不管怎样；不管用什么方法

similar_words.description: 二者含义接近，anyhow更侧重‘无论用何种方式’，anyway更常用来转换话题或总结

similar_words.description: 前者用于否定、疑问和肯定句表任何地方，后者表某个不确定的地方

similar_words.description: apart是副词，part多作名词和动词表‘部分；分开’

similar_words.description: 前者指公寓，后者指部门、系

definitions.example: The manager apologised to the customers for the delay of the product delivery.

apologise.etymology: 词根apo-表示‘离开’，词根log表示‘说话’，-ise构成动词，原指摆脱过错后说话，引申为道歉

similar_words.description: apology是名词，apologise是其对应的动词形式

definitions.example: The scale of human suffering in the disaster appals even the most experienced rescue workers.

definitions.example: Public opinion was appalled by the government's inaction during the crisis.

appall.etymology: 前缀ap-表强调，词根pal表示苍白，使脸色因恐惧变得苍白→使惊骇

similar_words.description: 英式英语拼写，词义与'appall'完全相同

definitions.example: The laboratory installed a new set of apparatus for chemical experiments.
The country's secret intelligence apparatus remained largely intact after the political reform.

apparatus.etymology: 前缀ap-表朝向，词根par表示准备，为特定用途准备好的东西→设备，仪器

similar_words.description: 泛指设备，`apparatus`侧重特定功能的成套装置或复杂设备

definitions.example: It is apparent that the company's new policy has failed to boost employee productivity.

similar_words.description: 二者都表示明显的，`apparent`侧重从观察中清晰看出，也可指表面而非本质

definitions.example: The charity launched a campaign to appeal for more donations to help homeless people.
The defendant decided to appeal against the guilty verdict made by the lower court.

definitions.example: A new challenger has appeared in the field of renewable energy technology.

appearance.etymology: appear（出现）加名词后缀-ance，表出现的状态或事物→外貌，出现

similar_words.description: 二者都指外貌，`appearance`更侧重整体给人的印象，是正式用语

definitions.example: The detailed statistical data is listed in the appendix at the end of the research report.

definitions.example: Stress and lack of sleep can greatly affect a person's appetite.

definitions.example: The audience stood up to applaud the young pianist's wonderful performance.
Many experts applaud the government's new measures to address climate change.

applaud.etymology: 前缀ap-表强调，词根laud表示赞美，公开赞美→鼓掌，称赞

definitions.example: The professor’s insightful speech won thunderous applause from the audience.

applause.etymology: 词根「plaud」表示「鼓掌，赞扬」，ap-加强语义，名词后缀-e，指公开的赞扬或鼓掌

definitions.example: Energy-saving household appliances have been widely promoted in recent years.

definitions.meaning: 适用的，合适的；可应用的
definitions.example: This new regulation is applicable to all employees working in the headquarters.

applicable.etymology: 词根「ply」表示「应用」，ap-朝向，able形容词后缀，表示能够应用的

similar_words.description: 是过去分词作形容词，意为应用的，实用性的，含义有区别

definitions.example: The application of artificial intelligence to medical diagnosis has improved accuracy greatly.

application.etymology: 词根「ply」表示「应用」，ap-朝向，tion名词后缀，指申请或应用的行为

similar_words.description: 意为电器器具，和application的申请、应用含义不同

definitions.example: Candidates need to apply for the postgraduate admission before the deadline.

apply.etymology: 词根「ply」表示「折叠、应用」，ap-朝向，引申为朝向某个目标提出申请或施加应用

similar_words.description: 词形相近，含义不同，applaud表示鼓掌称赞

definitions.meaning: 任命，委任；约定，确定（时间地点）
definitions.example: The board of directors decided to appoint a new chief executive officer.

appoint.etymology: ap-加强，point指观点、点位，指确定人员或点位，引申为任命或约定

similar_words.description: 是词根词，appoint由ap-加point构成，表示确定人选

definitions.example: You need to make an appointment in advance to see the professor.

appointment.etymology: appoint的名词形式，ment名词后缀，表示任命的行为或约定的会面

similar_words.description: 是appointment的动词原形，含义一致

definitions.example: The annual performance appraisal of staff is conducted at the end of each year.

appraisal.etymology: 词根「praise」此处表示「估价、评价」，ap-加强，al名词后缀，指评价行为

similar_words.description: 是appraisal的动词原形，意为评价估价

definitions.example: Experts were invited to appraise the value of the ancient collection.

appraise.etymology: 词根「praise」表示「估价」，ap-加强，动词词形，指做出评价

similar_words.description: 词形相近，appreciate多表示感激、欣赏，含义不同

definitions.meaning: 感激；欣赏，重视；充分意识到

The property has appreciated greatly in value since it was purchased.

appreciate.etymology: 词根「prec」表示「价值、价格」，ap-加强，iate动词后缀，引申为认识到价值即欣赏、感激

definitions.example: The police finally apprehended the suspect after a week of investigation.

definitions.example: Many students are apprehensive about the results of the postgraduate entrance examination.

similar_words.description: comprehensive表示综合的、全面的，和本词含义不同

definitions.example: The company has built its customer base through years of high-quality product service.
This study is based on data collected from more than two thousand participants across the country.

base.etymology: 来自拉丁语basisa，表示‘底部，基础’，是基础语义的词根

similar_words.description: 「base」侧重实体的基础或基地，「basis」侧重抽象的理论基础

definitions.example: Baseball has long been regarded as the national pastime of the United States.

baseball.etymology: 合成词，由base（垒）+ball（球）构成，直译就是‘垒球’，对应棒球运动

similar_words.description: 二者都是「球类运动」合成词，分别指棒球和篮球

similar_words.description: 「basement」是同根名词，由词根base引申而来，指建筑底部的空间

similar_words.description: 二者都表示基础的，「basic」更侧重日常通用义，「fundamental」更侧重抽象根本义

definitions.example: The Amazon basin covers a vast area of tropical rainforest with extremely rich biodiversity.

similar_words.description: 二者同根，都包含‘底部’的核心语义

similar_words.description: 「basis」用于抽象的理论、关系基础，「base」多用于实体基础或基地

definitions.example: In many western cultures, the bat is often associated with negative images such as darkness and evil.

bat.etymology: 语义来自古英语，两种词义分别对应不同原始词源，现在拼写统一为bat

similar_words.description: 拼写相近，「bat」是蝙蝠或球棒，「cat」是猫

similar_words.description: 拼写相近，「batch」指一批，「patch」指补丁、小块

definitions.example: Many people prefer to take a bath before going to bed to relax their muscles.

similar_words.description: bath是名词，bathe多为动词，表示洗澡游泳

definitions.example: Coastal tourists often bathe in the ocean during summer holidays.

bathe.etymology: 来自古英语，由bath动词化而来

similar_words.description: bathe是动词，bath多为名词，表示洗澡

definitions.example: The apartment has a separate kitchen and a bathroom with a shower.

bathroom.etymology: 合成词，由bath + room构成，字面意为沐浴的房间

similar_words.description: 二者都可表示洗手间，bathroom更委婉通用

definitions.example: The portable electronic device requires two AA batteries to work properly.
The old military battery was built on the top of the hill in the 19th century.

similar_words.description: 同词根bat，battle表示战斗，battery表示电池

definitions.example: The two nations have been in a prolonged battle over the border territory.

battle.etymology: 词根bat表示击打，-le为后缀，引申为战斗

similar_words.description: battle指具体战役，war指整体战争

definitions.example: The calm bay provides a safe anchorage for fishing boats during the storm.

similar_words.description: bay指较小海湾，gulf指更大的海湾

definitions.example: Thousands of tourists gather on the beach every year to enjoy the sunshine.

similar_words.description: beach指海边沙滩，coast指整个海岸线区域

definitions.example: A narrow beam of light came through the crack in the curtain.

similar_words.description: beam是横梁光束，bean是豆子，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: Soy beans are widely used to produce various food products in Asia.

bean.etymology: 源自古英语，指豆类作物

similar_words.description: bean是豆子，beam是横梁光束，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The old man with a gray beard sat quietly by the window reading newspaper.

beard.etymology: 源自古英语，指面部胡须

similar_words.description: beard是胡须，beast是野兽，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: Recent economic changes have a direct bearing on government policy making.

similar_words.description: 是动词bear的动名词/派生词，词义与动词bear差异较大

definitions.example: The film describes how a group of explorers fought against a wild beast in the jungle.

similar_words.description: 拼写首字母相同，词义完全不同

definitions.example: The beauty of this method lies in its simplicity and low cost.

beauty.etymology: 由形容词「beautiful」去后缀「-ful」加名词后缀「-y」构成

similar_words.description: 拼写首字母相同，词义完全不同

definitions.example: A single bee can carry nearly its own weight in nectar back to the hive.

bee.etymology: 源自古英语，本指会酿蜜的蜂类昆虫

similar_words.description: 同音形近词，词义完全不同

definitions.example: We need to confirm the meeting schedule with all participants beforehand.

similar_words.description: 同为时间相关词，beforehand侧重指提前做准备，before仅表时间先后

similar_words.description: 形近词，仅有首字母不同，词义差异大

definitions.example: The lawyer spoke on behalf of the defendant during the court hearing.

behave.etymology: 由前缀「be-」加词根「have」构成，原义为“持有自身态度”

similar_words.description: 拼写相近，词义词性都不同

definitions.example: The question of human being's origin has long been discussed by philosophers.

being.etymology: 由动词be变来的现在分词，派生作名词使用

similar_words.description: 「bearing」意为举止、方位，与「being」语义差异明显

similar_words.description: 和「believe」词性不同，「belief」是名词，「believe」是动词

definitions.example: The hungry traveler rested a hand on his empty belly and continued walking.

belly.etymology: 源自古英语，本身为基本词汇，无复杂词缀

similar_words.description: 「bell」意为钟、铃声，拼写和词义都不同

definitions.example: The old man sat on the park bench reading a newspaper every morning.

bench.etymology: 源自古英语，单音节基本名词

similar_words.description: 「bend」意为弯曲，拼写接近词义不同

definitions.example: He bent down to pick up the book that had fallen on the floor.

bend.etymology: 源自古英语，单音节基本动词

definitions.example: The small village lies beneath the mountain where abundant mineral resources are stored.

definitions.example: Regular physical exercise is beneficial to maintaining physical and mental health.

similar_words.description: 和同根名词「benefit」词性不同，词义同源

definitions.example: Many college students find that internships bring long-term benefits to their future career development.

反义词易混，malignant表（肿瘤）恶性的，benign表良性的

definitions.example: Besides professional knowledge, the interview also examines candidates' communication skills.

definitions.example: The spy betrayed the military secrets of his country to the enemy for huge profits.

definitions.meaning: 泄露，暴露（真实情感或意图）

近义易混，reveal泛指主动或被动泄露，betray侧重无意间暴露真实想法

definitions.meaning: （正式用语）饮料（常指除水以外的饮品）

beverage.etymology: 由词根bever-（饮用）和名词后缀-age构成，表饮品

形近易混，behavior表行为举止，beverage表饮料

近义易混，puzzle侧重让人困惑不解，bewilder侧重让人彻底头脑混乱

definitions.example: More and more urban residents choose to ride a bicycle to commute.

definitions.example: The local construction company submitted a bid for the new bridge project.

definitions.example: We were surprised by the high medical bill for the emergency treatment.

similar_words.description: 拼写相近，bind是动词，指捆绑、约束，含义不同

definitions.example: The climber bound the broken rope together to continue climbing.

similar_words.description: 拼写相近，bond多作名词，指纽带、债券，含义不同

definitions.example: The discovery of gene editing technology brings great changes to modern biology.

similar_words.description: 同含bio-（生命）词根，biography指传记，含义不同

definitions.example: Fossil evidence suggests that early hominids adopted a bipedal mode of locomotion.

都含有词根ped表示‘足’，pedal指踏板，词义与bipedal不同

definitions.example: The data shows that life expectancy at birth in this region has increased by 15 years.

birth.etymology: 由动词bear（生育）派生而来，表示生育的结果即出生

definitions.example: The study found that a large proportion of children consume sugary biscuits on a daily basis.

biscuit.etymology: 前缀bis-（bi-的变体）表示‘两次’，词根cuit表示‘烤’，原指经两次烘烤制成的食品，即饼干

definitions.example: A new study shows that mosquitoes are more likely to bite people with type O blood.

bite.etymology: 古英语传承的基础单词，作动词表咬的动作，派生为名词表咬的动作或结果

similar_words.description: 拼写仅差一个元音字母，bite意为咬，bait意为诱饵、引诱

definitions.example: The former rivals still hold bitter feelings towards each other after decades of conflict.

bitter.etymology: 源自古英语表示‘咬’的词根，引申为味道刺激的苦涩的，再引申出情绪上痛苦的含义

definitions.example: Researchers were confused by the bizarre results of the experiment that contradicted all previous hypotheses.

definitions.example: The former official was charged with blackmail and corruption by the local prosecutor.

definitions.example: The new type of electric razor is equipped with a sharper blade that reduces skin irritation.

definitions.example: Many experts blame the frequent extreme weather events on excessive carbon emissions.

blame.etymology: 源自古英语，核心含义为指责、归咎，可作动词和名词使用

similar_words.description: 二者都表示指责，blame强调归咎责任，accuse强调指控错误或罪行
拼写仅差倒数第二个字母，blame意为责怪、归咎，blaze意为火焰、闪耀

definitions.example: Researchers left a blank space in the questionnaire for respondents to add additional comments.
When the journalist asked the politician about the scandal, he gave a blank stare and refused to answer.

blank.etymology: 源自古法语借词，原义指白色的，引申为没有内容的空白的，再引申出情绪上茫然的含义

similar_words.description: 拼写仅差最后一个字母，blank意为空白的、茫然的，bland意为温和的、乏味的
二者都表示‘空的’，blank强调没有内容可填写，empty强调空间或容器中空无一物

definitions.example: The hiker wrapped himself in a thick woolen blanket to keep warm during the alpine camping trip.
A dense smog blanketed the industrial zone, triggering a series of air pollution warnings from the environmental agency.

blanket.etymology: 源自古法语blancet，原指白色织物，后引申为名词“毯子”；动词义由名词引申，表示“像盖毯子一样覆盖”

similar_words.description: 「blank」指“空白的；空白处”，「blanket」为“毯子；（大面积）覆盖”，形近但语义差异大

definitions.meaning: 爆炸；一阵（强烈的气流/响声）
definitions.example: The sudden blast of the dynamite shattered the old building's windows and scattered debris across the street.
The opposition leader blasted the government's new tax policy in a televised speech.

blast.etymology: 源自古英语blǣst，原指“吹风”，后引申为“爆炸的冲击力”“人为爆破”及“言语上的猛烈抨击”

similar_words.description: 「burst」侧重“（自然/突然）破裂、爆发”，「blast」侧重“人为爆破的冲击力或言语抨击”

definitions.example: The wildfire spread rapidly, sending a huge blaze into the night sky visible from miles away.

definitions.meaning: 燃烧；发光；（在树上）刻痕标记路线
definitions.example: The hikers blazed the trail by cutting small marks on the tree trunks to guide future travelers.

blaze.etymology: 源自古英语blæse，原指“火炬”，后引申为“火焰、光辉”；动词“刻痕标记”源于古代用火炬标记路径的习俗

similar_words.description: 「flame」指“单束或小范围的火焰”，「blaze」侧重“熊熊大火、大范围的光辉”

definitions.example: The bleak coastal town, with its gray skies and empty streets, was rarely visited by tourists in winter.
The economic report painted a bleak picture of the region's manufacturing sector, with factory closures expected to rise.

bleak.etymology: 源自古挪威语bleikr，原指“苍白的”，后引申为“环境荒凉、前景黯淡”

similar_words.description: 「gloomy」侧重“（情绪、氛围）阴郁的”，「bleak」侧重“（环境、前景）荒凉、无希望的”

definitions.example: The injured hiker was bleeding heavily from a cut on his leg when the rescue team arrived.
Many small businesses complained that the complex tax system was bleeding them dry of operating funds.

bleed.etymology: 源自古英语blēdan，原指“流血”，后引申为“织物渗色”及“榨取资源”的比喻义

similar_words.description: 「bleed」为动词“流血；榨取”，「blood」为名词“血液；血统”，二者为同根衍生词

definitions.example: The chef blended traditional Italian spices with local Asian ingredients to create a unique fusion dish.
This coffee is a smooth blend of beans from Colombia and Ethiopia.

blend.etymology: 源自古英语blendan，原指“混合”，核心含义为“使不同事物均匀融合”

similar_words.description: 「mix」为通用“混合”，「blend」侧重“（均匀、和谐地）融合”，常用于食品、文化等领域

definitions.example: The priest blessed the newly married couple during the church ceremony.

definitions.meaning: 使有幸得到（优势、天赋等）
definitions.example: She was blessed with a remarkable memory, which helped her excel in academic research.

bless.etymology: 源自古英语blētsian，原指“用牲血献祭以求庇佑”，后引申为“祝福、保佑”及“赋予优势”的比喻义

similar_words.description: 「pray」指“（主动）祈祷、祈求”，「bless」指“（他人或神）给予祝福、保佑”

definitions.meaning: 失明的；盲目的（无判断力的）
definitions.example: The blind poet relied on his sharp sense of hearing and memory to compose his famous works.

definitions.meaning: 使失明；蒙蔽（使无法判断）
definitions.example: His desire for wealth blinded him to the ethical risks of the business deal.

blind.etymology: 源自古英语blind，原指“失明的”，后引申为“缺乏判断力的”及“蒙蔽认知”的动词义

similar_words.description: 「deaf」指“失聪的”，「blind」指“失明的”，二者均为描述感官缺陷的核心形容词

definitions.example: The forensic team collected blood samples from the crime scene to identify the suspect's identity.
The young athlete had the blood of a champion, showing incredible determination in every competition.

blood.etymology: 源自古英语blōd，原指“血液”，后引申为“家族血统”及“个人气质”的比喻义

similar_words.description: 「blood」为名词“血液；血统”，「bleed」为动词“流血；榨取”，二者为同根衍生词

definitions.example: The bloody battle in the novel depicted the harsh realities of war and its impact on ordinary people.
It's bloody difficult to finish this academic thesis within the tight deadline set by the professor.

bloody.etymology: 由名词「blood」加形容词后缀「-y」构成，原指“沾血的”，后引申为“血腥的”；副词义为英式口语引申用法

similar_words.description: 「cruel」侧重“（人）残忍的”，「bloody」侧重“（事件、场景）血腥、残暴的”

definitions.meaning: v. 开花；繁荣，兴旺 n. 花；花期；繁荣期
definitions.example: Rural tourism has begun to bloom in regions with well-preserved natural landscapes, bringing new income to local residents.

bloom.etymology: 源自古英语blōstma，本义为「花」，无显性词缀拆分

similar_words.description: 语义相近均表开花/繁荣，bloom侧重花卉或快速繁荣，blossom侧重果树花或逐步发展

definitions.meaning: v. （果树等）开花；发展，长成 n. （果树的）花；花簇
definitions.example: The apple orchards in the mountainous area blossom in early spring, serving as a key tourist attraction for the county.

blossom.etymology: 源自古英语blōstm，与bloom同根，本义为「果树花」

similar_words.description: 语义相近均表开花/繁荣，blossom侧重果树花或逐步发展，bloom侧重花卉或快速繁荣

definitions.example: The female anthropologist wore a durable cotton blouse during her fieldwork in the tropical rainforest, as documented in her research diary.

blouse.etymology: 源自古法语bliaus，本义为「束腰外衣」，无显性词缀拆分

similar_words.description: 语义相近均表上衣，blouse特指女式短上衣/衬衫，shirt多指男式或通用衬衫

definitions.meaning: n. 蓝图；方案，设想 v. 制定蓝图；规划
definitions.example: The Ministry of Education released a five-year blueprint for improving the quality of vocational education across the country.

blueprint.etymology: 合成词，由blue（蓝色）+ print（印刷）构成，原指用蓝晒法制作的工程图纸

similar_words.description: 语义相近均表计划，blueprint侧重详细可实施的规划，scheme多指计划或计谋（偶含贬义）

definitions.meaning: n. 愚蠢的错误，疏忽 v. 犯愚蠢的错误；踉踉跄跄地走
definitions.example: The pharmaceutical company’s regulatory blunder resulted in a temporary suspension of its new drug approval process.

blunder.etymology: 源自古诺尔斯语blundra（闭眼），引申为「因疏忽犯错」

similar_words.description: 语义相近均表错误，blunder指因粗心/愚蠢导致的严重错误，mistake为通用错误

definitions.meaning: adj. 钝的；直言不讳的 v. 使迟钝；使减弱
definitions.example: The reviewer’s blunt assessment of the study’s methodological flaws forced the research team to revise their experimental design.

blunt.etymology: 源自古英语blunt（钝的），无显性词缀拆分

similar_words.description: 语义相近均表坦率，blunt直言不讳且可能生硬，frank坦率且真诚

definitions.meaning: v. （使）变模糊；（使）难以区分 n. 模糊的记忆/影像
definitions.example: The proliferation of short-video platforms has blurred the boundary between professional content production and amateur creation.

blur.etymology: 源自中古英语bluren（使模糊），无显性词缀拆分

similar_words.description: 拼写形近易混淆，blur指（使）变模糊，blush指（因情绪）脸红

definitions.meaning: v. （因羞愧、尴尬等）脸红 n. 脸红；羞愧
definitions.example: The graduate student blushed when her thesis advisor pointed out a critical oversight in her data analysis during the defense.

blush.etymology: 源自古英语blyscan（脸红），无显性词缀拆分

similar_words.description: 拼写形近易混淆，blush指（因情绪）脸红，blur指（使）变模糊

definitions.example: The board of directors of the automotive company approved a $2 billion investment in electric vehicle R&D.
International students must board the designated shuttle bus at the airport to reach the university campus.

board.etymology: 源自古英语bord（木板、餐桌），引申为「围坐的董事会」「登船（踏木板跳板）」

similar_words.description: 语义相近均表团体，board多指企业/机构的董事会/理事会，committee多指特定任务的委员会

definitions.meaning: v. 自夸，吹嘘；以有…而自豪 n. 自夸的话；引以为豪的事物
definitions.example: The university boasts a top-ranked engineering program, with over 30% of its faculty members being national award recipients.

boast.etymology: 源自古英语bēst（吹嘘），无显性词缀拆分

similar_words.description: 语义相近均表夸耀，boast可指中性的「以…自豪」，brag多含贬义的「吹嘘」

definitions.example: Laboratory technicians boil water samples to sterilize them before conducting chemical analyses.
The dermatologist warned that untreated boils could lead to more severe bacterial infections.

boil.etymology: 源自古英语bēlian，与日耳曼语族表「冒泡、沸腾」的词汇同源，无明显派生词缀

similar_words.description: 「boil」指液体沸腾或使煮沸，「broil」指烤焙食物或激烈争吵

definitions.example: The team’s bold decision to abandon the traditional method led to a breakthrough in renewable energy research.
The hikers struggled to climb the bold rock face without professional equipment.

bold.etymology: 源自古英语beald（强壮的、勇敢的），无明显派生词缀

similar_words.description: 「bold」指大胆的或陡峭的，「bald」指秃头的、光秃的

definitions.example: The maintenance worker tightened the loose bolt on the bridge’s steel frame to ensure structural safety.
High temperatures caused the lettuce to bolt, making its leaves bitter and unfit for consumption.

bolt.etymology: 源自古英语bolt（箭、门闩），与古诺斯语bolti（箭）同源，无明显派生词缀

similar_words.description: 「bolt」指螺栓、门闩或抽薹，「volt」指电压单位伏特

definitions.example: The historical documentary detailed how the atomic bomb transformed modern warfare and global politics.
The much-hyped film bombed at the box office, earning less than a tenth of its production budget.

bomb.etymology: 源自拉丁语bombus（嗡嗡声），后引申为爆炸物，无明显派生词缀

similar_words.description: 「bomb」指炸弹或惨败，「comb」指梳子或梳理

definitions.example: Shared cultural traditions serve as a strong bond between the two immigrant communities in the city.
Covalent bonds, formed by shared electrons, are essential to the structure of organic molecules.

bond.etymology: 源自古英语bonda（束缚者），与「bind（束缚）」同根，无复杂派生词缀

similar_words.description: 「bond」指纽带或结合，「bound」是bind的过去分词，也可指边界、受束缚的

definitions.example: Archaeologists discovered ancient human bone fragments at the excavation site, dating back over 10,000 years.
The bone of contention in the negotiation was the distribution of research funding between universities.

bone.etymology: 源自古英语bān，与日耳曼语族表「骨头」的词汇同源，无明显派生词缀

similar_words.description: 「bone」指骨头或核心问题，「bane」指祸根、克星

definitions.example: Employees who meet the annual performance targets will receive a substantial year-end bonus.
The sunny weather during the hiking trip was a welcome bonus for the exhausted team.

bonus.etymology: 源自拉丁语bonus（意为「好的」），由表「益处」的语义引申为「额外奖励」

similar_words.description: 「bonus」指奖金或额外好处，「bounce」指弹跳、反弹或拒付支票

definitions.example: The tech sector boomed in the 1990s, creating millions of high-paying jobs across the United States.
The crane operator adjusted the boom to lift the heavy construction materials onto the rooftop.

boom.etymology: 源自拟声词，模拟爆炸或雷声的隆隆声，后引申为「繁荣（因发展迅速的声响感）」

similar_words.description: 「boom」指繁荣或吊杆，「bloom」指开花或短暂繁荣期

definitions.example: Government investment in public transportation is expected to boost economic growth in rural areas.
The new engine model is equipped with a turbocharger to boost its horsepower output.

boost.etymology: 源自方言bouse（推动），与古英语būtan（推）同源，无明显派生词缀

similar_words.description: 「boost」指促进或提高，「boot」指靴子、启动或解雇

definitions.meaning: 靴子；（汽车的）后备箱（英式）
definitions.example: He packed his camping gear into the boot of his car before heading to the national park.
The company booted three senior executives for violating the anti-corruption policy.

boot.etymology: 源自古英语bōt（帮助、补救），后引申为「靴子（保护脚的辅助物）」，再引申为「启动、解雇」等义

similar_words.description: 「boot」指靴子、启动或解雇，「boost」指促进或提高

definitions.meaning: （集市、展览的）摊位；（公用电话）亭
definitions.example: Many graduate students set up booths at the campus career fair to promote their entrepreneurial projects.

booth.etymology: 源自古诺斯语bóth，原指临时搭建的小屋，后引申为摊位、亭等小型封闭空间

similar_words.description: 「booth」指摊位、亭，「boot」指靴子或汽车后备箱，拼写仅差末尾的字母h

definitions.meaning: n. 边界，边境；边缘；v. 毗邻，与…接壤
definitions.example: The border between the two countries has been a hot topic in international political studies.

border.etymology: 源自古法语bordure，由bord（边、边缘）加名词后缀-ure构成，表边界、边缘

similar_words.description: 「border」侧重两国或地区的实际分界线，「boundary」侧重抽象的或领土的正式边界

definitions.meaning: v. 使厌烦，使厌倦；n. 令人厌烦的人/事
definitions.example: His tedious lecture on medieval literature bored most of the postgraduate students in the audience.
The geologists bored a 60-meter-deep hole to collect core samples of ancient rock formations.

bore.etymology: 有两个词源：表「使厌烦」源自古英语borian；表「钻孔」源自古诺斯语bora，二者词义无直接关联

similar_words.description: 「bore」指使厌烦或钻孔，「bare」指赤裸的、空的，拼写差中间的字母o与a

definitions.example: She is a born academic researcher with a keen insight into interdisciplinary studies.

born.etymology: 是动词bear（生育、承受）的过去分词作形容词，源自古英语beran的过去分词boren

similar_words.description: 「born」是形容词，表出生的、天生的；「borne」是动词bear的过去分词，表被携带、被承受，拼写差末尾的字母e

definitions.meaning: 胸怀，内心；（引申）知己，密友
definitions.example: Her bosom friend, a fellow PhD candidate, assisted her in polishing the academic manuscript.

bosom.etymology: 源自古英语bōsm，原指胸部、怀抱，后引申为胸怀、内心及密友

similar_words.description: 「bosom」指胸怀、知己，「boss」指老板、指挥，拼写差中间的字母o与s

definitions.meaning: n. 老板，上司；v. 指挥，发号施令
definitions.example: The project boss, a senior professor, required all team members to submit their research drafts by the end of the month.

boss.etymology: 源自荷兰语baas，原指主人、工头，后引申为老板、上司及指挥的动作

similar_words.description: 「boss」指老板、指挥，「bosom」指胸怀、知己，拼写差中间的字母o与s

definitions.meaning: n. 底部，底端；adj. 底部的，最后的
definitions.example: The key clue to the reading comprehension question is printed at the bottom of Page 52 in the test paper.

bottom.etymology: 源自古英语botm，原指物体的底部、根基，后引申为最后的、底层的

similar_words.description: 「bottom」指底部，「button」指按钮、纽扣，拼写差中间的字母o与u及末尾的m与n

definitions.meaning: v. 弹起，反弹；（经济等）回升；n. 弹跳，反弹
definitions.example: The latest economic report indicates that domestic consumption bounced back sharply in the third quarter.

bounce.etymology: 源自中古荷兰语bounsen，原指猛击、弹起，后引申为经济回升等义

similar_words.description: 「bounce」侧重因弹性弹起或经济回升，「bound」侧重主动跳跃或必然的状态

definitions.meaning: 一定的，必然的；（用于be bound for）前往的
definitions.example: Without systematic review, candidates are bound to encounter difficulties in the postgraduate entrance examination.
The research strictly stays within the bounds of ethical academic norms.

bound.etymology: 有两个词源：表「一定的、必然的」源自古英语bindan（束缚）的过去分词；表「跳跃」源自古诺斯语bunda；名词义「界限」由束缚义引申而来

similar_words.description: 「bound」侧重主动跳跃或必然的状态，「bounce」侧重因弹性弹起或经济回升

definitions.meaning: 边界，界限（多指正式的、抽象的或领土的边界）
definitions.example: The academic paper discusses the blurred boundary between artificial intelligence and human creativity.

boundary.etymology: 由bound（界限）加名词后缀-ary（表范围、场所）构成，指边界、界限

similar_words.description: 「boundary」侧重抽象的或领土的正式边界，「border」侧重两国或地区的实际分界线

definitions.example: On graduation day, each student bowed to the faculty members before receiving their official diploma.
Lifeboats were quickly lowered from the bow of the sinking cargo ship to rescue stranded sailors.

bow.etymology: 源自古英语「boga」，本义为「弯曲的物体」，衍生出「弓、鞠躬（弯腰动作）、船头（弯曲的船首）」等义

similar_words.description: 「bough」指树木的粗枝，「bow」指鞠躬、弓或船头，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: Excessive academic stress may cause discomfort in the bowels and disrupt normal digestive function.
Geologists drilled deep into the bowels of the Antarctic ice sheet to extract ancient climate data.

bowel.etymology: 源自中古法语「boel」，本义为「内脏」，后引申出「内部深处」的比喻义

similar_words.description: 「bowel」指肠或内部深处，「bowl」指碗，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: She poured the hot bone soup into a ceramic bowl to let it cool down properly.

bowl.etymology: 源自古英语「bolla」，本义为「圆形容器」，后衍生出「碗状区域」等义

similar_words.description: 「bowl」指碗或碗状区域，「bowel」指肠或内部深处，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: Many postgraduate students choose bowling as a casual weekend activity to relieve intense study pressure.

bowling.etymology: 由词根「bowl（滚球）」加名词后缀「-ing」构成，表「滚球类运动」，特指保龄球

similar_words.description: 「bowling」指保龄球运动，「bowing」指鞠躬的动作或动名词，拼写仅差一个字母易混淆

definitions.example: Environmental activists called on global consumers to boycott products made from endangered wildlife materials.

boycott.etymology: 源自19世纪爱尔兰地主「Charles Boycott」的名字，因拒绝减租遭当地民众集体抵制，后引申为「集体抵制」的通用义

similar_words.description: 「boycott」指民众自发的集体抵制行为，「ban」指官方发布的明令禁止规定

definitions.example: Engineers used steel beams to brace the old stone bridge against potential structural collapse.
She braced herself for the harsh feedback from her doctoral thesis supervisor.

brace.etymology: 源自拉丁语「brachium（手臂）」，原指用手臂支撑的动作，后衍生出「支架、支撑、振作（精神支撑）」等义

similar_words.description: 「brace」指支撑结构或支撑、振作的动作，「bracket」指括号或墙上的托架，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: Please enclose the supplementary statistical data in square brackets in your academic paper.
Progressive tax rates are applied to different income brackets in most Western developed countries.

bracket.etymology: 源自古法语「braguette」，本义为「盔甲的护胸板」，后引申为「托架、括号（形似托架的符号）、等级段」等义

similar_words.description: 「bracket」指括号、托架或等级段，「brace」指支撑结构或支撑、振作的动作，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: Neuroscientists are studying how the human brain processes visual information under low-light conditions.
She has the brains and perseverance to succeed in the highly competitive graduate admission test.

brain.etymology: 源自古英语「brægen」，本义为「脑髓」，后引申出「智慧、智力」的比喻义

similar_words.description: 「brain」侧重生理上的脑或具体的智力能力，「mind」侧重抽象的思维、意识或记忆

definitions.example: The truck driver braked suddenly to avoid hitting a stray dog crossing the highway.
The central bank raised benchmark interest rates to brake the rapid expansion of market credit.

brake.etymology: 源自古英语「brecan（打破）」，原指阻止运动的装置，后引申出「抑制、约束」的抽象义

similar_words.description: 「brake」指刹车、制动或抑制的动作，「break」指打破、折断或中断，拼写完全同音形近易混淆

definitions.example: The local branch of the national library provides specialized academic resources for postgraduate students.

branch.etymology: 源自古英语「branc」，本义为「树木的细枝」，后引申出「机构、学科的分支」等义

similar_words.description: 「bough」指树木的粗枝，「branch」可指树木的细枝或机构、学科的分支，含义范围更广

definitions.example: Numerous studies in consumer behavior have shown that a reputable brand can enhance customers' willingness to pay a premium price.
The tabloid tried to brand the celebrity as a fraud without any verified evidence.

brand.etymology: 源自古英语brand，原指「火、火炬」，古代用火炬给牲畜打烙印，后引申为「标记、品牌」，作动词时引申为「污蔑、扣帽子」。

similar_words.description: trademark侧重官方注册的商标，brand侧重消费者认知的品牌形象。

definitions.example: After the formal banquet, the gentlemen were invited to the study for brandy and cigars.

brandy.etymology: 源自荷兰语brandewijn（意为「烧酒」），后缩写为brandy，指以水果为原料蒸馏制成的烈性酒。

similar_words.description: brand为名词「品牌」或动词「污蔑」，brandy为名词「白兰地」，拼写仅差末尾的字母y，易混淆。

definitions.example: The ancient temple's door handles were crafted from polished brass, which retained a golden luster after centuries.
He had the brass to ask for a promotion after missing three important deadlines in a row.

brass.etymology: 源自古英语bræs，原指铜锌合金（黄铜），因黄铜色显眼，引申出「厚颜无耻」的僻义。

similar_words.description: bronze为青铜（铜锡合金），brass为黄铜（铜锌合金），均为铜基合金但成分不同。

definitions.example: The brave rescue team ventured into the collapsed building to search for survivors despite the ongoing aftershocks.
She braved the harsh winter storm to deliver life-saving medicine to the remote village.

brave.etymology: 源自意大利语bravo，原指「野蛮的」，后引申为「勇敢的」，作动词时表示「像勇士般面对」。

similar_words.description: courageous侧重基于道德信念的勇敢，brave侧重行动上的果断无畏。

definitions.meaning: （协议、法律、承诺等的）违背，违反
definitions.example: The multinational corporation was fined heavily for breach of environmental protection regulations.
The court ruled that the defendant had breached the terms of the employment contract.

breach.etymology: 源自古法语breche（意为「打破」），原指「打破的缺口」，后引申为对约定、规则的「违背」。

similar_words.description: violation侧重违反具体规则的行为，breach侧重对契约、承诺等的违背。

definitions.meaning: 宽度，广度；（知识、经验等的）广泛程度
definitions.example: The breadth of the scholar's interdisciplinary knowledge made her research stand out in the academic community.

breadth.etymology: 由古英语brād（宽的）加名词后缀-th构成，表「宽的性质或状态」。

similar_words.description: breadth为「宽度、广度」，breath为「呼吸」，拼写仅差「a」与「e」的位置，极易混淆。

definitions.meaning: （机器、系统的）故障；（关系、谈判的）破裂
definitions.example: The breakdown of the peace negotiations led to a resurgence of violence in the region.
The financial report included a detailed breakdown of the company's quarterly revenue and expenses.

breakdown.etymology: 由短语动词「break down」（分解、出故障）转化而来，原指「分解成碎片」，后引申为故障、破裂、明细等义。

similar_words.description: collapse侧重突然的完全崩溃，breakdown侧重逐渐出现的故障或破裂。

definitions.example: She clutched the old locket to her breast, remembering her late grandmother.

definitions.meaning: 勇敢面对，承担（压力、困难等）
definitions.example: The young politician had to breast intense public scrutiny after her controversial speech.

breast.etymology: 源自古英语brēost（胸部），原指身体前部，作动词时引申为「挺胸面对」。

similar_words.description: chest侧重胸部的骨骼及胸腔部分，breast侧重胸部的软组织或情感层面的「胸膛」。

definitions.example: After climbing the steep mountain path, she stopped to catch her breath and admire the view.
The new manager's innovative ideas were a breath of fresh air for the stagnant company.

breath.etymology: 源自古英语brǣth（气味、呼吸），原指呼出的气味，后引申为呼吸、气息等义。

similar_words.description: breath为名词「呼吸」，breathe为动词「呼吸」，breathe末尾多一个字母e，极易混淆。

definitions.example: The yoga instructor reminded the students to breathe slowly and deeply during the meditation session.
He breathed the confidential plan to his trusted ally before the meeting.

breathe.etymology: 源自breath的动词形式，古英语brǣthan（呼吸），后引申为「低声说」（像呼出气息般轻声说话）。

similar_words.description: breathe为动词「呼吸」，breath为名词「呼吸」，breathe末尾多一个字母e，极易混淆。

definitions.example: Farmers breed high-yield dairy cows to boost milk production in the agricultural demonstration zone.
This rare breed of sheep is known for its soft and thick wool.

breed.etymology: 来自古英语bredan，本义为‘孵化、生育’，核心语义与‘繁衍、培育’相关，无复杂词缀

similar_words.description: breed侧重‘繁殖、培育’或‘品种’，breeze侧重‘微风’或‘轻快行进’，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: A gentle breeze carried the scent of cherry blossoms across the university campus.

definitions.meaning: 轻快地行进；轻易通过（考试等）
definitions.example: She breezed through the postgraduate entrance exam after months of diligent preparation.

breeze.etymology: 来自古西班牙语briza，原指‘东北风’，后泛指‘微风’，动词义由‘风的轻快’引申而来

similar_words.description: breeze侧重‘微风’或‘轻快行进’，breed侧重‘繁殖、培育’或‘品种’，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The former official was sentenced to prison for bribing regulatory staff to evade inspection.
The company was fined heavily for offering bribes to local government officials.

bribe.etymology: 来自古法语bribe，原指‘面包屑’，后引申为‘小恩小惠’，最终演变为‘贿赂’的核心义

similar_words.description: bribe指‘贿赂（动/名）’，bride指‘新娘’，拼写仅差一个字母易混淆

definitions.example: The ancient city wall, built with blue bricks, has stood intact for over 600 years.
The abandoned factory's windows were bricked up to prevent trespassing.

brick.etymology: 来自荷兰语brik，本义为‘陶土烧制的块状建筑材料’，即‘砖’，动词义由‘用砖覆盖’引申而来

similar_words.description: brick是名词‘砖’或动词‘用砖堵住’，break是动词‘打破’或名词‘休息’，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The bride and groom exchanged vows in the historic church surrounded by family and friends.

bride.etymology: 来自古英语bryd，本义为‘即将结婚的女子’，即‘新娘’，语义无明显引申

similar_words.description: bride指‘新娘’，bribe指‘贿赂（动/名）’，拼写仅差一个字母易混淆

definitions.example: The editor asked the contributor to submit a brief summary of the academic paper within three days.
The coach briefed the athletes on the new competition rules before the match.

brief.etymology: 来自拉丁语brevis（短的），衍生出‘短暂的、简洁的’形容词义，后引申出‘摘要’和‘简要介绍’的动/名词义

similar_words.description: brief是形容词‘短暂的’、动词‘简要介绍’，briefcase是同根名词‘公文包’，语义相关

definitions.example: The job applicant placed his resume and portfolio in a leather briefcase before entering the interview room.

briefcase.etymology: 由同根词brief（指文件、摘要）+ case（容器）合成，指‘装文件的容器’，即‘公文包’

similar_words.description: briefcase是‘公文包’，brief是其同根词，表‘摘要、短暂的’等义

definitions.example: The brilliant young scholar was awarded a national research grant for his innovative work in artificial intelligence.
The brilliant sunset over the sea painted the sky in shades of orange and purple.

brilliant.etymology: 来自拉丁语brillare（发光），原指‘明亮的’，后引申为‘才华横溢的、杰出的’（如‘发光的才华’）

similar_words.description: brilliant侧重‘才华横溢的、杰出的’，语义较强；bright侧重‘明亮的、聪明的（尤指孩童）’，语义较宽泛

definitions.example: She filled the teacup to the brim before serving it to the guest.
The little girl's eyes brimmed with joy when she received the birthday gift from her parents.

brim.etymology: 来自古英语brym，本义为‘水边、边缘’，动词义由‘容器边缘满到溢出’引申为‘（情感等）充满’

similar_words.description: brim可作动词表‘充满’或名词表‘边缘’，rim仅作名词表‘边缘’，拼写相近且语义相关

definitions.example: The old man took a brisk walk in the park every morning to keep fit.
During the holiday season, the local souvenir shop did brisk business with tourists from home and abroad.

brisk.etymology: 来自中古英语brisk，本义为‘活泼的’，后引申为‘轻快的’（动作）和‘兴隆的’（生意，如活跃的交易）

similar_words.description: brisk是形容词‘轻快的、兴隆的’，risk是名词/动词‘风险、冒险’，拼写仅差一个字母易混淆

definitions.example: Dry, brittle branches snapped under the weight of the heavy snow covering the forest.

brittle.etymology: 来自古英语「brytel」，词根「-brit-」表‘打破’，加形容词后缀「-le」，表‘易被打破的’

similar_words.description: 「brittle」指易碎的、脆的，「bristle」指刚毛、鬃毛，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The broad river separating the two provinces is a key shipping route for goods.
Postgraduate students need a broad range of academic knowledge to conduct interdisciplinary research.

broad.etymology: 来自古英语「brād」，基础形容词，无复杂词缀，表‘宽的、广的’

similar_words.description: 「broad」为形容词表宽的、广泛的，「board」为名词表木板、董事会，拼写仅一个字母之差易混淆

definitions.example: The official press conference on postgraduate enrollment will be broadcast live across all major media platforms.
His weekly broadcast on postgraduate exam strategies has a large and loyal audience.

broadcast.etymology: 合成词，由「broad（广泛的）+ cast（播送、投掷）」构成，字面义为‘广泛播送’

similar_words.description: 「broadcast」可指电台、电视或网络广播，「telecast」特指电视广播，二者语义范围不同

definitions.meaning: 小册子，手册（用于宣传、介绍）
definitions.example: The university's graduate admissions office distributes a detailed brochure to all prospective postgraduate applicants.

brochure.etymology: 来自法语「brochure」，词根「broch-」表‘缝、钉’，原指缝订而成的小型册子，后引申为宣传手册

similar_words.description: 「brochure」多为印刷精美的宣传或介绍类小册子，「pamphlet」多为内容简短、常带观点的小册子

definitions.example: The archaeologists discovered a well-preserved bronze vessel from the Warring States Period in the excavation site.
The setting sun cast a warm bronze glow over the campus library's roof.

bronze.etymology: 来自意大利语「bronzo」，原指铜锡合金（青铜），后引申为青铜色的

similar_words.description: 「bronze」指青铜（铜锡合金）或青铜色的，「brass」指黄铜（铜锌合金），二者均为铜合金但成分不同，拼写相近

definitions.example: A small brook runs through the university's botanical garden, providing water for various rare plants.
No reputable academic journal will brook any form of data fabrication or plagiarism.

brook.etymology: 来自古英语「brōc」，原指‘小溪’；动词义由‘让水流过’引申为‘忍受、容忍’，属于熟词僻义

similar_words.description: 「brook」指规模较小的天然小溪，「creek」指稍大的溪流或海湾的小湾

definitions.example: The janitor used a broom to sweep the fallen pine needles off the classroom corridor every morning.
Bright yellow broom flowers bloom along the hillside near the research station in early summer.

broom.etymology: 来自古英语「brōm」，原指‘金雀花’（其枝条可制作扫帚），后引申为‘扫帚’

similar_words.description: 「broom」指扫帚或金雀花，「boom」指繁荣、激增或轰鸣声，拼写仅一个字母之差易混淆

definitions.example: After three hours of continuous exam practice, he wiped the sweat from his brow with a handkerchief.
We finally reached the brow of the mountain and saw the entire valley with scattered villages below.

brow.etymology: 来自古英语「brū」，原指‘眉毛、额’，后因山脊形似眉脊引申为‘山脊’

similar_words.description: 「brow」指额、眉毛或山脊，「brown」指棕色（的），拼写仅一个字母之差易混淆

definitions.meaning: 浏览（书刊、网页、数据库等）
definitions.example: Most postgraduate students browse academic databases such as Web of Science and CNKI daily to track latest research.
The deer were browsing on the tender leaves of oak trees in the forest near the research center.

browse.etymology: 来自古英语「brōsian」，原指‘动物吃草’，后引申为人类‘随意浏览’（像动物吃草一样随意取食信息）

similar_words.description: 「browse」指随意、悠闲地浏览，「scan」指快速扫视以寻找特定信息，二者浏览目的不同

definitions.example: The cyclist got a large bruise on her arm after falling off the bike on the way to the postgraduate exam hall.
The delicate glassware bruises easily if not handled with extreme care during transportation.

bruise.etymology: 来自古英语「brȳsan」，原指‘压碎、碾碎’，后引申为‘瘀伤、挫伤’（皮肤被压碎后的痕迹）

similar_words.description: 「bruise」指瘀伤或挫伤，「brush」指刷子、刷或轻触，拼写仅一个字母之差易混淆

definitions.example: The brutal military intervention caused massive casualties and destroyed local infrastructure.

brutal.etymology: 来自拉丁语「brutus」（愚笨、野蛮的），加形容词后缀「-al」构成，表具有野蛮属性的

similar_words.description: 「brute」可作名词（畜生、残暴的人）或形容词（蛮横的），二者同根「brut-」（表野蛮），「brutal」仅作形容词，侧重残酷无情的

definitions.meaning: 气泡；（经济、金融领域的）虚假繁荣泡沫
definitions.example: The dot-com bubble in the late 1990s burst eventually, leading to a wave of tech company bankruptcies.

bubble.etymology: 来自中古英语「bobel」（水泡），为拟声词，模拟气泡破裂的声音

similar_words.description: 「babble」作动词表唠叨、（水流）潺潺作响，与「bubble」拼写仅差一个字母，语义完全不同

definitions.example: The volunteer carried a bucket of fresh water to the elderly residents in the remote village.
It bucketed down throughout the weekend, forcing the outdoor archaeological excavation to be suspended.

bucket.etymology: 来自古英语「buc」（容器、桶），原指皮革制的盛水容器

similar_words.description: 「buck」作名词表雄鹿、（非正式）美元，与「bucket」拼写接近，语义差异显著

definitions.meaning: 芽，花蕾；（思想、计划等的）萌芽
definitions.example: The bud of a new cross-disciplinary research project began to take shape after the academic forum.

bud.etymology: 来自中古英语「budde」（芽、花蕾），可能与德语「Knospe」（芽）同源

similar_words.description: 「bid」作动词/名词表投标、出价、命令，与「bud」拼写仅差一个字母，语义不同

definitions.meaning: 预算（尤指政府、企业或家庭的收支计划）
definitions.example: The Ministry of Education announced an increase in the annual budget for basic education and vocational training.
Many students choose budget accommodation near the university to reduce living expenses.

budget.etymology: 来自古法语「bougette」（小钱包），原指装硬币的小皮袋，18世纪引申为收支计划

similar_words.description: 「estimate」侧重对数量、成本的大致估算，「budget」侧重有计划的收支安排
「buddy」作名词表朋友、伙伴（非正式），与「budget」拼写接近，语义不同

definitions.example: The international academic symposium provided a Western-style buffet for all registered participants.
The coastal research station was buffeted by the strong gales and storm surges from the typhoon.

buffet.etymology: 来自古法语「buffeter」（猛击），名词义自助餐源于19世纪美国的餐柜（buffet），后引申为自助餐饮形式

similar_words.description: 「buffalo」作名词表水牛、野牛，与「buffet」拼写接近，语义完全不同

definitions.example: The agricultural researcher found that the new pesticide could effectively kill the bugs damaging the rice crops.
The software engineer spent three days fixing the critical bug in the data analysis program.

bug.etymology: 来自中古英语「bugge」（怪物、妖怪），后指小昆虫，1940年代因计算机故障被称为「bug」而引申出漏洞义

similar_words.description: 「bag」作名词表包、袋，与「bug」拼写仅差一个字母，语义不同

definitions.example: The lab technician replaced the burnt-out bulb in the spectrophotometer to continue the chemical analysis.

bulb.etymology: 来自拉丁语「bulbus」（球茎、洋葱头），因电灯泡形状类似植物球茎而得名

similar_words.description: 「bulk」作名词表大量、主体，与「bulb」拼写接近，语义不同

definitions.meaning: 大量；（某事物的）主体，大部分
definitions.example: The bulk of the research findings in the paper were derived from large-scale clinical trials.

bulk.etymology: 来自古挪威语「bulki」（堆、大量），原指堆积的货物，后引申为主体、大部分

similar_words.description: 「bull」作名词表公牛、股市看涨者，与「bulk」拼写接近，语义不同

definitions.example: The rancher raised several strong bulls for breeding and agricultural labor in the countryside.
Financial analysts predicted that the bull market in the technology sector would last for at least another quarter.

bull.etymology: 来自古英语「bula」（公牛），与德语「Bul」（公牛）同源，金融义牛市、看涨者源于18世纪证券市场术语

similar_words.description: 「pull」作动词表拉、拖，与「bull」拼写仅差一个字母，语义不同

definitions.example: The soldier ducked to avoid the bullet fired from the enemy's sentry post.

bullet.etymology: 源自古法语boulette（小球），无常见考研级可拆分词缀

similar_words.description: bullet指子弹，bulletin指官方公告或简报

definitions.example: The university's graduate school posted a bulletin on its official platform regarding the 2025 postgraduate re-examination arrangement.

bulletin.etymology: bul-（原指折叠成球状的文件）+ -letin（小词后缀），指小型公告文件

similar_words.description: bulletin指官方公告，bullet指子弹

definitions.example: Educational studies indicate that students who are bullied during adolescence have a higher risk of anxiety disorders.

bully.etymology: 词源较为模糊，无常见考研级可拆分词缀

similar_words.description: bully指欺凌或欺凌者，bull指公牛

definitions.example: She bumped into a senior postgraduate student while rushing to attend the academic seminar held by the college.

bump.etymology: 拟声词，模拟物体碰撞的声响，无常见考研级词缀

similar_words.description: bump指碰撞或肿块，bunch指束、串或群

definitions.example: The research team collected a bunch of first-hand data through field surveys to support their thesis.

bunch.etymology: 源自古英语bunc（堆），无常见考研级可拆分词缀

similar_words.description: bunch多指松散的束、群，bundle多指捆绑好的捆、包

definitions.example: The archivist organized a bundle of old academic manuscripts and placed them in the climate-controlled storage room.

bundle.etymology: bund-（捆绑）+ -le（小词后缀），指捆绑好的小型包裹

similar_words.description: bundle指捆绑好的捆、包，bunch多指松散的束、群

definitions.example: The increasing tuition fees for postgraduate programs have imposed a heavy financial burden on many rural households.

burden.etymology: 源自古英语byrthen（负荷），无常见考研级可拆分词缀

similar_words.description: burden多指精神、经济等抽象负担，load多指物理重量或具体负载

definitions.example: The Bureau of Postgraduate Education issued a new regulation on the supervision of thesis defense procedures.
She put her postgraduate admission notice in the bottom drawer of the wooden bureau in her dormitory.

bureau.etymology: 源自古法语bureau（办公桌，原指办公场所），无常见考研级可拆分词缀

similar_words.description: bureau指政府机构或衣柜，bureaucracy指官僚制度或官僚机构

definitions.example: The academic community has long criticized the rigid bureaucracy that hinders the efficiency of scientific research funding allocation.

bureaucracy.etymology: bureau-（办公机构）+ -cracy（统治后缀，如「democracy」），指由办公机构主导的统治制度

similar_words.description: bureaucracy指官僚制度或机构，bureau指具体的政府局或衣柜

definitions.example: The burglar broke into the research institute's office and stole several confidential documents related to national key scientific projects.

burglar.etymology: burg-（盗窃）+ -lar（指人后缀，如「scholar」），指实施盗窃行为的人

similar_words.description: burglar指窃贼（人），burglary指盗窃行为

definitions.example: Many historic wooden structures burned to the ground in the wildfire that swept through the mountainous region.

definitions.meaning: （内心）充满（强烈情感，如愤怒、渴望）
definitions.example: She burned with anger when she learned the unfair treatment of the vulnerable rural residents.

burn.etymology: 源自古英语byrnan，为日耳曼语族本族语动词，无派生构词结构

similar_words.description: 「burn」侧重燃烧或情感灼烧；「burst」侧重突然破裂或情感迸发
「combust」为正式学术用语，指化学燃烧；「burn」为通用词，范围更广

definitions.example: The dam burst after days of heavy rain, causing severe flooding in the downstream villages.
New research ideas burst forth during the interdisciplinary academic seminar on climate change.

burst.etymology: 源自古英语berstan，为日耳曼语族本族语动词，表「破裂」核心义

similar_words.description: 「explode」侧重物理爆炸或情感爆发；「burst」侧重容器破裂或状态突然转变
「burst」表破裂/迸发；「burn」表燃烧/情感灼烧

definitions.example: Archaeologists carefully reburied the unearthed ancient pottery to protect it from environmental damage.
He buried his childhood trauma deep in his heart and never mentioned it to anyone.

bury.etymology: 源自古英语byrgan，为日耳曼语族本族语动词，表「埋葬」核心义

similar_words.description: 「bury」为动词，表埋葬；「berry」为名词，表浆果

definitions.example: The wildlife reserve is covered with dense bushes that provide shelter for rare small mammals.

bush.etymology: 源自古英语bysc，为日耳曼语族本族语名词，表「灌木」核心义

similar_words.description: 「bush」指独立灌木或灌木丛；「brush」指刷子或低矮灌木丛

definitions.example: The government introduced tax cuts to support small and medium-sized businesses during the economic recession.
It is none of your business to interfere with the academic choices of other students.

business.etymology: 由古英语bisignis（忙碌）派生，后缀-ness表名词，原指「忙碌的事」，后衍生出「商业」义

similar_words.description: 「commerce」为正式用语，指大规模贸易；「business」为通用词，涵盖生意、事务等多义
「affair」侧重重大事务或绯闻；「business」侧重商业或私人/机构的日常事务

definitions.example: The local butcher sells organic grass-fed beef at the weekly farmers' market.
The dictator was labeled a butcher for his brutal suppression of political dissidents.

butcher.etymology: 源自古法语bouchier（杀羊者），进入英语后指屠夫、肉商

similar_words.description: 「butcher」指屠夫；「butler」指男管家

definitions.example: Many processed snacks contain high levels of saturated fat from butter and palm oil.

butter.etymology: 源自古英语butere，来自拉丁语butyrum，表「黄油」核心义

similar_words.description: 「butter」为名词，指黄油；「batter」为名词/动词，指面糊或猛击

definitions.example: The ecological study found that butterfly populations have declined sharply due to pesticide overuse.

butterfly.etymology: 由「butter（黄油）」和「fly（飞虫）」构成，相传蝴蝶因偷食黄油而得名

similar_words.description: 「butterfly」指蝴蝶；「dragonfly」指蜻蜓

definitions.example: Press the red emergency button to stop the industrial machine immediately in case of malfunction.
She buttoned her wool coat tightly to keep out the freezing winter wind.

button.etymology: 源自古法语bouton（芽、纽扣），进入英语后指纽扣、按钮

similar_words.description: 「button」指纽扣/按钮；「baton」指指挥棒或警棍

definitions.example: Some enterprises try to bypass environmental regulations by relocating factories to remote rural areas.

definitions.meaning: 旁道；（医疗）旁路（如搭桥手术）
definitions.example: The elderly patient underwent a coronary artery bypass surgery to treat severe heart disease.

bypass.etymology: 由「by（在旁）」和「pass（通过）」构成，指从旁通过的路径或行为

similar_words.description: 「bypass」为动词/名词，表绕过；「passby」为名词，表过路人

definitions.example: After missing the last campus shuttle, she hailed a cab to the downtown library.

cab.etymology: 源自中古英语，原指车厢，现多用于指出租汽车

similar_words.description: cab指出租汽车，cap指帽子、盖子

definitions.example: The dormitory cafeteria served braised cabbage with tofu for vegetarian students.

cabbage.etymology: 源自中古法语caboche（意为「头」），因卷心菜形似头部得名

similar_words.description: cabbage指卷心菜，cabbie指出租车司机（口语）

definitions.example: The field research team stayed in a log cabin near the mountain for two weeks.

cabin.etymology: 源自古法语cabane，原指小棚屋，后引申为小木屋、客舱

similar_words.description: cabin指小木屋/客舱，cabinet指内阁/橱柜

definitions.meaning: 内阁（政府核心决策机构）
definitions.example: The prime minister summoned the cabinet to discuss the new climate policy.

cabinet.etymology: 源自拉丁语capanna（小棚屋），后引申为「内阁（密室议事的核心团体）」

similar_words.description: cabinet指内阁/橱柜，cabin指小木屋/客舱

definitions.example: The submarine cable failure caused a three-day internet blackout in the coastal region.

cable.etymology: 源自拉丁语capulum（绳索），现多指电缆、缆绳

similar_words.description: cable指电缆/缆绳，table指桌子、表格

definitions.example: Many postgraduate students hold group discussions in the campus cafe after class.

cafe.etymology: 源自法语café，原指咖啡，后指售卖咖啡的小型场所

similar_words.description: cafe指小型非自助咖啡馆/餐馆，cafeteria指自助餐厅

definitions.meaning: 自助餐厅（尤指学校、工厂的）
definitions.example: The university cafeteria provides discounted meal plans for low-income students.

cafeteria.etymology: 源自西班牙语cafetería，原指咖啡馆，后引申为自助式餐厅

similar_words.description: cafeteria指自助餐厅，cafe指小型非自助咖啡馆/餐馆

definitions.example: The behavioral study observed the reaction of mice in a controlled experimental cage.

cage.etymology: 源自拉丁语cavea（洞穴、囚笼），现多指笼子

similar_words.description: cage指笼子，cafe指咖啡馆

definitions.example: The graduation ceremony concluded with a cake-cutting ceremony for all graduates.
The hiker used a cake of beeswax to waterproof his hiking boots.

cake.etymology: 源自古英语kaka，原指小型烘焙面食，后引申为蛋糕、饼状物

similar_words.description: cake指蛋糕/饼状物，cage指笼子

definitions.example: Adequate calcium intake is crucial for preventing osteoporosis in elderly populations.

calcium.etymology: 源自拉丁语calx（石灰），因钙为石灰主要成分得名，词根calc-表「石灰、钙」

similar_words.description: calcium指化学元素钙，calculus指微积分、结石，二者同根calc-

definitions.example: Researchers calculated that the global urban population would account for 68% of the total population by 2050, according to 「a UN report」.
Scientists calculated that the newly discovered dinosaur fossil dates back to approximately 65 million years ago.

calculate.etymology: 源自拉丁语「calculare」，原指用小石子（calculus）计数，核心词根为「calcul-（计算）」

similar_words.description: estimate侧重粗略估计，calculate侧重基于数据的精确计算或推测

definitions.example: The academic calendar of most British universities is divided into three terms: autumn, spring and summer.

calendar.etymology: 源自拉丁语「calendae」（古罗马每月首日，还债登记日），后引申为记录日期的日历或校历

similar_words.description: calendar指按年/月/日排列的固定时间记录表，schedule指具体的日程安排或计划

definitions.example: The judge remained calm throughout the intense cross-examination, ensuring the fair trial of the case.
The teacher tried to calm the nervous students before the final examination.

calm.etymology: 源自古法语「calme」，原指天气平静，后引申为人的情绪镇定或环境无骚动

similar_words.description: calm侧重人情绪镇定或环境无骚动，still侧重完全静止、无动静

definitions.example: Nutritionists emphasize that reducing excessive calorie intake is crucial for preventing obesity and related chronic diseases.

calorie.etymology: 源自拉丁语「calor（热）」，核心词根为「calor-（热）」，指衡量食物热量的单位

similar_words.description: calorie为标准拼写，calory为非正式变体拼写

definitions.example: Camels, known as 「ships of the desert」, can survive for weeks without water due to their unique physiological structure.

camel.etymology: 源自希腊语「kamelos」，经拉丁语传入英语，指适应沙漠环境的骆驼

similar_words.description: camel为沙漠骆驼（单峰/双峰），llama为南美羊驼，均属驼科动物

definitions.example: The surveillance camera installed at the bank entrance captured the entire process of the robbery, providing critical clues for the police.

camera.etymology: 源自拉丁语「camera（房间、暗箱）」，原指早期照相机的暗箱结构，后引申为照相机或摄像机

similar_words.description: camcorder是camera与recorder的复合词，指便携式摄像机

definitions.example: The two opposing political camps in the parliament failed to reach a consensus on 「the carbon neutrality bill」 after three rounds of negotiations.
Many college students choose to camp in the national forest during their summer vacation to experience nature.

camp.etymology: 源自拉丁语「campus（田野、战场）」，核心词根为「camp-（田野）」，原指军队在田野的营地，后引申为营地或阵营

similar_words.description: camp指营地/阵营，campus指大学校园，二者同属「camp-（田野）」词根，易因拼写相近混淆

definitions.example: The anti-smoking campaign launched by 「the Ministry of Health」 has significantly reduced the smoking rate among adolescents in urban areas.
The presidential candidate campaigned across the country to appeal for more support from working-class voters.

campaign.etymology: 源自词根「camp-（田野）」，原指军队在田野展开的军事战役，后引申为社会运动或竞选活动

similar_words.description: camp指营地/阵营，campaign指由阵营发起的运动或战役，二者同根

definitions.example: Many top universities have established international branch campuses to attract global talent and enhance their academic influence.

campus.etymology: 源自拉丁语「campus（田野）」，原指大学的开阔场地，后引申为大学校园

similar_words.description: campus指大学校园，camp指营地/阵营，二者同属「camp-（田野）」词根，易因拼写相近混淆

definitions.example: 「The Panama Canal」, completed in 1914, has greatly shortened the shipping distance between the Atlantic and Pacific Oceans.

canal.etymology: 源自拉丁语「canna（管、芦苇）」，原指用芦苇制成的导水管，后引申为人工开凿的运河或沟渠

similar_words.description: canal指人工开凿的运河/沟渠，channel指天然或人工的水道、频道

definitions.example: Due to the unexpected public health event, the international academic forum scheduled for next month has been canceled.

cancel.etymology: 源自拉丁语cancellare（用交叉线划掉），词根本身为cancel-，表‘划掉、消除’

similar_words.description: 「cancel」为动词表取消、撤销，「council」为名词表委员会、理事会，拼写仅首字母后字母不同

definitions.example: Early screening and diagnosis are crucial for improving the survival rate of cancer patients.
Corruption is often regarded as a cancer that erodes the foundation of a fair society.

cancer.etymology: 源自拉丁语cancer（螃蟹），因肿瘤扩散形态类似蟹钳得名，词根本身为cancer-

similar_words.description: 「cancer」为名词表癌症、弊端，「cancel」为动词表取消、撤销，拼写仅差一个字母

definitions.example: Over 1.2 million candidates registered for the national postgraduate entrance examination in 2024.

candidate.etymology: 源自拉丁语candidatus（穿白衣的人，古罗马候选人需穿白衣以示公正），词根candid-（白、纯洁），后缀-ate（表人）

similar_words.description: 「candidate」多指公职竞选或考试的候选人、考生，「applicant」多指求职或入学的申请者

definitions.example: During the power outage, we lit a candle to continue reading the academic literature.

candle.etymology: 源自古英语candel，来自拉丁语candela（蜡烛），词根cand-（发光、炽热）

similar_words.description: 「candle」为名词表蜡烛，「candy」为名词表糖果，拼写仅后缀字母不同

definitions.example: Excessive intake of candy is closely associated with dental caries and childhood obesity.

candy.etymology: 源自阿拉伯语qandi（糖），经中世纪拉丁语传入英语，词根本身为candy-

similar_words.description: 「candy」为名词表糖果，「candle」为名词表蜡烛，拼写仅后缀字母不同

definitions.example: The ancient fortress was equipped with dozens of cannons to defend against enemy invasions.

cannon.etymology: 源自意大利语cannone（大管、大炮），词根cann-（管、筒），后缀-one（表大）

similar_words.description: 「cannon」为名词表大炮，「canoe」为名词/动词表独木舟/划独木舟，拼写仅差一个字母

definitions.meaning: n. 独木舟；v. 划独木舟
definitions.example: We rented a canoe to explore the untouched lake surrounded by primitive forests.

canoe.etymology: 源自加勒比语canoa（独木舟），经西班牙语传入英语，词根本身为canoe-

similar_words.description: 「canoe」为名词/动词表独木舟/划独木舟，「cannon」为名词表大炮，拼写仅差一个字母

definitions.example: Most college students choose to eat in the campus canteen to cut down on living expenses.

canteen.etymology: 源自意大利语cantina（储存食物饮料的仓库，后指食堂），词根本身为cant-（原指‘角落、储物处’）

similar_words.description: 「canteen」多指机构内部的食堂、小卖部，「cafeteria」多指对外营业的自助餐厅

definitions.example: The renowned artist used a huge canvas to depict the landscape of rural England.

canvas.etymology: 源自拉丁语cannabis（大麻，早期帆布用大麻纤维制作），词根本身为canv-（纤维、织物）

similar_words.description: 「canvas」为名词表帆布、画布，「canvass」为动词表游说、拉选票，拼写仅一个字母之差（canvas为单s，canvass为双s）

definitions.example: He wore a baseball cap to shield his eyes from the harsh sunlight during the field research.

definitions.meaning: n. （价格、数量的）上限；v. 限制
definitions.example: The local government has imposed a cap on residential rent to protect low-income tenants.

cap.etymology: 源自古英语cæppe（帽子），词根本身为cap-（覆盖物、顶部）

similar_words.description: 「cap」为名词/动词表帽子/限制，「cab」为名词表出租车，拼写仅一个字母不同

definitions.example: Only a capable research team can collect and analyze such a large volume of experimental data accurately within the deadline.

capable.etymology: 词根「cap-」（抓住、掌握）+形容词后缀「-able」（可…的），意为能掌握事物、具备相应能力的

similar_words.description: 均表有能力的，「capable」侧重具备做某事的一般能力，「competent」侧重胜任某一具体岗位或任务

definitions.example: The newly-built conference hall has a seating capacity of 5,000, which can meet the needs of large-scale academic forums.

capacity.etymology: 词根「cap-」（容纳、掌握）+名词后缀「-ity」（表属性、状态），指容纳的空间量或具备的内在能力

similar_words.description: 同根易混词，「capacity」为名词，表容量/能力；「capable」为形容词，表有能力的

definitions.example: In maritime history, well-known capes such as the Cape of Good Hope have long served as crucial navigation markers for global sailors.

cape.etymology: 词根「cap-」（头、顶端），原指头部，引申为陆地向海洋突出的顶端即海角

similar_words.description: 形近易混词，「cape」指海角/披肩，「cap」指帽子

definitions.meaning: n. 资本，资金；首都 a. 资本的；大写的
definitions.example: Venture capital plays a vital role in supporting the growth of innovative start-ups in high-tech industries.

capital.etymology: 词根「cap-」（头、首要）+名词后缀「-al」（表事物），原指首要的事物，引申为首都、资本等核心含义

similar_words.description: 形近易混词，「capital」指首都/资本，「capitol」指（美国）国会大厦

definitions.example: Many academic papers have discussed the impact of global capitalism on the economic development of developing countries.

capitalism.etymology: 由核心词「capital」（资本）+名词后缀「-ism」（表主义、制度）构成，指以资本为核心生产要素的经济制度

similar_words.description: 同后缀易混词，「capitalism」指资本主义，「socialism」指社会主义

definitions.example: The medical capsule designed by the research team can reach the deep part of the digestive tract and transmit real-time images.

capsule.etymology: 词根「cap-」（包裹）+指小后缀「-sule」，指被包裹的小型密闭容器，引申为胶囊、太空舱等含义

similar_words.description: 形近易混词，「capsule」指胶囊/太空舱，「capsize」指（船、艇）倾覆

definitions.example: The captain of the women's volleyball team led her teammates to win the championship in the international competition.

captain.etymology: 词根「cap-」（头、首）+词根「-tain」（持有、担任），指担任头领角色的人，如队长、船长

similar_words.description: 形近易混词，「captain」指队长/船长，「capable」指有能力的

definitions.meaning: a. 被俘虏的，被监禁的 n. 俘虏，囚徒
definitions.example: The human rights organization called for the immediate release of all civilians held captive in the conflict zone.

captive.etymology: 词根「cap-」（俘获、抓住）+形容词后缀「-ive」，指被抓住的状态，引申为俘虏

similar_words.description: 同根易混词，「captive」侧重状态（被俘虏的/俘虏），「capture」侧重动作（俘获/捕获）

definitions.meaning: v. 俘获，捕获；夺取，占领 n. 俘获，捕获
definitions.example: Wildlife photographers use high-speed cameras to capture the rare moment when a snow leopard hunts its prey.

capture.etymology: 词根「cap-」（抓住）+名词后缀「-ure」，作动词指抓住的动作，作名词指捕获的行为或结果

similar_words.description: 同根易混词，「capture」侧重动作（俘获），「captive」侧重状态（被俘虏的）

definitions.example: Nutritionists suggest that athletes should consume an appropriate amount of carbohydrate to replenish energy after intense training.

carbohydrate.etymology: 词根「carbo-」（碳）+词根「hydr-」（水）+名词后缀「-ate」，指由碳、氢、氧组成的糖类有机化合物

similar_words.description: 形近同根易混词，「carbohydrate」指碳水化合物，「carbon」指碳元素

definitions.meaning: 碳（化学元素，环保语境常用的碳排放核心要素）
definitions.example: Many scientists argue that reducing carbon emissions is crucial to mitigating global climate change.

carbon.etymology: 源自拉丁语「carbo」（木炭），因碳最初从木炭中提取得名

similar_words.description: 二者同根于拉丁语「carbo」；carbon指化学元素「碳」，carbohydrate指「碳水化合物（糖类）」

definitions.example: The cardinal principle of academic research is to uphold objectivity and methodological rigor.
The cardinal delivered a speech on interfaith dialogue at the international conference.

cardinal.etymology: 源自拉丁语「cardinalis」（枢纽的，关键的），原指铰链，引申为「核心的」；后用于指天主教会核心神职人员

similar_words.description: cardinal作形容词指「主要的、核心的」，capital作形容词指「大写的、资本的」，作名词指「首都、资本」

definitions.example: Many postgraduates prioritize long-term career development over immediate high salaries when selecting jobs.

career.etymology: 源自拉丁语「carraria」（车行道），原指马车行驶的轨道，引申为「人生轨迹、职业发展路径」

similar_words.description: career指「职业、事业」，carrier指「载体、运输公司」

definitions.example: The elderly woman gently caressed the old photo, recalling her youth in the countryside.

caress.etymology: 源自拉丁语「carus」（亲爱的），引申为「亲昵的抚摸」

similar_words.description: caress作动词/名词指「爱抚」，care作动词/名词指「关心、照顾」

definitions.example: The cargo ship carrying medical supplies was stranded in the Suez Canal due to a navigation accident.

cargo.etymology: 源自西班牙语「cargar」（装载），引申为「装载的货物」

similar_words.description: cargo指「运输的货物」，carriage指「马车、货物运输费」

definitions.example: The skilled carpenter spent a month crafting a solid oak bookshelf for the university library.

carpenter.etymology: 源自拉丁语「carpentum」（两轮车），原指制造车辆的工匠，后泛指「木匠」

similar_words.description: carpenter指「木匠」，carpet指「地毯」

definitions.example: The hand-woven silk carpet in the reception hall was a gift from a foreign diplomatic delegation.

carpet.etymology: 源自古法语「carpite」（厚布料），引申为「铺地的厚织物」

similar_words.description: carpet指「地毯」，carpenter指「木匠」

definitions.example: In the Victorian era, nobles often traveled in luxurious horse-drawn carriages.
The export invoice clearly lists the cost of goods and the carriage to the destination port.

carriage.etymology: 源自拉丁语「carrus」（车），引申为「马车、运输相关费用」

similar_words.description: carriage指「马车、货物运输费」，cargo指「运输的货物」

definitions.example: Ticks are a common carrier of Lyme disease in forested areas.
The leading global carrier has launched a new cargo route between China and Europe.

carrier.etymology: 由动词「carry」（携带）加名词后缀「-er」构成，指「携带人/物的主体」

similar_words.description: carrier指「载体、运输公司」，career指「职业、事业」

definitions.example: Nutritionists suggest that eating carrots can help improve night vision due to their high vitamin A content.
The local government used tax incentives as a carrot to attract high-tech enterprises.

carrot.etymology: 源自希腊语「karoton」（胡萝卜），后因「胡萝卜加大棒」的比喻引申为「诱惑物、诱饵」

similar_words.description: carrot指「胡萝卜、诱饵」，parrot指「鹦鹉、鹦鹉学舌的人」

definitions.meaning: （两轮或四轮的）运货马车；购物车
definitions.example: In 19th-century rural England, farmers relied on horse-drawn carts to deliver their harvest to local markets.

cart.etymology: 源自日耳曼语族，原指'用于搬运的木质车辆'

similar_words.description: 「cart」指运货马车或购物车，「chart」指图表或航图

definitions.example: The editorial cartoon in The New York Times satirized the government's delayed response to the climate crisis.

cartoon.etymology: 由词根「cart（硬纸板，早期漫画的印刷载体）」加指小后缀「-oon」构成

similar_words.description: 「cartoon」指漫画或动画片，「carton」指硬纸盒或塑料盒

definitions.example: Ancient Egyptian artisans carved intricate hieroglyphs on the walls of royal tombs to record historical events.
She carved out a successful career in environmental law despite facing numerous obstacles.

carve.etymology: 源自古英语「ceorfan」，原义为'切割、砍削'

similar_words.description: 「carve」指雕刻或切割，「curve」指曲线或使弯曲

definitions.example: Many local small businesses still prefer cash payments to avoid high credit card transaction fees.
He went to the bank to cash the check his grandmother had sent him for graduation.

cash.etymology: 源自意大利语「cassa」，原指'存放现金的箱子'

similar_words.description: 「cash」指现金，「cache」指隐藏处或计算机缓存

definitions.example: Before the rise of digital music, people used cassettes to record and play songs on portable tape players.

cassette.etymology: 法语借词，由「casse（小盒子）」加指小后缀「-ette」构成

similar_words.description: 「cassette」指盒式磁带或暗盒，「casket」指小盒子或棺材

definitions.meaning: 投，掷；铸造；选派（演员）；使产生（怀疑等）
definitions.example: The latest archaeological findings cast doubt on the widely accepted theory about the origin of human civilization.
The film's all-star cast contributed significantly to its box office success.

cast.etymology: 源自古英语「castan」，原义为'投掷、抛'

similar_words.description: 「cast」指投、选派或演员表，「caste」指印度的种姓制度

definitions.example: The medieval castle perched on the hill has been preserved as a national heritage site for historical research.

castle.etymology: 源自拉丁语「castellum」，原指'小型军事堡垒'

similar_words.description: 「castle」指城堡或堡垒，「cattle」指牛（集合名词）

definitions.meaning: 随便的；非正式的；临时的
definitions.example: The retail chain hired 200 casual workers to cope with the surge in customer demand during the Christmas season.

casual.etymology: 由词根「cas（落下、发生）」加形容词后缀「-ual」构成，原指'偶然发生的'

similar_words.description: 「casual」指随便的或临时的，「causal」指因果关系的

definitions.meaning: （事故、战争的）伤亡人员；受害者
definitions.example: The severe train crash last month caused 30 casualties and left dozens of passengers injured.

casualty.etymology: 由形容词「casual（偶然的）」加名词后缀「-ty」构成，指'偶然事故的受害者'

similar_words.description: 「casual」是形容词，指随便的或临时的；「casualty」是名词，指伤亡人员或受害者

definitions.example: The university library's online catalog enables students to search for academic resources by author, title, or keyword.
The museum staff spent three months cataloging the newly donated collection of ancient pottery.

catalog.etymology: 由前缀「cata-（完全、全面）」加词根「log（说、记录）」构成，指'全面记录的目录'

similar_words.description: 「catalog」指目录或编目录，「category」指类别或范畴

definitions.example: The 2008 financial catastrophe led to widespread unemployment and severe economic recession across developed and developing countries alike.

catastrophe.etymology: 词根cata-表「彻底、向下」，strophe表「转向」，原指希腊戏剧中命运的急转直下，后引申为大灾难。

similar_words.description: 均指严重灾祸，catastrophe侧重造成大规模破坏、影响深远的灾难性事件，calamity侧重给群体带来巨大痛苦的不幸事件。

definitions.example: In this sociological study, participants were divided into four categories according to their annual income and educational background.

category.etymology: 源自希腊语kategoria，原指「陈述、分类」，-ory为名词后缀表事物类别，后引申为正式分类中的范畴。

similar_words.description: 均可表类别，category侧重正式分类体系中的门类或范畴，class侧重按等级、性质划分的类别。

definitions.meaning: 迎合，满足（需求）；提供餐饮服务
definitions.example: Many social media platforms cater to young users' preference for short, entertaining video content to increase user stickiness.

cater.etymology: 源自古法语acater（置办食物），原指为宴会准备餐饮，后引申为迎合他人需求的含义，常搭配短语「cater to」。

similar_words.description: 均可表提供服务，cater侧重专门迎合特定需求或提供活动餐饮，常搭配介词「to」，serve泛指各类服务行为。

definitions.example: The 13th-century Gothic cathedral in Paris is recognized as one of the most valuable cultural heritage sites in the world.

cathedral.etymology: 词根cathedra表「主教宝座」（kata-向下+hedra座位），-al为名词后缀，指设有主教宝座的大型教堂。

similar_words.description: 均为基督教宗教建筑，cathedral指主教驻地的大型主教堂，chapel指附属于机构的小型礼拜堂。

definitions.meaning: 天主教的；天主教徒（首字母大写）
definitions.example: In some South American countries, Catholics account for more than 70% of the total population.

definitions.meaning: 普遍的，广泛的（首字母小写）
definitions.example: A scholar with a catholic range of interests can often draw innovative insights from different academic fields.

catholic.etymology: 词根kata-表「遍及」，holos表「全部」，原义为「普世的、普遍的」，后因天主教自称普世教会，衍生出宗教相关含义。

similar_words.description: 均为基督教派相关词汇，Catholic指天主教的/教徒，Protestant指新教的/教徒；小写catholic为僻义，表广泛的。

definitions.example: To prevent the spread of infectious livestock diseases, local authorities have ordered a ban on cross-provincial transport of cattle.

cattle.etymology: 源自古法语catel（财产、牲畜），中世纪时牲畜是核心家庭财产，后词义缩小特指牛的总称。

similar_words.description: 拼写高度形近，cattle是集体名词指牛的总称，castle指城堡。

definitions.example: The new medical research aims to identify the root cause of the rare neurological disorder among elderly patients.
Rapid urbanization has caused significant changes in the lifestyle and values of rural residents in China.

cause.etymology: 源自拉丁语causa（原因、理由），词义沿用至今，兼有名词和动词用法，是考研核心高频词。

similar_words.description: 均可表原因，cause侧重导致某事发生的客观起因，常搭配「of」，reason侧重解释行为的主观理由，常搭配「for」。

definitions.example: The economic report urges policymakers to exercise caution when introducing new regulations affecting small and medium enterprises.
The travel agency cautioned tourists against carrying large amounts of cash when visiting crowded scenic spots.

caution.etymology: 词根caut-表「小心、谨慎」，-ion为名词后缀，后衍生出动词用法，常见搭配「with caution」。

similar_words.description: 同根表与「小心」相关的含义，caution指谨慎、警告，precaution指预先采取的预防措施。

definitions.example: Investors tend to be more cautious about making large-scale investments during periods of global economic uncertainty.

cautious.etymology: 词根caut-表「小心」，-ious为形容词后缀表「具有…性质的」，意为具备小心特质的，是考研态度题高频选项词。

similar_words.description: 均表谨慎的，cautious侧重行动前仔细评估风险、避免出错，wary侧重对潜在危险或圈套保持警觉。

definitions.example: The prehistoric rock paintings discovered in the deep limestone cave provide important clues about the life of early human beings.
The roof of the old abandoned house caved in during the heavy rainstorm last week.

cave.etymology: 源自拉丁语cavus（中空的），天然中空的空间即为洞穴，后衍生出塌陷、屈服的动词含义，常见短语「cave in」。

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，cave指天然形成的洞穴，cage指用于关住动物或人的笼子。

definitions.example: Many small and medium-sized enterprises were forced to cease operations during the economic recession.
The cease of the decade-long armed conflict finally brought peace to the war-torn region.

cease.etymology: 源自拉丁语cessare，意为‘停止’，为词根cess-的衍生词，同根词有access、recess等

similar_words.description: 「cease」意为停止、终止，「seize」意为抓住、夺取，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The high ceilings and large French windows make the old apartment look quite spacious and bright.

definitions.meaning: （价格、工资等的）最高限额，上限
definitions.example: The local authority has set a ceiling on public transport fares to ease the financial burden of low-income groups.

ceiling.etymology: 源自古法语celer（隐藏），原指遮盖屋顶的层，后因空间隐喻引申出经济领域的限额义

similar_words.description: 「ceiling」指天花板或价格上限，「sealing」指密封、封口，二者仅首字母拼写不同易混淆

definitions.example: People across the nation hold grand activities to celebrate the traditional Spring Festival every year.
The newly published book celebrates the extraordinary achievements of female scientists in the field of astrophysics.

celebrate.etymology: 源自拉丁语celebrare，意为‘频繁举行；庆祝’，词根celebr-表示‘荣誉、名望’

similar_words.description: 「celebrate」的宾语多为节日、事件等事物；「congratulate」的宾语多为人，后常接介词on表示就某事祝贺

definitions.example: Many social media celebrities have launched public welfare campaigns to promote garbage classification.
His groundbreaking research on genetic editing earned him international celebrity within the academic circle.

celebrity.etymology: 源自词根celebr-（荣誉、名望），加名词后缀-ity，原指名望，后引申为有名望的人即名流

similar_words.description: 「celebrity」指名流或名望，「celerity」指敏捷、迅速，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The research found that this new drug can effectively kill cancer cells without damaging healthy human tissues.
The political prisoner was locked in a dark, windowless cell for three years before being released.

cell.etymology: 源自拉丁语cella，意为‘小房间、储藏室’，后因隐喻引申出细胞、囚室等含义

similar_words.description: 「cell」为名词，指细胞、囚室等；「sell」为动词，指出售、售卖，二者发音相同拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The old couple stored a large amount of potatoes and cabbages in the cellar for the long cold winter.
This five-star hotel has a premium cellar with more than 2,000 bottles of top-grade wines from around the world.

cellar.etymology: 由cell（小房间）加名词后缀-ar构成，指位于地下的储藏用小房间

similar_words.description: 「cellar」指地窖或窖藏酒，「seller」指卖家、销售者，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The construction workers mixed cement, sand and water to pour the solid floor of the new residential building.
The two sides signed a series of cultural exchange agreements to cement their bilateral cooperative relationship.

cement.etymology: 源自拉丁语caementum，意为‘碎石、石料’，原指建筑用粘合剂，后引申出巩固抽象关系的动词义

similar_words.description: 「cement」作动词多搭配关系、协议等抽象名词；「consolidate」可搭配地位、权力、成果等更多类别的抽象名词

definitions.example: The local government has allocated special funds to renovate the martyrs' cemetery in the western suburban area.

cemetery.etymology: 源自希腊语koimeterion，原意为‘睡觉的场所’，后婉指墓地、公墓

similar_words.description: 「cemetery」多指城镇规划的公立公墓，「graveyard」多指教堂附属的小型私人墓地

definitions.example: The national census conducted every decade provides crucial data for the government to formulate targeted public policies.

census.etymology: 源自拉丁语censere，意为‘评估、登记’，原指古罗马时期的人口与财产登记制度

similar_words.description: 「census」指人口普查、统计调查，「consensus」指共识、一致意见，二者拼写高度相近是考研高频易混词

definitions.meaning: 分（美元、欧元等的辅币单位）
definitions.example: The price of this daily necessity has risen by 15 cents due to the ongoing global supply chain crisis.

definitions.meaning: 百（多用于固定搭配或复合词）
definitions.example: The latest survey shows that more than 70 per cent of college students are willing to take part in grassroots voluntary services.

cent.etymology: 源自拉丁语centum，意为‘百’，是century、centimeter等词汇的共同词根

similar_words.description: 「cent」指辅币单位分或百，「scent」指气味、香味，二者发音相同拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: Climate data shows that the global average surface temperature has risen by about 1 degree centigrade since the pre-industrial era.

centigrade.etymology: 词根centi-（百分之一）+ grade（等级、度数），原指分为百度的温度计量体系

similar_words.description: 二者均可表示摄氏的，「centigrade」侧重百分度制的计量表述，「celsius」是国际标准计量学术语

definitions.example: The archaeologists found a delicate jade ornament only 2 centimetres long in the tomb of a noble from the Han Dynasty.
