centimetre.etymology: 词根centi-（百分之一）+ metre（米），即一米的百分之一，为长度单位

similar_words.description: 二者均为长度单位，「centimetre」是厘米，「millimetre」是毫米，拼写接近易混淆

definitions.example: The central area of the ancient city has been listed as a World Cultural Heritage Site by UNESCO.
Protecting biodiversity is central to the sustainable development strategy proposed by the United Nations.

central.etymology: 来自centre（中心）+ 形容词后缀-al，表与中心相关的

similar_words.description: 二者均可表示核心的，「central」侧重居于中心位置或起主导作用，「core」侧重要事物最本质的核心部分

definitions.example: The community centre provides free legal consultation and career training services for local residents.
The issue of youth employment has become the centre of public debate in many European countries.

centre.etymology: 来自拉丁语centrum，原指几何中心点，后引申为各类活动的核心区域

similar_words.description: 二者均可表示中间，「centre」多指引明确的中心区域或关注焦点，「middle」泛指两端或两点之间的中间部分，范围更宽泛

definitions.example: The past century has witnessed dramatic changes in global population structure and economic growth patterns.

century.etymology: 词根cent-（百）+ 名词后缀-ury，表一百年的时间周期

similar_words.description: 二者均为时间单位，「century」指一百年的周期，「decade」指十年的周期

definitions.example: Rising global temperatures are posing a serious threat to the yield of staple cereal crops such as wheat and rice.

cereal.etymology: 源自罗马神话中的谷物女神Ceres（刻瑞斯），后引申为谷物、谷类作物的含义

similar_words.description: 二者均可表示谷物，「cereal」多指大规模种植的谷类作物，「grain」可泛指谷物颗粒或其他细小颗粒

definitions.example: The award ceremony for outstanding scientific researchers will be broadcast live on national television this Friday.

ceremony.etymology: 来自拉丁语caerimonia，原指宗教祭祀仪式，后泛指各类正式的典礼活动

similar_words.description: 二者均可表示仪式，「ceremony」多指正式公开的大型典礼，「ritual」多指宗教或传统的固定礼节仪式

definitions.example: Researchers are certain that regular exercise can reduce the risk of various chronic diseases by a large margin.
Certain types of plastics can take hundreds of years to decompose in the natural environment.

certain.etymology: 来自拉丁语certus（确定的），后引申出特定的、某一等含义

similar_words.description: 二者均可表示确定的，「certain」语气更正式客观，多用于书面语，「sure」更口语化，侧重主观确信

definitions.example: In the face of rapid technological innovation, there is no absolute certainty about the future development of the job market.

certainty.etymology: 由形容词certain（确定的）+ 名词后缀-ty构成，表确定的状态或必然的事物

similar_words.description: 二者均可表示确信，「certainty」侧重客观上的确定性或必然事件，「certitude」侧重主观上的坚信态度

definitions.example: Candidates for the postgraduate admission must submit their undergraduate diploma and academic transcript certificate before the deadline.

certificate.etymology: 词根cert-（确定、证明）+ 连接成分-i- + 词根-fic（做）+ 名词后缀-ate，即用来证明资质的正式文件

similar_words.description: 二者均可表示证明文件，「certificate」侧重证明身份、学历、资质的文件，「license」侧重官方颁发的从事特定活动的许可证

definitions.example: The academic committee will certify that the candidate has met all the graduation requirements.

certify.etymology: cert（拉丁语词根，表「确定、确信」）+ -ify（动词后缀，表「使…」），原义为「使确定」，引申为「证明、颁发证书」

similar_words.description: certify为动词，表「证明、颁发证书」的动作；certificate为同根名词，表「证书、证明文件」

definitions.example: The research paper identified a complex chain of interactions between gut microbiota and immune function.

chain.etymology: 源自古法语「chaeine」，原指「金属链条」，引申为「一系列连贯的事物」

similar_words.description: chain强调事物间紧密相连成链状的系列；sequence强调事物按固定逻辑顺序排列的序列

definitions.meaning: 主席，董事长；（会议的）主持人
definitions.example: The chairman of the admissions committee announced the new evaluation criteria for postgraduate applicants.

chairman.etymology: chair（动词义「主持、执掌」）+ man（名词后缀，表「人」），原指「执掌会议的人」，引申为「主席、董事长」

similar_words.description: chairman传统指男性主席/主持人；chairperson为无性别特指的中性称谓

definitions.example: The professor marked the key theoretical points on the blackboard with a piece of white chalk.

chalk.etymology: 源自古英语「cealc」，原指「白垩土」，后引申为「用白垩制成的粉笔」

similar_words.description: chalk为名词，表「粉笔、白垩」；choke为动词，表「窒息、阻塞」

definitions.example: A growing number of economists challenge the prevailing view that low inflation is always beneficial for economic growth.

challenge.etymology: 源自古法语「chalengier」，原指「指责、反驳」，引申为「提出挑战、质疑」

similar_words.description: challenge侧重对既有观点、权威的理性质疑或提出挑战；defy侧重公然违抗规则、权威

definitions.example: The upper legislative chamber voted to amend the bill concerning higher education funding.

chamber.etymology: 源自拉丁语「camera」（表「房间」），经古法语演化后，引申为「封闭的房间、会议厅」

similar_words.description: chamber为名词，表「房间、会议厅」；camera为名词，表「照相机、摄像机」

definitions.example: The research team toasted their groundbreaking discovery with a bottle of champagne after the press conference.

champagne.etymology: 源自法国东北部的香槟地区「Champagne」的地名，因该地区出产的起泡酒得名，表「香槟酒」

similar_words.description: champagne为名词，表「香槟酒」；campaign为名词，表「（政治、商业）运动、战役」

definitions.example: The renowned scholar has long championed the integration of interdisciplinary research in social sciences.

champion.etymology: 源自拉丁语「campus」（表「战场」），原指「战场胜者」，后引申为「冠军、支持者」，作动词表「拥护、支持」

similar_words.description: champion为名词/动词，表「冠军、拥护」；champ为动词，表「（出声地）大嚼」

definitions.example: Statistical analysis shows that there is a 70% chance that the new drug will pass the clinical trial.

chance.etymology: 源自拉丁语「cadere」（表「落下、发生」），原指「偶然发生的事件」，引申为「机会、可能性」

similar_words.description: chance侧重偶然出现的机会或发生的可能性；opportunity侧重有利的、可主动把握的机遇

definitions.example: The chancellor of the state university outlined a five-year plan to enhance international academic exchanges.

chancellor.etymology: 源自拉丁语「cancellarius」（表「守门人、秘书」），后引申为「（大学）校长、（国家）总理」等高级职位称谓

similar_words.description: chancellor为名词，表「（大学）校长、（德国等）总理」；councilor为名词，表「（地方）议会议员」

definitions.example: Social media has become a crucial channel for academic information dissemination among postgraduate entrance exam takers.
The national education fund channels substantial grants to support postgraduate research projects.

channel.etymology: 源自拉丁语「canalis」（管道），原指输水管道，后引申为信息渠道、电视频道，动词义引申为‘引导、输送资源’

similar_words.description: 二者同根，「canal」指人工开凿的运河，「channel」侧重信息、资源的渠道或电视频道

definitions.example: The sudden outbreak of the pandemic plunged the international supply chain into chaos.

chaos.etymology: 源自希腊语「khaos」（创世前的混沌状态），后泛指任何无序的混乱状态

similar_words.description: 「mess」侧重日常的脏乱或个人麻烦，「chaos」侧重大规模、系统性的无序混乱

definitions.example: The chap working at the campus library recommended a comprehensive vocabulary book for postgraduate English preparation.
Prolonged exposure to cold winds may lead to chaps on the hands of outdoor postgraduate volunteers.

chap.etymology: 源自古英语「ceap」（交易、商人），原指年轻商人，后引申为‘小伙子’；‘皲裂’义为后期引申

similar_words.description: 二者均指‘家伙’，「guy」为通用口语，「chap」更具英式口语色彩

definitions.example: The third chapter of the academic monograph elaborates on corpus linguistics methodology, marking a new chapter in linguistic research.

chapter.etymology: 源自拉丁语「capitulum」（小的头部），原指书籍的章节标题，后引申为‘章、回’，再引申为‘历史篇章’

similar_words.description: 「section」指书籍、文件的小节，层级低于「chapter」（正式的章、回）

definitions.example: The research focuses on how social environment shapes the moral character of college students.
The protagonist in the novel is a complex character with both virtues and flaws.

character.etymology: 源自希腊语「kharakter」（刻痕、印记），原指雕刻的印记，后引申为‘性格、品质’（内在印记）和‘人物、角色’（文学印记）

similar_words.description: 「personality」侧重外在表现的个性，「character」侧重内在的道德品质或人物特质

definitions.example: The high emphasis on logical reasoning is characteristic of postgraduate entrance exam English reading questions.
One key characteristic of effective academic writing is clarity of argumentation.

characteristic.etymology: 源自「character」（特征）+ 形容词后缀「-istic」，表‘具有…性质的’，名词化后表‘特征’

similar_words.description: 「feature」侧重外在显眼的特征，「characteristic」侧重内在本质的典型特征

definitions.example: Postgraduate academic research is often characterized by rigorous data analysis and critical thinking.

characterize.etymology: 源自「characteristic」（特征）+ 动词后缀「-ize」，表‘使具有…特征’，即‘以…为特征’

similar_words.description: 「describe」侧重具体细节的描述，「characterize」侧重概括事物的本质特征

definitions.example: The university charges a moderate tuition fee for its postgraduate programs.
The researcher was charged with academic misconduct for fabricating experimental data.

charge.etymology: 源自拉丁语「carrus」（车），原指‘装货、加载’，后引申为‘收费（加载费用）’、‘指控（加载罪名）’等义

similar_words.description: 二者均表‘指控’，「accuse」搭配介词of，「charge」搭配介词with；「charge」还有‘收费’义，「accuse」无此义

definitions.example: The postgraduate student union launched a book donation campaign to assist local charity schools in rural areas.
Academic reviewers should treat early-career postgraduate researchers with charity and constructive criticism.

charity.etymology: 源自拉丁语「caritas」（爱、慈爱），原指宗教意义上的仁爱，后引申为‘慈善’、‘宽容’

similar_words.description: 「philanthropy」指大规模的公益慈善（常由机构或富人开展），「charity」侧重小额、日常的慈善或施舍

definitions.example: The charm of academic research lies in its endless exploration of unknown fields.
Her profound knowledge and elegant demeanor charmed the audience at the postgraduate academic forum.

charm.etymology: 源自拉丁语「carmen」（歌、咒语），原指有魔力的咒语，后引申为‘魅力、吸引力’，动词义为‘迷住’

similar_words.description: 「appeal」侧重普遍的吸引力，「charm」侧重个人或事物的独特迷人特质

definitions.example: Researchers compiled a detailed chart to illustrate the correlation between urbanization and carbon emissions.
The team charted the migratory routes of Arctic terns using satellite tracking data.

chart.etymology: 源自拉丁语charta（纸、书面文件），原指用于记录的纸页，后引申为图表、海图等可视化记录工具，作动词时表示绘制此类记录的动作

similar_words.description: 近义易混，「chart」侧重带数据统计的各类图表（含海图），「graph」侧重以坐标、线条呈现的曲线图

definitions.example: The Magna Carta, signed in 1215, is a historic charter that limited the absolute power of British monarchs.
The archaeological team chartered a small aircraft to reach the remote excavation site in the Andes.

charter.etymology: 由词根「chart（纸、正式文件）」加后缀-er构成，原指载有规则的正式书面文件，后引申为宪章；作动词时源于包租契约的书面属性

similar_words.description: 形近易混，「chart」指数据图表或记录动作，「charter」指宪章、章程或包租服务
近义易混，「charter」侧重组织、国际机构的章程，「constitution」侧重国家的根本大法

definitions.example: Many postgraduate applicants chase top-tier universities to enhance their academic competitiveness.
The police chase across the city ended with the arrest of the suspect involved in academic fraud.

chase.etymology: 源自古法语chacier，原指狩猎时的追逐动作，后引申为对人、目标的追求

similar_words.description: 近义易混，「chase」侧重快速、急切的追逐（人或具体目标），「pursue」侧重长期、有规划的追求（事业、理想）

definitions.example: Postgraduate students often chat with their supervisors via video calls to discuss research progress.

chat.etymology: 源自中古英语chatten，为拟声词，模仿人们轻松闲谈时的细碎声响

similar_words.description: 近义易混，「chat」侧重非正式、轻松的日常闲谈，「talk」可指正式或非正式的各类交谈

definitions.meaning: 欺骗；作弊（尤指考试、学术）
definitions.example: Candidates who cheat in the national postgraduate entrance examination will be disqualified and banned from taking the exam for years.
The journal retracted the paper after confirming the author was a cheat who falsified experimental data.

cheat.etymology: 源自古英语ceatan，原指以欺诈手段获取利益，后特指考试、学术等场合的作弊行为

similar_words.description: 近义易混，「cheat」侧重为私利在规则下作弊（如考试、游戏），「deceive」侧重用谎言、假象蒙蔽他人

definitions.example: The young scholar felt a flush rise to her cheeks when her thesis was praised by the conference chair.
He had the cheek to ask for a letter of recommendation after plagiarizing his supervisor's research findings.

cheek.etymology: 源自古英语ceace，原指颌部、面部侧面，后特指脸颊；引申义「厚脸皮」源于面部表情所传达的无礼态度

similar_words.description: 形近易混，「cheek」指面部两侧的面颊，「chin」指面部下方的下巴

definitions.example: The audience cheered loudly when the Nobel laureate finished his keynote speech on environmental protection.
The cheer of the graduates echoed through the auditorium during the commencement ceremony.

cheer.etymology: 源自中古英语chere，原指面容、心情，后引申为通过呼喊、动作表达的喜悦或鼓励

similar_words.description: 近义易混，「cheer」侧重用呼喊、手势等欢呼加油，「applaud」侧重以鼓掌方式表达喝彩

definitions.example: Nutritionists point out that low-fat cheese is a good source of protein and calcium for postgraduate students with busy schedules.

cheese.etymology: 源自拉丁语caseus，原指经发酵、凝固的乳制品，后传入英语演变为cheese

similar_words.description: 近义易混，「cheese」指发酵的固态干酪，「cream」指未发酵的液态或半固态乳脂

definitions.meaning: 主厨；厨师（尤指专业高级餐厅的）
definitions.example: The Michelin-starred chef collaborated with food scientists to develop nutritious meals for university cafeterias.

chef.etymology: 源自法语chef de cuisine（厨房主管），缩写为chef，后传入英语特指专业高级厨师

similar_words.description: 近义易混，「chef」侧重受过专业训练、在高级场所工作的主厨，「cook」泛指会做饭的人或普通厨师

definitions.example: The research team analyzed the chemical composition of atmospheric particulate matter in urban areas.
The environmental protection agency banned the use of toxic chemicals in agricultural production nationwide.

chemical.etymology: 由名词chemistry（化学）加形容词后缀-al构成，作名词时特指化学合成的制品或天然化学物质

similar_words.description: 近义易混，「chemical」指与化学学科、物质相关的，「physical」指与物理学科、物质形态相关的

definitions.example: A team of chemists from the top university has developed a new biodegradable material to replace plastic.

chemist.etymology: 词根「chem-」（化学）+ 后缀「-ist」（表示从事某专业的人员）

similar_words.description: 均为表职业的「-ist」结尾名词，chemist指化学家，physicist指物理学家

definitions.meaning: 化学（学科）；（物质间的）化学反应/相互作用
definitions.example: The research paper analyzes the chemistry of ocean acidification and its impact on marine ecosystems.

chemistry.etymology: 词根「chem-」（化学）+ 后缀「-istry」（表示学科领域）

similar_words.description: 同根词，chemistry指化学学科，chemist指从事化学研究的人

definitions.example: The non-profit organization received a cheque of £100,000 from a private donor to support rural education.

cheque.etymology: 源自古法语「eschequier」（棋盘），因支票存根的格子类似棋盘格，后引申为金融凭证“支票”

similar_words.description: cheque为英式拼写，专指「支票」；check为美式拼写，多表「检查；核对」，也可指支票

definitions.example: Many senior citizens cherish the memories of their childhood spent in small traditional villages.

cherish.etymology: 词根「cher-」（=care，关心）+ 后缀「-ish」（动词后缀，表动作）

similar_words.description: 形近词，cherish为动词表“珍视”，cherry为名词表“樱桃”

definitions.example: Nutritionists point out that cherry extracts contain high levels of antioxidants beneficial to heart health.

cherry.etymology: 源自古法语「cherise」，原指樱桃树的果实，后泛指樱桃相关事物

similar_words.description: 形近词，cherry为名词表“樱桃”，cherish为动词表“珍视”

definitions.example: Educational studies have shown that playing chess regularly can enhance children's logical reasoning and strategic thinking skills.

chess.etymology: 源自古法语「esches」，原指棋盘上的棋子，后泛指国际象棋游戏

similar_words.description: 形近词，chess指“国际象棋”，chest指“胸膛；箱子”

definitions.example: The clinical report indicates that patients with chronic bronchitis often experience persistent discomfort in the chest.

chest.etymology: 源自古英语「cist」（箱子、容器），后因胸膛形似封闭容器引申为“胸部”

similar_words.description: 形近词，chest指“胸膛；箱子”，chess指“国际象棋”

definitions.example: Dental experts recommend that adults chew each mouthful of food at least 20 times to aid digestion.

definitions.meaning: 仔细考虑，反复思量（熟词僻义）
definitions.example: The project manager chewed over the investment proposal for a week before submitting it to the board.

chew.etymology: 源自古英语「ceowan」（咬碎、咀嚼），引申为“反复思考、掂量”

similar_words.description: 形近词，chew为动词表“咀嚼”，chow为名词表「食物（非正式）」

definitions.example: The agricultural study examines the impact of free-range farming on the nutritional value of chicken meat.

chicken.etymology: 源自古英语「cycen」，原指雏鸡，后泛指家鸡或鸡肉

similar_words.description: 形近词，chicken泛指“鸡；鸡肉”，chick特指“小鸡；雏鸟”

definitions.meaning: 主要的，首要的；首领，长官
definitions.example: The chief cause of the rapid decline in polar bear populations is the shrinking of Arctic sea ice due to global warming.

chief.etymology: 源自古法语「chief」（头、首领），引申为“处于核心地位的、主要的”

similar_words.description: 形近词，chief表“主要的；首领”，chef表「厨师」

definitions.example: Many social scientists argue that childhood experiences shape one's personality in adulthood.

childhood.etymology: 由「child（儿童）」加名词后缀「-hood（表时期或状态）」构成

similar_words.description: 均以「-hood」结尾，「childhood」指童年时期，「neighborhood」指邻里、街区

definitions.example: A sudden chill swept through the classroom when the window was left open on a winter morning.
The unexpected failure of the experiment chilled the enthusiasm of the research team.

chill.etymology: 源自古英语「cyle」，原指寒冷，后引申出情绪层面的含义

similar_words.description: 形近且语义相关，「chill」可作名词、动词，「chilly」仅作形容词，表寒冷的、怕冷的

definitions.example: Black smoke billowed from the chimney of the old factory, raising concerns about air pollution.

chimney.etymology: 源自拉丁语「caminus」，原指炉灶，后引申为排烟的烟囱

similar_words.description: 前四个字母形近，「chimney」指烟囱，「chimera」指幻想、妄想（考研低频）

definitions.example: She rested her chin on her hand, staring thoughtfully at the passage in the textbook.

chin.etymology: 源自古英语「cin」，与古高地德语「kin」同源，指面部下巴部位

similar_words.description: 均为短词且以「ch」开头，「chin」指下巴，「chop」指切碎、劈

definitions.example: The new generation of smartphone chips consumes less power while offering higher performance.
She chipped her favorite ceramic mug when washing it.

chip.etymology: 源自古英语「cipp」，原指小木块，后引申为碎片、电子芯片

similar_words.description: 形近且均为短词，「chip」指芯片、碎片，「clip」指夹子、修剪

definitions.example: Researchers found that dark chocolate contains antioxidants that may benefit heart health.

chocolate.etymology: 源自纳瓦特尔语「chocolatl」，经西班牙语传入英语，原指巧克力饮品，后指巧克力制品

similar_words.description: 语义相关，「chocolate」指巧克力制品，「cocoa」指可可粉、可可饮料

definitions.example: In the graduate admission process, applicants often face a tough choice between prestigious universities and favorable majors.
The store only sells choice fruits imported from tropical regions.

choice.etymology: 动词「choose」的名词形式，源自古法语「chois」，指选择行为

similar_words.description: 同根词，「choice」为名词/形容词，表选择或优质的；「choose」为动词，表选择

definitions.example: The thick smoke from the burning building made many residents choke and cough.
The strict regulations choked the development of small local businesses.

choke.etymology: 源自古英语「ceocian」，原指窒息，后引申为抑制发展的含义

similar_words.description: 形近且均为短词，「choke」指窒息、抑制，「coke」指焦炭、可口可乐

definitions.example: When selecting research topics, graduate students should choose areas that align with their academic interests and career goals.
She chose to remain silent instead of arguing with her supervisor about the experimental design.

choose.etymology: 源自古英语「ceosan」，与古高地德语「kiosan」同源，指挑选行为

similar_words.description: 同根词，「choose」为动词，表选择；「choice」为名词/形容词，表选择或优质的

definitions.example: The chef carefully chopped the vegetables into small cubes for the gourmet dish.
The restaurant's signature dish is grilled lamb chops with rosemary sauce.

chop.etymology: 源自古英语「coppian」，原指砍、劈，后引申为切碎的动作或肉块

similar_words.description: 均为短词且以「ch」开头，「chop」指切碎、劈或排骨；「chip」指芯片、碎片

definitions.meaning: 合唱队；（众人的）齐声（表达）
definitions.example: The environmental proposal was met with a chorus of approval from the conference attendees.
The crowd chorused their demand for better public transportation.

chorus.etymology: 源自希腊语「khoros」，原指古希腊戏剧中的舞蹈合唱队，词根「chor-」表“合唱、集体歌舞”

similar_words.description: 「choir」特指教堂的唱诗班，「chorus」可指普通合唱队或众人的齐声表态

definitions.example: Many devout followers believe that Christ sacrificed himself to redeem human sins.

Christ.etymology: 源自希腊语「Christos」，对应希伯来语「Messiah」（弥赛亚），意为“受膏者”

similar_words.description: 「Christ」是专有名词指基督，「Christian」是名词/形容词指基督徒或基督教的

definitions.example: Christians around the world hold various ceremonies to celebrate religious festivals.
The organization has been providing Christian charitable services to the poor for decades.

Christian.etymology: 由专有名词「Christ」加形容词后缀「-ian」构成，表“与…相关的人或事物”

similar_words.description: 「Christian」泛指所有基督徒，「Catholic」特指基督教分支中的天主教徒

definitions.meaning: 圣诞节（基督教纪念耶稣诞生的节日）
definitions.example: Most Western families prepare a feast and exchange gifts with relatives on Christmas Day.

Christmas.etymology: 由「Christ」（基督）加古英语「mas」（弥撒，宗教仪式）构成，原指“基督的弥撒日”

similar_words.description: 「Christmas」是纪念基督诞生的圣诞节，「Easter」是纪念基督复活的复活节

definitions.meaning: 染色体（生物遗传物质的载体）
definitions.example: Genetic disorders may arise when chromosomes fail to separate accurately during cell division.

chromosome.etymology: 由希腊语「chroma」（颜色）加「soma」（身体）构成，因染色体易被碱性染色剂着色得名

similar_words.description: 「chromosome」是承载基因的染色体，「gene」是控制生物性状的基本遗传单位

definitions.meaning: （疾病）慢性的；（问题）长期的、持续的
definitions.example: Chronic unemployment has become a pressing social issue in many industrialized nations.

chronic.etymology: 源自希腊语「chronos」（时间）加形容词后缀「-ic」构成，原指“持续长时间的”

similar_words.description: 「chronic」指慢性的、长期的，「acute」指急性的、剧烈的（常形容疾病或突发问题）

definitions.example: The 12th-century stone church on the hill has been listed as a world cultural heritage site.

church.etymology: 源自古英语「cirice」，源自希腊语「kuriakon」，意为“主的房子”

similar_words.description: 「church」指大型教堂或教会组织，「chapel」指附属于机构的小型教堂

definitions.example: The guest lit a hand-rolled Cuban cigar during the informal diplomatic reception.

cigar.etymology: 源自西班牙语「cigarro」，原指卷制烟草的小筒

similar_words.description: 「cigar」是较粗的雪茄，「cigarette」是较细的香烟

definitions.example: The university campus has banned the sale of cigarets to safeguard students' physical health.

cigaret.etymology: 是「cigarette」的美式简化拼法，去掉了末尾的「te」，源自法语「cigarette」（小雪茄）

similar_words.description: 「cigaret」是细香烟，「cigar」是粗雪茄

definitions.example: She only shares her academic ideas with a small circle of trusted research partners.
The rescue helicopter circled the mountainous area for hours searching for the missing hikers.

circle.etymology: 源自拉丁语「circulus」，是「circus」（圆、环）的指小词，词根「circ-」表“圆、环”

similar_words.description: 「circle」指具体的圆或抽象圈子，「cycle」指循环周期或自行车

definitions.example: The microchip’s integrated circuit processes data at an unprecedented speed.
The federal circuit court handles appeals from lower federal courts.

circuit.etymology: 词根circ-（圆、环）+ 名词后缀-uit，原指环形的路线或物体，后衍生出电路、巡回等含义

similar_words.description: 同根词，「circle」指圆形实体或绕圈动作，「circuit」侧重环形的线路、路线或巡回活动

definitions.example: The satellite follows a circular orbit around the Earth.
The university sent a circular to all faculty about the new grading policy.

circular.etymology: 词根circ-（圆）+ 形容词后缀-ular（表具有…性质的）；作名词时由形容词转义，指可环形传阅的官方文件

similar_words.description: 「circular」侧重几何意义上的圆形、环形，多形容事物形状；「round」更通用，可形容人或物的圆润状态

definitions.example: Warm air circulates through the building’s energy-efficient ventilation system.
Rumors about the company’s bankruptcy began to circulate on social media.

circulate.etymology: 词根circ-（圆）+ 连接成分-ul- + 动词后缀-ate，表绕圈移动，引申为循环、传播

similar_words.description: 「circulate」侧重有规律的循环、流转（如空气、信息、货币）；「spread」侧重无固定方向的扩散

definitions.example: The research team measured the circumference of the ancient stone ring to estimate its construction date.

circumference.etymology: 前缀circum-（环绕、周围）+ 词根fer-（带来、携带）+ 名词后缀-ence，表绕一圈的长度

similar_words.description: 「circumference」特指圆形的周长；「perimeter」指任意封闭图形的周长，或场地的周边

definitions.example: The traveling circus set up its big top in the town’s central park for a week-long performance.
The city hall became a circus during the public hearing on the new housing policy.

circus.etymology: 源自拉丁语circus（圆、环形场地），原指古罗马的环形竞技场，后转指马戏团表演的环形场地，再引申为马戏团本身

similar_words.description: 「circus」侧重有动物、杂技表演的马戏团活动；「carnival」侧重狂欢节、嘉年华的节庆活动

definitions.example: The research paper cited 15 peer-reviewed studies to support its conclusion.
The traffic officer cited the driver for running a red light.

cite.etymology: 源自拉丁语citare（召唤、引用），词根cit-（召唤、移动）+ 动词后缀-e，引申为引用、传唤

similar_words.description: 「cite」侧重引用观点、资料的来源，不要求逐字复制；「quote」侧重逐字引用原文内容

definitions.meaning: 公民，市民（具有某国国籍的人）
definitions.example: Every eligible citizen is entitled to vote in the presidential election.

citizen.etymology: 词根civ-（城市、公民）+ 名词后缀-izen（表具有…身份的人），原指城市居民，后指具有某国国籍的公民

similar_words.description: 「citizen」侧重具有某国国籍的公民；「resident」侧重在某地居住的居民，不一定拥有当地国籍

definitions.example: The civil rights movement of the 1960s transformed American society.
The moderator urged all participants to maintain a civil tone during the debate.

civil.etymology: 词根civ-（城市、公民）+ 形容词后缀-il，表与公民相关的，引申为文明的、有礼貌的

similar_words.description: 「civil」作形容词，侧重公民的、民用的；「civilian」作名词指平民，作形容词指平民的

definitions.meaning: 平民，百姓（非军人、非公职人员）
definitions.example: The international organization provided humanitarian aid to war-affected civilians.
The military base has a separate area for civilian employees and their families.

civilian.etymology: 词根civ-（城市、公民）+ 后缀-ian（表与…相关的人/的），指非军事身份的公民

similar_words.description: 「civilian」侧重非军事、非公职的平民；「citizen」侧重具有某国国籍的公民

definitions.meaning: 礼貌，客气（尤指公共场合的得体举止）
definitions.example: The scholar argued that civility is essential for constructive public dialogue.

civility.etymology: 词根civ-（公民、文明）+ 名词后缀-ility（表性质、状态），指公民应有的文明礼貌举止

similar_words.description: 「civility」侧重正式场合或公共语境下的得体、有礼貌；「politeness」更通用，指日常的礼貌行为

definitions.example: Archaeologists have unearthed relics that shed light on the development of ancient Mesopotamian civilization.

civilization.etymology: 由形容词「civil（文明的、公民的）」加名词后缀「-ization（表行为或结果）」构成，表「文明化的结果→文明」

similar_words.description: 同一单词的英式拼写形式，考研中两种拼法均有效

definitions.example: The missionaries aimed to civilize the indigenous tribes, though their methods often sparked controversy.

civilize.etymology: 由形容词「civil（文明的）」加动词后缀「-ize（使…化）」构成，表「使变得文明→教化」

similar_words.description: 同一单词的英式拼写形式，考研中两种拼法均有效

definitions.example: The scientist claimed that the new discovery would revolutionize the field of environmental science.
The insurance company rejected his claim for compensation due to insufficient evidence.

claim.etymology: 源自拉丁语「clamare（呼喊）」，可联想「喊出观点→声称；喊出诉求→索要」

similar_words.description: 同根词，claim表声称、索要，acclaim表公开赞扬或欢呼

definitions.example: The audience clapped loudly to express their admiration for the pianist’s performance.

clap.etymology: 拟声词，模拟拍手时的清脆声响

similar_words.description: 形近词，clap表拍手、鼓掌，clasp表紧握、抱紧

definitions.example: The spokesperson held a press conference to clarify the government’s new policy on carbon emissions.

clarify.etymology: 由词根「clar-（=clear，清楚的）」加动词后缀「-ify（使…）」构成，表「使变得清楚→澄清」

similar_words.description: 同根词，clarify为动词表澄清，clarity为名词表清晰、清楚

definitions.example: The professor’s lecture was praised for its clarity and logical structure.

clarity.etymology: 由词根「clar-（=clear，清楚的）」加名词后缀「-ity（表性质）」构成，表「清楚的性质→清晰」

similar_words.description: 形近词，clarity表清晰，charity表慈善或慈善机构

definitions.example: The two political parties clashed over the proposed reform of the healthcare system.
The clash between the protesters and the police resulted in several injuries.

clash.etymology: 拟声词，模拟物体碰撞或冲突时的声响

similar_words.description: 形近词，clash表冲突、抵触，crash表坠毁、崩溃

definitions.example: The child clasped his mother’s hand tightly as they crossed the busy street.

clasp.etymology: 源自古英语「claspan」，本义为「紧握、扣紧」

similar_words.description: 形近词，clasp表紧握、抱紧，clap表拍手、鼓掌

definitions.example: This is a classic example of how climate change affects agricultural production.
「Pride and Prejudice」 is widely regarded as a classic of English literature.

classic.etymology: 源自拉丁语「classicus（最高等级的）」，表「一流的→经典的」

similar_words.description: 易混词，classic表经典的（可指优秀作品或典型情况），classical表古典的（常指古希腊罗马文化或古典艺术）

definitions.meaning: 古典的（常指古希腊罗马文化或古典艺术）
definitions.example: The museum houses a large collection of classical sculptures from ancient Greece and Rome.

classical.etymology: 由形容词「classic（经典的）」加后缀「-al（…的）」构成，侧重「古典流派的」

similar_words.description: 易混词，classical表古典的（侧重文化或艺术流派），classic表经典的（侧重优秀或典型性）

definitions.example: The classification of these ancient artifacts is based on their production techniques and historical periods.

classification.etymology: 由「class（类别，等级）」+连接字母i+「-fication（名词后缀，表行为或结果）」构成

similar_words.description: 二者均表分类，classification侧重按既定标准进行系统划分，常用于学术、官方语境；categorization侧重对事物进行归类的动作，适用范围更广

definitions.example: Researchers classify the participants into three groups according to their daily sleep duration and exercise habits.

classify.etymology: 由「class（类别）」+连接字母i+「-fy（动词后缀，意为使…）」构成

similar_words.description: 二者均为表分类的动词，classify更强调按照官方或科学的标准划分；categorize侧重根据事物的特征进行归类

definitions.example: Several of my undergraduate classmates have published papers in top international academic journals.

similar_words.description: classmate特指同一班级的同学，schoolmate泛指同一学校的校友或同学

definitions.meaning: （法律、合同等正式文件的）条款
definitions.example: The employment contract contains a clause that prohibits employees from disclosing confidential company information.
In English writing, an adverbial clause can be used to indicate time, reason, condition or concession.

clause.etymology: 源自拉丁语clausa（闭合、结束），原指契约的收尾条款，后引申出语法领域的分句义

similar_words.description: clause指包含主谓结构的分句或从句，phrase指没有主谓结构的短语，二者为语法领域易混概念

definitions.example: The fossil specimen clearly preserves the sharp claw of a Cretaceous predatory dinosaur.

claw.etymology: 源自古英语clāwa，指动物的尖爪

similar_words.description: claw侧重带尖甲的爪或甲壳类动物的螯，paw侧重哺乳动物带肉垫的爪

definitions.example: Many Neolithic settlements in the Yellow River Basin used local clay to make pottery and building materials.

clay.etymology: 源自古英语clǣg，指具有黏性的泥土

similar_words.description: clay指具有可塑性、可用于制陶制砖的黏性黏土，mud指湿软的泥浆或稀泥

definitions.example: The local clergy collaborated with charity organizations to provide food and shelter for homeless people.

clergy.etymology: 源自古法语clergié，原指受过教育、能读写的人，后特指基督教神职人员

similar_words.description: 二者拼写相近易混淆，clergy指神职人员总称，clerk指职员、店员或文书

definitions.example: The front desk clerk informed me that the seminar on postgraduate admission would be held in the lecture hall.

clerk.etymology: 与clergy同根，原指能读写的神职人员，后引申为从事文书、服务工作的职员

similar_words.description: 二者拼写相近易混淆，clerk指职员、店员等从事文书或服务工作的人，clergy指基督教神职人员总称

definitions.meaning: 聪明的，机灵的；巧妙的，精巧的
definitions.example: The research team proposed a clever method to reduce the error rate of data analysis by 30 percent.

clever.etymology: 源自古英语clifer，原意为熟练的、灵巧的

similar_words.description: clever侧重头脑灵活、学习能力强或构思巧妙，smart侧重反应敏捷、处事精明

definitions.example: You can click the 'download' button on the university's official website to get the past examination papers for postgraduate admission.
When the professor explained the core logic of the economic model, I finally clicked and solved the problem that had confused me for weeks.

click.etymology: 拟声词，模仿硬物碰撞发出的清脆咔嗒声，后引申出鼠标点击、突然领会的含义

similar_words.description: 二者均为拟声词，click多指鼠标点击或小物件的咔嗒声，clink多指玻璃、金属碰撞的叮当声

definitions.example: The law firm prioritizes the interests of every client it represents.

client.etymology: 来自拉丁语cliens，原指依附贵族的门客，词根cli-表「倾斜、依附」，后缀-ent表「人」

similar_words.description: 「client」多指接受专业服务（如法律、咨询）的客户，「customer」多指购买商品的顾客

definitions.example: The hikers were cautious when they approached the steep cliff overlooking the ocean.

cliff.etymology: 来自古英语clif，指岩石陡坡，无复杂词缀

similar_words.description: 「cliff」多指海边或高山的陡峭悬崖，「bluff」多指河边或湖边的陡坡

definitions.example: The local climate is mild and suitable for growing citrus fruits.
The current political climate makes it difficult to pass new environmental regulations.

climate.etymology: 来自希腊语klima，原指「地球倾斜形成的气候带」，引申为气候、氛围

similar_words.description: 「weather」指短期的天气状况，「climate」指某一地区长期的气候状态或社会氛围

definitions.example: The climax of the novel comes when the protagonist confronts his long-lost father.

climax.etymology: 来自希腊语klimax，词根cli-表「倾斜、攀升」，后缀-max表「最大」，指攀升至最高点

similar_words.description: 「climax」多指情节、事件发展的高潮，「peak」多指数量、水平的峰值

definitions.example: The plane began to climb to an altitude of 30,000 feet to avoid turbulence.

climb.etymology: 来自古英语climban，表「手脚并用攀爬」，无复杂词缀

similar_words.description: 「climb」多指手脚并用或逐步爬升，「ascend」多指正式、缓慢的上升（如飞机、职位）

definitions.example: Despite the harsh criticism, she clung to her original research hypothesis.

cling.etymology: 来自古英语clingan，表「黏附、紧紧抓住」，无复杂词缀

similar_words.description: 「cling」多指情感、信念上的坚持或物理上的紧紧抓抱，「stick」多指物理黏附或口语化的坚持

definitions.example: The community clinic provides free medical services for low-income families.

clinic.etymology: 来自希腊语klinē，原指「医生诊疗时斜靠的床」，词根clin-表「倾斜」，后缀-ic表「场所」

similar_words.description: 「clinic」是小型诊所或门诊部，「hospital」是大型综合医院

definitions.example: The gardener clipped the overgrown branches of the rose bush.
The government announced plans to clip public spending on non-essential projects.

clip.etymology: 来自古英语clyppan，表「剪、夹」，无复杂词缀

similar_words.description: 「clip」多指用剪刀修剪或快速剪取，「cut」是通用的「切、剪」

definitions.example: She wore a thick woolen cloak to protect herself from the cold wind.
He cloaked his disappointment with a forced smile.

cloak.etymology: 来自古法语cloque，原指「钟形外衣」，引申为掩盖、掩饰

similar_words.description: 「cloak」多指用行为或外衣掩饰真实意图，「mask」多指用面具或伪装物掩盖

definitions.example: The old grandfather clock in the hallway strikes every hour.

clock.etymology: 来自古英语clocca，原指「钟的滴答声」，引申为时钟

similar_words.description: 「clock」是挂钟、座钟等大型计时工具，「watch」是手表、怀表等小型计时工具

definitions.example: Turn the precision knob clockwise to calibrate the experimental device to the required parameter.

clockwise.etymology: 由词根clock（时钟）加后缀-wise（表方向的副词/形容词后缀）构成，指与时钟指针转动方向一致的方向

similar_words.description: counterclockwise指逆时针方向的/地，与clockwise方向相反，形近且语义对立易混

definitions.meaning: 克隆体；（技术或思想的）复制品
definitions.example: Researchers published a paper on the first successful clone of an endangered primate species.
Some companies illegally clone patented electronic products to gain illegal profits.

clone.etymology: 源自希腊语klon（嫩枝，原指植物扦插繁殖的幼株），后引申为生物学意义的克隆及广义的复制品

similar_words.description: cloze指英语考试的完形填空题型，clone指克隆或复制品，形近易混

definitions.example: The graduate student stored her thesis drafts and reference books in the dormitory closet.
The policy makers held a closet discussion to adjust the economic stimulus plan before the public announcement.

closet.etymology: 源自古法语clos（关闭的），原指封闭的小空间，后引申为壁橱；又由“封闭的”引申出形容词义“隐秘的”

similar_words.description: close多作形容词表距离近或关系亲密，closet核心义为壁橱、僻义为隐秘的，同根形近易混

definitions.example: The fashion designer selected a waterproof cloth to make the outdoor activity uniforms for the expedition team.

cloth.etymology: 源自古英语clath，原指覆盖身体或物品的织物，现多指布料

similar_words.description: cloth是名词表布料，clothe是动词表给…穿衣或供衣，同根形近易混

definitions.example: The non-profit organization launched a campaign to clothe the homeless children in the disaster-stricken area.

clothe.etymology: 由名词cloth（布料）动词化而来，后缀-e表动词词性，指用布料覆盖身体（穿衣）或提供衣物

similar_words.description: clothe是动词表穿衣或供衣，cloth是名词表布料，同根形近易混

definitions.example: The factory produced a large quantity of cold-resistant clothing for the Antarctic research team.

clothing.etymology: 由名词cloth（布料）加后缀-ing（表总称的名词后缀）构成，指各类衣服的总称

similar_words.description: clothing是衣服的总称（不可数名词），clothes指具体的多件衣服（只有复数形式），同根近义易混

definitions.meaning: 俱乐部，社团；（运动用的）球杆
definitions.example: The university's debate club won the first prize in the national college debating competition.
In the card game poker, a club is marked with a three-leaf clover symbol.

club.etymology: 源自古挪威语klubba（棍棒），原指用棍棒聚集的人群，后引申为俱乐部；又因棍棒的形状引申为球杆，纸牌梅花因形似三叶草（早期用棍棒符号）得名

similar_words.description: cube指立方体或立方数，club指俱乐部、球杆或梅花，形近易混

definitions.example: The historian found a key clue to the origin of the ancient castle in a 17th-century diary.

clue.etymology: 源自古英语clew（线团），原指古希腊神话中走出迷宫的线团，后引申为解决问题的线索

similar_words.description: clue指解决谜题或案件的具体线索，cue指表演、行动的提示或暗示，近义形近易混

definitions.meaning: 笨拙的；不灵活的；（言辞）不得体的
definitions.example: The clumsy new assistant spilled a bottle of chemical reagent while operating the lab equipment.

clumsy.etymology: 源自古挪威语klumsa（麻木的，僵硬的），后引申为动作笨拙或言辞不得体的

similar_words.description: clumsy侧重肢体动作的笨拙或物品的笨重，awkward侧重处境尴尬或行为举止不得体，近义易混

definitions.example: A cluster of high-tech enterprises has been formed in the city's economic development zone.
The wild flowers cluster along the bank of the small river in the mountainous area.

cluster.etymology: 源自古英语clyster（簇，束），原指植物的丛生簇，后引申为各类同类事物的密集群体，作动词表聚集

similar_words.description: cluster侧重同类事物的密集群（可指人、物、数据等），bunch侧重成串或成束的具体物品（如葡萄、花束），近义易混

definitions.example: In the face of the sudden economic crisis, small business owners clutched every possible opportunity to survive.
The novice driver stalled the car because he released the clutch too quickly.

clutch.etymology: 源自古英语clyccan，表「紧握」，名词义「离合器」由动作逻辑引申而来

similar_words.description: clench侧重「咬紧（牙关）或紧握（拳头）」，clutch侧重因紧急或恐惧时的紧抓

definitions.example: The national football coach announced the new training schedule for the upcoming World Cup qualifier.

definitions.meaning: 辅导，指导（尤指备考或技能提升）
definitions.example: She was coached by a retired professor to prepare for the postgraduate entrance interview.

coach.etymology: 源自匈牙利语kocsi，原指一种四轮长途马车，后引申为「承载知识的指导者（教练）」，动词义由「用马车运送」引申为「辅导」

similar_words.description: instructor泛指各类指导者，coach侧重体育或备考的针对性、个性化辅导

definitions.example: Many European countries are phasing out coal-fired power plants to achieve carbon neutrality.

coal.etymology: 源自古英语col，为原生基础词，无复杂词缀

similar_words.description: charcoal指「人工烧制的木炭」，coal指「天然形成的煤」

definitions.example: The two opposition parties formed a coalition to pass the long-delayed climate change bill.

coalition.etymology: 由前缀co-（共同）与词根alesc（生长，来自拉丁语coalescere「联合」）构成，指「共同联合的团体」

similar_words.description: alliance侧重国家或组织的长期联盟，coalition侧重政党或利益团体的临时、针对性联合

definitions.example: The coarse texture of the ancient pottery reflects the primitive craftsmanship of the Neolithic Age.

coarse.etymology: 源自古英语cors，表「粗糙的」，后引申为「言语粗俗的」

similar_words.description: crude侧重「未经加工的；粗略的」，coarse侧重「质地粗糙的；言语粗俗的」

definitions.example: The marine protected areas along the Atlantic coast are home to numerous endangered marine species.

coast.etymology: 源自古英语cost，表「海岸」，动词义由「沿海岸航行」引申为「靠惯性滑行」

similar_words.description: shore侧重与水直接接壤的窄陆地区域，coast侧重较长的海岸线整体区域

definitions.example: She put on a waterproof coat before going out in the heavy rain.
The metal bridge was coated with a special paint to prevent corrosion.

coat.etymology: 源自古英语cote，表「外套」，动词义由「给…穿外套」引申为「给…涂覆」

similar_words.description: caffeine是「咖啡因（合法兴奋剂，存在于咖啡、茶中）」，cocaine是「可卡因（非法毒品）」

definitions.example: The cock crowed at the break of dawn, marking the start of farmers' daily work.
The plumber turned the cock to shut off the water supply for pipe repair.

cock.etymology: 源自古英语coc，表「公鸡」，后因旋塞的形状或开关动作类似公鸡的鸡冠或动作引申出「旋塞」义

similar_words.description: rooster是美式英语的「公鸡」，cock是英式英语的「公鸡」且有其他僻义

definitions.example: The cybersecurity team developed a new code to protect the company's confidential data.

code.etymology: 源自拉丁语codex，原指「书写用的书板」，后引申为「法典、准则」，现代义「代码」由计算机领域引申而来

similar_words.description: cipher侧重「用于加密的密码符号系统」，code侧重「计算机代码或社会行为准则」

definitions.meaning: 认知的（与认知过程、认知能力相关的）

cognitive.etymology: 词根cogn（知道、认识，如「recognize」中的cogn） + 形容词后缀-itive（表「与…相关的」），表与认识过程相关的

similar_words.description: 同根于词根cogn（知道），「cognitive」侧重「认知过程/能力的属性」，「cognizant」侧重「人知晓、察觉的状态」

definitions.meaning: 有凝聚力的；黏合性的（指群体、物质结合紧密）

cohesive.etymology: 前缀co-（共同） + 词根hes（黏附，与「coherent」的词根her同源） + 形容词后缀-ive（表「具有…性质的」），表共同黏合、结合紧密的

similar_words.description: 同含前缀co-（共同）与黏附类词根，「cohesive」侧重「群体/物质的黏合性」，「coherent」侧重「逻辑/内容的连贯性」

definitions.meaning: 盘绕，卷曲（使成圈状缠绕）

definitions.meaning: 线圈，卷（盘绕成圈的物体）

similar_words.description: 均表「卷曲」，「coil」侧重「成圈状的大卷盘绕（如蛇、电线）」，「curl」侧重「小幅度的弯曲（如头发、纸边）」

definitions.meaning: 创造（新词、新术语）【熟词僻义】

coin.etymology: 源自拉丁语cuneus（楔子，因古代硬币多为楔形），原义为「铸造硬币」，后引申为「创造新词」

similar_words.description: 拼写形近易混，「coin」表「硬币/创造新词」，「coil」表「盘绕/线圈」

definitions.meaning: 同时发生；（观点、利益等）相符，一致

similar_words.description: 拼写形近易混，「coincidence」表「巧合/一致」，「incidence」表「发生率、发生范围」

similar_words.description: 均有「冷漠的」义，「cold」侧重「毫无感情的冰冷态度」，「cool」侧重「冷静或故作疏离的态度」

definitions.meaning: 合作，协作（指专业领域内的共同研究、工作）

collaborate.etymology: 前缀col-（co-的变体，表「共同」，用于l开头的词根前） + 词根labor（劳动、工作，如「laboratory」中的labor） + 动词后缀-ate，表「共同劳动→合作」

definitions.meaning: （建筑物、体系、精神等）倒塌，崩溃；（价格）暴跌

definitions.example: She adjusted the stiff collar of her academic gown before the graduation ceremony.

similar_words.description: 「collar」指衣领、颈圈；「cellar」指地窖、地下室，二者拼写相近易混淆

similar_words.description: 「collection」是名词，表收集物、作品集；「collective」是形容词，表集体的、共同的，二者同根易混淆

similar_words.description: 「collective」是形容词，表集体的；「collection」是名词，表收集物，二者同根易混淆

definitions.meaning: 碰撞；相撞；（意见、利益）冲突

similar_words.description: 「collide」表碰撞、冲突；「collude」表勾结、串通，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The general commanded his troops to retreat before the enemy's reinforcement arrived.

definitions.meaning: （对语言、技能的）掌握；运用能力

commemorate.etymology: com-（共同） + memor-（记忆，源自拉丁语memor） + -ate（动词后缀，使…）

definitions.example: The research center rejected the commercial sponsorship to maintain its academic independence.

committee.etymology: com-（共同） + mit-（送、派，源自拉丁语mittere） + -ee（名词后缀，表「被委托的人」）

definitions.example: Crude oil is one of the most actively traded commodities in the global financial market.

commodity.etymology: com-（共同）+ mod-（度量、标准）+ -ity（名词后缀），原指可按共同标准交易的物品，引申为商品、大宗商品

commonwealth.etymology: com-（共同）+ wealth（福利、财富），原指为共同福利结成的共同体，引申为联邦、共和国

similar_words.description: commonwealth侧重成员通过协议结成的共同体，republic指实行共和制的主权国家

definitions.example: Researchers often communicate their findings through academic journals and international conferences.

communicate.etymology: com-（共同）+ muni-（服务、公共）+ -ate（动词后缀），原指为公共事务传递信息，引申为交流、沟通

similar_words.description: communicate侧重思想、信息的双向传递，contact侧重建立联系的动作

definitions.example: Effective cross-cultural communication is a key skill for global business managers.

communication.etymology: communicate（交流）+ -ation（名词后缀），表示交流、沟通的行为或过程

similar_words.description: communication侧重信息的传递，interaction侧重双方的互动行为

definitions.example: The Communist Manifesto, co-authored by Marx and Engels, laid the theoretical foundation of communism.

communism.etymology: com-（共同）+ mun-（公共的）+ -ism（主义），指生产资料公有的社会意识形态，即共产主义

similar_words.description: communism是共产主义，socialism是社会主义，二者属于不同发展阶段的社会形态

definitions.example: The local community has launched a campaign to promote environmental protection awareness.

community.etymology: com-（共同）+ mun-（公共的）+ -ity（名词后缀），指居住在同一地区或有共同特征的群体，引申为社区、共同体

similar_words.description: community侧重小型、有共同特征的群体，society指大范围的社会整体

definitions.example: Many young professionals in megacities commute between suburban homes and downtown offices daily.

commute.etymology: com-（共同）+ mut-（改变），原指改变行程往返，引申为通勤；法律语境中指改变刑罚即减刑

similar_words.description: commute特指上下班的定期往返，travel泛指一般的旅行、出行

definitions.example: The textbook provides a compact overview of modern Western philosophy for undergraduate students.

compact.etymology: com-（共同）+ pact-（系紧、压紧），原指共同压紧的，引申为紧凑的、简洁的

similar_words.description: compact侧重内容或体积紧凑，concise侧重语言简洁、无冗余

definitions.example: During the ecological research field trip, she found a reliable companion who shared her passion for protecting endangered species.

companion.etymology: com-（共同）+ pan-（面包，原指共享面包的人）+ -ion（名词后缀），原指一起用餐的伙伴，引申为同伴

similar_words.description: 同根词，accompany为动词表「陪伴」，companion为名词表「同伴」

definitions.example: The efficacy of the new medical treatment is comparable to that of the standard therapy, with fewer side effects.

comparable.etymology: com-（共同）+ par-（相等）+ -able（可…的），表可放在一起比较的

similar_words.description: 同根形近词，comparable表「可比较的」，comparative表「比较的；相对的」

definitions.example: Economists emphasize the importance of comparative advantage in international trade to boost global resource allocation efficiency.

comparative.etymology: com-（共同）+ par-（相等）+ -ative（有…性质的），表具有比较性质的

similar_words.description: 同根形近词，comparative表「比较的；相对的」，comparable表「可比较的」

definitions.example: When comparing the cognitive abilities of primates, researchers strictly control variables such as age and living environment.

compare.etymology: com-（共同）+ par-（相等），表放在一起评判是否相等

similar_words.description: 近义动词，compare侧重「比较异同」，contrast侧重「突出对比差异」

definitions.example: By comparison, the renewable energy project has a significantly lower long-term operational cost than the fossil fuel-based one.

comparison.etymology: com-（共同）+ par-（相等）+ -ison（名词后缀），表比较的行为或结果

similar_words.description: 近义名词，comparison强调「比较过程/结果」，contrast强调「明显差异」

definitions.example: The storage compartment of the laboratory ultra-low temperature freezer is divided into labeled sections for different biological samples.

compartment.etymology: com-（共同）+ part-（部分）+ -ment（名词后缀），表被分割成的独立部分

similar_words.description: 形近词，compartment表「隔间」，department表「部门；系」

definitions.example: The anthropologist relied on a compass to navigate the remote Amazon rainforest during her 12-month field study.
The academic paper clarifies the compass of data privacy regulations applicable to educational research projects.

compass.etymology: com-（共同）+ pass-（行走，延伸），原指共同行走的范围，后引申为指示行走方向的指南针

similar_words.description: 形近词，compass为名词表「指南针；范围」，compress为动词表「压缩」

definitions.example: The award-winning documentary about refugee children aroused widespread compassion and donation drives across Europe.

compassion.etymology: com-（共同）+ pass-（感情）+ -ion（名词后缀），表共同感受到的痛苦，引申为同情

similar_words.description: 近义名词，compassion侧重「带有行动意愿的同情」，sympathy侧重「情感上的共鸣」

definitions.example: The new digital learning platform is compatible with various operating systems and mobile devices.

compatible.etymology: com-（共同）+ pat-（忍受，适应）+ -ible（可…的），原指能共同适应的，引申为兼容的

similar_words.description: 形近词，compatible表「兼容的」，comparable表「可比较的」

definitions.example: Strict carbon emission reduction targets compel manufacturing enterprises to invest in green technology upgrades.

compel.etymology: com-（加强语气）+ pel-（推，驱使），字面义为强力推动，引申为强迫

similar_words.description: 近义动词，compel侧重「外力强制」，impel侧重「内在动机驱动」

definitions.meaning: 令人信服的；引人入胜的
definitions.example: The study provides compelling evidence that regular physical activity reduces the risk of cardiovascular diseases.

compelling.etymology: 由已考单词「compel」的现在分词转化而来，com-（加强语气）+pel（推动）→ 极具说服力的、引人入胜的

similar_words.description: compelling指令人信服/引人入胜的，compulsive指强迫性的、难以自控的

definitions.example: The manufacturing company will compensate the local residents for the environmental damage caused by its factory.

compensate.etymology: com-（共同）+pens（称量、支付）+ate（动词后缀）→ 共同称量后支付等价物→补偿、弥补

similar_words.description: compensate指对损失/付出的补偿，complement指使事物更完整的补充

definitions.example: Victims of the data breach are eligible for financial compensation from the tech firm.

compensation.etymology: 由动词「compensate」派生而来，com-+pens+ation（名词后缀）→补偿（金）、赔偿（金）

similar_words.description: compensation指补偿/赔偿，complementation指事物间的互补作用

definitions.meaning: 竞争；角逐（为获得利益、名次等）
definitions.example: Small local retailers struggle to compete with national chain stores in terms of pricing and inventory.

compete.etymology: com-（共同）+pet（追求、寻求）+e→ 共同追求同一目标→竞争、角逐

similar_words.description: compete侧重为争夺利益/名次的竞争，contend还可指主张、争辩观点

definitions.meaning: 有能力的；能胜任的（指人或事物）
definitions.example: A competent project manager should be able to coordinate multiple tasks and resolve conflicts efficiently.

competent.etymology: com-（加强语气）+pet（追求）+ent（形容词后缀）→ 能胜任追求目标的→有能力的、能胜任的

similar_words.description: competent指人的能力胜任，competitive指与竞争相关的、有竞争力的

definitions.example: Intense global competition has forced domestic manufacturers to upgrade their production technology.

competition.etymology: 由动词「compete」派生而来，com-+pet+ition（名词后缀）→竞争、角逐、比赛

similar_words.description: competition侧重泛指竞争（可无明确规则），contest多指有规则的正式比赛/角逐

definitions.meaning: 竞争的；有竞争力的；好胜的
definitions.example: To stay competitive in the international market, the company has invested heavily in research and development.

competitive.etymology: 由名词「competition」派生而来，com-+pet+itive（形容词后缀）→竞争的、有竞争力的

similar_words.description: competitive指与竞争相关的，competent指人的能力胜任

definitions.example: The academic research team spent nine months compiling data from 15 peer-reviewed journals for their systematic review.

compile.etymology: com-（共同）+pile（堆积）→ 把分散的资料共同堆积整理→汇编、编纂

similar_words.description: compile指汇编已有资料，compose指创作、组成新的内容

definitions.meaning: 抱怨；投诉（表达不满或诉求）
definitions.example: Many local residents complain that the new high-speed railway construction has caused severe noise pollution.

complain.etymology: com-（加强语气）+plain（捶打、抱怨）→ 捶胸顿足表达不满→抱怨、投诉

similar_words.description: complain指表达不满/投诉，explain指阐述、解释某事物

definitions.meaning: 抱怨；投诉（正式或非正式的不满表达）
definitions.example: The consumer protection association receives over 100 formal complaints about product quality each week.
The elderly patient consulted the doctor about a minor complaint of frequent headaches.

complaint.etymology: 由动词「complain」派生而来，com-+plain+t（名词后缀）→抱怨；引申为身体的轻微不适、小毛病

similar_words.description: complaint指抱怨/轻微疾病，compliance指遵守、顺从（规则等）

definitions.example: The new research findings complement the existing theories on Arctic climate change.
In the sentence 'The teacher found the student diligent', 'diligent' serves as the object complement.

complement.etymology: 前缀com-（共同）+ 词根ple-（填满）+ 后缀-ment（名词/动词后缀），共同填满空缺引申为补充、补足

similar_words.description: 「complement」表补充、补足；「compliment」表赞美、恭维，二者拼写仅倒数第二个字母有差异（前者为e，后者为i）

definitions.example: Postgraduate students must complete the assigned literature review before the mid-term assessment.

complete.etymology: 前缀com-（完全）+ 词根ple-（填满）+ 后缀-e，完全填满引申为完整的、完成的

similar_words.description: 「complete」侧重事物无缺失、完整无缺；「comprehensive」侧重内容全面、覆盖面广

definitions.example: The complex neural network in the brain requires interdisciplinary research to fully understand.
The novel analyzes the protagonist's inferiority complex rooted in childhood poverty.

complex.etymology: 前缀com-（共同）+ 词根plex-（折叠），多重折叠引申为复杂的、复合体

similar_words.description: 「complex」侧重事物内部结构、体系的复杂；「complicated」侧重过程、问题的复杂难办

definitions.meaning: 遵守；依从（尤指对规则、要求的服从）
definitions.example: All academic institutions must ensure compliance with the national ethical guidelines for human research.

compliance.etymology: 前缀com-（共同）+ 词根pli-（弯曲、顺从）+ 后缀-ance（名词后缀），共同顺从规则引申为遵守、依从

similar_words.description: 「compliance」是名词，表遵守的行为或状态；「comply」是动词，表遵守、服从（常与with搭配）

definitions.example: Unexpected policy changes may complicate the implementation of the poverty alleviation project.

complicate.etymology: 前缀com-（共同）+ 词根plic-（折叠）+ 后缀-ate（动词后缀），多重折叠引申为使复杂化

similar_words.description: 「complicate」是动词，表使…变复杂；「complex」是形容词/名词，表复杂的/复合体

definitions.example: The complicated international trade negotiations lasted for more than two years.

complicated.etymology: 动词complicate的过去分词作形容词，表被折叠得很繁琐的→复杂的

similar_words.description: 「complicated」侧重过程、问题的复杂难处理；「complex」侧重结构、体系的复杂

definitions.example: A sudden shortage of semiconductors added a new complication to the automotive production plan.
Uncontrolled hypertension can lead to severe complications such as stroke and heart failure.

complication.etymology: 前缀com-（共同）+ 词根plic-（折叠）+ 后缀-ation（名词后缀），折叠的状态引申为复杂情况

similar_words.description: 「complication」侧重突发的、额外的复杂问题；「complexity」侧重事物本身固有的复杂性

definitions.example: The Nobel laureate paid a high compliment to the young scientist's pioneering research.

compliment.etymology: 前缀com-（共同）+ 词根pli-（折叠、表达敬意）+ 后缀-ment（名词/动词后缀），原指表达敬意的话语引申为赞美、恭维

similar_words.description: 「compliment」表赞美、恭维；「complement」表补充、补足，二者拼写仅倒数第二个字母有差异（前者为i，后者为e）

definitions.meaning: 遵守；服从（常与介词with搭配）
definitions.example: All participants in the clinical trial must comply with the strict testing protocols.

comply.etymology: 前缀com-（共同）+ 词根ply-（折叠、顺从），共同顺从规则引申为遵守、服从

similar_words.description: 「comply」侧重遵守规则、要求（常与with搭配）；「obey」侧重服从权威、命令（及物动词）

definitions.example: The core component of the 5G communication system is its high-frequency signal processor.

component.etymology: 前缀com-（共同）+ 词根pon-（放置）+ 后缀-ent（名词/形容词后缀），放在一起的部分引申为组成部分

similar_words.description: 「component」侧重机器、系统的具体组成部件；「element」侧重化学、抽象事物的基本元素

definitions.example: All living tissues are composed of cells, the basic units of life.
Beethoven composed his Ninth Symphony while completely deaf.

compose.etymology: com-（共同、一起）+ pos-（放置）+ -e（动词后缀），将元素放置整合为整体→组成；引申为将音符、文字编排整合→创作

similar_words.description: compose多以被动形式「be composed of」表示由…构成；comprise主动形式即可表示包含、由…构成，无需被动

definitions.meaning: 合成的，复合的；合成物，复合材料
definitions.example: The new aerospace vehicle uses a lightweight composite material to enhance fuel efficiency.

composite.etymology: com-（共同）+ pos-（放置）+ -ite（形容词/名词后缀），将不同材料放置结合→合成的；引申为合成产物→复合材料

similar_words.description: composite侧重材料、工程结构的合成复合（如碳纤维复合材料）；compound侧重化学、语言范畴的复合（如化合物、复合词）

definitions.example: The composition of the atmosphere has changed significantly due to human activities.
The professor assigned a 400-word composition on the theme of 'sustainable development'.

composition.etymology: com-（共同）+ pos-（放置）+ -ition（名词后缀），元素放置整合的状态→构成；引申为文字、音乐的编排成果→作品

similar_words.description: composition侧重事物的组成成分或比例（如土壤构成）；constitution侧重事物的结构、体质或宪法（熟义）

definitions.meaning: 化合物，复合物；复合的，混合的
definitions.example: Carbon dioxide is a compound made up of carbon and oxygen.
Lack of sleep can compound the risk of cognitive decline in elderly people.

compound.etymology: com-（共同）+ pound（放置，源自拉丁语ponere），将不同物质放置结合→化合物；引申为使问题叠加恶化→使加重

similar_words.description: compound侧重化学或语言范畴的复合（如化合物、复合词「blackboard」）；composite侧重材料或工程范畴的合成（如航空复合材料）

definitions.example: It takes time for students to comprehend the philosophical theories in Kant's works.
The term 'urban planning' comprehends infrastructure design and environmental protection.

comprehend.etymology: com-（完全）+ prehend（抓住，源自拉丁语prehendere），完全抓住（内容本质）→理解；完全抓住（范围边界）→包含

similar_words.description: comprehend侧重全面、深入地理解复杂抽象内容；understand侧重普通层面的明白、知晓

definitions.example: Reading comprehension accounts for 40% of the total score in the postgraduate English exam.
The new regulation has a wide comprehension of all commercial activities in the region.

comprehension.etymology: com-（完全）+ prehens（抓住）+ -ion（名词后缀），完全抓住的状态→理解力；完全抓住的范围→包含

similar_words.description: comprehension指理解、理解力；apprehension指忧虑、逮捕或（较少指）理解，考研常考其「忧虑」义

definitions.example: The research institute released a comprehensive report on global biodiversity loss.
Comprehensive schools in the UK admit students of all academic abilities.

comprehensive.etymology: com-（完全）+ prehens（抓住）+ -ive（形容词后缀），完全抓住所有方面的→全面的；引申为招收所有学生的学校→综合中学

similar_words.description: comprehensive侧重内容全面、涵盖所有细节（如综合报告）；extensive侧重范围广、面积大（如广泛调查）

definitions.meaning: 压缩，压紧（物理/抽象内容）
definitions.example: Scientists use special equipment to compress air into liquid form for industrial use.
The editor asked the author to compress the 600-page thesis into a 200-page monograph.

compress.etymology: com-（共同）+ press（压），将物体或内容一起压紧→压缩；引申为缩减篇幅→精简

similar_words.description: compress指压缩、压紧或精简（物理/抽象）；depress指使沮丧、使经济萧条

definitions.example: The postgraduate curriculum comprises core courses and elective courses.
The United Nations comprises 193 member states as of 2024.

comprise.etymology: com-（共同）+ prise（抓住，源自prehendere的变体），共同抓住所有组成部分→包含；引申为整体由部分构成→由…组成

similar_words.description: comprise无需被动即可表示「由…组成」；compose需用被动形式「be composed of」表示该含义

definitions.example: The two negotiating parties finally reached a compromise on the trade tariff issue.
Disclosing classified data will compromise the national security of the country.

compromise.etymology: com-（共同）+ promise（承诺），双方共同做出让步承诺→妥协；引申为打破原则承诺→危及

similar_words.description: compromise指妥协、折中或危及；promise指承诺、诺言或有希望

definitions.example: Compulsory education laws require all children to attend school until a certain age in most countries.

compulsory.etymology: com-（共同、全部）+ puls（驱动、推动）+ -ory（形容词后缀）→ 被共同驱动的→ 强制性的

similar_words.description: compulsory指制度规定的强制性，compulsive指人无法自控的强迫性

definitions.example: Researchers computed the average speed of the experimental vehicles using data collected over six months.

compute.etymology: com-（共同）+ put（思考、计算）+ -e（动词后缀）→ 共同计算→ 估算

similar_words.description: compute侧重用技术或精密方法计算，calculate侧重普通或复杂运算

definitions.example: The veteran often gathered with his old comrades to share stories of their military service.

comrade.etymology: com-（共同）+ rade（=ride，原指同路出行的人）→ 同路的伙伴→ 同志、战友

similar_words.description: comrade侧重有共同政治/军事目标的伙伴，companion侧重普通同伴

definitions.example: The witness attempted to conceal the critical evidence from the investigative team.

conceal.etymology: con-（完全、彻底）+ ceal（隐藏）→ 完全隐藏→ 隐瞒

similar_words.description: conceal意为隐藏、隐瞒，reveal意为揭示、展现，为反义形近词

definitions.example: The presidential candidate conceded the election shortly after the final results were announced.
The government conceded a small increase in welfare spending to avoid public unrest.

concede.etymology: con-（完全）+ cede（屈服、让步）→ 完全屈服→ 承认、让步

similar_words.description: concede侧重主动让步或承认，recede侧重事物后退、减弱

definitions.example: She conceived the framework for her groundbreaking sociological study during a year of fieldwork.
The medical study found that regular exercise can improve the ability to conceive naturally.

conceive.etymology: con-（共同）+ ceive（拿、取）→ 共同获取（想法/生命）→ 构想、怀孕

similar_words.description: conceive意为构想、怀孕，deceive意为欺骗、欺诈

definitions.meaning: 集中（注意力、精力或资源）
definitions.example: Professors encourage students to concentrate on analyzing primary sources rather than relying solely on secondary materials.
The factory uses a special process to concentrate the fruit juice before packaging.

concentrate.etymology: con-（共同）+ centr（中心）+ -ate（动词后缀）→ 共同汇聚到中心→ 集中、浓缩

similar_words.description: concentrate侧重将分散的精力/资源集中到一处，focus侧重对准焦点或核心问题

definitions.example: Continuous noise in the study area severely impaired the participants' concentration on the cognitive tasks.
The lab tested the concentration of heavy metals in the river water samples collected weekly.

concentration.etymology: con-（共同）+ centr（中心）+ -ation（名词后缀）→ 汇聚到中心的状态→ 专注、浓度

similar_words.description: concentration指主动集中的注意力状态，attention指被动或主动的注意行为

definitions.example: The concept of 'shared value' has become a key theme in contemporary business ethics research.

concept.etymology: com-（共同）+ cept（拿、取）→ 共同获取的统一想法→ 概念、观念

similar_words.description: concept指具体的、已形成的概念，conception指构想的过程或抽象设想

definitions.example: The rising unemployment rate among young graduates is a major concern for the government.
The policy change concerns all international students enrolled in the university's graduate programs.

concern.etymology: con-（共同）+ cern（区分、察觉）→ 共同察觉的重要之事→ 关心、涉及

similar_words.description: concern指关心、涉及，discern指识别、分辨

definitions.example: Many peer-reviewed papers concerning climate change have been cited in national policy documents.

concerning.etymology: 前缀con-（表‘共同、完全’）+词根cern（表‘关注、区分’）+后缀-ing（动词转介词后缀），由动词concern派生而来

similar_words.description: 均为正式介词表‘关于’，concerning多用于学术、官方文本，regarding适用范围更广

definitions.example: The Royal Philharmonic Orchestra will give a charity concert featuring works by Beethoven next month.
Educators and parents should act in concert to promote children’s mental health development.

concert.etymology: 前缀con-（表‘共同’）+词根cert（表‘确定、安排’）→ 共同安排的演出→音乐会；引申为‘一致协作’

similar_words.description: 拼写相近，concert表‘音乐会/一致’，consent表‘同意、准许’

definitions.example: The labor union made a minor concession to reach a wage agreement with the enterprise management.
The local government offers tax concessions to enterprises investing in renewable energy projects.

concession.etymology: 前缀con-（表‘共同’）+词根cess（表‘行走、让步’）+后缀-ion（名词后缀）→ 共同做出的让步→妥协

similar_words.description: 拼写相近，concession表‘让步/优惠’，confession表‘供认、坦白’

definitions.example: A high-quality research proposal should be concise, logical and focused on core research objectives.

concise.etymology: 前缀con-（表‘完全’）+词根cise（表‘切割、削减’）+后缀-e → 削减冗余部分→简洁的

similar_words.description: 拼写相近且均为形容词，concise侧重‘无冗余的简洁’，precise侧重‘准确无误的精确’

definitions.example: After analyzing 3 years of survey data, researchers concluded that regular exercise improves cognitive function.
The 27th UN Climate Change Conference concluded with a global agreement on carbon emission reduction.

conclude.etymology: 前缀con-（表‘共同’）+词根clud（表‘关闭’）+后缀-e → 共同关闭（进程）→结束；引申为‘经推理得出结论’

similar_words.description: 均为动词表‘推断’，conclude侧重‘基于证据得出最终结论’，deduce侧重‘从一般原理演绎具体结论’

definitions.example: The paper’s conclusion is supported by extensive experimental data and rigorous statistical analysis.
The documentary’s conclusion highlights the urgent need for global biodiversity conservation.

conclusion.etymology: 前缀con-（表‘共同’）+词根clus（表‘关闭’）+后缀-ion（名词后缀）→ 关闭的状态→结尾；引申为‘最终推论’

similar_words.description: 拼写相近，conclusion表‘结论/结尾’，collision表‘碰撞、冲突’

definitions.example: The forensic team found conclusive evidence linking the suspect to the crime scene.

conclusive.etymology: 前缀con-（表‘完全’）+词根clus（表‘关闭’）+后缀-ive（形容词后缀）→ 完全关闭（无争议）→结论性的

similar_words.description: 均为形容词表‘决定性的’，conclusive侧重‘证据确凿、无可辩驳’，decisive侧重‘起关键作用的’

definitions.meaning: 具体的，实在的（与抽象相对）
definitions.example: Instead of vague promises, we need concrete strategies to address the urban housing crisis.
The construction workers reinforced the concrete foundation with steel bars to ensure stability.

concrete.etymology: 前缀con-（表‘共同’）+词根cre（表‘生长、制造’）+后缀-e → 共同制成的固体材料→混凝土；引申为‘非抽象的、具体的’

similar_words.description: 均为形容词表‘具体的’，concrete侧重‘与抽象相对的实在’，specific侧重‘明确的、特定的’

definitions.example: The United Nations General Assembly unanimously condemned the violation of international humanitarian law.
The court condemned the former official to life imprisonment for massive corruption.

condemn.etymology: 前缀con-（表‘完全’）+词根demn（表‘惩罚、伤害’）→ 完全惩罚→判刑；引申为‘公开谴责’

similar_words.description: 均为动词表‘指责’，condemn侧重‘正式、公开的强烈谴责’，blame侧重‘一般性的归咎’

definitions.example: You need to condense your 5000-word dissertation into a 500-word abstract for the academic conference.
When warm humid air meets a cold window pane, it condenses into tiny water droplets.

condense.etymology: 前缀con-（表‘共同’）+词根dens（表‘浓密’）+后缀-e → 使变浓密→凝结；引申为‘压缩内容’

similar_words.description: 均为动词表‘压缩’，condense侧重‘使内容精简或物质凝结’，compress侧重‘通过挤压使体积变小’

definitions.example: Good working conditions are essential for the productivity of academic researchers.
The growth of microbial populations is conditioned by temperature and nutrient availability.

condition.etymology: 词根con-（共同）+ dit（承诺、约定）+ -ion（名词后缀），原指共同约定的条款，引申为条件、状况；作动词时表设定条件制约

similar_words.description: 「condition」侧重具体的条件或个体状况；「situation」侧重整体的形势、局面

definitions.example: The interdisciplinary team conducted a three-year longitudinal study on adolescent mental health.
His unethical conduct during the experiment led to the retraction of his paper.

conduct.etymology: 词根con-（共同）+ duct（引导），原指共同引导进程，引申为实施、指挥；作名词时表引导下的行为举止

similar_words.description: 「conduct」侧重实施有组织的活动（如研究、演出）；「perform」侧重执行任务或表演

definitions.example: The symphony conductor signaled the string section to begin the second movement.

conductor.etymology: 由动词「conduct」（引导）加后缀-or（表人/物）构成，指执行引导的人（指挥）或物（导体）

similar_words.description: 「conductor」是「conduct」的派生名词，表引导的主体；「conduct」作动词表实施、引导，名词表行为

definitions.example: The university will confer an honorary doctorate on the Nobel Prize-winning economist.

confer.etymology: 词根con-（共同）+ fer（带来），原指共同带来意见商议，引申为授予（将荣誉带来给某人）

similar_words.description: 「confer」意为授予、商议；「refer」意为提及、参考，后常接介词to

definitions.example: The annual international conference on artificial intelligence attracted over 2,000 scholars worldwide.

conference.etymology: 由动词「confer」（商议）加后缀-ence（名词后缀）构成，指商议的正式场合，即会议

similar_words.description: 「conference」侧重大型、正式的学术或行业会议；「seminar」侧重小型的专题研讨会

definitions.example: The suspect confessed to leaking the confidential research data after hours of interrogation.

confess.etymology: 词根con-（完全）+ fess（说），原指完全说出实情，引申为坦白、承认

similar_words.description: 「confess」意为坦白错误或罪行；「profess」意为公开宣称、表明（信念等）

definitions.example: Building confidence in one's analytical skills is critical for success in graduate-level exams.
The editor shared the manuscript details with me in strict confidence.

confidence.etymology: 词根con-（完全）+ fid（相信）+ -ence（名词后缀），原指完全的信任，引申为信心；作「秘密」讲时指仅告知信任者的内容

similar_words.description: 「confidence」侧重对自身能力或他人的主观信任感，也可表秘密；「trust」侧重对他人的客观信赖与委托

definitions.example: The lead researcher is confident that her findings will revolutionize the field of genetics.

confident.etymology: 词根con-（完全）+ fid（相信）+ -ent（形容词后缀），意为充满信心的

similar_words.description: 「confident」表主观的自信；「confidential」表客观的机密性，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The government refused to release the confidential documents related to national security.
She is my confidential assistant who handles all my sensitive affairs.

confidential.etymology: 词根con-（完全）+ fid（相信）+ -ential（形容词后缀），原指完全可信任的，引申为仅限信任者知晓的（机密的）

similar_words.description: 「confidential」侧重官方、正式场合的涉密内容，需严格管控；「secret」侧重个人或一般的私密信息

definitions.example: Due to the pandemic, the research team was confined to the university campus for six months.
The study explores the moral confines of genetic engineering in human reproduction.

confine.etymology: 词根con-（共同）+ fin（界限）+ -e，原指共同设定界限，引申为限制；作复数名词时表界限、范围

similar_words.description: 「confine」意为限制、禁闭；「refine」意为精炼、改善，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The latest archaeological findings confirm the existence of an ancient trade route connecting East Asia and Central Asia.

confirm.etymology: 前缀con-（表「完全、共同」）+ 词根firm（表「固定、坚定」），使信息完全固定下来即证实、确认

similar_words.description: confirm侧重核实已有信息的真实性，affirm侧重断言、重申立场

definitions.example: The conflict between economic growth and ecological conservation remains a key issue in global governance.
His personal values conflict with the corporate culture of the new company.

conflict.etymology: 前缀con-（表「共同、一起」）+ 词根flict（表「打击，来自拉丁语fligere」），互相打击即冲突

similar_words.description: conflict多指利益、观点的对立冲突，contradiction侧重言论、事实的逻辑矛盾

definitions.example: All research activities must conform to the ethical guidelines set by the academic association.

conform.etymology: 前缀con-（表「共同、相同」）+ 词根form（表「形状、形式」），使形式相同即符合

similar_words.description: conform表「符合规则/标准」，confirm表「证实、确认信息」

definitions.example: The design of the product is in strict conformity with the national safety standards.

conformity.etymology: 动词conform（符合）+ 名词后缀-ity（表「性质、状态」），表符合的状态

similar_words.description: conformity侧重符合既定规则，uniformity侧重事物之间的完全一致

definitions.example: Modern educators must confront the challenge of integrating technology into traditional teaching methods.

confront.etymology: 前缀con-（表「共同、一起」）+ 词根front（表「前面」），一起站到前面即面对

similar_words.description: confront侧重主动面对困难或问题，encounter侧重偶然遇到人或事

definitions.example: The overlapping concepts in the textbook may confuse first-year graduate students.

confuse.etymology: 前缀con-（表「共同、一起」）+ 词根fuse（表「灌注，来自拉丁语fundere」），混合灌注即混淆

similar_words.description: confuse侧重因混淆事物而困惑，puzzle侧重因难以理解而困惑

definitions.example: The sudden change of the exam schedule caused great confusion among the candidates.

confusion.etymology: 动词confuse（使困惑）+ 名词后缀-ion（表「状态、结果」），表困惑的状态

similar_words.description: confusion侧重思维或认知的混乱，chaos侧重环境或秩序的完全无序

definitions.example: The Nobel Committee congratulated the physicist on his pioneering research in quantum mechanics.

congratulate.etymology: 前缀con-（表「共同、一起」）+ 词根gratul（表「高兴，来自拉丁语gratus」）+ 动词后缀-ate，共同表达高兴即祝贺

similar_words.description: congratulate侧重对他人成就的祝贺，celebrate侧重举办活动庆祝事件

definitions.example: She received a flood of congratulations from her peers after publishing her first academic paper.

congratulation.etymology: 动词congratulate（祝贺）+ 名词后缀-ion（表「行为、结果」），表祝贺的行为或话语

similar_words.description: congratulation特指针对成就的祝贺，greeting泛指日常问候

definitions.example: The new climate bill was finally approved by the US Congress after three rounds of voting.

congress.etymology: 前缀con-（表「共同、一起」）+ 词根gress（表「行走，来自拉丁语gradi」），走到一起议事即代表大会、国会

similar_words.description: congress指美国等国家的国会，parliament指英国等国家的议会

definitions.example: The study of Arctic ice melting is conducted in conjunction with satellite monitoring data.
The word 'but' is a frequently used coordinating conjunction in academic writing.

conjunction.etymology: con-（共同）+ junct（连接）+ -ion（名词后缀），表共同连接的状态或事物

similar_words.description: 「junction」指连接点、枢纽，「conjunction」核心义为事件的关联，僻义为语法连词

definitions.example: Researchers have connected the decline of pollinators to the excessive use of pesticides.

connect.etymology: con-（共同）+ nect（绑缚），表将事物绑在一起

similar_words.description: 「contact」作动词指联络人，「connect」侧重事物间的逻辑或物理连接

definitions.example: The study reveals a significant connection between sleep quality and cognitive performance.

connection.etymology: connect（动词连接）+ -ion（名词后缀），表连接的状态或关系

similar_words.description: 「connection」泛指事物间的联系，「conjunction」侧重事件同时发生的关联

definitions.example: In literary analysis, the word 'forest' often carries mysterious or symbolic connotations.

connotation.etymology: con-（共同）+ not（标记）+ -ation（名词后缀），表共同隐含的标记意义

similar_words.description: 「denotation」指字面意义，「connotation」指隐含的情感或文化内涵

definitions.example: She managed to conquer her stage fright by participating in numerous public speaking events.

conquer.etymology: con-（完全）+ quer（寻求、获得），表完全获得（领土等）

similar_words.description: 「conquer」是动词，表实施征服或克服，「conquest」是名词，表征服或克服的行为或成果

definitions.example: The conquest of disease remains one of the most important goals of medical research.

conquest.etymology: con-（完全）+ quest（寻求），表寻求获得的结果

similar_words.description: 「conquest」是名词，表征服的结果或行为，「conquer」是动词，表实施征服

definitions.example: The journalist acted out of conscience to expose the government's corruption.

conscience.etymology: con-（共同）+ sci（知晓）+ -ence（名词后缀），表共同知晓的道德认知

similar_words.description: 「conscience」是名词，表良心、良知，「conscious」是形容词，表有意识的

definitions.example: The conscientious editor proofread the manuscript three times before publication.

conscientious.etymology: conscience（名词良心）+ -tious（形容词后缀），表凭良心行事的

similar_words.description: 「conscientious」指做事认真尽责的，「conscious」指有意识的

definitions.example: More consumers are becoming conscious of the environmental impact of fast fashion.

conscious.etymology: con-（共同）+ sci（知晓）+ -ous（形容词后缀），表共同知晓的、有意识的

similar_words.description: 「conscious」是形容词，表有意识的，「conscience」是名词，表良心

definitions.example: The company has reported 12 consecutive months of revenue growth.

consecutive.etymology: con-（共同）+ secut（跟随）+ -ive（形容词后缀），表共同跟随的、连续的

similar_words.description: 「consecutive」指时间或顺序上连续的，「consequent」指作为结果的、随之发生的

definitions.example: The scientific community has reached a broad consensus on the necessity of reducing fossil fuel dependence.

consensus.etymology: con-（共同）+ sens（感觉）+ -us（名词后缀），共同的认知与判断→共识

similar_words.description: 「consensus」为名词，指群体达成的共识；「consent」可作动词/名词，表个体的同意、准许

definitions.example: All human subjects must consent to participate in the clinical trial after being informed of potential risks.
The research project cannot be launched without the ethical committee's written consent.

consent.etymology: con-（共同）+ sent（感觉），想法达成一致→同意

similar_words.description: 「consent」侧重个体的同意许可；「consensus」侧重群体的一致意见

definitions.example: The decision to cut public health funding will bring about severe consequences for low-income families.
The discovery of ancient human remains is a matter of great consequence for archaeological research.

consequence.etymology: con-（全部）+ sequ（跟随）+ -ence（名词后缀），随之而来的全部事物→结果；引申为重要性

similar_words.description: 「consequence」侧重因果关系的结果；「subsequence」侧重时间顺序上随后发生的事

definitions.example: Most traditional retail stores suffered sharp declines in sales during the pandemic; consequently, many shifted to online business models.

consequently.etymology: consequent（作为结果的）+ -ly（副词后缀），表因果逻辑连接

similar_words.description: 「consequently」表因果逻辑（因此）；「subsequently」表时间先后逻辑（随后）

definitions.example: The new regulation aims to strengthen the conservation of endangered marine species.
The conservation of momentum is a basic principle that applies to all mechanical motions.

conservation.etymology: con-（全部）+ serv（保存、保持）+ -ation（名词后缀），全部保留下来→保护；引申为物理上的守恒

similar_words.description: 「conservation」指保护、节约；「conversation」指交谈、会话

definitions.example: The Conservative government announced a series of measures to boost domestic manufacturing.
Several Conservatives in parliament voted against the proposed climate bill.

Conservative.etymology: 源自conserve（保存）+ -ative（形容词后缀），主张保留现有制度的→保守的；首字母大写特指英国保守党的/保守党人

similar_words.description: 「Conservative」指英国保守党/保守派的；「Liberal」指英国自由党/自由派的，是英国政坛两大主流派别

definitions.example: Households are encouraged to conserve electricity by using energy-efficient appliances.
The homemade berry conserve is sold at the local farmers' market every weekend.

conserve.etymology: con-（全部）+ serv（保持、保留），全部保留以防损耗→保存；引申为经加工保留风味的蜜饯

similar_words.description: 「conserve」侧重为避免浪费、破坏而节约保存；「reserve」侧重为特定用途预留、储备

definitions.example: Postgraduate applicants are advised to consider their research interests carefully before selecting supervisors.
Many linguists consider code-switching a common phenomenon in bilingual communities.

consider.etymology: con-（共同）+ sid（坐）+ -er（动词后缀），众人坐在一起商讨→考虑

similar_words.description: 「consider」表经过思考后得出判断，可接复合宾语；「regard」表基于印象看待，常与介词as搭配

definitions.example: The development of 5G technology has attracted considerable investment from global tech firms.
She was a considerable figure in the field of feminist literary criticism in the 20th century.

considerable.etymology: consider（考虑）+ -able（形容词后缀），值得认真考虑的→程度大的；重要的

similar_words.description: 「considerable」侧重数量、程度之大；「considerate」侧重人性格体贴、考虑周到

definitions.example: It was considerate of the organizer to provide sign language interpretation for hearing-impaired attendees.

considerate.etymology: consider（考虑）+ -ate（形容词后缀），做事多为他人考虑的→体贴的

similar_words.description: 「considerate」用于形容人考虑周全、体贴他人；「considerable」用于形容数量或程度之大

definitions.example: After careful consideration, the academic committee decided to revise the research funding criteria.
In commercial contracts, valid consideration is required for an agreement to be legally binding.

consideration.etymology: con-（共同）+sider（原指观察星象，引申为思考）+-ation（名词后缀），原指共同观察星象以做决策，引申为考虑

similar_words.description: 「considerate」的名词形式，表「体贴」；consideration表「考虑」，二者为同根易混词

definitions.example: The research team for the climate project consists of 12 scholars from 5 countries.
The success of the reform consists in its ability to adapt to local conditions.

consist.etymology: con-（共同）+sist（站），站在一起形成整体→组成

similar_words.description: consist为不及物动词，需与of/in连用；compose为及物动词，可直接接宾语表「组成」

definitions.meaning: 一致的，始终如一的（常与with连用）
definitions.example: The witness's testimony is consistent with the video evidence provided by the police.

consistent.etymology: con-（共同）+sist（站）+-ent（形容词后缀），站在一起的→一致的

similar_words.description: consistent侧重「前后内容/行为无矛盾」；constant侧重「状态/动作持续不断」

definitions.example: The psychologist consoled the survivors of the natural disaster with professional counseling.
The engineer adjusted the parameters on the satellite launch console.

console.etymology: con-（强调）+sol（安慰）+-e，加强安慰的动作→安慰

similar_words.description: console侧重正式场合对悲痛者的安慰；comfort适用范围更广，可指日常安慰

definitions.example: The university took measures to consolidate its advantages in the field of artificial intelligence.
The company decided to consolidate its three regional offices into one central headquarters.

consolidate.etymology: con-（共同）+solid（固体的）+-ate（动词后缀），使共同成为坚实的整体→巩固；合并

similar_words.description: consolidate侧重「巩固已有地位/成果」；strengthen侧重「增强力量/强度」

definitions.example: The black-and-white mural was conspicuous against the plain white wall of the campus.

conspicuous.etymology: con-（完全）+spic（看）+-uous（形容词后缀），完全能被看见的→显眼的

similar_words.description: conspicuous侧重「因与众不同而显眼」；obvious侧重「容易被察觉的明显」

definitions.example: The journalist exposed a conspiracy between politicians and business tycoons to manipulate the market.

conspiracy.etymology: con-（共同）+spir（呼吸）+-acy（名词后缀），同呼吸（保持秘密）→密谋

similar_words.description: conspiracy特指「多人参与的非法/秘密阴谋」；plot可指个人或多人的计划，也可指「（故事的）情节」

definitions.example: Several senior managers were charged with conspiring to falsify the company's financial statements.

conspire.etymology: con-（共同）+spir（呼吸）+-e，同呼吸（保持秘密）→密谋

similar_words.description: conspire仅指「多人密谋做坏事」；plot可指「策划任何事」，也可作名词表「阴谋/情节」

definitions.meaning: 持续的，不断的；始终如一的
definitions.example: The constant rise in global temperature has posed a serious threat to biodiversity.

constant.etymology: con-（强调）+st（站）+-ant（形容词后缀），站着不动的→持续的；始终如一的

similar_words.description: constant侧重「动作/状态持续不间断」；consistent侧重「前后内容/行为一致无矛盾」

definitions.example: The parliamentary candidate held a town hall meeting to listen to the demands of his constituents.
Protein is an essential constituent of a balanced diet for athletes.

constituent.etymology: con-（共同）+stit（站）+-uent（名词后缀），站在一起的人/物→选民；组成部分

similar_words.description: constituent可指「选民」或「抽象/具体组成部分」；component仅指「具体的机械/系统组成部件」

definitions.example: Female workers constitute nearly half of the total labor force in the service industry.
Failing to disclose relevant information to investors constitutes a breach of securities regulations.

constitute.etymology: con-（共同、一起）+ stitut（站立、建立）+ 动词后缀-e，多个个体共同站在一起形成整体，引申为组成、构成

similar_words.description: 「constituent」为名词表「成分、选民」或形容词表「组成的」，「constitute」为动词表动作「组成、构成」

definitions.example: The country's constitution stipulates that all citizens are equal before the law.
People with a weak constitution are more likely to catch flu in winter.

constitution.etymology: con-（共同）+ stitut（建立）+ 名词后缀-ion，全体国民共同建立的根本准则，引申为宪法；后引申指人身体的先天构建基础即体质

similar_words.description: 「constitute」为动词表「组成、构成」，「constitution」为名词，核心义为「宪法、章程」

definitions.example: Strict environmental regulations have constrained the development of high-pollution traditional industries.

constrain.etymology: con-（加强语气）+ strain（拉紧、束缚），紧紧束缚住，引申为限制、约束

similar_words.description: 二者均有「约束」之义，「constrain」侧重外力、规则或客观条件的限制，「restrain」侧重主观抑制情绪、冲动或行为

definitions.meaning: 建造，构建；创立（理论、体系）
definitions.example: The professor constructed a new analytical framework to study the impact of digitalization on employment.
Many sociologists argue that masculinity is a social construct rather than an innate trait.

construct.etymology: con-（共同）+ struct（建造），共同搭建形成整体，引申为建造、构建；作名词时引申为人为搭建的抽象观念

similar_words.description: 「structure」多作名词表事物的具体「结构、构造」，「construct」多作动词表「构建、创立」，作名词时为抽象义「构想」

definitions.example: The construction of the new subway line will ease traffic congestion in the downtown area.
The ambiguous clause in the contract is open to different constructions.

construction.etymology: con-（共同）+ struct（建造）+ 名词后缀-ion，建造的过程或结果，引申为建造、建筑物；后引申为对文本、话语的构建式解读

similar_words.description: 「destruction」为名词表「破坏、毁灭」，与「construction」拼写接近且语义相反

definitions.meaning: 咨询，请教；查阅（资料、地图等）
definitions.example: Before drafting the new policy, the government will consult experts from various fields.

consult.etymology: con-（共同）+ sult（商议、商讨），共同商议讨论，引申为咨询、请教

similar_words.description: 「insult」为动词/名词表「侮辱、辱骂」，与「consult」拼写仅首字母不同，含义完全无关

definitions.example: The hospital invited a senior medical consultant to guide the treatment of rare diseases.

consultant.etymology: consult（咨询、请教）+ 名词后缀-ant（表人），提供专业咨询服务的人，即顾问

similar_words.description: 二者均表「顾问」，「consultant」多指商业、医疗等专业领域的外聘专家顾问，「counselor」侧重提供心理、教育等辅导服务的顾问

definitions.example: The rapid development of the manufacturing sector consumes a large amount of fossil fuels every year.
She was consumed by the desire to unravel the mystery behind the ancient painting.

consume.etymology: con-（完全、全部）+ sume（拿、取），全部拿走用掉，引申为消耗、消费；后引申为情感完全占据内心即使全神贯注

similar_words.description: 「assume」为动词表「假定、假设；承担」，与「consume」拼写接近，含义差异较大

definitions.example: The government has launched a campaign to encourage green consumption and reduce plastic waste.

consumption.etymology: consum（=consume 消耗、消费）+ 名词后缀-ption，消耗的行为或数量，即消耗、消费

similar_words.description: 「assumption」为名词表「假定、假设」，与「consumption」拼写接近，含义完全无关

definitions.example: The study shows that frequent contact with peers can improve children's social skills.
Building good professional contacts is beneficial for long-term career development.

contact.etymology: con-（共同）+ tact（接触），双方共同接触，引申为联系、接触；后引申为接触到的社会资源即人脉

similar_words.description: 「contract」为名词表「合同、契约」或动词表「收缩、订约」，与「contact」拼写仅中间一个字母不同，含义差异较大

definitions.example: The report contains detailed data on the correlation between social class and educational attainment.
Local authorities took immediate measures to contain the outbreak of the infectious disease.

contain.etymology: 前缀「con-」表示「共同、一起」，词根「tain」源自拉丁语「tenere」（意为「持有、握住」），共同持有某事物→引申为包含、容纳或控制事态、情绪等

similar_words.description: 两者同根（词根「tain」），「contain」表持有在内（包含），「attain」表朝目标持有（获得）

definitions.example: The chemical sample was stored in a sealed glass container to avoid external contamination.

container.etymology: 由动词「contain（容纳）」+名词后缀「-er（表器物）」派生而来，指用于容纳物品的载体

similar_words.description: 「container」是「contain」的派生名词，「contain」表动作（容纳），「container」表载体（容器）

definitions.example: Industrial waste has contaminated the river water, posing a serious threat to local aquatic ecosystems.

contaminate.etymology: 前缀「con-」表加强语气，词根「tamin」源自拉丁语「tangere」（意为「接触」），加强接触不洁之物→引申为污染、玷污

similar_words.description: 两者均表污染，「contaminate」侧重因接触异物而被玷污，「pollute」侧重造成大规模环境破坏的污染

definitions.example: The government is contemplating a series of regulatory reforms to stabilize the real estate market.

contemplate.etymology: 前缀「con-」表「共同、全面」，词根「templ」源自拉丁语「templum」（意为「神庙、观察空间」），在观察空间内全面思考→引申为深思、考虑

similar_words.description: 两者词形相近，「contemplate」表思考（动词），「contaminate」表污染（动词）

definitions.example: Contemporary sociologists often use big data to analyze social mobility patterns.
She was a contemporary of the Nobel Prize-winning physicist and collaborated with him on early research.

contemporary.etymology: 前缀「con-」表「共同」，词根「tempor」意为「时间」，后缀「-ary」表形容词或名词，同一时间维度的→引申为当代的/同代人

similar_words.description: 两者同根（词根「tempor」），「contemporary」表同一时间（当代/同代），「temporary」表短暂时间（暂时的）
