definitions.example: His open contempt for mainstream academic theories made him a polarizing figure in the field.

contempt.etymology: 前缀「con-」表加强语气，词根「tempt」源自拉丁语「temnere」（意为「轻视」），加强轻视的态度→引申为蔑视

similar_words.description: 两者词形相近，「contempt」表轻视（名词），「contend」表竞争/主张（动词）

definitions.example: Three major tech companies are contending for dominance in the global artificial intelligence market.
Critics contend that the new tax policy will exacerbate income inequality among urban residents.

contend.etymology: 前缀「con-」表「共同」，词根「tend」意为「伸展」，共同伸展（争夺资源/空间）→引申为竞争；坚持伸展（观点）→引申为声称、主张

similar_words.description: 两者词形相近，「contend」表竞争/主张（动词），「content」表内容/满意（名词/形容词）

definitions.example: The study analyzed the protein content of various plant-based meat products available on the market.
Despite the limited research funding, the team remained content with their preliminary findings.

content.etymology: 前缀「con-」表「共同」，词根「ten」意为「持有」，共同持有的事物→引申为内容/含量；内心持有的状态→引申为满意的

similar_words.description: 两者词形相近，「content」表内容/满意（名/形），「contend」表竞争/主张（动词）

definitions.example: The proposed reform of the higher education system has sparked fierce contention among educators.
The core contention of the paper is that early childhood education has long-term impacts on cognitive development.

contention.etymology: 由动词「contend（竞争/主张）」+名词后缀「-ion」派生而来，表竞争的状态（争论）或主张的内容（论点）

similar_words.description: 两者均含「cont-」前缀，「contention」表争论/论点（名词），「contest」表竞赛/质疑（动/名）

definitions.example: The national English speech contest provides a platform for students to showcase their language proficiency.
The opposition party decided to contest the election results due to alleged voting irregularities.

contest.etymology: 前缀「con-」表「共同」，词根「test」意为「见证」，共同见证比拼→引申为竞赛；对见证的结果提出异议→引申为质疑

similar_words.description: 两者均含「cont-」前缀，「contest」表竞赛/质疑（动/名），「contention」表争论/论点（名词）

definitions.meaning: （文章、事件的）上下文，语境，背景
definitions.example: To understand the author’s attitude, we must analyze the sentence within its full context.

context.etymology: con-（共同）+ text（词根：编织、文本），原指「编织在一起的内容」，引申为上下文、语境

similar_words.description: 「context」指上下文、语境；「content」指内容、容量

definitions.example: Antarctica is the coldest continent on Earth, with extreme environmental conditions.

continent.etymology: con-（共同）+ tin（词根：持有）+ -ent（名词后缀），原指「连在一起的陆地」，即大陆、洲

similar_words.description: 「continent」指大陆、洲（名词）；「continual」指连续的（形容词）

definitions.meaning: （频繁、有间断的）连续的，频繁的
definitions.example: The continual interruptions from the audience made it hard for the speaker to finish his lecture.

continual.etymology: con-（共同）+ tin（词根：持有）+ -ual（形容词后缀），表示「持续持有某种状态的」

similar_words.description: 「continual」指频繁且有间断的连续；「continuous」指无间断的连续

definitions.example: Many European countries continue to face economic challenges amid the global supply chain crisis.

continue.etymology: con-（共同）+ tinu（词根：持有）+ -e（动词后缀），指「保持原有状态」，引申为继续、持续

similar_words.description: 「continue」侧重无中断或中断后继续；「proceed」侧重按程序、步骤继续

definitions.example: The continuous rainfall in southern China has caused severe flood disasters in recent months.

continuous.etymology: con-（共同）+ tinu（词根：持有）+ -ous（形容词后缀），表示「无间断保持某种状态的」

similar_words.description: 「continuous」指无间断的连续；「continual」指频繁且有间断的连续

definitions.example: The two companies signed a formal contract to cooperate on the new energy project.
When exposed to cold air, human skin will contract to reduce heat loss.

contract.etymology: con-（共同）+ tract（词根：拉），作名词指「拉在一起的约定」即合同；作动词指「拉到一起」即收缩

similar_words.description: 「contract」指合同、收缩；「contrast」指对比、对照

definitions.example: His recent statement contradicts his earlier promise to reduce carbon emissions.

contradict.etymology: contra-（相反）+ dict（词根：说），指「说相反的话」，引申为反驳、与…矛盾

similar_words.description: 「contradict」是动词，指反驳、与…矛盾；「contrary」是形容词/名词，指相反的、相反

definitions.example: There is a clear contradiction between the government’s policy and its actual implementation.

contradiction.etymology: contra-（相反）+ dict（词根：说）+ -ion（名词后缀），指「相反说法的状态」，即矛盾、不一致

similar_words.description: 「contradiction」是名词，指矛盾；「contradict」是动词，指反驳、与…矛盾

definitions.meaning: adj. 相反的，相对的；n. 相反，反面
definitions.example: Contrary to popular belief, moderate exercise does not increase the risk of heart disease.

contrary.etymology: contra-（相反）+ -ary（形容词/名词后缀），指「相反的（事物）」

similar_words.description: 「contrary」侧重性质、方向的相反；「contrast」侧重两者的差异对比

definitions.meaning: v. 对比，对照；n. 对比，对照；差异
definitions.example: The report contrasts the economic growth rates of developed and developing countries over the past decade.

contrast.etymology: contra-（相反）+ st（词根：站），指「站在相反位置进行比较」，引申为对比、对照

similar_words.description: 「contrast」侧重找差异的对比；「compare」侧重找异同的对比

definitions.meaning: 贡献，捐献（时间、金钱、专业知识等）
definitions.example: Many senior professors contribute their valuable time and expertise to mentoring undergraduate research projects.
She regularly contributes articles on environmental policy to a leading social science journal.

contribute.etymology: con-（共同）+ 词根「tribut（给予，来自拉丁语tribuere）」+ -e（动词后缀）→ 共同给予→ 贡献、投稿

similar_words.description: 二者同根「tribut（给予）」，contribute表「贡献、投稿」，distribute表「分配、分发」
二者同根「tribut（给予）」，contribute表「贡献、投稿」，attribute表「把…归因于」

definitions.example: The Nobel Prize Committee recognized his groundbreaking contribution to the field of renewable energy technology.
The editorial board received over 200 contributions for the special issue on climate change.

contribution.etymology: con-（共同）+ 词根「tribut（给予）」+ -ion（名词后缀）→ 共同给予的事物→ 贡献物、投稿

similar_words.description: 二者同根「tribut（给予）」，contribution表「贡献、投稿」，distribution表「分配、分布」

definitions.meaning: 设法做到（尤指克服困难）
definitions.example: Despite limited funding, the small ecological research team contrived to complete the field survey in the remote mountain area within two months.
The investigators found that the two suspects had contrived to steal confidential data by hacking the company's server.

contrive.etymology: con-（加强语气）+ 词根「trive（转动，来自拉丁语trivium「三岔路」，引申为「反复盘算」）」→ 反复盘算→ 设法做到、谋划

similar_words.description: 二者拼写形近，contend表「竞争、主张」，contrive表「设法做到、谋划」

definitions.example: Global health organizations are working together to control the outbreak of the new variant of the virus.
The central bank has implemented policies to maintain control over domestic inflation.

control.etymology: 来自中古英语contreroller「核对账目者」，由拉丁语contrarotulare（对照卷轴核对）演变而来→ 引申为「控制、支配」

similar_words.description: 二者为近义词，control侧重直接、具体的控制或支配，govern侧重对国家、机构的系统治理

definitions.example: The controversial proposal to build a new dam in the protected forest area has sparked fierce debate among environmentalists and local residents.

controversial.etymology: controvers（=controversy「争论」）+ -ial（形容词后缀）→ 与争论相关的→ 有争议的

similar_words.description: 二者为近义词，controversial多修饰引起广泛公众争议的事物，contentious可修饰好争论的人或易起争端的议题

definitions.meaning: 争论，争议（尤指公开的、长期的）
definitions.example: The authenticity of the ancient manuscript has been the subject of intense controversy in academic circles for decades.

controversy.etymology: contro-（相反）+ 词根「vers（转，来自拉丁语vertere）」+ -y（名词后缀）→ 转向相反方向→ 争论、争议

similar_words.description: 二者为近义词，controversy多指长期、公开的大规模争论，dispute多指具体的、局部的争执或争端

definitions.example: The development of mobile payment has greatly improved the convenience of daily transactions for Chinese consumers.
The new residential complex is equipped with various modern conveniences such as a 24-hour gym and a community library.

convenience.etymology: con-（共同）+ 词根「veni（来，来自拉丁语venire）」+ -ence（名词后缀）→ 方便来到（使用）→ 便利、便利设施

similar_words.description: 二者为近义词，convenience侧重事物带来的方便、省事，comfort侧重身心的舒适、安逸

definitions.example: The newly opened subway line provides a convenient way for commuters to travel between the suburbs and the city center.

convenient.etymology: con-（共同）+ 词根「veni（来）」+ -ent（形容词后缀）→ 方便来到的→ 便利的、方便的

similar_words.description: 二者为近义词，convenient侧重时间、地点等整体的便利，handy侧重物品顺手或位置近便

definitions.example: It is a widely observed convention in academic writing to use formal language and avoid colloquial expressions.
The annual international convention on artificial intelligence attracts thousands of researchers and industry experts from around the world.

convention.etymology: con-（共同）+ 词根「vent（来，来自拉丁语venire）」+ -ion（名词后缀）→ 共同来到一起→ 大会；引申为群体共同遵守的「惯例、习俗」

similar_words.description: 二者为近义词，convention侧重社会、行业等群体公认的正式惯例，custom侧重个人或民族长期形成的传统习俗

definitions.meaning: 传统的，符合惯例的（常考搭配「conventional wisdom」表「传统观念」）
definitions.example: Conventional wisdom has long held that left-handed people are more creative, but recent large-scale studies have found no such correlation.
Most small local businesses still rely on conventional marketing methods such as flyers and community advertisements.

conventional.etymology: convention（惯例、习俗）+ -al（形容词后缀）→ 符合惯例的→ 传统的、常规的

similar_words.description: 二者为近义词，conventional侧重符合社会或行业惯例、常规的，traditional侧重具有历史传承的传统的

definitions.meaning: 相反地；反过来（表逻辑反向推导）
definitions.example: Conversely, species with narrow ecological niches are far more vulnerable to sudden climate fluctuations.

conversely.etymology: 前缀con-（表强调）+词根vers（转）+副词后缀-ly，字面义为「转到相反方向」，引申为逻辑层面的反向关联

similar_words.description: conversely侧重逻辑层面的反向关联，contrarily侧重行为或态度上的故意逆反

definitions.meaning: 转变；转换（尤指性质、用途或能源的本质转变）
definitions.example: The large-scale conversion of farmland to urban construction sites has triggered widespread ecological concerns.

conversion.etymology: 前缀con-（表强调）+词根vers（转）+名词后缀-ion，指从一种状态转向另一种状态的过程

similar_words.description: conversion指性质或用途的本质转变，diversion指方向的临时转移或消遣活动

definitions.meaning: （使）转变；（使）转换（性质、用途等）；改变（信仰、观点）
definitions.example: Many traditional manufacturing enterprises are converting their production lines to adopt low-carbon technologies.

convert.etymology: 前缀con-（表强调）+词根vert（转），指主动转向另一种状态或立场

similar_words.description: convert指主动转变为新状态，revert指恢复到原来的状态或立场

definitions.meaning: 传达（思想、情感、信息）；运输（货物、人员）
definitions.example: Through symbolic imagery, the poem conveys the poet's profound sense of alienation in modern society.

convey.etymology: 前缀con-（表共同）+词根vey（=way，路，运输），字面义为「一起运送」，引申为传递抽象的信息或情感

similar_words.description: convey指传递信息或运输普通货物，convoy指武装护送人员或重要物资

definitions.example: After reviewing the forensic evidence, the jury convicted the suspect of intentional homicide.
The prison implemented a rehabilitation program for long-term convicts.

convict.etymology: 前缀con-（表强调）+词根vict（=conquer，征服），字面义为「在法律上征服被告」，即证明其有罪

similar_words.description: convict为法律术语指定罪，convince为普通动词指说服使确信

definitions.example: Her unshakable conviction that scientific progress can solve global challenges drives her research.
The conviction of the former official for corruption sent a strong signal against malpractice.

conviction.etymology: 前缀con-（表强调）+词根vict（征服）+名词后缀-ion，既指「内心被信念征服的状态（坚定信念）」，也指「法律上被证明有罪的状态（定罪）」

similar_words.description: conviction指信念或定罪，convention指社会惯例或正式大会

definitions.meaning: 使确信；说服（尤指从理智上信服）
definitions.example: Empirical data has convinced most economists that inflation is closely linked to monetary policy.

convince.etymology: 前缀con-（表强调）+词根vinc（=conquer，征服）+动词后缀-e，字面义为「征服对方的疑虑」，引申为使对方信服

similar_words.description: convince指说服使理智上信服，convict为法律术语指宣判有罪

definitions.meaning: 合作；协作（尤指多方平等配合）
definitions.example: International organizations must cooperate to formulate effective policies for global climate governance.

cooperate.etymology: 前缀co-（=together，共同）+词根oper（=work，工作）+动词后缀-ate，字面义为「共同工作」，即合作

similar_words.description: cooperate侧重多方平等自愿合作，coordinate侧重某方统筹协调各方行动

definitions.meaning: 合作的；协作的；愿意配合的
definitions.example: Cooperative research between universities and industries accelerates the commercialization of technological innovations.

definitions.meaning: 合作社（尤指经济合作组织）
definitions.example: The agricultural cooperative helps small farmers access better marketing channels.

cooperative.etymology: 前缀co-（共同）+词根oper（工作）+形容词后缀-ative，表「具有共同工作性质的」

similar_words.description: cooperative侧重愿意配合的态度或松散的合作，collaborative侧重有明确分工的系统性协作（尤指学术领域）

definitions.meaning: 协调；统筹（使各方行动一致）
definitions.example: The local government is coordinating emergency response teams to deal with the flood disaster.
The satellite system can pinpoint the exact coordinates of any location on Earth.

coordinate.etymology: 前缀co-（共同）+词根ordin（=order，顺序）+动词后缀-ate，字面义为「按共同顺序排列」，引申为协调各方

similar_words.description: coordinate侧重统筹协调以达成一致（常含主导方），cooperate侧重多方平等自愿配合

definitions.example: The cop stopped the speeding car near the university campus to check the driver's license.

cop.etymology: 源自中古英语cop，原义为‘抓住’，后引申为‘抓罪犯的人’即警察

similar_words.description: cop为名词表警察，cope为动词表应对，拼写仅末尾字母不同，易混淆

definitions.example: Many undergraduate students struggle to cope with the intense pressure of postgraduate entrance examination preparation.

cope.etymology: 源自古法语coper，原义为‘竞争、对抗’，后引申为应对困难处境

similar_words.description: cope为动词表应对，cop为名词表警察，拼写仅末尾字母不同，易混淆

definitions.example: Copper is widely applied in electrical engineering due to its excellent electrical conductivity.

copper.etymology: 源自拉丁语cuprum，原指塞浦路斯（古代主要产铜地），后引申为铜这种金属

similar_words.description: copper为名词表铜/铜币，cop为名词表警察，拼写前三个字母相同，易混淆

definitions.example: Academic journals require authors to transfer part of their copyright before publishing the paper.

copyright.etymology: 合成词，由copy（复制）+ right（权利）构成，指复制、发行作品的专有合法权利

similar_words.description: copyright指文学、艺术等作品的版权，patent指发明创造的专利，二者均为知识产权范畴但适用对象不同

definitions.meaning: 细绳，粗线；（人体的）索状组织
definitions.example: The researcher used a nylon cord to fix the experimental equipment in place.

cord.etymology: 源自拉丁语chorda，原指琴弦，后引申为各类细绳或人体索状组织

similar_words.description: cord为名词表细绳，core为名词表核心，拼写仅中间字母不同，易混淆

definitions.example: The academic conference organizer extended a cordial welcome to all participating scholars.

cordial.etymology: 源自拉丁语cordialis，由词根cor（心）+ 形容词后缀-ial构成，原指‘发自内心的’，后引申为热情的

similar_words.description: 二者同根于cor（心），cordial为形容词表发自内心的热情，core为名词表事物的核心部分

definitions.example: The core of this sociological study lies in its analysis of urban-rural income gap.

core.etymology: 源自拉丁语cor（心），指事物的中心、关键部分

similar_words.description: core为名词表核心，cord为名词表细绳，拼写仅中间字母不同，易混淆

definitions.example: The agricultural research institute developed a new corn variety with high drought resistance.

corn.etymology: 源自古英语corn，原指谷物的颗粒，后引申为各类谷物或专指玉米（美式英语）

similar_words.description: corn为名词表谷物/玉米，coin为名词表硬币，拼写仅末尾字母不同，易混淆

definitions.meaning: 公司，法人团体（尤指大公司）
definitions.example: The multinational corporation has established 12 regional branches across the Asia-Pacific region.

corporation.etymology: 由词根corpor（身体、团体）+ 名词后缀-ation构成，指结成合法团体的法人组织，即公司

similar_words.description: corporation为名词表公司，cooperation为名词表合作，拼写前半部分相近，易混淆

definitions.example: Only the correct answer to each reading comprehension question will be awarded full marks in the postgraduate entrance exam.
Postgraduate tutors often correct students' academic writing to improve their logical expression.

correct.etymology: 由前缀cor-（完全、彻底）+ 词根rect（正、直）构成，形容词义指完全正确的，动词义指使变正确即纠正

similar_words.description: correct为普通用词，指纠正具体错误；rectify为正式用词，指彻底纠正偏差或弊端

definitions.example: Studies consistently correlate regular physical activity with a reduced risk of cardiovascular disease.

correlate.etymology: cor-（共同、一起）+ relat（关系）+ -ate（动词后缀），表「使事物产生共同关系」

similar_words.description: 均以cor-开头，correlate侧重「（使）事物间产生关联」，correspond侧重「事物间符合、相一致或人与人通信」

definitions.example: The experimental results correspond closely with the theoretical predictions.

correspond.etymology: cor-（共同）+ respond（回应、作答），表「共同回应、彼此呼应」

similar_words.description: 均以cor-开头，correspond侧重「符合/通信」，correlate侧重「（使）关联」

definitions.meaning: 通信（信件往来）；一致，符合
definitions.example: The scholar maintained regular correspondence with her academic peers across different universities.

correspondence.etymology: correspond（动词，符合/通信）+ -ence（名词后缀），表「通信行为或状态」

similar_words.description: 均由correspond衍生，correspondence表「通信行为/信件/一致」，correspondent表「通信者/记者」

definitions.example: The war correspondent risked his life to report from the front line.

correspondent.etymology: correspond（动词，通信）+ -ent（名词后缀，表人），表「从事通信工作的人」

similar_words.description: 同根衍生，correspondent指「人（记者/通信者）」，correspondence指「事（通信/一致）」

definitions.example: An increase in consumer demand led to a corresponding rise in commodity prices.

corresponding.etymology: correspond（动词，符合/对应）+ -ing（形容词后缀），表「具有对应性质的」

similar_words.description: 均表「相关的」，corresponding侧重「对应的、相应的」，correlative侧重「相互关联的」

definitions.meaning: 走廊，通道；（地理）走廊地带
definitions.example: The newly built high-speed railway corridor connects the coastal city to the inland industrial zone.

corridor.etymology: 源自拉丁语corridere（跑），表「供人通行的狭长通道」

similar_words.description: 拼写相近，corridor指「走廊/通道」，corrode指「腐蚀、侵蚀」

definitions.meaning: 腐蚀，侵蚀（物理或比喻义）
definitions.example: Long-term exposure to salt water can corrode metal pipes and cause leakage.

corrode.etymology: cor-（完全）+ rod（咬）+ -e（动词后缀），表「完全咬蚀掉」

similar_words.description: 拼写相近，corrode指「物理/抽象事物的腐蚀」，corrupt指「腐败的/使腐败」

definitions.meaning: adj. 腐败的，贪污的；v. 使腐败，使堕落
definitions.example: The corrupt government officials were sentenced to prison for embezzlement.

corrupt.etymology: cor-（完全）+ rupt（破、裂），表「完全破损、堕落变质」

similar_words.description: 拼写相近，corrupt侧重「人的腐败/制度的腐朽」，corrode侧重「物质/抽象事物的腐蚀」

definitions.example: Scientists are analyzing cosmic rays to explore the origin of the universe.

cosmic.etymology: cosm（宇宙）+ -ic（形容词后缀），表「与宇宙相关的」

similar_words.description: 同根，cosmic是形容词「宇宙的」，cosmos是名词「宇宙」

definitions.example: The company’s wrong decision turned out to be a costly mistake.

costly.etymology: cost（名词，成本、代价）+ -ly（形容词后缀），表「成本高的、代价大的」

similar_words.description: 均表「贵的」，costly侧重「代价高（不仅指金钱）」，expensive侧重「价格高（多指金钱）」

definitions.example: The actors in the historical play wore elaborate 18th-century costumes to recreate the period accurately.

costume.etymology: 来自拉丁词根「cost-（风俗、惯例）」，后缀-ume为名词性后缀，原指符合特定风俗的服装，后引申为戏服等

similar_words.description: custom指“风俗；习惯”，costume指“戏服；特定场合的服装”，二者形近易混

definitions.example: The small cottage by the lake had a cosy living room with a fireplace and soft woolen blankets.

cosy.etymology: 源自中古英语「cōs（舒适）」，后缀-y为形容词性后缀，无明显复杂词根拆分

similar_words.description: cozy为美式拼写，cosy为英式拼写，二者词义完全相同，均指“舒适温暖的”

definitions.meaning: 村舍；小屋（尤指乡村的小型住宅）
definitions.example: Many British tourists prefer to rent a quiet cottage in the countryside rather than stay in a busy city hotel.

cottage.etymology: 来自古英语词根「cott-（小棚屋）」，后缀-age为名词性后缀（表场所），指乡村的小型住宅

similar_words.description: cabin指“船舱；小木屋”，多为木质且简陋；cottage指“乡村村舍”，多为温馨的小型住宅

definitions.example: Cotton is one of the most important cash crops in many developing countries, supporting millions of small-scale farmers.

cotton.etymology: 源自阿拉伯语「qutn（棉花）」，为外来借词，无英语内部词根拆分

similar_words.description: button指“纽扣”，cotton指“棉花；棉布”，二者形近易混

definitions.example: The psychiatrist’s office had a soft leather couch where patients could sit or lie during sessions.
The government’s statement was carefully couched in ambiguous language to avoid direct commitment.

couch.etymology: 源自古法语「couche（躺卧场所）」，名词义为“长沙发”；动词义由“将话语置于特定框架中”引申为“表达、措辞”

similar_words.description: sofa指“普通客厅沙发”，多为多人座；couch作名词指“可躺卧的长沙发/卧榻”，还可作动词表“措辞”

definitions.example: Patients with respiratory infections may cough frequently and should avoid close contact with others.
He had a persistent cough that lasted for three weeks after catching a cold.

cough.etymology: 为拟声词，模拟咳嗽时的短促气流声，无词根词缀拆分

similar_words.description: choke指“窒息；呛住”，因异物堵塞呼吸道导致；cough指“咳嗽”，为呼吸道的反射性动作

definitions.example: The local council has approved a new plan to build more affordable housing in the downtown area.

council.etymology: 来自拉丁词根「consil-（商议、咨询）」，后缀-cil为名词性后缀，指由多人组成的商议机构

similar_words.description: counsel作动词指“劝告”，作名词指“忠告；法律顾问”；council指“委员会；理事会”，为机构名词，二者形近易混，是考研高频易混词

definitions.meaning: 劝告；建议（尤指专业领域）
definitions.example: School counselors often counsel students on career choices and mental health issues.
The defendant’s counsel argued that the evidence against his client was insufficient.

counsel.etymology: 与council同源，来自拉丁词根「consil-（商议、咨询）」，作名词指“商议的内容（忠告）”或“提供商议的人（法律顾问）”，作动词指“提供商议（劝告）”

similar_words.description: council指“委员会；理事会”，为机构名词；counsel作动词指“劝告”，作名词指“忠告；法律顾问”，二者形近易混，是考研高频易混词

definitions.example: Researchers counted the number of species present in each sample to assess biodiversity levels.

definitions.meaning: 有重要性；有价值（常与for搭配）
definitions.example: In a job interview, your communication skills count for more than just your academic qualifications.

count.etymology: 来自拉丁词根「comput-（计算）」，与compute同源，原指“计算”，后引申为“有价值”（因“被计入统计范畴”即“有价值”）

similar_words.description: account指“账户；解释；认为”，count指“数；有价值”，二者形近易混，且均有固定搭配考点

definitions.example: The customer asked the sales clerk behind the counter to explain the features of the new smartphone.

definitions.meaning: 相反地（常与to搭配，表与…相悖）
definitions.example: This policy runs counter to the government’s stated goal of reducing carbon emissions.

counter.etymology: 来自拉丁前缀「contra-（相反）」，后缀-er为名词/副词后缀，原指“对面的人/物”，引申为柜台（对面的服务台）、相反地（方向/理念相悖）

similar_words.description: count指“数；有价值”，counter指“柜台；相反地；反驳”，二者形近易混

definitions.example: Each research team’s counterpart in the Sino-US collaborative project shared confidential experimental data.

counterpart.etymology: 前缀counter-（表示「相对、相反」）+ 词根part（部分），构成表示与另一方相对应的实体

similar_words.description: 「counter」多作名词表柜台、动词表反驳，「counterpart」表相对应的人或物

definitions.example: Many rural counties in the United States have faced population decline due to the lack of high-paying jobs.

county.etymology: 词根count-（原指伯爵领地）+ 后缀-y（表地方），后引申为行政区划的郡或县

similar_words.description: 「country」表国家、乡村，「county」表英美行政区划中的郡或县

definitions.example: A couple of peer-reviewed studies have confirmed the link between air pollution and cardiovascular diseases.
Rising energy costs, coupled with supply chain bottlenecks, have pushed up commodity prices worldwide.

couple.etymology: 源自拉丁语copula（连接、纽带），原指连接在一起的两个事物

similar_words.description: 「pair」指严格配对的两个物品，「couple」可指夫妇或非正式的几个事物

definitions.example: Many e-commerce platforms offer digital coupons to attract consumers during major shopping festivals.

coupon.etymology: 词根cou-（=cop-，连接）+ 后缀-on（表小物件），原指可兑换的凭证

similar_words.description: 「couple」表一对、夫妇，「coupon」表优惠券、配给券等凭证

definitions.example: The whistleblower demonstrated remarkable courage by exposing the pharmaceutical company’s fraudulent clinical trials.

courage.etymology: 词根cour-（=cord-，心）+ 后缀-age（表抽象名词），指发自内心的胆识

similar_words.description: 「encourage」是动词表鼓励，「courage」是名词表勇气、胆量

definitions.example: The course of global climate change has been significantly accelerated by human activities.
The cargo ship altered its course to avoid the severe storm in the Atlantic Ocean.

course.etymology: 词根cour-（跑）+ 后缀-se（表路线、过程），原指跑的路径，后引申为多义

similar_words.description: 「coarse」是形容词表粗糙的、粗俗的，「course」是名词表课程、过程等

definitions.example: The Supreme Court ruled that the state’s new voting law violated the federal constitution.
The presidential candidate tried to court rural voters by promising agricultural subsidies.

court.etymology: 词根cour-（封闭的庭院）+ 后缀-t（表场所），原指贵族庭院，后引申为多义

similar_words.description: 「count」作动词表计数、名词表伯爵，「court」表法院、球场等

definitions.example: The international conference organizers provided free translation services out of courtesy to foreign delegates.

courtesy.etymology: 词根court（宫廷）+ 后缀-esy（表抽象名词），原指宫廷礼仪，后引申为礼貌

similar_words.description: 「courage」表勇气，「courtesy」表礼貌、谦恭或承蒙好意

definitions.example: The traditional Siheyuan, a typical Chinese courtyard residence, symbolizes the harmony between man and nature.

courtyard.etymology: 合成词，由court（庭院）和yard（院子）构成，指建筑物围合的封闭庭院

similar_words.description: 「court」可表法院、球场，「courtyard」专指建筑物围合的庭院、院子

definitions.meaning: 堂兄弟姐妹，表兄弟姐妹
definitions.example: My cousin, a postdoctoral researcher in computational biology, published a paper in a top-tier scientific journal.

cousin.etymology: 源自拉丁语consobrinus（母亲姐妹的儿子，即表亲），后泛指堂/表兄弟姐妹

similar_words.description: 「cozen」是动词表欺骗、哄骗，「cousin」是名词表堂/表兄弟姐妹

definitions.example: News coverage of the Arctic ice melting has drawn global attention to climate crisis.

coverage.etymology: 由动词「cover（覆盖）」+名词后缀「-age」构成，表「覆盖的行为或结果」，引申为「新闻报道、承保范围」等义

similar_words.description: 「coverage」侧重「覆盖范围、新闻报道」，「covering」侧重「覆盖物、遮蔽物」，二者为同根形近词易混

definitions.example: The organic dairy farm raises over 80 cows to produce premium milk.
The dictator tried to cow political opponents into silence with violent threats.

cow.etymology: 源自古日耳曼语，原指「母牛」；动词义为后起引申义，由「用牛驱赶威慑」衍生为「恐吓」

similar_words.description: 「cow」作动词表「恐吓他人」，「cower」表「因恐惧蜷缩、畏缩」，二者形近易混

definitions.example: He was condemned as a coward for abandoning his teammates during the emergency drill.

coward.etymology: 由动词「cow（恐吓）」+贬义名词后缀「-ard」构成，指「被恐吓住的人→懦夫」

similar_words.description: 「coward」指具体的胆小之人，「craven」可作形容词表「胆小的」或名词表「懦夫」，语义相近

definitions.example: Many coastal resorts offer fresh crab dishes as their culinary highlight.
She always crabs about the noisy neighborhood but never moves out.

crab.etymology: 源自古英语「crabba」，原指「螃蟹」；动词义由「螃蟹横行的暴躁习性」引申为「抱怨、发牢骚」

similar_words.description: 「crab」指螃蟹，「lobster」指龙虾，均为甲壳类海鲜，易被混淆

definitions.example: Cyber experts are working to crack the encrypted code of the ransomware.
A tiny crack in the spacecraft's window triggered an emergency landing.

crack.etymology: 源自古英语「cracian」，为拟声词，模仿物体破裂的声音，引申为「破裂、破解」等义

similar_words.description: 「crack」表「破裂、破解」，「crash」表「碰撞、坠毁、崩溃」，二者形近易混

definitions.meaning: 摇篮；（文明、思想的）发源地
definitions.example: The Yellow River Basin is widely recognized as the cradle of Chinese civilization.

definitions.meaning: 轻轻抱着；把…置于摇篮中
definitions.example: The nurse cradled the premature baby gently in her arms.

cradle.etymology: 源自古英语「cradol」，原指「婴儿摇篮」；名词义引申为「发源地」，动词义由「放于摇篮中」引申为「轻轻抱着」

similar_words.description: 「cradle」侧重指「文明、文化等的发源地」，「source」侧重指「河流、信息等的来源」，语义相近但适用范围不同

definitions.example: Traditional ceramic craft of Jingdezhen has been inscribed on the UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage List.
The lobbyist used political craft to influence the passage of the bill.

craft.etymology: 源自古英语「cræft」，原指「力量、技能」，引申为「工艺、手艺」，后衍生出「诡计」的贬义引申义

similar_words.description: 「craft」侧重「实用的工艺、手艺」，「art」侧重「审美性的艺术创作」，语义相近

definitions.example: A 50-ton crane was deployed to lift the collapsed bridge debris.
She craned her neck to catch a glimpse of the celebrity at the airport.

crane.etymology: 源自古英语「cran」，原指「鹤」；因起重机的长臂形似鹤的长颈，故引申为「起重机」，动词义由「鹤伸脖子」引申而来

similar_words.description: 「crane」指鹤，「heron」指苍鹭，均为长颈涉禽，易被混淆

definitions.meaning: 碰撞；坠毁；（系统、股市等）崩溃
definitions.example: The housing market crashed in 2008, triggering a global financial crisis.
The plane crash in the mountains killed all 150 passengers on board.

crash.etymology: 源自拟声词，模仿物体剧烈碰撞的声音，引申为「碰撞、坠毁、系统崩溃」等义

similar_words.description: 「crash」表「碰撞、坠毁、崩溃」，「crack」表「破裂、破解」，二者形近易混

definitions.meaning: 爬行；（车辆、时间等）缓慢行进
definitions.example: Traffic crawled along the highway for three hours due to the multi-vehicle accident.
He always crawls to his manager in the hope of getting a promotion.

crawl.etymology: 源自古英语「crawlian」，原指「四肢着地爬行」，引申为「缓慢行进」，后衍生出「巴结奉承」的贬义引申义

similar_words.description: 「crawl」侧重「四肢着地的爬行、缓慢行进」，「creep」侧重「蹑手蹑脚地移动、偷偷行进」，语义相近

definitions.example: Many college students are crazy about participating in environmental protection campaigns organized by non-profit organizations.

crazy.etymology: 源自中古英语crasig，原指「破碎的、精神失常的」，后引申为「疯狂的；狂热的」

similar_words.description: crazy为形容词表「疯狂的；狂热的」，craze为名词表「狂热；风尚」

definitions.example: The cream of the country's young scientists gathered at the international academic forum to share research findings.

cream.etymology: 源自古法语cresme，原指「宗教仪式用的圣油」，后引申为「奶油；精英、精华」

similar_words.description: cream为名词表「奶油；精华」，scream为动词/名词表「尖叫」

definitions.example: Digital technology has created new opportunities for rural residents to sell agricultural products online.

create.etymology: 源自拉丁语creare（创造、产生），由词根cre-（创造）加动词后缀-ate构成

similar_words.description: create强调从无到有创造抽象或具体事物，invent强调发明新的科技产品或方法

definitions.example: Creative problem-solving skills are essential for postgraduate students to conduct independent research.

creative.etymology: 由词根cre-（创造）加形容词后缀-ative（表「具有…性质的」）构成，表「具有创造能力的」

similar_words.description: creative为形容词表「有创造力的」，create为动词表「创造；创建」

definitions.meaning: 生物；动物；（具有某特点的）人
definitions.example: Marine creatures are facing severe threats from ocean plastic pollution and climate change.

creature.etymology: 由词根cre-（创造）加名词后缀-ature（表「被创造的事物」）构成，原指「上帝创造的万物」，后引申为「生物；（某类）人」

similar_words.description: creature为名词表「生物；（某类）人」，feature为名词表「特征；特写」

definitions.meaning: （罕见，多以复数出现）资格证明；资质证明
definitions.example: A single credential is rarely sufficient to verify a scholar's academic competence in interdisciplinary research.

credential.etymology: 由词根cred-（相信）加名词后缀-ential（表「与…相关的事物」）构成，原指「令人信服的证明」

similar_words.description: credential为单数名词，表「单个资质证明」；credentials为复数名词，表「全套资质证书」

definitions.meaning: 资格证书；资质证明；（身份）证件
definitions.example: Candidates for the postgraduate program must submit their academic credentials and recommendation letters before the deadline.

credentials.etymology: credential的复数形式，由词根cred-（相信）加名词后缀-ential构成，表「全套可信的证明文件」

similar_words.description: credentials指证明身份或资质的全套文件，certificate指单一的官方证明文件（如毕业证书）

definitions.example: The wildlife photographer had to creep quietly to avoid disturbing the nesting birds in the wetland.
Consumer prices in the region have been creeping up for six consecutive months, affecting low-income households.

creep.etymology: 源自古英语creopan，原指「四肢贴地爬行」，后引申为「蹑手蹑脚地走；缓慢上升」

similar_words.description: creep强调缓慢、偷偷地移动，crawl强调四肢贴地爬行（如昆虫、婴儿）

definitions.meaning: （船、飞机等的）全体工作人员；（协作的）一组人
definitions.example: The medical rescue crew arrived at the disaster site within 15 minutes after receiving the emergency call.

crew.etymology: 源自古法语creue（补充、增加），原指「补充的船员」，后引申为「（交通工具的）全体工作人员；一组人」

similar_words.description: crew为名词表「全体工作人员；一组人」，crow为名词表「乌鸦」

definitions.example: Cricket is one of the most popular sports in Commonwealth countries such as the United Kingdom and Australia.

cricket.etymology: 源自古法语criquet，原指「蟋蟀的鸣叫声」，后因板球击球声类似蟋蟀叫，引申为「板球运动」

similar_words.description: cricket为名词表「蟋蟀；板球运动」，ticket为名词表「票；入场券」

definitions.example: Cyber crime has become a growing threat to social stability in many developed countries.

crime.etymology: 源自拉丁语crimen，表「指控、罪行」，无常见可分解的派生词缀

similar_words.description: 「crime」指法律层面界定的罪行，「sin」指宗教或道德层面的罪孽

definitions.example: The judge delivered a severe sentence to the criminal suspect who committed multiple frauds.
The police have arrested three criminals involved in the bank robbery.

criminal.etymology: 由名词「crime」加形容词后缀「-al」派生而来，「-al」表「与…相关的」

similar_words.description: 「criminal」可指涉嫌或已定罪的罪犯，「convict」特指经法院判决有罪的囚犯

definitions.meaning: 严重削弱；使（系统、组织等）瘫痪
definitions.example: The sudden supply chain disruption crippled the company’s global production network.
The local community has launched a program to support cripples in accessing public facilities.

cripple.etymology: 源自古英语crypel，表「跛足的」，无复杂词缀分解

similar_words.description: 「cripple」作动词侧重「使系统/组织瘫痪、严重削弱」，「disable」侧重「使身体丧失功能」

definitions.example: The 2008 global financial crisis exposed deep flaws in the international monetary system.

crisis.etymology: 源自希腊语krisis，表「判断、转折点」，无常见派生词缀

similar_words.description: 「crisis」指长期积累的、影响重大的危机，「emergency」指突发的、需立即处理的紧急情况

definitions.example: The crisp autumn breeze carried the scent of fallen maple leaves across the campus.
The manager gave a crisp response to the employees’ request for a pay raise.

crisp.etymology: 源自拉丁语crispus，表「卷曲的」，引申为「脆的、利落的」

similar_words.description: 「crisp」侧重食物等脆而有嚼劲、口感清爽，「brittle」侧重物体硬而易碎、无弹性

definitions.meaning: （评判的）标准；准则（复数形式为criteria）
definitions.example: Academic performance is not the only criterion for evaluating a student’s overall quality.

criterion.etymology: 源自希腊语kriterion，由词根「krinein（判断）」派生，表「判断的工具」

similar_words.description: 「criterion」侧重正式的、用于专业评判的抽象标准（常作复数criteria），「standard」侧重通用的、具体的水平或规范

definitions.meaning: （文学、艺术等的）评论家；批评家
definitions.example: The film critic praised the director’s innovative use of long takes in the new movie.

critic.etymology: 源自希腊语kritikos，由词根「krinein（判断）」派生，表「善于判断的人」

similar_words.description: 「critic」指专业的、有权威的评论家（如文学、电影领域），「reviewer」指普通的评论者（如书评人）

definitions.example: Professors encourage students to take a critical attitude towards textbook knowledge rather than accept it blindly.
The next 48 hours will be critical for the patient’s recovery after the major surgery.

critical.etymology: 由名词「critic」加形容词后缀「-al」派生而来，「-al」表「与…相关的」

similar_words.description: 「critical」可表「批评的」或「关键的」，「crucial」仅表「至关重要的」，语气更强

definitions.example: The new policy has drawn widespread criticism from environmental groups for its loose emission regulations.

criticism.etymology: 由名词「critic」加名词后缀「-ism」派生而来，「-ism」表「行为、学说」

similar_words.description: 「criticism」侧重带有评判性（常含否定）的批评或专业评论，「comment」侧重一般性的意见或评述

definitions.example: Some economists criticize the government’s tax cut policy for widening the wealth gap.

criticize.etymology: 由名词「critic」加动词后缀「-ize」派生而来，「-ize」表「使…化、从事…」

similar_words.description: 「criticize」侧重针对行为、观点的理性批评，「blame」侧重针对过错的指责、归咎

definitions.meaning: 庄稼，作物；（同时出现的）一批（人/事物）
definitions.example: Drought has severely damaged the crop of winter wheat in northern regions, threatening local food security.

definitions.meaning: （问题、麻烦）突然出现（常与up连用）；裁剪（图像）
definitions.example: Unexpected technical issues cropped up during the online lecture, delaying the presentation by 15 minutes.

crop.etymology: 无复杂词缀，源自古英语cropp，原指庄稼穗，后语义扩展为作物、批量事物等

similar_words.description: crop多指生长中或已收割的「作物、庄稼」，也可指一批事物；harvest侧重「收获（行为或成果）」

definitions.example: The newly built bridge allows vehicles to cross the river without waiting for ferries, improving regional transportation efficiency.

definitions.meaning: 生气的，恼怒的（常与with连用）
definitions.example: The manager was cross with the team for missing the crucial project deadline.

cross.etymology: 来自拉丁语crux（十字架），后通过古法语进入英语，语义从「十字架」扩展为「交叉、生气」（因十字架象征惩罚，引出生气义）

similar_words.description: cross可作动词（交叉、穿越）、形容词（生气的）或名词（十字架）；across是介词/副词，表「穿过、在对面」，不能作动词

definitions.example: Ecologists have observed that the crow population in urban areas has increased due to easy access to food waste.

definitions.meaning: （因胜利、成功）得意洋洋，自鸣得意
definitions.example: He crowed about his promotion at the dinner party, making his colleagues feel uncomfortable.

crow.etymology: 源自古英语crāwe，拟声词（模拟乌鸦叫声），后扩展出雄鸡啼叫、人得意发声的语义

similar_words.description: crow拼写为c-r-o-w，指「乌鸦」或「得意洋洋」；cow拼写为c-o-w，指「奶牛」或作动词表「恐吓」

definitions.example: Large crowds gathered in the city square to celebrate the 70th anniversary of the country's founding.
Shoppers crowded into the mall during the annual discount season, leading to temporary congestion.

crowd.etymology: 源自古英语crūdan，原指「推挤」，后扩展为「人群、挤满」的语义

similar_words.description: crowd侧重「人数众多的普通人群」；throng语气更正式，指「密集的、有目的移动的人群」

definitions.example: The royal crown, adorned with precious gems, is displayed in the national museum as a symbol of historical heritage.
Her latest novel, which won the Pulitzer Prize, crowned her 30-year writing career.

crown.etymology: 来自拉丁语corona（花环、王冠），通过古法语进入英语，语义从「王冠」扩展为「王权、圆满完成」

similar_words.description: crown是corona的英语变体，指「王冠、王位」；corona多指天文/医学领域的「日冕、冠部」

definitions.example: The crucial experiment conducted by the research team provided definitive evidence for the new biological theory.

crucial.etymology: 来自拉丁语crux（十字架），原指「十字路口（需做关键抉择的地方）」，后演变为「至关重要的」语义

similar_words.description: crucial侧重「对结果起决定性作用的」；critical侧重「危急的、批评的」，也可表「关键的」但语气更侧重紧迫感

definitions.meaning: 天然的，未加工的；粗略的，粗糙的
definitions.example: The country's exports mainly consist of crude oil and mineral resources, which account for 40% of its total foreign trade revenue.
The speaker's crude remarks offended many audience members, leading to a walkout during the conference.

crude.etymology: 来自拉丁语crudus（生的、未熟的），后扩展为「未加工的、粗略的、粗俗的」语义

similar_words.description: crude侧重「未加工的（多为工业原料）、粗略的」；raw侧重「生的（食物）、未加工的（天然材料）、原始状态的」

definitions.meaning: 残忍的，残酷的；（天气、命运等）严酷的
definitions.example: The cruel reality of unemployment has forced many young people to leave their hometowns for better job opportunities.

cruel.etymology: 来自拉丁语crudelis（残忍的），与crude（生的）同根，原指「像生肉一样冷酷无情的」

similar_words.description: cruel侧重「行为或态度上的残忍、不近人情」；brutal语气更强，指「暴力的、野蛮的极端残忍」

definitions.meaning: 乘船游览；巡航；（汽车）平稳行驶
definitions.example: The luxury liner cruised along the Mediterranean coast, offering passengers a view of ancient coastal cities.

definitions.meaning: （为猎艳、找生意等）缓慢徘徊，游荡
definitions.example: Suspicious vehicles were reported to be cruising the residential area after midnight, prompting police patrols.

cruise.etymology: 来自荷兰语kruisen（交叉航行），原指船舶的交叉巡航，后扩展为「游览、徘徊」的语义

similar_words.description: cruise作动词/名词表「巡航、游览」；cruiser是名词，指「巡洋舰、游艇、巡逻车」

definitions.meaning: 压碎，碾碎；（军事、精神上）镇压，击溃
definitions.example: The earthquake crushed hundreds of buildings in the rural area, leaving thousands of people homeless.
Many teenage girls have a crush on the popular actor, buying all his merchandise and attending his fan meetings.

crush.etymology: 来自古法语croissir（压碎），后扩展为「镇压、迷恋」（迷恋义源于「心被压碎般的悸动」）

similar_words.description: crush指「压碎、镇压、迷恋」；crash指「碰撞、坠毁、崩溃（系统/经济）」

definitions.example: Geologists study the composition and movement of the Earth's crust to predict seismic activities.

crust.etymology: 源自拉丁语「crusta」（硬壳），词根「crust-」表“硬壳”，后引申为“地壳”等义

similar_words.description: 「crust」指硬壳、地壳，「rust」指铁锈、生锈

definitions.example: The lab technician observed the formation of tiny salt crystals under the microscope.

crystal.etymology: 源自古希腊语「krystallos」（原指冰），后引申为“水晶、晶体”，词根「crystall-」表水晶相关含义

similar_words.description: 「crystal」为名词指晶体、水晶，「crystalline」为形容词指水晶般的、结晶的

definitions.example: The math problem required students to calculate the volume of a cube with a side length of 5 centimeters.

cube.etymology: 源自拉丁语「cubus」（立方体），词根「cub-」表“躺、平放”，因立方体多平放而得名

similar_words.description: 「cube」指立方体、立方数，「cub」指幼兽（如熊崽）

definitions.example: Nutritionists often recommend adding fresh cucumber to salads for its high water content and low calories.

cucumber.etymology: 源自拉丁语「cucumis」，经古法语「cocombre」演变而来，指葫芦科的黄瓜植物

similar_words.description: 「cucumber」指黄瓜，「cumbersome」指笨重的、难处理的

definitions.example: The speaker waited for a cue from the host before starting his keynote address at the academic conference.

cue.etymology: 源自拉丁语「quando」（何时），原指剧本中提示演员出场的“何时”标记，后引申为“暗示、提示”

similar_words.description: 「cue」多指表演或人际沟通中的提示、暗示，「clue」多指解谜或调查中的线索

definitions.example: Universities are supposed to cultivate students' critical thinking and innovative abilities rather than just imparting knowledge.

cultivate.etymology: 词根「cult-」表“耕种、培养”（源自拉丁语动词「colere」的过去分词「cultus」），后缀「-ate」为动词后缀，由“耕种”引申为“培养抽象能力”

similar_words.description: 「cultivate」为动词指培养、耕作，「culture」为名词指文化、培养

definitions.meaning: 文化；（动植物、细胞的）培养
definitions.example: The study of ancient Greek culture helps scholars understand the origin of Western political and philosophical thoughts.

culture.etymology: 词根「cult-」表“耕种、培养”，后缀「-ure」为名词后缀，由“耕种的成果”引申为“文化”，又引申为“生物培养”

similar_words.description: 「culture」侧重特定群体的习俗、价值观等文化现象，「civilization」侧重人类文明发展的阶段或整体成果

definitions.example: The cunning fox managed to steal the farmer's chicken without being noticed in the folk tale, which is often used to teach moral lessons.

cunning.etymology: 源自古英语「cunnan」（知道），原指“有技巧的”，后贬义引申为“用计谋欺骗的狡猾”

similar_words.description: 「cunning」侧重用精心设计的计谋欺骗的狡猾，「sly」侧重偷偷摸摸的诡诈

definitions.example: She stored the clean plates and bowls in the kitchen cupboard to keep them dust-free.

cupboard.etymology: 复合词，由「cup（杯子）」+「board（木板）」构成，原指放杯子的木板，后引申为“存放餐具的橱柜”

similar_words.description: 「cupboard」侧重家庭存放餐具的橱柜，「cabinet」侧重办公或政府用的橱柜、内阁

definitions.example: The government implemented a series of policies to curb the rapid rise in housing prices in major cities.

curb.etymology: 源自古法语「courbe」（弯曲），原指马嚼子的弯曲部分（用于控制马匹），后引申为“控制、约束”的抽象含义

similar_words.description: 「curb」表控制、约束，「curve」表曲线、弯曲

definitions.example: Many researchers are exploring effective measures to cure the long-term unemployment crisis in rural areas.
The only viable cure for global plastic pollution is cross-border coordinated action.

cure.etymology: 源自拉丁语cura，原义为「关怀、照料」，后引申为通过照料实现治愈或解决的含义

similar_words.description: 「cue」为形近词，意为「暗示；提示」，与「cure」拼写仅差一个字母，易混淆

definitions.example: Curiosity drives scholars to continuously explore the unknown mechanisms of human cognition.

curiosity.etymology: cur-（关心、留意）+ -iosity（名词后缀，表状态），指「对未知事物主动留意的状态」

similar_words.description: 「curious」为同根形容词，是「curiosity」的形容词形式，用于描述人或事物的好奇属性

definitions.example: The curious graduate student asked the supervisor a series of in-depth questions about neural networks.
There was a curious pause in the courtroom after the witness finished testifying.

curious.etymology: cur-（关心、留意）+ -ious（形容词后缀），原指「留意他人动向的」，后引申为好奇的、奇特的含义

similar_words.description: 「strange」为近义词，「curious」侧重因反常而引发好奇的奇特，「strange」侧重因陌生而产生的怪异感

definitions.example: The cat curled up on the soft cushion next to the fireplace to keep warm.
She twisted a lock of her curl around her finger while thinking about the problem.

curl.etymology: 源自古荷兰语krul，原指「卷曲的物体」，后引申为动词「卷曲」的含义

similar_words.description: 「curve」为近义词，「curl」指卷成螺旋或圈状的弯曲，「curve」指形成平滑的弧形弯曲

definitions.example: The central bank has implemented policies to stabilize the country's currency exchange rate.
This new interpretation of the classic text has gained wide currency in academic circles.

currency.etymology: curr-（跑、流动）+ -ency（名词后缀），原指「流动的事物」，后引申为作为流动交换媒介的「货币」，以及观点等的「流行传播」

similar_words.description: 「current」为同根词，作形容词表「当前的」，作名词表「水流；思潮」，与「currency」共享「curr-」（流动）词根

definitions.example: Current data indicates that the proportion of renewable energy in power generation has increased by 5%.
A current of environmental protection has swept through the global youth community.

current.etymology: curr-（跑、流动）+ -ent（形容词/名词后缀），表「流动的（状态）」或「流动的事物」

similar_words.description: 「currency」为同根名词，侧重「流动的媒介（货币）」或「流动的传播（流行）」，与「current」核心语义（流动）同源

definitions.example: The university is revising its undergraduate curriculum to integrate more practical training modules.

curriculum.etymology: 源自拉丁语curriculum，原指「赛跑的跑道」，后引申为学生学习的「进程」即课程体系

similar_words.description: 「course」为近义词，「curriculum」指学校整体的课程设置体系，「course」指单门具体课程

definitions.example: In ancient folk tales, villagers would curse those who destroyed the sacred forest.
Illiteracy has long been regarded as a curse that hinders social development.

curse.etymology: 源自古英语curs，原指「通过言语施加的厄运」，后引申为「祸根」的含义

similar_words.description: 「damn」为近义词，均表「诅咒」，「curse」侧重施加厄运的言语，「damn」多含强烈谴责或愤怒语气

definitions.example: The director pulled back the curtain to reveal the elaborate set design for the new play.
The company tried to curtain the true cause of the product recall from the public.

curtain.etymology: 源自拉丁语cortina，原指「帐篷的围布」，后引申为窗帘、帷幕等遮蔽物

similar_words.description: 「screen」为近义词，「curtain」多指布制的遮蔽物，「screen」多指网状、电子或其他材质的遮蔽/显示装置

definitions.example: The research report shows a downward curve in the incidence of infectious diseases over the past five years.
The mountain road curves gently to the right as it approaches the summit.

curve.etymology: 源自拉丁语curvus，原指「弯曲的」，后引申为名词「曲线」和动词「弯曲」的含义

similar_words.description: 「curl」为近义词，「curve」指形成平滑的弧形弯曲，「curl」指卷成螺旋或圈状的弯曲

definitions.meaning: 垫子；（经济、社会等的）缓冲物
definitions.example: Social security funds often act as a cushion against sudden economic downturns.
The policy was designed to cushion the impact of inflation on low-income families.

cushion.etymology: 源自拉丁语cuscus（原指填充垫的原料），后缀-ion构成具体/抽象名词，表「具有某种性质的事物」

similar_words.description: 两者均含缓冲之义，cushion可指实体垫子或抽象的社会/经济缓冲，buffer更侧重物理或技术层面的缓冲装置或措施

definitions.example: It is a traditional custom for Chinese families to eat dumplings during the Spring Festival.
Small local shops rely heavily on regular custom to stay in business.

custom.etymology: 源自拉丁语consuetudo（习惯），进入中古英语后演变为表群体传统行为的名词

similar_words.description: custom侧重群体长期形成的传统习俗，habit侧重个人长期养成的行为习惯

definitions.example: It is customary to shake hands when meeting someone for the first time in many countries.

customary.etymology: 由名词custom（习俗）加形容词后缀-ary（表「与…相关的」）构成，意为「符合习俗的」

similar_words.description: customary侧重符合社会或群体惯例的，habitual侧重个人习惯性的行为

definitions.meaning: 顾客，主顾；（特定服务的）客户
definitions.example: E-commerce platforms use data analytics to tailor recommendations for individual customers.

customer.etymology: 由名词custom（惠顾）加名词后缀-er（表「实施某动作的人」）构成，指光顾商家的人

similar_words.description: customer侧重普通消费场景的顾客，client侧重专业服务（如法律、金融）的长期客户

definitions.example: Protecting personal data in cyberspace has become a top priority for global regulators.

cyberspace.etymology: 由前缀cyber-（表「计算机、网络相关的」）加名词space（空间）构成，指虚拟的网络环境

similar_words.description: cyberspace指整体的虚拟网络空间，network指具体的计算机网络或社交网络

definitions.example: The business cycle, which includes expansion and recession, is a core concept in macroeconomics.
Many urban residents cycle to work to reduce carbon emissions and save time.

cycle.etymology: 源自希腊语kyklos（圆圈，循环），后引申为循环的过程或圆形交通工具

similar_words.description: cycle侧重循环的过程或周期，circle侧重圆形的实体或抽象范围

definitions.example: In the mechanical engineering test, students calculated the pressure inside a car engine’s cylinder.

cylinder.etymology: 源自希腊语kulindros（滚筒，圆柱体），后缀-er表具体事物，属于几何/工程术语

similar_words.description: 两者均为几何形体，cylinder指圆柱体，sphere指球体

definitions.example: Developing a consistent daily study schedule is crucial for postgraduate entrance exam preparation.

daily.etymology: 由名词day（天）加后缀-ly（表「每…的/地」）构成，可作形容词、副词或名词

similar_words.description: daily可作多种词性，everyday仅作形容词，侧重「日常的、普通的」

definitions.meaning: 乳制品；乳品厂；乳制品的
definitions.example: The government has tightened regulations on dairy product quality to ensure public food safety.

dairy.etymology: 源自中古英语deye（负责挤奶的女佣人），后缀-ery表「场所或产品」，后引申为乳制品相关的事物

similar_words.description: 拼写仅差一个字母，dairy指乳制品或乳品厂，daily指每日的、日常的

definitions.example: The construction of large dams can provide hydroelectric power but may also disrupt local ecosystems.
The breeder selected a strong and healthy dam to produce high-quality offspring.

dam.etymology: 源自古英语dæm（拦截水流的建筑），后引申为家畜中的母兽

similar_words.description: 拼写仅差一个字母，dam指水坝或母兽，damn指诅咒、严厉谴责

definitions.example: Severe climate damage has forced many Arctic species to migrate to cooler habitats.

damage.etymology: 源自拉丁语「damnum」（损失），中古英语时期演变为该词，兼具名词、动词词性

similar_words.description: 均表损害，damage侧重可修复的有形或无形损失，harm侧重对健康、权益的直接伤害

definitions.example: Critics damned the new censorship policy as a severe threat to press freedom.

damn.etymology: 源自拉丁语「damnum」（损失），与「damage」同根，原指“造成损失”，后演变为谴责、诅咒义

similar_words.description: 均表谴责，damn多带强烈情绪色彩，condemn为正式官方用语，侧重法律或舆论层面的谴责

definitions.example: The damp walls of the old library were covered with faint mildew after weeks of rain.

damp.etymology: 源自古英语「damp」（蒸汽、湿气），现多作形容词表潮湿状态

similar_words.description: 「damp」为形容词/名词表潮湿（的），「dampen」为其动词形式，表‘使潮湿；使沮丧’

definitions.example: Many endangered species are in imminent danger of extinction due to habitat destruction.

danger.etymology: 源自拉丁语「dominus」（主人），原指“主人的权力范围（含风险）”，后演变为通用的危险义

similar_words.description: 均表危险，danger为通用词，hazard侧重意外、潜在的突发风险

definitions.example: Driving under the influence of alcohol is proven to be extremely dangerous to public safety.

dangerous.etymology: 由「danger」+ 形容词后缀「-ous」构成，表‘具有危险性质的’

similar_words.description: 均表危险的，dangerous为通用词，perilous为正式用语，侧重极度、迫近的危险

definitions.example: No journalist dared to question the dictator's authoritarian policies publicly.

dare.etymology: 源自古英语「darr」（敢），兼具实义动词与情态动词词性

similar_words.description: 「dare」为动词表‘敢’，「daring」为其形容词形式，表‘勇敢的，大胆的’

definitions.meaning: 心爱的人；亲爱的（称呼）；可爱的
definitions.example: The young pianist, once the darling of the classical music circle, suddenly retired from the stage.

darling.etymology: 源自古英语「deor」（亲爱的）+ 指小后缀「-ling」，原指“小亲爱的”，后泛指心爱的人或物

similar_words.description: 均表亲爱的，darling多作名词表心爱的人或亲昵称呼，dear多作形容词表亲爱的、昂贵的

definitions.example: She dashed across the crowded square to catch the last train to her hometown.
The author used a dash to insert an ironic comment in the middle of the narrative.

dash.etymology: 源自古英语「dascian」（猛击），后演变为猛冲义，破折号义为引申用法

similar_words.description: 均表猛冲，dash侧重突然、短距离的急促冲跑，rush侧重持续、急促的奔忙

definitions.example: The research team analyzed the data collected from 10,000 online questionnaires.

data.etymology: 源自拉丁语「datum」（给予的东西）的复数形式，现代英语中常作不可数名词使用

similar_words.description: 「data」为「datum」的复数形式，现多作不可数名词表‘数据’，「datum」为单数形式，指单个数据项，较少使用

definitions.example: The university's academic database contains millions of peer-reviewed journal articles.

database.etymology: 合成词，由「data」（数据）+「base」（基础、库）构成，指存储数据的结构化系统

similar_words.description: 均与数据相关，database为存储数据的结构化系统，dataset为特定的一组数据集合

definitions.example: Each datum obtained from the field survey must be cross-checked with existing academic literature.

datum.etymology: 源自拉丁语datum，原义为'被给予的事物'，是data的单数形式，考研中多指单个研究数据或观测值

similar_words.description: data是datum的复数形式，考研中data更常用，需注意主谓一致（data作主语时谓语用复数）

definitions.example: The ecological research team set out for the coastal wetland at dawn to monitor seabird migration.
The era of precision medicine dawned with the popularization of gene sequencing technology.

dawn.etymology: 源自古英语dagian，与day（白昼）同根，原指'天刚亮的时刻'，后引申为'时代的开端'

similar_words.description: 二者均指黎明，dawn更具书面文学性，daybreak更侧重客观时间点

definitions.example: The psychological experiment requires participants to complete cognitive tasks in controlled natural daylight.

daylight.etymology: 复合名词，由day（白天）+ light（光线）构成，指白昼的自然可见光线

similar_words.description: sunlight特指太阳发出的光线，daylight指白昼所有可见光线，范围更广

definitions.example: Most nocturnal mammals have evolved visual systems adapted to low light, not daytime brightness.

daytime.etymology: 复合名词，由day（白天）+ time（时间段）构成，指从日出到日落的时间范围

similar_words.description: daytime侧重时间范围，daylight侧重光线属性，二者不可完全替换

definitions.example: The glare from the glacier's surface dazzled the expedition members, forcing them to wear polarized sunglasses.
The Nobel laureate's keynote speech dazzled the audience with its innovative insights into climate change.

dazzle.etymology: 源自古法语dazer，原义为'使困惑'，后演变为'因强光而目眩'，再引申为'因出色而倾倒'

similar_words.description: daze作动词/名词指（使）茫然、恍惚；dazzle指（使）目眩或赞叹，形近易混

definitions.example: Graduate school applicants must submit their recommendation letters before the specified deadline to be considered.

deadline.etymology: 源自美国内战时期战俘营的'死亡警戒线'，后引申为'不可逾越的截止时间'

similar_words.description: deadlock指谈判或进程的僵局，deadline指截止日期，形近易混

definitions.example: The invasive fungal disease has a deadly effect on the native oak tree population in the Appalachian region.
There was a deadly silence in the conference room when the university's budget cut plan was announced.

deadly.etymology: 由dead（死亡的）+ 形容词后缀-ly构成，原义为'导致死亡的'，后引申为'程度极深的'

similar_words.description: 二者均指致命的，deadly更通用，fatal更正式，常用于医学、法律等专业语境

definitions.example: The university has established a special support center for deaf and hard-of-hearing undergraduate students.
The local government was deaf to the residents' repeated complaints about industrial air pollution for six months.

deaf.etymology: 源自古英语deaf，原义为'听觉丧失的'，后引申为'不愿倾听的'

similar_words.description: deaf指听觉障碍，dumb指语言障碍（哑的），二者易被错误等同

definitions.example: College administrators need to deal with the rising demand for mental health services among graduate students.
The two top research institutions signed a deal to co-develop a new mRNA vaccine candidate.

deal.etymology: 源自古英语dǣlan，原义为'分配'，后演变为'处理事务'或'达成交易'

similar_words.description: dealt是deal的过去式/过去分词，易与表示'行为、事迹'的deed形近混淆

definitions.example: The authorized academic textbook dealer offered a 15% discount to graduate students during the fall semester.

dealer.etymology: 由deal（交易）+ 名词后缀-er（表人）构成，指从事商品或金融交易的从业者

similar_words.description: retailer特指面向消费者的零售商，dealer可指各类经销商，范围更广

definitions.example: The dean of the College of Social Sciences has launched a new research initiative on urban inequality.

dean.etymology: 源自拉丁语，原指教会的首席祭司，后引申为大学的学院院长等行政管理者，无明显可拆分的考研常见词缀

similar_words.description: 「dean」为名词，指大学学院院长等行政管理者；「deem」为动词，意为认为、视为

definitions.example: Epidemiological studies have linked long-term exposure to fine particulate matter to a higher risk of premature death.

death.etymology: 源自古英语deāth，为基础核心词，无复杂词缀拆分

similar_words.description: 「death」为名词，指死亡；「deaf」为形容词，指聋的、不愿倾听的

definitions.example: The parliamentary debate over immigration reform lasted for three consecutive days.
Economists debate whether minimum wage hikes will reduce employment rates.

debate.etymology: de-（加强意义）+ bat（=battle 战斗、争执），原指「互相争执」，引申为公开的辩论或争论

similar_words.description: 「debate」多指公开、正式的观点交锋；「dispute」侧重因分歧产生的争执、纠纷，可指口头或书面的争议

definitions.example: The government has introduced policies to reduce the country’s external debt burden.

debt.etymology: de-（向下、表被动）+ bt（=bit 咬），原指「被咬住的（需偿还的）款项」，引申为债务

similar_words.description: 「debt」为名词，指需偿还的债务；「debit」为名词/动词，指会计中的借方或记入借方

definitions.example: Over the past decade, renewable energy technologies have become increasingly cost-effective worldwide.

decade.etymology: dec-（表示「十」的词根）+ -ade（名词后缀，表周期），指十年的时间周期

similar_words.description: 「decade」为名词，指十年；「decay」为动词/名词，指腐烂、衰退

definitions.meaning: 腐烂，腐朽；（机构、制度等）衰退，衰落
definitions.example: If not preserved properly, ancient wooden artifacts will decay rapidly in humid environments.

definitions.meaning: 腐烂，腐朽；（机构、制度等的）衰退，衰落
definitions.example: The decay of the public education system has become a pressing social issue.

decay.etymology: de-（向下、表恶化）+ cay（=cad 落下），原指「落下、衰败」，引申为物质腐烂或抽象事物的衰退

similar_words.description: 「decay」可指有机物质的自然腐烂或抽象事物的衰退；「rot」侧重有机物质因微生物作用而腐烂，语义更具体

definitions.example: The corporate scandal involved deliberate deceit to inflate the company’s stock price.

deceit.etymology: de-（加强意义）+ ceit（=ceive 拿、抓），原指「用计谋抓住（对方）」，引申为欺诈行为

similar_words.description: 「deceit」多指故意的、带有恶意的欺骗行为或品质；「deception」侧重欺骗的手段或过程，语义更中性

definitions.example: Some online platforms use algorithmic tricks to deceive users into clicking on paid advertisements.

deceive.etymology: de-（加强意义）+ ceive（=capere 拿、抓），原指「用计谋控制（对方）」，引申为欺骗、蒙骗

similar_words.description: 「deceive」为动词，指欺骗；「perceive」为动词，指察觉、感知

definitions.meaning: 正派的，得体的；相当好的，不错的
definitions.example: The new labor law requires employers to provide decent working conditions for all staff.

decent.etymology: de-（加强意义）+ cent（=cant 说、唱），原指「说话得体的」，引申为行为正派、质量不错的

similar_words.description: 「decent」为形容词，指得体的、不错的；「descent」为名词，指下降、血统

definitions.example: The research team converted the raw data into decimal values for statistical analysis.
Students are required to round the final result to two decimal places in the exam.

decimal.etymology: decim-（表示「第十、十分之一」的词根）+ -al（形容词/名词后缀），指与十进制或小数相关的

similar_words.description: 「decimal」指十进制的、小数；「decibel」为名词，指分贝（音量单位）

definitions.example: The university admissions committee’s decision to prioritize holistic evaluation has received positive feedback.

decision.etymology: 由前缀de-（加强义）+词根cis（=cut/decide，表切割、决断）+名词后缀-ion构成，指「做出最终决断的结果」

similar_words.description: 二者为同根词，「decision」是名词，表「决定」；「decisive」是形容词，表「决定性的」

definitions.example: The decisive test result confirmed the hypothesis proposed by the research team.

decisive.etymology: 由前缀de-（加强义）+词根cis（=cut/decide，表切割、决断）+形容词后缀-ive构成，指「具有决断力的」

similar_words.description: 二者为同根词，「decisive」是形容词，表「决定性的」；「decision」是名词，表「决定」

definitions.example: When the storm approached, all crew members were required to stay below the main deck.
The campus auditorium was decked with red banners for the annual graduation ceremony.

deck.etymology: 源自古英语dec，原指「覆盖物」，后引申为「船体的覆盖层（甲板）」；动词义由「用物品覆盖装饰」引申而来

similar_words.description: 二者音近形近，「deck」表「甲板；装饰」；「desk」表「书桌」

definitions.example: The international organization released a joint declaration calling for global climate action.

declaration.etymology: 由前缀de-（加强义）+词根clar（=clear，表清楚、明确）+名词后缀-ation构成，指「明确宣布的内容」

similar_words.description: 二者为同根词，「declaration」是名词，表「声明」；「declare」是动词，表「宣布」

definitions.meaning: 宣布，声明；申报（纳税品等）
definitions.example: The researchers declared their conflicts of interest before publishing the study results.

declare.etymology: 由前缀de-（加强义）+词根clar（=clear，表清楚、明确）构成，指「明确地公开告知」

similar_words.description: 二者词形相近易混，「declare」表「宣布」；「decline」表「下降；婉拒」

definitions.example: The number of rural primary school students has declined sharply in the past decade.
The economic decline of the region was caused by the closure of major factories.

decline.etymology: 由前缀de-（向下）+词根clin（=lean，表倾斜）构成，指「向下倾斜」，引申为「下降；婉拒」

similar_words.description: 二者为同根词，「decline」表「下降；婉拒」；「incline」表「倾斜；倾向于」

definitions.example: Microorganisms decompose organic waste into nutrients that enrich the soil.

decompose.etymology: 由前缀de-（分开、去除）+前缀com-（共同）+词根pos（=put，表放置）+动词后缀-e构成，指「把放在一起的东西分开」，引申为「分解；腐烂」

similar_words.description: 二者为同根词，「decompose」表「分解；腐烂」；「compose」表「组成；作曲」，词义相反

definitions.example: The students decorated the library walls with hand-painted murals to celebrate World Book Day.

decorate.etymology: 由词根decor（=beauty，表美观、装饰）+动词后缀-ate构成，指「使美观」

similar_words.description: 二者为同根词，「decorate」是动词，表「装饰」；「decorative」是形容词，表「装饰性的」

definitions.example: The new traffic regulations are expected to decrease the number of road accidents significantly.
A noticeable decrease in air pollution has been observed since the implementation of the clean energy policy.

decrease.etymology: 由前缀de-（向下）+词根creas（=grow，表增长）构成，指「向下增长」，即「减少」

similar_words.description: 二者为同根反义词，「decrease」表「减少」；「increase」表「增加」

definitions.meaning: 致力于；献身于；题献（著作等）
definitions.example: The Nobel Prize-winning scientist dedicated her entire career to researching renewable energy.

dedicate.etymology: 由前缀de-（加强义）+词根dic（=say，表说、宣誓）+动词后缀-ate构成，原指「宣誓献身」，引申为「致力于」

similar_words.description: 二者为近义词，均表「致力于」，「dedicate」更侧重正式、长期的献身，常与介词「to」搭配

definitions.example: Researchers can deduce the ancient climate from the layers of sediment in the lake bed.

deduce.etymology: 前缀de-（表加强、向下）+ 词根duce（表引导），引导出逻辑结论→推论

similar_words.description: 两者为同根形近词，deduce表推论、推断，deduct表扣除、减去

definitions.example: The tax authority will deduct the eligible housing loan interest from your annual taxable income.

deduct.etymology: 前缀de-（表去除、向下）+ 词根duce（表引导），引导出（去除）部分金额→扣除

similar_words.description: 两者为同根形近词，deduct表扣除、减去，deduce表推论、推断

definitions.example: His selfless deeds during the epidemic were widely reported by mainstream media.
The transfer of real estate must be documented in a formal signed deed.

deed.etymology: 源自古英语dēd，原指「所做之事」，后引申为对约定行为的书面证明→契约

similar_words.description: 两者为形近词，deed为名词，表行为、事迹或契约；deem为动词，表认为、视为

definitions.example: The academic committee deemed the research paper unqualified for publication.

deem.etymology: 源自古英语dēman，原指「判断、裁决」，后引申为主观上认为

similar_words.description: 两者为形近词，deem为动词表认为、视为，deed为名词表行为、事迹或契约

definitions.example: A herd of deer was observed foraging in the protected forest reserve last month.

deer.etymology: 源自古英语dēor，原指「野兽」，中古英语后词义缩小为特指「鹿」

similar_words.description: 两者为形近词，deer为名词表鹿，dear为形容词表亲爱的、昂贵的

definitions.example: The underdog team defeated the defending champion in the semi-final match.
The ruling party’s defeat in the parliamentary election led to a cabinet reshuffle.

defeat.etymology: 前缀de-（表相反）+ 词根feat（表做、行动），做出相反的行动→击败；行动的相反结果→失败

similar_words.description: 两者为形近词，defeat表击败、失败，defect表缺陷、叛变

definitions.example: The automobile manufacturer recalled 100,000 vehicles due to a brake system defect.
Several intelligence officers defected to the neighboring country during the political crisis.

defect.etymology: 前缀de-（表离开）+ 词根fect（表做、行动），离开正常的行动标准→缺陷；离开所属阵营→叛变

similar_words.description: 两者为形近词，defect表缺陷、叛变，defeat表击败、失败

definitions.example: The country has allocated 15% of its annual budget to national defence construction.
The defence lawyer presented new evidence to prove his client’s innocence.

defence.etymology: 前缀de-（表加强）+ 词根fenc（表打击、保卫）+ 名词后缀-e，加强保卫的行为→防御；法律上的保卫行为→辩护

similar_words.description: 两者为同根词，defence为名词表防御、辩护，defend为动词表保卫、辩护

definitions.example: The border troops are tasked with defending the country’s territorial sovereignty.
The scholar defended his controversial academic viewpoint with rigorous data analysis.

defend.etymology: 前缀de-（表加强）+ 词根fend（表打击、保卫），加强保卫的动作→防御；为他人/观点提供保卫→辩护

similar_words.description: 两者为同根词，defend为动词表保卫、辩护，defence为名词表防御、辩护

definitions.example: Long-term iron deficiency may cause anemia and other chronic health conditions.

deficiency.etymology: 前缀de-（表否定、不足）+ 词根fic（表做、制作）+ 名词后缀-iency，做得不够的状态→缺乏、不足

similar_words.description: 两者为近义词，deficiency表抽象的缺乏、不足，defect表具体的缺陷、缺点

definitions.example: The government has been struggling to reduce the fiscal deficit caused by excessive public spending.

deficit.etymology: de-（不足）+ fic（做）+ -it（名词后缀），意为做得不足、有所亏欠，引申为财政赤字或贸易逆差

similar_words.description: 「deficit」侧重财政、贸易领域的差额亏损；「deficiency」侧重数量、质量上的缺乏不足

definitions.example: Scholars often define social mobility as the ability of individuals to move between different social classes.

define.etymology: de-（加强）+ fin（界限）+ -e（动词后缀），意为定下明确界限，引申为下定义、界定范围

similar_words.description: 「define」为动词，指下定义、做界定；「definite」为形容词，指明确的、肯定的

definitions.example: There is no definite evidence to prove that the new policy can effectively curb inflation.

definite.etymology: de-（加强）+ fin（界限）+ -ite（形容词后缀），意为有明确界限的，引申为确定的、毋庸置疑的

similar_words.description: 「definite」指有限定范围的、明确的；「infinite」加否定前缀in-，指无限的、无穷尽的

definitions.example: The definition of 'sustainable development' has been widely discussed in academic circles over the past decades.

definition.etymology: de-（加强）+ fin（界限）+ -ition（名词后缀），意为定下界限的具体内容，引申为对概念的正式定义

similar_words.description: 「definition」指对术语、概念的严谨正式定义；「description」指对人或事物的一般性描述

definitions.example: High temperatures can deform the plastic components of the device, leading to functional failures.

deform.etymology: de-（破坏、偏离）+ form（形状），意为破坏原有形状，引申为使变形、使结构畸形

similar_words.description: 「deform」前缀de-表破坏，指使变形；「reform」前缀re-表重新，指改革、改进制度或方法

definitions.example: Teenagers may defy their parents' rules to express their independent identity.
The beauty of the ancient architecture defies description, attracting millions of tourists every year.

defy.etymology: de-（不、离开）+ fy（=fid，信任），意为不信任、不服从，引申为违抗；进一步引申为使常规逻辑无法实现

similar_words.description: 「defy」指违抗规则、挑战常理；「deny」指否认事实、拒绝要求

definitions.example: Researchers found that the degenerate species could no longer adapt to the changing environmental conditions.
Without proper mental stimulation, one's cognitive abilities may degenerate rapidly in old age.

degenerate.etymology: de-（向下、变差）+ gener（出生、种族）+ -ate（后缀），意为个体或种群素质向下衰退，引申为退化、堕落

similar_words.description: 「degenerate」前缀de-表向下，指退化、衰退；「generate」词根gener表出生，指产生、生成

definitions.example: The degree of public support for the new carbon reduction policy is significantly higher than expected.
A growing number of young people believe that a master's degree can improve their competitiveness in the job market.

degree.etymology: de-（向下）+ gree（=grad，步、等级），意为按步骤划分的层级，引申为程度、学位、温度/角度单位

similar_words.description: 「degree」侧重抽象程度或正式学位；「grade」侧重具体的等级、成绩或年级

definitions.example: The launch of the new satellite has been delayed due to adverse weather conditions.
Unexpected delays in the global supply chain have severely impacted the export industry of many countries.

delay.etymology: de-（离开、放下）+ lay（放置），意为放下不做、延后处理，引申为延迟、耽搁

similar_words.description: 「delay」前缀de-表离开，指延迟、耽搁；「relay」前缀re-表再，指转播、接替传送

definitions.example: Each member state will send five delegates to participate in the annual meeting of the World Health Organization.
Senior managers usually delegate routine administrative tasks to junior staff to focus on strategic planning.

delegate.etymology: de-（加强）+ leg（选择）+ -ate（后缀），意为被挑选出来执行任务的人，引申为代表；进一步引申为委派代表、授权任务

similar_words.description: 「delegate」多指官方委派的参会代表；「representative」可指各类机构或群体的代表，适用范围更广

definitions.example: Researchers may delete irrelevant data points to ensure the accuracy of the experiment.

delete.etymology: 前缀de-（去除）+ 词根「lete（擦、抹）」构成，本义为擦除、抹掉

similar_words.description: delete指删除内容、数据等，deduct指扣除金额、分数等

definitions.example: The court ruled that the company’s violation of environmental regulations was deliberate.
The committee will deliberate on the new policy before making a final decision.

deliberate.etymology: 前缀de-（完全）+ 词根「liber（权衡、平衡）」+ 后缀-ate（形容词/动词后缀）构成，指经过完全权衡后作出的

similar_words.description: deliberate指故意的或深思熟虑的，delicate指纤细、精致或微妙的

definitions.meaning: 纤细的；精致的；微妙的（如平衡、关系等）
definitions.example: The delicate balance between economic growth and environmental protection has become a hot topic in academic research.

delicate.etymology: 前缀de-（加强）+ 词根「lic（吸引）」+ 后缀-ate（形容词后缀）构成，原指吸引人的，引申为精致的

similar_words.description: delicate指纤细、精致或微妙的，deliberate指故意的或深思熟虑的

definitions.example: The local restaurant is famous for its delicious homemade desserts.

definitions.meaning: 令人愉快的；妙趣横生的（尤指讽刺等）
definitions.example: There is a delicious irony in the fact that the environmental activist drives a gas-guzzling car.

delicious.etymology: 前缀de-（加强）+ 词根「lic（吸引）」+ 后缀-ious（形容词后缀）构成，原指引诱味觉的，引申为美味的

similar_words.description: delicious侧重味觉美味或引申的妙趣，delightful侧重情感上的令人愉悦

definitions.example: To the delight of the audience, the famous pianist gave an extra performance.
The children’s laughter delights the elderly couple every morning.

delight.etymology: 前缀de-（加强）+ 词根「light（明亮、愉悦）」构成，指让心情变得明亮愉悦

similar_words.description: 二者同根于「light（明亮）」，delight表心情愉悦，enlighten表启发、启蒙

definitions.meaning: 递送，传送；发表（演讲、声明等）
definitions.example: The president will deliver a televised address to the nation tonight to explain the new economic policy.
The experienced midwife helped deliver the baby safely in the remote mountain village.

deliver.etymology: 前缀de-（完全）+ 词根「liver（释放、递送）」构成，本义为完全释放（货物等）

similar_words.description: deliver指递送、发表或接生，delegate指委派、代表

definitions.meaning: 递送，传送；（演讲的）表达方式
definitions.example: Her clear and persuasive delivery made the academic lecture accessible to all participants.
The hospital provides professional care for women during delivery and postnatal recovery.

delivery.etymology: 动词deliver（递送、发表）+ 后缀-y（名词后缀）构成，表动作或结果

similar_words.description: delivery与递送、演讲或分娩相关，delegation与代表团、委派相关

definitions.meaning: 需求；要求（尤指合理或迫切的）
definitions.example: The rising global demand for electric vehicles has boosted the development of lithium-ion battery technology.
The research project demands a high level of attention to detail and analytical skills.

demand.etymology: 前缀de-（完全）+ 词根「mand（命令、要求）」构成，指完全的、强烈的要求

similar_words.description: 二者同根于「mand（命令）」，demand侧重合理/迫切的要求，command侧重上级的命令

definitions.example: The report analyzes the challenges and prospects of consolidating democracy in emerging economies.

democracy.etymology: 词根「demo（人民）」+ 词根「cracy（统治）」构成，意为人民的统治

similar_words.description: 二者同根于「cracy（统治）」，democracy指民主制度，autocracy指独裁制度

definitions.example: The democratic decision-making process ensures that all members of the organization have a voice.

democratic.etymology: 词根「demo（人民）」+ 词根「crat（统治者）」+ 后缀-ic（形容词后缀）构成，意为人民统治的

similar_words.description: democratic是形容词，意为民主的；democracy是名词，意为民主制度

definitions.example: Several peer-reviewed studies demonstrate that long-term exposure to air pollution is associated with increased risk of cardiovascular diseases.
The sharp decline in sales demonstrates that the new marketing strategy has failed to achieve its expected effect.

demonstrate.etymology: de-（加强意义）+ monstr（显示）+ -ate（动词后缀），字面含义为「展示出来以证实」，引申为证明、表明

similar_words.description: 「demonstrate」侧重通过实验、数据等实证来证明；「illustrate」侧重用例子、图表等具象方式说明
「demonstrate」指证明、表明；「remonstrate」指抗议、规劝

definitions.example: The company issued an official denial of the allegations that it had violated labor protection laws.
The denial of equal educational opportunities to children from poor families is a serious social injustice.

denial.etymology: de-（否定）+ ni（=neg 否认）+ -al（名词后缀），表示否认的行为或状态

similar_words.description: 「denial」侧重对事实、指控的否认或对基本权利的剥夺；「rejection」侧重对申请、提议的直接回绝

definitions.example: In economic research, the term 「GDP growth」 denotes the annual increase in the total value of goods and services produced in a country.
A persistent decline in consumer confidence denotes that the economy may enter a recession in the coming quarters.

denote.etymology: de-（完全、明确）+ note（标记、指示），字面含义为「做明确标记」，引申为表示、意指

similar_words.description: 「denote」指词语、符号的字面明确含义；「connote」指隐含的、联想的附加含义

definitions.example: Many international organizations denounce the military action that has caused massive civilian casualties in the conflict zone.
The former employee denounced the company’s tax evasion practices to the local regulatory authority.

denounce.etymology: de-（否定、反对）+ nounce（=nunci 宣布、告知），字面含义为「公开宣布反对」，引申为谴责、告发

similar_words.description: 「denounce」侧重公开、猛烈地指责，带有强烈情绪；「condemn」侧重正式从道义或制度层面谴责
二者为同根词，「denounce」是公开谴责；「announce」是公开宣布、通告

definitions.example: The dense tropical rainforest is home to thousands of rare plant and animal species that have not been fully studied.
The philosopher’s dense writing style, full of abstract concepts, makes his works inaccessible to most ordinary readers.

dense.etymology: 源自拉丁语densus（浓密的），无常见可拆分词缀，同源名词为density

similar_words.description: 「dense」侧重事物在空间内排列紧凑（如人群、植被、文字密度）；「thick」侧重厚度大、黏稠（如厚墙、浓汤）

definitions.example: High population density in megacities often leads to problems such as traffic congestion and soaring housing prices.
The high density of core arguments in this academic paper makes it a key reference for researchers in the field.

density.etymology: dens（=dense 浓密的）+ -ity（名词后缀，表性质、程度），表示浓密的程度

similar_words.description: 「density」侧重事物分布的密集程度；「intensity」侧重力量、情感或活动的强烈程度

definitions.example: The national health insurance policy covers most costs of basic dental treatment for citizens under the age of 18.

dental.etymology: dent（=tooth 牙齿）+ -al（形容词后缀，表相关的），表示与牙齿相关的

similar_words.description: 「dental」指与牙齿相关的；「rental」指租赁的、租金的，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: My dentist advised me to reduce the intake of sugary drinks to prevent tooth decay and gum disease.

dentist.etymology: dent（=tooth 牙齿）+ -ist（名词后缀，表从事某职业的人），表示从事牙齿治疗的医生

similar_words.description: 「dentist」是牙科医生；「denture」是假牙、义齿，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The official denied that the government had any plan to raise the personal income tax rate in the next fiscal year.
The immigration authority denied his visa application because he failed to provide sufficient proof of financial support.

deny.etymology: de-（否定）+ ny（=neg 否认），同源名词为denial，常用结构为deny sth/deny doing sth/deny sb sth

similar_words.description: 「deny」是否认、拒绝给予；「defy」是违抗、公然反抗，二者拼写相近易混淆
「deny」侧重否认事实或剥夺权利；「refuse」侧重拒绝接受某物或做某事

definitions.example: The research team will depart for the remote mountainous area tomorrow to conduct a field survey on local ecological conditions.
The young scholar’s new theory departs from the mainstream academic view and provides a novel perspective to explain the social phenomenon.

depart.etymology: de-（离开）+ part（部分、分开），字面含义为「与原有整体分离」，引申为离开、偏离

similar_words.description: 「depart」是离开、背离；「impart」是传授、告知，二者拼写相近易混淆
「depart」为正式用语，侧重启程前往某地；「leave」为通用用语，侧重离开某地或某人

definitions.example: The sociology department of Peking University has published a series of studies on urban-rural migration.

department.etymology: 词根词缀：de-（分离）+part（部分）+-ment（名词后缀），分离出的特定功能部分→部门/系

similar_words.description: 拼写相近，apartment意为公寓，department意为部门/系

definitions.meaning: 离开，启程；背离（常与from搭配）
definitions.example: Her radical departure from the conventional research methods sparked heated debate in the academic circle.

departure.etymology: 词根词缀：depart（离开）+-ure（名词后缀），表动作或状态→离开/背离

similar_words.description: 同根词，department意为部门/系，departure意为离开/背离

definitions.meaning: 依赖，依靠；取决于（常与on/upon搭配）
definitions.example: Whether the new carbon reduction target can be met depends on the joint efforts of governments and enterprises worldwide.

depend.etymology: 词根词缀：de-（向下）+pend（悬挂，表‘依附’），挂于下方→依赖/取决于

similar_words.description: 同根词，depend为动词表依赖，dependent为形容词表依赖的

definitions.meaning: 依赖的；受……影响的（常与on/upon搭配）
definitions.example: Many small and medium-sized enterprises are heavily dependent on bank loans to maintain their daily operations.

dependent.etymology: 词根词缀：depend（依赖）+-ent（形容词后缀，表‘具有……性质的’）→依赖的

similar_words.description: 同根词，dependent为形容词表依赖的，dependence为名词表依赖（状态）

definitions.meaning: 描绘，描述（尤指用文字或图像细致呈现）
definitions.example: The 2023 national environmental report depicts the steady improvement of air quality in major Chinese cities.

depict.etymology: 词根词缀：de-（加强语气）+pict（画，表‘描绘’）→细致描绘/描述

similar_words.description: 近义易混，depict侧重细致呈现，describe侧重 general 描述

definitions.example: International students are required to pay a tuition deposit before receiving their admission letters.
More young people choose to deposit their savings in low-risk financial products to combat inflation.

deposit.etymology: 词根词缀：de-（向下）+posit（放置，表‘存放’），放下存放→存款/存储

similar_words.description: 拼写相近，deposit意为存款/存储，depict意为描绘

definitions.meaning: 使沮丧；使萧条（尤指经济）
definitions.example: The continuous rise in raw material prices has depressed the profit margins of the manufacturing industry.

depress.etymology: 词根词缀：de-（向下）+press（压，表‘压制’），向下压制→使沮丧/使萧条

similar_words.description: 同根词，depress侧重使情绪或经济低迷，suppress侧重压制（行为、言论等）

definitions.meaning: 沮丧，抑郁；经济萧条（期，常与Great搭配指大萧条）
definitions.example: The Great Depression of the 1930s prompted governments to adopt Keynesian economic policies to stimulate demand.

depression.etymology: 词根词缀：depress（使沮丧/萧条）+-ion（名词后缀，表状态）→沮丧/萧条（期）

similar_words.description: 同根词，depression为名词表沮丧/萧条，depress为动词表使沮丧/萧条

definitions.example: The discriminatory policy deprived ethnic minority groups of equal access to public education resources.

deprive.etymology: 词根词缀：de-（去除）+priv（私人的，表‘所有物’），去除私人所有物→剥夺

similar_words.description: 拼写相近，deprive意为剥夺，derive意为起源于/获得

definitions.meaning: 深度；（思想、知识等的）深奥，深刻
definitions.example: The Nobel Prize-winning economist’s research on income inequality shows exceptional depth of analysis.

depth.etymology: 词根词缀：deep（深的）+-th（名词后缀，表性质或状态）→深度

similar_words.description: 同根词，depth为名词表深度，deep为形容词表深的

definitions.meaning: 副手，代理；（机构的）副职人员
definitions.example: As the deputy editor-in-chief of 「Cell Reports」, he is responsible for reviewing submitted research papers.

deputy.etymology: de-（表「副的，次的」）+ put-（表「指派，安排」）+ -y（名词后缀），指被指派担任副职的人员

similar_words.description: deputy指机构中的副手、代理人员，delegate指被委派的代表或参会代表

definitions.example: Some conservative scholars deride the new theoretical framework published in 「Science」 as lacking empirical support.

deride.etymology: de-（表「向下，贬低」）+ rid-（表「笑」）+ -e（动词后缀），字面为「朝下笑」，引申为嘲笑

similar_words.description: deride为动词表嘲笑，rid为动词表使摆脱、去除

definitions.example: The core argument of the paper derives from a decade of field research on urban poverty in developing countries.

definitions.meaning: 获得（抽象事物，如见解、满足感）
definitions.example: Researchers often derive valuable insights from analyzing seemingly trivial data points.

derive.etymology: de-（表「从…来」）+ riv-（表「河流」）+ -e（动词后缀），字面为「从河流引出」，引申为源自、获得

similar_words.description: derive为动词表源自、获得，deprive为动词表剥夺、使丧失

definitions.example: As the rover descends through the Martian atmosphere, its heat shield protects sensitive scientific instruments.
The rare 16th-century manuscript descends directly from the author's eldest grandson.

descend.etymology: de-（表「向下」）+ scend-（表「爬，攀登」）+ -e（动词后缀），字面为「向下爬」，引申为下降、传下来

similar_words.description: descend表向下移动（下降），ascend表向上移动（上升），为反义形近词

definitions.meaning: 后代，后裔；（学术、技术的）衍生物
definitions.example: Many modern artificial intelligence algorithms are descendants of early neural network models developed in the 1950s.

descendant.etymology: de-（表「向下」）+ scend-（表「爬，攀登」）+ -ant（表「人/物」的名词后缀），指向下传的人或衍生物

similar_words.description: descendant为名词表后代、衍生物，descendent为形容词表下降的、世袭的

definitions.example: The gradual descent of global average sea levels in the late Pleistocene epoch is well-documented in geological records.
The study explores how individuals of mixed descent navigate cultural identity in contemporary European societies.

descent.etymology: de-（表「向下」）+ scend-（表「爬，攀登」）+ -t（名词后缀），指下降的状态或血统出身

similar_words.description: descent为名词表下降、血统，descend为动词表下降、传下来，为同根不同词性

definitions.example: The ethnographic study describes the daily rituals of the indigenous Amazonian community with remarkable detail.

describe.etymology: de-（表「完全，彻底」）+ scrib-（表「写」）+ -e（动词后缀），字面为「完全写下」，引申为描述、形容

similar_words.description: describe为动词表描述，prescribe为动词表开处方、规定

definitions.example: The graduate program application requires candidates to provide a detailed description of their previous research experience.

description.etymology: de-（表「完全，彻底」）+ scrib-（表「写」）+ -tion（名词后缀），指描述的行为或内容

similar_words.description: description为名词表描述，prescription为名词表处方、规定

definitions.example: The Gobi Desert has experienced significant vegetation restoration in recent decades due to national ecological protection policies.
Soldiers who desert their posts during wartime face severe legal consequences under international military law.

desert.etymology: 作名词时，源自拉丁语「desertum」（无人居住的地方）；作动词时，de-（表「离开」）+ sert-（表「加入，连接」），指脱离连接，引申为抛弃、擅离

similar_words.description: desert可作名词表沙漠、动词表抛弃，dessert为名词表餐后甜点

definitions.example: Scholars who dedicate their lives to fundamental scientific research on climate change deserve greater public support and recognition.

deserve.etymology: de-（表「完全，彻底」）+ serv-（表「服务，保持」）+ -e（动词后缀），指完全符合应得的标准，引申为应得、值得

similar_words.description: deserve为动词表应得，reserve为动词表保留、预订，为同根形近词

definitions.example: The researchers designed a series of controlled experiments to test the cognitive ability of primates.
The award-winning building’s design integrates traditional Chinese elements with modern architecture.

design.etymology: 由前缀de-（加强意义）+词根sign-（标记、符号）构成，原义为「做标记以规划」，引申为设计、构思等义

similar_words.description: 同根词，assign意为「分配、指派」，design意为「设计、构思」，核心均与「标记」相关但语义不同

definitions.example: The academic committee designated Professor Lee as the chief editor of the new linguistic journal.

designate.etymology: 由前缀de-（加强意义）+词根sign-（标记）+动词后缀-ate构成，意为「标记出来以确定」，引申为指定、指派

similar_words.description: 近义词，appoint侧重官方正式任命，designate侧重明确指定某身份或职能

definitions.example: A position with opportunities for professional growth is highly desirable for new graduates.

desirable.etymology: 由动词desire（渴望）+形容词后缀-able（能…的）构成，意为「能被渴望的」，即理想的、值得拥有的

similar_words.description: 同根词，desirous主语为人，表「渴望的」；desirable主语为物，表「值得渴望的」

definitions.example: Many rural students desire to receive higher education in urban key universities.
The desire for social recognition drives many young people to pursue career success.

desire.etymology: 来自拉丁语，核心词根为-sid-（星），原义为「仰望星辰祈愿」，考研中核心关联「渴望、愿望」义

similar_words.description: 近义词，crave语气更强，表「热切渴望、渴求」；desire为通用词，表「渴望、期望」

definitions.example: The desolate former industrial zone has been redeveloped into a public park in recent years.
Years of armed conflict desolated the once prosperous border town.

desolate.etymology: 由前缀de-（完全）+词根sol-（单独）+后缀-ate构成，意为「完全孤立的」，引申为荒凉的、使荒芜等义

similar_words.description: 同根词，solitary侧重「独居的、单个的」，desolate侧重「地方荒凉的或人孤寂的」

definitions.example: The failure of the long-term research project plunged the entire team into despair.
He despaired of ever recovering the lost research data after the computer crash.

despair.etymology: 由前缀de-（表相反）+词根sper-（希望）构成，意为「没有希望」，即绝望

similar_words.description: 同根词，desperation为名词，表「绝望、拼命」；despair可作名词或动词表「绝望」

definitions.example: The humanitarian organization despatched emergency supplies to the flood-hit region without delay.
The general received a despatch from the front line detailing the military situation.

despatch.etymology: 由前缀de-（离开）+词根spatch-（快速）构成，原义为「快速离开」，引申为派遣、发送等义，为dispatch的英式拼写

similar_words.description: 同词异拼，dispatch为美式拼写，despatch为英式拼写，考研中二者用法完全一致

definitions.example: The desperate survivors clung to the floating debris waiting for rescue.
The remote village is desperate for qualified teachers and medical staff.

desperate.etymology: 由前缀de-（表相反）+词根sper-（希望）+形容词后缀-ate构成，意为「没有希望的」，引申为绝望的、极需要的

similar_words.description: 同根词，despairing侧重「感到绝望的」，desperate侧重「因绝望而拼命的或极需要的」

definitions.example: Many scholars despise the superficial commercialization of academic research achievements.

despise.etymology: 由前缀de-（向下）+词根spis-（看，为spect-的变体）构成，原义为「向下看」，引申为鄙视、轻视

similar_words.description: 近义词，disdain语气更正式，表「蔑视、不屑」；despise为通用词，表「鄙视、轻视」

definitions.example: Despite the significant obstacles, the research team managed to complete the project on schedule.

despite.etymology: 由前缀de-（向下）+词根spit-（看，为spis-的变体）构成，原义为「蔑视」，后引申为介词「尽管、不管」

similar_words.description: 易混词，despite为介词，后接名词/动名词；though为连词，后接完整句子，二者均表转折
