definitions.example: After the main course, most diners at the upscale research conference venue chose a seasonal fruit-based dessert.

dessert.etymology: 源自法语desservir（撤去餐具）的过去分词，原指撤去主菜后供应的食物，无考研常见可拆分词根词缀

similar_words.description: 拼写仅相差一个字母s，desert作名词指沙漠、作动词指抛弃，dessert仅作名词指餐后甜点

definitions.example: Many overseas students regard world-renowned research institutions as their ideal destination for doctoral studies.

destination.etymology: 由前缀de-（表‘完全’）+ 词根stin（表‘站立’）+ 名词后缀-ation构成，表‘最终站立的位置’即目的地

similar_words.description: 同根词，均含stin（站立）词根，destination指具体的目的地或目标，destiny指抽象的命运安排

definitions.example: The protagonist in the literary work struggles to break free from the predetermined destiny imposed by his social background.

destiny.etymology: 由前缀de-（表‘完全’）+ 词根stin（表‘站立’）+ 名词后缀-y构成，表‘已定下的终极立场’即命运

similar_words.description: 同根词，均含stin（站立）词根，destiny指抽象的命运安排，destination指具体的目的地

definitions.example: The severe wildfire destroyed a large area of protected forest and disrupted the local ecological balance.

destroy.etymology: 由前缀de-（表‘否定、拆除’）+ 词根stroy（=struct，表‘建造’）构成，表‘拆除已建造的事物’即摧毁

similar_words.description: 同根词，均含struct（建造）词根，construct指建造、构建，destroy指破坏、摧毁，语义相反

definitions.example: The academic paper analyzes the long-term destruction of marine ecosystems caused by overfishing and plastic pollution.

destruction.etymology: 由前缀de-（表‘否定’）+ 词根struct（表‘建造’）+ 名词后缀-ion构成，表‘破坏建造物的行为或结果’

similar_words.description: 拼写对应，construction指建造、建设，destruction指破坏、毁灭，语义相反

definitions.example: Long-term exposure to social media’s negative content can have a destructive impact on adolescents’ self-esteem.

destructive.etymology: 由前缀de-（表‘否定’）+ 词根struct（表‘建造’）+ 形容词后缀-ive构成，表‘具有破坏性质的’

similar_words.description: 同根形容词，destructive表‘主动具有破坏性的’，destructible表‘被动可被破坏的’

definitions.example: The archaeologist carefully detached the ancient pottery fragment from the surrounding soil to avoid damage.
The military command decided to detach a specialized unit to assist in the disaster relief efforts.

detach.etymology: 由前缀de-（表‘离开’）+ 词根tach（=sticky，表‘粘’）构成，表‘使粘在一起的事物分离’

similar_words.description: 前缀相反的同根词，attach指附上、连接，detach指分离、拆卸

definitions.example: The academic supervisor required the graduate student to provide more empirical details in the research proposal.
The witness detailed the sequence of events during the accident to the investigative team.

detail.etymology: 由前缀de-（表‘完全、细分’）+ 词根tail（=cut，表‘切’）构成，表‘把整体切分成小部分’即细节

similar_words.description: 同根词，均含tail（切割）词根，tailor指裁缝（切割布料的人），detail指细节（整体切分的部分）

definitions.example: The customs authority decided to detain the cargo ship for violating international trade regulations.
The scholar was detained by an urgent academic seminar and missed the scheduled train to the conference.

detain.etymology: 由前缀de-（表‘完全、持续’）+ 词根tain（=hold，表‘握、持’）构成，表‘持续握住不放’即拘留或耽搁

similar_words.description: 同根词，均含tain（持有）词根，detain指拘留、耽搁，retain指保留、保持

definitions.example: The advanced imaging technology can detect early signs of cancer that are invisible to the naked eye.

detect.etymology: 由前缀de-（表‘揭开、去除’）+ 词根tect（=cover，表‘盖’）构成，表‘揭开盖子发现隐藏的事物’即探测、发现

similar_words.description: 同根词，均含tect（覆盖）词根，detect指揭开盖子发现，protect指盖上盖子保护

definitions.example: The detective collected forensic evidence to trace the suspect’s movement during the investigation.

detective.etymology: 前缀de-（表「除去、揭开」）+ 词根tect（表「覆盖、隐藏」）+ 后缀-ive（表「具有某种性质的人/物」），原义为「揭开隐藏之事的人」

similar_words.description: 「detective」指从事侦查工作的人（侦探），「detector」指用于检测的设备（探测器）

definitions.example: The airport’s high-sensitivity metal detector alerted staff to the forbidden item in the passenger’s carry-on bag.

detector.etymology: 前缀de-（表「除去、揭开」）+ 词根tect（表「覆盖、隐藏」）+ 后缀-or（表「具有某种功能的物」），原义为「揭开隐藏事物的设备」

similar_words.description: 「detector」指用于检测的设备，「detective」指从事侦查工作的人

definitions.example: Without regular preservation measures, the historic mural’s color and texture began to deteriorate rapidly.

deteriorate.etymology: 词根deterior（表「更坏的」，源自拉丁语）+ 后缀-ate（表「使成为…的动词后缀」），原义为「使变得更坏」

similar_words.description: 「deteriorate」侧重状态（如环境、健康）的逐渐恶化，「degenerate」侧重道德、品质的堕落或衰退

definitions.example: Researchers conducted a 5-year longitudinal study to determine the long-term effects of air pollution on cardiovascular health.

determine.etymology: 前缀de-（表「加强」）+ 词根termin（表「边界、终点」，源自拉丁语）+ 后缀-e，原义为「划下边界、定下结论」

similar_words.description: 「determine」侧重经调查、分析后客观确定，「decide」侧重主观做出选择或判断

definitions.example: Any participant who deviates from the experimental protocol will be excluded from the final data analysis.

deviate.etymology: 前缀de-（表「离开」）+ 词根vi（表「道路」，源自拉丁语via）+ 后缀-ate（表「使成为…的动词后缀」），原义为「离开既定道路」

similar_words.description: 「deviate」是动词，表「偏离（行为）」；「deviant」是形容词/名词，表「偏离常规的（人）」

definitions.example: The portable medical device allows doctors to monitor patients’ vital signs remotely in rural areas.

definitions.meaning: 手段；策略（尤指文学手法）
definitions.example: The author employs magical realism as a literary device to explore the tension between tradition and modernity.

device.etymology: 前缀de-（表「分开」）+ 词根vic（表「看」，源自拉丁语videre）+ 后缀-e，原义为「经仔细考量后发明的事物」

similar_words.description: 「device」是名词，表「装置/手段」；「devise」是动词，表「设计、策划」

definitions.example: In the medieval allegory, the devil tempts the protagonist with promises of unlimited power and wealth.
Restoring the damaged ancient manuscript was a real devil of a task for the archival team.

devil.etymology: 源自古英语「deofol」，最早来自古希腊语「diabolos」（原义为「诽谤者」），后衍生出「魔鬼」的核心含义

similar_words.description: 「devil」指具体的魔鬼实体，「evil」泛指抽象的邪恶、罪恶或恶行

definitions.example: The engineering team devised a new energy-efficient system to reduce the factory’s carbon emissions.

devise.etymology: 前缀de-（表「加强」）+ 词根vis（表「看」，源自拉丁语videre）+ 后缀-e，原义为「经仔细观察后想出方案」

similar_words.description: 「devise」是动词，表「设计、策划」；「device」是名词，表「装置/手段」

definitions.meaning: 致力于；奉献给（常与to搭配）
definitions.example: Many linguists devote their entire careers to studying the evolution of endangered indigenous languages.

devote.etymology: 前缀de-（表「加强」）+ 词根vot（表「发誓」，源自拉丁语vovere）+ 后缀-e，原义为「发誓奉献」

similar_words.description: 「devote」侧重奉献时间、精力等抽象事物，「donate」侧重捐赠金钱、物品等具体事物

definitions.example: The morning dew glistened on the pine needles as the first rays of sunlight broke through the forest canopy.

dew.etymology: 源自古英语「deaw」，与德语「tau」（露水）同源，指夜间气温下降时凝结在物体表面的细小水珠

similar_words.description: 「dew」是名词，表「露水」；「due」是形容词，表「预期的、应得的」

definitions.meaning: 诊断（疾病）；判断（问题本质）
definitions.example: Doctors can diagnose some rare genetic disorders through advanced genetic testing.

diagnose.etymology: 前缀dia-（通过、经由）+词根gnos（知晓）+动词后缀-e，指通过检查知晓病情或问题本质

similar_words.description: diagnose为动词，diagnosis为其对应的名词形式，意为‘诊断；诊断结果’

definitions.example: The academic paper includes a clear diagram illustrating the experimental procedure.

diagram.etymology: 前缀dia-（贯通、穿过）+词根gram（书写、绘制），指能清晰贯通展示信息的绘图

similar_words.description: diagram侧重展示结构、流程的示意图，graph侧重展示数据变化的统计图

definitions.meaning: 拨（电话号码）；表盘；拨号盘
definitions.example: He dialed the contact number of the research institute to inquire about the project.

dial.etymology: 源自拉丁语dies（日），原指日晷的刻度盘，后引申为各类表盘及拨号动作

similar_words.description: dial为‘拨号/表盘’，deal为‘交易；处理’，拼写仅首字母后一位不同

definitions.example: The sociolinguistics study explores the influence of local dialect on urban residents' communication.

dialect.etymology: 前缀dia-（在特定群体之间）+词根lect（言说），指特定地区群体使用的语言变体

similar_words.description: dialect是包含词汇、语法的完整语言变体，accent仅指发音上的口音

definitions.meaning: 对话；（戏剧/小说中的）对白
definitions.example: The film's natural dialog reveals the subtle emotional changes of the main characters.

dialog.etymology: 前缀dia-（相互）+词根log（言说），指两人或多人之间的交谈内容

similar_words.description: dialog是多人对话，monolog是单人独白（前缀mono-表‘单一’）

definitions.example: The geometry problem requires calculating the diameter of a circle with a given circumference.

diameter.etymology: 前缀dia-（穿过）+词根meter（测量），指穿过圆心测量的圆的最长线段

similar_words.description: radius是圆的半径，diameter是直径，长度为半径的2倍

definitions.example: The economic report analyzes the consumption trend of diamond among young Chinese consumers.

diamond.etymology: 源自希腊语adamas（不可征服的），因钻石硬度极高而得名，后也指菱形

similar_words.description: diamond为‘钻石/菱形’，diadem为‘王冠；冕’，拼写前四位相同

definitions.example: The laboratory researcher kept a detailed diary to record daily experimental data.

diary.etymology: 词根di（日）+名词后缀-ary，指每日记录的文字内容

similar_words.description: diary为‘日记/日志’，dairy为‘乳制品；奶场’，拼写仅第二个字母不同

definitions.meaning: 口述（令他人记录）；命令，支配
definitions.example: The project manager dictated a progress report to his assistant during the offline meeting.

dictate.etymology: 词根dict（言说）+动词后缀-ate，指大声言说（令他人记录或服从）

similar_words.description: dictate为动词‘口述/命令’，dictator为名词‘独裁者’

definitions.example: The postgraduate entrance English test includes a dictation section to evaluate listening competence.

dictation.etymology: 词根dict（言说）+名词后缀-ation，指口述的行为或供听写的内容

similar_words.description: dictation为名词‘口述/听写’，dictate为其对应的动词形式

definitions.example: Researchers found that a Mediterranean diet is beneficial for long-term cardiovascular health.
Many young adults choose to diet excessively to pursue a slim figure, which may harm their health.

diet.etymology: 来自中古英语diet，原指一日的餐食，源自拉丁语diaeta（生活方式、饮食规定）

similar_words.description: 二者拼写高度接近，「diet」指饮食或节食，「diety」指神、造物主

definitions.example: Opinions differ greatly among scholars on the social impact of artificial intelligence development.

differ.etymology: 源自拉丁语differre，前缀dif-（表分离）+词根fer（表携带、带来），字面含义为分开携带，引申为事物间存在差异、观点有分歧

similar_words.description: 二者拼写接近，「differ」指不同、有分歧，「defer」指推迟、遵从

definitions.example: There is a marked difference in academic performance between students with active learning habits and those without.

difference.etymology: 为动词differ的名词形式，后缀-ence为抽象名词后缀，表状态或性质，指事物间的差异或观点的分歧

similar_words.description: 二者为同根词，「difference」指差异、分歧，「indifference」指漠不关心、冷漠

definitions.example: Critical thinking ability differentiates qualified researchers from ordinary learners.

differentiate.etymology: 为动词differ的派生词，后缀-iate为动词后缀，表使…，含义为使事物产生差异或分辨出事物的不同

similar_words.description: 二者为近义词，都含“区分、辨别”之意；「differentiate」侧重通过对比找出差异，「distinguish」侧重凭借特征分辨出事物

definitions.example: Many small enterprises encountered unprecedented operational difficulty during the epidemic.

difficulty.etymology: 源自形容词difficult，后缀-y为抽象名词后缀，表状态或性质，指遇到的困难或所处的困境

similar_words.description: 二者尾缀拼写接近，「difficulty」指困难、困境，「facility」指设施、才能

definitions.example: Sustainable development concepts have diffused gradually around the world in recent decades.
The reviewer criticized the scholar’s new book for being diffuse and lacking a clear core argument.

diffuse.etymology: 源自拉丁语diffusus，前缀dif-（表分开）+词根fus（表流动）+后缀-e，字面含义为四散流动，引申为扩散、漫射，后又引申为文章冗长的

similar_words.description: 二者拼写高度接近，「diffuse」指扩散、冗长的，「defuse」指拆除爆炸装置引线、缓和紧张局势

definitions.example: Human digestive systems need 4 to 6 hours to digest high-protein foods completely.
It usually takes readers much time to digest the complex logical reasoning in philosophical works.

digest.etymology: 源自拉丁语digestus，前缀di-（表分开）+词根gest（表携带、运送），字面含义为分类运送，引申为分解消化食物，进一步引申为理解吸收知识

similar_words.description: 二者为同根词，「digest」指消化、领会，「congest」指堵塞、充血

definitions.meaning: 数字的，数码的；数字显示的
definitions.example: Digital media has fundamentally changed the way people acquire and spread information.

digital.etymology: 源自名词digit（手指、数位），后缀-al为形容词后缀，表与…相关的，原指用手指计数的，后引申为数字的、数码的

similar_words.description: 二者为近义词，都含“数字的”之意；「digital」侧重与数码技术、电子数字相关，「numerical」侧重表示数值、数量的

definitions.example: The law protects every citizen’s right to live with dignity, regardless of their economic status.

dignity.etymology: 源自拉丁语dignus（有价值的、值得的），后缀-ity为抽象名词后缀，表性质或状态，引申为人的尊严或事物的庄严

similar_words.description: 二者为同根词，「dignity」指尊严、庄严，「dignitary」指显要人物、高官

definitions.example: Many college graduates are caught in the dilemma of choosing between postgraduate study and employment.

dilemma.etymology: 源自希腊语，前缀di-（表两个）+词根lemma（表命题、前提），字面含义为同时成立的两个对立命题，引申为进退两难的窘境

similar_words.description: 二者为近义词，都含“困境”之意；「dilemma」特指需在两个都不理想的选项中抉择的两难境地，「predicament」泛指艰难的处境

definitions.example: Diligent graduate students often spend long hours in the library reviewing academic literature and analyzing research data.

diligent.etymology: 来自拉丁语diligere，由di-（加强）+ lig（选择、专注）+ -ent（形容词后缀）构成，原指用心专注做事，后引申为勤奋的

similar_words.description: 「diligent」意为勤奋的，「dilate」意为（使）膨胀、扩大，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.meaning: 稀释，冲淡；削弱（影响力、质量等）
definitions.example: Critics argue that excessive product placement in films may dilute the artistic integrity of the works.

dilute.etymology: 来自拉丁语diluere，由di-（分开、散开）+ luere（冲洗）构成，原指冲散稀释液体，后引申为削弱抽象事物

similar_words.description: 「dilute」意为稀释、削弱，「dilate」意为（使）膨胀、扩大，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.meaning: 昏暗的；（记忆、视线）模糊的
definitions.example: In the dim light of the 19th-century manuscript room, the historian struggled to decipher the faded ink characters.

dim.etymology: 源自古英语dim，原指光线昏暗，后引申为视觉、记忆的模糊状态

similar_words.description: 「dim」侧重光线昏暗或感官（视觉、记忆）的模糊，「vague」侧重表达、概念的模糊不清

definitions.example: Cultural background is a crucial dimension that cannot be ignored when studying cross-cultural communication patterns.

dimension.etymology: 来自拉丁语dimensio，由di-（分开）+ mens（测量）+ -ion（名词后缀）构成，原指测量的范围，后引申为维度或事物的层面

similar_words.description: 「dimension」侧重可量化或有空间感的层面，「aspect」侧重事物的具体外观或某一特定角度的方面

definitions.meaning: （使）减少，降低；削弱（声望、权力等）
definitions.example: As public awareness of environmental protection grows, the public's tolerance for industrial pollution continues to diminish.

diminish.etymology: 来自拉丁语diminuere，由di-（加强）+ min（小）+ -ish（动词后缀）构成，意为使变小，引申为减少、削弱

similar_words.description: 「diminish」侧重逐渐减少或削弱（可指具体或抽象事物），「dwindle」侧重数量持续减少直至几乎耗尽

definitions.meaning: 进餐，用餐（尤指正式场合）
definitions.example: The Nobel Prize laureates will dine with the Swedish royal family at the annual award ceremony banquet.

dine.etymology: 来自拉丁语disjejunare，原指打破斋戒（吃晚餐），后泛指正式场合的进餐

similar_words.description: 「dine」意为进餐，「din」意为喧嚣、噪音，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.meaning: v. 浸，蘸；（数量、价格等）短暂下降；n. 浸泡；短暂下降
definitions.example: After the release of weaker-than-expected economic data, the national currency dipped by 1.2% against the US dollar in intraday trading.

dip.etymology: 源自古英语dyppan，原指将物体浸入液体，后引申为数量的短暂下降

similar_words.description: 「dip」侧重短暂的浸蘸或小幅度短暂下降，「immerse」侧重完全浸入液体或全身心沉浸于某事物

definitions.example: Candidates applying for the postgraduate program in literature must submit a notarized copy of their undergraduate diploma.

diploma.etymology: 来自希腊语diploma，由diplo-（双的、折叠的）+ -ma（名词后缀）构成，原指折叠的官方文书，后特指毕业文凭

similar_words.description: 「diploma」意为毕业文凭，「diplomat」意为外交官，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The senior diplomat from the European Union played a key role in mediating the territorial dispute between the two neighboring countries.

definitions.meaning: 善于交际的人，圆滑的人
definitions.example: As a natural diplomat in the corporate team, he always manages to ease tensions and reach consensus during meetings.

diplomat.etymology: 与「diploma」同根，由diplo-（折叠的外交文书）+ -mat（表人）构成，原指持外交文书的官员，后引申为善于交际的人

similar_words.description: 「diplomat」意为外交官，「diploma」意为毕业文凭，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.meaning: 外交的；善于交际的，圆滑的
definitions.example: The two countries issued a joint statement after the diplomatic talks, pledging to strengthen cooperation in renewable energy development.

diplomatic.etymology: 是「diplomat」的形容词形式，由diplo- + -matic（形容词后缀）构成，意为与外交相关的，后引申为处事圆滑的

similar_words.description: 「diplomatic」侧重外交场合的得体或处事的圆滑老练，「tactful」侧重言行顾及他人感受、避免冲突

definitions.example: Many researchers advocate direct observation of animal behaviors in their natural habitats.
The professor directed the graduate students to revise their thesis drafts carefully.

direct.etymology: 来自拉丁语「dirigere」，由前缀「dis-（分开、离开）」和词根「reg（指引、统治）」构成，本义为「分开指引」，引申为直接的、指导等含义

similar_words.description: 均含引导、指挥之意；direct侧重明确指示方向或要求，conduct侧重组织、引领活动或人群
是direct加否定前缀「in-」构成的同根反义词，意为间接的

definitions.example: Without a clear research direction, the team wasted several months on irrelevant experiments.

direction.etymology: 由动词「direct（指引）」加名词后缀「-ion（表动作或状态）」构成，表指引的动作或结果

similar_words.description: 均含指导之意；direction侧重宏观方向或原则性指导，instruction侧重具体操作指令

definitions.example: The collected data will be directly transmitted to the central database for further analysis.

directly.etymology: 由形容词「direct（直接的）」加副词后缀「-ly」构成

similar_words.description: 均含立刻之意；directly侧重动作的直接性，immediately侧重时间的紧迫性

definitions.example: The director of the research institute emphasized the importance of interdisciplinary cooperation.

director.etymology: 由动词「direct（指导）」加名词后缀「-or（表执行动作的人）」构成，指负责指导的人

similar_words.description: 均含管理者之意；director侧重机构或项目的最高指导者，manager侧重日常运营管理者

definitions.example: The university library's online directory allows users to search for books by author, title or subject.

directory.etymology: 由动词「direct（指引）」加名词后缀「-ory（表工具或场所）」构成，指引查找的工具→目录

similar_words.description: 均含目录之意；directory侧重按字母顺序排列的人名、机构名名录或计算机文件目录，catalogue侧重书籍、商品的分类目录

definitions.example: Field researchers wore protective gloves to avoid getting dirt on their hands while collecting soil samples.

dirt.etymology: 来自古英语「drit」，本义为泥土、污物

similar_words.description: 均含污物之意；dirt侧重潮湿的泥土或粘附的污渍，dust侧重干燥的灰尘

definitions.example: A severe traffic accident disabled him from walking, so he had to use a wheelchair for daily mobility.

disable.etymology: 由否定前缀「dis-（使相反）」加形容词「able（有能力的）」构成，使失去能力→使残疾

similar_words.description: 是disable的同根反义词，加前缀「en-（使）」，意为使能够、使成为可能
均含损害能力之意；disable侧重完全丧失能力，impair侧重部分削弱功能

definitions.example: Numerous rare species have disappeared from the earth due to habitat destruction and climate change.

disappear.etymology: 由否定前缀「dis-（使相反）」加动词「appear（出现）」构成，不出现→消失

similar_words.description: 均含消失之意；disappear侧重突然或逐渐的可见性消失，vanish侧重神秘地、无痕迹地消失

definitions.example: The team's unexpected defeat in the final match deeply disappointed their loyal supporters.

disappoint.etymology: 由否定前缀「dis-（未履行）」加动词「appoint（约定、任命）」构成，原义为「未履行约定」，引申为使失望

similar_words.description: 均含使受挫之意；disappoint侧重因期望未实现而失望，frustrate侧重因受阻而沮丧

definitions.example: The sudden natural disaster caused severe damage to the local infrastructure and agricultural production.

disaster.etymology: 来自希腊语「disastron」，由前缀「dis-（坏的、错误的）」加「astron（星）」构成，原义为「星位不正（凶兆）」，引申为灾难

similar_words.description: 均含灾难之意；disaster侧重人为或自然的重大不幸事件，catastrophe侧重毁灭性的、极其严重的灾难

definitions.example: The disastrous oil spill polluted hundreds of miles of coastal ecosystems and killed thousands of marine animals.

disastrous.etymology: dis-（表「坏、否定」）+ aster（词根，表「星」，原指星象不祥）+ -ous（形容词后缀，表「具有…性质的」）

similar_words.description: 同根名词，disaster为名词「灾难」，disastrous为其派生形容词「灾难性的」

definitions.example: The research data is stored on a compact disc which can be accessed by any standard computer drive.

disc.etymology: 直接借自拉丁语discus，原指「圆盘、铁饼」，后引申为唱片、磁盘等义

similar_words.description: 美式拼写形式，考研阅读中disc与disk通用，均指圆盘类物品或存储介质

definitions.meaning: 丢弃；抛弃（尤指不再有用的物品）
definitions.example: Many consumers discard perfectly functional smartphones just to purchase the latest models with minor upgrades.

discard.etymology: dis-（表「离开、去掉」）+ card（原指纸牌游戏中丢弃的牌），引申为「丢弃、抛弃」

similar_words.description: disregard意为「忽视、不顾」，discard意为「丢弃、抛弃」，易因拼写接近混淆词义

definitions.meaning: 识别；辨明（尤指细微差异或隐藏的事物）
definitions.example: Using advanced microscopy, scientists can discern subtle structural changes in cell membranes caused by viral infections.

discern.etymology: dis-（表「分开、分离」）+ cern（词根，表「区分、辨别」），引申为「识别、辨明」

similar_words.description: concern意为「关心、涉及」，discern意为「辨明、识别」，同根且拼写接近，易混淆词义

definitions.meaning: 排出（液体、气体、有毒废物等）
definitions.example: The local environmental bureau fined the chemical plant for discharging untreated wastewater into the nearby river.

definitions.meaning: 正式解除（职务、职责）；解雇
definitions.example: The senior manager was discharged from his position for embezzling company funds.

discharge.etymology: dis-（表「离开、解除」）+ charge（词根，表「装载、负荷」），原指「卸下装载物」，引申为「排出、解除」

similar_words.description: dismiss侧重「随意解雇、解散」，discharge侧重「正式解除职务或排出物质」，语义侧重点不同

definitions.example: Military training programs place great emphasis on strict discipline to ensure operational efficiency.
Digital humanities is an interdisciplinary discipline that combines computer science and traditional humanities research.

discipline.etymology: dis-（表「规范、分开」）+ ciplin（词根，表「教导、训练」）+ -e（名词后缀），原指「教导的规则」，引申为「纪律、学科」

similar_words.description: disciple意为「门徒、信徒」，discipline意为「纪律、学科」，同根但词义差异较大，易因拼写接近混淆

definitions.meaning: 揭露；透露（秘密、机密信息）
definitions.example: The whistleblower disclosed confidential internal documents proving the company's illegal price-fixing practices.

disclose.etymology: dis-（表「打开、揭露」）+ close（表「关闭」），原指「打开关闭的事物」，引申为「揭露、透露」

similar_words.description: discover侧重「发现未知事物」，disclose侧重「揭露隐藏的秘密或信息」，词义侧重点不同

definitions.example: Full-time students are eligible for a 20% discount on all public transportation fares in the city.

definitions.meaning: 低估；忽视（观点、证据等的价值）
definitions.example: Economists warn that policymakers should not discount the long-term economic impact of rising housing prices.

discount.etymology: dis-（表「去掉、除去」）+ count（表「计算」），原指「去掉部分计算金额」，引申为「折扣」，再引申为「低估」

similar_words.description: rebate指「付款后返还的返利」，discount指「购买时直接减免的折扣」，均为价格优惠但形式不同

definitions.example: Constant negative feedback from her supervisor discouraged her from applying for the senior research position.

discourage.etymology: dis-（表「相反、否定」）+ courage（表「勇气」），原指「使失去勇气」，引申为「使气馁、阻碍」

similar_words.description: encourage意为「鼓励、激励」，discourage意为「使气馁、阻碍」，为反义词，仅前缀不同

definitions.example: The professor's discourse on postcolonial literary theory has been widely cited in academic publications worldwide.

discourse.etymology: dis-（表「贯穿、遍及」）+ course（词根，表「跑、行进」），原指「跑遍的内容」，引申为「话语、论述」

similar_words.description: course意为「课程、进程」，discourse意为「话语、论述」，同根但词义差异较大，易因拼写接近混淆

definitions.example: Archaeologists discovered a well-preserved mural in the ancient tomb last year, which provided new clues to ancient civilization.

discover.etymology: dis-（表「除去」）+ cover（覆盖），除去覆盖物使事物显现→发现

similar_words.description: discover指发现已存在的事物，invent指创造从未存在的新事物

definitions.example: The discovery of penicillin revolutionized the treatment of bacterial infections and saved millions of lives.

discovery.etymology: discover（发现）+ -y（名词后缀，表行为或结果）→发现（成果）

similar_words.description: discovery指已存事物的发现成果，invention指新创造的发明物

definitions.example: As a confidential assistant to the CEO, she remained discreet about the company's unannounced merger plans.

discreet.etymology: dis-（表「分开」）+ creet（来自拉丁语cernere「分辨」），能分辨言行的分寸→谨慎的

similar_words.description: discreet指言行谨慎得体的，discrete指离散的、互不关联的

definitions.example: There is a significant discrepancy between the two research teams' findings on the impact of microplastics on marine life.

discrepancy.etymology: dis-（表「分开」）+ crep（来自拉丁语crepare「破裂」）+ -ancy（名词后缀，表状态），破裂脱节的状态→不一致

similar_words.description: discrepancy侧重说法、数据间的细微不一致，disparity侧重数量、程度上的巨大差距

definitions.example: Many countries have enacted laws to prohibit employers from discriminating against job applicants based on their age or disability.

discriminate.etymology: dis-（表「分开」）+ crimin（来自拉丁语crimen「区分」）+ -ate（动词后缀），刻意分开对待→歧视

similar_words.description: discriminate可指贬义的歧视或中性的辨别，distinguish仅指中性的辨别、区分

definitions.example: Racial discrimination in housing and education remains a pressing social issue in the United States.

discrimination.etymology: discriminate（歧视/辨别）+ -ion（名词后缀，表行为或结果）→歧视（行为）/辨别力

similar_words.description: discrimination指基于偏见的区别对待（行为），prejudice指主观的先入为主的偏见（态度）

definitions.example: The renowned scholar disdained to engage with the unsubstantiated claims made by the pseudoscientist at the conference.

disdain.etymology: dis-（表「不」）+ dain（来自拉丁语dignus「有价值的」，即尊严），不认可对方的价值→鄙视

similar_words.description: disdain侧重因认为对方低劣而轻蔑，despise侧重极端厌恶或憎恨

definitions.example: The lack of clean drinking water has contributed to the outbreak of waterborne diseases in the rural area.

disease.etymology: dis-（表「不」）+ ease（舒适），身体不舒适的状态→疾病

similar_words.description: disease指具体的、可诊断的疾病，illness指生病的抽象状态或患病期间

definitions.example: The former mayor's embezzlement scandal became a national disgrace and damaged the government's credibility.

disgrace.etymology: dis-（表「失去」）+ grace（荣誉、体面），失去荣誉与体面→耻辱

similar_words.description: disgrace侧重因行为不端导致的公众耻辱，shame侧重个人内心的羞愧感

definitions.example: The undercover journalist disguised himself as a factory worker to expose the illegal labor practices.

disguise.etymology: dis-（表「改变」）+ guise（外表、装束），改变外表以掩盖身份→伪装

similar_words.description: disguise可指伪装身份或掩饰情感，mask侧重用具体物品遮盖或掩饰（更具象）

definitions.meaning: n. 厌恶，反感；v. 使厌恶，使反感
definitions.example: The graphic footage of factory waste dumping disgusts most environmental researchers.

disgust.etymology: dis-（表否定、相反）+gust（原指味觉，引申为心理感受），字面义为「令人产生不适感受」

similar_words.description: 「disdain」侧重因轻视而产生的鄙视，「disgust」侧重因生理或道德反感产生的强烈厌恶

definitions.meaning: n. 不喜欢，反感；v. 不喜欢，厌恶
definitions.example: Many graduate candidates express their dislike of rigid exam-oriented teaching modes.

dislike.etymology: dis-（表否定）+like（喜欢），字面义为「不喜欢」

similar_words.description: 「dislike」是普通程度的不喜欢，「disgust」是带有强烈生理/道德反感的厌恶

definitions.meaning: n. 惊愕，沮丧；v. 使惊愕，使沮丧
definitions.example: To the research team's dismay, their groundbreaking paper was rejected by the top academic journal.

dismay.etymology: dis-（表强调）+may（原指勇气、力量），字面义为「使丧失勇气」，引申为惊愕沮丧

similar_words.description: 「dismay」侧重因意外事件产生的惊愕与沮丧，「disappoint」侧重因期望落空产生的失望

definitions.meaning: v. 不予考虑，驳回（主张、观点）；解雇，解散（人员、群体）
definitions.example: The court dismissed the defendant's appeal due to insufficient valid evidence.

dismiss.etymology: dis-（表分开、离开）+miss（送、派遣），字面义为「送走、打发走」，引申为驳回、解雇等义

similar_words.description: 「dismiss」侧重对观点主张的驳回或人员的解雇，「discard」侧重对无用物品的丢弃

definitions.meaning: n. 混乱，骚乱；（生理、心理）失调，紊乱；v. 使混乱，使失调
definitions.example: Sleep disorder has become a prevalent health issue among postgraduate entrance exam candidates.

disorder.etymology: dis-（表否定）+order（秩序），字面义为「无秩序」，引申为生理/心理的失调紊乱

similar_words.description: 「disorder」侧重原有秩序被破坏的混乱，「chaos」侧重完全无秩序的混沌状态

definitions.meaning: v. （使）分散，驱散（群体、物质）；传播，散布（信息、知识）
definitions.example: The local police used non-lethal weapons to disperse the unruly crowd gathered in the city center.

disperse.etymology: dis-（表分开）+sperse（来自拉丁语spargere，意为「散布」），字面义为「分散开」

similar_words.description: 「disperse」是分散、驱散，「dispense」是分配、分发，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.meaning: v. 取代，替代（原有事物、人员）；迫使（居民）离开家园
definitions.example: Intelligent automated systems are gradually displacing manual labor in the manufacturing industry.

displace.etymology: dis-（表离开）+place（位置），字面义为「离开原有位置」，引申为取代、迫使流离

similar_words.description: 「displace」侧重因外力被迫离开或被替代，「replace」侧重主动进行替换

definitions.meaning: v. 展示，陈列（物品）；表现（品质、情感）；n. 展示，陈列；表现
definitions.example: The national museum displayed a precious collection of ancient Chinese bronze wares last month.

display.etymology: dis-（表分开、展开）+play（原指折叠的布，引申为展开），字面义为「展开来展示」

similar_words.description: 「display」侧重公开摆放展示，「exhibit」侧重在展览中正式展出

definitions.meaning: n. 处理，处置（废物、事务）；支配权，使用权
definitions.example: Proper disposal of hazardous chemical waste is a core task of environmental protection departments.

disposal.etymology: dis-（表分开）+pos（放置）+al（名词后缀），来自动词「dispose」，字面义为「放置到合适位置的处理行为」

similar_words.description: 「disposal」是处理、处置，「disposition」是性情、倾向，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.meaning: 处理，处置（常与of连用）；安排，布置
definitions.example: The municipal government has issued strict regulations to dispose of domestic waste in a standardized way.
Long-term exposure to negative emotions may dispose individuals to suffer from mental health problems.

dispose.etymology: dis-（表分开）+pose（放置），字面义为「分开放置到合适位置」，引申为处理、安排等义

similar_words.description: 「dispose」是处理、安排，「propose」是提议、建议，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: Her calm disposition helped her maintain objectivity while analyzing controversial research data.
The disposition of the research equipment was supervised by the laboratory manager.

disposition.etymology: 由前缀dis-（分离）、词根pos（放置）和名词后缀-ition构成，原指物品的分开放置，后引申为性格、安排等含义

similar_words.description: 均含词根pos（放置），dispose为动词表‘处理、安排’，disposition为名词表‘性格、处置’，易混淆词性与核心义

definitions.example: Scholars from top universities disputed the validity of the new economic model at the annual symposium.
The territorial dispute between the two regions was resolved through diplomatic negotiation.

dispute.etymology: 由前缀dis-（相反）、词根put（思考）和后缀-e构成，原指想法相悖，后引申为争论、争端

similar_words.description: 均表争论，dispute侧重因分歧产生的激烈争端（可作动/名词），debate侧重正式场合的理性辩论（多作动/名词）

definitions.example: The company was fined for disregarding environmental protection regulations in its production process.
His disregard for academic integrity led to the revocation of his postgraduate admission.

disregard.etymology: 由前缀dis-（否定）和单词regard（注意、关注）构成，指不关注，即忽视、不顾

similar_words.description: 均表忽视，disregard侧重故意不顾规则或权威要求，ignore侧重无意识或有意忽略具体事物

definitions.example: The sudden network failure disrupted the online defense of the doctoral dissertation.

disrupt.etymology: 由前缀dis-（分离）和词根rupt（断裂）构成，原指使断裂分离，后引申为扰乱、破坏

similar_words.description: 均表扰乱，disrupt侧重完全打乱原有秩序或核心进程，disturb侧重轻度干扰或打扰他人

definitions.example: The thick fog around the astronomical observatory dissipated as the sun rose higher.
The graduate student dissipated his research grant on trivial entertainment instead of essential experiments.

dissipate.etymology: 由前缀dis-（分离）、词根sip（投掷）和动词后缀-ate构成，原指扔开分散，后引申为消散、浪费

similar_words.description: 均表分散，dissipate侧重无形事物（如雾、精力）的消散或浪费，disperse侧重有形群体（如人群）的散开

definitions.example: The chemist dissolved the solid reagent in distilled water to prepare the experimental solution.
The academic committee was dissolved after the completion of the national research project.

dissolve.etymology: 由前缀dis-（分离）和词根solv（松开）构成，原指使松开分离，后引申为溶解、解散

similar_words.description: 均表融化/溶解，dissolve侧重固体在液体中的溶解，melt侧重固体受热融化成液体

definitions.example: The short distance between the two universities facilitates regular academic exchanges between professors.
His constant arrogance distanced him from his fellow graduate students in the research team.

distance.etymology: 由前缀dis-（分离）、词根st（站立）和名词后缀-ance构成，原指分开站立的间距，后引申为距离、疏远

similar_words.description: 均表间距，distance侧重空间或抽象关系的距离，interval侧重时间或事件之间的间隔

definitions.example: Astronomers are using advanced telescopes to study distant galaxies and the origin of the universe.
Her distant attitude made it difficult for her to integrate into the graduate student community.

distant.etymology: 由前缀dis-（分离）、词根st（站立）和形容词后缀-ant构成，原指分开站立的，后引申为遥远的、疏远的

similar_words.description: 均表遥远，distant侧重空间或时间上的远距离，remote侧重地理位置偏僻或抽象意义上的渺茫

definitions.example: The laboratory technician distilled pure ethanol from the industrial alcohol for the experiment.
The professor distilled the core arguments of the 600-page academic monograph into a 20-minute lecture.

distill.etymology: 由前缀dis-（分离）和词根still（滴）构成，原指通过滴滤分离物质，后引申为蒸馏、提炼

similar_words.description: 均表提炼，distill侧重通过蒸馏或浓缩获取精华，extract侧重从物质或文本中提取具体成分或信息

definitions.example: There is a distinct difference in the experimental results between the control group and the experimental group.
Each research method has distinct advantages and limitations in social science studies.

distinct.etymology: 由前缀dis-（分离）和词根sting（刺）构成，原指刺开以区分，后引申为清晰的、不同的

similar_words.description: 均含表‘分离’的词根sting，distinct表‘清晰的、不同的’，distinctive表‘有特色的、起区别作用的’，易混淆语义

definitions.example: The distinction between theoretical research and empirical study is clearly reflected in this paper.
She received a distinction for her exceptional performance in the postgraduate entrance examination.

distinction.etymology: dis-（分开）+ stinct（刺，标记）+ -ion（名词后缀），原指“分开标记的特征”，引申为差别、荣誉

similar_words.description: 「distinct」为形容词，表“不同的、清晰的”；「distinction」为名词，表“差别、荣誉”，二者为同根词，易混淆词性

definitions.example: It is crucial to distinguish between correlation and causality in social science research.
His groundbreaking research in genetics distinguished him from other scholars in the field.

distinguish.etymology: dis-（分开）+ stingu（刺，标记）+ -ish（动词后缀），原指“分开标记以区分”，引申为使杰出

similar_words.description: 「distinct」为形容词，表“不同的”；「distinguish」为动词，表“区分”，二者为同根词，易混淆词性与含义

definitions.example: Some tabloids tend to distort the truth to attract more readers, which is unethical.

distort.etymology: dis-（偏离）+ tort（扭），原指“偏离正常形态的扭曲”，引申为歪曲事实

similar_words.description: 「distort」表“扭曲、歪曲”，词根为tort（扭）；「distract」表“使分心”，词根为tract（拉），二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: Constant notifications on mobile phones can distract students from their review for the postgraduate exam.

distract.etymology: dis-（分开）+ tract（拉），原指“把注意力拉开”，引申为分心

similar_words.description: 「distract」表“使分心”，词根为tract（拉）；「distort」表“扭曲”，词根为tort（扭），二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The distress of the residents affected by the flood prompted the government to take immediate relief measures.
The news of her failure in the exam distressed her for a whole week.

distress.etymology: dis-（不）+ stress（压力），原指“超出承受范围的压力”，引申为痛苦、忧虑

similar_words.description: 「stress」表“压力、强调”；「distress」表“（压力导致的）痛苦、危难”，二者为同根词，易混淆含义

definitions.example: The university will distribute scholarship certificates to outstanding graduates next month.
The research data shows that this species is mainly distributed in tropical rainforests.

distribute.etymology: dis-（分开）+ tribute（给予），原指“分开给予”，引申为分发、分布

similar_words.description: 「contribute」表“贡献、捐赠”；「distribute」表“分发、分布”，二者词根均为tribute（给予），易混淆含义

definitions.example: The new high-tech district has attracted a large number of innovative enterprises since its establishment.

district.etymology: dis-（分开）+ strict（拉，划定），原指“划定界限的区域”，引申为行政区

similar_words.description: 「district」指有明确边界的行政区或特定功能区域；「region」指范围更大的地理区域，二者为近义词易混淆

definitions.example: Please keep quiet so as not to disturb the candidates taking the postgraduate entrance examination.
The sudden change in the exam schedule disturbed many students' review plans.

disturb.etymology: dis-（分开）+ turb（搅动），原指“搅动平静的状态”，引申为打扰、使不安

similar_words.description: 「disturb」侧重打扰平静状态或他人的正常活动；「interrupt」侧重打断正在进行的动作或对话，二者为近义词易混淆

definitions.example: The constant disturbance from the street market made it hard for the students to concentrate on their studies.
The police were dispatched to quell the minor disturbance in the city center.

disturbance.etymology: dis- + turb（搅动）+ -ance（名词后缀），为「disturb」的名词形式，表干扰、骚乱

similar_words.description: 「disturb」为动词，表“打扰”；「disturbance」为名词，表“干扰、骚乱”，二者为同根词，易混淆词性

definitions.example: The farmers dug a ditch to irrigate the corn fields during the dry season.
He ditched his outdated study methods and adopted a more efficient strategy for exam preparation.

ditch.etymology: 源自古英语dic，原指“挖掘的排水沟渠”，后引申为动词“抛弃”

similar_words.description: 「ditch」指小型的排水沟或田间沟渠；「trench」指军事或大型工程的深沟，二者为近义词易混淆

definitions.example: Marine biologists often dive to collect samples of deep-sea coral reefs for ecological research.

definitions.meaning: 潜心研究；投身（某领域）（熟词僻义）
definitions.example: The postgraduate student dived into 19th-century newspaper archives to find unexamined evidence for her thesis.

dive.etymology: 源自古英语「dȳfan」，本义为‘浸入水中’，后引申出‘深入、投身’的抽象义

similar_words.description: 形近易混，dove为名词‘鸽子’，dive为动词/名词表‘跳水；潜水；潜心研究’

definitions.example: The study recruited participants from diverse cultural and socioeconomic backgrounds to ensure representativeness.

diverse.etymology: 源自拉丁语「diversus」，由前缀「di-（分开）」+ 词根「vers（转）」构成，表‘转向不同方向的’，引申为‘多样的’

similar_words.description: 同根易混，diverse侧重事物种类的多元差异，divergent侧重观点或方向的分歧、发散
近义易混，diverse强调差异性的多元，various强调数量多且不同

definitions.example: For postgraduate applicants, listening to light music serves as a pleasant diversion from intense review.
The sudden fire alarm caused a diversion of the audience's attention from the academic lecture.

diversion.etymology: 源自拉丁语「diversio」，由前缀「di-（分开）」+ 词根「vers（转）」+ 名词后缀「-ion」构成，本义为‘转向他处’

similar_words.description: 形近易混，diversion为名词‘消遣；转移’，division为名词‘划分；部门；分歧’

definitions.example: The government diverted emergency funds from infrastructure projects to support pandemic relief efforts.

definitions.meaning: 使娱乐；使消遣（熟词僻义）
definitions.example: The humorous anecdotes in the professor's lecture diverted tired students from their stressful schedule.

divert.etymology: 源自拉丁语「divertere」，由前缀「di-（分开）」+ 词根「vertere（转）」构成，表‘转向他方’

similar_words.description: 同根易混，divert表‘转移；使转向’，convert表‘转变；转换（性质/形式）’

definitions.example: Political ideologies continue to divide the nation's legislative body on key policy issues.
If you divide 36 by 6, you get the integer 6, a basic arithmetic operation tested in quantitative reasoning.

divide.etymology: 源自拉丁语「dividere」，由前缀「di-（分开）」+ 词根「vid（分开）」构成，表‘整体拆分为部分’

similar_words.description: 近义易混，divide侧重有意识地整体划分，split侧重强行劈开或不可逆的分裂

definitions.example: The listed company declared a 15% increase in annual dividend to reward its long-term shareholders.
Consistent daily review habits will pay dividends in the final postgraduate entrance examination.

dividend.etymology: 源自拉丁语「dividendum」，由词根「divid（分开）」+ 名词后缀「-endum」构成，原指‘被分割的财产’，后引申为商务义‘股息’

similar_words.description: 同根易混，dividend为名词‘股息；回报’，divide为动词/名词表‘划分；分歧’

definitions.example: The experienced literary critic could divine the author's hidden political intentions from subtle textual details.
Medieval European cathedrals were designed to evoke a sense of divine awe among worshippers.

divine.etymology: 源自拉丁语「divinus」，由词根「div（神）」构成，原指‘与神相关的’，后引申出动词义‘凭借神力推测’

similar_words.description: 同根易混，divine可作动词表‘推测’或形容词表‘神圣的’，divinity为名词‘神性；神学’

definitions.example: The university's academic division of social sciences offers interdisciplinary programs in sociology and anthropology.

division.etymology: 源自拉丁语「divisio」，由词根「divid（分开）」+ 名词后缀「-ion」构成，表‘分开的状态或结果’

similar_words.description: 形近易混，division为名词‘划分；部门；分歧’，diversion为名词‘消遣；转移’

definitions.example: Sociological studies show that economic instability is a leading cause of divorce among young urban couples.

definitions.meaning: （思想、事物的）分离；脱离（熟词僻义）
definitions.example: His theoretical framework is a divorce from the empirical data collected in the field study.

divorce.etymology: 源自拉丁语「divortium」，由词根「div（分开）」构成，表‘夫妻分离’，后引申为抽象义‘思想/事物的分离’

similar_words.description: 形近易混，divorce为动词/名词表‘离婚；脱离’，diverse为形容词表‘多样的’

definitions.meaning: 头晕目眩的；使人头晕的
definitions.example: The steep mountain trail and thin high-altitude air made the field researchers feel dizzy and short of breath.

dizzy.etymology: 源自古英语「dysig」，本义为‘愚蠢的’，后因‘头脑不清’的语义关联引申为‘头晕的’

similar_words.description: 形近易混，dizzy为形容词表‘头晕的’，dazzle为动词/名词表‘使眼花；耀眼’

definitions.example: The cargo ship was moored at the dock waiting for customs inspection.
His monthly salary was docked by 10% for being absent three times without leave.

dock.etymology: 源自中古英语docke，原指船舶停泊的围合区域；动词义由「限制泊位」的动作引申为「扣减（薪酬）」

similar_words.description: 「dock」指码头或扣减薪酬，「deck」指甲板或装饰，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: After five years of rigorous laboratory research, she finally obtained her doctorate in molecular genetics.

doctorate.etymology: 由词根「doctor（博士）」加名词后缀「-ate（表身份、职位）」构成，指获得博士头衔所需的学位资格

similar_words.description: 「doctorate」指博士学位（身份资格），「doctor」指医生或博士（具体身份持有者），二者为同根衍生词

definitions.example: The official document issued by the Ministry of Ecology and Environment clarified the new regulations on industrial waste discharge.
The unearthed bamboo slips document the administrative systems of the Western Han Dynasty.

document.etymology: 源自拉丁语docere（教导），加中缀-u-和名词后缀-ment，原指「用以教导的书面材料」，后引申为文件；动词义由「用文件记录」引申而来

similar_words.description: 「document」指具体文件或记录动作，「documentary」指纪实性的（形容词）或纪录片（名词），二者为同根衍生词

definitions.example: The documentary evidence submitted by the plaintiff played a crucial role in winning the lawsuit.
This award-winning documentary explores the long-term impact of deforestation on Amazonian biodiversity.

documentary.etymology: 由词根「document（文件）」加形容词/名词后缀「-ary（表与…相关的事物）」构成，指基于真实文献或事实的内容或作品

similar_words.description: 「documentary」侧重纪实属性或纪实作品，「document」侧重具体书面文件，二者为同根衍生词

definitions.example: The little girl carefully placed her hand-painted porcelain doll on the shelf before going to bed.

doll.etymology: 源自中古英语dol，原指「愚蠢的人」，后因玩偶无自主意识的特征引申为「人形玩具」

similar_words.description: 「doll」指玩偶，「dollop」指（食物的）一团（如a dollop of cream），二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: Quantum communication is a rapidly developing domain in the field of modern physics.

domain.etymology: 源自拉丁语dominus（主人），加名词后缀-ain（表场所、范围），原指「领主的领地」，后引申为「知识或活动的专属范围」

similar_words.description: 「domain」指领域或领地（场所/范围），「dominate」指支配、统治（动作），二者同根于「dom-（主人）」

definitions.example: The ancient Roman Pantheon is renowned for its massive concrete dome and central oculus.

dome.etymology: 源自拉丁语domus（房屋），原指「圆形的房屋顶部」，后泛指各类建筑的圆顶结构

similar_words.description: 「dome」指建筑圆顶，「domestic」指国内的/家庭的，二者同根于「dom-（家、房屋）」

definitions.example: To boost economic recovery, the government has implemented a series of policies to stimulate domestic consumption.

domestic.etymology: 由词根「dom-（家、房屋）」加形容词后缀「-estic」构成，原指「与家庭相关的」，后引申为「国内的（相对于国外的）」

similar_words.description: 「domestic」侧重国内或家庭属性，「dominant」侧重支配、占优势的状态，二者同根于「dom-（主人）」

definitions.meaning: 占优势的，支配的，统治的
definitions.example: In primate societies, the dominant individual usually has priority access to food and mating resources.

dominant.etymology: 由词根「dom-（主人）」加中缀「-in-」和形容词后缀「-ant（表具有…性质的）」构成，指「像主人一样具有支配权的」

similar_words.description: 「dominant」是形容词，表占优势的状态；「dominate」是动词，表支配、统治的动作，二者为同根衍生词

definitions.example: The multinational tech company has dominated the global semiconductor market for nearly two decades.

dominate.etymology: 由词根「dom-（主人）」加中缀「-in-」和动词后缀「-ate（表使成为）」构成，指「使成为主人」，引申为「支配、统治」

similar_words.description: 「dominate」是动词，表支配的动作；「dominant」是形容词，表支配的状态，二者为同根衍生词

definitions.example: Many wealthy individuals donate a portion of their income to non-profit organizations dedicated to environmental protection.

donate.etymology: 源自拉丁语donare（给予），词根don=给，同根词有donor（捐赠者）

similar_words.description: donate侧重无偿捐赠财物、器官等具体物品；contribute侧重贡献（可指财物、精力、文章等，也可表促成）

definitions.example: In some rural areas of developing countries, donkeys are still used as a means of transporting agricultural products.

donkey.etymology: 源自中古英语dunkey，可能与dun（暗褐色）有关，因驴的毛色得名

similar_words.description: donkey指驴，monkey指猴子，二者拼写形近易混

definitions.example: The ancient Mayan prophecy of global doom has been widely misinterpreted by the mass media.
Without effective policy adjustments, the country's economic recession is doomed to continue.

doom.etymology: 源自古英语dom（判决、命运），与词根dom=判断有关，同根词有domain（领域）

similar_words.description: doom指厄运、注定毁灭；gloom指忧郁、昏暗，二者拼写形近易混

definitions.example: A group of homeless people huddled in the doorway of the closed bookstore to avoid the cold rain.

similar_words.description: doorway指门的出入口（门口）；hallway指走廊、门厅（室内通道）

definitions.example: Most freshmen choose to live in the campus dorm to get familiar with their classmates quickly.

dorm.etymology: dormitory的缩写形式，源自拉丁语dormire（睡觉），词根dorm=睡

similar_words.description: dorm是dormitory的非正式缩写，考研阅读中偶见，dormitory为正式用词

definitions.meaning: 休眠的，蛰伏的；（活动、组织）暂停的
definitions.example: The dormant volcano has not erupted for over 500 years, but scientists still monitor its activity closely.
A large amount of dormant capital in the market can be activated to boost the real economy.

dormant.etymology: 源自拉丁语dormire（睡觉），词根dorm=睡，后缀-ant表形容词，意为「处于…状态的」

similar_words.description: dormant指休眠的、蛰伏的；dormitive指催眠的（药物），二者同根dorm=睡

definitions.example: Patients must take the prescribed dose of antibiotics strictly to avoid drug resistance.

dose.etymology: 源自希腊语dosis（给予），与词根don=给有关（和donate同根）

similar_words.description: dose指具体的一剂、一次用量；dosage指（药物的）总剂量、配药比例

definitions.example: The map is marked with small red dots to indicate the locations of ancient archaeological sites.
Small cottages dot the rolling hills along the country road.

dot.etymology: 源自古英语dott（点），为拟声或象形词

similar_words.description: dot指小圆点、小数点；spot指斑点、地点，二者拼写形近易混

definitions.example: Many scholars doubt the authenticity of the newly discovered medieval manuscript.
There is no doubt that renewable energy will play a crucial role in combating climate change.

doubt.etymology: 源自拉丁语dubitare（犹豫），词根dub=二，引申为「犹豫、不确定」，同根词有dubious（可疑的）

similar_words.description: doubt侧重对真实性、正确性的怀疑（无明确证据）；suspect侧重对某人/某事有嫌疑（有一定依据）

definitions.meaning: 鸽子（尤指白鸽，象征和平）
definitions.example: The release of doves during the opening ceremony symbolized the wish for global peace.

definitions.meaning: 鸽派人士（主张和平谈判的政治人物）
definitions.example: The foreign minister, a known dove, advocated for diplomatic solutions to the territorial dispute.

dove.etymology: 源自古英语dufe（鸽子），与日耳曼语族词汇同源

similar_words.description: dove常指白鸽（象征和平）；pigeon指家鸽、野鸽（无特殊象征义）

definitions.meaning: adv. 在楼下；adj. 楼下的
definitions.example: She walked downstairs to prepare breakfast for her roommates in the dormitory.

downstairs.etymology: 合成词，由表方向的成分down（向下）与名词stairs（楼梯）组合而成

similar_words.description: upstairs表示「在楼上」，与downstairs方位相反

definitions.meaning: adv. 在市中心；n. 市中心区
definitions.example: Many young graduates choose to rent apartments downtown for convenient commuting.

downtown.etymology: 合成词，由down（原指城市下游的核心商业区）与名词town（城镇）组合而成

similar_words.description: uptown指「（城市）住宅区（尤指高档区）」，与downtown的商业核心功能不同

definitions.meaning: adj. 向下的；下降的；adv. 向下地
definitions.example: The report shows a downward trend in global carbon emissions over the past decade.

downward.etymology: 派生词，由表方向的成分down（向下）与表方向的后缀-ward组合而成

similar_words.description: upward表示「向上的；上升的」，与downward趋势方向相反

definitions.meaning: v. 打盹；打瞌睡；n. 小睡
definitions.example: During the long lecture on macroeconomics, he couldn’t help but doze off for a few minutes.

doze.etymology: 源自中古英语dozen（困倦），无显性词根词缀的基础动词

similar_words.description: doze表动作「打盹」，dose表名词「剂量；一剂（药）」，词性与语义完全不同

definitions.meaning: n. 一打（12个）；大量（常构成短语dozens of）
definitions.example: Dozens of academic papers on artificial intelligence have been published in this journal this year.

dozen.etymology: 源自古法语dozaine（表12个的数量单位），外来词

similar_words.description: dozen表数量「一打」，doze表动作「打盹」，语义无关联

definitions.meaning: n. 草稿；草案；v. 起草；草拟
definitions.example: The committee has submitted a draft proposal on environmental protection to the local government.
Many young men were called up for the draft during the Second World War.

draft.etymology: 源自中古荷兰语dracht（携带；草拟），派生词

similar_words.description: draft指「草稿；起草」，craft指「手艺；航天器」，语义无关联

definitions.meaning: v. 拖；拉；（时间）拖沓；n. 拖累；拖沓
definitions.example: The cross-border trade negotiation dragged on for months due to conflicting tariff demands.

drag.etymology: 源自古英语dragan（拖曳），基础动词

similar_words.description: drag指「拖；拖沓」，drug指「药物；毒品」，语义完全不同

definitions.meaning: 龙；（喻）悍妇（考研罕见，仅本义为核心）
definitions.example: The dragon is a symbolic creature in traditional Chinese culture, often appearing in classical literary works.

dragon.etymology: 源自古希腊语drakon（大蛇；传说中的龙），外来词

similar_words.description: drag为动/名词表「拖；拖沓」，dragon为名词表「龙」，语义无关联

definitions.meaning: v. 排水；耗尽（资源、精力）；n. 排水管；消耗
definitions.example: Years of excessive industrial exploitation have drained the groundwater resources of this arid region.

drain.etymology: 源自古英语drēahnian（排水），基础动词

similar_words.description: drain指「排水；耗尽」，train指「火车；训练」，语义完全不同

definitions.meaning: 戏剧；戏剧性事件；戏剧艺术
definitions.example: The 2008 global financial crisis was a political and economic drama that reshaped international relations.

drama.etymology: 源自古希腊语drama（行动；戏剧表演），外来词

similar_words.description: drama指「戏剧；戏剧性事件」，trauma指「创伤；精神创伤」，语义完全不同

definitions.meaning: 戏剧性的；急剧的，显著的
definitions.example: The dramatic changes in global temperature have drawn extensive attention from environmental research institutions.

dramatic.etymology: 词根「drama（戏剧）」+ 形容词后缀「-tic」，表“与…相关的”，引申为“急剧的”

similar_words.description: 「dramatic」侧重变化的急剧性或事件的戏剧性，「drastic」侧重措施的严厉性或程度的极端性

definitions.meaning: 将…改编成剧本；戏剧化地表达
definitions.example: The literary scholar was invited to dramatize the classic novel for a national educational drama project.

dramatize.etymology: 词根「drama（戏剧）」+ 动词后缀「-ize（使…化）」，表“使成为戏剧形式”

similar_words.description: 「dramatize」是动词，表改编/戏剧化表达；「dramatic」是形容词，表戏剧性的/急剧的

definitions.example: Many European countries have adopted drastic emission-reduction policies to meet the Paris Agreement targets.

drastic.etymology: 与「drama」同根，源自古希腊语表“有力行动”的词根，引申为“严厉的、极端的”

similar_words.description: 「drastic」侧重措施的严厉性或程度的极端性，「dramatic」侧重变化的急剧性或事件的戏剧性

definitions.example: One major drawback of remote work is the weakening of team cohesion among employees.

drawback.etymology: 合成词「draw（拉）+ back（回）」，原指“阻碍拉拽的因素”，引申为“缺点”

similar_words.description: 「drawback」指缺点、不利条件，「drawer」指抽屉或开票人，二者形近义不同

definitions.example: The researcher stored all her original experimental data in the locked drawer of her office desk.
The drawer of the commercial draft must verify the payee's identity before issuing the document.

drawer.etymology: 词根「draw（拉）」+ 名词后缀「-er（表人/物）」，指“可拉拽的容器（抽屉）”或“执行出票动作的人（开票人）”

similar_words.description: 「drawer」指抽屉或开票人，「drawback」指缺点、不利条件，二者形近义不同

definitions.example: The civil engineering team submitted a precise technical drawing of the high-speed rail station to the construction bureau.

drawing.etymology: 词根「draw（画/拉）」+ 名词后缀「-ing（表动作或结果）」

similar_words.description: 「drawing」侧重素描、工程绘图，「painting」侧重油画、水彩画等上色作品

definitions.example: Many postgraduate candidates dread the comprehensive interview process of top-tier universities.

dread.etymology: 源自古英语「drædan」，表“惧怕、担忧”

similar_words.description: 「dread」表惧怕、担心，「dream」表梦想、做梦，二者形近义不同

definitions.example: Numerous young scholars dream of publishing their research findings in top international academic journals.

dream.etymology: 源自古英语「drēam」，原指“快乐、幻象”，引申为“梦想”

similar_words.description: 「dream」表梦想、做梦，「dread」表惧怕、担心，二者形近义不同

definitions.example: The study reveals a gradual drift of high-tech industries from coastal cities to inland provincial capitals.

drift.etymology: 源自古英语「drift」，原指“水流推动的漂流物”，引申为“趋势”

similar_words.description: 「drift」表漂流、趋势，「draft」表草稿、汇票，二者形近义不同

definitions.example: Science graduate students must undergo strict experimental drill to master core research techniques.

drill.etymology: 源自古高地德语「drillen」，原指“钻孔”，引申为“反复训练”

similar_words.description: 「drill」表钻孔、训练，「drift」表漂流、趋势，二者形近义不同

definitions.example: Water drips continuously from the cracked pipe in the abandoned factory, forming a tiny pool on the concrete floor.
The nurse adjusted the IV drip to ensure the patient received the correct dosage of medication.

drip.etymology: 源自古英语「dryppan」，为拟声词源，模拟液体滴落的声音

similar_words.description: 「drip」侧重连续缓慢的小量滴落，「drop」可指单次滴落或任意量的液滴/下降

definitions.example: Global sea levels may drop slightly in some polar regions due to increased ice accumulation, but the overall trend is upward.
A sharp drop in consumer confidence has led to a significant reduction in retail sales this quarter.

drop.etymology: 源自古英语「dropa」（液滴）和「droppian」（落下），为拟声词源

similar_words.description: 「drop」适用范围更广，可指物体下落、数值下降等，「drip」仅指液体的小量滴落

definitions.example: The prolonged drought in the Midwest has reduced corn production by 25%, pushing up food prices nationwide.

drought.etymology: 源自古英语「drūgath」，由表“干燥”的词根「drūg」（同「dry」的词源）派生，指长期无雨的灾害性天气

similar_words.description: 「drought」指干旱灾害，「famine」指因灾害等导致的粮食短缺饥荒

definitions.example: The loud engine noise of the passing truck drowned out the speaker’s words during the outdoor press conference.

drown.etymology: 源自古英语「druncnian」，表“使沉浸、溺亡”，与「drink」的词源相关

similar_words.description: 「drown」侧重生物溺亡或声音被掩盖，「sink」侧重物体在液体中下沉

definitions.example: Clinical trials are underway to test the effectiveness of the new drug in treating rare neurological diseases.
The detective suspected that the victim had been drugged before the robbery to prevent resistance.

drug.etymology: 源自古法语「drogue」，原指“干草药”，后引申为药物、毒品

similar_words.description: 「drug」可指合法药物或非法毒品，「medicine」仅指用于治病的药物

definitions.example: The engineer inspected the large steel drum used to store industrial chemicals in the factory.
He drummed his fingers on the desk impatiently while waiting for the job interview to start.

drum.etymology: 源自中古荷兰语「tromme」，为拟声词源，模拟打鼓的咚咚声

similar_words.description: 「drum」指鼓或打鼓，「drone」指无人机、雄蜂或持续的嗡嗡声

definitions.example: The police officer administered a breathalyzer test to the driver who appeared drunk after the traffic accident.
A homeless drunk was found sleeping on the bench in the city park early in the morning.

drunk.etymology: 由动词「drink」的过去分词转化而来，表“因饮酒过量而醉的”

similar_words.description: 「drunk」由「drink」派生，指醉酒状态或醉汉，「drink」指“喝”或饮料

definitions.meaning: 怀疑的，无把握的；可疑的
definitions.example: Most peer reviewers are dubious about the study’s conclusion, as its sample size is far too small to be statistically significant.

dubious.etymology: 源自拉丁语「dubius」，表“动摇的、不确定的”，由表“二”的词根「duo」派生，暗含“在两者间犹豫”的含义

similar_words.description: 「dubious」可指事物可疑或人持怀疑态度，「doubtful」仅指主观上的不确定

definitions.example: Wild ducks migrate to the southern wetlands every winter to avoid the harsh cold of the north.
The journalist ducked behind a wall to avoid being spotted by the security guards at the government building.

duck.etymology: 源自古英语「duce」，原指“会潜水的鸟”，因鸭子擅长潜水得名

similar_words.description: 「duck」指体型较小的鸭子，「goose」指体型较大的鹅

definitions.example: The annual financial report is due on the 15th of next month, so the accounting team is working overtime to finalize it.

due.etymology: 源自拉丁语「debere」（欠），由前缀「de-」（从）+词根「habere」（有）构成，表“应付出的、到期的”

similar_words.description: 「due」是形容词，表“到期的、应得的”，「owe」是动词，表“欠（钱、人情等）”

definitions.example: The dull account of medieval agricultural reforms failed to arouse the readers' interest.

dull.etymology: 源自古英语dol（愚钝的），无复杂词缀，为基础形容词

similar_words.description: dull侧重事物乏味或人反应迟钝；dumb侧重生理上不能说话或不愿说话

definitions.meaning: 哑的，不能说话的；沉默的
definitions.example: The dumb witness could not provide verbal testimony for the court.

dumb.etymology: 源自古英语dumb（哑的），无复杂词缀，为基础形容词

similar_words.description: dumb指生理哑或刻意沉默；dull指事物乏味或人反应慢

definitions.example: The government accused the foreign company of dumping cheap steel in the domestic market.

dump.etymology: 源自荷兰语dompen（倾倒），无复杂词缀，含商务常用义

similar_words.description: dump为动词表倾倒、倾销；damp为形容词表潮湿的

definitions.example: Please submit a duplicate of your graduation certificate along with the application form.

duplicate.etymology: 词根「du-（二）」+「plic（折叠）」+「-ate（形/名后缀）」，表“折叠成两份的”

similar_words.description: duplicate侧重完全相同的正式复制品；copy为通用的复制品

definitions.example: The durable electronic devices produced by this brand enjoy a high reputation among consumers.

durable.etymology: 词根「dur（持续）」+「-able（形后缀，可…的）」，表“可持续使用的”

similar_words.description: durable侧重物品耐用、可持久使用；permanent侧重状态永久不变

definitions.example: The duration of the clinical trial was set to 18 months to collect sufficient data.

duration.etymology: 词根「dur（持续）」+「-ation（名后缀）」，表“持续的状态或时间”

similar_words.description: duration侧重动作/状态持续的时长；period为通用的时间段

definitions.example: The researchers returned to the camp at dusk after a long day of field investigation.

dusk.etymology: 源自古英语dox（黑暗的），无复杂词缀，为基础名词

similar_words.description: dusk指日落前后的黄昏；dawn指日出前后的黎明

definitions.example: The old academic books on the shelf were covered with a thick layer of dust.

dust.etymology: 源自古英语dust（灰尘），无复杂词缀，为基础词

similar_words.description: dust指灰尘或擦灰；dusk指黄昏

definitions.example: It is the duty of every citizen to abide by the law and protect the environment.
The government imposed a 10% import duty on luxury goods to regulate domestic consumption.

duty.etymology: 源自古法语devoir（义务），与「due（应得的）」同根，含商务常用义

similar_words.description: duty侧重道德/法律责任，也可指关税；obligation侧重因合同/承诺产生的义务

definitions.example: The classic fairy tale depicts seven dwarfs who assist a homeless princess.

definitions.meaning: 使显得矮小，使相形见绌
definitions.example: The newly built skyscraper dwarfs all the surrounding old buildings in the financial district.

dwarf.etymology: 源自古英语dweorg（矮神），无复杂词缀，动词义为考研熟词僻义

similar_words.description: dwarf可作名词（矮子）或动词（使相形见绌）；midget仅作名词指侏儒，口语化

definitions.meaning: 居住；栖息（尤指长久居住）
definitions.example: Numerous indigenous communities still dwell in remote mountainous regions of South America.
Candidates should not dwell on minor mistakes during the postgraduate entrance exam interview.

dwell.etymology: 源于古英语dwellan，本义为「停留、逗留」，后引申为长久居住或反复思考。

similar_words.description: 均含「居住」义，dwell侧重长久安稳的居住或栖息，reside更正式，常指合法固定居住。
形近易混，dwell表居住/细想，swell表肿胀/增强。

definitions.meaning: 住处；住宅（尤指长期居住的场所）
definitions.example: Archaeologists uncovered a well-preserved primitive dwelling dating back to the Bronze Age.

dwelling.etymology: 动词dwell的动名词派生词，由「居住」的动作引申为居住的场所。

similar_words.description: 均为「住宅」，dwelling侧重普通居住场所，residence更正式，常指官方或高端住宅。

definitions.example: This natural dye extracted from indigo plants is widely used in traditional textile production.
Many young people dye their hair in various colors to express individuality.

dye.etymology: 源于古英语deag，本义为「颜色」，后引申为染料或染色动作。

similar_words.description: 形近易混，dye表染料/染色，die表死亡/消逝。

definitions.meaning: 动态的；有活力的；动力的
definitions.example: The dynamic development of digital economy has become a key driver of global economic growth.

dynamic.etymology: 源于希腊语dynamis「力量」，后引申为与动力、活力、动态变化相关的含义。

similar_words.description: 均含「有活力的」义，dynamic侧重事物的动态变化或发展活力，energetic侧重人的精力充沛。

definitions.example: The Song Dynasty is famous for its remarkable achievements in science, technology and literature.

dynasty.etymology: 源于希腊语dynasteia「统治权」，后指由同一家族世代统治的王朝或朝代。

similar_words.description: 均与统治相关，dynasty指统治的家族朝代，empire指疆域广阔的帝国政权。

definitions.example: Most postgraduate candidates are eager to receive admission notifications from their target institutions.

eager.etymology: 源于古法语aigre「尖锐的、急切的」，后引申为对事物充满渴望的状态。

similar_words.description: 均含「急切的」义，eager侧重积极主动的渴望，anxious侧重因担忧或不安而急切。

definitions.meaning: 鹰（尤指体型较大的猛禽）
definitions.example: The bald eagle is recognized as the national bird of the United States.

eagle.etymology: 源于古法语aigle，源自拉丁语aquila「鹰」，指体型较大的猛禽。

similar_words.description: 均为猛禽，eagle指体型较大的鹰，hawk指体型较小的隼或鹰类。

definitions.meaning: 赚得；获得（成绩、荣誉等，强调通过努力）
definitions.example: She earned a national scholarship for her outstanding academic performance in college.

earn.etymology: 源于古英语earnian「赚得、应得」，强调通过自身努力获得报酬或荣誉。

similar_words.description: 均含「获得」义，earn侧重通过努力或劳动获得，gain侧重通过各种方式（包括偶然）获得。

definitions.example: The earnest research attitude of the team led to the breakthrough in the experiment.
The buyer paid an earnest to secure the right to purchase the rare academic book collection.

earnest.etymology: 源于古英语eornost「认真的」，名词义由「诚挚的承诺」引申为保证交易的定金。

similar_words.description: 均含「认真的」义，earnest侧重态度诚挚恳切，serious侧重严肃严谨的状态。

definitions.example: The 2008 Wenchuan earthquake caused massive damage to local infrastructure and residents' lives.

earthquake.etymology: 合成词，由earth「地球、大地」+quake「震动」组合而成，指大地的剧烈震动。

similar_words.description: 均指地面震动，earthquake指大规模破坏性地震，tremor指轻微震动或小地震。

definitions.example: She completed the complex translation task with remarkable ease.
The new subsidy policy helped ease the financial burden on rural households.

ease.etymology: 源自古法语aise，原指‘闲暇、舒适’，无明显可拆分的现代英语词缀

similar_words.description: easel指画架，与ease拼写仅差一个字母l，易形近混淆

definitions.meaning: 复活节（基督教纪念耶稣复活的重大节日）
definitions.example: Many Western families attend church services and exchange eggs during Easter.

Easter.etymology: 源自古英语Ēastre，原指日耳曼民族的春季女神节庆，后被基督教借用指复活节

similar_words.description: Easter为专有名词指复活节；eastern为形容词指东部的，拼写相近易混

definitions.example: The eastern coastal provinces of China have developed rapidly in recent decades.

eastern.etymology: 由名词east（东）加形容词后缀-ern（表‘方向的’）构成

similar_words.description: eastern为形容词指东部的；Easter为专有名词指复活节，拼写相近易混

definitions.example: The ebb of public support forced the politician to withdraw his proposal.
Enthusiasm for the online learning platform began to ebb after technical glitches occurred frequently.

ebb.etymology: 源自古英语ebba，原指海水退潮，后引申为抽象的‘衰退、衰落’

similar_words.description: ebb常与flow构成固定搭配ebb and flow，表‘起伏、兴衰’，flow为流动，与ebb语义相反但常搭配使用

definitions.example: The eccentric scientist always wore a lab coat with colorful patches and talked to his test tubes.
The small town was known for its reclusive eccentrics who lived in the old castle.

eccentric.etymology: 由前缀ec-（表‘偏离、出’）加形容词centric（中心的）构成，原指偏离中心的，后引申为行为反常的

similar_words.description: eccentric表古怪的；ecstatic表狂喜的，均以ec-开头，易形近混淆

definitions.example: The echo of her laughter lingered in the empty concert hall for several seconds.
The findings of the recent survey echo the conclusions of the 2022 academic study on youth employment.

echo.etymology: 源自古希腊神话人物Ēkhō（回声女神），原指声音的反射，后引申为观点的呼应

similar_words.description: echo表回响、呼应，可指声音或观点；reverberate表（声音）回响、回荡，更强调持续的震动效果

definitions.example: A total lunar eclipse will be visible across most of North America on October 8.
The young athlete's world record performance eclipsed the achievements of all her predecessors.

eclipse.etymology: 由前缀ec-（表‘出’）加词根lipse（表‘离开’，源自希腊语leipein）构成，原指天体被遮蔽，后引申为使…黯然失色

similar_words.description: eclipse表日食/使黯然失色；ellipse表椭圆，拼写相近易混（仅第二个字母c与l不同）

definitions.example: The research project focuses on the impact of plastic waste on the marine ecology of the South Pacific.

ecology.etymology: 由前缀eco-（表‘生态、家园’，源自希腊语oikos）加后缀-logy（表‘学科’）构成，指研究生物与环境关系的学科

similar_words.description: ecology表生态学；economy表经济，均以eco-开头，易形近混淆

definitions.meaning: 经济的；经济学的；与经济相关的
definitions.example: The central bank adjusted interest rates to maintain stable economic growth amid global uncertainty.

economic.etymology: 由前缀eco-（表‘家园、管理’）加形容词后缀-nomic（表‘与…管理相关的’）构成，指与经济或经济学相关的

similar_words.description: economic表经济的、经济学的（侧重宏观经济或学科领域）；economical表节约的、划算的（侧重成本效益或节俭）

definitions.example: Choosing public transport is more economical than driving a private car for daily commuting.

economical.etymology: 由形容词economic加后缀-al构成，侧重指节约、划算的，而非宏观经济相关的

similar_words.description: economical表节约的、划算的（侧重个体成本或节俭）；economic表经济的、经济学的（侧重宏观经济或学科）

definitions.example: Economics is a compulsory course for postgraduate candidates majoring in business administration.

economics.etymology: 由形容词「economic」（经济的）加名词后缀「-ics」构成，「-ics」常用于表示学科类名词

similar_words.description: 「economics」指经济学学科，「economy」指经济状况或经济体

definitions.example: Many developing countries are striving to boost their domestic economy amid global trade frictions.

economy.etymology: 源自希腊语「oikonomia」（家庭管理），后演变为指代经济相关概念，与「economic」「economics」为同根词

similar_words.description: 「economy」指经济状况或经济体，「economics」指经济学学科

definitions.example: On the edge of the industrial zone, there is a wetland park that provides habitat for migratory birds.
Enterprises with core technological patents can gain a decisive edge in the global market.

edge.etymology: 源自古英语「ecg」（边缘），无明显可拆分的现代英语词缀

similar_words.description: 「edge」多指有竞争力的微弱核心优势，「advantage」为通用优势

definitions.example: Researchers have confirmed that most of the wild berries in this protected area are edible and non-toxic.

edible.etymology: 由词根「ed-」（吃，源自拉丁语「edere」）加形容词后缀「-ible」（可…的）构成

similar_words.description: 「edible」为正式学术用语（考研阅读常用），「eatable」为口语化表达

definitions.example: The professor asked his research assistant to edit the final draft of the paper to meet the journal's formatting requirements.

edit.etymology: 由前缀「e-」（出）加词根「dit」（给，源自拉丁语「dare」）构成，原指给出整理好的内容，后演变为编辑

similar_words.description: 「edit」侧重内容的整理、删减、排版，「revise」侧重修改错误或更新内容

definitions.example: The revised edition of the sociological monograph includes empirical case studies from emerging economies.

edition.etymology: 由动词「edit」（编辑）加名词后缀「-ion」构成，指编辑后的版本

similar_words.description: 「edition」多指书籍、报刊的版次，「version」侧重同一内容的不同变体（如译本、改编版）

definitions.example: The academic editor must ensure that all submitted papers comply with the journal's ethical and formatting standards.

editor.etymology: 由动词「edit」（编辑）加名词后缀「-or」（表人）构成，指从事编辑工作的人

similar_words.description: 「editor」负责内容的编辑审核，「publisher」负责出版发行

definitions.example: The editorial board of the top international journal rejected the paper due to methodological flaws.
The editorial in the British Medical Journal called for stricter regulations on tobacco advertising.

editorial.etymology: 由名词「editor」（编辑）加形容词后缀「-ial」构成，作名词时指编辑撰写的代表编辑部观点的社论

similar_words.description: 「editorial」特指报刊的社论（代表编辑部观点），「article」为普通文章

definitions.example: The government has launched a series of programs to educate rural children in digital literacy.

educate.etymology: 由前缀「e-」（出）加词根「duc」（引导，源自拉丁语「ducere」）加动词后缀「-ate」构成，原指引导学生学习，即教育

similar_words.description: 「educate」侧重系统的知识教育，「cultivate」侧重培养品质、技能或兴趣

definitions.example: Higher education plays a pivotal role in enhancing a country's overall innovation capacity.

education.etymology: 由动词「educate」（教育）加名词后缀「-ion」构成，指教育的行为或过程

similar_words.description: 「education」为广义的教育（含学校、家庭、社会），「schooling」特指学校教育

definitions.example: The new environmental policy has exerted a far-reaching effect on urban air quality.
The committee effected a series of reforms to streamline the administrative approval process.

effect.etymology: 前缀「ef-」（向外）+ 词根「fect」（做、制作），字面义为「做出的结果」，引申为「效果、影响」；作动词时表「做出既定结果→实现」

similar_words.description: 「affect」多作动词表「影响」，「effect」多作名词表「效果、影响」，作动词表「实现」，二者常因拼写接近混淆

definitions.example: Regular physical exercise is an effective way to enhance mental resilience.

effective.etymology: 名词「effect」（效果）+ 形容词后缀「-ive」（有…性质的），表「有效果的」

similar_words.description: 「effective」强调「产生预期效果的」，「efficient」强调「高效的、省时省力的」

definitions.example: The manufacturing plant introduced intelligent equipment to improve production efficiency.

efficiency.etymology: 形容词「efficient」（高效的）+ 名词后缀「-ency」（性质、状态），表「高效的状态→效率」

similar_words.description: 「efficiency」侧重「做事的投入产出比即效率」，「effectiveness」侧重「达成目标的有效性」

definitions.example: A well-structured workflow makes the research team more efficient.

efficient.etymology: 前缀「ef-」（向外）+ 词根「fic」（做）+ 形容词后缀「-ient」，字面义为「能做出成果的」，引申为「高效的」

similar_words.description: 「efficient」强调「高效、省时省力」，「effective」强调「产生预期效果」

definitions.example: Archaeologists made great efforts to unravel the mystery of the ancient site.

effort.etymology: 前缀「ef-」（向外）+ 词根「fort」（力量），字面义为「使出的力量」，引申为「努力」

similar_words.description: 「effort」为普通用词，指一般性努力；「endeavor」为正式用词，尤指长期、坚定的努力

definitions.example: Excessive ego may hinder one's ability to accept constructive criticism.

ego.etymology: 源自古希腊语，原指「我」，后引入心理学领域表「自我意识」

similar_words.description: 「ego」侧重心理学意义上的「自我意识」，「self」侧重泛指的「自我、本身」

definitions.example: The study recruited eighteen participants aged between 20 and 35.

eighteen.etymology: 基数词「eight」（八）+ 后缀「-teen」（表十），表「八加十」

similar_words.description: 「eighteen」是基数词「十八」，「eighty」是基数词「八十」，二者因拼写接近易混淆

definitions.example: Eighty percent of the respondents supported the new traffic regulation.

eighty.etymology: 基数词「eight」（八）+ 后缀「-ty」（表十的倍数），表「八个十」

similar_words.description: 「eighty」是基数词「八十」，「eighteen」是基数词「十八」，二者因拼写接近易混淆

definitions.example: The security guard ejected the disruptive spectator from the sports stadium.

eject.etymology: 前缀「e-」（向外）+ 词根「ject」（扔），字面义为「扔出去」，引申为「驱逐、弹出」

similar_words.description: 「eject」表「驱逐、弹出」，「reject」表「拒绝、驳回」，二者同根「ject」但语义不同

definitions.example: The scholar presented an elaborate analysis of the social data trends.
The professor asked the student to elaborate on her research hypothesis.

elaborate.etymology: 前缀「e-」（向外）+ 词根「labor」（工作）+ 后缀「-ate」，字面义为「做出大量工作的」，引申为「详尽的」；作动词时表「投入大量工作→精心制作、详细说明」

similar_words.description: 「elaborate」侧重「详细阐述、精心制作」，「illustrate」侧重「举例说明、图解」

definitions.example: Years elapse before the long-term ecological effects of the mining project are fully evaluated.

elapse.etymology: e-（向外）+ laps（滑、走）+ 词尾e → 时间滑走 → 流逝

similar_words.description: 「elapse」为不及物动词，仅指时间流逝；「lapse」可作名词/动词，表失误、权利终止或短暂的时间流逝

definitions.example: The government has adopted an elastic fiscal policy to cope with the fluctuating economic situation.

elastic.etymology: elast（伸展）+ -ic（形容词后缀）→ 可伸展的 → 有弹性的

similar_words.description: 「elastic」侧重物理弹性或政策的可调整性；「flexible」侧重人的适应能力或事物的易弯曲性

definitions.example: She had to elbow her way through the crowded conference hall to reach the keynote speaker.

elbow.etymology: 源自古英语「elnboga」，与古英语「eln」（肘长，长度单位）相关

similar_words.description: 「elbow」指肘或用肘挤；「bow」指弓/鞠躬，小写时发音有/baʊ/（鞠躬）与/bəʊ/（弓）之分

definitions.meaning: （尤指家庭成员中）年龄较大的；长辈
definitions.example: The elder sister was entrusted with managing the family’s charitable foundation.

elder.etymology: 源自古英语「ieldra」，为「old」的比较级形式，特指群体内年长的

similar_words.description: 「elder」仅指群体内（尤指家庭成员）的年长者，作定语；「older」可指任何人/物的年龄更大，可作定语/表语

definitions.meaning: 上了年纪的（委婉语）；老年人
definitions.example: The municipal government has launched a home care program for elderly residents living alone.

elderly.etymology: elder（年长的）+ -ly（形容词后缀）→ 上了年纪的

similar_words.description: 「elderly」为委婉语，指老年群体；「old」为通用词，可指任何年龄大的或陈旧的事物

definitions.example: The academic committee will elect a new director to oversee the national research project.
The president-elect will hold a press conference to outline his policy agenda next week.

elect.etymology: e-（出）+ lect（选）→ 从群体中选出 → 选举

similar_words.description: 「elect」侧重官方或集体的投票选举；「select」侧重精心挑选（不一定通过投票）

definitions.example: The transportation department is promoting the use of electric vehicles to reduce air pollution.

electric.etymology: electr（电）+ -ic（形容词后缀）→ 与电直接相关的

similar_words.description: 「electric」侧重直接用电或带电的物品（如electric fan）；「electrical」侧重与电相关的学科、设备的电气属性

definitions.example: He obtained a master’s degree in electrical engineering from a renowned technical university.

electrical.etymology: electr（电）+ -ical（形容词后缀，表与…相关的）→ 与电相关的

similar_words.description: 「electrical」侧重电的学科领域或设备的电气性能；「electric」侧重直接使用电力的物品

definitions.example: An experienced electrician was summoned to repair the faulty circuit in the research laboratory.

electrician.etymology: electr（电）+ -ician（从事某专业的人）→ 从事电气工作的人 → 电工

similar_words.description: 「electrician」指人（电工/电气技师）；「electronics」指学科（电子学）

definitions.example: Many remote rural areas in the country still lack access to stable electricity due to inadequate infrastructure.

electricity.etymology: electr（电）+ -icity（名词后缀，表属性）→ 电的属性 → 电、电能

similar_words.description: 「electricity」指宏观的电能、电力；「electron」指微观的电子（物理粒子）

definitions.example: An electron is a subatomic particle with a negative electric charge, which is a core concept in college physics courses for science and engineering majors.

electron.etymology: 词根「electr-」（电）+ 名词后缀「-on」（表微观粒子）

similar_words.description: 「electron」指带负电的电子，「neutron」指不带电的中子，均为亚原子粒子，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.meaning: 电子的；与电子设备相关的
definitions.example: Most academic conferences now require electronic versions of research papers for initial submission and peer review.

electronic.etymology: 词根「electr-」（电）+ 形容词后缀「-onic」（表与…相关的）

similar_words.description: 「electronic」侧重与电子、电子器件相关（如electronic mail）；「electrical」侧重与电力、电路相关（如electrical equipment）

definitions.example: The professor’s elegant dressing and clear speech left a deep impression on the audience at the international symposium.

definitions.meaning: （方案、论证）精妙的，简洁有效的
definitions.example: The economist proposed an elegant model to explain the correlation between inflation and employment rates.

elegant.etymology: 前缀「e-」（向外，引申为出众）+ 词根「leg-」（选择，引申为精致）+ 形容词后缀「-ant」

similar_words.description: 「elegant」侧重举止、设计的雅致精巧且得体；「graceful」侧重动作、姿态的优美自然

definitions.meaning: 要素；基本组成部分；化学元素
definitions.example: Mutual respect is an essential element in building sustainable cross-cultural communication channels.

element.etymology: 词根「elem-」（基础）+ 名词后缀「-ent」，原指事物的基础组成部分

similar_words.description: 「element」侧重事物的抽象基本要素或化学元素；「component」侧重机器、系统的具体组成部件

definitions.meaning: 基本的；初级的；基础的（知识、教育）
definitions.example: This textbook covers elementary principles of genetics for first-year biology students.

elementary.etymology: 单词「element」（基础）+ 形容词后缀「-ary」（表与…相关的）

similar_words.description: 「elementary」侧重知识、技能的入门基础级；「primary」侧重顺序上的首要、最初的

definitions.meaning: 大象（陆地大型哺乳动物）
definitions.example: Wildlife conservationists are working to protect the remaining habitats of African elephants from human encroachment.

elephant.etymology: 源自希腊语「elephas」，原指象牙，后引申为大象

similar_words.description: 「elephant」指大型哺乳动物大象；「phantom」指幻影、幽灵，拼写后半部分相近易混淆

definitions.meaning: 提升；抬高；提高（地位、水平、品质）
definitions.example: The groundbreaking research may elevate the status of renewable energy in the global energy structure.

elevate.etymology: 前缀「e-」（向上）+ 词根「lev-」（举起，轻）+ 动词后缀「-ate」

similar_words.description: 「elevate」侧重正式提升地位、水平或抬高抽象事物；「lift」侧重用体力或机械举起具体物体

definitions.example: The high-rise office building is equipped with 12 high-speed elevators to meet the daily commuting needs of employees.

elevator.etymology: 动词「elevate」（提升）+ 名词后缀「-or」（表工具、设备）

similar_words.description: 「elevator」为美式英语的‘电梯’；「lift」为英式英语的‘电梯’，需注意语域差异

definitions.meaning: 有资格的；符合条件的（申请、参选等）
definitions.example: Only students with a GPA of 3.5 or above are eligible to apply for the university’s merit-based scholarship.

eligible.etymology: 前缀「e-」（向外，引申为符合）+ 词根「lig-」（选择）+ 形容词后缀「-ible」（表可…的）

similar_words.description: 「eligible」侧重符合特定外部条件（如身份、分数）而有资格；「qualified」侧重具备所需技能、学历而合格

definitions.meaning: 消除；排除；淘汰（对手、障碍、贫困等）
definitions.example: The United Nations has set a target to eliminate extreme poverty in all its forms by 2030.

eliminate.etymology: 前缀「e-」（向外，离开）+ 词根「limin-」（界限，门槛，引申为排除）+ 动词后缀「-ate」

similar_words.description: 「eliminate」侧重彻底消除、排除或淘汰（如对手、贫困）；「remove」侧重移走、去除具体或抽象事物

definitions.example: Members of the scientific elite are often invited to advise government agencies on technological innovation policies.

elite.etymology: e-(出)+lit(=lect 选)+-ite(表人/物的后缀)→被选拔出的人→精英

similar_words.description: 「elite」指被选拔出的精英，「eligible」指有资格被选拔的

definitions.example: The eloquent speech delivered by the environmental activist moved the audience to support reforestation efforts.

eloquent.etymology: e-(出)+loqu(=speak 说)+-ent(形容词后缀)→善于表达的→雄辩的

similar_words.description: 「eloquent」侧重言辞有感染力说服力，「articulate」侧重表达清晰有条理

definitions.example: When local resources are exhausted, researchers must look elsewhere for viable solutions to energy shortages.

elsewhere.etymology: else(别的)+where(哪里)→别的地方→在别处

similar_words.description: 「elsewhere」指特定范围外的别处，「anywhere」指无范围限制的任何地方

definitions.meaning: 开始，着手（尤指新的重要事务）
definitions.example: After graduating, many students embark on a journey of self-discovery and professional exploration.

embark.etymology: em-(进入)+bark(=ship 船)→登船→引申为开始（事务）

similar_words.description: 「embark」指开始着手或登乘，「embed」指嵌入、植入

definitions.example: The unexpected question from the interviewer did not embarrass the well-prepared candidate.

embarrass.etymology: em-(进入)+barr(=bar 障碍)+-ass(动词后缀)→被障碍挡住→陷入困境→使尴尬

similar_words.description: 「embarrass」指使尴尬窘迫，「embargo」指贸易禁运（名/动）

definitions.example: The Chinese embassy in Washington regularly organizes cultural exchange events to promote mutual understanding.

embassy.etymology: em-(进入)+bassy(=basis 基础，指外交基础机构)→大使馆

similar_words.description: 「embassy」指大使馆机构，「ambassador」指派驻外国的大使

definitions.example: Sensors are embedded in the bridge structure to monitor its structural integrity in real time.

embed.etymology: em-(进入)+bed(床，引申为凹槽、基础)→放入凹槽→嵌入

similar_words.description: 「embed」指嵌入植入，「embark」指开始着手或登乘

definitions.example: The design of the new museum embodies the cultural heritage and modern aesthetic tastes of the region.

similar_words.description: 「embody」侧重具体体现抽象品质思想，「represent」侧重代表象征

definitions.example: More and more rural schools are embracing digital learning platforms to enhance teaching quality.

embrace.etymology: em-(进入)+brace(=arm 臂)→进入臂弯→拥抱→引申为欣然接受

similar_words.description: 「embrace」侧重主动欣然接受，「adopt」侧重正式采纳采用

definitions.example: A new generation of renewable energy technologies has emerged as a viable alternative to fossil fuels.

emerge.etymology: e-(出)+merge(=sink 沉)→从沉没状态出来→出现

similar_words.description: 「emerge」指出现浮现，「immerge」指浸入沉入

definitions.example: During the COVID-19 pandemic, many countries activated their national emergency response systems immediately.

emergency.etymology: 前缀e-（出）+ 词根merg（沉）+ 名词后缀-ency，原指“突然浮出水面的突发状况”

similar_words.description: 均含“紧急”义，emergency指具体的紧急事件或状况，urgency指抽象的紧急性

definitions.example: Emerging technologies such as artificial intelligence are reshaping the global job market.

emerging.etymology: 前缀e-（出）+ 词根merg（沉）+ 形容词后缀-ing，由动词emerge（浮现）派生而来

similar_words.description: 拼写相近，emerging指事物新兴的，eminent指人杰出的

definitions.meaning: 移居国外，移民（从本国移出）
definitions.example: Many skilled workers emigrate from developing countries to seek better career opportunities in Western nations.

emigrate.etymology: 前缀e-（出）+ 词根migr（迁移）+ 动词后缀-ate，与immigrate（移入他国）相对

similar_words.description: 均表“移民”，emigrate指从本国移出，immigrate指移入他国

definitions.example: The eminent historian was invited to deliver a keynote speech at the international academic conference.

eminent.etymology: 前缀e-（出）+ 词根min（突出）+ 形容词后缀-ent，原指“超出常人的”

similar_words.description: 拼写相近，eminent指人杰出的，emerging指事物新兴的

definitions.meaning: 发出，射出（光、热、气体等）；散发
definitions.example: Industrial factories often emit large amounts of carbon dioxide, contributing to global warming.

emit.etymology: 前缀e-（出）+ 词根mit（送、放），词根mit意为“发送”

similar_words.description: 拼写相近，emit指发出，omit指省略、遗漏

definitions.example: The author uses vivid metaphors to convey the complex emotion of loneliness in his novel.

emotion.etymology: 前缀e-（出）+ 词根mot（移动）+ 名词后缀-ion，原指“内心涌动的感受”

similar_words.description: 均含“情感”义，emotion泛指各种情绪，affection特指喜爱、慈爱

definitions.example: The ancient Roman emperor issued a decree to expand the empire's territory across Europe.

emperor.etymology: 源自拉丁语imperator（指挥官），后引申为“皇帝”，与empire同根

similar_words.description: 均指君主，emperor指帝国的皇帝，king指王国的国王

definitions.example: Many educational institutions place great emphasis on students' critical thinking skills rather than rote memorization.

emphasis.etymology: 前缀em-（使）+ 词根phas（显示）+ 名词后缀-is，原指“使突出显示”

similar_words.description: 同根词，emphasis为名词“强调”，emphasize为动词“强调”

definitions.example: The report emphasizes that immediate action is needed to address the issue of water pollution.

emphasize.etymology: 前缀em-（使）+ 词根phas（显示）+ 动词后缀-ize，由名词emphasis派生而来

similar_words.description: 同根词，emphasize为动词“强调”，emphasis为名词“强调”

definitions.example: The British Empire was once the largest colonial empire in world history.

empire.etymology: 源自拉丁语imperium（统治权），与emperor同根

similar_words.description: 均指国家，empire指由皇帝统治的帝国，kingdom指由国王统治的王国

definitions.meaning: 以经验为依据的；经验主义的
definitions.example: These empirical findings are widely cited in peer-reviewed journals of behavioral science.

empirical.etymology: 由前缀em-（进入）+ 词根pir（尝试、经验）+ 形容词后缀-ical构成

similar_words.description: 为同根名词，指经验主义者；「empirical」为形容词，表以经验为依据的

definitions.example: Many high-tech companies employ top talents with competitive salaries and benefits.
The study employs a mixed-methods approach to analyze the survey data comprehensively.

employ.etymology: 由前缀em-（使）+ 词根ploy（使用、参与）构成

similar_words.description: 为近义词，「deploy」侧重部署资源、兵力等；「employ」侧重雇用人员或使用方法

definitions.example: Each employee is required to complete the annual occupational safety training program.

employee.etymology: 由动词employ（雇用）+ 名词后缀-ee（表受动者）构成

similar_words.description: 为同根对应词，「employee」是受雇者（雇员），「employer」是雇用者（雇主）

definitions.example: The employer shall pay the employee's salary in full and on time as stipulated in the labor contract.

employer.etymology: 由动词employ（雇用）+ 名词后缀-er（表施动者）构成

similar_words.description: 为同根对应词，「employer」是雇用者（雇主），「employee」是受雇者（雇员）

definitions.example: The government has implemented a series of targeted policies to stabilize employment amid the economic downturn.

employment.etymology: 由动词employ（雇用）+ 名词后缀-ment构成

similar_words.description: 为同根反义词，「employment」指就业状态，「unemployment」指失业状态

definitions.example: The new cloud computing platform enables researchers to process large datasets efficiently.

enable.etymology: 由前缀en-（使）+ 形容词able（能够的）构成

similar_words.description: 为近义词，「enable」侧重使具备做某事的条件或能力，「empower」侧重赋予某人权力或自主权

definitions.example: The archaeological site is enclosed by a high fence to prevent unauthorized entry.
Please enclose a copy of your academic transcript with the graduate school application form.

enclose.etymology: 由前缀en-（使）+ 动词close（关闭）构成

similar_words.description: 为近义词，「enclose」侧重随函附上实物，「attach」侧重在文件上附加内容（如电子附件）

definitions.example: The national wildlife reserve has a spacious enclosure for the protection of endangered red-crowned cranes.
The official letter from the university came with an enclosure of three recommendation letters.

enclosure.etymology: 由动词enclose（围起、附上）+ 名词后缀-ure构成

similar_words.description: 为近义词，「enclosure」多指随函的实物附件，「attachment」多指电子文档或邮件的附件

definitions.meaning: 遭遇（问题、困难）；偶遇（某人）
definitions.example: The research team encountered unexpected data discrepancies during the field investigation.
Her chance encounter with a renowned linguist at the academic conference inspired her research topic.

encounter.etymology: 由前缀en-（进入）+ 词根counter（面对、相反）构成
