similar_words.description: 为近义词，「encounter」侧重意外或偶然遭遇，「confront」侧重主动面对困难或敌人

definitions.example: College professors should encourage students to challenge established theories rather than accept them blindly.

encourage.etymology: 由前缀en-（使）+ 名词courage（勇气）构成

similar_words.description: 为近义词，「encourage」侧重给予勇气或支持以鼓励行动，「inspire」侧重激发灵感或热情

definitions.example: Many humanities academic papers cite content from authoritative encyclopedias as supplementary reference materials.

encyclopedia.etymology: 前缀en-（使进入）+词根cyclo（环、圈）+词根ped（教育、知识）+后缀-ia（表名词），原指涵盖各领域知识的综合性书籍。

similar_words.description: 英式拼写变体，美式英语常用encyclopedia，英式英语常用encyclopaedia，词义完全一致。

definitions.example: Despite repeated experimental failures, the research team never abandoned their endeavor to develop a new cancer treatment.
The local government endeavors to improve medical service accessibility for residents in remote mountainous areas.

endeavor.etymology: 前缀en-（使）+词根deavor（职责、义务），原指为履行职责所付出的持续努力。

similar_words.description: 英式拼写变体，美式英语常用endeavor，英式英语常用endeavour，词义完全一致。

definitions.example: The open ending of the science fiction film has sparked extensive discussion among audiences and film critics.

ending.etymology: 词根end（结束）+后缀-ing（表名词，指动作结果或状态），指故事、事件等的收尾部分。

similar_words.description: ending侧重故事、活动等具体事件的收尾；conclusion侧重逻辑推理、正式会议等得出的结论或收尾。

definitions.example: The renowned entrepreneur endowed a special research fund for renewable energy technology at his alma mater.
The Nobel Prize winner in physics was endowed with extraordinary mathematical talent from childhood.

endow.etymology: 前缀en-（使）+词根dow（给予、捐赠），原指给予财物或天赋特质。

similar_words.description: endow可指捐赠财物或天生赋予禀赋，是考研常用词；endue为正式书面用词，仅指赋予某种品质，使用频率较低。

definitions.example: Completing the ultra-marathon requires not only strong physical fitness but also extraordinary mental endurance.

endurance.etymology: 前缀en-（使）+词根dur（持续、坚硬）+后缀-ance（表名词，指状态或性质），指长时间忍受困难的能力。

similar_words.description: 二者为同根形近词；endurance指忍受的能力，duration指事件持续的时间长度。

definitions.example: The displaced villagers had to endure food shortages and low temperatures during their temporary relocation.
The traditional hand-weaving craft has endured in this ethnic minority village for over 800 years.

endure.etymology: 前缀en-（使）+词根dur（持续、坚硬），原指使持续、硬扛困境。

similar_words.description: endure侧重长时间忍受重大痛苦或困境，语义较强；tolerate侧重容忍不同的观点或行为，语义较弱。

definitions.example: During the national liberation movement, people from all walks of life united to fight against the common enemy.
Long-term high-sugar diet is a major enemy of human metabolic health.

enemy.etymology: 来自拉丁语inimicus，前缀en-=in-（不）+词根emy=ami（朋友），原指「非朋友」，即敌人。

similar_words.description: enemy为常用词，可指个人、群体或国家的敌人；foe为正式或文学用词，多指死敌，语义更强。

definitions.example: The energetic young scholar often participates in international academic conferences to exchange research ideas.

energetic.etymology: 名词energy（精力）+后缀-etic（表形容词，指「有…性质的」），指充满活力的状态。

similar_words.description: energetic侧重人主观上精力旺盛、充满活力；vigorous侧重身体健壮、行动有力。

definitions.example: Regular moderate exercise can help middle-aged people maintain physical energy and mental alertness.
Global cooperation is essential to promote the transformation of energy structure and reduce carbon emissions.

energy.etymology: 来自希腊语energeia，前缀en-（在…中）+词根erg（工作、活动）+后缀-y（表名词），原指活动的能力，后引申为精力、能源。

similar_words.description: energy可指人的精力或物理、能源领域的能量；power侧重权力、动力或物理上的功率。

definitions.example: The market supervision department has launched a special campaign to enforce food safety regulations.
The conference organizer had to arrange additional staff to enforce order in the overcrowded venue.

enforce.etymology: 前缀en-（使）+词根force（力量、强迫），原指用力量推行规则或要求。

similar_words.description: enforce侧重强制执行已有的规定、法律；impose侧重把要求、负担等强加给他人，常搭配介词on/upon。

definitions.example: Many postgraduates engage in interdisciplinary research to broaden their academic horizons.
The professor’s vivid lecture engaged the students’ full attention throughout the two-hour session.

engage.etymology: 前缀en-（使处于某种状态）+ 词根gage（承诺、担保，源自古法语），本义为‘使作出承诺’，引申为参与、吸引等义

similar_words.description: engage 常接介词in表主动参与，involve 常表被动卷入

definitions.example: The study examines the impact of online learning platforms on college students’ academic engagement.
Their engagement was announced in the local newspaper after a year of dating.

engagement.etymology: 动词engage的名词形式，后缀-ment表行为或状态，指参与的状态或订婚的约定

similar_words.description: engagement 侧重主动参与的状态，commitment 侧重需履行的承诺责任

definitions.example: The new energy vehicle’s electric engine reduces carbon emissions by 40 percent compared to traditional ones.
Basic scientific research is widely recognized as the engine of national technological progress.

engine.etymology: 源自古法语engin（技能、机械装置），原指精巧的机械，后特指发动机，引申为动力义

similar_words.description: engine 侧重提供动力的装置，generator 侧重产生电能的装置

definitions.example: The aerospace engineer designed a new component to improve the rocket’s fuel efficiency.
The opposition party engineered a series of parliamentary debates to challenge the new tax policy.

engineer.etymology: 名词engine（机械）+ 后缀-er（表人），原指制造机械的人，后指工程师；动词义由‘操纵机械’引申为策划

similar_words.description: engineer 侧重工程设计与研发，mechanic 侧重机械维修与调试

definitions.example: She earned a master’s degree in environmental engineering from a top university in Europe.
The investigative report exposed the engineering of public opinion by the media conglomerate.

engineering.etymology: 名词engine（动力、机械）+ 后缀-ing（表学科或行为），指工程学科或策划行为

similar_words.description: engineering 侧重工程实践与设计，technology 侧重技术成果与应用

definitions.example: Regular participation in academic seminars can enhance students’ critical thinking skills.
The museum’s new exhibition of Renaissance art has greatly enhanced its international reputation.

enhance.etymology: 前缀en-（使）+ 词根hance（高，源自古法语haucier），本义为‘使增高’，引申为提高、增强

similar_words.description: enhance 侧重提升质量、价值，increase 侧重数量、规模的增加

definitions.example: The local government plans to enlarge the urban green space to improve residents’ quality of life.
The keynote speaker enlarged on the potential risks of artificial intelligence in the workplace.

enlarge.etymology: 前缀en-（使）+ 形容词large（大的），本义为‘使变大’，引申为扩展、详述

similar_words.description: enlarge 侧重尺寸、空间的扩大，expand 侧重范围、领域的扩张

definitions.example: The award-winning documentary aims to enlighten viewers about the hidden impacts of climate change on coastal communities.

enlighten.etymology: 前缀en-（使）+ 词根light（光）+ 后缀-en（动词后缀），本义为‘使…有光’，引申为启发、开导

similar_words.description: enlighten 侧重传授知识消除无知，inspire 侧重激发情感或创造力

definitions.example: Many young adults enlisted in the military to gain leadership experience and serve their country.
The non-profit organization enlisted the support of local enterprises to fund the rural education project.

enlist.etymology: 前缀en-（使进入）+ 名词list（名单），本义为‘使进入名单’，引申为招募、谋取支持

similar_words.description: enlist 侧重参军或争取支持，enroll 侧重注册入学、入会

definitions.meaning: 巨大的，庞大的（数量、体积、程度）
definitions.example: The construction of the cross-sea bridge required an enormous amount of financial and technical resources.
The new medical breakthrough has brought enormous hope to patients with rare diseases.

enormous.etymology: 前缀e-（ex-，超出）+ 词根norm（标准、规则）+ 后缀-ous（形容词后缀），本义为‘超出常规的’，引申为巨大的

similar_words.description: enormous 侧重超出常规的大，immense 侧重无法衡量的规模大

definitions.example: Students may enquire about course registration procedures via the official university portal.

enquire.etymology: 前缀en-（使动）+ 词根quir（=quer，寻求）+ 后缀-e，字面义为寻求答案

similar_words.description: 二者为同词义的拼写变体，enquire多用于英式非正式语境，inquire为通用正式用法

definitions.example: Reading extensively can enrich one's spiritual world and broaden one's horizons.

enrich.etymology: 前缀en-（使成为）+ 单词rich（丰富的，富裕的），为使动结构

similar_words.description: enrich侧重使内容、内涵更丰富，enhance侧重使价值、水平、吸引力等提升

definitions.example: The university plans to enroll 3000 undergraduate students for the upcoming academic year.

enroll.etymology: 前缀en-（进入）+ 单词roll（名册，名单），字面义为进入官方名单

similar_words.description: enroll多指入学、会员注册，enlist多指参军、参与志愿或科研项目

definitions.example: We must take effective measures to ensure the safety of all participants in the academic conference.

ensure.etymology: 前缀en-（使成为）+ 单词sure（确定的），字面义为使某事确定

similar_words.description: ensure侧重确保某结果发生，insure侧重为财产、人身等投保

definitions.example: Undertaking such a large-scale research project entails a great deal of time, funding and human resources.

entail.etymology: 前缀en-（使）+ 词根tail（切割，引申为限定、附带），原指财产限定继承，引申为某事必然附带的要求

similar_words.description: entail侧重指某事必然需要的前提条件，involve侧重指事件包含涉及的人或内容

definitions.meaning: 进入；开始从事（某行业、领域）
definitions.example: Many graduates choose to enter the field of artificial intelligence after completing their studies.

enter.etymology: 来自拉丁语intrare（进入），前缀en-表进入状态

similar_words.description: entry是enter的名词形式，表「进入（权）；条目；参赛作品」

definitions.example: Many small and medium-sized enterprises have played a crucial role in promoting regional economic development.

enterprise.etymology: 前缀enter-（=inter，在…之间）+ 词根pris（抓取、承担）+ 名词后缀-e，原指承担风险的经营活动，引申为企业、事业心

similar_words.description: enterprise可指各类规模的企业，也可表进取心，corporation多指大型法人集团、股份公司

definitions.example: The professor decided to entertain his visiting colleagues at a famous local restaurant.
Many senior scholars no longer entertain the traditional view that social science research must follow fixed paradigms.

entertain.etymology: 前缀enter-（=inter，在…之间）+ 词根tain（持有、保持），原指维持接待，引申为持有观点

similar_words.description: 二者同根（tain表持有），entertain表暂持某种想法，maintain表长期坚持、维持某种观点

definitions.example: Reading books used to be one of the most popular forms of entertainment for people before the invention of smartphones.

entertainment.etymology: 动词entertain（娱乐、招待）+ 名词后缀-ment，表行为或状态

similar_words.description: entertainment侧重供大众消遣的娱乐活动或行业，recreation侧重工作之余的个人休闲放松

definitions.example: His enthusiasm for environmental protection has inspired many students to participate in voluntary activities.

enthusiasm.etymology: 前缀en-（进入）+ 词根thus（=theos，神）+ 名词后缀-iasm，原指受神感召的狂热，引申为对事物的热忱

similar_words.description: enthusiasm侧重对某事的积极投入与兴趣，passion侧重更强烈的热爱、激情

definitions.example: Many graduate students are enthusiastic about participating in interdisciplinary research projects to expand their academic horizons.

enthusiastic.etymology: en-（使处于…状态）+thusiast（热情者，源自古希腊语，意为受神灵感召的人）+-ic（形容词后缀，表具有…性质的）

similar_words.description: 「enthusiastic」表积极正向的热情与热衷，「zealous」表过度狂热的热情，带有偏执意味

definitions.example: The entire research team spent six months collecting and analyzing the first-hand field data for their dissertation.

entire.etymology: en-（完全）+tire（源自拉丁语tornare，原指完整的轮圈，引申为“完整的”）

similar_words.description: 「entire」强调无缺失、无中断的整体，更正式；「whole」侧重统一的完整体，使用范围更广

definitions.example: According to the university's regulation, full-time postgraduate students are entitled to a monthly academic subsidy.
The renowned sociologist entitled his latest monograph 'The Structural Transformation of Urban Public Spaces'.

entitle.etymology: en-（使）+title（标题；权利，源自拉丁语titulus）

similar_words.description: 「entitle」是动词，表使有权或给题名；「title」是名词，表标题、头衔、权利

definitions.meaning: 实体；独立存在体（法律/经济学术语）
definitions.example: A limited liability company is regarded as a separate legal entity from its shareholders in most commercial legal systems.

entity.etymology: ent-（存在，源自拉丁语esse的现在分词ens）+-ity（名词后缀，表性质或存在体）

similar_words.description: 「entity」指独立存在的实体；「identity」指身份、同一性、个性

definitions.example: The main entrance of the national library is equipped with an intelligent security system to protect precious collections.
Only candidates who pass the strict assessment can gain entrance to the university's doctoral program.

similar_words.description: 「entrance」侧重入口或进入的权利；「entry」侧重进入的行为或条目

definitions.meaning: 企业家；创业者（经济学术语）
definitions.example: A visionary entrepreneur can identify potential market demands and take calculated risks to launch innovative products.

entrepreneur.etymology: entre-（在…之间，源自法语）+pren（=take，拿，源自拉丁语prehendere）+-eur（表人的名词后缀，源自法语），原指在买卖双方间周旋的商人

similar_words.description: 「entrepreneur」指创业主体（企业家/创业者）；「enterprise」指创业活动或企业实体

definitions.example: The entry of advanced foreign technologies has greatly promoted the upgrading of China's manufacturing industry.
Each entry in this academic citation database includes detailed publication information and abstracts.

similar_words.description: 「entry」侧重进入的行为或书面条目；「entrance」侧重物理入口或进入的资格

definitions.example: The confidential peer review comments were sealed in a marked envelope and sent to the corresponding author.

envelope.etymology: en-（在…内部）+velope（=wrap，包裹，源自拉丁语volvere）

similar_words.description: 「envelope」是名词，表信封/封套；「envelop」是动词，表包围、笼罩

definitions.example: Maintaining a stable political and economic environment is essential for attracting foreign direct investment.

environment.etymology: en-（在…内部）+viron（=round，周围，源自拉丁语virere）+-ment（名词后缀，表状态）

similar_words.description: 「environment」侧重宏观的自然、社会或制度环境；「surroundings」侧重具体的周遭物理环境

definitions.example: Many policy researchers envisage a gradual transition to a low-carbon economy in the next decade.

envisage.etymology: en-（使）+vis（看，源自拉丁语videre）+-age（动词后缀，表动作）

similar_words.description: 「envisage」侧重基于现实的客观设想或规划；「envision」侧重带有理想色彩的主观想象或憧憬

definitions.example: Social media often fuels irrational envy among young adults comparing their lives to others’ curated posts.
Many economists envy the stable growth rate of this emerging economy amid global downturn.

envy.etymology: 词根vid-意为「看」，前缀en-表「进入」，本义是带着敌意盯着看，引申为嫉妒、羡慕的含义。

similar_words.description: 「envy」侧重因他人拥有优势而产生的渴望或怨恨，「jealousy」侧重担心自身所有物被夺走的猜忌情绪。

definitions.meaning: 流行病；（不良现象的）盛行，泛滥
definitions.example: The 1918 influenza epidemic killed tens of millions of people worldwide, prompting a series of global public health reforms.
Rumors about job cuts became epidemic in the company after the announcement of budget cuts.

epidemic.etymology: 前缀epi-意为「在…之间」，词根dem-意为「人民」，后缀-ic表形容词，本义是在人群中流传的，引申为流行病、盛行的含义。

similar_words.description: 「epidemic」指大规模跨区域流行的疾病或现象，「endemic」指某一地区特有的地方性疾病或现象。

definitions.meaning: （事件发展中的）插曲，一段经历
definitions.example: This unexpected episode of trade friction forced both countries to renegotiate their bilateral economic agreements.
The documentary series’ final episode focuses on the impact of climate change on Arctic indigenous communities.

episode.etymology: 前缀epi-意为「在…之上」，词根sode意为「路、段落」，原指古希腊悲剧中插入的表演段落，引申为插曲、剧集的含义。

similar_words.description: 「episode」侧重事件发展中相对独立的短小片段或插曲，「chapter」侧重人生或历史进程中较长的阶段或篇章。

definitions.meaning: 纪元；（具有重大意义的）时代，时期
definitions.example: The invention of semiconductor chips marked a new epoch in the development of global information technology.

epoch.etymology: 前缀epi-意为「在…之上」，词根och意为「持、停止」，本义是划定时间的节点，引申为纪元、重要时代的含义。

similar_words.description: 「epoch」侧重作为新时代开端的标志性时间节点，「era」侧重由某个标志性事件开启的整个历史时期。

definitions.example: The core principle of the regulation is to ensure equal opportunities for all applicants regardless of their educational backgrounds.
The total output of the factory this year equals that of the previous two years combined.

equal.etymology: 词根aequ/equ-意为「平、相等」，后缀-al表形容词，本义是平坦的，引申为相等的、平等的含义。

similar_words.description: 「equal」侧重数量、地位、权利等完全相同，「equivalent」侧重价值、功能、意义等大致相当的。

definitions.example: Gender equality in workplace payment and promotion has been a heated topic in social science research for decades.

equality.etymology: 由形容词equal加名词后缀-ity构成，表「平等的性质或状态」。

similar_words.description: 「equality」侧重无差别的绝对平等，「equity」侧重根据个体差异调整的相对公平、公正，后者是考研英语完型常考的易混词。

definitions.example: Students are required to solve three complex mathematical equations within 20 minutes in the final examination.

definitions.meaning: （需平衡多种因素的）综合体，平衡关系
definitions.example: How to maintain stable employment while curbing inflation is a tricky equation for most central banks.

equation.etymology: 词根equ-意为「平、相等」，后缀-ation表名词，指使两边相等的式子，引申为平衡关系的含义。

similar_words.description: 「equation」侧重两边相等的数学式或需平衡的复杂关系，「formula」侧重通用的计算公式或解决方案。

definitions.example: Countries near the equator have abundant solar energy resources due to year-round direct sunlight.

equator.etymology: 词根equ-意为「平、相等」，后缀-ator表名词，原指使昼夜长度相等的地理分界线，即赤道。

similar_words.description: 「equator」是地理概念，指赤道；「equation」是数学或抽象概念，指等式、平衡关系，二者为易混的同根形近词。

definitions.example: The local government has allocated funds to equip all rural primary schools with multimedia teaching facilities.
Internship experiences equip graduates with practical skills that cannot be learned from textbooks alone.

equip.etymology: 词根equ-意为「平、齐全」，后缀-ip表动词，本义给…配齐所需物品，引申为装备、使具备能力的含义。

similar_words.description: 「equip」侧重配备功能性的设备、工具或技能，「furnish」侧重给房屋等场所配备家具等生活设施。

definitions.meaning: 设备，装备；（所需的）知识、技能
definitions.example: The space station is equipped with advanced scientific equipment to conduct experiments in zero-gravity environments.

equipment.etymology: 由动词equip加名词后缀-ment构成，表装备的行为或具体的装备物品。

similar_words.description: 「equipment」指具体的设备、装备，多为不可数名词；「facility」指公共配套设施，常用复数形式facilities。

definitions.example: The nutritional value of a cup of milk is roughly equivalent to that of two eggs.
The new regulation has no equivalent in the previous legal system of the country.

equivalent.etymology: 词根「equ-」（相等、平等）+ 词根「val-」（价值、效力）+ 形容词后缀「-ent」，字面含义为「价值相等的」，引申为等效的、等价的，作名词时指等价物

similar_words.description: 「equal」侧重数量、大小、地位等完全均等；「equivalent」侧重价值、效力、功能等大致相当或等效

definitions.example: The widespread application of artificial intelligence is opening up a brand-new era of social production.

era.etymology: 源自拉丁语「aera」，原指纪年的起点，后引申为具有特定特征的历史时期，为基础名词，无复杂词缀

similar_words.description: 「era」指具有鲜明特征的普通历史时期，适用范围广；「epoch」尤指具有里程碑意义的纪元或时代转折点，语体更正式

definitions.example: Please erase the irrelevant data from the spreadsheet to ensure the accuracy of the analysis.
The trauma of the war can never be erased from the memory of the survivors.

erase.etymology: 前缀「e-」（同「ex-」，向外、去除）+ 词根「ras-」（刮、擦）+ 动词后缀「-e」，字面含义为「刮除出去」，引申为擦除、抹去

similar_words.description: 「erase」侧重擦拭、消除实体痕迹或抽象记忆；「delete」侧重删除文字、数据、文件等数字或书面内容

definitions.example: The local government plans to erect a memorial statue for the heroes who sacrificed in the flood rescue.
Yoga practitioners are required to keep their spines erect during most standing poses.

erect.etymology: 前缀「e-」（向上）+ 词根「rect-」（正、直），字面含义为「向上拉直」，引申为竖立、建造，作形容词时表直立的

similar_words.description: 「erect」可作动词/形容词，表竖立、直立的；「erupt」为动词，表喷发、爆发，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: Coastal erosion has become a serious problem threatening the livelihoods of local fishermen.
Continuous layoffs have led to the erosion of employees’ trust in the company’s management.

erosion.etymology: 前缀「e-」（同「ex-」，向外）+ 词根「ros-」（同「ras-」，刮、磨）+ 名词后缀「-ion」，字面含义为「向外刮磨的损耗」，引申为侵蚀、逐步削弱

similar_words.description: 「erosion」侧重自然力造成的侵蚀，也可指抽象事物的损耗；「corrosion」侧重化学物质导致的锈蚀，多用于具体事物

definitions.example: The economist’s erroneous prediction of the economic recession was widely criticized by academia.

erroneous.etymology: 词根「err-」（犯错、偏离）+ 形容词后缀「-oneous」（表具有某种性质的），字面含义为「具有犯错性质的」，引申为错误的、不正确的

similar_words.description: 「erroneous」为正式书面语，侧重认知、判断违反事实的错误；「false」为常用词，可表虚假的、伪造的，适用范围更广

definitions.example: A small calculation error can lead to the failure of the entire space mission.
The research team adjusted the sampling method to reduce the margin of error in the survey results.

error.etymology: 词根「err-」（犯错、偏离正确标准）+ 名词后缀「-or」（表动作或结果），字面含义为「犯错的结果」，引申为错误、误差

similar_words.description: 「error」多用于正式语境，可指过失、数据误差等，侧重偏离正确标准的偏差；「mistake」多用于日常语境，侧重疏忽导致的错误

definitions.example: The volcano erupted without warning, forcing thousands of residents to evacuate their homes.

definitions.meaning: （冲突、情绪、疫情等）突然爆发，迸发
definitions.example: A wave of protests erupted across the nation when the new tax policy was officially announced.

erupt.etymology: 前缀「e-」（同「ex-」，向外）+ 词根「rupt-」（破、裂），字面含义为「向外破裂」，引申为喷发、爆发

similar_words.description: 「erupt」侧重火山、冲突、疫情等大规模事物的猛烈爆发；「burst」侧重物体炸裂或个人情绪的突然迸发，适用更日常

definitions.example: If not properly handled, the minor trade dispute may escalate into a full-scale economic war between the two countries.

escalate.etymology: 源自名词「escalator」（自动扶梯），词根可追溯至拉丁语「scala」（梯子），字面含义为「沿梯子向上攀升」，引申为（冲突、危机等）逐步升级

similar_words.description: 「escalate」侧重冲突、危机在规模、严重程度上的逐步升级；「intensify」侧重压力、感受等在强度上的加深

definitions.example: Many migrant workers choose to work in big cities to escape the poverty of their rural hometowns.
The title of the classic novel escapes me for the moment, but I can describe its plot clearly.

escape.etymology: 前缀「es-」（同「ex-」，向外）+ 词根「cap-」（抓、握）+ 动词后缀「-e」，原指从抓捕中脱身，引申为逃跑、逃避，僻义指被遗忘

similar_words.description: 「escape」侧重成功逃脱危险或束缚，强调结果；「flee」侧重因恐惧仓皇逃离的动作，强调紧迫性

definitions.meaning: 护送，护航（尤指为保障安全）
definitions.example: The military vehicle escorted the humanitarian aid convoy through the war-torn region.
A team of special agents served as the president’s escort during the official visit.

escort.etymology: e-（=ex-向外）+scort（引导），原指引导外出的人，引申为护送的行为或人员

similar_words.description: 均含引导义；escort侧重为安全或礼仪护送，conduct侧重指挥、引导行为或活动

definitions.example: Climate change poses a severe threat to agricultural production, especially in low-lying coastal areas.

especially.etymology: es-（=ex-加强）+special（特殊的）+-ly（副词后缀），强调在整体中突出某一事物的程度

similar_words.description: especially侧重整体中突出某部分，specially侧重为某一特定目的或对象

definitions.example: The professor assigned a 1500-word essay on the ethical implications of artificial intelligence.
She essayed a smile despite the overwhelming grief of losing her mentor.

essay.etymology: 来自法语essai，原指试验、尝试，后引申为散文（一种尝试性表达思想的文体）

similar_words.description: 均指学术文章；essay侧重短的论述性散文或课程论文，paper侧重正式的学术研究论文或报告

definitions.example: The essence of educational reform lies in prioritizing students’ all-round development rather than test scores.

essence.etymology: ess-（存在，源自拉丁语esse）+-ence（名词后缀），指事物得以存在的核心要素→本质

similar_words.description: 均含本质义；essence侧重抽象的、最核心的本质，substance侧重具体的物质或实质内容

definitions.meaning: 必不可少的，极其重要的；本质的
definitions.example: Basic digital literacy is essential for college students to adapt to the modern information society.
The survival kit includes all the essentials for a 72-hour emergency evacuation.

essential.etymology: ess-（存在）+-ential（形容词后缀），指事物存在所必需的→必不可少的

similar_words.description: 均含重要义；essential侧重对事物存在或运作的必要性，vital侧重对生命或活力的关键性

definitions.example: The university was established in 1902 and has since become a leading institution for scientific research.
The study establishes a clear correlation between regular exercise and improved cognitive function in elderly adults.

establish.etymology: e-（=ex-出）+stabl（站立，源自拉丁语stare）+-ish（动词后缀），使事物站立稳固→建立、确立

similar_words.description: 均含建立义；establish侧重建立制度、机构或确立权威，found侧重创立组织、企业等具体实体

definitions.example: The establishment of the environmental protection bureau has strengthened the supervision of industrial pollution.
The novel satirizes the hypocrisy and corruption of the political establishment in the 19th century.

establishment.etymology: establish（建立）+-ment（名词后缀），指建立的事物或掌权的群体

similar_words.description: 均指机构；establishment侧重具体的建立的组织或设施，institution侧重长期存在的制度或社会机构

definitions.example: The wealthy businessman owns a large estate in the countryside with a private lake and forest.
The lawyer is responsible for managing the deceased’s estate according to the will.

estate.etymology: 源自拉丁语status（状态、地位），原指与社会地位对应的财产，后引申为地产、遗产

similar_words.description: 均指财产；estate侧重大型地产或遗产，property侧重一般的个人或集体财产

definitions.example: Professor Smith is highly esteemed by his colleagues for his rigorous academic attitude and groundbreaking research.
She esteems honesty as the most valuable virtue in interpersonal relationships.

esteem.etymology: e-（=ex-加强）+steem（估价，源自拉丁语aestimare），原指高度估价，引申为对人的尊重

similar_words.description: 均含尊重义；esteem侧重因品德、成就而高度敬重，respect侧重一般的尊重或遵守规则

definitions.example: Economists estimate that the country’s GDP will grow by 4.5% this year amid post-pandemic recovery.
Critics estimate the film as a milestone in the history of Chinese independent cinema.

estimate.etymology: e-（=ex-出）+tim（估价，源自拉丁语aestimare）+-ate（动词/名词后缀），指估算数值或评价事物

similar_words.description: 均含估价义；estimate侧重粗略的估算或初步评价，evaluate侧重系统的、详细的正式评估

definitions.example: The eternal value of ancient Greek philosophy lies in its in-depth exploration of human nature and morality.

eternal.etymology: 词根「etern-」（来自拉丁语aeternus，意为「永远的」）+形容词后缀「-al」，表「具有…性质的」

similar_words.description: 「eternal」意为永恒的、不朽的，「external」意为外部的、外面的，拼写仅中间字母不同易混淆

definitions.example: The exhibition displays ethnic handicrafts and folk tales from 23 minority groups in southwest China.

ethnic.etymology: 词根「ethn-」（来自希腊语ethnos，意为「民族、种族」）+形容词后缀「-ic」，表「与…相关的」

similar_words.description: 「ethnic」指民族的、种族的，「ethic」指道德准则、伦理规范，拼写仅最后一个字母不同易混淆

definitions.example: Local emergency management department ordered residents to evacuate the mountainous area before the heavy rainfall arrived.

evacuate.etymology: 前缀「e-」（表「出、外」）+词根「vacu-」（意为「空」）+动词后缀「-ate」，表「使空出、撤离」

similar_words.description: 「evacuate」指人员或物资的疏散撤离，「evade」指逃避责任、规则等，同前缀拼写易混淆

definitions.example: Some multinational corporations attempt to evade tax by transferring profits to low-tax offshore regions.

evade.etymology: 前缀「e-」（表「出、外」）+词根「vad-」（意为「走」），表「走出（约束范围）、逃避」

similar_words.description: 「evade」指主动逃避，「invade」指强行入侵，同词根拼写易混淆

definitions.example: Researchers need to evaluate the long-term effectiveness of the new environmental policy through quantitative data.

evaluate.etymology: 前缀「e-」（表「出」）+词根「valu-」（意为「价值」）+动词后缀「-ate」，表「评出价值、评估」

similar_words.description: 「evaluate」指系统全面的评估，「estimate」指对数量、成本的粗略估算，语义侧重不同易混用

definitions.example: Under high temperature, the moisture in the soil will evaporate rapidly and affect crop growth.

evaporate.etymology: 前缀「e-」（表「出、外」）+词根「vapor-」（意为「蒸汽」）+动词后缀「-ate」，表「散出蒸汽、蒸发」

similar_words.description: 「evaporate」多指液体自然蒸发，「vaporize」可指任何形态物质的汽化，语义相近易混用

definitions.example: On the eve of the national college entrance examination, most candidates choose to review key knowledge points calmly.

eve.etymology: 来自古英语æfen，原指「傍晚」，后引申为「重大事件的前夕」

similar_words.description: 「eve」指前夕、前一天，「even」指甚至、平坦的，拼写仅一个字母不同易混淆

definitions.example: The 2020 global pandemic was a pivotal event that reshaped the development pattern of the digital economy.

event.etymology: 前缀「e-」（表「出」）+词根「vent-」（意为「来」），表「出现的事、事件」

similar_words.description: 「event」指重大或公开的事件，「incident」指小事件或突发事端，语义范围不同易混用

definitions.example: After three years of repeated trials and adjustments, the research team eventually developed a new energy storage material.

eventually.etymology: 形容词「eventual」（意为「最终的」）+副词后缀「-ly」，「eventual」派生自名词「event」，表「最终发生的」

similar_words.description: 「eventually」强调经过曲折过程后最终，「finally」强调顺序上的最后，语义侧重不同易混用

definitions.example: Mastering practical everyday English expressions is crucial for overseas students' daily communication.

everyday.etymology: 合成形容词，由限定词「every」+名词「day」构成，表「每日发生的、日常的」

similar_words.description: 「everyday」是形容词，修饰名词（如everyday life）；「every day」是副词短语，作时间状语，用法极易混淆

definitions.example: Scientific evidence shows that regular moderate exercise reduces the risk of chronic diseases.

evidence.etymology: 词根e-（表‘向外’）+vid（表‘看’）+名词后缀-ence，指‘能被看到的依据→证据’

similar_words.description: 二者为同根词；evidence是名词，表「证据、依据」；evident是形容词，表「明显的」

definitions.example: It is evident that the digital transformation has reshaped the global economic structure.

evident.etymology: 词根e-（表‘向外’）+vid（表‘看’）+形容词后缀-ent，指‘能被看到的→明显的’

similar_words.description: 二者为近义词；evident侧重有证据支撑的明显；obvious侧重无需证据即可感知的明显

definitions.example: The evil dictator's policies caused massive humanitarian disasters in the region.
Literary works often explore the conflict between good and evil in human nature.

evil.etymology: 源自古英语yfel，表‘坏的、恶劣的’，后引申为道德层面的邪恶

similar_words.description: 二者为近义词；evil侧重道德上的邪恶、罪恶；vile侧重令人厌恶的、卑劣的品质

definitions.example: The nostalgic melody evoked warm memories of my college years.
In ancient tribal rituals, shamans tried to evoke the spirits of ancestors for guidance.

evoke.etymology: 词根e-（表‘向外’）+vok（表‘呼喊’）+动词后缀-e，指‘呼喊出来→唤起’

similar_words.description: 二者为同根词；evoke侧重唤起情感、记忆；invoke侧重援引、求助（如法律、神力）

definitions.example: Darwin's theory of natural selection is the core of modern biological evolution research.

evolution.etymology: 词根e-（表‘向外’）+volut（表‘转动’）+名词后缀-ion，指‘转动展开→演变、进化’

similar_words.description: 二者为同根词；evolution表渐进的进化、演变；revolution表突变的革命、变革

definitions.example: Over thousands of years, human languages have evolved to adapt to social changes.

evolve.etymology: 词根e-（表‘向外’）+volv（表‘转动’）+动词后缀-e，指‘转动展开→演变、进化’

similar_words.description: 二者为近义词；evolve侧重长期、渐进的演变（如生物、语言）；develop侧重事物的生长、发展

definitions.example: Researchers must collect exact data to ensure the validity of their experiments.
The colonial rulers exacted heavy tributes from the local indigenous population.

exact.etymology: 词根ex-（表‘完全’）+act（表‘做’），指‘完全做出来的→精确的；硬要做出来的→强求’

similar_words.description: 二者为近义词；exact侧重细节、数量的完全一致；accurate侧重符合事实或标准的准确

definitions.example: Some social media influencers exaggerate product effects to boost sales.

exaggerate.etymology: 词根ex-（表‘完全’）+agger（表‘堆积’）+动词后缀-ate，指‘完全堆积起来→夸大’

similar_words.description: 二者为近义词；exaggerate侧重主观上的夸张、言过其实；overstate侧重陈述内容的超出事实

definitions.example: The audit team will examine the company's financial records for irregularities.
College professors examine students' academic ability through various assessment methods.

examine.etymology: 词根ex-（表‘完全’）+amin（表‘测试’）+动词后缀-e，指‘完全测试→检查、考察’

similar_words.description: 二者为近义词；examine侧重细致的检查、分析；inspect侧重官方的、有目的的视察

definitions.example: The number of applicants for the postgraduate program exceeded the admission quota by 30%.

exceed.etymology: 词根ex-（表‘向外’）+ceed（表‘走’），指‘走出去→超过、超出’

similar_words.description: 二者为同根词；exceed是动词，表「超过」；excess是名词/形容词，表「过量（的）」

definitions.example: Such exceedingly complex climate models require extensive data validation by climatologists.

exceedingly.etymology: 由前缀ex-（超过）、词根ceed（行走）、形容词后缀-ing及副词后缀-ly构成，本义为「走得超出范围地」，引申为「极其」

similar_words.description: 「exceedingly」为正式书面语表「极其」，「extremely」为通用词表「非常」

definitions.example: Many top-tier graduate applicants excel at integrating quantitative and qualitative research approaches.

excel.etymology: 由前缀ex-（向外）、词根cel（升高，来自拉丁语celsus）构成，本义为「高出」，引申为「擅长，突出」

similar_words.description: 「excel」为动词表「擅长」，「exceed」为动词表「超过（数量、限度）」

definitions.example: The study provides an excellent framework for analyzing the impact of digitalization on labor markets.

excellent.etymology: 由前缀ex-（向外）、词根cel（升高）及形容词后缀-ent构成，本义为「高出的」，引申为「优秀的，杰出的」

similar_words.description: 「excellent」表「优秀的（常规赞扬）」，「exceptional」表「杰出的（程度超优）或例外的」

definitions.example: With the exception of two anomalous data points, all results align with the theoretical hypothesis.

exception.etymology: 由前缀ex-（向外）、词根cept（拿取，来自拉丁语capere）及名词后缀-ion构成，本义为「被拿出去的事物」，引申为「例外」

similar_words.description: 「exception」为名词表「例外」，「excerpt」为名词/动词表「摘录」

definitions.example: Her exceptional proficiency in cross-cultural communication secured her a position in the international organization.
The court granted an exceptional extension for the submission of the appeal brief.

exceptional.etymology: 由名词exception（例外）加形容词后缀-al构成，既指「例外的」，又因「超出常规」引申为「杰出的」

similar_words.description: 「exceptional」表「杰出的（程度超优）或例外的」，「excellent」表「优秀的（常规赞扬）」

definitions.meaning: n. 摘录，节选；v. 摘录，节选
definitions.example: The professor assigned an excerpt from Marx’s *Capital* to illustrate the concept of commodity fetishism.

excerpt.etymology: 由前缀ex-（向外）、词根cerpt（摘取，来自拉丁语carpere）构成，本义为「摘出的内容」，引申为「摘录，节选」

similar_words.description: 「excerpt」为名词/动词表「摘录」，「exception」为名词表「例外」

definitions.meaning: n. 过量，过剩；adj. 过量的，额外的
definitions.example: The government has imposed a tax on excess corporate profits to curb wealth inequality.

excess.etymology: 由前缀ex-（超过）、词根cess（行走，来自拉丁语cedere）构成，本义为「走得超出限度」，引申为「过量，过剩」

similar_words.description: 「excess」可作名词/形容词表「过量（的，中性）」，「excessive」为形容词表「过度的（含贬义）」

definitions.example: Excessive exposure to social media has been linked to increased rates of anxiety among young adults.

excessive.etymology: 由名词excess（过量）加形容词后缀-ive构成，表「过度的，过分的」

similar_words.description: 「excessive」为形容词表「过度的（贬义）」，「excess」可作名词/形容词表「过量（的，中性）」

definitions.meaning: v. 交换，交流；n. 交换，交流
definitions.example: The two research institutions have signed a memorandum of understanding to exchange academic resources and faculty members.

exchange.etymology: 由前缀ex-（相互）、词根change（交换）构成，表「交换，交流」

similar_words.description: 「exchange」表「（双方）交换/交流（常用）」，「interchange」表「（频繁）交替/互换」

definitions.example: The discovery of a new exoplanet excited the global astronomical community.
The policy proposal excited widespread criticism from environmental advocacy groups.

excite.etymology: 由前缀ex-（向外）、词根cite（召唤，来自拉丁语citare）构成，本义为「召唤出来」，引申为「使兴奋；激发」

similar_words.description: 「excite」表「使兴奋/激发（中性或积极）」，「incite」表「煽动，挑起（贬义）」

definitions.example: The announcement of the national scholarship results sparked widespread excitement among undergraduate students.

excitement.etymology: 前缀ex-（向外）+ 词根cite（唤起）+ 名词后缀-ment，由动词「excite」派生而来

similar_words.description: 「excitement」多指普遍、持续的兴奋情绪；「thrill」侧重突发的、强烈的激动或刺激感

definitions.meaning: 令人兴奋的；使人激动的
definitions.example: The breakthrough in renewable energy technology is an exciting development for global environmental protection.

exciting.etymology: 前缀ex-（向外）+ 词根cite（唤起）+ 形容词后缀-ing，为动词「excite」的现在分词作形容词

similar_words.description: 「exciting」修饰事物，表事物具有令人兴奋的属性；「excited」修饰人，表人处于兴奋状态，为考研语法高频考点

definitions.example: When the archaeologist uncovered the intact bronze ritual vessel, she exclaimed with uncontrolled delight.

exclaim.etymology: 前缀ex-（向外）+ 词根claim（呼喊），词根claim表‘呼喊、宣称’

similar_words.description: 「exclaim」指因情绪激动而呼喊；「proclaim」指官方或正式地宣告、声明，为同根形近词

definitions.example: The clinical trial excludes participants who have a history of severe allergic reactions to the test drug.

exclude.etymology: 前缀ex-（向外）+ 词根clude（关闭），词根clude表‘关闭’，如「include」为in-+clude（关在里面→包含）

similar_words.description: 「exclude」指将…排除在外；「include」指将…包含在内，二者为反义形近词

definitions.example: The academic journal published an exclusive paper on the new findings of quantum physics.

exclusive.etymology: 前缀ex-（向外）+ 词根clus（关闭，clude的变体）+ 形容词后缀-ive，由动词「exclude」派生而来

similar_words.description: 「exclusive」指排他的、专有的；「inclusive」指包含的、范围广泛的，二者为反义形近词

definitions.example: The biology department organized a one-day excursion to the wetland reserve for specimen collection.

excursion.etymology: 前缀ex-（向外）+ 词根curs（跑）+ 名词后缀-ion，词根curs表‘跑’，跑出去→短途出行

similar_words.description: 「excursion」侧重短途休闲或调研旅行；「expedition」侧重有明确目的的长途探险或考察

definitions.example: The local government must execute the poverty alleviation policy with precision to achieve the expected results.
The war criminal was executed by a military tribunal after being convicted of genocide.

execute.etymology: 前缀ex-（彻底）+ 词根secu（跟随）+ 动词后缀-ate，原指‘跟随到底’，后引申为执行、处决

similar_words.description: 「execute」侧重按计划或命令严格执行；「implement」侧重使政策、方案等正式生效并推行

definitions.example: The executive committee of the university is responsible for formulating annual development plans.
She was appointed as the chief executive officer of the tech startup last quarter.

executive.etymology: 由动词「execute」派生而来，ex-+secu+-ive（形容词/名词后缀），表与执行相关的（人）

similar_words.description: 「executive」指行政主管或执行的；「executor」特指法律上的遗嘱执行人，为同根形近词

definitions.example: The case study exemplifies how digital transformation can enhance the efficiency of traditional manufacturing.

exemplify.etymology: 前缀ex-（向外）+ 词根empl（拿，example的词根变体）+ 动词后缀-ify，拿出例子→例证

similar_words.description: 「exemplify」侧重用具体案例证明观点；「illustrate」侧重用图表、例子等解释说明内容

definitions.example: Students with severe physical disabilities are exempt from the mandatory military training.
The new tax regulation exempts micro-enterprises from paying value-added tax for three years.

exempt.etymology: 前缀ex-（向外）+ 词根empt（拿），拿出去→免除义务或责任

similar_words.description: 「exempt」侧重义务、税收等的法定免除；「immune」侧重疾病、批评等的免疫或豁免

definitions.meaning: 施加（影响、压力等）；运用（权力、努力）
definitions.example: Parents may exert a subtle influence on their children’s academic performance and career choices.

exert.etymology: 词根ex-（向外）+ ert（=erg，表“工作、做功”），字面义为“使出力量”

similar_words.description: exert为动词，exertion为其名词形式，表“施加；努力”

definitions.meaning: 使筋疲力尽；耗尽（资源、精力）
definitions.example: Long hours of intensive review exhausted most candidates preparing for the postgraduate entrance examination.

exhaust.etymology: 词根ex-（向外）+ haust（表“抽、拉”），字面义为“把力气/资源抽干”

similar_words.description: exhaust为动词/名词，exhaustion为其名词形式，表“筋疲力尽；耗尽”

definitions.meaning: 展示，陈列；表现出（品质、情感等）
definitions.example: The recent study exhibits a significant positive correlation between sleep quality and learning efficiency.

exhibit.etymology: 词根ex-（向外）+ hibit（表“拿、持”），字面义为“拿出来展示”

similar_words.description: exhibit可作动词或名词（表展示、展品），exhibition仅作名词，指具体的展览会

definitions.example: The annual national book exhibition attracts numerous publishers and readers across the country.

exhibition.etymology: 词根ex-（向外）+ hibit（表“拿、持”）+ 名词后缀-ion，表“拿出来展示的活动”

similar_words.description: exhibition为名词（表展览会），exhibit可作动词或名词（表展示、展品）

definitions.meaning: 流放，流亡；流亡者/流放，放逐
definitions.example: The famous writer lived in exile abroad for 15 years due to political reasons.

exile.etymology: 词根ex-（向外）+ ile（表“走”），字面义为“被赶出去”

similar_words.description: exile侧重因政治原因被强制流放或流亡，expatriate侧重自愿离开祖国居住国外

definitions.example: Astronomers are still exploring whether intelligent life exists on other planets in the Milky Way.

exist.etymology: 词根ex-（向外）+ sist（表“站”），字面义为“站出来，存在”

similar_words.description: exist为动词（表存在），exit为动词或名词（表出口、退出）

definitions.example: The existence of ancient human settlements in this area has been confirmed by archaeological excavations.

existence.etymology: 动词exist（存在）+ 名词后缀-ence，表“存在的状态或生存”

similar_words.description: existence为exist的名词形式，侧重存在的状态或生存状况

definitions.example: Passengers are required to locate the nearest emergency exit immediately after boarding the train.

exit.etymology: 词根ex-（向外）+ it（表“走”），字面义为“走出去的地方或动作”

similar_words.description: exit为动词或名词（表出口、退出），exist为动词（表存在）

definitions.meaning: 异国的，外来的；奇异的，别致的
definitions.example: The introduction of exotic animal species may pose a threat to the local ecological system.

exotic.etymology: 词根ex-（向外、外部）+ 形容词后缀-otic，字面义为“来自外部的”

similar_words.description: exotic侧重异国的且带有奇异、新奇感，foreign仅指外国的，无奇异含义

definitions.example: The university plans to expand its postgraduate enrollment scale to meet the growing demand for higher education.

expand.etymology: 词根ex-（向外）+ pand（表“展开、伸展”），字面义为“向外展开”

similar_words.description: expand侧重范围、体积的扩大或膨胀，extend侧重时间、空间的延伸或延长

definitions.example: The rapid expansion of digital technology has transformed the way people access information and communicate.

expansion.etymology: 前缀ex-表示‘向外’，词根pans表示‘伸展’，名词后缀-ion表动作或状态，指向外伸展的行为或结果

similar_words.description: 二者拼写相近，expansion表规模、范围的扩张或膨胀，expedition表远征、探险或考察队

definitions.example: Against all expectations, the previously unknown writer won the national literary award with his debut novel.

expectation.etymology: 前缀ex-表示‘向前’，词根pect表示‘看’，名词后缀-ation表状态或结果，指向前看的心理状态即预期

similar_words.description: 二者为同根近义词，expectancy侧重客观的预期（如寿命、事件发生的可能性），expectation侧重主观的期望或期待

definitions.example: A group of geologists joined an international expedition to study volcanic activity in the Pacific Ring of Fire.

expedition.etymology: 前缀ex-表示‘向外’，词根ped表示‘行走’，名词后缀-ition表动作或团体，指远程出行的行动或团队

similar_words.description: 二者均含出行含义，expedition指长途的探险、远征或科学考察，excursion指短途的游览或远足

definitions.example: The university committee voted to expel three students who were caught cheating in the final examination.

expel.etymology: 前缀ex-表示‘向外’，词根pel表示‘推、驱使’，指向外推赶的动作

similar_words.description: 二者均含强迫离开的含义，expel侧重官方的驱逐或开除（如学籍、出境），exile侧重政治流放或被迫流亡

definitions.meaning: 花费，消耗（时间、金钱、精力等）
definitions.example: The research team expended nearly two years conducting field surveys to collect reliable data for their study.

expend.etymology: 前缀ex-表示‘向外’，词根pend表示‘支付、称量’，指向外支付或耗用的动作

similar_words.description: 二者均表花费，expend为正式用语，侧重消耗时间、精力等资源，spend为日常用语，侧重花费金钱或时间

definitions.example: The government’s new budget proposes a significant increase in expenditure on public healthcare and education.

expenditure.etymology: 前缀ex-表示‘向外’，词根pend表示‘支付’，名词后缀-iture表行为结果，指支出的费用或耗用的资源

similar_words.description: 二者为同根近义词，expenditure侧重正式的、大规模的支出（如政府财政、企业预算），expense侧重日常的、个人的花费或代价

definitions.example: Many developing countries have achieved rapid economic growth at the expense of ecological balance.

expense.etymology: 前缀ex-表示‘向外’，词根pens表示‘支付’，名词后缀-e表支付的金额或付出的代价

similar_words.description: 二者为同根近义词，expense侧重日常个人或小范围的开支，也可指抽象的代价，expenditure侧重官方或大规模的正式支出

definitions.example: Volunteering in rural schools provided me with precious experience in dealing with children from disadvantaged backgrounds.
The coastal city experienced an unprecedented typhoon last year, causing severe damage to local infrastructure.

experience.etymology: 前缀ex-表示‘完全’，词根peri表示‘尝试、经历’，名词后缀-ence表状态或结果，指尝试过的内容即经验或经历

similar_words.description: 二者为同根形近词，experience侧重个人的经验或经历，experiment侧重科学的实验或试验

definitions.example: Scientists carried out a series of controlled experiments to verify the hypothesis about gene mutation.
The company is experimenting with a new marketing strategy to attract younger consumers.

experiment.etymology: 前缀ex-表示‘完全’，词根peri表示‘尝试、试验’，名词后缀-ment表行为或活动，指为验证假设而进行的尝试活动

similar_words.description: 二者为同根形近词，experiment侧重科学实验或商业尝试，experience侧重个人经验或亲身经历

definitions.example: The experimental vaccine developed by the research team has been approved for clinical trials on a small scale.

experimental.etymology: 名词experiment（实验）加形容词后缀-al，表与实验相关的

similar_words.description: 二者为同根形近词，experimental表与实验相关的、试验性的，experiential表基于经验的、来自经验的

definitions.example: An expert in behavioral economics published a paper in a top journal analyzing how social norms influence consumer decisions.
She is expert at designing controlled experiments that minimize confounding variables in psychological research.

expert.etymology: 由前缀ex-（表“完全”）和拉丁语词根pert（表“尝试、经验”）构成，原指“完全有经验的人”，引申为专家；作形容词时表“熟练的”

similar_words.description: 二者均以ex-开头，词形相近；「expert」为名词/形容词，指专家或熟练的，「exert」为动词，指施加（影响、压力等）
二者均含“熟练的”之意；「expert」侧重指在某一领域有专业知识的，「adept」侧重指技能娴熟的

definitions.example: The interdisciplinary research project requires expertise in both computational linguistics and cognitive neuroscience.

expertise.etymology: 由「expert」（专家）加后缀-ise（动词后缀，表“使成为”）和-e（名词后缀）构成，指专家具备的专门知识或技能

similar_words.description: 二者均指专业领域；「expertise」侧重指专门的知识或技能，「specialty」侧重指个人或机构的专业方向

definitions.example: The international trade agreement will expire at the end of this year, so negotiators are rushing to reach a renewal deal.
The renowned physicist expired peacefully in his sleep at the age of 92, leaving a legacy of groundbreaking research.

expire.etymology: 由前缀ex-（表“出”）和拉丁语词根spir（表“呼吸”）构成，原指“呼出最后一口气”，引申为“死亡”；后引申为（期限）“届满失效”

similar_words.description: 二者均含词根spir（呼吸）；「expire」指到期或死亡，「inspire」指鼓舞或激发
二者均含“结束”之意；「expire」侧重指期限届满失效，「terminate」侧重指人为终止（协议等）

definitions.example: The Nobel laureate used a simple thought experiment to explain the complex principles of general relativity to non-specialist audiences.

explain.etymology: 由前缀ex-（表“完全”）和词根plain（表“平坦、清晰”）构成，原指“使完全清晰”，引申为“解释、说明”

similar_words.description: 二者词形相近；「explain」指解释，「complain」指抱怨
二者均含“解释”之意；「explain」侧重指把难懂的事物讲清楚，「interpret」侧重指对语言、符号或行为的解读

definitions.example: The research team provided a rigorous explanation for the anomalous results observed in their phase III clinical trial.

explanation.etymology: 由「explain」（解释）加名词后缀-ation构成，表解释的动作或内容

similar_words.description: 二者均指解释的内容；「explanation」侧重指对事实或原因的说明，「interpretation」侧重指对意义的解读

definitions.example: The university's code of conduct includes explicit provisions prohibiting academic plagiarism and research misconduct.

explicit.etymology: 由前缀ex-（表“出”）、词根plic（表“折叠”）和形容词后缀-it构成，原指“把折叠的事物展开”，引申为“明确的、清晰的”

similar_words.description: 二者词形相近，为反义词；「explicit」指明确的、外在的，「implicit」指含蓄的、内在的

definitions.example: The industrial chemical plant exploded due to a failure in the pressure regulation system, causing widespread air and water pollution.
The recent genetic studies have exploded the long-held myth that modern humans evolved exclusively from a single African population.

explode.etymology: 由前缀ex-（表“出”）和拉丁语词根plod（表“拍手、爆裂”）构成，原指“拍手把（演员）赶下台”，引申为“爆炸”；后引申为“推翻（理论等）”

similar_words.description: 二者词形相近，为反义词；「explode」指向外爆炸、爆发，「implode」指向内坍塌、崩溃

definitions.example: The investigative report exposed how fast-fashion brands exploit underpaid workers in Southeast Asian factories to cut production costs.
The polar explorer's daring exploits in the Arctic were chronicled in a bestselling memoir that won multiple literary awards.

exploit.etymology: 由前缀ex-（表“出”）和词根ploit（表“折叠、利用”）构成，原指“展开利用（织物）”，引申为“剥削、利用”；作名词时表“英勇事迹”

similar_words.description: 二者词形相近；「exploit」指剥削或利用，「explore」指探索或探究
二者均含“利用”之意；「exploit」多含贬义（剥削或不正当利用），「utilize」为中性词，指有效利用

definitions.example: The United Nations report condemned the systematic exploitation of tropical rainforests for commercial logging and agricultural expansion.

exploitation.etymology: 由「exploit」（剥削、利用）加名词后缀-ation构成，表剥削或利用的行为或状态

similar_words.description: 二者均为ex-开头的名词；「exploitation」指剥削或利用，「exploration」指探索或探究

definitions.example: The research project aims to explore the causal relationship between gut microbiota and mental health disorders using longitudinal data.

explore.etymology: 由前缀ex-（表“出”）和拉丁语词根plor（表“呼喊、寻找”）构成，原指“外出寻找呼喊”，引申为“探索、探究”

similar_words.description: 二者词形相近；「explore」指探索或探究，「exploit」指剥削或利用
二者均含“探究”之意；「explore」侧重指全面、系统的探索，「investigate」侧重指对具体问题的调查

definitions.example: The explosion of digital media has reshaped the way people access and share information worldwide.

explosion.etymology: ex-（向外）+ plos（破裂，词根来自「explode」）+ -ion（名词后缀），原指向外破裂的状态，引申为数量或规模的激增

similar_words.description: 「explosion」侧重突然的爆炸或数量激增；「expansion」侧重范围、规模的逐渐扩张

definitions.example: The explosive growth of cross-border e-commerce has posed new regulatory challenges for governments.

explosive.etymology: ex-（向外）+ plos（破裂）+ -ive（形容词后缀），原指易向外破裂的，引申为增长迅猛的

similar_words.description: 「explosive」表爆炸性的、迅猛的；「expansive」表广阔的、健谈的

definitions.meaning: 出口，输出；出口商品，出口额
definitions.example: Many Southeast Asian countries rely heavily on exporting tropical fruits to boost their national economies.

export.etymology: ex-（向外）+ port（搬运，词根来自拉丁语portare），指将货物向外搬运至外国，即出口

similar_words.description: 「export」指向外国输出商品或服务（出口）；「import」指从外国输入商品或服务（进口）

definitions.meaning: 暴露，揭露；使接触（某事物）
definitions.example: Academic programs should expose students to diverse theoretical frameworks to foster critical thinking.

expose.etymology: ex-（向外）+ pos（放置，词根来自拉丁语ponere）+ -e，指向外放置（隐藏的事物），即暴露、揭露，引申为使接触外部事物

similar_words.description: 「expose」表暴露、使接触；「impose」表强加（义务、税收等）、征收

definitions.example: Prolonged exposure to ultraviolet radiation increases the risk of skin damage and related diseases.

exposure.etymology: expose（v. 暴露）+ -ure（名词后缀），指暴露的状态或行为，引申为接触、曝光

similar_words.description: 「exposure」表暴露、接触；「exposition」表（理论的）阐述、博览会

definitions.meaning: 表达，表示；明确的，特快的
definitions.example: In his keynote speech, the scholar expressed deep concern about the ethical risks of artificial intelligence.

express.etymology: ex-（向外）+ press（压，词根来自拉丁语premere），指向外压出（情感、思想），即表达，引申为明确的、特快的

similar_words.description: 「express」表表达、明确的；「impress」表给…留下印象、使铭记

definitions.example: The use of formal academic expressions is required in most graduate-level research papers.

expression.etymology: express（v. 表达）+ -ion（名词后缀），指表达的行为或结果，也指面部的表情

similar_words.description: 「expression」表表达、表情；「impression」表印象、感想

definitions.example: The exhibition features exquisite handicrafts made by traditional artisans from rural China.

exquisite.etymology: ex-（完全）+ quis（寻求，词根来自拉丁语quaerere）+ -ite，指经过完全挑选后选出的，即精美的，引申为敏锐的

similar_words.description: 「exquisite」表精美的、敏锐的；「explicit」表明确的、清晰的

definitions.example: The research team decided to extend the study period to collect more comprehensive data.

extend.etymology: ex-（向外）+ tend（伸展，词根来自拉丁语tendere），指向外伸展，即延伸、延长，引申为扩大、扩展

similar_words.description: 「extend」侧重时间、空间上的线性延长；「expand」侧重范围、体积上的整体扩大

definitions.meaning: 延伸，延长；扩建部分；延期
definitions.example: The company applied for a three-month extension of the project deadline due to supply chain disruptions.

extension.etymology: extend（v. 延伸）+ -ion（名词后缀），指延伸的行为或结果，也指扩建部分或延期

similar_words.description: 「extension」侧重时间、空间上的延长或延期；「expansion」侧重规模、范围上的扩张

definitions.example: Extensive research has been conducted to explore the correlation between climate change and agricultural productivity.

extensive.etymology: ex-（向外）+ tens（伸展）+ -ive（形容词后缀）→ 向外伸展的→广泛的

similar_words.description: 「extensive」表“广泛的、广阔的”，侧重覆盖范围广；「intensive」表“密集的、加强的”，侧重程度深，二者为易混的反义相关形容词。

definitions.example: The extent of global warming's impact on coastal ecosystems has been underestimated by many policymakers.

extent.etymology: ex-（向外）+ tens（伸展）+ -t（名词后缀，表状态）→ 伸展的范围→程度、范围

similar_words.description: 「extent」是名词，表“程度、范围”；「extensive」是形容词，表“广泛的”，二者为同根形近易混词。

definitions.example: The exterior walls of the historic castle have been restored to their original 17th-century appearance.

exterior.etymology: exter-（外部的，ex的比较级形式）+ -ior（形容词/名词后缀，表比较级含义）→ 更靠外的→外部的/外表

similar_words.description: 「exterior」侧重“物体表面的、外在的”，多指具体事物的外部；「external」侧重“与内部相对的、外来的”，可指抽象或具体事物。

definitions.example: The company's financial crisis was caused by both internal mismanagement and external market fluctuations.

external.etymology: exter-（外部的）+ -nal（形容词后缀）→ 外部的

similar_words.description: 「external」侧重“与内部相对的、外来的”，可指抽象或具体事物；「exterior」侧重“物体表面的、外在的”，多指具体事物的外部。

definitions.example: Many prehistoric species became extinct due to sudden climate changes and natural disasters.

extinct.etymology: ex-（离开、消失）+ stinct（刺，引申为“存在的标记”）→ 失去存在标记的→灭绝的

similar_words.description: 「extinct」是形容词，表“灭绝的”；「extinguish」是动词，表“熄灭、扑灭”，二者为形近易混词。

definitions.example: Firefighters took several hours to extinguish the blaze that broke out in the industrial warehouse.

extinguish.etymology: ex-（使离开）+ stingu（压制、熄灭）+ -ish（动词后缀）→ 使熄灭→扑灭

similar_words.description: 「extinguish」是动词，表“熄灭、扑灭”；「extinct」是形容词，表“灭绝的”，二者为形近易混词。

definitions.meaning: 额外的（adj）；额外地（adv）；额外之物（n）
definitions.example: Students are required to pay an extra fee for the optional field trip organized by the biology department.

extra.etymology: 来自拉丁语extra（在…之外），直接借用为英语词汇

similar_words.description: 「extra」侧重“超出常规的、额外的”，可作多词性；「additional」侧重“附加的、追加的”，仅作形容词，多用于正式语境。

definitions.example: Researchers can extract valuable genetic information from the fossilized remains of ancient organisms.

extract.etymology: ex-（向外）+ tract（拉、拽）→ 向外拉→提取

similar_words.description: 「extract」表“提取、摘录”；「abstract」表“抽象的；摘要”，二者为同根形近易混词。

definitions.example: The scientist made extraordinary contributions to the field of quantum physics, earning him the Nobel Prize.

extraordinary.etymology: extra-（超出）+ ordinary（普通的）→ 超出普通的→非凡的

similar_words.description: 「extraordinary」侧重“超出常规的、非凡的”，语气较强；「exceptional」侧重“杰出的、例外的”，多用于正面评价。

definitions.example: The millionaire's extravagant lifestyle, including private jets and luxury villas, drew widespread media attention.

extravagant.etymology: extra-（超出）+ vag（漫游、游荡，引申为无节制花费）+ -ant（形容词后缀）→ 超出常规花费的→奢侈的

similar_words.description: 「extravagant」侧重“挥霍无度的、过度的”，含贬义；「luxurious」侧重“奢华的、舒适的”，为中性或褒义。

definitions.example: The extreme weather events caused by climate change have threatened food security in many rural regions.
Researchers are exploring the extremes of deep-sea ecosystems to discover new species.

extreme.etymology: 前缀ex-（超出）+词根-treme（边界、极限），源自拉丁语extremus，意为超出正常边界的状态或性质

similar_words.description: 前者侧重状态的极端性，后者侧重数量或程度的过度性

definitions.meaning: 眉毛（常考搭配raise eyebrows表惊讶或反对）
definitions.example: The scholar's controversial views on educational reform raised eyebrows among the academic community.

eyebrow.etymology: 复合词，由eye（眼睛）和brow（眉、额部）构成，指生长于眼睛上方的毛发

similar_words.description: 前者指眼睛上方的眉毛，后者指眼睛边缘的睫毛

definitions.example: Regular exposure to electronic screens may lead to gradual deterioration of eyesight among teenagers.

eyesight.etymology: 复合词，由eye（眼睛）和sight（视觉、视力）构成，指眼睛感知光线的生理能力

similar_words.description: 前者侧重生理层面的视力，后者可指抽象的视野或远见

definitions.meaning: 寓言（多含寓意与拟人手法）
definitions.example: The fable 「The Tortoise and the Hare」 conveys the moral that perseverance outweighs natural talent.

fable.etymology: 词根fabul-（叙述、讲述），源自拉丁语fari（说），指带有道德寓意的虚构短篇叙述

similar_words.description: 前者多含动物拟人化情节，后者多为带有宗教或道德说教的短篇故事

definitions.example: The designer uses eco-friendly fabric to make sustainable clothing for the fashion show.
The widespread pandemic has disrupted the social fabric of many countries, triggering economic recession.

fabric.etymology: 词根fabric-（编织、构造），源自拉丁语fabrica（作坊、构造物），原指编织而成的布料，引申为事物的整体结构

similar_words.description: 前者可指织物或抽象结构，后者仅指纺织制成的织物

definitions.example: The journalist was fired for fabricating data to sensationalize his report on environmental pollution.
Local artisans fabricate traditional pottery using ancient techniques passed down for generations.

fabricate.etymology: 词根fabric-（编织、构造）+动词后缀-ate，原指手工编织制作物品，后引申为捏造虚假内容

similar_words.description: 前者可指捏造虚假内容，后者侧重大规模工业生产制造

definitions.example: The research team achieved fabulous results in cancer treatment after five years of dedicated experiments.
The ancient book records fabulous creatures that are said to inhabit the remote mountains.

fabulous.etymology: 词根fabul-（叙述、讲述）+形容词后缀-ous，原指传说中叙述的，后引申为令人赞叹的

similar_words.description: 前者多用于书面语表极好的，后者多用于口语表极好的

definitions.example: The new online learning platform will facilitate the exchange of academic resources between universities.

facilitate.etymology: 词根facil-（容易的，源自拉丁语facilis）+动词后缀-ate，意为使事务开展变得更容易

similar_words.description: 前者侧重使事务开展更便利，后者侧重推动事物的发展或提升

definitions.example: The newly built hospital is equipped with advanced medical facilities to serve local residents.
She has a remarkable facility for mastering foreign languages within a short period of time.

facility.etymology: 词根facil-（容易的）+名词后缀-ity，原指做事容易的特质，后引申为配套设施或天赋才能

similar_words.description: 前者多指含建筑、设备的整体设施，后者仅指具体的工具或设备

definitions.example: It is an undeniable fact that climate change poses a serious threat to global biodiversity.

fact.etymology: 词根fact-（做、行动，源自拉丁语facere），原指已完成的行动，后引申为客观存在的事实

similar_words.description: 前者侧重客观存在的具体事实，后者侧重符合本质的抽象真相

definitions.example: A crucial factor contributing to the widening gap between the rich and the poor is unequal access to high-quality education.

factor.etymology: 词根「fact」（做、制造）+ 名词后缀「-or」（表事物）→ 促成某结果的事物→因素

similar_words.description: 「faction」意为派系、派别，为名词；「factor」意为因素、要素，为名词，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.meaning: （高等院校的）学院，系；全体教员
definitions.example: The faculty of law at this university has recruited three renowned professors specializing in international commercial law this year.
Critical thinking is an essential faculty for postgraduate students to conduct academic research.

faculty.etymology: 词根「fac」（做）+ 后缀「-ult」（表性质）+ 名词后缀「-y」→ 做事情的能力或机构→才能；学院

similar_words.description: 「facility」意为设施、设备，为名词；「faculty」意为学院、教员或才能，二者为同根词（词根「fac」=做），易混淆

definitions.example: As time goes by, the vivid memory of her childhood in the countryside has gradually faded.

fade.etymology: 源自古诺尔斯语，为原生动词，表“逐渐变弱消失”

similar_words.description: 「fad」意为一时的风尚、狂热，为名词；「fade」意为逐渐消失、褪色，为动词，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The failure of the company’s new product launch was attributed to insufficient market research.
The sudden power failure caused the entire production line to stop working for three hours.

failure.etymology: 动词「fail」（失败）+ 名词后缀「-ure」→ 失败；故障

similar_words.description: 「falter」意为蹒跚、犹豫，为动词；「failure」意为失败、故障，为名词，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: There was a faint hope that the missing hikers would be found before sunset.
The dehydrated hiker fainted halfway up the mountain due to extreme heat.

faint.etymology: 源自古法语，为原生词，表“虚弱的”

similar_words.description: 「feint」意为佯攻、虚晃，为名词/动词；「faint」意为微弱的、昏倒，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The judge promised to give a fair trial to every defendant regardless of their social status.
An international high-tech fair will be held in the city’s exhibition center next month.

fair.etymology: 源自古英语，为原生词，表“合适的、公平的”

similar_words.description: 「fare」意为费用、进展，为名词/动词；「fair」意为公平的、博览会，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: All participants in the essay contest will be evaluated fairly by a panel of professional writers.
The research paper is fairly well-written, though it needs minor revisions in the conclusion part.

fairly.etymology: 形容词「fair」（公平的）+ 副词后缀「-ly」→ 公平地；相当地

similar_words.description: 「rather」表“相当”时语气更强，常含负面或强调意味；「fairly」表“相当”时语气较弱，常含正面或中性意味

definitions.example: The fairy tale tells the story of a young girl who befriends a kind fairy living in the forest.

fairy.etymology: 源自古法语，为原生名词，表“仙女”

similar_words.description: 「ferry」意为渡船、摆渡，为名词/动词；「fairy」意为仙女、小精灵，为名词，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The public’s faith in the government’s ability to handle the crisis has been gradually restored.
People of different faiths gathered together to celebrate the international peace day.

faith.etymology: 源自拉丁语「fides」（信任），为原生名词，表“信任、信仰”

similar_words.description: 「fate」意为命运，为名词；「faith」意为信任、信仰，为名词，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The dog remained faithful to its owner even after the owner passed away.
The journalist’s report is a faithful account of the events that took place during the protest.

faithful.etymology: 名词「faith」（信任、信仰）+ 形容词后缀「-ful」（表“充满…的”）→ 充满信任的→忠实的

similar_words.description: 「fateful」意为命运攸关的、决定性的，为形容词；「faithful」意为忠实的、如实的，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: Consumers are warned to distinguish fake nutritional supplements from genuine ones on e-commerce platforms.
He tried to fake the experimental data to get his paper published in a top academic journal.

fake.etymology: 源自18世纪英国俚语，原指伪造的艺术品，无明确古典词根

similar_words.description: fake侧重指物品、身份、行为的伪造冒充；false侧重指信息、判断、状态的与事实不符

definitions.example: The research conclusion was proved false after repeated replication of the core experiment.
Some species evolved false eye patterns on their wings to confuse potential predators.

false.etymology: 源自拉丁语falsus（欺骗的），词根fall-=to deceive（欺骗），与fault同根

similar_words.description: false侧重状态、信息的不真实；fake侧重物品、行为的人为伪造

definitions.meaning: （嗓音、脚步等）颤抖，摇晃；犹豫，畏缩
definitions.example: When the researcher was questioned about his unethical data manipulation, his voice began to falter.
Global supply chain disruptions caused many small businesses' growth to falter in 2022.

falter.etymology: 源自古英语falterian（蹒跚行走），词根falt-=to stumble（绊倒）

similar_words.description: falter侧重行动、声音的不稳或行动上的犹豫；waver侧重意志、立场的主观动摇

definitions.example: The young biologist gained international fame for his breakthrough research on gene editing technology.

fame.etymology: 源自拉丁语fama（传闻、名声），词根fam-=to speak（说），与famous同根

similar_words.description: fame侧重广为人知的名气，可褒可贬；reputation侧重公众对某人的具体口碑与评价

definitions.example: The concept of 'information cocoon' is familiar to most scholars studying digital media effects.
His familiar tone with the newly hired female professor made other faculty members uncomfortable.

familiar.etymology: 源自拉丁语familiaris（家庭的），词根famil-=family（家庭），原指属于家庭内部的、无拘束的

similar_words.description: familiar指熟悉的、不拘束的；familial指家庭的、家族的，多用于正式学术语境

definitions.example: The prolonged drought and armed conflict led to a severe famine that affected over 2 million people in the region.

famine.etymology: 源自拉丁语fames（饥饿），词根fam-=hunger（饥饿）

similar_words.description: famine指饥荒，拼写结尾为-ine；fame指名声，拼写结尾为-e，二者词源与含义完全不同

definitions.example: The documentary on climate change has attracted millions of fans across different age groups.
The biased media coverage fanned public panic about the potential side effects of the new vaccine.

fan.etymology: 作「风扇」义源自古英语fann（簸箕、扇具）；作「爱好者」义为fanatic的口语缩写

similar_words.description: fan指普通的爱好者，中性；fanatic指极端狂热的分子，多含贬义

definitions.meaning: 花哨的，精致的；昂贵的，高档的
definitions.example: The marketing study found that young consumers are more likely to pay premium prices for fancy packaging.
Many college graduates fancy pursuing a career in the cultural and creative industry after graduation.

fancy.etymology: 源自古法语fantaisie（想象），词根phant-=to appear（显现），与fantasy同根

similar_words.description: fancy指一时的喜好、花哨的装饰或普通的想象；fantasy指脱离现实的系统性幻想、空想

definitions.meaning: 极好的，了不起的；巨大的
definitions.example: Fantastic advances in quantum computing have opened up new possibilities for data processing.
The proposal to solve the aging population problem within a decade was dismissed as fantastic by economists.

fantastic.etymology: 源自希腊语phantastikos（想象的），词根phant-=to appear（显现），与fantasy同根

similar_words.description: fantastic侧重指令人惊叹的或脱离现实的；fabulous侧重指传说中的或极其出色的

definitions.example: Psychological studies show that moderate fantasy can stimulate creativity in adolescents.

fantasy.etymology: 源自希腊语phantasia（想象、表象），词根phant-=to appear（显现）

similar_words.description: fantasy指脱离现实的系统性幻想或虚构题材；fancy指一时的奇想或花哨的装饰

definitions.example: High-speed rail passengers can get a 20% discount on the fare if they book tickets 15 days in advance.
Small and medium-sized enterprises fared much worse than large corporations during the global financial crisis.

fare.etymology: 源自古英语fær，原指旅程、旅途费用，名词义表票价，动词义由“走完旅程”引申为进展、过得

similar_words.description: 二者词形相近，fare可作名词表票价、动词表进展；fair作形容词表公平的，作名词表集市、展会

definitions.example: Before leaving for the overseas exchange program, she gave a warm farewell to her tutors and classmates.

farewell.etymology: 由动词fare（出行、走）和副词well（好）合成，原义为“祝你出行顺利”，后引申为告别、辞行

similar_words.description: 二者为同根词，farewell表告别，fare表票价或进展

definitions.example: Many local farmers have shifted to ecological planting methods to reduce pesticide use.

farmer.etymology: 由名词farm（农场）加表示从业者的名词后缀-er构成，指从事农业生产或经营农场的人

similar_words.description: 二者词形相近，farmer指农民、农场主；farther是far的比较级，表距离更远的（地）

definitions.example: The remote meteorological station is 20 miles farther away from the town than we expected.

farther.etymology: 为形容词far的比较级形式之一，专指空间、距离上的更远，区别于表程度的further

similar_words.description: 二者均为far的比较级，farther仅表示距离上的更远；further可表距离或程度上的更进一步，还可作副词表“此外”

definitions.example: The unique research perspective of the sociologist’s paper fascinates all the academic reviewers.

fascinate.etymology: 源自拉丁语fascinare（施魔法迷惑），现引申为事物极具吸引力使人入迷

similar_words.description: 二者为近义词，fascinate语气较强，指使人入迷；attract为普通用词，指引起注意或兴趣

definitions.example: The annual international fashion week has a significant impact on the global clothing industry.
The research team collected data in a scientific fashion to ensure the reliability of the conclusion.

fashion.etymology: 源自拉丁语factio（制作、样式），原指制作的款式，后引申为流行时尚、行事方式

similar_words.description: 二者为近义词，fashion侧重某一时期流行的时尚或行事方式；style侧重个人或群体特有的风格

definitions.example: Low-carbon lifestyles have become increasingly fashionable among young people in recent years.

fashionable.etymology: 由名词fashion加形容词后缀-able构成，意为符合当下流行时尚的

similar_words.description: 二者为近义词，fashionable侧重符合当下流行潮流的；popular侧重受大众广泛欢迎的

definitions.example: All passengers are required to fasten their seat belts during the take-off and landing of the aircraft.
The students fastened their attention on the professor’s explanation of the complex economic theory.

fasten.etymology: 源自古英语fæstnian（使牢固），与形容词fast（牢固的）同根，引申出固定、集中注意力等含义

similar_words.description: 二者为近义词，fasten侧重扣紧、固定物体或集中注意力；tie侧重用绳子、带子等系、绑

definitions.meaning: 肥胖的；多脂肪的；脂肪，肥肉
definitions.example: Medical research shows that excessive intake of high-fat food is associated with various chronic diseases.
The biotech company gained fat profits from its newly developed vaccine last year.

fat.etymology: 源自古英语fǣtt（油脂多的、肥胖的），后引申出利润丰厚的含义

similar_words.description: 二者为近义词，fat为常用词，可指人或物多脂肪的，也可指利润丰厚；obese为医学术语，指过度肥胖的

definitions.meaning: 致命的；灾难性的，毁灭性的
definitions.example: The sudden breakdown of the core equipment had a fatal impact on the entire engineering project.

fatal.etymology: 源自拉丁语fatum（命运、天命），原指命中注定的，后引申为致命的、灾难性的

similar_words.description: 二者为近义词，fatal侧重导致死亡或毁灭性后果的，强调结果不可挽回；deadly侧重可能致死的，也可表极度的

definitions.example: Many historians argue that the fate of ancient civilizations is closely tied to long-term environmental changes.

fate.etymology: 源自拉丁语fatum（预言的事），词根fat-表‘说、预言’，与同根词fatal（致命的、命运注定的）关联

similar_words.description: fate为名词表‘命运’，fatal为形容词表‘致命的、命运注定的’，二者为同根形近词，词性与词义侧重不同
二者均表‘命运’，fate侧重不可改变的命中注定，destiny侧重预先注定的重大或美好归宿

definitions.example: Prolonged exposure to blue light from electronic devices can lead to visual fatigue and reduced cognitive efficiency.

fatigue.etymology: 源自拉丁语fatigare（使疲惫），词根fat-表‘耗尽’，后缀-igue为名词后缀表状态

similar_words.description: fat为形容词表‘肥胖的’，fatigue为名词表‘疲劳’，二者为形近词，词性与词义完全不同

definitions.example: The peer review panel identified a fundamental fault in the research’s data collection methodology.

fault.etymology: 源自古法语faute（错误），词根fal-表‘欺骗、错误’，与同根词false（错误的、虚假的）关联

similar_words.description: fault为名词表‘过错、缺陷’，false为形容词表‘错误的、虚假的’，二者为同根形近词，词性与词义侧重不同

definitions.example: The faulty sensor in the space probe caused inaccurate data transmission during the mission.

faulty.etymology: 由名词fault（错误、缺陷）加形容词后缀-y构成，表‘有错误的、有缺陷的’

similar_words.description: faulty表‘有缺陷的’，faultless表‘无缺陷的、完美的’，二者为反义形近词

definitions.example: A majority of survey respondents expressed their favor for policies promoting renewable energy adoption.

favor.etymology: 源自拉丁语favor（好感），词根fav-表‘喜爱’，与同根词favorable（有利的）关联

similar_words.description: favor为名词/动词表‘偏爱、赞同’，flavor为名词表‘风味、味道’，二者为形近词，词义完全不同

definitions.example: The favorable economic conditions in the region attracted substantial foreign direct investment.

favorable.etymology: 由名词favor（赞同、好感）加形容词后缀-able构成，表‘有…性质的’，引申为‘有利的、赞同的’

similar_words.description: favorable表‘有利的、外界赞同的’，favorite表‘个人最喜爱的’，二者为同根形近词，词义侧重不同

definitions.example: Jane Austen’s 「Pride and Prejudice」 is a long-standing favorite among college English literature courses.

favorite.etymology: 由名词favor（喜爱）加后缀-ite构成，可作形容词‘最喜欢的’或名词‘最喜爱的人/物’

similar_words.description: 二者均表‘偏爱的’，favorite侧重个人主观的最喜爱，preferred侧重经选择后的优先偏爱

definitions.example: The academic journal requires that revised manuscripts be submitted via both email and fax for official verification.

fax.etymology: 为名词facsimile（摹本）的缩写，源自拉丁语facere（做）+ simile（相似的），原指摹制文件，后引申为‘传真’

similar_words.description: fax为名词/动词表‘传真’，fix为动词表‘修理、固定’，二者为形近词，词义完全不同

definitions.example: Fear of job loss has prompted many workers to pursue additional professional certification programs.

fear.etymology: 源自古英语fær（危险），词根fer-表‘害怕’，与同根词fearful（可怕的）关联

similar_words.description: fear为名词/动词表‘恐惧、害怕’，fair为形容词表‘公平的、白皙的’，二者为形近词，词义完全不同

definitions.example: The fearful impact of ocean acidification on coral reefs has been widely documented in marine biology studies.

fearful.etymology: 由名词fear（恐惧）加形容词后缀-ful构成，表‘充满…的’，引申为‘可怕的、担心的’

similar_words.description: fearful表‘可怕的、担心的’，fearless表‘无畏的、勇敢的’，二者为反义形近词

definitions.example: The research team concluded that the proposed renewable energy project is economically feasible.

feasible.etymology: feas（做，执行）+ -ible（形容词后缀，表「可…的」）

similar_words.description: feasible侧重客观条件（如经济、技术）上可行，practicable侧重实际操作层面可实施

definitions.meaning: 盛宴；（学术、文化等的）盛会
definitions.example: The annual international academic feast attracted scholars from over 50 countries.

feast.etymology: 源自古法语festa，原指宗教节日的庆祝宴会

similar_words.description: feast指盛宴或专题盛会，fest指一般性的节日或庆祝活动

definitions.example: Winning three consecutive national robotics competitions is an extraordinary feat for the undergraduate team.

feat.etymology: 源自拉丁语factum（已完成的事），同根词有fact、factor

similar_words.description: feat指功绩或技艺，feast指盛宴或盛会，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The ornithologist analyzed the micro-structure of the seabird's feather to study its migration habits.

feather.etymology: 源自古英语feather，为原始日耳曼语同源词

similar_words.description: feather指鸟类的羽毛，leather指动物皮革，拼写首字母不同易混淆

definitions.example: One prominent feature of the new artificial intelligence system is its ability to process natural language efficiently.
The documentary features interviews with Nobel Prize-winning scientists in climate research.

feature.etymology: feat（做，制作）+ -ure（名词后缀，表行为或结果），原指「制作出的东西」，后引申为特征

similar_words.description: feature指特征或以…为特色，future指未来，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The federal government issued a new regulation to strengthen the protection of endangered species.

federal.etymology: feder（联盟，联合）+ -al（形容词后缀），同根词有federation

similar_words.description: federal是形容词「联邦的」，federation是名词「联邦，联盟」，为同根词

definitions.example: The International Federation of Red Cross is a global humanitarian organization.

federation.etymology: feder（联盟，联合）+ -ation（名词后缀，表行为或状态）

similar_words.description: federation是名词「联邦，联盟」，federal是形容词「联邦的」，为同根词

definitions.example: Postgraduate applicants are required to pay a non-refundable application fee before the deadline.

fee.etymology: 源自古法语fee，原指「报酬，赏金」

similar_words.description: fee指各类费用、酬金，fare指车费、船费等交通费用，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.meaning: 虚弱的，无力的；微弱的，薄弱的
definitions.example: The feeble economic recovery in the region failed to create enough job opportunities for young people.

feeble.etymology: feeb（弱）+ -le（形容词后缀，表「易于…的」）

similar_words.description: feeble指虚弱的、薄弱的，fable指寓言，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The research center feeds real-time environmental data to the national climate monitoring system.
The dairy farm uses organic feed to improve the quality of cow's milk.

feed.etymology: 源自古英语fedan，为原始日耳曼语同源词

similar_words.description: feed侧重喂养、供给食物或信息，breed侧重繁殖、培育品种

definitions.example: The research team collected detailed feedback from 200 participants to refine their experimental design.

feedback.etymology: 由「feed（供给）」+「back（返回）」构成的合成词，原指机器的反馈信号，后引申为信息层面的反馈

similar_words.description: 二者为同根合成词，「feedback」侧重「反馈的信息」，「feed」侧重「供给、喂养」

definitions.example: The author’s vivid description of the old cottage conveys a deep feeling of childhood nostalgia.

feeling.etymology: 由「feel（感觉）」+「-ing（名词后缀，表动作或状态）」构成，指内心的主观感受或情感状态

similar_words.description: 二者为近义词，「feeling」侧重「主观的、具体的日常感受」，「emotion」侧重「强烈的、外显的情感」

definitions.example: After tripping over a loose stone, he fell heavily on the icy sidewalk.
In the medieval epic, the fell dragon burned down entire villages and terrorized the locals.

fell.etymology: 作动词时为「fall」的过去式，源自古英语「feallan」；作形容词时源自古英语「fel」，意为“残酷的”，为熟词僻义

similar_words.description: 「fell」作动词时是「fall」的过去式，表「落下；跌倒」；作形容词时为僻义，表「凶猛的」，与「fall」的动词含义无关

definitions.meaning: 同伴；伙伴；（身份相同的）同僚
definitions.example: Her fellow graduate students assisted her in conducting fieldwork in the remote Tibetan mountain area.

fellow.etymology: 源自古英语「feolaga」，由「feoh（财产）」+「lagan（放置）」构成，原指共同出资的合伙人，后引申为身份相同的同伴

similar_words.description: 二者为近义词，「fellow」侧重「身份相同的同伴（如同窗、同研究员）」，「colleague」侧重「职场或学术领域的同事」

definitions.meaning: 友谊；团体；（学术）研究奖学金
definitions.example: He received a prestigious national research fellowship to conduct a two-year study on Arctic climate change.

fellowship.etymology: 由「fellow（同伴）」+「-ship（名词后缀，表状态或身份）」构成，原指同伴间的友谊或团体，后引申为带研究任务的学术奖学金

similar_words.description: 二者为近义词，「fellowship」多指「带研究任务的学术奖学金」，「scholarship」泛指各类奖学金

definitions.meaning: 女性的；雌性的（n. 女性；雌性）
definitions.example: The study examines the career barriers faced by female engineers in male-dominated tech industries.

female.etymology: 源自拉丁语「femella」，意为“年轻女性”，后泛指人或动物的雌性

similar_words.description: 二者为形近且近义，「female」侧重「性别属性的客观描述（人或动物）」，「feminine」侧重「具有女性气质的」

definitions.example: The essay explores how traditional feminine roles have been redefined by contemporary feminist movements.

feminine.etymology: 源自拉丁语「femininus」，意为“女性的”，与「female」同根，但侧重女性气质而非性别本身

similar_words.description: 二者为形近且近义，「feminine」侧重「女性气质的」，「female」侧重「性别属性的客观描述」

definitions.example: The farmer built a tall wooden fence to keep his cattle from wandering into the neighboring cornfield.
The CEO fenced around the question of planned layoffs during the annual shareholders’ meeting.

fence.etymology: 源自古英语「fens」，意为“防御工事”，后引申为栅栏；作动词时源自击剑术语的“躲闪”，后引申为搪塞问题

similar_words.description: 二者为形近词，「fence」侧重「栅栏或搪塞问题」，「fend」侧重「照料自己或抵挡攻击」

definitions.example: The cross-river ferry carries hundreds of passengers and vehicles between the two cities daily.

ferry.etymology: 源自古英语「ferian」，意为“运送”，后引申为专门渡运人或物的渡船或渡口

similar_words.description: 二者为近义词，「ferry」侧重「渡运人或物过河/海峡」，「freight」侧重「运输大宗货物（尤指陆路或航空）」

definitions.example: The fertile alluvial soil along the Yangtze River supports abundant rice and wheat production.
The Nobel Prize-winning physicist has a fertile mind for groundbreaking theoretical research.

fertile.etymology: 源自拉丁语「fertilis」，由「ferre（带来、产生）」+「-tilis（形容词后缀）」构成，原指土地能产生作物，后引申为有创造力的

similar_words.description: 二者为同根词，「fertile」是形容词，表「肥沃的；多产的」，「fertilize」是动词，表「使肥沃；施肥」

definitions.example: Organic fertilizer is widely promoted in ecological agriculture to reduce chemical pollution.

fertilizer.etymology: 由形容词「fertile」（肥沃的）加名词后缀「-izer」构成，表‘使土壤肥沃的物品’

similar_words.description: 同根衍生词，「fertile」为形容词表‘肥沃的；多产的’，「fertilizer」为名词表‘肥料’

definitions.example: Many intangible cultural heritage festivals are held annually to preserve traditional customs.

festival.etymology: 由词根「fest-」（源于feast，表‘宴会、节日’）加名词后缀「-ival」构成

similar_words.description: 同根词，「feast」侧重‘盛宴；宗教节日’，「festival」侧重‘公共庆典；节日活动’

definitions.example: The laboratory assistant was asked to fetch the precision instruments from the storage room.

fetch.etymology: 源自古英语fetian，表‘取来、带来’，无现代英语常用词根拆分

similar_words.description: 近义动词，「fetch」指‘去取再带回（往返动作）’，「bring」指‘从别处带到说话处（单程动作）’

definitions.example: Feudal hierarchy was a prominent social structure in medieval Europe.

feudal.etymology: 由词根「feud-」（源于fief，表‘封地’）加形容词后缀「-al」构成

similar_words.description: 形近异义词，「feud」为名词表‘世仇；长期不和’，「feudal」为形容词表‘封建的’

definitions.example: The doctor advised the patient to rest until the fever subsided.
Tech startup fever has swept across major cities in recent years.

fever.etymology: 源自古英语fēfor，表‘发热、发烧’，无现代英语常用词根拆分

similar_words.description: 同根衍生词，「feverish」为形容词表‘发烧的；狂热的’

definitions.example: Dietary fiber helps improve gastrointestinal function and maintain metabolic balance.

fiber.etymology: 由词根「fib-」（表‘线、纤维’）加名词后缀「-er」构成

similar_words.description: 英式拼写变体，考研阅读中偶有出现

definitions.example: Dystopian fiction often reflects people’s concerns about social inequality and technological abuse.

fiction.etymology: 由词根「fict-」（表‘虚构、假装’）加名词后缀「-ion」构成

similar_words.description: 形近异义词，「friction」为名词表‘摩擦；矛盾’，「fiction」为名词表‘小说；虚构’

definitions.example: Fierce competition in the higher education sector has prompted universities to innovate teaching methods.

fierce.etymology: 源自古法语fier，表‘凶猛的、勇敢的’，无现代英语常用词根拆分

similar_words.description: 近义形容词，「fierce」侧重‘程度强烈、竞争激烈’，「violent」侧重‘带有暴力性的、破坏性的’

definitions.example: Local communities are fighting against the construction of a polluting factory near their homes.
The fight for gender equality has made significant progress in the past decades.

fight.etymology: 源自古英语feohtan，表‘战斗、搏斗’，无现代英语常用词根拆分

similar_words.description: 形近异义词，「fright」为名词表‘惊吓、恐怖’，「fight」为动词/名词表‘战斗’

definitions.example: Official figures reveal that the national carbon emission has decreased by 18% compared to five years ago.
Economists figure that the global economic growth rate will slow down slightly next year.

figure.etymology: 由词根「fig-」（表‘形状、形成’）加名词后缀「-ure」构成，动词义由‘塑造形象’引申为‘估计、推算’

similar_words.description: 近义名词，「digit」侧重‘单个数字、数位’，「figure」侧重‘统计数据、整体数字’

definitions.example: Researchers retrieved relevant data from the official file published by the Ministry of Education.
Many consumers filed complaints with the consumer protection agency over defective products.

file.etymology: 来自拉丁语filum（线），原指用线串起的纸质记录，后引申为文件、档案，动词义由「归档」动作引申为提交申请等

similar_words.description: 「file」可作名词表文件、档案，作动词表提交申请；「pile」可作名词表堆、堆积，作动词表堆放

definitions.example: Air filters are widely used in biological laboratories to ensure the purity of experimental environments.
Journalists must filter out unreliable sources when collecting materials for in-depth reports.

filter.etymology: 来自中古英语filtrum（毛毡，早期用于过滤的材料），后引申为过滤装置及过滤动作

similar_words.description: 「filter」为通用词汇，可作名词或动词，表过滤（装置/动作）；「filtrate」为专业术语，作名词表滤液，作动词表（化学领域的）过滤

definitions.example: The final decision of the university academic committee will be officially announced next week.
She won the gold medal in the women's 400m final at the national college sports meeting.

final.etymology: 来自拉丁语finalis（结尾的），词根fin-表示「结束、界限」，与finite、finance等同根

similar_words.description: 「final」作形容词表最终的，作名词表决赛；「finale」为名词，专指（音乐、戏剧等的）终曲、结局

definitions.example: After three years of dedicated research, she finally published her findings in a top international journal.

definitions.meaning: 最后（用于列举最后一项）
definitions.example: Finally, we should stress the significance of interdisciplinary cooperation in solving complex scientific problems.

finally.etymology: 由形容词final（最终的）加副词后缀-ly构成，表时间或逻辑上的最后

similar_words.description: 「finally」侧重时间或列举顺序上的最后；「eventually」侧重经过困难、努力或漫长过程后的最终结果

definitions.example: The central government has implemented a series of policies to stabilize the national finance amid economic downturn.
The provincial government financed the construction of the new rural primary school to improve local education conditions.

finance.etymology: 来自拉丁语finis（结束、界限），原指「结清债务」，后引申为与资金管理相关的财政、金融领域，动词义由「提供资金结清需求」引申而来

similar_words.description: 「finance」作名词表财政、金融，作动词表资助；「financing」为名词，专指融资行为或资金筹措

definitions.example: Many small and medium-sized enterprises faced severe financial pressure during the 2020 global pandemic.

financial.etymology: 由名词finance（财政、金融）加形容词后缀-ial构成，表与资金相关的属性

similar_words.description: 「financial」泛指各类金融、财务相关的；「fiscal」特指政府财政相关的（如fiscal policy财政政策）

definitions.example: The key findings of the climate change study have attracted widespread attention from the international academic community.
After a two-month trial, the judge announced the findings of the court regarding the intellectual property dispute.

finding.etymology: 由动词find（发现）加名词后缀-ing构成，原指发现的行为，后引申为科研调查结果及法庭裁决

similar_words.description: 「finding」侧重科研、调查等系统研究得出的结果；「discovery」侧重偶然或突破性的发现

definitions.example: She wore a delicate pearl ring on her middle finger during the graduation ceremony.
The pianist gently fingered the piano keys to adjust his playing state before the concert.

finger.etymology: 来自古英语finger，原指手的指状肢体，动词义由「用手指操作」的动作引申而来

similar_words.description: 「finger」指手指；「toe」指脚趾

definitions.example: Human beings must make rational use of finite natural resources to achieve long-term sustainable development.
In English grammar, a finite verb must agree with its subject in person and number, such as 'he works'.

finite.etymology: 来自拉丁语finitus（限定的），词根fin-表示「结束、界限」，与final、finance等同根，后缀-ite表形容词属性

similar_words.description: 「finite」表有限的；「infinite」为其反义词，表无限的，二者词根均为fin-（界限）

definitions.example: The firemen arrived at the fire scene within three minutes and successfully rescued the trapped elderly people.

definitions.meaning: 司炉工（负责维护炉火的工人）
definitions.example: In the 19th century, a fireman on a steam train was responsible for maintaining the furnace to ensure normal operation.

fireman.etymology: 由名词fire（火）加man（人）构成，原指与炉火打交道的工人，后引申为消防队员

similar_words.description: 「fireman」为传统说法，可指消防队员或司炉工；「firefighter」为正式中性说法，专指消防队员

definitions.example: The cottage's cozy atmosphere was enhanced by the crackling fire in the stone fireplace.

fireplace.etymology: 合成词，由fire（火）和place（场所）构成，指生火的专用场所

similar_words.description: 均以fire开头，fireman指消防员，fireplace指壁炉

definitions.example: The government took a firm stance on carbon emission reduction to address climate change.
The accounting firm was responsible for auditing the corporation's annual financial statements.

firm.etymology: 源自拉丁语firmus（牢固的、稳定的），后衍生出形容词「坚定的」和名词「商行」义

similar_words.description: 词形相近，farm指农场，firm指坚定的或商行

definitions.example: The local fisherman relied on traditional net-fishing techniques to make a living for decades.

fisherman.etymology: 合成词，由fisher（捕鱼者）和man（人）构成，指以捕鱼为职业的人

similar_words.description: 同根词，fisherman指渔民（人），fishery指渔业或渔场（领域/场所）

definitions.example: The coastal country implemented a seasonal fishing ban to protect its offshore fishery resources.

fishery.etymology: 由词根fisher（捕鱼）加名词后缀-y构成，表捕鱼的领域或场所，即渔业、渔场

similar_words.description: 同根词，fishery指渔业或渔场，fisherman指渔民

definitions.example: The protesters raised their fists in the air to demand equal rights and social justice.

fist.etymology: 源自古英语fyst，原指握紧的手掌

similar_words.description: 词形相近，fist指拳头，fit指适合的

definitions.example: It is fitting that the university awarded the professor a lifetime honor for his academic contributions.
She scheduled a final fitting for her graduation gown one week before the ceremony.

fitting.etymology: 由动词fit（适合）加后缀-ing构成，作形容词表合适的，作名词表试穿、配件

similar_words.description: 词形相近，fitting指合适的或试穿，fixing指固定或修理
