definitions.example: The worker used screws to fix the solar panels firmly to the roof of the building.
The team worked collaboratively to fix the technical glitch in the data analysis system.

fix.etymology: 源自拉丁语figere（固定、钉牢），后衍生出解决、处理等引申义

similar_words.description: 同根词，fix是动词表固定、解决，fixture是名词表固定装置

definitions.example: The bathroom renovation included replacing outdated fixtures such as the sink and toilet.
The football association released the fixture list for the upcoming national league season.

fixture.etymology: 由动词fix（固定）加名词后缀-ture构成，表固定的事物（即固定装置），后衍生出赛事预定场次义

similar_words.description: 同根词，fixture是名词表固定装置或赛事场次，fix是动词表固定、解决

definitions.example: The national flag was hoisted at the opening ceremony of the international academic conference.
After three hours of continuous discussion, the participants' enthusiasm began to flag.

flag.etymology: 源自古英语flaga（布制旗帜），后因旗帜下垂的状态引申出动词「衰退」义

similar_words.description: 词形相近，flag指旗帜或衰退，flame指火焰或燃烧

definitions.example: The dry twigs caught fire and sent bright flames spreading through the forest underbrush.

flame.etymology: 源自拉丁语flamma（火焰），后衍生出动词燃烧、爆发等义

similar_words.description: 词形相近，flame指火焰或燃烧，flag指旗帜或衰退

definitions.example: The seagulls flapped their wings wildly as they took off from the coastal pier.
The local residents were in a flap over the unexpected power outage.

flap.etymology: 拟声词，源自古英语「flappe」，模拟物体快速拍打或振动的声响

similar_words.description: 「flap」侧重快速拍打（如振翅）或情绪慌乱，「flop」侧重笨重落下或（作品/计划）失败

definitions.example: The campfire flared up when a gust of wind swept through the pine forest.
A distress flare was detected by the coast guard within ten minutes.

flare.etymology: 源自拉丁语「flagrare」（燃烧），同根词有考研核心词「flagrant」（公然的）

similar_words.description: 「flare」侧重火焰或情绪的突然、短暂爆发，「glare」侧重刺眼的强光或愤怒的注视

definitions.example: A bolt of lightning flashed across the pitch-black sky during the thunderstorm.
The news of the scientific breakthrough spread across the campus in a flash.

flash.etymology: 拟声词，源自古英语「flæsc」，模拟闪光的瞬间声响

similar_words.description: 「flash」侧重快速、短暂的闪光或念头闪现，「flare」侧重火焰或冲突的持续闪烁/爆发

definitions.example: The architect designed a building with a completely flat roof to install solar panels.
The academic lecture felt flat without case studies or interactive discussions.

flat.etymology: 源自古诺尔斯语「flatr」（平的），同根词有「flatten」（使变平）

similar_words.description: 「flat」侧重表面无起伏，「level」侧重处于水平状态或无倾斜

definitions.example: Some reporters flatter celebrities to gain exclusive interview opportunities.
The warm, soft lighting in the gallery flattered the delicate watercolor paintings.

flatter.etymology: 源自古法语「flater」（抚摸，讨好），同根词有「flattery」（奉承）

similar_words.description: 「flatter」指奉承讨好他人，「flaunt」指炫耀、卖弄自身优势

definitions.example: The traditional dish has a unique flavor blending sweet, sour and spicy notes.
The documentary captures the authentic flavor of rural life in Southwest China.

flavor.etymology: 源自拉丁语「flavus」（黄色的，最初指香料的色泽），同根词有「flavorful」（美味的）

similar_words.description: 「flavor」侧重食物的综合风味或事物的地域/时代特色，「taste」侧重味觉感受或审美品味

definitions.example: A tiny flaw in the steel beam led to the collapse of the temporary bridge.
A single logical error flawed the entire argument of the research paper.

flaw.etymology: 源自古诺斯语「flaga」（碎片，瑕疵），同根词有「flawed」（有缺陷的）

similar_words.description: 「flaw」侧重物体或论证的细微、内在缺陷，「fault」侧重人的过错或应承担的责任

definitions.example: Thousands of civilians fled the conflict zone to seek asylum in neighboring nations.
The two-week summer program fled by before the students could finish all their assignments.

flee.etymology: 源自古英语「fleon」（逃跑），同根词有「fleeing」（逃跑的）

similar_words.description: 「flee」是动词，指逃跑或时间飞逝；「flea」是名词，指跳蚤，二者易因拼写混淆

definitions.example: The national naval fleet conducted a joint military exercise in the western Pacific.
The fleet runner won the 200-meter race with a personal best time.

fleet.etymology: 源自古英语「fleot」（船，浮标），同根词有「fleetness」（快速）

similar_words.description: 「fleet」作名词指舰队/船队，作形容词指快速的；「flee」是动词，指逃跑，二者易因拼写混淆

definitions.example: Nutritionists recommend eating lean flesh such as chicken or fish for a balanced diet.
Medieval philosophers often explored the tension between the flesh and the soul.

flesh.etymology: 源自古英语「flæsc」（肉），同根词有「fleshy」（多肉的）

similar_words.description: 「flesh」指肉、肉体，「fresh」指新鲜的，二者易因拼写混淆

definitions.meaning: 灵活的，可调整的（指计划、制度、人等）
definitions.example: Universities are encouraged to design flexible curricula to meet the diverse needs of students with different career goals.

definitions.meaning: 柔韧的，易弯曲的（指物体）
definitions.example: The flexible rubber tube can be bent into various shapes without breaking.

flexible.etymology: 词根flex-表「弯曲」，后缀-ible表「可…的」，字面义「可弯曲的」，引申为「灵活的」

similar_words.description: 同根反义词，inflexible指「僵化的、不可变通的」，与flexible语义完全相反

definitions.example: He flung the unsatisfactory research proposal onto the professor’s desk in a fit of irritation.

definitions.meaning: 一时的放纵，短暂的行乐
definitions.example: The gap year is often viewed as a short fling before young people commit to formal education or career.

fling.etymology: 源自中古英语flingan，表「猛推、猛扔」，无常见考研词根，为单音节动作动词

similar_words.description: 形近词，sling指「用吊索悬挂或抛送」，fling指「无辅助的猛力扔掷」

definitions.example: After the monetary reform, the country’s currency was allowed to float against other major currencies in the foreign exchange market.

float.etymology: 与flow（流动）同源，源自古英语flotian，表「浮在水面」，引申为「（数值等）浮动」

similar_words.description: 形近词，fleet指「舰队；快速的」，float指「漂浮；浮动」，语义完全不同

definitions.meaning: 聚集，群集（指人或动物成群移动）
definitions.example: Every year, thousands of scholars flock to the international academic conference to exchange cutting-edge research findings.
A flock of wild geese flew over the lake at dawn.

flock.etymology: 源自古英语floc，表「一群（动物）」，引申为动词「群集」

similar_words.description: 形近词，block指「块；街区；阻碍」，flock指「群；聚集」，语义完全不同

definitions.meaning: 充斥；淹没（指大量事物涌入或水覆盖）
definitions.example: During the Double Eleven shopping festival, e-commerce platforms are flooded with promotional advertisements and discount information.
The local government built a new dam to prevent flood damage in the rainy season.

flood.etymology: 与flow同源，源自古英语flōd，表「洪水」，引申为动词「淹没；充斥」

similar_words.description: 形近词，blood指「血液」，flood指「洪水；充斥」，拼写仅首字母不同

definitions.example: The traditional pastry-making process requires high-gluten flour and precise temperature control.

flour.etymology: 与flower同源，源自古法语flour（原指「花」），因面粉是谷物的精华，如花般提炼而来

similar_words.description: 形近词，flower指「花」，flour指「面粉」，拼写仅差一个字母i，极易混淆

definitions.example: With the support of national cultural policies, traditional Chinese opera has begun to flourish among young audiences in recent years.
The poet added a few linguistic flourishes to the last stanza to enhance its artistic effect.

flourish.etymology: 词根flour-（=flower花），后缀-ish（动词后缀），字面义「像花一样盛开」，引申为「繁荣」

similar_words.description: 同根词，flour指「面粉」，flourish指「繁荣」，同源但语义差异极大

definitions.meaning: （名词）流量，流动；（动词）流动，持续移动
definitions.example: The smooth flow of logistics is a key factor in ensuring the stability of the global supply chain.

flow.etymology: 源自古英语flōwan，为基础词根，表「液体流动」，引申为「（事物）持续移动」

similar_words.description: 同根近义词，float指「漂浮」，flow指「流动」，均与液体或事物的移动相关

definitions.example: Public health departments usually launch flu vaccination campaigns for high-risk groups before the winter outbreak.

flu.etymology: 为医学术语influenza（流感）的口语化缩写形式，无复杂词缀

similar_words.description: 形近词，flue指「烟道、通风管」，flu指「流感」，拼写仅差一个字母e

definitions.meaning: 波动，起伏（指数值、情绪等不规则变动）
definitions.example: According to the latest statistical report, the consumer price index has fluctuated within a narrow range over the past six months.

fluctuate.etymology: 词根fluct-表「波浪」，后缀-uate（动词后缀），字面义「像波浪一样起伏」，引申为「（数值等）波动」

similar_words.description: 同根近义词，flux指「流动、变迁」，fluctuate指「（连续的）波动」，均与「变动」相关

definitions.example: Students with fluent English communication skills are more likely to stand out in postgraduate admission interviews.

fluent.etymology: 词根「flu-」（表「流动」）+ 形容词后缀「-ent」（表「具有…性质的」），引申为「能顺畅流动的」

similar_words.description: fluent 侧重语言、动作的流畅性；fluid 侧重液体、状态的流动性或易变性

definitions.example: The global economic situation remains fluid and unpredictable amid ongoing geopolitical frictions.
Engineers need to calculate the pressure of the fluid in the pipeline to ensure safety.

fluid.etymology: 词根「flu-」（表「流动」）+ 形容词后缀「-id」（表「具有…特征的」），引申为「易流动、易变化的」

similar_words.description: fluid 侧重状态的易变性或物质的流动性；fluent 侧重语言、动作的流畅连贯性

definitions.example: The built-in wardrobe is flush with the wall to maximize the usable space of the bedroom.
She flushed when the professor praised her innovative research design in public.

flush.etymology: 源自拉丁语「fluxus」（表「流动」），引申为「血流上涌（脸红）、水流冲洗、表面齐平（如水流平整）」

similar_words.description: flush 侧重齐平、脸红、冲洗；flash 侧重闪光、闪现、快速移动

definitions.example: The real estate foam in the early 2000s triggered a nationwide financial crisis in the US.

foam.etymology: 源自古英语「fam」，本义为液体表面的气泡集合体，引申为「经济领域的虚假繁荣泡沫」

similar_words.description: foam 指泡沫、起泡沫；roam 指漫游、闲逛（无目的的行走）

definitions.meaning: 集中（注意力、精力、资源等）
definitions.example: Postgraduate candidates should focus on improving their academic writing skills for thesis submission.
The focus of the academic conference is the application of artificial intelligence in environmental protection.

focus.etymology: 源自拉丁语「focus」（本义为「火炉、炉膛」，是热量集中的中心），引申为「注意力、资源的集中点」

similar_words.description: focus 侧重将目标集中于单一中心；concentrate 侧重聚集、浓缩，语义更强调密度或数量的集中

definitions.example: The conflicting experimental data fogged the researchers’ judgment on the drug’s efficacy.
The heavy fog delayed the start of the scientific expedition to the polar region.

fog.etymology: 源自古英语「fog」，本义为「雾气」，引申为「使视线或思维模糊的状态」

similar_words.description: fog 是名词/动词，表雾/使模糊；foggy 是形容词，表「有雾的、模糊的」

definitions.example: Hundreds of small retail stores folded during the pandemic due to plummeting customer traffic.
She folded the research papers neatly and placed them in the file folder.

fold.etymology: 源自古英语「fealdan」，本义为「折叠」，引申为「企业像被折叠一样停止运营（倒闭）」

similar_words.description: fold 侧重折叠、企业倒闭；hold 侧重握住、持有、举行（活动）

definitions.example: The exhibition showcases a variety of folk handicrafts from ethnic minority regions in China.
Folk in rural areas often have a stronger sense of community than those in big cities.

folk.etymology: 源自古英语「folc」，本义为「人民、民族」，引申为「来自民间的、非官方的」

similar_words.description: folk 指人们、民间的；fork 指叉子、分岔（道路、河流）

definitions.meaning: 遵循，遵守（规则、逻辑、指导）
definitions.example: All research participants must follow the ethical guidelines approved by the university’s review board.
I couldn’t follow the professor’s complex argument about quantum physics in the lecture.

follow.etymology: 源自古英语「folgian」，本义为「跟随」，引申为「遵循规则、领会逻辑」

similar_words.description: follow 是动词，表跟随、遵循、理解；fellow 是名词/形容词，表同伴、同类的

definitions.example: The following statistics demonstrate the correlation between social media use and mental health issues.
The environmental activist has a large following on social media platforms.

following.etymology: 动词「follow」（跟随）+ 形容词后缀「-ing」，引申为「跟随在后面的、下述的」

similar_words.description: following 是形容词/名词，表下述的、追随者；followed 是动词过去式/过去分词，表被跟随、遵循

definitions.example: Many retired people are fond of gardening, a hobby that helps them stay active and socially connected.

fond.etymology: 源自古法语fondre（沉迷、熔化），后演变为形容词表对人或事物的喜爱

similar_words.description: 「fond」为形容词表喜爱，「found」为动词表建立或「find」的过去式/过去分词

definitions.example: The protagonist in the novel refused to be labeled a fool for his innovative yet controversial ideas.
Some unethical advertisers fool consumers into buying overpriced products with false claims.

fool.etymology: 源自古法语fol（愚蠢的），衍生出名词「傻瓜」和动词「愚弄」义

similar_words.description: 「fool」表傻瓜/愚弄，「foul」为形容词表污秽的或动词表犯规

definitions.example: It would be foolish to ignore the long-term risks of environmental pollution when pursuing short-term economic gains.

foolish.etymology: 由名词「fool」加形容词后缀「-ish」构成，表具有傻瓜性质的

similar_words.description: 「foolish」侧重缺乏智慧的傻，「foolhardy」侧重鲁莽不顾后果的愚勇

definitions.example: The detective followed the faint footstep in the muddy path to trace the suspect’s escape route.

footstep.etymology: 合成词，由「foot（脚）」+「step（步）」构成，表脚的动作或留下的痕迹

similar_words.description: 「footstep」侧重脚步动作或声音，「footprint」侧重脚留下的实体痕迹（足迹）

definitions.example: University regulations forbid students from bringing electronic devices into the examination hall to prevent cheating.

forbid.etymology: 由前缀「for-（表反对、禁止）」+「bid（命令、吩咐）」构成，即「命令反对」

similar_words.description: 「forbid」为普通用词，多指官方或长辈的禁止；「prohibit」为正式用词，侧重法律或规章制度的强制禁止

definitions.example: The force of the earthquake caused severe damage to the ancient buildings in the town.
The sharp decline in sales forced the company to lay off a large number of employees.

force.etymology: 源自拉丁语fortis（强壮的），衍生出「力量」「强迫」等义

similar_words.description: 「force」为名词/动词表力量/强迫，「enforce」为动词表强制实施（法律、规定等）

definitions.example: The fore section of the research vessel was equipped with advanced ocean-monitoring devices.

fore.etymology: 源自古英语fore（在前），为「fore-」前缀的词根形式，表位置在前

similar_words.description: 「fore」表在前的/前部，「four」为基数词表数量四

definitions.example: The International Monetary Fund forecast a 3.2% growth rate for the global economy this year.
The weather forecast warned of severe thunderstorms in the southern provinces this weekend.

forecast.etymology: 由前缀「fore-（在前）」+「cast（计算、投掷）」构成，即提前计算或推测

similar_words.description: 「forecast」侧重对天气、经济等可量化领域的系统预报，「predict」侧重基于经验或数据的一般性预测

definitions.example: Sweat beaded on his forehead as he struggled to finish the difficult academic paper before the deadline.

forehead.etymology: 合成词，由前缀「fore-（在前）」+「head（头）」构成，指头的前部

similar_words.description: 「forehead」指头的前部（前额），「forearm」指胳膊的前部（前臂）

definitions.example: The city’s international culture festival attracts thousands of foreigners from all over the world every year.

foreigner.etymology: 由形容词「foreign（外国的）」加表人的后缀「-er」构成

similar_words.description: 「foreigner」侧重国籍为外国的人，「stranger」侧重不认识的人（陌生人）

definitions.example: The foremost challenge for environmental scientists is to quantify the impact of microplastics on marine ecosystems.

foremost.etymology: 由前缀fore-（先、前）和形容词most（最）合成，表地位或重要性居首

similar_words.description: 「foremost」强调地位或重要性居首，「utmost」强调程度或范围达到极限

definitions.example: Financial analysts failed to foresee the sudden collapse of the regional banking system.

foresee.etymology: 由前缀fore-（预先）和动词see（看）合成，表预先看到事物的发展趋势

similar_words.description: 「foresee」侧重凭借直觉或分析预见，「forecast」侧重基于数据或规律预报（如天气、经济）

definitions.example: Under current policies, renewable energy will account for 30% of total energy consumption in the foreseeable future.

foreseeable.etymology: 由动词foresee（预见）加形容词后缀-able（可…的）派生而来

similar_words.description: 「foreseeable」侧重人可预先感知的，「predictable」侧重可通过规律预测的，常含贬义

definitions.example: The temperate forest ecosystem provides essential habitat for numerous endangered species.

forest.etymology: 源自拉丁语forestis，原指「门外的（未开垦的）土地」，后引申为大面积树木覆盖的区域

similar_words.description: 「forest」指大面积的原始或人工森林，「wood」指小面积的树林，常用复数形式woods

definitions.example: The discovery of penicillin changed the history of medicine forever.

forever.etymology: 由介词for（为了）和副词ever（永远）合成，表时间上的永久持续

similar_words.description: 「forever」为日常及书面通用词，「eternally」为正式文学或宗教用词，语气更强

definitions.meaning: （因违规、违约等）丧失（权利、资格等）
definitions.example: Athletes who test positive for banned substances will forfeit their medals and competition eligibility.
The court ordered the company to pay a $1 million forfeit for violating environmental regulations.

forfeit.etymology: 源自古法语forfait，原指「犯罪、过错」，后引申为因过错需付出的代价或丧失的东西

similar_words.description: 「forfeit」侧重因自身违规被迫丧失，「confiscate」侧重官方强制没收（如海关没收违禁品）

definitions.example: The suspect was arrested for attempting to forge a university diploma to apply for the job.
The ancient craftsmen could forge delicate bronze artifacts with remarkable precision.

forge.etymology: 源自拉丁语fabricare（制造），原指锻造金属，后引申为「制造虚假物品」即伪造

similar_words.description: 「forge」侧重伪造文件、签名等具体文本或物品，「fabricate」侧重编造谎言、证据等抽象内容

definitions.example: Many philosophers argue that the ability to forgive is a sign of moral maturity and emotional resilience.

forgive.etymology: 由前缀for-（完全）和动词give（给予）合成，原指「完全给予（放弃怨恨）」，后引申为原谅

similar_words.description: 「forgive」侧重个人情感上的宽恕，「pardon」侧重官方或权威的赦免（如总统赦免罪犯）

definitions.example: At the fork in the hiking trail, hikers must choose between the steep mountain path and the gentle valley route.
Western dining etiquette requires using a fork and knife to eat most main courses.

fork.etymology: 源自古英语forca，原指「叉状工具」，后引申为道路、河流等呈Y形的分岔点

similar_words.description: 「fork」特指道路、河流等的分岔点（呈Y形），「branch」泛指树枝、机构分支等任何分支

definitions.example: Researchers must submit a formal proposal outlining their study objectives and methodology before receiving funding.

formal.etymology: 由名词form（形式）加形容词后缀-al（与…相关的）派生而来，表符合特定形式要求的

similar_words.description: 「formal」侧重符合礼仪、规范或形式要求，「official」侧重具有官方身份或权威性

definitions.example: The research paper must follow the specified academic format to be accepted by the journal.

definitions.meaning: 使格式化；按特定格式整理
definitions.example: Students need to format their datasets properly before submitting them for statistical analysis.

format.etymology: 源自拉丁语「forma」（形式），加名词/动词后缀「-at(e)」，表“具有特定形式的事物/使具有特定形式”

similar_words.description: 同根词，「form」表泛指的「形式、形状」，「format」表特指的「固定格式、版式」

definitions.example: The formation of new social norms is closely linked to technological advancements in the digital age.

formation.etymology: 由词根「form」（形式）加名词后缀「-ation」（表行为或结果）构成，指“形成的过程或结果”

similar_words.description: 同根词，「formation」表「形成的过程/结果」，「format」表「已成型的固定格式」

definitions.example: Between the two hypotheses, the former is supported by more empirical evidence from recent studies.

former.etymology: 由前缀「fore-」（前）加古英语比较级后缀「-mer」构成，表“在前面的，前者的”

similar_words.description: 对应词，「the former」指代前文提到的两个事物中的「前者」，「the latter」指代「后者」，为考研阅读高频指代考点

definitions.example: Completing the cross-disciplinary research project within six months proved a formidable challenge for the team.

formidable.etymology: 源自拉丁语「formidabilis」，由词根「formid」（=fear，害怕）加形容词后缀「-able」（可…的）构成，原义“令人害怕的”，现引申为“难对付的”

similar_words.description: 形近近义词，「forbidding」侧重「外表令人生畏、望而却步」，「formidable」侧重「事物难度或实力令人敬畏、难对付」

definitions.example: The government proposed a new formula for distributing educational resources to underdeveloped regions.

formula.etymology: 源自拉丁语「formula」（小的形式），由词根「form」（形式）加指小后缀「-ula」构成，指“固定的模式、公式”

similar_words.description: 同根词，「formula」表「已成型的方案、公式」，「formulation」表「制定方案/公式的过程」

definitions.example: The committee spent three months formulating a comprehensive strategy to tackle climate change.

formulate.etymology: 由词根「form」（形式）加指小后缀「-ul」加动词后缀「-ate」构成，表“使形成固定形式→系统制定、明确表达”

similar_words.description: 同根词，「formulate」是动词，表「制定、构想」，「formula」是名词，表「制定的结果（方案、公式）」

definitions.example: The formulation of the new economic policy took into account the concerns of small business owners.

formulation.etymology: 由动词「formulate」加名词后缀「-ion」（表行为或结果）构成，指“制定的过程或结果”

similar_words.description: 同根词，「formulation」表「制定的过程或表述」，「formula」表「最终的方案、公式」

definitions.meaning: 向前；往外（常与set、bring等搭配构成短语）
definitions.example: The scholar set forth a groundbreaking theory that challenged traditional academic views in his latest book.

forth.etymology: 源自古英语「forth」，与前缀「fore-」（前）同源，表“向前、往外”

similar_words.description: 近义词，「forth」更正式，多用于书面语固定搭配（如set forth），「forward」更通用，可表「向前的/地」

definitions.example: The forthcoming academic conference will focus on the application of artificial intelligence in education.
The official was not forthcoming about the details of the new policy.

forthcoming.etymology: 由副词「forth」（向前）加现在分词「coming」（来的）合成，表“即将到来的”

similar_words.description: 近义词，「forthcoming」更正式，多用于学术或官方语境，「upcoming」更口语化，适用范围更广

definitions.example: The research team will submit their interim report in a fortnight to the funding agency.

fortnight.etymology: 源自古英语「feowertyne niht」（十四夜），是「fourteen nights」的缩写形式，指“两周的时间”

similar_words.description: 易混词，「biweekly」可表「两周一次的」或「每周两次的」，易产生歧义；「fortnight」仅指「两周的时间」，无歧义

definitions.example: Fortunate survivors of the forest fire received timely medical treatment and material support from the government.

fortunate.etymology: 源自名词「fortune」（运气）+形容词后缀「-ate」，表“有运气的”

similar_words.description: 均表“幸运的”，「fortunate」为正式书面语，符合考研阅读的正式语境；「lucky」偏口语化，较少出现在考研正式文本中

definitions.example: Many social science studies indicate that personal effort outweighs good fortune in achieving long-term career success.

fortune.etymology: 源自拉丁语「fortuna」（命运、运气），为「fortunate」的名词形式

similar_words.description: 同根词，「fortune」为名词，表“运气、财富”；「fortunate」为形容词，表“幸运的”

definitions.meaning: 论坛，讨论会；（古罗马）公共集会场所
definitions.example: The international forum on sustainable development gathered policymakers and scholars to discuss global climate challenges.

forum.etymology: 源自拉丁语「forum」（古罗马公共集会场所），现代引申为“公开讨论的平台”

similar_words.description: 均表“学术讨论会”，「forum」侧重公开参与、广泛讨论；「symposium」侧重专题性、专业性的学术研讨

definitions.example: Paleontologists have unearthed well-preserved dinosaur fossils in the remote mountainous area, shedding new light on prehistoric ecosystems.
Some veteran managers in traditional industries are regarded as fossils for rejecting digital transformation strategies.

fossil.etymology: 源自拉丁语「fossilis」（被挖掘出的），由动词「fodere」（挖掘）派生，原指挖掘出的古生物遗迹

similar_words.description: 同根词，「fossil」为名词，表“化石、顽固的人”；「fossilize」为动词，表“使成化石、使僵化”

definitions.example: Top universities aim to foster students' critical thinking and innovative abilities through interdisciplinary courses.
The local welfare institution provides support for foster families to ensure the healthy growth of orphaned children.

foster.etymology: 源自古英语「fostrian」（养育），与名词「food」（食物）同源，原指提供食物抚育后代

similar_words.description: 均表“培养”，「foster」侧重促进事物发展、扶植；「nurture」侧重悉心照料、培育个体成长

definitions.example: The industrial park was fined for releasing foul exhaust gas that violated national environmental protection standards.

definitions.meaning: 不正当行为，阴谋（尤指谋杀）
definitions.example: The forensic team found evidence of foul play during the investigation of the famous writer's sudden death.

foul.etymology: 源自古英语「ful」（肮脏的），与德语「faul」（腐烂的）同源，引申为“犯规、不正当行为”

similar_words.description: 拼写仅差一个字母，「foul」表“肮脏的、犯规、不正当行为”；「fowl」表“家禽”，极易混淆

definitions.example: Tsinghua University, founded in 1911, has cultivated a large number of outstanding talents for China's scientific and technological development.

definitions.meaning: （find的过去式/过去分词）找到，发现
definitions.example: The research team found a significant correlation between sleep deprivation and cognitive decline in middle-aged adults.

found.etymology: 作“建立”讲时，源自拉丁语「fundare」（奠基），与「foundation」同根；作「find」的过去式/过去分词讲时，源自古英语「findan」

similar_words.description: 「found」作动词原形表“建立”，作「find」的过去式/过去分词表“找到”；「find」为动词原形，表“找到、发现”

definitions.example: A solid foundation in mathematical thinking is essential for pursuing advanced studies in computer science.
The construction team reinforced the foundation of the building to withstand potential earthquakes.

foundation.etymology: 源自动词「found」（建立）+名词后缀「-ation」，表“建立的基础或机构”

similar_words.description: 均表“基础”，「foundation」侧重抽象的理论、知识或组织基础；「base」侧重具体的物理基础或基地

definitions.example: The ancient library of Alexandria was regarded as a fountain of knowledge for scholars across the Mediterranean region.

fountain.etymology: 源自拉丁语「fons」（泉水），与名词「fount」（泉水）同源，引申为“知识等的源泉”

similar_words.description: 拼写相近，「fountain」表“喷泉、源泉”；「font」表“字体、圣洗池”，易混淆

definitions.example: The shrewd negotiator was known as a fox in the business circle for his exceptional bargaining skills.

fox.etymology: 源自古英语「fox」，与德语「fuchs」（狐狸）同源，引申为“狡猾的人”

similar_words.description: 拼写仅差首字母，「fox」表“狐狸、狡猾的人”；「ox」表“公牛”，易混淆

definitions.example: Only a small fraction of the local population can afford to buy a new apartment in the city center.

fraction.etymology: 词根frac-（源自拉丁语frangere「打破」）+名词后缀-tion，指被打破后形成的小块，引申为「小部分」；数学领域指整体拆分的部分，即「分数」。

similar_words.description: 「fraction」表抽象的小部分或数学分数；「fracture」表实体的断裂动作或骨折状态，拼写相近易混淆。

definitions.meaning: v. 断裂，折断；n. 骨折，断裂
definitions.example: The intense pressure from the landslide caused the rock cliff to fracture and slide down.

fracture.etymology: 词根frac-（打破）+后缀-ture（可作名词/动词后缀），表打破的动作或状态，引申为「断裂」；医学领域指「骨折」。

similar_words.description: 「fracture」侧重断裂的动作或骨折的状态；「fragment」侧重破碎后形成的小块或文本片段，语义相关易混淆。

definitions.example: The fragile ecosystem of the Arctic is severely threatened by global warming.

fragile.etymology: 词根frag-（打破）+形容词后缀-ile（表「易...的」），指易被打破的，引申为「易碎的」；抽象义指「脆弱的」（如生态、情绪）。

similar_words.description: 「fragile」词根为frag-，表易碎的、脆弱的；「fragrant」词根为fragr-，表芳香的，拼写高度相似易混淆。

definitions.meaning: n. 碎片，片段；v. 分裂，破碎
definitions.example: The archaeologist discovered a fragment of a bronze sword dating back to the Han Dynasty.

fragment.etymology: 词根frag-（打破）+名词后缀-ment，指被打破后形成的小块，即「碎片、片段」；动词义由名词引申，表「分裂、破碎」。

similar_words.description: 「fragment」侧重破碎后的实体或文本片段；「fracture」侧重断裂的动作或骨折状态，语义相关易混淆。

definitions.example: The fragrant osmanthus flowers in the campus fill the air with a sweet scent every autumn.

fragrant.etymology: 词根fragr-（源自拉丁语fragrans「散发香味的」）+形容词后缀-ant，指散发香味的，即「芳香的」。

similar_words.description: 「fragrant」词根为fragr-，表芳香的；「fragile」词根为frag-，表易碎的，拼写高度相似易混淆。

definitions.meaning: n. 框架，结构；v. 设计，构建
definitions.example: The civil engineer designed a steel frame to support the weight of the new bridge.
The innocent journalist was framed by the corrupt official and imprisoned without trial.

frame.etymology: 源自古英语framian「推进，构建」，引申为具体的「框架、结构」；动词义「诬陷、捏造」为口语化引申，属考研常考熟词僻义。

similar_words.description: 「frame」指单个具体的框架或构建/诬陷动作；「framework」指整体抽象的体系或框架，同根易混淆。

definitions.example: The new environmental law provides a comprehensive framework for regulating industrial emissions.

framework.etymology: 由frame（框架）+work（表「结构、体系」的复合成分）构成，指整体的工作结构或体系，考研常考政策、理论的「框架」。

similar_words.description: 「framework」侧重抽象的整体体系；「frame」侧重具体的单个框架或动作，同根易混淆。

definitions.example: The supervisor gave a frank feedback on the intern's work, pointing out both advantages and areas for improvement.

frank.etymology: 源自古法语franc「自由的，无拘束的」，引申为说话毫无拘束、不隐瞒，即「坦率的」。

similar_words.description: 「frank」侧重毫无保留的直白；「candid」侧重公正、不偏不倚的坦诚，语义相近易混淆。

definitions.example: The financial institution was investigated for committing fraud to mislead investors.

fraud.etymology: 源自拉丁语fraus「欺骗」，引申为「欺诈、诈骗」的行为，也可指实施欺诈的人，即「骗子」，属考研常考的商务/法律类词汇。

similar_words.description: 「fraud」为名词，表欺诈、骗子；「frank」为形容词，表坦率的，拼写相近易混淆。

definitions.example: The right to freedom of expression is enshrined in the constitution of many countries.

freedom.etymology: 由形容词free（自由的）+名词后缀-dom（表「状态、领域」）构成，指自由的状态或自主的权利，即「自由、自主」。

similar_words.description: 「freedom」侧重个体的自主选择；「liberty」侧重法律或社会赋予的正式自由，语义相近易混淆。

definitions.example: In extreme cold, the water pipes in old residential buildings may freeze and burst without proper insulation.
The witness froze immediately when she spotted the suspect’s distinctive scar in the courtroom.

freeze.etymology: 源自古英语freosan，为原生英语核心词汇，无显性拉丁词缀，核心语义关联极寒下的凝固状态

similar_words.description: freeze表结冰、呆住；frieze指墙壁或织物的水平装饰带

definitions.example: The international trade port handles more than 12 million tons of container freight annually.
His acceptance speech was freighted with gratitude towards his late mentor.

freight.etymology: 源自中古荷兰语vracht，本义为负载、运输，无复杂词缀拆分

similar_words.description: freight指货运、货物；flight指飞行、航班

definitions.example: The frequency of extreme droughts has risen significantly in arid regions over the past two decades.

frequency.etymology: 来自拉丁语frequentia，由词根frequ-（频繁的、拥挤的）加名词后缀-ency构成，表频繁的状态或度量

similar_words.description: frequency表频率、频繁；frenzy表狂怒、狂暴状态

definitions.example: Frequent changes in government policies may undermine business investment confidence.
The scholar frequented the national archives to collect primary sources for his research.

frequent.etymology: 来自拉丁语frequent-（拥挤的），形容词形式直接沿用词根语义，动词形式由形容词转化而来

similar_words.description: frequent为形容词/动词，表频繁的/常去；frequency为名词，表频率、频繁

definitions.example: Urban residents often drive to suburban farms to buy fresh vegetables and fruits on weekends.
The manager warned the intern for making fresh remarks to a senior client.

fresh.etymology: 源自古英语fersc，为原生英语核心词，无显性词缀，核心语义关联未变质、未污染的状态

similar_words.description: fresh表新鲜的、新近的；flesh表肉体、果肉

definitions.example: Engineers use special lubricants to reduce friction between mechanical parts and extend equipment lifespan.
Cultural friction between immigrant communities and local residents is a common topic in sociological research.

friction.etymology: 来自拉丁语fricare（摩擦），由词根fric-加名词后缀-tion构成，表摩擦的动作或状态

similar_words.description: friction表摩擦、冲突；fraction表分数、小部分

definitions.example: Households are advised to store raw meat and cooked food separately in the fridge to avoid cross-contamination.

fridge.etymology: 为refrigerator的口语缩写形式，原词由re-（再）+ friger-（冷）+ -ator（表器物的后缀）构成，缩写后省略核心词缀部分

similar_words.description: fridge指冰箱；fringe指边缘、刘海

definitions.example: The sudden crash of thunder gave the napping toddler a terrible fright.

fright.etymology: 源自古英语fyrhto，为原生英语词汇，核心语义关联强烈的恐惧情绪，无显性词缀

similar_words.description: fright表惊吓、恐怖；flight表飞行、航班

definitions.example: The unexpected siren from the fire truck frightened the stray cats hiding under the porch.

frighten.etymology: 由名词fright加动词后缀-en构成，表使产生恐惧情绪的动作

similar_words.description: frighten为动词，表使惊吓；fright为名词，表惊吓、恐怖

definitions.example: Many groundbreaking theories in psychology first emerged in the fringe of mainstream academic research.
The small village was fringed with dense pine forests and crystal-clear streams.

fringe.etymology: 源自拉丁语fimbria（边缘），进入英语后拼写简化，核心语义关联事物的边界或次要部分

similar_words.description: fringe指边缘、刘海；fridge指冰箱

definitions.example: Wetland conservation is crucial for protecting frogs and other amphibian species.

frog.etymology: 源自古英语frogga，无明显可拆解的考研核心词根词缀

similar_words.description: fog是名词‘雾’，frog是名词‘青蛙’，二者仅差一个字母r，易形近混淆

definitions.example: The two countries signed an agreement to strengthen frontier security cooperation.
Biomedical engineers are pushing the frontier of gene editing technology.

frontier.etymology: 由词根front（前面、前部）加后缀-ier构成，原指‘边界的前方区域’，引申为学术领域的前沿

similar_words.description: border多指明确划分的国界线，frontier多指未开发的边疆或学术前沿

definitions.example: A sudden heavy frost destroyed most of the orchard's peach blossoms last spring.

frost.etymology: 源自古英语frost，与动词freeze（冻结）同根，表‘冻结形成的白色冰晶’

similar_words.description: froth是名词/动词‘泡沫；起泡沫’，frost是名词‘霜’，易形近混淆

definitions.example: The professor frowned at the student's incomplete research proposal during the defense.

frown.etymology: 源自古英语fronian，表‘皱眉表示不满’，无明显可拆解的考研核心词根词缀

similar_words.description: brown是形容词‘棕色的’，frown是动词/名词‘皱眉’，易形近混淆

definitions.meaning: 富有成效的，硕果累累的
definitions.example: The two academic institutions have achieved fruitful results in joint research on climate change.

fruitful.etymology: 由名词fruit（果实）加后缀-ful（充满…的）构成，原指‘结满果实的’，引申为‘有成效的’

similar_words.description: fruitful指‘有成效的’，fruitless指‘徒劳的、无结果的’，二者为反义词

definitions.example: The unexpected technical failure frustrated the research team's plan to launch the satellite on schedule.

frustrate.etymology: 源自拉丁语frustra（徒劳地），表‘使努力白费、受挫’

similar_words.description: frustrate是动词‘使挫败’，frustrating是形容词‘令人沮丧的’，为同根派生词

definitions.example: Many fast-food restaurants fry potatoes and chicken to serve as main menu items.

fry.etymology: 源自古英语fry，表‘用热油烹饪’，无明显可拆解的考研核心词根词缀

similar_words.description: cry是动词/名词‘哭；叫喊’，fry是动词/名词‘油炸；油炸食品’，易形近混淆

definitions.example: Governments worldwide are promoting renewable energy to reduce reliance on fossil fuels.
The leaked internal documents fueled public skepticism about the company's environmental claims.

fuel.etymology: 源自拉丁语focus（火炉），原指‘供火炉燃烧的物质’，后引申为‘刺激事物发展的因素’

similar_words.description: foul是形容词‘恶臭的；犯规的’，fuel是名词/动词‘燃料；刺激’，易形近混淆

definitions.example: Undergraduate students must fulfill all course requirements to obtain a bachelor's degree.

fulfill.etymology: 由形容词full（满的）加动词fill（填充）缩合而成，表‘使圆满、完成’

similar_words.description: fulfill是动词‘履行’，fulfillment是名词‘履行；实现’，为同根派生词

definitions.example: Workers in the chemical plant must wear masks to avoid inhaling toxic fumes.
She fumed quietly when her research paper was rejected without detailed feedback.

fume.etymology: 源自拉丁语fumus（烟），原指‘冒烟’，引申为‘因愤怒而脸色发青（像冒烟）’

similar_words.description: fuse是名词/动词‘保险丝；熔化’，fume是名词/动词‘烟雾；发怒’，易形近混淆

definitions.example: The primary function of the human liver is to filter toxins from the blood.
Even under extreme weather, the communication system can still function properly.

function.etymology: 源自拉丁语 functio（履行、执行），词根为 funct-（执行），后引申为事物的作用、功能或运行状态

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，fraction 意为「分数；小部分」，function 意为「功能；运行」

definitions.example: The local government has established a special fund to support disabled students.
The cutting-edge medical research is funded by a private charitable organization.

fund.etymology: 源自拉丁语 fundus（底部、基础），原指作为财产基础的土地，后引申为资金、基金

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，found 意为「建立；找到（find的过去式）」，fund 意为「基金；资助」

definitions.example: There are fundamental differences between the two theories of social development.
Respect for human dignity is one of the fundamentals of a civilized society.

fundamental.etymology: 源自拉丁语 fundamentum（基础），由词根 fund-（基础）加形容词后缀 -al 构成，表「与基础相关的」

similar_words.description: 语义近义，elementary 侧重「初级的、入门级的」，fundamental 侧重「根本的、本质的」

definitions.example: The funeral of the renowned historian was attended by scholars from all over the country.

funeral.etymology: 源自拉丁语 funus（葬礼），加名词后缀 -al 构成，指丧葬仪式

similar_words.description: 语义近义，burial 侧重「埋葬行为或埋葬地点」，funeral 侧重「丧葬仪式本身」

definitions.example: Her funny stories about campus life made the entire audience burst into laughter.
There’s something funny about the sudden change in his attitude towards the project.

funny.etymology: 源自名词 fun（乐趣）加形容词后缀 -y，原指「充满乐趣的」，后引申出「奇怪的」僻义

similar_words.description: 语义近义，amusing 仅指「引人发笑的」，funny 可表「滑稽的」或「奇怪的」

definitions.example: The illegal trade in wild animal fur has been strictly banned by the government.

fur.etymology: 源自古英语 fyrr，原指动物的软毛或毛皮，是基础名词

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，fir 意为「冷杉（树木）」，fur 意为「毛皮；软毛」

definitions.example: He was furious when he discovered that his important documents had been destroyed.
The furious hurricane caused severe damage to the coastal towns.

furious.etymology: 源自拉丁语 furiosus（疯狂的），词根为 fur-（狂怒），后引申出「猛烈的」僻义

similar_words.description: 语义近义，fierce 侧重「态度或行为的凶猛、激烈」，furious 侧重「情绪的狂怒」或「自然现象的猛烈」

definitions.example: The steel factory’s furnace operates 24 hours a day to meet production demands.

furnace.etymology: 源自拉丁语 fornax（炉子），加名词后缀 -ace 构成，指工业用的熔炉或取暖用的火炉

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，furnish 意为「供应；布置家具」，furnace 意为「熔炉；火炉」

definitions.example: The university library will furnish students with free access to digital academic resources.
We plan to furnish the new apartment with simple but practical furniture.

furnish.etymology: 源自古法语 furnir（提供），词根为 furn-（提供），后引申出「布置家具」的含义

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，furnace 意为「熔炉；火炉」，furnish 意为「供应；布置家具」

definitions.example: The newly decorated house is equipped with eco-friendly and comfortable furniture.

furniture.etymology: 源自动词 furnish（布置）加名词后缀 -ture，指用于布置房间的各类家具物品

similar_words.description: 同根词，furnish 是动词「供应；布置」，furniture 是名词「家具」

definitions.example: Further analysis of the survey data revealed a significant correlation between sleep quality and work efficiency.
The international cooperation project aims to further the development of clean energy technologies worldwide.

further.etymology: 源自古英语「furthor」，为「far」的比较级变体，由表具体距离的“更远地”引申为抽象的“进一步地”，后演变为动词义“促进”。

similar_words.description: 「farther」仅指具体空间距离的“更远”，「further」可表抽象的“进一步”或兼表具体距离，还可作动词。

definitions.example: Furthermore, the study failed to control for variables such as participants' age and educational background.

furthermore.etymology: 由「further」（进一步）+「more」（更多）复合而成，用于书面语中表递进逻辑关系。

similar_words.description: 二者均为表递进的连接副词，「furthermore」更侧重正式学术语境，是考研阅读议论文的高频衔接词。

definitions.meaning: 狂怒，暴怒；（自然现象的）狂暴
definitions.example: In a fit of fury, the CEO rejected the unethical marketing proposal put forward by the sales department.

fury.etymology: 源自拉丁语「furia」（狂怒），同根于形容词「furious」，后引申为自然现象的“狂暴”义。

similar_words.description: 「anger」为普通的“愤怒”，「fury」指极端失控的“狂怒”，语气强度远高于前者。

definitions.example: The new design fuses traditional Chinese architectural elements with modern energy-saving technologies.

fuse.etymology: 源自拉丁语「fundere」（熔化），同根于「confuse」（一同熔化→混淆），后引申出“融合”“保险丝”等义。

similar_words.description: 二者同根，「confuse」表“混淆”，「fuse」表“融合”，语义相反但词根均与“熔化”相关。

definitions.meaning: 大惊小怪（的事）；小题大做
definitions.example: Many educators argue that parents should not make a fuss over their children's minor test failures.

fuss.etymology: 源自中古荷兰语「fus」（忙乱、骚动），后演变为表“大惊小怪”的常用词。

similar_words.description: 二者形近（仅一个字母差异），「fuse」指“融合；保险丝”，「fuss」指“大惊小怪”，词义完全不同。

definitions.example: Attempts to solve the traffic congestion problem without optimizing urban planning are largely futile.

futile.etymology: 源自拉丁语「futilis」（无用的、易碎的），后演变为表“徒劳无效”的形容词。

similar_words.description: 二者均表“徒劳的”，「futile」侧重“注定失败、毫无效果”，「fruitless」侧重“未取得预期成果”。

definitions.example: The researcher emphasized the futility of relying on anecdotal evidence to support a scientific hypothesis.

futility.etymology: 源自拉丁语「futilitas」，为形容词「futile」的名词派生形式，表“徒劳无效”的状态。

similar_words.description: 二者形近（仅差首字母「f」），「utility」指“效用、实用”，「futility」指“徒劳、无效”，语义完全相反。

definitions.example: Undergraduate students can gain valuable research experience by assisting professors with their projects.
My grandfather's old pocket watch gains three minutes every day, so he has to adjust it regularly.

gain.etymology: 源自古英语「gagnian」（获得、受益），后引申出钟表“走快”的僻义。

similar_words.description: 二者形近（仅差一个字母），「grain」指“谷物、颗粒”，「gain」指“获得、收益”，词义完全不同。

definitions.example: The James Webb Space Telescope has captured high-resolution images of distant galaxies billions of light-years away.
The academic summit gathered a galaxy of leading scholars in the field of artificial intelligence.

galaxy.etymology: 源自希腊语「galaxias」（牛奶色的），因银河看起来像牛奶色的光带得名，后引申为“一群杰出者”的比喻义。

similar_words.description: 二者均为天文术语，「nebula」指“星云”，「galaxy」指“由恒星、星云等组成的星系”，易被混淆。

definitions.example: The National Gallery in London is home to a vast collection of European masterpieces from the Renaissance period.

definitions.meaning: 旁听席；（剧场的）顶层楼座
definitions.example: Members of the public can observe the parliamentary debate from the public gallery of the House of Commons.

gallery.etymology: 源自意大利语「galleria」（走廊、长廊），原指展示艺术品的长廊，后引申为“画廊”“旁听席”等义。

similar_words.description: 二者形近（仅差一个字母），「galley」指“船载厨房；校样”，「gallery」指“画廊；旁听席”，词义完全不同。

definitions.example: According to the environmental protection report, a typical family uses approximately 30 gallons of water daily for domestic purposes.

gallon.etymology: 源自古法语galon，最初指用于量取液体的容器容量单位，后成为国际通用液量单位

similar_words.description: 「gallon」为容量单位，「gallop」为动词/名词表疾驰

definitions.example: The cavalry galloped across the plain to reinforce the front line.

gallop.etymology: 源自古法语galoper，与表示「快速移动」的日耳曼语词根相关，原指马的快速奔跑

similar_words.description: 「gallop」表疾驰，「gallon」为容量单位

definitions.example: Some entrepreneurs gamble heavily on emerging technologies without conducting thorough market research.

gamble.etymology: 源自古英语gamen（游戏、娱乐），后引申为以财物冒险即赌博，再引申为一般意义的冒险

similar_words.description: 「gamble」指赌博/冒险，「gambol」指（动物或人）嬉戏蹦跳

definitions.example: A gang of environmental activists staged a peaceful protest against deforestation.

gang.etymology: 源自古英语gang（行走、行程），原指结伴行走的群体，后引申为团伙、帮派

similar_words.description: 「gang」常指有组织的团伙（尤指不良群体），「band」多指有共同目的的临时或固定群体

definitions.meaning: 监狱；监禁（英式拼写，同美式jail）
definitions.example: In the 19th century, many debtors were thrown into gaol for failing to repay small loans.

gaol.etymology: 源自古法语gaiole，为美式英语「jail」的英式拼写形式，指监狱或监禁行为

similar_words.description: 「gaol」为英式拼写的「监狱/监禁」，「jail」为美式拼写，二者同义

definitions.example: The report highlights a growing gap between educational resources in urban and rural areas.

gap.etymology: 源自古英语gæp（缺口、裂缝），后引申为抽象的差距、分歧

similar_words.description: 「gap」指缺口/差距，「gasp」指（因惊讶/疼痛）喘气

definitions.example: The abandoned garage was renovated into a community library for local residents.

garage.etymology: 源自法语garage（遮蔽处、存放处），原指存放车辆的遮蔽空间，后引申为汽车修理厂

similar_words.description: 「garage」指车库或汽车修理厂，「garbage」指垃圾

definitions.meaning: 垃圾，废弃物；无意义的内容
definitions.example: Improper garbage disposal has become a major cause of soil and water pollution in suburban areas.

garbage.etymology: 源自中古英语garbage（家禽内脏废弃物），后泛指各类垃圾、无意义的内容

similar_words.description: 「garbage」指垃圾，「garage」指车库或汽车修理厂

definitions.example: Recent studies suggest that regular consumption of garlic may help reduce the risk of cardiovascular diseases.

garlic.etymology: 源自古英语garleac，由gar（矛）和leac（韭葱）组成，因蒜叶形似长矛而得名

similar_words.description: 「garlic」指大蒜，「garland」指花环

definitions.example: The textile industry has adopted sustainable materials to produce eco-friendly garments.

garment.etymology: 源自古法语garnement，由动词garnir（装备、装饰）派生，指穿在身上的装备即衣服

similar_words.description: 「garment」指衣服，「garret」指阁楼

definitions.example: The emission of greenhouse gases contributes significantly to global climate change.
The government has issued a warning about potential gas leaks in old residential buildings.

gas.etymology: 源自拉丁语「chaos」的变体，后演变为指气态物质的统称，也可引申指煤气、汽油等

similar_words.description: 「gas」为名词，表气体、煤气等；「gasp」为动词/名词，表喘气、喘息，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The rising price of gasoline has imposed heavy pressure on household transportation budgets.

gasoline.etymology: 由词根「gas（气体）」+ 后缀「-oline（表油类物质）」构成，特指由气态烃类制成的机动车燃油

similar_words.description: 「gas」可泛指气体或美式口语中的汽油；「gasoline」为正式书面语的汽油，更常用于学术或正式文本

definitions.meaning: 喘气，喘息（尤指因缺氧或劳累）
definitions.example: When reaching the summit of the mountain, the hikers gasped for fresh air after a long climb.
A gasp of surprise escaped from the audience when the magician pulled a rabbit out of his hat.

gasp.etymology: 源自古挪威语「gapa（张口）」，引申为因缺氧、惊讶等而张口喘气

similar_words.description: 「gasp」表喘气相关义；「gas」表气体相关义，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: Protesters gathered in the central square to demand greater transparency in government affairs.
Researchers gathered a large amount of data to analyze the correlation between diet and chronic diseases.

gather.etymology: 源自古英语「gaderian」，本义为将分散的人或物聚拢，后引申为收集、推断等义

similar_words.description: 「gather」侧重自然或随意的聚拢；「collect」侧重有计划、有条理地收集（如邮票、数据）

definitions.meaning: 判定，评估（抽象事物的程度、影响等）
definitions.example: It is difficult to gauge the exact impact of the new trade policy on small and medium-sized enterprises.
The engineer checked the pressure gauge to ensure the safe operation of the industrial equipment.

gauge.etymology: 源自古法语「gauger（测量容量）」，原指测量酒类容量的器具，后引申为各类测量工具及评估、判定的动作

similar_words.description: 「gauge」侧重精准评估抽象事物（如影响、情绪）；「measure」侧重具体测量长度、重量等物理量

definitions.meaning: 快乐的，愉快的（正式旧用法）
definitions.example: The gay atmosphere of the traditional lantern festival attracted thousands of domestic and foreign tourists.

gay.etymology: 源自古法语「gai（快乐的）」，原指欢快、愉悦的状态，后衍生出其他语义

similar_words.description: 「gay」为较正式的旧用法表快乐；「joyful」为常用义表快乐的，适用范围更广

definitions.meaning: 凝视，注视（常带好奇、欣赏等情绪）
definitions.example: She gazed at the ancient mural in the museum, lost in thought about its historical background.

gaze.etymology: 源自中古英语「gasen」，指长时间专注地注视，常带有特定情绪

similar_words.description: 「gaze」侧重温和、专注的凝视（如欣赏）；「stare」侧重无礼或惊讶的瞪视

definitions.meaning: 使适应，使适合（常与to/towards搭配）
definitions.example: The curriculum is geared to meet the demands of students preparing for postgraduate entrance exams.
The mechanic replaced the worn gear to restore the normal operation of the agricultural machine.

gear.etymology: 源自古英语「gearo（准备好的）」，原指装备、器具，后引申为机械齿轮及调整适应的动作

similar_words.description: 「gear」常与介词搭配表使适应特定需求；「adapt」侧重调整自身以适应新环境

definitions.meaning: 性别（侧重社会文化层面）
definitions.example: The study examines the gender gap in career advancement among graduates from top universities.

gender.etymology: 源自拉丁语「genus（种类、性别）」，后专门指代社会文化或生理层面的性别属性

similar_words.description: 「gender」侧重社会文化层面的性别；「sex」侧重生理层面的性别

definitions.meaning: 基因（生物遗传基本单位）
definitions.example: Scientists have identified a specific gene that may increase the risk of developing certain cardiovascular diseases.

gene.etymology: 由德国生物学家约翰森创造，源自希腊语「genos（种族、起源）」，指控制生物遗传性状的基本单位

similar_words.description: 「gene」是单个遗传单位；「genome」是某一生物全部基因的总和

definitions.example: A genealogist traced the noble family's lineage back to the 12th century through ancient parish and tax records.

genealogist.etymology: 词根「gene（种族、出生）」+ 连接成分-a- + 后缀-logist（从事某领域研究的学者），指研究家族世系源流的专业人士。

similar_words.description: genealogist指研究家谱的系谱学者，geneticist指研究遗传学的遗传学家，二者为同根形近易混词。

definitions.example: The general trend of global economic development is shifting towards digital and low-carbon industries.
Please provide a general summary of the research findings without going into too many technical details.

general.etymology: 词根「gen（种类、产生）」+ 后缀-er + -al（形容词后缀），本指属于某一类的全部，引申为普遍的、大体的。

similar_words.description: general侧重涵盖全体的、普遍的，generic侧重类属的、无品牌标识的，二者为形近近义易混词。

definitions.example: Education researchers warn against generalizing the findings of a single regional study to all student groups nationwide.
The eco-friendly farming technique was tested in pilot areas before being generalized across the whole province.

generalize.etymology: 形容词general（普遍的）+ 后缀-ize（使…化），指使变得普遍，引申为概括、推广。

similar_words.description: generalize侧重从具体案例推导出普遍适用的结论，summarize侧重对已有内容提炼核心要点，二者为近义易混词。

definitions.meaning: 产生；生成（能量、收益、岗位等）
definitions.example: The new free trade zone is expected to generate over 3,000 new jobs and 500 million yuan in annual tax revenue.
The professor's controversial speech on climate policy generated fierce debate among academic circles.

generate.etymology: 词根「gen（产生、生育）」+ 后缀-er + -ate（动词后缀），意为使产生、使形成。

similar_words.description: generate意为产生、引发，degenerate意为衰退、堕落，二者为同根形近易混词。

definitions.example: The post-1990s generation has become the main consumer force in China's cultural and creative industries.
Nuclear power currently accounts for about 10% of global electricity generation.

generation.etymology: 词根「gen（产生）」+ 后缀-er + -ation（名词后缀），指产生的过程，也指同一时期出生的一代人。

similar_words.description: generation指一代人或生成过程，regeneration指再生、复兴，二者为同根形近易混词。

definitions.example: The remote mountain village installed a hydroelectric generator to solve its long-term power shortage problem.
The renowned research institute has long been a generator of cutting-edge technological innovations.

generator.etymology: 动词generate（产生）+ 后缀-or（表人或物），指产生能量、价值等的设备或主体。

similar_words.description: generator多指产生能量、收益或创意的设备或机构，creator多指创作作品、创立事物的个人，二者为近义易混词。

definitions.example: The retired professor made a generous donation of 2 million yuan to support rural education in western China.
The research team won a generous grant from the National Natural Science Foundation to conduct biotech research.

generous.etymology: 词根「gen（产生）」+ 后缀-er + -ous（形容词后缀，富于…的），本指出产丰富的，引申为慷慨的、大量的。

similar_words.description: generous指慷慨的、大量的，ingenious指灵巧的、有独创性的，二者为形近易混词，拼写易混淆。

definitions.meaning: 基因的；遗传的；遗传学的
definitions.example: Scientists have identified a genetic marker that is closely associated with the risk of developing type 2 diabetes.

genetic.etymology: 名词gene（基因）+ 后缀-tic（形容词后缀，与…相关的），指与基因或遗传相关的。

similar_words.description: genetic指基因的、遗传的，generic指一般的、类属的，二者为形近易混词，拼写仅一个字母之差。

definitions.example: Marie Curie, a scientific genius, was the first person to win Nobel Prizes in two different scientific fields.
The classic novel perfectly captures the genius of the 1920s American Jazz Age through its vivid character portrayal.

genius.etymology: 词根「gen（产生、生育）」+ 后缀-ius，本指守护个人的神灵，引申为与生俱来的天赋，后扩展指某一群体的特有精神。

similar_words.description: genius侧重极高的、与生俱来的天赋或天才人物，talent侧重某一领域的才能（可后天培养），二者为近义易混词。

definitions.example: The therapist used a gentle approach to help the child overcome his fear of social interaction.

definitions.meaning: （坡度、声音、光线等）平缓的；柔和的
definitions.example: The gentle slope of the valley makes it an ideal location for growing high-quality tea.

gentle.etymology: 词根「gen（出生、种族）」+ 后缀-tle，本指出身高贵的，后引申为举止温和的、柔和的。

similar_words.description: gentle侧重态度、动作温和不粗暴，tender侧重柔软、体贴或娇嫩的，二者为近义易混词。

definitions.meaning: 绅士；（对男子的尊称）先生
definitions.example: Many 19th-century British novels portray a gentleman as someone with noble moral character and a strong sense of social responsibility.

gentleman.etymology: 由形容词「gentle」（温和有礼的）加名词「man」（人）构成，原指出身良好、举止得体的成年男子

similar_words.description: gentry是集合名词，指绅士阶层、上流人士；gentleman是可数名词，指个体的绅士或对男子的尊称

definitions.example: The professor spoke gently to the nervous undergraduate to ease his anxiety before the thesis defense.

gently.etymology: 由形容词「gentle」（温和的）加副词后缀-ly构成，表动作的方式属性

similar_words.description: lightly侧重动作的力度轻、程度浅；gently侧重动作的方式温柔、和缓，带有体贴的意味

definitions.example: The archaeological team confirmed that the unearthed bronze vessel was a genuine product of the Shang Dynasty.

genuine.etymology: 来自拉丁语词根gen-（出生、产生），原指天生的、天然的，引申为非伪造的、真诚的

similar_words.description: genuine侧重事物本身为真、非人工伪造，也可指人的情感真挚；authentic侧重经过权威验证、符合事实的

definitions.example: The study of human geography helps scholars analyze the interaction between population distribution and regional economic development.

geography.etymology: 由词根geo-（地球）加后缀-graphy（描述、书写）构成，原指对地球表面特征的描述

similar_words.description: geography是研究地球表面自然环境与人文活动分布的地理学；geology是研究地球内部结构与演化历史的地质学

definitions.example: Geology provides crucial evidence for understanding the formation of natural resources such as oil and minerals.

geology.etymology: 由词根geo-（地球）加后缀-logy（学科）构成，指研究地球物质组成与演化规律的学科

similar_words.description: geology侧重研究地球的内部结构与演化过程；geography侧重研究地球表面的地理现象与人文互动

definitions.example: Ancient Greek mathematicians laid the theoretical foundation of plane geometry that is still used today.

geometry.etymology: 由词根geo-（地球）加后缀-metry（测量）构成，原指土地测量技术，后演变为研究空间形状与规律的数学分支

similar_words.description: geometry是研究空间关系的数学分支几何学；geography是研究地球表面特征的地理学科

definitions.example: Washing hands with soap regularly is an effective way to prevent the spread of disease-causing germs.
The germ of the concept of ecological civilization can be traced back to environmental protection movements in the mid-20th century.

germ.etymology: 来自拉丁语「germen」（芽、种子），原指植物幼芽，引申为致病的细菌，也可指事物的最初形态

similar_words.description: germ是通用语境下的细菌、病菌；bacterium是微生物学学术术语，特指细菌，复数形式为bacteria

definitions.example: The union leader made a gesture of compromise to promote the negotiation with the management.

gesture.etymology: 来自拉丁语词根ger-（做、行动），引申为用以表达意思或态度的动作

similar_words.description: gesture指用以表意的动态手势、表态动作；posture指身体的静态姿势、体态，也可指立场态度

definitions.example: Many Gothic novels feature ghosts as symbols of unresolved guilt or regret in the characters’ inner world.
When the award winner delivered his speech, there was a ghost of nervousness in his otherwise calm voice.

ghost.etymology: 来自古英语「gast」（灵魂、幽灵），后引申为隐约的痕迹、极少量的事物

similar_words.description: ghost指鬼魂、幽灵，也可指微量；host指主人、主持人，也可指宿主、大量，二者仅首字母不同易混淆

definitions.example: Shakespeare is widely regarded as a giant in the history of English literature, whose works have influenced generations of writers.
The construction of the giant cross-sea bridge has brought great convenience to the economic exchanges between the two regions.

giant.etymology: 来自希腊语「gigas」，原指神话中的巨人，引申为某领域的杰出人物，也可作形容词指体积或规模巨大的

similar_words.description: giant作形容词指体积、规模较大的，也可作名词；gigantic是形容词，侧重程度、规模极大的，语气更强

definitions.meaning: 巨大的，庞大的（侧重规模、尺寸远超寻常）
definitions.example: The construction of the gigantic offshore wind farm has drawn widespread attention from environmentalists and policymakers.

gigantic.etymology: 源自希腊语「gigas」（巨人），加形容词后缀「-ic」，原指“巨人般的”，后引申为“巨大的”

similar_words.description: giant可作名词‘巨人’或形容词‘巨大的’，侧重体积；gigantic仅作形容词，强调规模远超寻常的庞大

definitions.meaning: v. 咯咯笑（尤指儿童或紧张时）；n. 咯咯的笑声
definitions.example: The study found that participants often giggle nervously when asked about sensitive financial situations.

giggle.etymology: 拟声词，模仿人类咯咯笑的声音

similar_words.description: giggle多指儿童或紧张时的咯咯笑；chuckle指成年人因愉悦而发出的低声轻笑

definitions.meaning: 魅力，吸引力（尤指时尚、名流相关的表面魅力）
definitions.example: The 1960s Hollywood film industry was renowned for its glamorous stars and red-carpet glamor.

glamor.etymology: 源自苏格兰语「gramarye」（魔法），后引申为“能迷惑人的魅力”，为美式拼写

similar_words.description: glamor为美式拼写，glamour为英式拼写，二者词义完全相同

definitions.meaning: 魅力，诱惑力（尤指表面的、令人向往的吸引力）
definitions.example: Many young people are attracted by the glamour of the fashion industry without recognizing its harsh working conditions.

glamour.etymology: 源自苏格兰语「gramarye」（魔法），后引申为“能迷惑人的魅力”，为英式拼写

similar_words.description: glamour为英式拼写，glamor为美式拼写，二者词义完全相同

definitions.meaning: v. 扫视，瞥一眼（主动快速看）；n. 一瞥
definitions.example: Researchers usually glance through the raw data to check for obvious anomalies before conducting formal analysis.

glance.etymology: 源自古法语「glacier」（滑动），原指“目光快速滑动”，后引申为“扫视”

similar_words.description: glance侧重主动、快速的扫视动作；glimpse侧重被动、短暂看到的结果

definitions.meaning: v. 怒目而视；发出刺眼的光；n. 怒视；刺眼的光
definitions.example: Under the glare of the media spotlight, the company's CEO had to publicly address the product safety scandal.

glare.etymology: 源自古英语「glær」（闪耀），原指“刺眼的强光”，后引申为“带有敌意的怒视”

similar_words.description: glare侧重带有愤怒或敌意的怒视；stare侧重因好奇、惊讶等长时间的注视

definitions.meaning: v. 滑行，滑动（平稳无声）；n. 滑行
definitions.example: The new maglev train can glide smoothly along the tracks without making obvious noise or vibration.

glide.etymology: 源自古英语「glīdan」（滑行），本义与现代词义一致

similar_words.description: glide侧重平稳、无声的滑行；slide侧重因重力或外力产生的滑动，可能伴随摩擦

definitions.meaning: v. 瞥见（短暂看到）；n. 一瞥，短暂的体验
definitions.example: Through the crowded conference hall, I caught a glimpse of the Nobel Prize-winning physicist who was giving a keynote speech.

glimpse.etymology: 源自古英语「glimsian」（闪烁），原指“短暂看到的光影”，后引申为“瞥见”

similar_words.description: glimpse侧重被动、短暂看到的结果；glance侧重主动、快速的扫视动作

definitions.meaning: v. 闪烁，闪耀（耀眼）；n. 闪光，表面的魅力
definitions.example: The glitter of skyscrapers in the financial district masks the high-pressure working environment of white-collar employees.

glitter.etymology: 源自古英语「gliterian」（闪耀），本义与现代词义一致

similar_words.description: glitter侧重耀眼、持续的闪光；sparkle侧重细碎、跳跃的闪光

definitions.meaning: 全球的，全世界的；整体的
definitions.example: The global outbreak of the COVID-19 epidemic has accelerated the transformation of traditional working modes worldwide.

global.etymology: 源自拉丁语「globus」（球体），加形容词后缀「-al」，原指“球体的”，后引申为“全球的”

similar_words.description: global侧重地理范围的‘全球的’；universal侧重适用范围的‘普遍的，全体的’

definitions.example: Many environmental organizations call on global citizens to take joint actions to protect the fragile ecosystem of the globe.

globe.etymology: 来自拉丁语「globus」（球状物），本义为球形物体，后引申为地球、世界

similar_words.description: 「global」是形容词，意为全球的；「globe」是名词，意为地球、世界

definitions.meaning: （天气、场所）阴暗的，昏暗的；（情绪、前景）沮丧的，阴郁的
definitions.example: The gloomy economic forecast released by the central bank has sparked widespread concern among small business owners.

gloomy.etymology: 由名词「gloom」（黑暗、忧郁）加形容词后缀「-y」构成，表具有某种性质的

similar_words.description: 「glum」侧重指人闷闷不乐的神情；「gloomy」可修饰环境、前景或人的情绪

definitions.meaning: 光荣的，荣耀的；辉煌的，壮丽的
definitions.example: The Chinese nation has a glorious history of five thousand years, which is admired by scholars worldwide.

glorious.etymology: 由名词「glory」（光荣）加形容词后缀「-ous」（表充满……的）构成

similar_words.description: 「glamorous」意为迷人的、富有魅力的；「glorious」意为光荣的、辉煌的，二者拼法相近易混淆

definitions.example: Scientists who make breakthroughs in basic research deserve eternal glory from the whole society.

glory.etymology: 来自拉丁语「gloria」（荣耀、名声），直接继承其核心含义

similar_words.description: 「honor」侧重指个人或集体的名誉、敬意；「glory」侧重指崇高的、广为传颂的荣耀

definitions.example: In the cold winter of northern China, wearing thick woolen gloves is essential for outdoor workers.

glove.etymology: 来自古英语「glof」，本义为覆盖手部的防护用品

similar_words.description: 「grove」意为小树林、树丛；「glove」意为手套，二者拼法仅有一个字母之差易混淆

definitions.meaning: （无焰地）发光，发热；（柔和的）光亮，光辉
definitions.example: The campfire made the surrounding woods glow with a warm, golden light after sunset.

glow.etymology: 来自古英语「glowan」（发光、发热），本义为物体发出柔和的光热

similar_words.description: 「glare」指发出刺眼的强光或怒视；「glow」指发出柔和的光热，二者语义侧重不同

definitions.meaning: 胶水；（注意力、目光）紧盯，粘住（引申义）
definitions.example: A large number of adolescents are glued to short-video platforms, which has raised widespread concern about their mental health.

glue.etymology: 来自古法语「glu」（胶水），后引申出动词“粘合、紧盯”的含义

similar_words.description: 「clue」意为线索、提示；「glue」意为胶水、紧盯，二者拼法相近易混淆

definitions.meaning: 目标，目的；（体育赛事的）球门，进球
definitions.example: The core goal of this educational reform is to reduce the excessive academic burden on primary and secondary school students.

goal.etymology: 来自古英语「gal」（边界、终点），后引申为行动的目标

similar_words.description: 「aim」侧重指抽象的目的或瞄准的动作；「goal」侧重指具体的、可量化的目标

definitions.example: The shepherd led his flock of goats to graze on the sunny hillside every morning.
In the financial fraud case, the junior accountant was made a goat to shield the company's senior executives from legal punishment.

goat.etymology: 来自古英语「gat」（山羊），后引申出“替罪羊”的比喻义

similar_words.description: 「sheep」指绵羊，无比喻义；「goat」指山羊，还可喻指替罪羊

definitions.meaning: （首字母大写，特指基督教的）上帝，天主
definitions.example: In Western classical literature, God is often portrayed as a symbol of absolute justice and mercy.

definitions.meaning: （首字母小写时）极受崇敬的人/物，偶像
definitions.example: For generations of basketball fans, Michael Jordan is a god on the professional court.

God.etymology: 来自古英语「God」（神、上帝），首字母大写时特指基督教的主神

similar_words.description: 「goddess」指女神；「God」（首字母大写）指基督教的上帝，首字母小写时指一般的神或偶像

definitions.example: Ancient civilizations used gold as a universal medium of exchange in cross-regional trade.

gold.etymology: 源于古英语gold，原指金属黄金，后引申指金色

similar_words.description: gold为名词表黄金/金色；gild为动词表给…镀金，拼写形近易混

definitions.meaning: 金色的；黄金般的；美好的
definitions.example: The Golden Age of Athenian democracy laid a cornerstone for Western political thought.

golden.etymology: 由词根gold（黄金）+ 形容词后缀-en（表“具有…性质的”）构成

similar_words.description: gold为名词表黄金/金色；golden为形容词表金色的、黄金般的，为同根衍生词

definitions.example: Many academic conferences arrange golf activities to promote informal communication among scholars.

golf.etymology: 源于苏格兰方言golf，原指一种用球杆击球入洞的户外球类运动

similar_words.description: golf指高尔夫球运动；gulf指海湾、鸿沟，拼写形近易混

definitions.example: She whispered a soft goodby to her advisor before leaving for overseas study.

goodby.etymology: 由短语good by（原指“愿上帝与你同在”的缩略）演变而来，为goodbye的变体拼写

similar_words.description: goodby为goodbye的非正式/古体变体拼写，语义完全相同，均表告别

definitions.example: The professor’s consistent acts of goodness earned him widespread respect in the academic community.

goodness.etymology: 由词根good（好的）+ 名词后缀-ness（表“性质、状态”）构成

similar_words.description: goodness侧重内在的道德美德；kindness侧重待人友善的具体行为，语义侧重不同

definitions.example: Cross-border e-commerce platforms are required to declare imported goods in accordance with customs regulations.

goods.etymology: 由good（有价值的）的复数形式演变而来，指具有使用价值的商品或货物

similar_words.description: goods为常用词，可指各类商品/货物；merchandise为正式商务用词，专指待售的商品

definitions.example: The research on the migratory routes of wild goose provides insights into ecological changes.

goose.etymology: 源于古英语gōs，原指家禽鹅，后引申指鹅肉

similar_words.description: goose为单数形式（鹅）；geese为其不规则复数形式，拼写变化易混

definitions.example: The gorgeous mural in the ancient library has been preserved as a national cultural heritage.

gorgeous.etymology: 源自古法语gorgias，原指“艳丽的、华美的”，后引申指极好的

similar_words.description: gorgeous侧重色彩艳丽、外观夺目；magnificent侧重规模宏大、气势雄伟，修饰对象不同

definitions.example: Baseless gossip about the research team’s funding sources spread quickly in the academic circle.
Some scholars tend to gossip about colleagues’ personal lives during conference breaks.

gossip.etymology: 源于古英语godsibb（原指教父/教母，引申为密友间的闲聊），后演变为“流言蜚语”

similar_words.description: gossip侧重无根据的私人话题闲言；rumor侧重传播的不实公共消息，语义范围不同

definitions.example: The country’s constitution stipulates that the elected government shall govern in accordance with the law.
Environmental factors largely govern the distribution of endemic plant species in this region.

govern.etymology: 源于希腊语kybernan（原指“掌舵”），引申为“统治、支配”

similar_words.description: govern侧重宏观的统治、支配；regulate侧重具体的调控、规范，动作性质不同

definitions.example: The central government has implemented targeted policies to support small and medium-sized enterprises.
The efficient government of the university has won widespread praise from students and faculty.

government.etymology: 由动词govern（统治、管理）加名词后缀-ment构成，表「统治/管理的行为或机构」

similar_words.description: government指国家或地方政府的整体机构，administration常指某届政府的行政班子或管理部门

definitions.example: The governor of the central bank announced a cut in benchmark interest rates to boost domestic consumption.

governor.etymology: 由动词govern（统治、管理）加名词后缀-or（表人）构成，指「行使统治/管理权力的人」

similar_words.description: governor多指政府或公立机构的主管，director多指企业或私立机构的董事、主管

definitions.meaning: （正式、学术或司法场合的）长袍；礼服
definitions.example: The Nobel laureate wore a silk formal gown to the award presentation ceremony.

gown.etymology: 源自古法语gone，原指宽松的外衣，后特指正式场合的长袍

similar_words.description: gown多指学术、司法或正式晚宴的长袍，robe可泛指各类长袍（含宗教、日常用）

definitions.example: The young researcher grabbed the rare opportunity to conduct experiments in the national key laboratory.
The illegal grab of public resources has been severely punished by the authorities.

grab.etymology: 来自中古英语graben，原指「用力抓取」

similar_words.description: grab强调动作快速、粗鲁的抓取，grasp强调紧紧抓住或理解掌握

definitions.example: The pianist performed the sonata with extraordinary grace and precision.
The professor granted the student a three-day grace to revise and submit his thesis.

grace.etymology: 来自拉丁语gratia，原指「愉悦、恩惠」，后引申为优雅、宽限等义

similar_words.description: grace侧重姿态、动作的自然优美，elegance侧重气质、品味的高雅精致

definitions.example: Her graceful response to the sharp question impressed all the audience at the academic forum.

graceful.etymology: 由名词grace（优雅）加形容词后缀-ful（充满…的）构成，表「充满优雅的」

similar_words.description: graceful侧重姿态、动作或言辞的优雅得体；gracious侧重待人态度的亲切和蔼、有礼

definitions.example: The gracious host arranged a comfortable accommodation for every visiting scholar.

gracious.etymology: 由名词grace（恩惠、优雅）加形容词后缀-ous（具有…性质的）构成，表「具有恩惠/优雅性质的」

similar_words.description: gracious侧重待人接物的态度亲切；graceful侧重姿态、动作的优美

definitions.example: The gradual decline of biodiversity has become a pressing global environmental issue.

gradual.etymology: 来自拉丁语gradus（步）加形容词后缀-ual，表「一步步的、逐步的」

similar_words.description: gradual侧重过程缓慢、逐步推进；progressive侧重进步的、先进的

definitions.example: She graduated from Tsinghua University with a doctorate in environmental science in 2023.
The university offers various career guidance programs for its graduates.

graduate.etymology: 来自拉丁语graduari（迈步、完成学业），由词根grad（步）加后缀-uate构成，表「完成学业迈出新一步的人或行为」

similar_words.description: graduate指毕业生或研究生（已获学位）；undergraduate指本科生（未获学士学位）

definitions.example: The country has established a stable grain reserve system to ensure food security.
The carpenter chose wood with a beautiful natural grain to make the dining table.

grain.etymology: 来自拉丁语granum（谷粒），后引申为颗粒、纹理等义

similar_words.description: grain泛指各类谷物、谷粒；corn在美式英语中特指玉米，英式英语中泛指谷物

definitions.example: Many non-native English learners struggle with complex grammar structures in academic texts.

grammar.etymology: 来自希腊语gramma（书写、字母），引申为书写的规则（语法）；词缀：gramm-（书写规则） + -ar（名词后缀，表事物）

similar_words.description: 「grammar」指语法、文法，「gramme」指重量单位克，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The scientific experiment required adding exactly 6 gramme of the reagent to the beaker.

gramme.etymology: 来自希腊语gramma（刻痕、标记），古代用刻痕计量，引申为重量单位；无复杂词缀

similar_words.description: 「gramme」指重量单位克，「grammar」指语法、文法，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.meaning: 宏伟的，壮丽的（指建筑、景色等）
definitions.example: The grand palace on the hill has become a popular tourist attraction in the region.
The economist proposed a grand plan to reduce global income inequality.

grand.etymology: 来自拉丁语grandis（大的、伟大的），为形容词原词，无复杂词缀

similar_words.description: 「grand」为形容词，表宏伟的、宏大的；「grant」作动词表授予、名词表拨款，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: My grandmother often shares her life experiences with me to teach me valuable lessons.

grandmother.etymology: 复合词，由grand-（表示祖辈的、上一代的） + mother（母亲）构成，指母亲的母亲或父亲的母亲

similar_words.description: 「grandmother」指（外）祖母，「grandfather」指（外）祖父，均为对祖辈的称谓

definitions.meaning: （正式或官方）授予，准予（权利、奖学金等）
definitions.example: The academic committee will grant 20 research fellowships to young scholars this year.

definitions.meaning: （政府或机构发放的）拨款，补助金
definitions.example: The environmental project received a $1 million grant from the United Nations Development Programme.

grant.etymology: 来自古法语granter（同意、给予），词根gran-表“同意”，引申为正式授予或给予资金

similar_words.description: 「grant」作动词表授予、名词表拨款；「grand」为形容词，表宏伟的、宏大的，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The premium red wine is brewed from hand-picked grapes grown in the Mediterranean climate zone.

grape.etymology: 来自古法语grape（葡萄藤、葡萄），为原生名词，无复杂词缀

similar_words.description: 「grape」指水果葡萄，「graph」指图表、曲线图，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.meaning: 图表，曲线图（用于展示数据关系）
definitions.example: The graph in the research paper clearly shows the upward trend of global carbon emissions since 1950.

graph.etymology: 来自希腊语graphein（书写、绘制），引申为用线条绘制的图表；词根graph-表“书写、绘制”

similar_words.description: 「graph」指具体的图表，「graphic」为形容词，表图表的、生动的，二者同根且拼写相近

definitions.meaning: 图表的，用图表示的（用于学术或商务文本）
definitions.example: The annual report includes graphic analyses of the company's market share changes over the past decade.
The journalist wrote a graphic account of the disaster area, which aroused public concern.

graphic.etymology: 同根graph-（书写、绘制） + -ic（形容词后缀，表“与…相关的”），原指与绘制相关的，引申为生动的

similar_words.description: 「graphic」为形容词，表图表的、生动的；「graph」指具体的图表，二者同根且拼写相近

definitions.meaning: 理解，领会（抽象概念、知识等，考研常考引申义）
definitions.example: It is essential for postgraduate candidates to grasp the core logic of academic arguments.
A solid grasp of English grammar and vocabulary is the foundation of high scores in the postgraduate English exam.

grasp.etymology: 来自古英语græpsan（抓住、握住），由本义“物理上抓住”引申为抽象的“掌握、理解”

similar_words.description: 「grasp」可表物理抓住或抽象理解，「grab」侧重突然抢夺、夺取，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: I am extremely grateful to my mentor for his patient guidance throughout my postgraduate application process.

grateful.etymology: 来自拉丁语gratus（令人愉快的、感激的），词缀：grat-（愉快的、感激的） + -ful（形容词后缀，表“充满…的”），即充满感激之情的

similar_words.description: 「grateful」指感激的、感谢的，「graceful」指优雅的、得体的，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: Many researchers express profound gratitude to their mentors for their guidance in academic pursuit.

gratitude.etymology: 源自拉丁语gratus（感激的）+名词后缀-itude，表抽象情感状态

similar_words.description: 同根「grat-」，gratitude为名词表「感激的情感」，grateful为形容词表「感到感激的」

definitions.example: Environmental scientists warned that ocean acidification poses a grave threat to coral reefs.
Archaeologists discovered a well-preserved medieval grave in the rural excavation site.

grave.etymology: 源自古法语grave，词根「grav-」表「重」，引申为「严重的」

similar_words.description: 同根「grav-」，grave为形容词表「严重/严肃的」，gravity为名词表「重力/严重性」

definitions.example: Newton's theory of universal gravitation explains the gravity governing planetary motion.
The judge emphasized the gravity of the financial fraud before announcing the sentence.

gravity.etymology: 词根「grav-」（重）+名词后缀-ity，表「重的状态」，引申为「严重性」

similar_words.description: 同根「grav-」，gravity为名词表「重力/严重性」，grave为形容词表「严重/严肃的」

definitions.example: Dozens of cattle graze peacefully on the lush grassland at the foot of the mountain.
The cyclist grazed his elbow when he fell off the bike on the rough road.

graze.etymology: 源自古英语grasian（吃草），与grass（草）同根

similar_words.description: 同根，graze为动词表「牲畜吃草」，grass为名词表「草」

definitions.example: The kitchen staff used a special detergent to remove the thick grease from the oven.
Maintenance workers greased the machine gears to reduce friction during operation.

grease.etymology: 源自拉丁语crassus（厚的、肥的），后演变为表「油脂」

similar_words.description: 同根，grease为名词表「油脂」，greasy为形容词表「油腻的、沾油脂的」

definitions.example: The greedy corporate executive was accused of embezzling funds from the company.

greedy.etymology: 源自古英语grædig（贪吃的），由名词greed（贪婪）+形容词后缀-y构成

similar_words.description: 同根，greedy为形容词表「贪婪的」，greed为名词表「贪婪（的品质）」

definitions.example: The excessive emission of greenhouse gases is a leading cause of global climate change.

greenhouse.etymology: 合成词，由green（绿色的）+house（房屋）构成，指培育绿色植物的暖房

similar_words.description: 形近易混，greenhouse为环境术语「温室」，常与「气体」搭配；glasshouse指「玻璃暖房」或军事俚语「禁闭室」

definitions.example: The hotel staff stood at the entrance to greet every arriving guest politely.
The refreshing scent of pine trees greeted us as we stepped into the forest.

similar_words.description: 同根，greet为动词表「问候的动作」，greeting为名词表「问候的话语/行为」

definitions.example: Traditional Chinese New Year greetings often convey wishes for good fortune and health.

greeting.etymology: 由动词greet（问候）+名词后缀-ing构成，表「问候的行为或话语」

similar_words.description: 同根，greeting为名词表「问候的话语/行为」，greet为动词表「问候的动作」

definitions.example: The elderly professor wore a tailored grey suit to the academic conference.

definitions.meaning: （局势、规则等）模糊的，不明确的
definitions.example: The regulation of social media content remains a grey area in many jurisdictions.

grey.etymology: 源自古英语grǣg（灰色的），为英式拼写

similar_words.description: 同一词汇的英美拼写变体，grey为英式拼写，gray为美式拼写

definitions.meaning: 悲伤，悲痛（尤指因失去亲友产生的强烈情绪）
definitions.example: Social support networks can effectively alleviate the intense grief caused by unexpected life events.

grief.etymology: 来自古法语grief，原指‘沉重的物质负担’，后引申为精神层面的悲伤情绪

similar_words.description: 「grief」为名词，表悲伤情绪；「grieve」为动词，表感到或使他人悲伤

definitions.example: Elderly individuals often grieve longer over the loss of a long-term spouse than younger people.

grieve.etymology: 同根于grief，来自古法语grever，原指‘使负重担’，后引申为使（人）感到悲伤

similar_words.description: 「grieve」为动词，表感到悲伤；「grief」为名词，表悲伤情绪

definitions.meaning: 严峻的，令人沮丧的；冷酷的
definitions.example: The grim economic outlook has prompted the government to launch a new fiscal stimulus package.

grim.etymology: 来自古英语grimm，原指‘凶猛的、残酷的’，后引申为形势严峻、令人沮丧的

similar_words.description: 「grim」侧重形势、表情的严峻冷酷；「gloomy」侧重氛围、心情的阴暗沮丧

definitions.meaning: 露齿而笑，咧嘴笑（常带释然或戏谑意味）
definitions.example: He grinned with relief when he learned that his postgraduate entrance exam score met the admission threshold.

grin.etymology: 来自古英语grennian，原指‘咧嘴露齿’，后引申为露齿而笑

similar_words.description: 「grin」指露齿的咧嘴笑，幅度较大；「smile」为通用的微笑，幅度较小

definitions.meaning: 磨碎，碾碎；长期苦学、苦干
definitions.example: Millions of Chinese students grind away at their studies every year to compete for limited postgraduate admission slots.

grind.etymology: 来自古英语grindan，原指‘通过摩擦磨碎’，后引申为长期苦学、苦干

similar_words.description: 「grind」侧重通过反复摩擦磨碎；「crush」侧重通过外力压碎

definitions.example: The professor’s in-depth lecture on cognitive linguistics gripped the attention of all postgraduate students in the auditorium.

grip.etymology: 来自古英语gripan，原指‘用手紧抓’，后引申为吸引（注意力）

similar_words.description: 「grip」侧重紧握物体的力度或吸引注意力的强度；「grasp」侧重理解知识或抓住机会

definitions.meaning: （因痛苦发出的）呻吟；（物体受压的）嘎吱声；抱怨
definitions.example: The old wooden bridge groaned under the weight of the overloaded truck, signaling potential safety hazards.

groan.etymology: 来自古英语granian，原指‘因痛苦发出低沉声响’，后引申为物体受压的嘎吱声或抱怨

similar_words.description: 「groan」侧重因痛苦、重压发出的低沉声响；「moan」侧重因不满、悲伤发出的连续呜咽

definitions.meaning: 食品杂货商；杂货店老板
definitions.example: The local grocer donated a large stock of non-perishable groceries to the community food bank during the pandemic.

grocer.etymology: 来自古法语grossier，原指‘批发商’，后引申为经营食品杂货的零售商

similar_words.description: 「grocer」指经营食品杂货的商人；「grocery」指杂货店，常用复数「groceries」表示杂货

definitions.example: Policy makers are still groping for balanced solutions to the conflict between economic growth and environmental protection.
The celebrity was sued for allegedly groping a female fan at a public event.

grope.etymology: 来自古英语grapian，原指‘用手抓取’，后引申为在黑暗或不确定中摸索，又衍生出猥亵的僻义

similar_words.description: 「grope」侧重在黑暗或不确定中摸索；「grip」侧重紧握物体或吸引注意力

definitions.meaning: 总的，毛的（与净的相对，常用于经济术语，如GDP中的G即为此义）
definitions.example: China’s gross domestic product (GDP) maintained a steady growth rate of 5.2% in the first half of 2023.

definitions.meaning: （电影、产品等）总共赚得（一定金额）
definitions.example: The Oscar-winning film grossed over $800 million at the global box office within two months of its release.

gross.etymology: 来自拉丁语grossus，原指‘厚的、大的’，后引申为总的、毛的（商业术语），又衍生出粗俗的、总共赚得的义项

similar_words.description: 「gross」表总的、毛的（如GDP中的G）；「net」表净的、纯的（如net profit）

definitions.example: The sustainable economic growth is a core target of the government's macroeconomic policy.

growth.etymology: 由动词「grow（生长、增长）」加名词后缀「-th（表状态或过程）」构成

similar_words.description: growth侧重自然或渐进的增长（如经济、人口），increase侧重数量或程度的具体增加

definitions.example: The manufacturer guarantees that this product will be free from defects for three years.
A written guarantee is required for this expensive electronic device.

guarantee.etymology: 由词根「guar（=guard，守护）」加后缀「-antee（表动作或事物的名词/动词后缀）」构成，意为“守护的承诺”

similar_words.description: guarantee侧重对产品质量或服务的商业承诺，warrant侧重法律或官方的正式担保

definitions.example: Armed troops guard the border to prevent illegal immigration.
The guard at the museum entrance checked every visitor's ID carefully.

guard.etymology: 源自古法语「garder」，意为“守护、看管”，同根词有「guardian（监护人）」

similar_words.description: guard作动词侧重主动守护人或物，ward作动词侧重防御、避开（如ward off疾病/危险）

definitions.example: College students often seek guidance from their professors on career planning.

guidance.etymology: 由动词「guide（指导）」加名词后缀「-ance（表行为或状态）」构成

similar_words.description: guidance侧重方向性的宏观指导（如职业、人生规划），instruction侧重具体的操作指令或教学内容

definitions.example: The seasoned explorer guided the team through the dense rainforest safely.
A reliable study guide is essential for preparing for the postgraduate entrance exam.

guide.etymology: 源自古法语「guider」，意为“引导、带领”，同根词有「guidance（指导）」

similar_words.description: guide侧重由有经验者给予方向性指导，lead侧重带领并主导行动进程

definitions.example: The research must comply with the ethical guidelines set by the academic committee.

guideline.etymology: 由「guide（指导）」加「line（线条，引申为规则、边界）」构成，意为“指导行为的规则线条”

similar_words.description: guideline侧重指导性的建议性准则，standard侧重强制性的统一衡量标准

definitions.example: She couldn't hide her guilt after lying to her parents about her exam results.
The evidence presented in court proved the suspect's guilt beyond doubt.

guilt.etymology: 源自古英语「gylt」，原意为“罪行、过错”，后引申出“内疚”的心理义

similar_words.description: guilt侧重因自身过错产生的内在内疚感，shame侧重因丢脸或被他人指责产生的外在羞愧感

definitions.example: He felt guilty for forgetting his best friend's birthday.
The jury found the defendant guilty of theft after a three-day trial.

guilty.etymology: 由名词「guilt（内疚、罪责）」加形容词后缀「-y（表具有…性质的）」构成

similar_words.description: guilty侧重因自身道德或法律过错产生的内疚，ashamed侧重因自身或他人的不当行为感到羞耻

definitions.example: Many college students learn to play the guitar as a way to relax after intense study.

guitar.etymology: 源自西班牙语「guitarra」，指一种弦乐器

similar_words.description: guitar指弦乐器吉他，guild指行业协会、行会，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The oil spill in the Gulf of Mexico caused severe ecological damage.

definitions.meaning: （意见、地位、阶层等的）鸿沟，分歧
definitions.example: There is a widening gulf between the rich and the poor in many developing countries.

gulf.etymology: 源自希腊语「kolpos」，原意为“海湾”，后引申出“隔阂、分歧”的比喻义

similar_words.description: gulf指面积较大、较深的海湾，bay指面积较小的海湾；gulf还可指分歧（僻义），bay无此义

definitions.example: Research shows that poor oral hygiene may lead to inflammation of the gums.
Students are not allowed to chew gum in most college lecture halls.

gum.etymology: 源自古英语goma，原指上颚，后引申为牙龈；另有义项来自拉丁语gummi（树胶），引申为口香糖

similar_words.description: 「gum」指牙龈、口香糖等，「gun」指枪、炮，形近易混

definitions.example: The suspect was arrested when he attempted to hide the unregistered gun in his luggage.

gun.etymology: 源自古挪威语gunnr，原指战争，后引申为作战用的枪支、火炮

similar_words.description: 「gun」指枪、炮，「gum」指牙龈、口香糖，形近易混

definitions.example: The biologist dissected the fish specimen to examine the structure of its guts.
The gut of the academic paper lies in its innovative framework for climate change analysis.

gut.etymology: 源自古英语guttas，原指肠道，后抽象引申为事物的核心部分

similar_words.description: 「gut」指肠道、核心，「gust」指阵风、情绪爆发，形近易混

definitions.example: The guy who assisted with the experiment is a postgraduate majoring in physics.
Workers tightened the guy of the billboard to prevent it from collapsing in strong winds.

guy.etymology: 源自人名Guy，原指盖伊（人名），后引申为对男子的称呼；牵索义来自荷兰语gei，原指系船索

similar_words.description: 「guy」指男子、牵索，「gay」指快乐的、同性恋的，形近易混

definitions.example: The university’s newly built gymnasium is equipped with high-standard fitness and sports facilities.

gymnasium.etymology: 源自希腊语gymnasion，原指古希腊运动员裸体训练的场所，后引申为室内体育馆、健身房

similar_words.description: 「gymnasium」指室内体育馆、健身房，「stadium」指室外体育场，近义易混

definitions.example: Psychological studies indicate that it takes about 66 days to form a stable daily habit.
The nun wore a plain white habit during the religious ceremony held in the chapel.

habit.etymology: 源自拉丁语habitus，原指状态、穿着，后引申为长期形成的习惯；道袍义保留了原指服饰的义项

similar_words.description: 「habit」侧重个人长期形成的行为习惯，「custom」侧重社会群体的习俗惯例，近义易混

definitions.example: Deforestation and urban expansion have severely damaged the natural habitat of giant pandas.

habitat.etymology: 源自拉丁语habitare（居住）+ 名词后缀-at，指动植物居住的特定环境

similar_words.description: 「habitat」指动植物的栖息地，「habit」指个人习惯、道袍，形近易混

definitions.example: The Nobel Prize winner was hailed as a pioneer in the field of quantum computing.
A sudden violent hail destroyed a large area of wheat crops in the northern region.

hail.etymology: 源自古英语hægel，原指冰雹，后引申为像冰雹一样密集落下，再抽象为公开赞扬

similar_words.description: 「hail」指赞扬、冰雹，「hale」指（老人）健壮的，形近易混

definitions.example: The annual academic conference was held in the main hall of the city’s cultural center.

hall.etymology: 源自古英语heall，原指大房子、公共集会的场所，后引申为大厅、礼堂

similar_words.description: 「hall」指大厅、礼堂，「haul」指拖运、大批捕获物，形近易混

definitions.example: The government ordered the illegal mining project to halt immediately to protect the local ecosystem.
The sudden halt of the high-speed train caused minor panic among the passengers.

halt.etymology: 源自古法语halte，原指军队停止前进的命令，后引申为各类动作的停止

similar_words.description: 「halt」指停止、中止，「hail」指赞扬、冰雹，形近易混

definitions.example: Many European families serve smoked ham with pickles during traditional Christmas dinners.

ham.etymology: 源自古英语「hamm」，原指动物后腿部位，后特指经腌制的猪后腿（即火腿）

similar_words.description: 拼写仅第二个字母不同，前者指火腿，后者指嗡嗡声或哼唱

definitions.example: Fast-food chains are increasingly promoting plant-based hamburgers to target eco-conscious consumers.

hamburger.etymology: 源自德国城市名「Hamburg」（汉堡），原指汉堡式牛肉饼，后引申为夹肉饼的汉堡包

similar_words.description: 前者指汉堡包，后者指德国城市汉堡，拼写差异为后缀「-er」

definitions.example: A skilled carpenter uses a claw hammer to both drive and pull out nails.
The academic advisor hammered home the significance of ethical research practices to graduate students.

hammer.etymology: 源自古英语「hamor」，与日耳曼语族表「打击」的词根相关，后引申出动词义

similar_words.description: 拼写前3个字母均为「ham」，第4个字母分别为「m」和「p」，前者指锤子/锤击，后者指妨碍/篮子

definitions.example: Heavy snowfall and icy roads hampered the delivery of emergency supplies to remote villages.
She sorted the dirty clothes and placed them in a wicker hamper in the laundry room.

hamper.etymology: 源自中古英语「hamperen」，原指「用篮子盛装」，后因篮子的束缚义引申出动词「妨碍」

similar_words.description: 拼写前3个字母均为「ham」，第4个字母分别为「p」和「m」，前者指妨碍/篮子，后者指锤子/锤击

definitions.example: All international students are advised to consult the university handbook for visa renewal procedures.

handbook.etymology: 合成词，由「hand（手）」和「book（书）」构成，指可手持查阅的小型实用参考书籍

similar_words.description: 均含「指南、手册」义，handbook侧重通用实用的参考手册，manual侧重操作说明或技术规范手册

definitions.example: He grabbed a handful of sunflower seeds from the bowl while watching the documentary.
Only a handful of researchers have access to the confidential archival materials in the library.

handful.etymology: 合成词，由「hand（手）」和「-ful（充满的）」构成，原指一手可握持的量，后引申为抽象的「少量」

similar_words.description: 均为「hand」开头的合成词，前者指一把/少数，后者指传单

definitions.example: Limited access to high-speed Internet is a significant handicap for rural students participating in online courses.
His lack of professional certification handicapped his chances of getting promoted in the accounting firm.

handicap.etymology: 源自古法语「handicap」，原指一种赌博游戏，后引申为「障碍、不利条件」

similar_words.description: 均含「妨碍」义，handicap侧重自身或外部条件导致的长期不利，hamper侧重临时外部因素阻碍行动

definitions.example: She took out a cotton handkerchief to wipe the dust off the antique book cover.

handkerchief.etymology: 合成词，由「hand（手）」和「kerchief（头巾，源自古法语）」构成，原指手持的头巾，后引申为手帕

similar_words.description: 均含「擦汗/涕的用品」义，handkerchief指可重复使用的布制手帕，tissue指一次性纸质纸巾

definitions.example: The customer service representative is trained to handle tricky inquiries with patience and professionalism.
Please pull the handle upward to unlock the fire exit door in case of emergency.

handle.etymology: 源自古英语「handlian」，与「hand（手）」相关，原指用手操作，后引申出「把手」和「处理」义
