similar_words.description: 均含「处理」义，handle侧重日常事务的常规应对，tackle侧重困难问题的集中攻坚

definitions.example: The handsome archaeologist gave a public lecture on the latest discoveries in the ancient tomb.
The local government allocated a handsome sum of money to renovate the historic city center.

handsome.etymology: 源自古英语「handsam」，原指「易处理的、合适的」，后引申为「英俊的」和「可观的」

similar_words.description: 均含「好看的」义，handsome多形容男子英俊或事物大方美观，pretty多形容女子或事物小巧漂亮

definitions.example: The handwriting on the ancient manuscript was so faint that researchers had to use infrared technology to decipher it.

handwriting.etymology: 合成词，由「hand（手）」+「writing（书写）」构成，指用手书写留下的痕迹或作品

similar_words.description: handwriting指手写的笔迹/书法，typewriting指打字的文稿/打字法

definitions.example: A pocket-sized dictionary is extremely handy for students taking translation tests.

handy.etymology: 派生词，由「hand（手）」+形容词后缀「-y（表有…特性的）」构成，指随手可用的或手巧的

similar_words.description: handy侧重「随手可用的」，convenient侧重「整体情况便利的」

definitions.example: The researcher hung the experimental samples in a temperature-controlled cabinet.
The historical records showed that three rebels were hanged for treason in 1798.

hang.etymology: 源自古英语「hangian」，无明显派生词缀，核心语义为「悬垂」

similar_words.description: hung是「悬挂」的过去式/过去分词，hanged是「绞死」的过去式/过去分词，考研易混

definitions.example: Hardly had the unexpected incident happened when the emergency team arrived at the scene.

happen.etymology: 派生词，由古英语词根「hap（机会、运气）」+动词后缀「-en（使成为）」构成，原指「偶然出现」

similar_words.description: happen侧重「偶然发生」，occur侧重「按计划或规律发生」

definitions.example: The polar research team was harassed by repeated equipment failures and harsh weather.

harass.etymology: 源自古法语「harer（激怒）」，引申为「不断打扰使疲惫」

similar_words.description: harass指骚扰/扰乱，harness指利用（能源）、给（马）套具

definitions.example: The university issued a strict policy to combat sexual harassment on campus.

harassment.etymology: 派生词，由动词「harass（骚扰）」+名词后缀「-ment（表行为/状态）」构成

similar_words.description: harassment是名词，表骚扰行为；harass是动词，表实施骚扰

definitions.example: The coastal city’s harbor was expanded to accommodate larger international cargo ships.
She harbored a deep resentment toward her former academic advisor for years.

harbor.etymology: 源自古英语「herebeorg（避难所）」，原指船只避难的地方，引申为港口；动词义由「藏匿」引申为「心怀」

similar_words.description: harbor侧重有遮蔽功能的港口，port侧重通商功能的港口

definitions.example: Repeated exposure to low temperatures hardened the outer layer of the experimental polymer.

harden.etymology: 派生词，由形容词「hard（硬的）」+动词后缀「-en（使变得）」构成

similar_words.description: harden侧重物理变硬或情感变冷酷，strengthen侧重力量或能力增强

definitions.meaning: 几乎不；几乎没有（常引导倒装结构，考研核心考点）
definitions.example: Hardly had the research paper been published when it sparked a heated debate among linguists.

hardly.etymology: 派生词，由「hard（努力地；硬的）」+副词后缀「-ly」构成，注意其并非hard的常规副词形式

similar_words.description: hardly表「几乎不」（半否定），hard作副词表「努力地；猛烈地」

definitions.example: The rural family endured great financial hardship to send their only child to a prestigious university.

hardship.etymology: 派生词，由形容词「hard（艰难的）」+名词后缀「-ship（表状态/性质）」构成

similar_words.description: hardship表生活或处境的困苦，hardness表物体的硬度

definitions.meaning: （计算机的）硬件；教学/科研设备
definitions.example: Educators are advocating for more investments in school hardware such as interactive whiteboards and lab devices.
The construction company imported new hardware for the cross-river bridge project.

hardware.etymology: 由形容词hard（硬的）+ 名词后缀-ware（表「制品、器具」）构成，原指金属制的硬器具，后引申为计算机硬件、设备等义

similar_words.description: hardware指硬件/设备，hardship指艰难困苦，词性相同但词义完全不同，易因拼写开头相同混淆

definitions.example: Long-term exposure to polluted air may cause permanent harm to the human respiratory system.
Excessive screen time can harm children’s visual development and cognitive abilities.

harm.etymology: 源自古英语hearm，核心词根为harm-，表「伤害、损害」，无复杂词缀

similar_words.description: harm指伤害/损害，hamper指妨碍或大篮子，词义差异大，易因拼写接近混淆

definitions.meaning: 和谐；融洽；（社会/生态的）协调
definitions.example: The government aims to promote harmony between urban development and ecological protection.

harmony.etymology: 由词根har-（=joint，表「连接」）+ 名词后缀-mony（表「状态」）构成，原指音乐中音符的协调连接，引申为事物间的和谐融洽

similar_words.description: harmony指和谐融洽，harm指伤害损害，词义相反，易因拼写开头相同混淆

definitions.example: Scientists are exploring ways to harness solar energy more efficiently to reduce carbon emissions.
The farmer adjusted the horse’s harness before plowing the sloped field.

harness.etymology: 源自古法语harnois，原指「盔甲」，后引申为马具，动词义由「给马套挽具」引申为「控制、利用（力量）」

similar_words.description: harness指利用/控制或马具，hardware指硬件/设备，词义完全不同，易因拼写开头相同混淆

definitions.meaning: （气候、条件）严酷的；（人、规则）严厉的
definitions.example: The harsh winter conditions in the Arctic make it difficult for many species to survive.

harsh.etymology: 源自中古英语harsk，表「粗糙的」，核心词根为harsh-，引申为「严酷的、严厉的」，无复杂词缀

similar_words.description: harsh指严厉/严酷的，rash指轻率的，词义不同，易因拼写结尾相同混淆

definitions.example: The annual grain harvest in this region has increased by 12% due to advanced farming techniques.
Farmers in the south are busy harvesting rice during the autumn season.

harvest.etymology: 由词根har-（=pick，表「采摘」）+ 词根vest（=gather，表「收集」）构成，原指采摘收集作物，引申为收获、收成

similar_words.description: harvest指收获/收割，harness指利用/控制或马具，词义不同，易因拼写开头相同混淆

definitions.example: The report was written in haste and contained several factual errors that affected its credibility.

haste.etymology: 源自古法语haste，表「匆忙」，核心词根为haste-，无复杂词缀，考研常考搭配为in haste

similar_words.description: haste是名词，指匆忙；hasty是形容词，指匆忙的/草率的，同根但词性不同，易混淆

definitions.example: Making a hasty decision without sufficient data may lead to unexpected losses in business negotiations.

hasty.etymology: 由名词haste（匆忙）+ 形容词后缀-y（表「具有…性质的」）构成，指「匆忙的、草率的」

similar_words.description: hasty是形容词，指匆忙的/草率的；haste是名词，指匆忙，同根但词性不同，易混淆

definitions.example: The bird spent three weeks hatching its eggs in the nest hidden in the oak tree.
The group hatched a secret plan to undermine the authority of the local government.

hatch.etymology: 源自古英语hæccan，表「孵出」，后由「孵出幼崽」引申为「策划（计划、阴谋）」

similar_words.description: hatch指孵出/策划，hitch指钩住/故障，词义不同，易因拼写结尾相同混淆

definitions.example: Many people hate the idea of working overtime on weekends, as it disrupts their family life.
The speech was filled with hate and prejudice, which sparked public outrage.

hate.etymology: 源自古英语hatian，表「憎恨」，核心词根为hate-，无复杂词缀

similar_words.description: hate指憎恨/厌恶，heat指热/加热，词义完全不同，易因拼写接近混淆

definitions.example: Racial hatred remains a persistent problem undermining social cohesion in some Western democracies.

hatred.etymology: 由动词「hate」（憎恨）加抽象名词后缀「-red」构成，表抽象的憎恶情感

similar_words.description: 「hatred」为名词，侧重深刻、持久的憎恶情感；「hate」可作动词或名词，表一般的憎恨，使用更宽泛

definitions.example: Fishermen hauled their nets full of salmon onto the deck before dawn.

haul.etymology: 源自古法语hauler，本义为「拉、拖」，无明显派生前缀或后缀，为根词

similar_words.description: 「haul」指用力拖运、运送；「hail」作名词指冰雹，作动词指致敬、招呼

definitions.example: Hawks circled high above the mountain valley searching for small prey.
Street vendors hawked handcrafted souvenirs to tourists near the historic site.

hawk.etymology: 源自古英语hafoc，本义为猛禽，无明显派生前缀或后缀，为根词

similar_words.description: 「hawk」指体型较小的猛禽鹰、隼；「eagle」指体型较大的雕

definitions.example: Farmers stacked bales of hay in the barn to feed cattle during the harsh winter.

hay.etymology: 源自古英语hieg，本义为干草，为根词

similar_words.description: 「hay」指用作牲畜饲料的干草；「hey」为口语化的招呼用语，表「嘿」

definitions.example: Exposure to toxic chemicals poses a serious hazard to the health of factory workers.
The researcher hazarded a guess about the long-term effects of climate change on crop yields.

hazard.etymology: 源自古法语hasard，本义为「机会、风险」，无明显派生前缀或后缀，为根词

similar_words.description: 「hazard」作名词指危险、危害物，作动词指冒险；「hazardous」为其形容词形式，表「危险的、有害的」

definitions.meaning: 头痛；令人头疼的人或事
definitions.example: The shortage of skilled labor has become a major headache for small manufacturing enterprises.

headache.etymology: 由复合词构成，「head」（头）+「ache」（疼痛），表头部的疼痛，引申为令人头疼的人或事

similar_words.description: 「headache」指头痛或令人头疼的事；「backache」指背部疼痛

definitions.example: The heading of the research paper clearly indicates its focus on renewable energy policies.

heading.etymology: 由「head」（头，引申为「开头、核心」）加名词后缀「-ing」构成，表位于开头的文字，即标题

similar_words.description: 「heading」多指文章、报告的章节小标题；「headline」多指报纸、新闻的大标题或头条

definitions.example: The newspaper's front-page headline highlighted the government's new education reform plan.

headline.etymology: 由复合词构成，「head」（开头）+「line」（行），表位于开头的一行文字，即大标题

similar_words.description: 「headline」多指报纸、新闻的大标题或头条；「heading」多指文章、报告的章节小标题

definitions.example: The headmaster delivered an inspiring speech at the school's opening ceremony.

headmaster.etymology: 由复合词构成，「head」（主管、领头）+「master」（主人、管理者），表学校的主管者，即校长

similar_words.description: 「headmaster」多指英式中小学的校长（尤指男校长）；「principal」可泛指各类学校的校长

definitions.example: The multinational corporation has its global headquarters in Geneva, Switzerland.

headquarters.etymology: 由复合词构成，「head」（核心、领头）+「quarters」（驻地、处所），表组织的核心驻地，即总部

similar_words.description: 「headquarters」指组织的核心总部；「branch」指组织的分支机构、分部

definitions.example: Time alone cannot heal the deep wounds left by racial discrimination.

heal.etymology: 源自古英语hælan，与形容词「whole」（完整的）同源，本义为‘使恢复完整’

similar_words.description: 形近易混，「heal」为动词表治愈、愈合，「heel」为名词表脚后跟、鞋跟

definitions.example: A heap of old academic documents was found in the attic of the university library.
Protesters heaped stones and debris at the entrance of the local government building.

heap.etymology: 源自古英语heap，与古高地德语houf（堆）同源，表‘堆积的物品’

similar_words.description: 近义，「heap」指杂乱无章的堆，「pile」指整齐堆叠的堆

definitions.example: Prolonged exposure to loud industrial noise may cause permanent damage to one's hearing.
The congressional hearing on climate policy lasted for over six hours.

hearing.etymology: 源自动词「hear」，加名词后缀-ing构成，表‘听的动作或能力’

similar_words.description: 同根，「hear」为动词表听、听见，「hearing」为名词表听力、听证会

definitions.example: The extreme summer heat has become a pressing issue for urban ecological planning.
The lab assistant heated the chemical solution to 75 degrees Celsius to initiate the reaction.

heat.etymology: 源自古英语hǣtu，与形容词「hot」（热的）同源，表‘热的状态’

similar_words.description: 同根，「hot」为形容词表热的，「heat」为名词/动词表热、加热

definitions.example: The dock workers heaved the heavy steel container onto the cargo ship.

heave.etymology: 源自古英语hebban，与原始日耳曼语*habjan（举起）同源

similar_words.description: 形近易混，「heave」为动词/名词表用力举起、拖曳，「heaven」为名词表天堂、天空

definitions.example: The romantic poet described the quiet mountain village as a heaven on earth.
The stars in the heavens twinkled brightly on that cloudless winter night.

heaven.etymology: 源自古英语heofon，与古高地德语himil（天堂、天空）同源

similar_words.description: 形近易混，「heaven」为名词表天堂、天空，「heave」为动词/名词表用力举起、拖曳

definitions.example: A neatly trimmed hedge separated the historic mansion from the public sidewalk.
Many institutional investors hedge against inflation by purchasing precious metals.

hedge.etymology: 源自古英语hecg，本义为‘篱笆、围栏’，后引申出‘规避’义

similar_words.description: 形近易混，「hedge」为名词/动词表树篱、规避，「ledge」为名词表壁架、窗台

definitions.example: She wore elegant high heels to the annual academic conference despite the discomfort.
The small sailboat heeled over sharply when a sudden gust of wind struck.

heel.etymology: 源自古英语hēla，与古诺斯语hæll（脚后跟）同源

similar_words.description: 形近易混，「heel」为名词/动词表脚后跟、（船）倾侧，「heal」为动词表治愈、愈合

definitions.example: The exact height of the newly discovered mountain peak was confirmed by satellite survey.
At the height of his artistic career, the painter held solo exhibitions in three major cities.

height.etymology: 源自形容词「high」，加名词后缀-t构成，类似「length」（来自「long」），表‘高的状态或程度’

similar_words.description: 同根，「high」为形容词表高的，「height」为名词表高度、顶峰

definitions.example: The environmental organization launched a campaign to heighten public awareness of marine pollution.

heighten.etymology: 由名词「height」加动词后缀-en构成，表‘使变高、增强’

similar_words.description: 同根，「height」为名词表高度、顶峰，「heighten」为动词表提高、增强

definitions.example: The eldest son was the sole heir to the family's vast estate and antique collection.

heir.etymology: 来自古法语「eir」，与「inherit（继承）」同根，表享有继承权的人

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，heir表继承人，hair表头发

definitions.example: Rescue teams deployed a helicopter to evacuate the stranded tourists from the snow-capped mountain.

helicopter.etymology: 由希腊语词根「helico-（螺旋）」与「pter-（翅膀）」合成，指带螺旋翼的飞行器

similar_words.description: 同根（helico-表螺旋），helix表螺旋线，helicopter表直升机

definitions.example: In numerous religious doctrines, hell is portrayed as a realm of eternal suffering for the wicked.
The refugees described the overcrowded camp as a living hell with scarce food and water.

hell.etymology: 来自古英语「hel」，原指地下的阴暗居所

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，hell表地狱/苦境，hall表大厅

definitions.example: Workers on construction sites are mandated to wear a helmet to guard against falling debris.

helmet.etymology: 来自古法语「helmet」，是「helm（头盔）」的指小形式

similar_words.description: 同根（helm表头盔/舵），helmsman表舵手，helmet表头盔

definitions.example: Peer feedback is particularly helpful for students to refine their academic writing skills.

helpful.etymology: 由「help（帮助）」加形容词后缀「-ful（充满…的）」构成

similar_words.description: 近义，helpful侧重主动提供帮助的属性，useful侧重事物本身的实用性

definitions.meaning: 半球（尤指地球的南北半球或大脑半球）
definitions.example: The left hemisphere of the human brain is generally associated with logical reasoning and language processing.

hemisphere.etymology: 由希腊语前缀「hemi-（半）」与词根「sphere（球）」合成

similar_words.description: 同根（sphere表球体），hemisphere表半球，sphere表球体/领域

definitions.example: The organic farm raises over 500 hens to supply free-range eggs to local supermarkets.

hen.etymology: 来自古英语「henn」，原指雌性鸟类

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，hen表母鸡，pen表钢笔/围栏

definitions.example: The sample size was too small; hence, the research findings cannot be generalized to the whole population.
Walk three miles hence, and you will reach the historic castle overlooking the valley.

hence.etymology: 来自古英语「hennes」，原指从那个地方

similar_words.description: 近义，均表逻辑因果，hence更侧重正式语境的推导，thus还可表方式或结果

definitions.example: Henceforth, all academic papers submitted to the journal must include a data availability statement.

henceforth.etymology: 由「hence（从此）」与「forth（向前）」合成的副词

similar_words.description: 同根（hence表从此），hence表因此/从此，henceforth专指从今以后

definitions.example: Many traditional Chinese medicines are derived from herbs with proven therapeutic effects.

herb.etymology: 来自拉丁语「herba」，原指草或草本植物

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，herb表草本植物，herd表兽群/放牧

definitions.example: The herd of wildebeests migrates across the Serengeti every year in search of fresh grass and water.
The rancher herded the sheep into the barn before the storm arrived.

herd.etymology: 源自古英语「heord」，原指牧放的兽群，同根词有herdsman（牧人）

similar_words.description: herd指兽群/放牧，herb指草本植物，拼写接近易混淆

definitions.example: The ancient architectural complex in Xi'an is regarded as a precious national heritage reflecting the wisdom of ancient Chinese craftsmen.

heritage.etymology: 由词根「her-」（继承，源自heir）+ 名词后缀「-itage」构成，意为“继承下来的事物”

similar_words.description: heritage侧重国家/民族的文化遗产，inheritance侧重个人继承的财产或特质

definitions.example: In the historical novel set during the Anti-Japanese War, the hero sacrifices his life to protect his fellow villagers.

hero.etymology: 源自希腊语「heros」，原指半神半人的传奇人物，后泛指具有英勇品质的人或文艺作品的男主角

similar_words.description: hero指男性英雄/男主角，heroine指女性英雄/女主角，为同根性别对应词

definitions.example: The medical workers made heroic efforts to treat patients during the COVID-19 pandemic.

heroic.etymology: 由名词「hero」（英雄）+ 形容词后缀「-ic」构成，表“具有英雄特质的”

similar_words.description: heroic为形容词表英勇的，heroin为名词表海洛因，拼写仅差末尾一个字母「c」

definitions.example: The documentary reveals the devastating physical and psychological effects of heroin addiction on young people.

heroin.etymology: 源自德语商标名「Heroin」，最初被用作止痛药，因药效显著被称为“英雄药”，后成为毒品名称

similar_words.description: heroin为海洛因，heroine为女英雄/女主角，拼写仅差末尾一个字母「e」

definitions.example: The feminist novel portrays a brave heroine who fights for equal rights in the male-dominated workplace.

heroine.etymology: 由名词「hero」（英雄）+ 阴性名词后缀「-ine」构成，特指女性英雄或文艺作品的女主角

similar_words.description: hero指男性英雄/男主角，heroine指女性英雄/女主角，为同根性别对应词

definitions.example: When faced with ethical dilemmas, many researchers hesitate to publish controversial findings without sufficient evidence.

hesitate.etymology: 源自拉丁语「haesitare」（犹豫），词根「haer-」表“黏住”，引申为“停滞、迟疑”

similar_words.description: hesitate为动词表犹豫，hesitation为其名词形式，表犹豫的行为或状态

definitions.example: The witness tried to hide the crucial evidence from the police during the criminal investigation.
In prehistoric times, humans used animal hides to make shelters and warm clothing.

hide.etymology: 动词义源自古英语「hydan」（隐藏），名词义源自古英语「hyd」（兽皮），同根词有hidden（隐藏的）

similar_words.description: hide为动词表隐藏或名词表兽皮，hinder为动词表阻碍，拼写接近易混淆

definitions.example: In most traditional corporations, a strict hierarchy determines the decision-making authority of each department.

hierarchy.etymology: 源自希腊语「hierarchia」（祭司等级），由「hier-」（神圣的）+ 「arch-」（统治）+ 名词后缀「-y」构成，原指宗教祭司的等级，后泛指一般等级制度

similar_words.description: hierarchy为名词表等级制度，hierarchical为其形容词形式，表“等级制的”

definitions.meaning: 高地，高原（常作复数Highlands指苏格兰高地）
definitions.example: The Scottish Highlands are famous for their rugged mountain landscapes and traditional Celtic culture.

highland.etymology: 为复合词，由形容词「high」（高的）+ 名词「land」（土地）构成，指地势较高的地区

similar_words.description: highland指高地，lowland指低地，为反义复合词，拼写对应易混淆

definitions.example: Numerous studies in environmental science highlight the irreversible damage caused by excessive carbon emissions.
The highlight of the 2022 Nobel Prize ceremony was the award speech delivered by the laureate in literature.

highlight.etymology: 由high（高的）和light（光）构成，原指将光线打在某物上使其醒目，后引申为强调、突出或最亮眼的部分

similar_words.description: 二者都表「强调」，highlight侧重通过突出呈现使受关注，emphasize侧重通过言语或行动强调重要性

definitions.example: The research findings on renewable energy are highly valued by policymakers around the world.

highly.etymology: 由形容词high（高的）加副词后缀-ly构成，用于修饰抽象的程度或评价

similar_words.description: high作副词多修饰具体动作表「高」（如fly high），highly多表抽象程度（如highly appreciated）

definitions.example: The newly built highway has cut the travel time between the two cities by half.
Digital platforms have become an important highway for small and medium-sized enterprises to expand overseas markets.

highway.etymology: 由high（主要的）和way（道路）构成，原指重要的通行干道，后引申为各类要素的流通通道

similar_words.description: highway可指普通干线公路或泛指流通通道，expressway特指全封闭的高速公路

definitions.example: The emergency response team managed to rescue all the passengers after the criminals attempted to hijack the coach.
Right-wing groups tried to hijack the rally against racial discrimination to spread extreme views.

hijack.etymology: 由high（公路）和jack（对男子的俗称）构成，原指旧时公路上的拦路抢劫，后引申为劫持、非法操控

similar_words.description: rob泛指抢劫财物，hijack特指劫持交通工具或非法操控公共事务

definitions.example: Taking a hike in the national park is a popular way for office workers to relax on weekends.

definitions.meaning: （价格、利率等的）大幅提高，急剧上升
definitions.example: The unexpected oil price hike has pushed up the operating costs of many logistics companies.

hike.etymology: 源自古英语表「猛拉、抬高」的词根，引申为费力行走（远足）和数值大幅提升

similar_words.description: rise泛指数量上升，hike特指价格、税收等的突然大幅上涨

definitions.example: Strict entry requirements have hindered many low-income students from applying for top universities.

hinder.etymology: 由hind（后面的）加动词后缀-er构成，意为使…落在后面，引申为阻碍、妨碍

similar_words.description: hinder是动词表阻碍，hinge多作名词表铰链，作动词表取决于

definitions.example: The rusted hinge of the old cabinet makes a harsh noise every time it is opened.

definitions.meaning: 取决于，以…为转移（常与on/upon连用）
definitions.example: The outcome of the trade negotiation hinges on whether both sides can make compromises on tariff issues.

hinge.etymology: 源自古英语表「悬挂」的词根，原指悬挂门的铰链，后引申为事物的关键，以及事情取决于某因素

similar_words.description: hinge作动词表取决于，作名词表铰链，hinder是动词表阻碍

definitions.example: The spokesperson did not hint at any possible adjustment to the current foreign trade policy.
There are faint hints that the domestic consumer market is gradually recovering from the epidemic.

hint.etymology: 源自古英语表「抓住」的词根，原指可被捕捉的蛛丝马迹，后引申为暗示、线索

similar_words.description: clue侧重解谜、破案的具体线索，hint侧重间接的暗示或微小迹象

definitions.example: Regular exercise can effectively strengthen the muscles around the hip and reduce the risk of injury.
The café located in the old town is decorated in a hip retro style and is very popular among young people.

hip.etymology: 古英语原有词汇，原指人体髋部，后在俚语中引申为时髦的、新潮的

similar_words.description: hip指髋部、臀部，thigh指大腿部位

definitions.example: To cope with the peak business season, the retail chain plans to hire 200 more part-time employees.
The hire of specialized construction equipment accounts for a large proportion of the project's total cost.

hire.etymology: 源自古英语，原指付费使用物品或获取劳动服务，即租用、雇用

similar_words.description: employ多指长期正式雇用员工，hire可指短期雇用或租用物品、场地

definitions.example: The historian analyzed newly declassified government documents to revise the established narrative of the Cold War.

historian.etymology: 由词根「history（历史）」加表人的名词后缀「-ian」构成，指研究历史的专业人员

similar_words.description: 同根形近词，「historian」为名词，指研究历史的人；「historic」为形容词，指有历史意义的

definitions.meaning: 有历史意义的，历史上著名的
definitions.example: The two heads of state signed a historic trade agreement that eliminated tariffs on more than 90 percent of bilateral goods.

historic.etymology: 由词根「history（历史）」加形容词后缀「-ic」构成，表具有历史重要性的

similar_words.description: 同根形近词，「historic」侧重事物具有历史意义或历史地位；「historical」侧重与历史研究、史实相关的

definitions.meaning: 历史的，史学的，与史实相关的
definitions.example: The research paper provides historical evidence to challenge the long-standing assumption about the origin of ancient agricultural techniques.

historical.etymology: 由词根「history（历史）」加形容词后缀「-ical」构成，表属于历史范畴的

similar_words.description: 同根形近词，「historical」侧重与历史研究、史实相关的；「historic」侧重事物具有历史意义或历史地位

definitions.example: Studying modern European history helps students understand the roots of contemporary political conflicts across the continent.

history.etymology: 源自拉丁语「historia」，原指叙事、纪事，进入英语后引申为客观发生的历史及史学研究

similar_words.description: 同源词，「history」指客观发生的历史或史学研究；「story」指虚构或真实的故事、叙事

definitions.example: The 2008 global financial crisis hit small and medium-sized manufacturing enterprises particularly hard in developing countries.

definitions.meaning: 轰动一时的成功作品（如书籍、电影）
definitions.example: The academic monograph on climate change became an unexpected hit among general readers and topped nonfiction bestseller lists.

hit.etymology: 源自古英语「hittan」，原义为击中、到达，后引申出袭击、热门作品等义

similar_words.description: 近义词，「hit」常指突发的击中、袭击或意外成功；「strike」侧重蓄意打击、罢工或钟的敲响

definitions.example: Hitherto, scientists have not obtained conclusive empirical evidence to verify the controversial theory about dark matter.

hitherto.etymology: 由古英语副词「hither（到这里）」加介词「to」构成，合指到当前的时间点为止

similar_words.description: 近义副词，「hitherto」描述过去到现在的状态，表“迄今”；「henceforth」描述现在到未来的状态，表“从今以后”

definitions.example: The engineering team used a specialized crane to hoist the 50-ton prefabricated bridge section into its designated position.

hoist.etymology: 源自中古荷兰语「hyssen」，原义为拉、拽，后引申为用机械装置吊起重物

similar_words.description: 近义词，「hoist」特指用机械装置吊起重物；「lift」可指用手或机械举起任何物体

definitions.meaning: 持有者，持有人；（职位的）担任者
definitions.example: The patent holder has the exclusive legal right to commercialize the new energy-saving technology for a 15-year period.

holder.etymology: 由动词「hold（持有、握住）」加表人或物的名词后缀「-er」构成，指持有物品或担任职位的主体

similar_words.description: 近义词，「holder」侧重长期持有物品或权利的人；「bearer」侧重临时携带某物（如票据、信件）的人

definitions.example: The environmental inspection team detected a 2-centimeter hole in the oil pipeline that caused the massive leak in the gulf.
Many young graduates fall into a financial hole after taking on high-interest loans to cover living expenses in big cities.

hole.etymology: 源自古英语「hol」，原指天然或人工形成的洞、孔，后引申出抽象的困境义

similar_words.description: 形近同根词，「hole」为名词，指具体的洞或抽象困境；「hollow」多作形容词，指空心的或空洞的

definitions.example: The biologists found that the hollow stems of certain bamboo species can store large amounts of water during dry seasons.

definitions.meaning: 空洞的，无意义的，虚假的
definitions.example: Critics argue that the policy proposal contains only hollow promises and lacks specific measures to address income inequality.

hollow.etymology: 源自古英语「holh」，与「hole」同源，原指空心的物体，后引申出抽象的空洞无意义义

similar_words.description: 形近同根词，「hollow」多作形容词，指空心的或空洞的；「hole」为名词，指具体的洞或抽象困境

definitions.example: In medieval Europe, holy sites were often protected by royal decrees to preserve their cultural and religious significance.

holy.etymology: 来自古英语hālig，核心词根为「hol-」，表「完整、神圣」

similar_words.description: 「holy」为形容词表神圣的，「holly」为名词表冬青植物，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: Researchers noted that the study sample was genetically homogeneous, which minimized confounding variables in the experiment.

homogeneous.etymology: 由希腊语前缀「homo-」（同）+ 词根「gen-」（种类、基因）+ 形容词后缀「-ous」构成

similar_words.description: 「homogeneous」表同质的，「heterogeneous」为其同根反义词，表异质的

definitions.example: An honest academic researcher must fully disclose all potential conflicts of interest related to their study.

honest.etymology: 来自拉丁语honestus，核心词根为「hon-」，表「荣誉、尊敬」

similar_words.description: 「honest」为形容词表诚实的，「honesty」为其同根名词表诚实

definitions.example: Bees produce honey by collecting nectar from flowers and processing it through enzymatic reactions in their hives.
In the novel, the elderly woman addressed her young granddaughter as 'honey' to show affection.

honey.etymology: 来自古英语hunig，属于原始日耳曼语核心词汇，无考研级可拆分词根词缀

similar_words.description: 「honey」表蜂蜜或亲爱的，「money」表金钱，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: Winning the national academic scholarship is a great honor for undergraduate students in the humanities.
The university will honor three distinguished alumni for their contributions to environmental protection.

honor.etymology: 来自拉丁语honor，核心词根为「hon-」，表「荣誉、尊敬」

similar_words.description: 「honor」为美式拼写，「honour」为英式拼写，词义词性完全一致

definitions.example: It is an honorable achievement to be selected as a keynote speaker at the annual international linguistics conference.

honorable.etymology: 由名词「honor」（荣誉）+ 形容词后缀「-able」（值得…的）构成

similar_words.description: 「honorable」表可敬的，「honorary」为同根形容词，表（职位等）荣誉的（无报酬的）

definitions.example: The fisherman attached a live bait to the sharp hook before casting his line into the lake.
The opening scene of the documentary hooks viewers with stunning footage of Arctic glaciers.

hook.etymology: 来自古英语hoc，属于原始日耳曼语核心词汇，无考研级可拆分词根词缀

similar_words.description: 「hook」作动词表吸引注意力，「catch」为其近义词，也可表吸引注意力

definitions.example: The injured athlete could only hop on one foot to reach the medical tent near the track.
Craft brewers often use organic hops to add a distinct bitter flavor and aroma to their beer.

hop.etymology: 来自古英语hoppian，属于原始日耳曼语核心词汇，无考研级可拆分词根词缀

similar_words.description: 「hop」表跳跃或啤酒花，「hope」表希望，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The research team is hopeful that the new gene-editing technique will treat the rare genetic disorder.
Several presidential hopefuls will outline their policy proposals at the upcoming campaign rally.

hopeful.etymology: 由名词「hope」（希望）+ 形容词后缀「-ful」（充满…的）构成

similar_words.description: 「hopeful」表有希望的，「hopeless」为其同根反义词，表绝望的

definitions.example: As the sun dipped below the horizon, the coastal town was illuminated by soft orange and purple hues.
Participating in international exchange programs can help college students broaden their cultural horizons.

horizon.etymology: 来自希腊语horizein（划分界限），核心词根为「hor-」，表「界限」

similar_words.description: 「horizon」为名词表地平线或眼界，「horizontal」为其同根形容词，表水平的

definitions.example: The engineer adjusted the horizontal axis of the graph to make the data trend clearer for the research report.

horizontal.etymology: 由名词horizon（地平线）加形容词后缀-al构成，原指与地平线平行的

similar_words.description: 二者均为描述空间方向的形容词，为考研阅读图表类高频考点：horizontal表水平、横向，vertical表垂直、纵向

definitions.example: The ancient Celtic warriors often carved patterns on the horn of their helmets to show their bravery.

horn.etymology: 源自古英语horn，原指动物坚硬的角，后引申为号角、喇叭等器物

similar_words.description: 二者拼写相近，易混淆：horn指动物的角、号角，thorn指植物的刺

definitions.example: The witness described the horrible scene of the industrial accident in the court testimony.

horrible.etymology: 由词根horr-（表“害怕、颤抖”）加形容词后缀-ible（表“可…的”）构成，表“令人害怕的”

similar_words.description: 二者均为表“可怕的”形容词：horrible侧重日常语境的可怕、令人不快，horrific更正式，侧重极度恐怖，后者考研出现频率极低

definitions.example: The Gothic novel uses dark settings and bizarre plots to create a strong sense of horror for readers.

horror.etymology: 由词根horr-（表“害怕、颤抖”）加名词后缀-or（表“状态、事物”）构成，表“恐惧、恐怖的事物”

similar_words.description: 二者均表“恐惧”：horror侧重因丑陋、残酷引发的厌恶式恐惧，terror侧重因生命威胁引发的极度恐慌，考研阅读中常考二者的语义差异

definitions.example: This new electric agricultural machine has a power output of 150 horsepower, suitable for tilling large areas of farmland.

horsepower.etymology: 合成词，由horse（马）+ power（功率）构成，原指一匹马的拉力，后成为功率计量单位

similar_words.description: 二者均为功率计量单位：horsepower为英制单位，kilowatt为公制单位，考研科技阅读中偶有涉及

definitions.example: Firefighters connected a high-pressure hose to the hydrant to extinguish the warehouse fire quickly.

hose.etymology: 源自古英语hosa，原指长筒袜，后因形状类似引申为软管、水管

similar_words.description: 二者拼写相近，易混淆：hose指软管、水管，horse指马

definitions.example: The study found that the hospitality of local residents is a key factor in attracting international tourists.

hospitality.etymology: 由词根hospit-（表“客人”，同host的词根）加名词后缀-ality（表“性质、状态”）构成，表“好客、款待的性质”

similar_words.description: 二者拼写相近，易混淆：hospital指医院，hospitality指好客、殷勤款待

definitions.example: The host of the international academic forum welcomed scholars from 28 countries and regions.
Peking University will host the 15th International Conference on Computational Linguistics next year.

host.etymology: 由词根hospit-（表“客人”）缩略而来，原指招待客人的人，后引申为主人、主持人、主办活动的动词义

similar_words.description: 二者拼写相近，易混淆：host指主人、主持人或主持活动，hostage指人质

definitions.example: The international humanitarian organization called for the unconditional release of all civilian hostages in the conflict zone.

hostage.etymology: 由host（主人）加名词后缀-age（表“身份、状态”）构成，原指作为抵押的“客人”，即人质

similar_words.description: 二者拼写相近，易混淆：hostage指人质，host指主人、主持人

definitions.example: The hostess of the environmental protection summit introduced the latest progress in global carbon emission reduction.

hostess.etymology: 由host（主人）加阴性名词后缀-ess构成，特指女性主人、女主持人

similar_words.description: 二者均表“主人/主持人”：hostess特指女性主人、女主持人，host可指男性或泛指主人、主持人

definitions.example: Many local residents remain hostile to the construction of the new chemical plant near their residential area.
The polar bear has evolved thick fur to survive in the hostile Arctic climate.

hostile.etymology: 源自拉丁语「hostis」（敌人），加形容词后缀「-ile」（表“具有…性质的”），表“具有敌人性质的”

similar_words.description: 形近易混，hospitable表‘好客的、热情的’，hostile表‘敌对的’，二者语义相反

definitions.meaning: 纠缠，追逼（尤指不断烦扰）
definitions.example: The celebrity was hounded by paparazzi wherever she went after the scandal broke.
A trained hound can detect faint scents that humans cannot.

hound.etymology: 源自古英语「hund」（狗），动词义由“用猎犬追逐”引申为“纠缠、追逼”

similar_words.description: 形近易混，hunt表‘打猎、搜寻’，hound作动词表‘纠缠、追逼’，语义不同

definitions.example: The survey collects data on the income and expenditure of each household in the sample.
Household appliances such as refrigerators and washing machines have become essential in modern life.

household.etymology: 由「house」（房子）+「hold」（持有、掌管）构成，指“掌管家庭事务的群体”

similar_words.description: 近义易混，domestic可表‘家庭的’也可表‘国内的’，household仅表‘家庭的、住户的’

definitions.example: The study explores the social status and economic contribution of housewives in rural areas.

housewife.etymology: 由「house」（家庭）+「wife」（妻子）构成，指“专职料理家庭事务的女性”

similar_words.description: 形近易混，housewife是‘家庭主妇’（非受雇），housekeeper是‘受雇的管家’

definitions.example: Gender inequality persists in the division of housework between men and women in many societies.

housework.etymology: 由「house」（家庭）+「work」（劳动）构成，指“家庭范围内的日常劳动”

similar_words.description: 形近易混，homework表‘学生的家庭作业’，housework表‘家务劳动’

definitions.example: The government has introduced a series of policies to solve the housing shortage in big cities.

housing.etymology: 由「house」（房子）+动名词后缀「-ing」构成，指“住房的集合或供给行为”

similar_words.description: 易混，house指具体的‘单栋房子’，housing指集合性的‘住房（供给）’

definitions.example: A seagull hovered above the fishing boat, waiting for scraps of food.

definitions.meaning: （价格、数量等）波动，徘徊（在某一水平）
definitions.example: The inflation rate has hovered around 3% for the past six months.

hover.etymology: 源自中古英语「hoveren」（摇摆、盘旋），本义指“在空中来回摇摆”，引申为“数值波动”

similar_words.description: 近义易混，linger侧重‘因不愿离开而逗留’，hover侧重‘在空中盘旋或（数值）在某水平波动’

definitions.example: The plan sounds feasible; however, it may face practical difficulties in implementation.

definitions.meaning: 无论如何，不管怎样（引导让步状语）
definitions.example: However hard the task may be, we must complete it on time.

however.etymology: 由「how」（如何）+「ever」（无论）构成，表“无论何种情况”，引申为转折义

similar_words.description: 近义易混，二者都表转折，however可放句首、句中、句末，nevertheless更正式，常放句首

definitions.meaning: （狼、狗等）嚎叫；（人因愤怒、痛苦）怒吼、哀号
definitions.example: The wolf howled at the moon in the middle of the silent forest.
A howl of protest erupted from the crowd when the decision was announced.

howl.etymology: 源自拟声词，模仿动物或人高亢的嚎叫声音

similar_words.description: 近义易混，growl侧重‘低沉的怒吼/低吼’，howl侧重‘高亢的嚎叫/哀号’

definitions.meaning: 挤在一起，蜷缩（尤指因寒冷、恐惧）
definitions.example: The refugees huddled together in the shelter to keep warm during the cold night.
The managers huddled in the conference room to discuss the emergency plan.

huddle.etymology: 源自中古英语「huderen」（躲藏、蜷缩），引申为“多人挤在一起”

similar_words.description: 近义易混，crouch侧重‘单个身体的蹲伏、蜷缩’，huddle侧重‘多人挤在一起’

definitions.meaning: v. 拥抱；紧抱 n. 拥抱
definitions.example: In her graduation speech, she emphasized that a warm hug can ease the anxiety of fresh graduates facing career uncertainty.

hug.etymology: 源自古诺斯语hugga，原指「安慰」，后引申为「拥抱」

similar_words.description: 「hug」侧重亲密的拥抱动作，「embrace」更正式，可指抽象的「接纳」

definitions.example: The huge investment in renewable energy has accelerated the transition to a low-carbon economy.

huge.etymology: 源自拉丁语augere（增加），衍生为「体积或程度增大的」

similar_words.description: 「huge」侧重体积、数量或程度的庞大，「vast」侧重范围、面积的广阔

definitions.meaning: v. 哼（曲子）；发出嗡嗡声 n. 嗡嗡声；哼哼声
definitions.example: The library was quiet except for the hum of the air conditioner and a student humming a classical melody.
The startup hub hums with innovative ideas and collaborative efforts every weekday.

hum.etymology: 拟声词，源自蜜蜂等发出的低沉嗡嗡声

similar_words.description: 「hum」多指低沉的嗡嗡声或哼歌，「buzz」多指尖锐的嗡嗡声或嘈杂的声响

definitions.meaning: adj. 人的；人类的 n. 人；人类
definitions.example: Human activities have exerted a profound impact on the global climate system over the past century.

human.etymology: 源自拉丁语humanus（人的，人性的），与表示「人」的词根homo同根

similar_words.description: 「human」指「人类的」，「humane」指「人道的，仁慈的」，二者形近易混

definitions.example: The award-winning novel explores the complexity of humanity through the protagonist's moral dilemmas.
Top universities worldwide require undergraduates to take core courses in the humanities to foster critical thinking.

humanity.etymology: 源自拉丁语humanitas（人性，教养），由humanus（人的）派生而来

similar_words.description: 「humanity」作「人类」时侧重人性或人类整体，「humankind」仅指「人类（总称）」，语义更客观

definitions.meaning: 谦逊的；（身份、地位）低下的；简陋的
definitions.example: The humble Nobel Prize winner refused all commercial endorsements and continued his basic research in a small lab.
The unexpected failure in the market humbled the once arrogant tech entrepreneur and forced him to reevaluate his strategies.

humble.etymology: 源自拉丁语humilis（低矮的，谦逊的），与表示「泥土」的词根humus同根，原指「贴近地面的」，引申为「谦逊的」

similar_words.description: 「humble」作形容词指「谦逊的」，作动词指「使谦逊」；「humiliate」指「羞辱，使丢脸」，含贬义

definitions.example: The humid climate in subtropical regions provides a favorable habitat for a wide range of rare plants.

humid.etymology: 源自拉丁语humidus（潮湿的），与表示「泥土」的词根humus同根

similar_words.description: 「humid」侧重空气或气候的潮湿，「moist」侧重物体表面的微湿

definitions.example: Agricultural scientists monitor the humidity of greenhouses to ensure the optimal growth of experimental crop varieties.

humidity.etymology: 由形容词humid（潮湿的）加名词后缀-ity派生而来

similar_words.description: 「humidity」特指空气的湿度，「moisture」泛指任何物体中的水分或湿气

definitions.example: The unethical tabloid report humiliated the public figure by spreading fabricated rumors about his private life.

humiliate.etymology: 源自拉丁语humiliare（使低下，羞辱），与表示「低矮的」的词根humilis同根，原指「使降到地面」，引申为「羞辱」

similar_words.description: 「humiliate」指恶意地「羞辱，使丢脸」，含强烈贬义；「humble」作动词指「使谦逊」，多含中性或褒义

definitions.example: A sense of humor is widely regarded as a valuable social trait that can ease interpersonal tensions.
The patient tutor decided to humor the curious elementary student by answering all his unusual questions after class.

humor.etymology: 源自拉丁语umor（体液），古代医学认为体液决定人的情绪，后引申为「幽默，情绪」

similar_words.description: 「humor」为名词/动词，「humorous」为派生形容词，指「幽默的」

definitions.example: Professor Smith’s humorous lecture style makes complex sociological theories accessible to all undergraduate students.

humorous.etymology: 来自名词「humor（幽默）」+形容词后缀-ous，表“具有…性质的”

similar_words.description: 二者形近易混，humorous指人或事物诙谐有趣，humane指人道的、仁慈的

definitions.example: The international charity aims to provide sustainable food aid for regions suffering from extreme hunger and poverty.
Many independent documentary filmmakers hunger for public attention to the marginalized groups they depict.

hunger.etymology: 源自古英语hungor，原指生理上的饥饿，后引申出抽象的渴望义

similar_words.description: 二者均可指饥荒，hunger还可表抽象的渴望，famine仅指大规模粮食短缺导致的严重饥荒

definitions.example: Archaeologists have been hunting for more relics to prove the existence of the ancient civilization along the river.
The global hunt for effective treatment for the rare disease has made significant progress in recent years.

hunt.etymology: 源自古英语huntian，原指追逐猎物，后引申为广义的搜寻

similar_words.description: 二者均有追逐、寻找义，hunt可指打猎或系统性搜寻，chase侧重快速追赶以捕获或获得某物

definitions.meaning: 猛扔，猛投；大声说出（恶言等）
definitions.example: Protesters outside the parliament building began to hurl insults and hard objects at the security personnel.

hurl.etymology: 源自中古英语hurlen，原指猛推、猛撞，后引申为猛掷、激烈说出

similar_words.description: 二者均指用力扔，hurl力度更大、动作更粗暴，fling可指随意或气愤地扔出

definitions.example: The 2022 hurricane caused catastrophic damage to the coastal transportation and power infrastructure of Florida.

hurricane.etymology: 源自西班牙语huracán，原指加勒比海地区的强风暴，后泛指大西洋和东太平洋的热带飓风

similar_words.description: 二者均指强热带风暴，hurricane主要指大西洋及东太平洋的飓风，typhoon主要指西太平洋的台风

definitions.example: Long-term exposure to ultraviolet rays may hurt the skin and increase the risk of skin cancer.
The young researcher felt deeply hurt when her innovative paper was rejected without detailed feedback.

hurt.etymology: 源自古英语hyrtan，原指刺、戳，后引申为身体或情感上的伤害

similar_words.description: 二者均有伤害义，hurt可指身体或情感伤害，程度可轻可重；injure多指意外造成的身体伤害，语体更正式

definitions.example: The local villagers built temporary huts on the high ground to shelter themselves from the upcoming flood.

hut.etymology: 源自中古英语hutte，来自古法语hutte，原指供人临时居住的简陋小型建筑

similar_words.description: 二者均指小型住所，hut多为简陋的临时棚屋，cabin可指木制小木屋或船舱

definitions.example: Hydrogen energy is widely regarded as a promising clean alternative to traditional fossil fuels.

hydrogen.etymology: 来自词根「hydro-（水）」+词根「-gen（产生）」，因氢气燃烧可生成水而得名

similar_words.description: 二者均为化学元素名词，形近易混，hydrogen指氢，oxygen指氧

definitions.example: The public strongly criticized the CEO’s hypocrisy, as he called for salary cuts for employees but gave himself a huge bonus.

hypocrisy.etymology: 来自词根「hypo-（在…下，虚假）」+词根「cris-（判断、评论）」+名词后缀-y，原指假装评论，后引申为伪善

similar_words.description: 二者形近易混，hypocrisy是表品德的抽象名词，指虚伪伪善；hypothesis是表学术论证的名词，指假设假说

definitions.example: The research team conducted a three-year longitudinal study to test the hypothesis that social interaction can slow cognitive decline.

hypothesis.etymology: 来自词根「hypo-（在…下）」+词根「thes-（放置）」+名词后缀-is，原指置于论点之下的前提，后引申为假设

similar_words.description: 二者形近易混，hypothesis指科研或论证中的假设假说，hypocrisy指为人处事的虚伪伪善

definitions.meaning: 歇斯底里的；情绪极度激动失控的
definitions.example: When the unexpected results of the clinical trial were released, some family members of the subjects became hysterical and could not be pacified temporarily.

hysterical.etymology: 词根hyster-（源出希腊语，原指子宫，古代医学认为歇斯底里症状与子宫异常相关）+ 形容词后缀-ical，表「与…相关的」

similar_words.description: 二者均含「情绪表现强烈」之义，hysterical指情绪失控的歇斯底里，histrionic指做作、刻意戏剧化的情绪表现

definitions.meaning: 理想的；完美的；最合适的
definitions.example: The ideal experimental setting for this study requires a constant temperature of 25 degrees Celsius and no direct sunlight.
For generations of scientists, the ideal of pursuing objective truth has been the driving force of their research.

ideal.etymology: 词根ide-（源出希腊语idea，指「理念；观念」）+ 后缀-al，可作形容词或名词

similar_words.description: 二者拼写形近，idea为名词，指「想法；主意；观念」，ideal为形容词/名词，表「理想的；理想」

definitions.example: To ensure the fairness of the test, all candidates were provided with identical question papers and answer sheets.

identical.etymology: 词根ident-（源出拉丁语idem，指「相同的」）+ 形容词后缀-ical，表「具有…性质的」

similar_words.description: 二者为同根词，identical为形容词，指「完全相同的」，identity为名词，指「身份；同一性」

definitions.example: Biometric technology, such as fingerprint scanning, has greatly enhanced the accuracy of personal identification in border control.

identification.etymology: 动词identify（识别）+ 名词后缀-ation，表「动作；结果」

similar_words.description: 二者为同根词，identification指「识别的动作或结果」，identity指「身份本身；同一性」

definitions.meaning: 识别；认出；确认（身份或事物）
definitions.example: After analyzing the DNA samples, researchers were able to identify the specific species of the endangered plant.

identify.etymology: 词根ident-（相同）+ 动词后缀-ify（表「使…化」），使与已知事物相同→识别出

similar_words.description: 二者均含「认出」之义，identify强调准确识别、确认身份或事物的本质，recognize侧重认出熟悉的人或事物

definitions.meaning: 身份；同一性；（文化、民族的）特征
definitions.example: Many second-generation immigrants face the challenge of reconciling their dual cultural identity.

identity.etymology: 词根ident-（相同）+ 名词后缀-ity（表「性质；状态」）

similar_words.description: 二者为同根词，identity指「身份本身或同一性」，identification指「识别身份的动作或结果」

definitions.example: The study examines how mainstream political ideology shapes public opinion on environmental policies.

ideology.etymology: 词根ide-（理念；观念）+ 名词后缀-ology（表「学科；体系」）

similar_words.description: 二者均含「思想体系」之义，ideology指系统的、影响广泛的意识形态，doctrine指特定群体的教义、信条或原则

definitions.example: Mastering English idioms is crucial for accurately interpreting the implicit meaning of academic and literary texts.

idiom.etymology: 词根idi-（源出希腊语idios，指「自身的；特有的」）+ 名词后缀-om，表「具有特定性质的事物」

similar_words.description: 二者拼写形近，idiom为名词，指「习语；成语」，idiot为名词，指「白痴；傻子」

definitions.example: Using offensive terms like 'idiot' in academic discussions violates the principle of rational and respectful communication.

idiot.etymology: 词根idi-（原指「没有专业知识的普通人」）+ 名词后缀-ot（表「人」），后词义演变为「愚蠢的人」

similar_words.description: 二者拼写形近，idiot指「愚蠢的人」，idiom指「习语；成语」

definitions.example: During the off-season, many agricultural machines lie idle in the barn to avoid unnecessary wear and tear.
Instead of idling away the winter break, she enrolled in an online course to improve her academic writing skills.

idle.etymology: 源出古英语idel，原意为「空的；未被使用的」

similar_words.description: 二者拼写形近，idle为形容词/动词，指「空闲的；虚度」，idol为名词，指「偶像；崇拜对象」

definitions.example: In the history lecture, the professor explained how the unfair tax policy ignited widespread resentment among the working class.

ignite.etymology: 词根ign-（源自拉丁语ignis，意为「火」）+ 动词后缀-ite，表「使…着火、激起」

similar_words.description: 「ignite」为动词，表点燃、激起；「ignorant」为形容词，表无知的，拼写前四个字母相同易混淆

definitions.meaning: 无知，愚昧（尤指对某事的不知）
definitions.example: Many social conflicts arise from people's ignorance of cultural differences between regions.

ignorance.etymology: 词根ign-（源自拉丁语ignorare，意为「不知道」）+ 名词后缀-orance，表「无知的状态」

similar_words.description: 「ignorance」为名词，表无知；「ignorant」为形容词，表无知的，同根词词性不同易混淆

definitions.meaning: 无知的，愚昧的；不知道的
definitions.example: Young people should not be ignorant of the historical events that shaped their country.

ignorant.etymology: 词根ign-（源自拉丁语ignorare，意为「不知道」）+ 形容词后缀-ant，表「具有不知性质的」

similar_words.description: 「ignorant」为形容词，表无知的；「ignite」为动词，表点燃、激起，拼写前四个字母相同易混淆

definitions.example: Researchers should not ignore the potential risks of their experiments when pursuing academic breakthroughs.

ignore.etymology: 源自拉丁语ignorare，由否定前缀in-（后因接gn-开头词根缩略为ign-）+ 词根gnar-（意为「知道」，同「know」的原始印欧语根）构成，表「不知道、忽视」

similar_words.description: 「ignore」侧重主观故意不理睬，「neglect」侧重疏忽、未尽责，语义侧重不同易混淆

definitions.example: The court ruled that the company's practice of dumping industrial waste into the river was illegal.

illegal.etymology: 否定前缀il-（用于l开头的词前，表「不、非」）+ 形容词legal（合法的），表「不合法的」

similar_words.description: 「illegal」指违反法律的，「illicit」指违反社会规则或道德的，拼写相近且语义有交叉易混淆

definitions.example: In some remote rural areas, a small number of elderly people are still illiterate due to limited educational resources in the past.

illiterate.etymology: 否定前缀il-（用于l开头的词前，表「不、非」）+ 形容词literate（识字的，源自拉丁语littera「字母」），表「不识字的」

similar_words.description: 「illiterate」表不识字的/文盲，「literate」表识字的/有读写能力的人，同根反义易混淆

definitions.example: Long-term exposure to air pollution may increase the risk of developing chronic respiratory illnesses.

illness.etymology: 形容词ill（生病的）+ 名词后缀-ness，表「患病的状态」

similar_words.description: 「illness」侧重患病的状态或感受，「disease」侧重具体的疾病种类，语义侧重不同易混淆

definitions.meaning: 照亮，照明；阐明，解释（抽象事物）
definitions.example: The professor used vivid analogies to illuminate the complex theoretical framework of cognitive psychology.

illuminate.etymology: 加强语气前缀il-（in-的l前变体，表「使…」）+ 词根lumin-（源自拉丁语lumen，意为「光」）+ 动词后缀-ate，表「使发光、阐明」

similar_words.description: 「illuminate」侧重照亮或阐明抽象道理，「illustrate」侧重用例子或图表说明，拼写相近且语义有交叉易混淆

definitions.example: The mirage in the desert is a typical optical illusion caused by the refraction of light.

illusion.etymology: 源自拉丁语illusio，词根lus-（意为「玩、欺骗」，同「ludicrous」的词根）+ 名词后缀-ion，表「被欺骗的感官体验、错觉」

similar_words.description: 「illusion」侧重感官上的错觉或假象，「delusion」侧重精神上的妄想或错误信念，语义侧重不同易混淆

definitions.meaning: （用例子、图表等）说明，阐明；给…加插图
definitions.example: The textbook uses numerous case studies to illustrate the application of economic principles in real business scenarios.

illustrate.etymology: 加强语气前缀il-（in-的l前变体，表「使…」）+ 词根lustr-（源自拉丁语lustrum，意为「光、照亮」，同「luster」的词根）+ 动词后缀-ate，表「用光照亮、用例子说明」

similar_words.description: 「illustrate」侧重用具体例子或图表说明，「illuminate」侧重照亮或阐明抽象道理，拼写相近且语义有交叉易混淆

definitions.example: Researchers used case studies as an illustration of how social norms influence consumer behavior.

illustration.etymology: 由动词「illustrate」（说明、阐明）加名词后缀「-ion」构成，表行为的结果或手段

similar_words.description: 「illumination」指照明、启迪，「illustration」指例证、插图
「demonstration」侧重用行动/实验证明，「illustration」侧重用例子、图表说明

definitions.example: The company aims to build a positive public image through its environmental initiatives.

image.etymology: 源自拉丁语「imago」（肖像、模仿），为英语基础核心词

similar_words.description: 「picture」侧重具体的绘画、照片，「image」可指抽象的形象、印象，也可指数字图像
「image」是名词表形象/图像，「imagine」是动词表想象

definitions.example: The protagonist in the novel interacts with an imaginary friend to cope with loneliness.

imaginary.etymology: 由名词「imagination」（想象）去「-ion」加形容词后缀「-ary」（表与…相关的）构成

similar_words.description: 「imaginative」表富有想象力的（描述人/作品特质），「imaginary」表想象出来的、虚构的（描述事物性质）

definitions.example: Creative education is designed to stimulate children's imagination and critical thinking.

imagination.etymology: 由动词「imagine」（想象）加名词后缀「-ation」构成，表行为或能力

similar_words.description: 「fantasy」侧重脱离现实的空想、幻想，「imagination」侧重正常的、创造性的想象能力

definitions.meaning: 富有想象力的；有创造力的
definitions.example: The imaginative plot of the science fiction novel has attracted a large number of young readers.

imaginative.etymology: 由名词「imagination」（想象）去「-ion」加形容词后缀「-ative」（表有…性质的）构成

similar_words.description: 「creative」侧重有创造力的，适用范围更广；「imaginative」侧重运用想象力的、新奇的，多描述思维或作品

definitions.example: It is hard to imagine how ancient people built the magnificent pyramids without modern machinery.

imagine.etymology: 源自拉丁语「imaginari」（想象、构思），为英语基础核心动词

similar_words.description: 「suppose」侧重假设、假定（用于推理），「imagine」侧重在脑海中构建形象、场景

definitions.example: Many young artists begin their careers by imitating the works of famous masters.

imitate.etymology: 源自拉丁语「imitari」（模仿），为英语核心动词

similar_words.description: 「simulate」侧重用技术手段模拟，「imitate」侧重人模仿行为、风格

definitions.example: The painting was exposed as a cheap imitation of the original Van Gogh work.

imitation.etymology: 由动词「imitate」（模仿）加名词后缀「-ion」构成，表行为或结果

similar_words.description: 「simulation」指模拟过程/系统，「imitation」指模仿行为或仿制品

definitions.example: The new policy had an immediate impact on the price of daily necessities.

immediate.etymology: 由前缀「im-」（不、非）+ 拉丁语「medius」（中间的）+ 形容词后缀「-ate」构成，原指「没有中间环节的」，引申为立即的、直接的

similar_words.description: 「instant」侧重瞬间的、即时的（多描述时间极短），「immediate」侧重直接的、紧接着的（可指空间、关系或时间）

definitions.example: The research project required immense amounts of time and financial investment.

immense.etymology: 由前缀「im-」（不、非）+ 拉丁语「mensus」（测量的）构成，原指「无法测量的」，引申为巨大的

similar_words.description: 「enormous」侧重尺寸、数量大得反常，「immense」侧重规模、程度大得超乎想象

definitions.example: Many postgraduate candidates immerse themselves in extensive academic reading to prepare for the national entrance examination.

immerse.etymology: 词根词缀：im-（进入）+ mers（浸、沉）+ -e（动词后缀），原指「浸入液体」，后引申为抽象的沉浸

similar_words.description: immerse多指抽象意义上的「沉浸于学习、思考等」，submerge多指具体的「淹没、浸没物体于液体中」

definitions.example: The urban integration of skilled immigrants has become a key issue in recent social science research.

immigrant.etymology: 词根词缀：im-（进入）+ migr（迁移）+ -ant（名词后缀，表人），指「从他国迁入的人」

similar_words.description: immigrant指「移入某国的移民」，emigrant指「移出本国的移民」，前缀im-表进入，em-表外出

definitions.example: Regular moderate exercise helps the human body stay immune to common infectious diseases.
Senior researchers are not immune to the pressure of publishing papers in top journals.

immune.etymology: 词根词缀：im-（不）+ mun（公共服务、义务）+ -e（形容词后缀），原指「不用承担公共义务的（即豁免的）」，后引申为「免疫的、不受影响的」

similar_words.description: immune指「免疫的、不受影响的」，immure指「监禁、禁闭」，拼写接近易混淆

definitions.example: The rapid popularization of digital learning tools has exerted a far-reaching impact on traditional educational models.
Severe air pollution may negatively impact students' learning efficiency in campus environments.

impact.etymology: 词根词缀：im-（进入）+ pact（压紧、撞击），原指「猛烈撞击」，后引申为「影响」

similar_words.description: impact强调「重大、深远的影响」，effect强调「结果、效果」，后者常作名词表「影响」时需搭配on

definitions.example: Prolonged sleep deprivation can significantly impair cognitive function and academic performance.

impair.etymology: 词根词缀：im-（使）+ pair（更坏），原指「使情况变糟」，引申为「损害、削弱」

similar_words.description: impair指「损害、削弱」，repair指「修理、修复」，拼写仅前缀不同易混淆

definitions.meaning: 传授（知识、技能），赋予（特性）
definitions.example: Outstanding mentors always strive to impart not only professional knowledge but also ethical values to their students.

impart.etymology: 词根词缀：im-（进入）+ part（部分），原指「将自己的部分（知识、特性等）给予他人」，引申为「传授、赋予」

similar_words.description: impart指「传授、赋予」，impact指「影响、冲击」，拼写接近易混淆词性和词义

definitions.example: The peer review process for academic papers requires all reviewers to remain strictly impartial.

impartial.etymology: 词根词缀：im-（不）+ partial（偏袒的），直接表示「不偏袒的、公正的」

similar_words.description: impartial指「公正的、不偏不倚的」，partial指「偏袒的、部分的」，为直接反义词

definitions.example: Some candidates grow increasingly impatient as the release date of postgraduate entrance exam results approaches.

impatient.etymology: 词根词缀：im-（不）+ patient（耐心的），直接表示「不耐烦的」

similar_words.description: impatient指「不耐烦的、急躁的」，patient指「耐心的」，为直接反义词

definitions.example: It is imperative for educational institutions to adapt their curricula to the changing demands of the digital age.
Protecting endangered species is an imperative that requires global cooperation.

imperative.etymology: 词根词缀：imper（命令、统治）+ -ative（形容词后缀），原指「命令的」，后引申为「必要的、迫切的」

similar_words.description: imperative指「必要的、迫切的」，imperial指「帝国的、皇家的」，拼写接近易混淆

definitions.example: The imperial examination system in ancient China had a profound influence on the selection of bureaucratic talents.

imperial.etymology: 词根词缀：imper（命令、统治）+ -ial（形容词后缀），原指「统治的」，引申为「帝国的、皇家的」

similar_words.description: imperial指「帝国的、皇家的」，imperative指「必要的、迫切的」，拼写接近易混淆

definitions.example: Technological innovation provides a crucial impetus for economic transformation in both developed and emerging economies.

impetus.etymology: im-（向内）+pet（寻求、冲）+us（名词后缀）→向内冲的力量→动力

similar_words.description: 「impetus」指推动事物发展的持续动力；「impulse」指突然的冲动或物理脉冲

definitions.example: Governments at all levels must implement targeted measures to narrow the urban-rural income gap.
Basic agricultural implements such as plows and sickles are essential for small-scale farming in rural areas.

implement.etymology: im-（使）+ple（填满、完成）+ment（后缀）→使完成某事→实施；后引申为完成某事的工具

similar_words.description: 「implement」指实施计划或工具；「complement」指补充、补足或补语

definitions.example: The latest research findings have far-reaching implications for the treatment of neurodegenerative diseases.

implication.etymology: im-（向内）+plic（折叠）+ation（名词后缀）→折叠在内未明说的内容→含义；引申为潜在影响

similar_words.description: 「implication」指隐含的含义或影响；「application」指申请或应用

definitions.example: There is an implicit agreement among team members that overtime work will be compensated with extra leave.
Military personnel are required to show implicit obedience to their commanding officers’ orders.

implicit.etymology: im-（向内）+plic（折叠）+it（形容词后缀）→折叠在内未表露的→含蓄的；引申为毫无保留的（绝对的）

similar_words.description: 「implicit」指含蓄的、未明说的；「explicit」指明确的、直截了当的

definitions.example: The author’s critical tone implies that he disagrees with the mainstream interpretation of historical events.

imply.etymology: im-（向内）+ply（折叠）→折叠在内未直接说出→暗示

similar_words.description: 「imply」指说话者/作者主动暗示；「infer」指听者/读者主动推断

definitions.example: The European Union has imposed temporary tariffs on imports of steel products to protect domestic manufacturers.
The judge emphasized the import of protecting citizens’ right to privacy in the digital age.

import.etymology: im-（进入）+port（搬运）→搬运进入国境→进口；引申为具有重要价值的事物→重要性

similar_words.description: 「import」指进口（商品）或重要性；「export」指出口（商品）

definitions.example: Promoting mental health among adolescents has gained unprecedented importance in global education policies.

importance.etymology: import（重要性）+ance（名词后缀）→重要性

similar_words.description: 「importance」侧重事物本身的重要程度；「significance」侧重事物的深层意义或历史影响

definitions.example: The local government has imposed strict restrictions on the use of single-use plastics to reduce environmental pollution.
Tax authorities will impose a heavy fine on enterprises that evade taxes intentionally.

impose.etymology: im-（在…之上）+pose（放置）→放置在他人之上→强加；引申为征收（税款等）

similar_words.description: 「impose」指强加或征收；「expose」指暴露或揭露

definitions.example: Without joint efforts of all countries, it is impossible to achieve the United Nations’ sustainable development goals by 2030.

impossible.etymology: im-（否定前缀，不）+possible（可能的）→不可能的

similar_words.description: 「impossible」指完全没有可能性；「improbable」指可能性极小但仍有可能

definitions.example: Her profound academic attainments and rigorous research attitude impressed the evaluation committee greatly.
Parents should impress the virtue of diligence on their children from a very early age.

impress.etymology: im-（在…之上）+press（压）→压在心上→留下印象；引申为使铭记

similar_words.description: 「impress」指给人留下印象；「express」指表达或快递

definitions.example: The speaker’s logical argument left a deep impression on the audience of the academic forum.

impression.etymology: 由前缀「im-（向内）」、词根「press（压）」和名词后缀「-ion」构成，原指向内压出的痕迹，后引申为印象、感想

similar_words.description: 「impress」为动词，意为给…留下深刻印象；「impression」为其名词形式，表印象、感想

definitions.meaning: 给人印象深刻的；令人钦佩的
definitions.example: The impressive data from the clinical trial has attracted the attention of top medical researchers.

impressive.etymology: 由前缀「im-（向内）」、词根「press（压）」和形容词后缀「-ive」构成，指能压入脑海、给人留下深刻印象的

similar_words.description: 「expressive」意为有表现力的，侧重表达能力；「impressive」意为给人印象深刻的，侧重他人的感受

definitions.example: Governments should take effective measures to improve the living conditions of low-income families.

improve.etymology: 由前缀「im-（=en-，使）」和词根「prove（证明，原指使更有价值）」构成，意为使变得更好

similar_words.description: 「prove」为动词，意为证明、证实；「improve」为其衍生词，意为改善、提高

definitions.example: The improvement in air quality in the city is closely related to the implementation of environmental regulations.

improvement.etymology: 由动词「improve（改善）」和名词后缀「-ment」构成，指改善的行为或结果

similar_words.description: 「betterment」为较正式用词，侧重福利等方面的改善；「improvement」为通用用词，表各类改善、改进

definitions.example: Resisting the impulse to make hasty decisions is crucial for successful career planning.
The device can detect weak electrical impulses in the human nervous system.

impulse.etymology: 由前缀「im-（向内）」、词根「puls（推、驱动）」和后缀「-e」构成，原指向内驱动的力量，后引申为冲动、突发念头，物理领域指脉冲

similar_words.description: 「pulse」可作名词/动词，表脉搏、搏动；「impulse」侧重冲动、突发念头，物理义表脉冲

definitions.example: The remote mountain village was inaccessible by road due to the heavy snowstorm.
The specialized academic paper was inaccessible to undergraduate students without prior background knowledge.

inaccessible.etymology: 由否定前缀「in-（不）」和形容词「accessible（可到达的）」构成，意为不可到达的、难懂的

similar_words.description: 「accessible」意为可到达的、易懂的；「inaccessible」为其否定形式，表难到达的、难懂的

definitions.example: Tax cuts are often used as an incentive to encourage enterprises to invest in green technologies.

incentive.etymology: 由前缀「in-（向内）」、词根「cent（唱、吟唱，原指用咒语激励）」和形容词后缀「-ive」转化为名词，指激励、刺激因素

similar_words.description: 「motive」侧重行为的动机、目的；「incentive」侧重促进行为的激励、刺激因素

definitions.example: The gap between the two pipes is only a few inches wide.
The car inched forward through the crowded traffic jam.

inch.etymology: 源自古英语「ynce」，原指拇指的宽度（古代长度单位），后引申为现代长度单位“英寸”，动词义由“一英寸一英寸缓慢移动”引申而来

similar_words.description: 「itch」作名词/动词，表痒、渴望；「inch」作名词表英寸，作动词表缓慢移动

definitions.meaning: （事件的）发生率；（疾病的）发病率
definitions.example: The incidence of diabetes has been rising steadily among young adults in recent years.
The angle of incidence of the light beam affects the reflection effect.

incidence.etymology: 由前缀「in-（向内）」、词根「cid（落下）」和名词后缀「-ence」构成，原指“落下的状态”，后引申为发生率、发病率，物理领域指入射

similar_words.description: 「incident」为名词，表事件、事变；「incidence」为名词，表发生率、发病率

definitions.meaning: 事件；事变（尤指不严重的或政治上的）
definitions.example: A minor incident between the two countries triggered a diplomatic dispute.
The book includes some incident details that are not essential to the main plot.

incident.etymology: 由前缀「in-（向内）」、词根「cid（落下）」和形容词/名词后缀「-ent」构成，原指“落下的事情”，后引申为事件、事变，形容词义指附带的、次要的

similar_words.description: 「incidence」为名词，表发生率、发病率；「incident」为名词，表事件、事变

definitions.example: Incidentally, the study also addresses the long-debated issue of social mobility in rural areas.

incidentally.etymology: 由前缀「in-」（加强语气）+ 词根「cid」（意为‘落下、发生’）+ 形容词后缀「-ent」+ 副词后缀「-ly」构成，原指‘偶然发生地’，后引申为‘顺便提及’

similar_words.description: 均为副词，「incidentally」表‘顺便’，用于引出附加信息；「accidentally」表‘意外地’，指非故意发生

definitions.example: Many young scholars incline to adopt interdisciplinary approaches in their research.
The hiking path follows a gentle incline to the observation tower.

incline.etymology: 由前缀「in-」（向内）+ 词根「clin」（意为‘倾斜’）构成，原指‘向内倾斜’，后引申为‘倾向’

similar_words.description: 均含词根「clin」，「incline」表‘倾向、倾斜’；「decline」表‘下降、拒绝’

definitions.example: The research sample includes 500 participants from diverse socioeconomic and ethnic backgrounds.

include.etymology: 由前缀「in-」（向内）+ 词根「clud」（意为‘关闭’）构成，原指‘关在内部’，引申为‘包含’

similar_words.description: 均含词根「clud」，「include」表‘包含’；「exclude」表‘排除’

definitions.meaning: 包含的；包括一切的（常接of）
definitions.example: The conference registration fee is inclusive of accommodation and all workshop materials.

inclusive.etymology: 由前缀「in-」（向内）+ 词根「clus」（意为‘关闭’）+ 形容词后缀「-ive」构成，表‘关在内部的’，即‘包含的’

similar_words.description: 均含词根「clus」，「inclusive」表‘包容的、包含的’；「exclusive」表‘排他的、专属的’

definitions.example: Household disposable income is a key indicator of regional economic well-being.

income.etymology: 由前缀「in-」（进入）+ 单词「come」构成，原指‘流入的财物’，即‘收入’

similar_words.description: 均为复合名词，「income」表‘收入’；「outcome」表‘结果’

definitions.example: The new curriculum incorporates cutting-edge research findings from multiple disciplines.

incorporate.etymology: 由前缀「in-」（进入）+ 词根「corpor」（意为‘身体、团体’）+ 动词后缀「-ate」构成，原指‘加入团体’，引申为‘合并、并入’

similar_words.description: 均含词根「corpor」，「incorporate」表‘合并、包含’；「cooperate」表‘合作’

definitions.example: Global carbon emissions continue to increase despite international efforts to curb climate change.
The report predicts a 12% increase in renewable energy investment by 2030.

increase.etymology: 由前缀「in-」（加强语气）+ 词根「creas」（意为‘增长’）构成

similar_words.description: 均为兼类词，「increase」表‘增加’；「decrease」表‘减少’

definitions.example: Digital literacy is becoming increasingly essential for individuals in the modern workforce.

increasingly.etymology: 由单词「increase」（增加）+ 形容词后缀「-ing」+ 副词后缀「-ly」构成

similar_words.description: 均为副词，「increasingly」表‘越来越多地’；「decreasingly」表‘越来越少地’

definitions.example: The research team made incredible progress in developing a new vaccine for infectious diseases.

incredible.etymology: 由前缀「in-」（表否定）+ 词根「cred」（意为‘相信’）+ 形容词后缀「-ible」构成，原指‘不能相信的’

similar_words.description: 均含词根「cred」，「incredible」表‘（事物）难以置信的’；「incredulous」表‘（人）怀疑的’

definitions.meaning: 招致；遭受（尤指损失、批评等不好的事物）
definitions.example: The firm may incur heavy regulatory fines if it fails to comply with environmental protection laws.

incur.etymology: 由前缀「in-」（进入）+ 词根「cur」（意为‘跑’）构成，原指‘跑进来（带来麻烦）’，引申为‘招致’

similar_words.description: 均含词根「cur」，「incur」表‘招致（不好的事）’；「occur」表‘发生’

definitions.meaning: 确实，的确（用于强调或确认事实）
definitions.example: Indeed, a 2022 study in 「Cell」 found that urban green spaces reduce mental stress significantly.

indeed.etymology: in-（加强语气）+ deed（事实、行为），原指「基于事实地」，引申为强调确认的语气

similar_words.description: 形近词，indeed表肯定强调，indecent表「下流的、不雅的」，含贬义

definitions.meaning: 独立，自主（指政治、经济或个人层面）
definitions.example: The region fought for economic independence to reduce its reliance on foreign imports.

independence.etymology: in-（否定，不）+ depend（依靠）+ -ence（名词后缀，表状态），指「不依靠他人/外物的状态」

similar_words.description: 同根反义，independence表「独立」，dependence表「依赖、依靠」

definitions.meaning: 独立的，自主的；无关联的
definitions.example: The university established an independent committee to investigate academic misconduct.

independent.etymology: in-（否定，不）+ depend（依靠）+ -ent（形容词后缀，表属性），指「不依靠他人/外物的」

similar_words.description: 同根反义，independent表「独立的」，dependent表「依赖的、依靠的」

definitions.meaning: （价格、比率等的）指数；（书籍的）索引
definitions.example: The Consumer Price Index (CPI) is a key indicator of inflation in most countries.
The online library indexes all peer-reviewed journals published after 2000.

index.etymology: 源自拉丁语index，原指「指出者、标志」，由in-（进入）+ dex（显示、指出）构成，引申为指数、索引

similar_words.description: 形近词，index表「指数、索引」，indict表法律语境下的「控告、起诉」

definitions.example: The latest census data indicate that the rural population is declining rapidly.

indicate.etymology: in-（加强语气）+ dic（说、指出）+ -ate（动词后缀），指「明确指出、表明」

similar_words.description: 同根词（词根dic=说），indicate表「表明」，dictate表「口述、命令」

definitions.example: There is no clear indication that the new tax policy will benefit low-income families.

indication.etymology: in-（加强语气）+ dic（说、指出）+ -ation（名词后缀，表行为/结果），指「表明的行为或结果」

similar_words.description: 同根词（词根dic=说），indication表「迹象」，dictation表「听写、口述」

definitions.meaning: 表明的，指示的（常与of搭配）
definitions.example: A persistent cough is often indicative of respiratory problems.
In English grammar, the indicative mood is used for factual statements.

indicative.etymology: in-（加强语气）+ dic（说、指出）+ -ative（形容词后缀，表属性），指「具有表明性质的」

similar_words.description: 同根词，indicative表「表明的」，indicant作名词表「指示物、标志」

definitions.example: Many teenagers are indifferent to traditional festivals due to the influence of social media.
His indifferent performance in the final exam failed to meet his parents' expectations.

indifferent.etymology: in-（否定，不）+ different（不同的），原指「无差别的、不偏不倚的」，引申为「漠不关心的、平庸的」

similar_words.description: 形近词，indifferent表「漠不关心的、平庸的」，different表「不同的、有差异的」

definitions.meaning: 愤怒的，愤慨的（因不公或冒犯）
definitions.example: The local residents were indignant at the factory's illegal discharge of sewage.

indignant.etymology: in-（加强语气）+ dign（尊严、尊贵）+ -ant（形容词后缀），指「因尊严受损而愤怒的」

similar_words.description: 形近词，indignant表「愤怒的」，indigenous表「本土的、土著的」

definitions.meaning: 愤怒，愤慨（因不公或冒犯）
definitions.example: The politician's corruption scandal sparked widespread public indignation.

indignation.etymology: in-（加强语气）+ dign（尊严、尊贵）+ -ation（名词后缀，表状态），指「因尊严受损产生的愤怒状态」

similar_words.description: 形近词，indignation表「愤怒」，indigence表「贫困、贫穷」

definitions.meaning: 必不可少的；不可或缺的
definitions.example: Effective communication is indispensable for the smooth operation of any interdisciplinary research team.

indispensable.etymology: in-（表否定）+ dispensable（可省去的），表示无法被省去的，即不可或缺的

similar_words.description: dispensable为同根词，意为「可有可无的」，与indispensable语义相反

definitions.example: The postgraduate program encourages students to develop their individual research interests.
The policy aims to protect the legitimate rights of each individual in the digital age.

individual.etymology: in-（表否定）+ dividual（可分割的），原指不可分割的独立个体，后引申为个人的、个体的

similar_words.description: indivisible为形近词，意为「不可分割的」，侧重事物的整体性，而individual侧重个体属性

definitions.example: The university has built a new indoor stadium for students to exercise in bad weather.

indoor.etymology: in-（在…内）+ door（门），指在房门之内的，即室内的、户内的

similar_words.description: outdoor为反义形近词，意为「户外的」，与indoor语义完全相反

definitions.example: Excessive exposure to blue light from electronic devices can induce sleep disorders.

induce.etymology: in-（进入）+ duce（引导），指引导进入某种状态，引申为诱导、导致

similar_words.description: deduce为同根形近词，意为「推断；演绎」，侧重逻辑推理，而induce侧重引导或引发结果

definitions.example: It is unwise for postgraduates to indulge in online entertainment at the expense of research progress.

indulge.etymology: in-（使…）+ dulge（顺从），指使顺从自身欲望，引申为沉溺、纵容

similar_words.description: induce为形近词，意为「诱导；导致」，侧重因果关系，而indulge侧重放任自身或他人欲望

definitions.example: The government has introduced a series of policies to promote industrial upgrading and low-carbon development.

industrial.etymology: industry（工业）+ -al（形容词后缀），表示与工业相关的，即工业的、产业的

similar_words.description: industrious为形近词，意为「勤奋的」，描述人的品质，而industrial描述与工业相关的事物

definitions.example: Many developing countries are striving to industrialize their economies to reduce poverty and unemployment.

industrialize.etymology: industrial（工业的）+ -ize（动词后缀，表“使…化”），即使…工业化

similar_words.description: industrial为同根形容词，意为「工业的」，是industrialize的形容词形式

definitions.example: The new energy vehicle industry has become a new growth point of the national economy.
His outstanding academic achievements are largely attributed to his industry and meticulous attitude.

industry.etymology: 源自拉丁语industria，原指“勤奋”，后因工业发展需要大量勤劳劳作，引申出“工业；产业”的含义

similar_words.description: industrious为同根形容词，意为「勤奋的」，对应industry的僻义「勤奋」，而industry的常用义为「工业」

definitions.example: The inertia of the traditional academic evaluation system hinders the cultivation of innovative talents.

inertia.etymology: in-（表否定）+ ert（=erg，工作）+ -ia（名词后缀），指不愿工作的状态，引申为惯性、惰性

similar_words.description: inherent为形近词，意为「固有的；内在的」，侧重事物的本质属性，而inertia侧重不活跃、不愿改变的状态

definitions.meaning: 不可避免的；必然发生的
definitions.example: With the rapid popularization of digital technology, the transformation of traditional media is inevitable.

inevitable.etymology: in-（表否定）+ evitable（可避免的），表示无法被避免的，即不可避免的、必然的

similar_words.description: unavoidable为近义词，意为「不可避免的」，语义与inevitable相近，但inevitable更侧重客观必然性

definitions.example: Renewable energy technology was still in its infancy when the government launched the subsidy program.

infancy.etymology: in-（表否定）+ fant（=speak，说话）+ -cy（名词后缀，表状态）；原指“不会说话的状态（婴儿期）”，后引申为“事物发展的初期”

similar_words.description: 「infantry」为名词，意为步兵，与「infancy」形近但语义完全无关

definitions.example: The study examines the impact of parental interaction on infant language acquisition.
The infant company struggled to secure funding in its first year.

infant.etymology: in-（表否定）+ fant（=speak，说话）；原指“不会说话的人（婴儿）”，后引申为形容词“初期的”

similar_words.description: 「infancy」为同根名词，表婴儿期或（事物发展的）初期，与「infant」语义相关但词性、所指不同

definitions.meaning: 传染，感染；（情感、思想等）感染，影响
definitions.example: The novel coronavirus can infect humans through close contact with infected individuals.

infect.etymology: in-（进入）+ fect（=do/make，做）；指“病毒等进入体内并造成损害”，后引申为“情感等感染他人”

similar_words.description: 「effect」多作名词表影响，作动词表实现，与「infect」（传染、感染）语义不同

definitions.example: Wearing masks is an effective measure to prevent the spread of viral infection.

infection.etymology: infect（v.感染）+ -ion（名词后缀，表动作/结果）

similar_words.description: 「inflection」为语言学名词，表词形屈折变化，与「infection」（感染）语义无关

definitions.meaning: 传染的，传染性的；有感染力的
definitions.example: The speaker’s infectious enthusiasm inspired all the audience at the academic conference.

infectious.etymology: infect（v.感染）+ -ious（形容词后缀，表“有…性质的”）

similar_words.description: 「contagious」也表传染性的，多指接触传染；「infectious」可指多种传播途径，还可表情感有感染力

definitions.meaning: 推断，推论（考研阅读推理题核心考点）
definitions.example: From the author’s tone in the passage, we can infer that he disapproves of the new policy.

infer.etymology: in-（进入）+ fer（=bring/carry，带来）；指“将已知信息带入思考，得出结论”

similar_words.description: 「imply」指说话者/作者主动暗示，「infer」指听者/读者被动推断，动作发出者不同

definitions.example: The scientist’s inference about climate change was validated by decades of observational data.

inference.etymology: infer（v.推断）+ -ence（名词后缀，表动作/结果）

similar_words.description: 「reference」为名词表参考、引文，与「inference」（推断）语义不同

definitions.meaning: 较差的，次的；地位较低的
definitions.example: Many consumers believe that domestic products are not inferior to imported ones in quality.
The manager treated his inferiors with respect and fairness.

inferior.etymology: infer（=lower，下方）+ -ior（形容词比较级后缀，表“更…的”）；原指“位置更低的”，后引申为“质量/地位更低的”，作名词指“地位更低的人”

similar_words.description: 「superior」为反义/形近词，表更好的、上级的，与「inferior」语义相反

definitions.example: The concept of infinite time has long puzzled philosophers and physicists alike.
The poet compared human ambition to the infinite of the universe.

infinite.etymology: in-（表否定）+ finite（有限的，来自fin=end，尽头）；指“没有尽头的”

similar_words.description: 「definite」为形容词表确定的、明确的，与「infinite」（无限的）语义相反且形近

definitions.meaning: 通货膨胀（考研经济类高频考点）；充气
definitions.example: The central bank adjusted monetary policy to control the rising inflation rate.

inflation.etymology: in-（进入）+ flat（=blow，吹）+ -ion（名词后缀，表动作/结果）；原指“充气”，后引申为经济学术语“通货膨胀（货币量膨胀）”

similar_words.description: 「deflation」为经济学术语，表通货紧缩，与「inflation」（通货膨胀）语义相反

definitions.example: Social media exerts a profound influence on young people's perception of social norms.
Environmental factors greatly influence the growth of plants in this region.

influence.etymology: 来自拉丁语「influere」，由前缀「in-」（进入）和词根「flu」（流动）构成，原指星体流出的气息对人的影响，后引申为“影响”

similar_words.description: 「influence」可作名词或动词，指间接、潜移默化的影响；「affect」多作动词，指直接、短暂的影响
「influence」侧重影响的过程或持续作用；「effect」作名词指“效果、结果”，作动词指“实现、产生”

definitions.meaning: 有影响力的；有支配力的
definitions.example: His influential paper on climate change has been cited by numerous scholars worldwide.

influential.etymology: 由名词「influence」（影响）加形容词后缀「-ial」构成，表“具有…性质的”

similar_words.description: 「influential」侧重因地位、观点带来的长期影响力；「authoritative」侧重因权威、专业带来的可信度

definitions.example: The university will inform all applicants of the admission results via official email.
Archaeological findings inform our understanding of prehistoric human social structures.

inform.etymology: 由前缀「in-」（进入）和词根「form」（形成、塑造）构成，原指“使形成认知”，后引申为“通知”

similar_words.description: 「inform」侧重正式告知信息，对象广泛；「notify」侧重官方或紧急通知，强调时效性

definitions.example: Infrared cameras can detect heat signatures even in complete darkness.

infrared.etymology: 由前缀「infra-」（在…之下）和单词「red」（红）构成，指波长比红光更长的不可见光

similar_words.description: 「infrared」指红外线（波长大于红光）；「ultraviolet」指紫外线（波长小于紫光）

definitions.example: The government plans to allocate more funds to improve the country's transportation infrastructure.

infrastructure.etymology: 由前缀「infra-」（在…之下）和单词「structure」（结构）构成，指支撑社会运行的底层基础结构

similar_words.description: 「infrastructure」指大型公共基础设施（如交通、能源）；「facility」指具体的场所或设施（如图书馆、实验室）

definitions.example: The researcher designed an ingenious experiment to test the new hypothesis.

ingenious.etymology: 由前缀「in-」（内在）、词根「gen」（产生、天赋）和形容词后缀「-ious」构成，指“有内在天赋的”，后引申为“巧妙的”

similar_words.description: 「ingenious」指（人）机灵的、（物）设计巧妙的；「ingenuous」指天真的、坦率的，二者为考研常考形近易混词

definitions.example: Creativity and persistence are essential ingredients for academic breakthroughs.

ingredient.etymology: 由前缀「in-」（进入）、词根「gred」（走、步入）和名词后缀「-ient」构成，指“步入混合物的东西”，即成分

similar_words.description: 「ingredient」侧重混合物的成分（如食品）或抽象要素；「component」侧重机器、系统的具体组成部分

definitions.example: A variety of rare bird species inhabit the wetlands along the Yangtze River.

inhabit.etymology: 由前缀「in-」（在…内）和词根「habit」（居住）构成，指“在内部长期居住”

similar_words.description: 「inhabit」可指人或动物长期居住/栖息；「dwell」为正式用语，仅指人居住，常与介词搭配

definitions.example: The inhabitants of this mountain village have preserved their traditional customs for centuries.

inhabitant.etymology: 由动词「inhabit」（居住）加名词后缀「-ant」（表人/物）构成，指居住的人或动物

similar_words.description: 「inhabitant」侧重某地区的长期居住者（可指人或动物）；「resident」侧重某场所的常住居民（如小区、酒店）

definitions.example: It is extremely harmful to inhale second-hand smoke for a long period.

inhale.etymology: 由前缀「in-」（进入）和词根「hale」（呼吸）构成，指“将气体吸入体内”

similar_words.description: 「inhale」指将气体吸入体内；「exhale」指将气体呼出体外

definitions.example: The desire for self-preservation is an inherent trait of all living organisms.

inherent.etymology: in-（在内）+ her（粘着、持有）+ -ent（形容词后缀），指内在持有的特质

similar_words.description: inherent 是形容词表固有的，inherit 是动词表继承（财产、特质等）

definitions.example: Many genetic diseases can be inherited from one's parents.

inherit.etymology: in-（向内）+ her（持有、继承）+ -it（动词后缀），指从内部承接而来

similar_words.description: inherit 是动词表继承，inherent 是形容词表固有的

definitions.example: Excessive anxiety can inhibit a student's ability to perform well in exams.

inhibit.etymology: in-（否定，使不）+ hibit（=hold，持、控），指使无法自由行动或发展

similar_words.description: inhibit 表抑制、阻碍（侧重限制发展或行动），prohibit 表正式禁止（侧重法律或规则约束）

definitions.example: The initial results of the study indicate a potential link between diet and mental health.

initial.etymology: in-（进入）+ it（=go，走）+ -ial（形容词后缀），指走入开头阶段的

similar_words.description: initial 是形容词表最初的，initiate 是动词表发起、开始

definitions.example: The university initiated a new program to support low-income students last year.

initiate.etymology: in-（进入）+ it（=go，走）+ -iate（动词后缀），指走入并启动某一进程

similar_words.description: initiate 是动词表发起，initial 是形容词表最初的

definitions.example: Taking the initiative to solve problems is highly valued in the workplace.

initiative.etymology: in-（进入）+ it（=go，走）+ -ative（名词后缀，表行为或性质），指主动发起的行动或方案

similar_words.description: initiative 是名词表倡议/主动性，initiate 是动词表发起

definitions.example: The government injected $500 million into the infrastructure sector to boost economic growth.

inject.etymology: in-（进入）+ ject（=throw，投掷、抛），指将某物投入内部

similar_words.description: inject 表注入，eject 表喷出、驱逐

definitions.example: The reckless driving incident injured three pedestrians and damaged two vehicles.

injure.etymology: in-（否定，使受损）+ jur（=hurt，伤害）+ -e，指使受到损害

similar_words.description: injure 是动词表伤害，injury 是名词表伤害、损害

definitions.example: Long-term exposure to loud noise can cause permanent injury to the auditory system.

injury.etymology: in-（否定，使受损）+ jur（=hurt，伤害）+ -y（名词后缀），指受到损害的状态或结果

similar_words.description: injury 是名词表伤害，injure 是动词表伤害

definitions.example: The official document must be signed in black ink to be valid.
The two countries inked a bilateral trade agreement during the summit.

ink.etymology: 来自古英语inc，原指书写用的墨水，后引申为签署正式文件的动作

similar_words.description: ink 指墨水或签署，link 指链接、联系

definitions.example: Many inland provinces have developed ecological tourism to boost local economic growth.
The explorers traveled inland to study the local flora and fauna.

inland.etymology: 前缀in-（向内）+ 词根land（土地），指向内的陆地区域

similar_words.description: 前者指内陆地区，后者指四面环水的岛屿

definitions.example: The narrow inlet serves as a critical habitat for various endangered marine species.

inlet.etymology: 前缀in-（向内）+ 词根let（古英语表「通道」），指向内的通道或水湾

similar_words.description: 前者指入口、水湾，后者指出口、经销店

definitions.example: The historic inn by the ancient canal has accommodated numerous travelers over centuries.

inn.etymology: 源自古英语「inn」，原指供旅行者暂住的小型食宿场所

similar_words.description: 前者指小型旅馆、客栈，后者指墨水

definitions.example: The study focuses on the inner mechanism of human emotional regulation.

inner.etymology: 前缀in-（向内）+ 形容词后缀-er（表比较级），指更靠内部的

similar_words.description: 前者为形容词「内部的、内心的」，后者为名词「小旅馆」

definitions.example: The defendant insisted that he was innocent and had no connection with the corporate fraud.
The innocent curiosity of preschool children has inspired many educational innovations.

innocent.etymology: 前缀in-（否定，表「不」）+ 词根noc（伤害，源自拉丁语nocere）+ 形容词后缀-ent，指不会造成伤害的

similar_words.description: 前者指无辜的、天真的，后者指数不清的

definitions.example: To maintain global competitiveness, high-tech enterprises must continuously innovate their core technologies.

innovate.etymology: 前缀in-（进入）+ 词根nov（新，源自拉丁语novus）+ 动词后缀-ate，指进入新的状态

similar_words.description: 前者指技术、理念的革新创新，后者指建筑物等的翻新修复

definitions.example: Digital innovation has fundamentally transformed the global economic and social landscape.

innovation.etymology: 前缀in-（进入）+ 词根nov（新）+ 名词后缀-ation，是innovate的名词形式，指创新的行为或成果

similar_words.description: 前者指理念、技术的创新成果，后者指建筑等的翻新工程

definitions.example: Archaeologists have uncovered innumerable relics reflecting the prosperity of the ancient Silk Road.

innumerable.etymology: 前缀in-（否定，表「不」）+ 词根numer（数，源自拉丁语numerus）+ 形容词后缀-able，指无法数清的

similar_words.description: 前者强调数量极多以至数不清，后者仅强调数量多（可计数）

definitions.meaning: 输入（的信息、数据）；投入（的资源）
definitions.example: The research project requires substantial financial and human input to achieve its objectives.
Please input the correct user ID to access the academic database.

input.etymology: 前缀in-（向内）+ 词根put（放置），指放入的资源或信息

similar_words.description: 前者指输入的资源或信息，后者指输出的成果或产量

definitions.example: The academic journal published a critical inquiry into the ethical risks of artificial intelligence.

inquiry.etymology: 前缀in-（进入）+ 词根quir（询问，源自拉丁语quaerere）+ 名词后缀-y，指深入的询问或调查

similar_words.description: 前者指正式的调查或询问，后者指简短的疑问或查询

definitions.example: Many migratory birds rely on insects as their primary food source to sustain long-distance flights.

insect.etymology: in-（进入）+ sect（切割，源拉丁语secare），因昆虫身体分节如切割而成

similar_words.description: 词形相近，前者为名词表「昆虫」，后者为动词表「插入」

definitions.example: Scientists insert a specific gene sequence into the bacterial genome to study its function.

insert.etymology: in-（向内）+ sert（放置，源拉丁语serere），向内放置→插入

similar_words.description: 词形相近，前者为动词表「插入」，后者为名词表「昆虫」

definitions.example: The confidential research data is stored inside a fireproof cabinet in the laboratory.

inside.etymology: in-（在…内）+ side（侧边、面），在内部的面→内部

similar_words.description: 均表「内部的」，inside侧重具体空间的内部，inner侧重抽象或更深入的内部（如「内心的」）

definitions.example: Her insight into the cultural barriers of cross-border communication has revolutionized international business studies.

insight.etymology: in-（向内）+ sight（视觉、观察），向内深入观察→洞察力

similar_words.description: 均为与「观察」相关的名词，insight表对事物本质的洞察力，foresight表对未来的预见力

definitions.meaning: 有洞察力的；富有深刻见解的
definitions.example: The professor’s insightful commentary on the novel revealed hidden themes of social inequality.

insightful.etymology: insight（洞察力）+ -ful（形容词后缀，表「充满…的」），充满洞察力的

similar_words.description: 同根词，前者为形容词表「有洞察力的」，后者为名词表「洞察力」

definitions.example: Environmental activists insist that governments should enforce stricter regulations on plastic waste disposal.

insist.etymology: in-（加强语气）+ sist（站立，源拉丁语stare），坚定站立→坚持

similar_words.description: 均表「坚持」，insist侧重坚持观点或要求（常接on/that从句），persist侧重坚持行动或状态（常接in）

definitions.example: Food safety regulators inspect factory premises regularly to ensure compliance with hygiene standards.

inspect.etymology: in-（向内）+ spect（看，源拉丁语specere），向内仔细查看→检查

similar_words.description: 词形相近，前者为动词表「检查、视察」，后者为动词表「期待、预料」

definitions.example: The artist drew inspiration from the traditional folk art of her hometown to create her award-winning series.

inspiration.etymology: in-（向内）+ spir（呼吸，源拉丁语spirare）+ -ation（名词后缀），原指神灵向人吹入灵气→灵感

similar_words.description: 均为与「呼吸」词根相关的名词，inspiration表「灵感、鼓舞」，aspiration表「志向、渴望」

definitions.example: The Nobel laureate’s dedication to scientific research has inspired a generation of young scholars.

inspire.etymology: in-（向内）+ spir（呼吸，源拉丁语spirare），原指吹入灵气→激励、赋予灵感

similar_words.description: 同根（spir-「呼吸」），前者为动词表「激励、赋予灵感」，后者为动词表「渴望、追求」

definitions.example: Tech companies install advanced cybersecurity systems to protect user data from unauthorized access.
The newly elected prime minister was installed in office in a formal ceremony at the parliament.

install.etymology: in-（向内）+ stall（放置、安置，源拉丁语stallum），向内安置→安装；引申为使就职

similar_words.description: 词形相近，前者为动词表「安装、任命」，后者为名词表「分期付款、（作品的）一期」

definitions.example: The installation of the new campus Wi-Fi system was completed before the start of the fall semester.
