The installation ceremony of the new university president will be held in the main auditorium next month.

installation.etymology: 词根in-（进入）+stall（放置）+ation（名词后缀），表“放置进去的行为或结果”

similar_words.description: 「installation」侧重设备安装或就职行为，「installment」侧重分期付款或连载作品的一期

definitions.example: Many postgraduate students choose to pay their tuition in monthly installments to reduce financial burden.
The academic journal will publish the third installment of the professor’s research paper next quarter.

installment.etymology: 词根in-（进入）+stall（放置）+ment（名词后缀），表“分批放置的款项或内容”

similar_words.description: 「installment」侧重分期付款或连载部分，「installation」侧重设备安装或就职行为

definitions.example: This case provides a typical instance of how academic plagiarism can damage a scholar’s reputation.
The report instances several studies to support its conclusion about online learning effectiveness.

instance.etymology: 词根in-（在…上）+st（站立）+ance（名词后缀），表“站在眼前的具体例子”

similar_words.description: 「instance」指具体的例子、实例，「instant」指时间上的瞬间或即刻的

definitions.example: The new learning platform offers instant feedback on students’ quiz submissions.
For an instant, the student hesitated before raising his hand to ask the professor a question.

instant.etymology: 词根in-（不）+st（站立）+ant（形容词/名词后缀），表“不停留的、即刻的”

similar_words.description: 「instant」侧重时间的短暂或行动的即刻，「instance」侧重具体的例子、实例

definitions.example: The sensor can detect an instantaneous change in temperature and send an alert to the research team immediately.

instantaneous.etymology: 词根instant（瞬间的）+aneous（形容词后缀，表“具有…性质的”），表“具有瞬间性的”

similar_words.description: 「instantaneous」更正式，侧重动作发生的绝对瞬间性；「instant」更常用，可指立即的或短暂的时刻

definitions.example: Human babies have an innate instinct to turn their heads toward sounds that resemble a caregiver’s voice.

instinct.etymology: 词根in-（内在的）+stinct（刺，引申为“内在的刺激”），表“内在驱动的本能反应”

similar_words.description: 「instinct」指天生的、无需学习的本能；「intuition」指基于感觉而非理性思考的直觉

definitions.example: The university’s environmental research institute has published several influential papers on climate change.
The education authority will institute a new assessment system for primary school students next year.

institute.etymology: 词根in-（进入）+stit（站立）+ute（动词/名词后缀），表“建立起来的机构或建立的动作”

similar_words.description: 「institute」侧重具体的科研院所或“建立”的动作；「institution」侧重抽象的制度或大型社会/教育机构

definitions.example: The local cultural institution offers free workshops on traditional Chinese painting for college students.
Higher education is regarded as a key social institution that promotes social mobility.

institution.etymology: 词根in-（进入）+stit（站立）+ution（名词后缀），表“建立起来的长期存在的事物”

similar_words.description: 「institution」侧重抽象制度或大型社会/教育机构；「institute」侧重具体科研院所或“建立”动作

definitions.example: The lab technician will instruct students on how to operate the advanced microscope safely.
The court instructed the jury to consider only the evidence presented during the trial.

instruct.etymology: 词根in-（进入）+struct（建造，引申为“构建知识体系”），表“引导他人构建知识的动作”

similar_words.description: 「instruct」指教导、指示（构建知识或规则）；「construct」指建造、构建（实体或抽象事物）

definitions.example: The professor’s clear instruction helped students understand the complex economic theory.
Before assembling the bookshelf, please read the manufacturer’s instructions carefully.

instruction.etymology: 词根instruct（指导）+ion（名词后缀），表“指导的行为或内容”

similar_words.description: 「instruction」侧重官方或专业的指导、操作说明；「direction」侧重方向或一般性的指示

definitions.example: Precise instruments are crucial for collecting reliable data in environmental research.

instrument.etymology: 「in-」（内部）+「stru-」（建造）+「-ment」（名词后缀），原指“内部构造的器具”，引申为仪器、工具

similar_words.description: 「implement」多指农业/手工工具或实施措施，「instrument」多指精密科学仪器或手段

definitions.meaning: 起重要作用的；用乐器演奏的
definitions.example: Community support was instrumental in the success of the local literacy program.

instrumental.etymology: 「instrument」（工具）+「-al」（形容词后缀），原指“作为工具的”，引申为起作用的

similar_words.description: 「vocal」指声乐的、口头表达的，「instrumental」指器乐的、起作用的，二者在音乐语境中易混淆

definitions.example: The renowned instrumentalist collaborated with a symphony orchestra for the charity concert.
As an instrumentalist, he argues that ideas are merely tools for solving practical problems.

instrumentalist.etymology: 「instrumental」（用乐器的）+「-ist」（表人的名词后缀），引申为乐器演奏者或持工具主义观点的人

similar_words.description: 「musician」是广义的音乐从业者，「instrumentalist」特指擅长演奏乐器的音乐家

definitions.meaning: 使隔热/隔音；使隔离（免受不良影响）
definitions.example: Schools should implement policies to insulate students from cyberbullying.

insulate.etymology: 「in-」（进入）+「sul-」（岛）+「-ate」（动词后缀），原指“使成岛状”，引申为隔离、绝缘

similar_words.description: 「isolate」侧重物理上的单独隔离（如隔离病患），「insulate」侧重隔热/隔音或免受抽象负面影响的隔离

definitions.example: His sarcastic comment was deemed an insult to the entire research team.
It is unacceptable to insult others based on their cultural background.

insult.etymology: 「in-」（否定）+「sult」（跳），原指“跳起来攻击”，引申为侮辱

similar_words.description: 「offend」侧重无意或有意的冒犯（程度较轻），「insult」侧重故意的、羞辱性的辱骂（程度较重）

definitions.example: The rising cost of medical insurance has become a major concern for working-class families.

similar_words.description: 「assurance」指保证、确信，「insurance」指经济领域的保险，二者形近且语义易混淆

definitions.example: Most homeowners insure their properties against natural disasters.
We must take extra steps to insure the accuracy of the experimental data.

insure.etymology: 「in-」（使）+「sure」（确定），原指“使确定”，引申为投保、确保

similar_words.description: 「insure」主要指经济上的投保，偶表确保；「ensure」主要指确保某事发生，为常用义，二者形近义近易混淆

definitions.meaning: 完好无损的；完整无缺的
definitions.example: The ancient pottery was unearthed intact, with no visible cracks or damage.

intact.etymology: 「in-」（不）+「tact」（接触），原指“未被触碰的”，引申为完好的

similar_words.description: 「entire」侧重整体的全部范围，「intact」侧重未被破坏、保持原有状态的完整

definitions.meaning: 不可或缺的；构成整体所必需的
definitions.example: Data analysis is an integral component of modern social science research.

integral.etymology: 「integr-」（完整）+「-al」（形容词后缀），原指“完整的”，引申为构成整体所必需的

similar_words.description: 「essential」侧重本质上的必要，「integral」侧重作为整体一部分的必要

definitions.example: The program aims to integrate marginalized groups into mainstream society.

integrate.etymology: 「integr-」（完整）+「-ate」（动词后缀），原指“使完整”，引申为合并、融入

similar_words.description: 「combine」侧重将不同事物简单结合，「integrate」侧重使事物形成有机统一的整体

definitions.example: Academic integrity is a fundamental principle that all graduate students must adhere to.
Any unauthorized modification to the original dataset will damage the integrity of the research.

integrity.etymology: 词根「integr-」（表「完整的」，与已收录的「integral」「integrate」同根）+ 名词后缀「-ity」（表抽象性质），原义为「完整的状态」，引申为「正直」（指人格无瑕疵的完整状态）

similar_words.description: 同根词，integral为形容词，表「必需的；构成整体的」；integrity为名词，侧重「道德正直」或「事物完整状态」
同根词，integrate为动词，表「整合；使一体化」；integrity为名词，表抽象性质

definitions.example: Intellectual property protection is essential for promoting innovation in academic circles.
Public intellectuals often publish commentaries on social issues in leading academic journals.

intellectual.etymology: 词根「intellect-」（表「智慧、思维」）+ 后缀「-ual」（可作形容词或名词后缀），与「intelligence」「intelligent」同根

similar_words.description: 形近词，intelligent为形容词，表「聪明的、有智力的」；intellectual可作形容词（表「与智力相关的」）或名词（表「知识分子」）
同根词，intellect为名词，表「智力、才智」；intellectual可作形容词或名词

definitions.example: Standardized tests are widely used to assess students' verbal and mathematical intelligence.
Military intelligence agencies collect and analyze information to support strategic decisions.

intelligence.etymology: 前缀「inter-」（在…之间）+ 词根「lig-」（选择、辨别）+ 名词后缀「-ence」，原义为「从众多事物中辨别→智力」，引申为「情报」（指经辨别筛选的信息）

similar_words.description: 同根词，intellect侧重「抽象的思维才智」；intelligence侧重「智力水平或经筛选的情报」
同根词，intelligent为形容词，表「聪明的」；intelligence为名词，表「智力；情报」

definitions.example: Intelligent algorithms are increasingly employed to process large volumes of academic research data.

intelligent.etymology: 前缀「inter-」（在…之间）+ 词根「lig-」（选择、辨别）+ 形容词后缀「-ent」，表「能辨别选择的→聪明的」，与「intelligence」同根

similar_words.description: 形近词，intelligent仅作形容词，表「聪明的」；intellectual可作形容词（表「智力的」）或名词（表「知识分子」）
近义词，clever侧重「机灵的、巧妙的」；intelligent侧重「智商高的、有逻辑思维的」

definitions.example: The abstract of a research paper must be concise and intelligible to readers from different academic backgrounds.

intelligible.etymology: 前缀「inter-」（在…之间）+ 词根「lig-」（选择、辨别）+ 形容词后缀「-ible」（表「可…的」），表「可被辨别理解的→易懂的」

similar_words.description: 形近词，intelligible表「可理解的」；intelligent表「聪明的」，二者词义完全不同
近义词，comprehensible侧重「内容可被理解的」；intelligible侧重「表达清晰易懂的」

definitions.example: Many Chinese undergraduates intend to pursue master's degrees in prestigious domestic universities.
The author's metaphor in the conclusion intends to challenge the dominant theoretical framework.

intend.etymology: 前缀「in-」（向内）+ 词根「tend-」（伸展），原义为「把心思向内伸展→打算」，与「intention」同根

similar_words.description: 同根词，tend为动词，表「倾向；照料」；intend为动词，表「打算；意指」
同根词，intention为名词，表「意图；目的」；intend为动词，表「打算」

definitions.example: Intense peer pressure in graduate school often motivates students to enhance their research skills.

intense.etymology: 前缀「in-」（加强语气）+ 词根「tens-」（伸展），表「伸展到极致的→强烈的」，与「intensity」「intensive」同根

similar_words.description: 形近词，intense侧重「程度深的、强烈的」；intensive侧重「频率高的、集中的」
近义词，extreme侧重「超出常规的极端的」；intense侧重「程度深的强烈的」

definitions.example: The intensity of laboratory experiments increases significantly as the research project progresses.

intensity.etymology: 来自形容词「intense」，词根「tens-」（伸展）+ 名词后缀「-ity」（表性质），表「强烈的程度→强度」

similar_words.description: 同根词，intense为形容词，表「强烈的」；intensity为名词，表「强度、剧烈程度」
形近词，intensive为形容词，表「集中的、密集的」；intensity为名词，表「强度」

definitions.example: Intensive literature review is a necessary step before conducting any empirical research.

intensive.etymology: 前缀「in-」（加强语气）+ 词根「tens-」（伸展）+ 形容词后缀「-ive」，表「加强伸展的→集中的」，与「intense」「intensity」同根

similar_words.description: 形近词，intensive侧重「频率高的、集中的」；intense侧重「程度深的、强烈的」
形近词，extensive表「广泛的、大规模的」；intensive表「集中的、密集的」

definitions.example: The researcher's primary intention was to investigate the impact of online learning on students' academic performance.

intention.etymology: 来自动词「intend」，前缀「in-」（向内）+ 词根「tend-」（伸展）+ 名词后缀「-ion」，表「心思伸展向的事物→意图」

similar_words.description: 同根词，intent可作名词（表「坚定的意图」）或形容词（表「专心的」）；intention为名词，表「一般的打算、意图」
近义词，purpose侧重「明确的行动目的」；intention侧重「内心的打算或意图」

definitions.example: Teachers are encouraged to interact frequently with students to enhance learning outcomes.

interact.etymology: inter-（相互）+ act（行动），指相互采取行动

similar_words.description: interact强调双方或多方的相互作用，react侧重单方面对刺激作出的反应

definitions.meaning: 交往；交流（尤指文化、人际间的正式往来）
definitions.example: Cultural intercourse between Eastern and Western countries promotes mutual understanding and respect.

intercourse.etymology: inter-（相互）+ course（跑，引申为往来），指相互之间的往来

similar_words.description: intercourse侧重正式的、大规模的交往，interaction侧重具体的、日常的互动行为

definitions.example: The research explores factors that shape adolescents' interest in environmental protection.
The new regulation is intended to protect the public interest rather than individual gains.

interest.etymology: inter-（在…之间）+ est（存在），指存在于人与事物之间的关联（引申为兴趣、利益）

similar_words.description: interest侧重涉及自身利害的抽象利益，benefit侧重具体的好处或益处

definitions.meaning: 界面；接口（尤指学科、系统间的交界）
definitions.example: The interface between sociology and public health has yielded valuable research findings.

interface.etymology: inter-（相互）+ face（面），指相互接触的交界面

similar_words.description: interface指不同事物或领域的交界界面，surface指物体的物理表面

definitions.meaning: 干涉；干预（尤指不当介入）
definitions.example: External powers have no right to interfere in the internal affairs of a sovereign state.
Constant noise from the construction site interfered with the graduate students' thesis writing.

interfere.etymology: inter-（在…之间）+ fere（击打），指打入中间进行干预

similar_words.description: interfere多含贬义，指不当干涉；intervene多为中性，指为调解而进行的干预

definitions.meaning: 干涉；干预（尤指不当的介入行为）
definitions.example: The court ruled that the government's interference in the press was a violation of freedom of speech.

interference.etymology: interfere（v. 干涉）+ -ence（名词后缀），指干涉的行为或状态

similar_words.description: interference多含贬义，指不当干涉；intervention多为中性，指调解性干预

definitions.example: The university appointed an interim president during the search for a permanent leader.

interim.etymology: inter-（在…之间）+ im（=em，进入），指进入两个阶段之间的过渡状态

similar_words.description: interim侧重过渡阶段的临时安排，temporary泛指短期的临时事物

definitions.example: The interior design of the library focuses on creating a quiet and comfortable reading environment.

interior.etymology: inter-（内部）+ -ior（形容词比较级后缀），指更靠内的

similar_words.description: interior指内部的，exterior指外部的，二者为反义词且拼写相近易混

definitions.example: The course is designed for learners with intermediate English proficiency, not absolute beginners.

intermediate.etymology: inter-（在…之间）+ medi（中间）+ -ate（形容词后缀），指处于中间位置的

similar_words.description: intermediate指处于两个极端之间的水平或阶段，medium指中等的尺寸或数量，拼写相近易混

definitions.example: Intermittent power outages during the storm disrupted the laboratory's long-term experiment.

intermittent.etymology: inter-（在…之间）+ mitt（送、放置）+ -ent（形容词后缀），指中间有中断的

similar_words.description: intermittent指断断续续的，continuous指连续不断的，二者为反义词易在语境中混淆

definitions.example: Listed companies are required to disclose their internal control reports to the public.

internal.etymology: 「inter-」（在…之间、内部） + 「-nal」（形容词后缀），表示处于内部范畴的

similar_words.description: 「internal」可指抽象范畴（如企业内控、国内事务）或具体事物的内部；「interior」侧重空间实体的内部（如建筑内部、内陆）

definitions.example: International academic exchanges help promote the development of cutting-edge research fields.

international.etymology: 「inter-」（在…之间） + 「nation」（国家） + 「-al」（形容词后缀），表示国家之间的

similar_words.description: 「international」指涉及多个国家的通用范畴；「multinational」特指跨国企业或涉及多国的商业主体

definitions.example: An increasing number of peer-reviewed academic papers are accessible via the Internet.

internet.etymology: 「inter-」（相互） + 「net」（网），指全球范围内相互连接的公共计算机网络

similar_words.description: 「internet」指全球公共互联网；「intranet」指企业或机构内部的局域网，仅内部人员可访问

definitions.example: Literary critics often interpret the novel as a reflection of structural inequality in 19th-century Britain.
A professional linguist was hired to interpret between the two foreign research delegations.

interpret.etymology: 「inter-」（在…之间） + 「pret」（=pric，价值，引申为阐释含义），表示在双方之间传递或阐释含义

similar_words.description: 「interpret」多指口头翻译或对抽象内容的解读；「translate」多指书面文字的翻译

definitions.example: Please do not interrupt the speaker during the academic symposium's keynote speech.

interrupt.etymology: 「inter-」（在…之间） + 「rupt」（断裂），表示在进程中间断裂、打断

similar_words.description: 「interrupt」侧重短暂打断原有进程（如讲话）；「disrupt」侧重彻底扰乱、破坏原有秩序（如供应链）

definitions.example: The two major expressways intersect at the western edge of the provincial capital.

intersect.etymology: 「inter-」（在…之间） + 「sect」（切割），表示在两者之间切割、交叉

similar_words.description: 「intersect」指线条、道路等的交叉；「intercept」指拦截、截获（如信号、车辆）

definitions.example: Traffic accidents frequently occur at the busy intersection near the university campus.

intersection.etymology: 「inter-」（在…之间） + 「sect」（切割） + 「-ion」（名词后缀），表示交叉的位置

similar_words.description: 「intersection」特指道路的十字路口；「junction」可指各类连接点（如铁路枢纽、电路节点）

definitions.example: Blood samples were collected at 15-minute intervals during the medical experiment.

interval.etymology: 「inter-」（在…之间） + 「val」（=wall，墙，引申为分隔物），表示两者之间的分隔时段或空间

similar_words.description: 「interval」指时间或空间的固定间隔；「interim」指过渡期间（如中期报告），侧重临时性

definitions.example: The central bank may intervene in the foreign exchange market to stabilize the domestic currency.

intervene.etymology: 「inter-」（在…之间） + 「ven」（来） + 「-e」，表示来到两者之间进行干预

similar_words.description: 「intervene」多指官方或有目的的中性/积极干预（如调控市场）；「interfere」多指无端干扰，多含贬义

definitions.example: The candidate performed excellently in the final interview for the postgraduate research assistant position.
The science journalist was assigned to interview the Nobel Prize winner in physiology.

interview.etymology: 「inter-」（相互） + 「view」（看），表示双方相互观察、交流的行为

similar_words.description: 「interview」指双方互动的面试或采访；「review」指回顾、审查或评论（如文献综述）

definitions.example: Most people only share their vulnerable feelings with a small circle of intimate friends.
The official report intimated that the policy would be adjusted in the next quarter.

intimate.etymology: 词根intra-表示「内部的」，后缀-mate表相关的人；形容词义由「内部伙伴般的」引申而来，动词义由「告知内部信息」引申而来。

similar_words.description: 两者拼写仅中间字母不同，「intimate」可作形容词表亲密的、作动词表暗示，「intimidate」为动词，意为恐吓、威胁。

definitions.example: The suspect tried to intimidate the witness into withdrawing his testimony in court.

intimidate.etymology: 前缀in-表加强，词根timid表示「害怕的」，后缀-ate为动词后缀，合起来表示「使感到害怕」，引申为恐吓、威胁。

similar_words.description: 两者拼写仅中间字母不同，「intimidate」为动词表恐吓，「intimate」可作形容词表亲密的、作动词表暗示。

definitions.meaning: 错综复杂的；精细复杂的
definitions.example: The intricate structure of the human brain still puzzles many neuroscientists around the world.

intricate.etymology: 前缀in-表加强，词根tric表示「琐碎、障碍」，后缀-ate为形容词后缀，合起来表示「充满琐碎细节与障碍的」，引申为错综复杂的。

similar_words.description: 两者均表示复杂的，「intricate」侧重结构、细节繁复精细，「complicated」侧重问题、关系因要素过多而难以理清。

definitions.example: The newly unearthed dinosaur fossil has intrigued paleontologists worldwide.
The novel depicts the political intrigue within the royal court of the 18th century.

intrigue.etymology: 前缀in-表内部，词根trig（同tric）表示「诡计」，原指「内部的秘密诡计」，后动词义引申为勾起好奇心。

similar_words.description: 两者拼写相近，「intrigue」可作动词表激起兴趣、作名词表密谋，「intricate」为形容词，意为错综复杂的。

definitions.meaning: 引人入胜的；新奇有趣的
definitions.example: The finding that gut bacteria affects mood is particularly intriguing for psychological researchers.

intriguing.etymology: 来自动词intrigue（激起兴趣），后缀-ing为形容词后缀，表「具有某种性质的」。

similar_words.description: 两者均表示吸引人的，「intriguing」侧重因神秘、新奇而引人探究，「fascinating」侧重因极具魅力而令人着迷。

definitions.example: The intrinsic value of art lies in its ability to arouse emotional resonance among audiences.

intrinsic.etymology: 前缀intra-表示「内部的」，后缀-insic表「属于…的」，合起来表示「属于事物本身内部的」，引申为固有的、本质的。

similar_words.description: 两者均表示内在的，「intrinsic」侧重事物本身固有的、不可缺少的属性，「inherent」侧重天生存在、无法与主体分离的性质。

definitions.example: The local government introduced a series of preferential policies to attract high-tech enterprises.
Several senators introduced a bill to strengthen the protection of endangered species.

introduce.etymology: 前缀intro-表示「向内、进入」，词根duce表示「引导」，合起来表示「引导进入」，引申为介绍、引进、提出等含义。

similar_words.description: 两者均为带-duce词根的动词，「introduce」意为介绍、引进，「induce」意为诱导、引起，拼写与词义易混淆。

definitions.example: The introduction of renewable energy has greatly reduced the region's dependence on fossil fuels.
The introduction of the academic paper clarifies the research background and core questions.

introduction.etymology: 来自动词introduce（介绍、引进），后缀-tion为名词后缀，表动作或状态。

similar_words.description: 两者均可表示著作的开头部分，「introduction」侧重对全书内容的铺垫性说明，「preface」侧重作者交代写作背景、目的的前言。

definitions.example: Reporters were criticized for intruding into the private lives of the disaster victims.

intrude.etymology: 前缀in-表示「进入」，词根trud表示「推、插入」，合起来表示「强行推入」，引申为闯入、侵扰。

similar_words.description: 两者均表示侵入，「intrude」侧重未经允许闯入私人领域或打扰他人，「invade」侧重大规模武装入侵或大量涌入。

definitions.example: Many netizens oppose the intrusive collection of personal information by social media platforms.

intrusive.etymology: 来自动词intrude（闯入、侵扰），后缀-sive为形容词后缀，表「具有某种性质的」。

similar_words.description: 两者均表示冒犯的，「intrusive」侧重因侵入私人领域而打扰他人，「offensive」侧重言行粗鲁无礼、令人反感。

definitions.example: Many leading scholars in behavioral economics rely on intuition to formulate initial research hypotheses.

intuition.etymology: 词根词缀：in-（不，非）+ tuit（看管，注意）+ -ion（名词后缀），字面义为「无需刻意看管就能感知的能力」，引申为直觉

similar_words.description: 「intuition」指未经逻辑推理的直觉判断，「instinct」指先天的、无需学习的本能反应
「intuition」指直觉，「tuition」指学费，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.meaning: 入侵，侵略；（未经许可）涌入，侵犯
definitions.example: Unregulated digital advertisements often invade users' personal space by popping up without consent.

invade.etymology: 词根词缀：in-（进入）+ vad（走）+ -e，字面义为「擅自走入」，引申为入侵、侵犯

similar_words.description: 「invade」多指大规模的群体或军事入侵，「intrude」侧重个人未经允许闯入私人领域

definitions.meaning: （法律、文件等）无效的，作废的；无事实依据的
definitions.example: The court declared the unsigned employment contract invalid due to procedural errors.
The local charity organizes weekly visits to elderly invalids in the community.

invalid.etymology: 词根词缀：in-（不，非）+ valid（有效的），形容词义为「无效的」，名词义由「失去工作能力的人」引申而来

similar_words.description: 「invalid」表「无效的」，「valid」表「有效的」，二者为反义形近词

definitions.example: The archival documents stored in the national library are invaluable for studying modern Chinese history.

invaluable.etymology: 词根词缀：in-（无法）+ valuable（可估价的），字面义为「无法估价的」，引申为极宝贵的（注意「in-」此处非否定义）

similar_words.description: 「invaluable」表「极宝贵的」，「valueless」表「无价值的」，二者因前缀表意差异易望文生义混淆

definitions.example: The invariable sequence of the four seasons is determined by the Earth's axial tilt and orbital motion.

invariable.etymology: 词根词缀：in-（不，非）+ variable（可变的），直接引申为不变的

similar_words.description: 「invariable」表「不变的」，「variable」表「可变的」，二者为反义同根词

definitions.example: In peer-reviewed academic journals, research findings are invariably supported by empirical data.

invariably.etymology: 词根词缀：in-（不，非）+ vari（变化）+ -ably（副词后缀），由形容词invariable派生而来

similar_words.description: 「invariably」是「invariable」的副词形式，修饰动作表「始终」

definitions.meaning: 入侵，侵略；（外来物种等的）涌入
definitions.example: The invasion of non-native fish species has caused a sharp decline in the native aquatic biodiversity.

invasion.etymology: 词根词缀：in-（进入）+ vas（走，vad的变体）+ -ion（名词后缀），由动词invade派生而来

similar_words.description: 「invasion」多指大规模的入侵或涌入，「intrusion」侧重个人的擅自闯入

definitions.example: To cover up their poor performance, some corporate executives invent false financial statements.

invent.etymology: 词根词缀：in-（进入）+ vent（来），字面义为「带来新事物」，引申为发明、捏造

similar_words.description: 「invent」指创造不存在的事物，「discover」指发现已存在的事物

definitions.example: The invention of movable type printing was a pivotal milestone in the history of human civilization.

invention.etymology: 词根词缀：in-（进入）+ vent（来）+ -ion（名词后缀），由动词invent派生而来

similar_words.description: 「invention」指发明创造的行为或成果，「discovery」指发现已存在事物的行为或成果

definitions.meaning: 库存，存货；详细清单，目录
definitions.example: The manufacturing firm implemented a new software system to optimize its inventory management and reduce storage costs.

inventory.etymology: 词根词缀：in-（进入）+ vent（来）+ -ory（名词后缀，表集合），字面义为「进来的物品的登记册」，引申为库存、清单

similar_words.description: 「inventory」侧重库存的详细清单或盘点，「stock」侧重库存的货物本身

definitions.example: Research has found an inverse correlation between household income and the incidence of juvenile delinquency.
In mathematics, the product of a number and its inverse equals 1.

inverse.etymology: in-（表相反）+ 词根vers（转）+ 后缀-e → 反转过来的，引申为相反的、反向的

similar_words.description: 「reverse」侧重位置、顺序的颠倒或状态的逆转，可作动词表“逆转”；「inverse」侧重逻辑、数量上的反向对应，多作形容词或名词

definitions.example: If you invert the order of the argumentative premises, the whole logical reasoning will be invalid.

invert.etymology: in-（表反转）+ 词根vert（转）→ 反转过来，引申为使颠倒、使倒置

similar_words.description: 「invert」是动词，表“使颠倒”的动作；「inverse」多作形容词或名词，表“相反的（事物）”的状态，易因拼写接近混淆

definitions.example: Many venture capital firms choose to invest in start-ups with core technological innovation capabilities.
Outstanding academic researchers usually invest countless hours in literature review and empirical analysis.

invest.etymology: in-（进入）+ 词根vest（衣服，原指将财物放入贴身衣物中保管）→ 投入资源以获取回报，引申为投资、投入

similar_words.description: 「invest」侧重投入资金或精力以获取收益；「investigate」侧重调查事件或研究问题，语义差异大，易因拼写接近混淆

definitions.example: The regulatory authority was assigned to investigate the financial fraud case of the listed company.
Neuroscientists have been investigating the neural mechanisms underlying human memory for decades.

investigate.etymology: in-（进入）+ 词根vestig（踪迹、脚印）+ 动词后缀-ate → 顺着踪迹深入探查，引申为调查、研究

similar_words.description: 「investigate」表调查、研究，「invest」表投资、投入，二者核心语义完全不同，易因拼写相近混淆

definitions.example: Sustained investment in renewable energy is crucial for achieving the goal of carbon neutrality.
Mastering academic writing skills requires consistent investment of time and targeted practice.

investment.etymology: in-（进入）+ 词根vest（衣服，表保管财物）+ 名词后缀-ment → 投入的资源，引申为投资、投入

similar_words.description: 「investment」表投资、投入，「investigation」表调查、研究，易因拼写和后缀接近混淆

definitions.example: Ultraviolet rays are invisible to the human eye but can cause great damage to skin cells.
Implicit bias is an invisible force that affects people's decision-making without their awareness.

invisible.etymology: in-（表否定，不）+ 词根vis（看）+ 形容词后缀-ible（可…的）→ 不可看见的，引申为无形的

similar_words.description: 「invisible」侧重无法被视觉感知的，可引申为难以察觉的；「intangible」侧重抽象的、无法触摸或量化的（如资产、文化价值）

definitions.example: She received an invitation to deliver a keynote speech at the international academic symposium on climate change.
Leaving your personal information exposed on unsecure websites is an open invitation to identity theft.

invitation.etymology: in-（向内）+ 词根vit（召唤）+ 名词后缀-ation → 向内召唤，引申为邀请，进一步引申为招致的诱因

similar_words.description: 「invitation」是名词，表邀请、诱因；「invite」是动词，表邀请、招致，为同根词，词性不同

definitions.example: The organizing committee invited over 50 renowned scholars from home and abroad to participate in the forum.
The government's sudden announcement of tax hikes invited widespread criticism from the working class.

invite.etymology: in-（向内）+ 词根vit（召唤）+ 后缀-e → 向内召唤，引申为邀请，进一步引申为招致不好的结果

similar_words.description: 「invite」是动词，表邀请、招致；「invitation」是名词，表邀请、诱因，为同根词，词性不同

definitions.example: The construction of the 5G network involves the cooperation of multiple sectors including telecommunications and civil engineering.
Teachers are advised to design interactive activities to involve students in the process of classroom learning.

involve.etymology: in-（进入）+ 词根volv（卷、转）+ 后缀-e → 卷进去，引申为涉及、使卷入

similar_words.description: 「involve」侧重作为必要组成部分包含，可涉及人或动作，还可表使卷入；「include」侧重整体中包含部分，仅表静态包含关系

definitions.example: Even under great external pressure, he maintained a firm inward belief in the correctness of his theory.
The windows of the ancient library open inward to prevent strong winds from damaging the rare manuscripts.

inward.etymology: in-（向内）+ 后缀-ward（表方向的形容词/副词后缀）→ 朝向内部的（地），引申为内心的

similar_words.description: 「inward」表向内的（地）、内心的；「outward」表向外的（地）、表面的，为反义同根词，易混淆方向含义

definitions.example: Iron is a fundamental raw material for steel production, and an electric iron is a common household appliance.
Her iron will helped her overcome the heavy academic pressure of postgraduate entrance exam preparation.

iron.etymology: 源自古英语「isern」，原指金属铁，后引申出铁制器具、性格坚韧等义

similar_words.description: 「iron」指纯铁或铁制品（如熨斗）；「steel」指钢，是铁的合金，二者均为考研常考的金属类词汇

definitions.example: It is ironic that the environmental activist who advocates zero waste often uses disposable plastic bottles.

ironic.etymology: 来自希腊语「eirōnikos」（假装无知的讽刺者），由「iron-」（表讽刺）+「-ic」（形容词后缀）构成，为「irony」的形容词形式

similar_words.description: 「ironic」为形容词，表‘讽刺的’；「irony」为名词，表‘讽刺（手法）；出人意料的巧合’，二者同根易混词性

definitions.meaning: 讽刺；反讽；出人意料的巧合
definitions.example: The core irony of the novel is that the protagonist’s most praised virtue becomes the cause of his downfall.

irony.etymology: 来自希腊语「eirōneia」（假装无知的讽刺），为「ironic」的同根名词，后引申出「意外反差」的义项

similar_words.description: 「irony」侧重‘反差性的讽刺（常含意外性）’；「sarcasm」侧重‘尖刻的口头讽刺’，二者为近义易混词

definitions.meaning: 不考虑的；不顾的（常与of搭配）
definitions.example: All postgraduate applicants will be evaluated strictly irrespective of their undergraduate institutions or family backgrounds.

irrespective.etymology: 由否定前缀「ir-」（表‘不’）+形容词「respective」（各自的）构成，意为‘不考虑各自情况的’

similar_words.description: 「irrespective」仅作形容词，常与「of」搭配，语气正式；「regardless」可作形容词或副词，搭配「of」时语气更通用，二者为近义易混词

definitions.meaning: 灌溉；滋润（抽象义，如心灵）
definitions.example: The local government has built a 100-kilometer canal to irrigate the arid farmland in the mountainous area.

irrigate.etymology: 来自拉丁语「irrigare」，由前缀「ir-」（表‘向内’）+词根「rigare」（浇水）构成，意为‘向内浇水灌溉’

similar_words.description: 「irrigate」表‘灌溉’；「irritate」表‘激怒；刺激’，二者拼写仅差一个字母，为形近易混词

definitions.example: The constant noise from the nearby construction site irritated the students who were preparing for the postgraduate entrance exam.

irritate.etymology: 来自拉丁语「irritare」（刺激），由前缀「ir-」（表‘加强’）+词根「ritare」（推动）构成，意为‘加强刺激’

similar_words.description: 「irritate」表‘激怒/刺激’；「irrigate」表‘灌溉’，二者拼写接近，为形近易混词

definitions.meaning: 岛；岛屿；（抽象）孤立的群体/区域
definitions.example: The remote tropical island is a habitat for many endangered species, which is often mentioned in environmental protection reading materials of the exam.

island.etymology: 源自古英语「īegland」，由「īeg」（岛）+「land」（土地）构成，原指被水环绕的土地，后引申出抽象的‘孤立区域’义

similar_words.description: 「island」为通用词，可指任何大小的岛屿；「isle」为正式/文学用词，多指小岛，二者为近义易混词

definitions.example: The British poet depicted the misty isle as a peaceful sanctuary isolated from the chaos of urban life.

isle.etymology: 源自古法语「isle」，为「island」的缩略同源变体，多用于正式文本或文学作品中

similar_words.description: 「isle」为正式/文学用词，多指小岛；「island」为通用词，可指任何大小的岛屿，二者为近义易混词

definitions.meaning: 隔离；孤立；分离（人/物/微生物）
definitions.example: During the epidemic, the local health department took strict measures to isolate confirmed cases and close contacts.

isolate.etymology: 来自拉丁语「insulare」（使成为岛），由前缀「in-」（表‘在…里’）+词根「insula」（岛）+动词后缀「-ate」构成，意为‘使像岛一样孤立’

similar_words.description: 「isolate」表‘隔离/孤立（人/物）’；「insulate」表‘绝缘/隔离（热量/电流/不良影响）’，二者拼写接近，为形近易混词

definitions.example: The issue of balanced regional development has been a hot topic in recent postgraduate entrance exam writing and reading sections.

definitions.meaning: 发布；颁布；发行（刊物/政策/邮票等）
definitions.example: The Ministry of Education issued a new regulation on postgraduate enrollment supervision in the first half of this year.

issue.etymology: 来自拉丁语「exire」（走出），由前缀「ex-」（表‘出’）+词根「ire」（走）构成，原指‘流出的事物’，后引申为‘议题、发布’等义

similar_words.description: 「issue」侧重‘需讨论/解决的重大社会/学术议题’；「problem」侧重‘具体的难题或困难’，二者为近义易混词

definitions.example: Each item on the academic conference's agenda was discussed in detail by participating scholars.
The local newspaper carried an item about the university's new research center.

item.etymology: 源自拉丁语「item」，原意为「同样地」，后引申为列表中的「条目、项目」

similar_words.description: 均指「条目」；item多指具体的项目、条款，entry多指词典、清单中的录入条目

definitions.example: The field researcher wore a waterproof jacket while collecting samples in the rainy forest.
The hardcover book's jacket features a painting that reflects its core theme.

jacket.etymology: 源自中古法语「jaquet」，原指「短上衣」，后引申出书籍护封义

similar_words.description: 均指「外套」；jacket指短款夹克，coat指长款大衣或正式外套

definitions.example: The unexpected traffic jam delayed the research team's arrival at the experimental base.
Many small academic institutions found themselves in a jam during the funding cut.

jam.etymology: 作「果酱」义源自古法语「jame」；作「堵塞」义为拟声词，模仿卡住的声音，后引申出「困境」义

similar_words.description: 均指「堵塞」；jam侧重拥挤性堵塞（如交通），block侧重障碍物造成的堵塞

definitions.example: The biologist stored the water samples in a sealed glass jar for lab testing.
The critic's harsh comments on the study jarred the entire research panel.

jar.etymology: 源自古法语「jarre」，原指「陶制容器」，后引申出动词「刺激」义（因碰撞罐子发出刺耳声）

similar_words.description: 均指「容器」；jar指广口的罐（多用玻璃），pot指带盖的壶、罐（多用陶或金属）

definitions.meaning: 行话，行业术语（尤指晦涩难懂的）
definitions.example: The research paper avoids excessive academic jargon to reach a broader audience.

jargon.etymology: 源自古法语「jargon」，原指「鸟的啁啾声」，后引申为「听不懂的群体内部用语」

similar_words.description: 均指「术语」；jargon侧重某一群体的内部行话（常晦涩），terminology指系统的专业术语体系

definitions.meaning: 颌；下巴（常用复数「jaws」指上下颌）
definitions.example: The paleontologist analyzed the fossilized jaw of the prehistoric fish to trace its evolution.

similar_words.description: 均指「下巴」；jaw指整个颌骨（含上下），chin仅指下巴尖

definitions.example: The cultural studies course examines jazz's role in shaping African American identity.

definitions.meaning: 使活泼，使有生气（常与「up」连用）
definitions.example: The editor suggested jazzing up the academic article's introduction to engage readers.

jazz.etymology: 源自美国黑人俚语，原指「活力」，后特指起源于美国黑人的爵士乐，再引申出动词义

similar_words.description: 均为美国黑人音乐类型；jazz是爵士乐（节奏灵活），blues是蓝调（节奏缓慢、情感忧伤）

definitions.example: Some young scholars are jealous of their mentors' prestigious academic awards.

definitions.meaning: 珍惜的，爱惜的（尤指对名誉、隐私等）
definitions.example: The Nobel laureate is jealous of his reputation for academic integrity.

jealous.etymology: 源自古法语「jalous」，原指「热情的」，后引申为「妒忌的」，再发展出「珍惜的」义

similar_words.description: 均指「羡慕的」；jealous侧重带有占有欲的妒忌（含对自身事物的珍惜），envious侧重单纯羡慕他人的优势

definitions.example: The student wore a pair of faded jeans to the casual academic workshop.

jeans.etymology: 源自意大利城市热那亚（Genoa）的旧称「Jean」，当地产的粗斜纹布被称为「jean」，后指用该布制作的裤子

similar_words.description: 均与牛仔裤相关；jeans指牛仔裤（成品），denim指制作牛仔裤的粗斜纹布料

definitions.example: The research team took a jet to the remote Arctic observation station.

definitions.meaning: 喷射；乘喷气式飞机旅行
definitions.example: The geyser began to jet hot water into the air during the field observation.

jet.etymology: 源自拉丁语「jacere」（投掷），原指「投掷出的物体」，后引申为「喷射流」「喷气式飞机」

similar_words.description: 均指「飞机」；jet特指喷气式飞机，plane是飞机的统称

definitions.example: The crown, studded with rare jewels, is the centerpiece of the museum’s royal collection.

jewel.etymology: 源自古法语jouel，原指玩具、装饰品，后引申为宝石或珍贵物品

similar_words.description: jewel指单个宝石或小件珠宝饰品，jewelry是珠宝的总称，为不可数名词

definitions.example: More brands are launching sustainable jewelry made from recycled precious metals.

jewelry.etymology: jewel的派生名词，后缀-ry表示集合名词，指珠宝的总称

similar_words.description: jewelry是珠宝的总称（不可数），jewel指单个宝石或小件珠宝饰品

definitions.example: Researchers found that 30 minutes of daily jogging can improve cardiovascular health significantly.
Seeing the vintage postcard jogged her memory of the seaside vacation she took as a child.

jog.etymology: 词源暂不明确，一说来自拟声，模拟慢跑时的脚步声

similar_words.description: jog指慢跑或唤起记忆，jolt指震动、猛推，二者词形相近易混淆

definitions.example: The two countries signed a joint agreement to strengthen cooperation in climate research.
Regular stretching can help relieve stiffness in the knee joints.

joint.etymology: 源自拉丁语iunctum（连接），原指连接的部位，后引申为‘共同的’义

similar_words.description: joint是形容词‘共同的’或名词‘关节’，join是动词‘连接，加入’，二者同根表‘连接’义

definitions.example: His self-deprecating joke lightened the tense atmosphere during the academic seminar.
She was not joking when she said she wanted to pursue a PhD in astrophysics.

joke.etymology: 源自拉丁语jocus（玩笑、戏谑），后传入英语指笑话或开玩笑

similar_words.description: joke指笑话或开玩笑，jolly是形容词‘快乐的’或副词‘很’，二者词形相近易混淆

definitions.example: The jolly atmosphere of the campus festival attracted hundreds of students and local residents.
That jolly well serves him right for violating the laboratory safety rules repeatedly.

jolly.etymology: 源自古法语jolif（快乐的），与joke同源于拉丁语jocus词根

similar_words.description: jolly与joke同源于拉丁语表‘玩笑’的词根，jolly指快乐的，joke指笑话

definitions.example: Her groundbreaking research on artificial intelligence was published in a top peer-reviewed journal.

journal.etymology: 源自拉丁语diurnalis（每日的），词根di-表‘天’，原指每日记录，后引申为期刊

similar_words.description: journal指学术期刊或日记，journalist指从事新闻报道的记者，二者为派生关系

definitions.example: The investigative journalist spent two years uncovering the corruption scandal in the pharmaceutical industry.

journalist.etymology: journal的派生名词，后缀-ist表示‘从事某职业的人’，指新闻工作者

similar_words.description: journalist多指从事深度调查、专业报道的新闻工作者，reporter多指现场播报的记者

definitions.example: The memoir chronicles the author’s emotional journey of recovering from a severe illness.

journey.etymology: 源自古法语journee（一日的行程），与journal同源于拉丁语diurnalis词根

similar_words.description: journey多指长途或具有情感意义的抽象/具象旅程，trip多指短途出行或旅行

definitions.example: Watching her daughter perform on the stage filled her with overwhelming joy.

joy.etymology: 源自古法语joie，与joke、jolly同源于拉丁语jocus（玩笑、欢乐）词根

similar_words.description: joy多指持续、深沉的喜悦之情，delight多指短暂、强烈的愉悦感

definitions.meaning: n. 法官；裁判员 v. 判断，评判
definitions.example: As a federal judge, she must judge evidence objectively without personal bias.

judge.etymology: 来自拉丁语judicare（判断），由jud-（判断）加名词/动词后缀构成

similar_words.description: 「judge」可作名词和动词；「judgement」是其派生名词，仅作名词，表判断（力）或司法判决。

definitions.example: The judge delivered the final judgement on the copyright infringement case after reviewing all evidence.

judgement.etymology: 由动词judge（判断）加名词后缀-ment构成，表行为或结果

similar_words.description: 「judgement」是「judge」的派生名词，仅作名词；「judge」兼具名词和动词词性，语义范围更广。

definitions.example: The judicial branch of government is responsible for interpreting laws and ensuring their fair application.

judicial.etymology: 来自拉丁语judicialis，由jud-（判断）加形容词后缀-icial（与…相关的）构成，表与司法判断相关的

similar_words.description: 「judicial」指司法的、法庭的（与法律相关）；「judicious」指明智的、有判断力的（形容人或决策），拼写接近但语义差异大，考研易混淆。

definitions.example: She poured the herbal tea from the ceramic jug into the teacups for the guests.
The white-collar criminal was sentenced to five years in the jug after being convicted of fraud.

jug.etymology: 来自古英语jugge，最初指装液体的大壶，俚义表监狱为熟词僻义

similar_words.description: 「jug」是名词，指大壶或（俚语）监狱；「juggle」是动词，指玩杂耍或巧妙应对（多重任务），拼写接近易混淆。

definitions.meaning: 玩杂耍；巧妙应对（多重任务）
definitions.example: Many graduate students have to juggle thesis writing, part-time jobs and extracurricular activities to meet graduation requirements.

juggle.etymology: 来自中古英语jugglen，与拉丁语joculari（开玩笑、逗乐）同源，最初指玩杂耍逗乐，后引申为巧妙应对

similar_words.description: 「juggle」是动词，指玩杂耍或巧妙应对；「jug」是名词，指大壶或监狱，拼写接近易混淆。

definitions.example: The railway junction in the southern province is a key hub for freight transportation across the country.

junction.etymology: 来自拉丁语junctio，由junct-（连接，来自jungere「连接」）加名词后缀-ion构成，表连接的地方

similar_words.description: 「junction」指具体的连接点（如道路/铁路交叉口）；「joint」指关节、接头或联合的（可作形容词），同根（均表连接）但语义侧重不同。

definitions.meaning: 丛林；（比喻）弱肉强食的领域
definitions.example: The high-tech industry is often portrayed as a jungle where innovative startups must compete fiercely with established giants.

jungle.etymology: 来自印地语jangal，原指荒地，后引申为热带丛林及比喻义的弱肉强食之地

similar_words.description: 「jungle」指热带茂密丛林，常带比喻义（如商业丛林）；「bush」指低矮灌木丛，无比喻义，二者均表植被但范围和用法不同。

definitions.meaning: adj. 较年幼的；资历浅的 n. 低年级学生；初级员工
definitions.example: Junior faculty members in this research institute are eligible for special funding to support their independent studies.

junior.etymology: 来自拉丁语juvenis（年轻的），加比较级后缀-ior构成，表更年轻的，后引申为资历浅的

similar_words.description: 「junior」指较年幼的、资历浅的；「senior」指较年长的、资历深的，二者是反义关系且拼写接近，考研常考辨析。

definitions.meaning: n. 废旧物品，垃圾 v. 丢弃
definitions.example: Researchers found that a large proportion of electronic junk is illegally dumped in developing countries, causing severe environmental pollution.

junk.etymology: 来自中古英语jonk，原指旧缆绳，后引申为无价值的废旧物品

similar_words.description: 「junk」指无价值的废旧物品（如电子垃圾），可作动词表丢弃；「trash」指通用垃圾，也可作动词表抨击，语义范围和用法略有差异。

definitions.example: In the US legal system, the jury is tasked with determining the facts of a case while the judge applies the relevant laws.

jury.etymology: 来自拉丁语jurare（发誓），原指发誓裁决的人，后专指陪审团

similar_words.description: 「jury」指由普通公民组成的陪审团，负责裁决案件事实；「judge」指职业法官，负责主持审判和适用法律，二者均为司法角色但职能完全不同。

definitions.example: The Supreme Court is responsible for upholding justice in the nation's legal system.

justice.etymology: 来自拉丁语justitia，由词根just（公正的）加名词后缀-ice构成，表抽象的公正概念

similar_words.description: justice侧重正义理念或司法体系，judgement侧重具体的判决或判断行为

definitions.example: Researchers must justify the ethical implications of their human-subject experiments.

justify.etymology: 由词根just（公正的）加动词后缀-ify（使…）构成，指使某事显得公正→证明正当

similar_words.description: justify侧重证明行为或观点的正当性，verify侧重证明信息的真实性

definitions.example: Juvenile delinquency prevention is a core topic in contemporary social policy research.

juvenile.etymology: 来自拉丁语juvenis（年轻的）加形容词后缀-ile，表与年轻者相关的属性

similar_words.description: juvenile侧重青少年群体或其行为特征，junior侧重级别较低者或年幼者（如低年级学生）

definitions.example: A keen observation of urban migration trends is crucial for writing a high-quality geography thesis.

keen.etymology: 源自古英语cēne，原指勇敢的，后引申为感知敏锐或兴趣浓厚的

similar_words.description: keen侧重人感知或兴趣的敏锐度，acute侧重问题的严重性或感官的尖锐性

definitions.example: The boiling kettle on the kitchen stove emitted a high-pitched whistle.

kettle.etymology: 源自古英语cetel，指用于加热液体的容器

similar_words.description: kettle指烧水的水壶，kennel指狗窝或养狗场

definitions.example: Typing on an ergonomic keyboard reduces the risk of wrist strain for long-hour researchers.

keyboard.etymology: 合成词，由key（按键）和board（板）构成，指带按键的输入装置

similar_words.description: keyboard指全功能键盘（如电脑键盘），keypad指小型数字或功能键盘（如手机数字键）

definitions.meaning: v. 踢；反抗 n. 踢；刺激感
definitions.example: During the demonstration, some participants tried to kick the metal barriers set up by the police.

kick.etymology: 源自古英语ciccian，指用脚撞击的动作，后引申为反抗或带来刺激的事物

similar_words.description: kick指用脚踢或反抗，pick指挑选、采摘或挖取

definitions.meaning: n. 小孩；小山羊 v. 开玩笑
definitions.example: The renowned linguist once joked, 'Don’t kid yourself that you can master a foreign language in a month.'

kid.etymology: 源自古英语cid，原指小山羊，后引申为对小孩的昵称；动词义由名词义引申为闹着玩

similar_words.description: kid指小孩或开玩笑，lid指容器的盖子

definitions.example: The thriller novel centers on a conspiracy to kidnap a senior government official for political leverage.

kidnap.etymology: 由kid（小孩）和古英语hnappian（偷窃）构成，原指偷拐小孩，后泛指绑架劫持

similar_words.description: kidnap指绑架劫持的行为，kidney指肾脏或（僻义）性格

definitions.example: Long-term intake of high-sodium food may impair kidney function and lead to chronic diseases.
The team leader is looking for a colleague of the right kidney to handle this sensitive negotiation.

kidney.etymology: 源自古英语cwith，原指人体的肾脏器官，后引申为表性格的僻义

similar_words.description: kidney指肾脏或性格，kidnap指绑架劫持的行为

definitions.example: Excessive pesticide use may kill beneficial insects that protect crop yields in agricultural ecosystems.

definitions.meaning: 使终止；使（计划、机会等）失败
definitions.example: His last-minute miscalculation killed the research team’s chance of securing the national grant.

kill.etymology: 源自古英语cwellan，为原生动词，无明确可拆分的词根词缀

similar_words.description: kill侧重杀死生物或扼杀抽象事物，语气直接；destroy侧重彻底破坏无生命物或抽象系统，程度更深
二者拼写接近，till意为「直到；耕种」，与kill语义完全不同

definitions.meaning: 千克；公斤（缩写为kg，也可作前缀表「千」）
definitions.example: The laboratory requires each sample to weigh no more than one kilo to ensure accurate testing.

kilo.etymology: 源自希腊语khilioi（千），可作度量前缀或独立名词指代「千克」

similar_words.description: 二者均为度量前缀，kilo表「千」，milli表「毫」，拼写接近易混淆

definitions.example: The ecological reserve stretches over 50 kilometres along the coastal line, protecting diverse marine species.

kilometre.etymology: 由前缀kilo-（千）+ metre（米）构成，为长度单位，表「一千米」

similar_words.description: 二者均为长度单位，由度量前缀+metre构成，centimetre表「厘米」，kilometre表「千米」

definitions.example: The anthropological study examines how kin networks shape economic cooperation in rural societies.
She is kin to the Nobel Prize-winning physicist who specializes in quantum mechanics.

kin.etymology: 源自古英语cynn（家族、种族），同根词有king（原指家族首领）

similar_words.description: kin为正式书面用语，指代亲属群体；relative侧重单个亲属成员
kin指代亲属，kith指代熟人朋友，二者常组成固定搭配kith and kin（亲友）

definitions.example: Many public kindergartens in China have incorporated STEM education into their daily curricula for young learners.

kindergarten.etymology: 源自德语，由kinder（孩子）+ garten（花园）构成，原指「儿童花园」

similar_words.description: kindergarten侧重3-6岁儿童的学前教育机构；nursery侧重托管低龄幼儿的场所，可含教育功能

definitions.example: The volunteer’s consistent kindness towards elderly residents earned her widespread recognition in the community.

kindness.etymology: 由形容词kind（善良的）+ 名词后缀-ness构成，表抽象品质或具体行为

similar_words.description: kindness侧重对他人的具体善意行为；goodness侧重人内在的道德本质

definitions.example: The medieval king issued a decree to protect the rights of serfs and promote agricultural development.
He is widely regarded as the king of contemporary documentary filmmaking for his innovative narrative techniques.

king.etymology: 源自古英语cyning（家族首领、统治者），同根词有kin（原指家族群体）

similar_words.description: 二者均为君主称号，king指男性君主；queen指女性君主或国王的配偶

definitions.example: The ancient Silk Road facilitated cultural exchanges between the Chinese kingdom and Western civilizations.
Modern biological taxonomy classifies all living organisms into five kingdoms, including the animal and plant kingdoms.

kingdom.etymology: 由king（国王）+ 名词后缀-dom（表领域、状态）构成，原指「国王统治的领土」

similar_words.description: kingdom侧重具体的君主制国家或生物分类的界；realm侧重抽象的领域或学术范围

definitions.meaning: 亲属关系；（基于共同背景的）亲切感、认同感
definitions.example: The cultural kinship between the two ethnic groups is evident in their shared folk songs and traditional crafts.

kinship.etymology: 由kin（亲属）+ 名词后缀-ship（表关系、状态）构成，表「亲属间的联系或情感共鸣」

similar_words.description: kinship侧重亲属关系或基于共同文化的认同感；relationship泛指各类人际关系

definitions.example: In the graduation photo, she placed a warm kiss on her mother’s cheek to express gratitude.
The golden sunlight kissed the tops of the pine trees, creating a peaceful atmosphere in the forest.

kiss.etymology: 源自古英语cyssan（吻），为原生动词，无明确可拆分的词根词缀

similar_words.description: 二者均为肢体亲密动作，kiss指亲吻；hug指拥抱

definitions.example: Researchers used a medical kit to collect blood samples from the experimental subjects.

kit.etymology: 源自中古英语kitte，原指盛放物品的容器，后引申为成套的工具或用品

similar_words.description: 「kit」指成套物品，「kite」多作名词指风筝或作动词表价格飞涨

definitions.example: Children flying kites on the suburban meadow caught the nature photographer’s attention.
Analysts warned that housing prices might kite in the overheated real estate market.

kite.etymology: 源自古英语cyta，原指随风飘动的器具，后动词义引申为价格快速上升

similar_words.description: 「kite」多作名词指风筝，动词表价格飞涨；「kit」指成套工具或用品

definitions.example: She knelt down and rubbed her sore knee after finishing the 5-kilometer race.

knee.etymology: 源自古英语cneo，属于人体部位基础名词

similar_words.description: 「knee」指身体部位膝盖，「kneel」指与膝盖相关的下跪动作

definitions.example: The activists knelt on the ground to demand equal rights for marginalized groups.

kneel.etymology: 源自古英语cneowlian，与「knee」（膝盖）同根，表借助膝盖完成的动作

similar_words.description: 「kneel」是动词表下跪动作，「knee」是名词表膝盖部位

definitions.example: My grandmother knits woolen scarves for her grandchildren every cold winter.
Shared life experiences knit the research team members into a cohesive group.

knit.etymology: 源自古英语cnyttan，原指系、绑，后引申为编织线绳或结合人群

similar_words.description: 「knit」指编织或使结合，「knot」指绳结或打结动作

definitions.meaning: （门、抽屉的）球形把手；旋钮；小块（黄油等）
definitions.example: Turn the temperature knob on the oven to 180 degrees Celsius before baking.

knob.etymology: 源自中古英语knobbe，原指圆形凸起的物体，后引申为器物的把手或旋钮

similar_words.description: 「knob」指球形把手或旋钮，「knot」指绳结或打结动作

definitions.example: The sailor tied a tight knot to secure the rope to the dock post.
The cargo ship sailed at a speed of 25 knots across the Pacific Ocean.

knot.etymology: 源自古英语cnotta，原指绳线的结，后引申为航速单位

similar_words.description: 「knot」指结或打结，「knit」指编织或使结合

definitions.example: Acquiring professional knowledge is crucial for college students to adapt to the competitive job market.

knowledge.etymology: 源自古英语cnawan（知道）+ 名词后缀「-ledge」，表通过学习或经验获得的认知内容

similar_words.description: 「knowledge」指通过学习获得的具体知识，「wisdom」指基于经验的智慧与判断力

definitions.example: The biologists conducted gene sequencing experiments in the molecular biology lab.

lab.etymology: 是「laboratory」的常用缩略形式，特指进行科学实验的场所

similar_words.description: 「lab」是实验室的缩写，「labour」指劳动、劳工或分娩

definitions.example: Many consumers check the nutrition label on food packages before making a purchase.
Critics often label the novel as a masterpiece of 20th-century modernist literature.

label.etymology: 源自古法语label，原指用于标记的布条，后引申为归类的动作

similar_words.description: 「label」可作动词表归类，「tag」多作名词指价格或身份识别标签

definitions.example: The growth of a country's economy is closely linked to the quality of its labor force.
She was in labor for eight hours before giving birth to a healthy baby.

labor.etymology: 来自拉丁语labor，本义为「辛劳、劳作」，词根lab-表「劳作」。

similar_words.description: 为labor的英式英语拼写变体，考研阅读中偶尔出现。

definitions.example: The vintage dress was decorated with delicate lace around the collar.

definitions.meaning: 使混合（尤指在饮料中加酒等）
definitions.example: He laced his coffee with a spoonful of brandy.

lace.etymology: 来自古法语las，本义为「绳、带」，后引申为「花边」，词根lac-表「绑、系」。

similar_words.description: 形近词，lass意为「少女」，lace意为「花边；系带」，拼写仅末尾字母不同。

definitions.example: The primary obstacle to the research project is a lack of professional researchers.
Many small businesses lack the capital to expand their operations.

lack.etymology: 来自中古英语lak，本义为「缺失、缺陷」，无明显词缀，为基础核心词汇。

similar_words.description: 均表「缺乏」，lack为通用词，可作名词或动词；shortage仅作名词，侧重「短缺的数量」。

definitions.example: The young lad volunteered to help the elderly carry their groceries.
The stable lad was responsible for feeding the horses every morning.

lad.etymology: 来自中古英语ladde，本义为「男仆」，后引申为「少年」，无明显词缀。

similar_words.description: 形近词，lad指「少年/小伙子」，lass指「少女/年轻女子」，拼写仅首字母不同。

definitions.meaning: 梯子；（职业、社会地位等的）阶梯
definitions.example: Many young people view higher education as a ladder to social mobility.
Her new tights laddered as she climbed the stone stairs.

ladder.etymology: 来自古英语hlæder，本义为「梯子」，词根lad-表「爬、升」。

similar_words.description: 均与「登高」相关，ladder指可移动的梯子；stair指固定的楼梯/台阶。

definitions.example: The elderly lady donated a large sum of money to the local animal charity.
Lady Thompson was awarded for her lifelong contributions to medical education.

lady.etymology: 来自古英语hlǣfdige，本义为「面包师的妻子」，后引申为「贵族女性」，词根hlaf-（面包）+dige（揉面者）。

similar_words.description: 均为对女性的尊称，lady为通用尊称，可指成年女性；madam更正式，多用于称呼陌生女性或信函开头。

definitions.example: The country's renewable energy sector lags behind its European competitors in technological innovation.
There is a time lag of three months between the application and the approval of the research grant.

lag.etymology: 来自中古英语laggen，本义为「拖、拉」，词根lag-表「慢、拖」。

similar_words.description: 同根词（lag-表「慢」），lag可作动词或名词，表「落后/滞后」；laggard为名词，指「落后者」。

definitions.example: The shepherd led the lambs to the lush green pasture on the hillside.
The little girl was as innocent as a lamb when facing the unfamiliar crowd.

lamb.etymology: 来自古英语lamb，本义为「羔羊」，无明显词缀，为基础核心词汇。

similar_words.description: 形近词，lamb指「羔羊」，limb指「肢体/分支」，拼写仅中间字母不同。

definitions.example: The lame old man needed a wooden cane to walk around the community garden.

definitions.meaning: 无说服力的；站不住脚的
definitions.example: The defendant offered a lame excuse for his absence from the court hearing.

lame.etymology: 来自古英语lama，本义为「跛的」，词根lam-表「损伤、弱」。

similar_words.description: 形近词，lame指「跛的/无说服力的」，lamb指「羔羊」，拼写仅末尾字母不同。

definitions.example: The adjustable desk lamp in the study was turned on to provide sufficient light for reading.
For many contemporary writers, nature is a lamp of creative inspiration.

lamp.etymology: 来自希腊语lampas，本义为「火炬」，词根lamp-表「发光、照耀」。

similar_words.description: 形近词，lamp指「灯」，camp指「营地/露营」，拼写仅首字母不同。

definitions.example: The strict landlady required all tenants to keep the corridors clean and quiet.

landlady.etymology: 合成词，由「land（房产、地产）」和「lady（女士）」组合而成，指管理房产的女性。

similar_words.description: 「landlord」指男房东或男店主，与landlady为性别对应的近义关系。

definitions.example: The landlord signed a one-year rental agreement with the new tenant.

landlord.etymology: 合成词，由「land（房产、地产）」和「lord（主人、领主）」组合而成，指管理房产的男性。

similar_words.description: 「landlady」指女房东或女店主，与landlord为性别对应的近义关系。

definitions.example: Cyclists are advised to ride in the designated bicycle lane to avoid traffic risks.

lane.etymology: 源自古英语「lān」，原指狭窄的田间小径，后引申为各类狭窄通道。

similar_words.description: 「line」指线条、线路或队列，lane指狭窄的小路、车道或通道。

definitions.example: The red lanterns hanging along the street added a festive atmosphere to the town.

lantern.etymology: 源自拉丁语「lanterna」，指带灯罩的照明工具，后传入英语沿用至今。

similar_words.description: 「lamp」指普通电灯、油灯等照明设备，lantern指带罩的灯笼或提灯。

definitions.example: She rested her chin on her hands while a cat curled up on her lap.
The runner accelerated on the last lap to overtake his opponent.

lap.etymology: 源自古英语「læppa」，原指衣服下摆，后引申为坐时的大腿部，再引申为赛道的一圈。

similar_words.description: 「lip」指嘴唇，lap指坐时的大腿部或赛道的一圈，二者形近易混。

definitions.example: A momentary lapse of concentration caused him to mispronounce the key word in the speech.
The visa will lapse if the holder fails to enter the country within 90 days.

lapse.etymology: 源自拉丁语「lapsus（滑落、跌倒）」，引申为行为上的失误，后扩展到权利等的失效。

similar_words.description: 二者同根（laps=滑落），「elapse」指（时间）流逝，lapse指失误或（权利等）失效。

definitions.meaning: 笔记本电脑；便携式电脑
definitions.example: Many graduate students use a laptop to process experimental data and write papers.

laptop.etymology: 合成词，由「lap（坐时的大腿部）」和「top（顶部）」组合而成，指可放在腿上使用的便携式电脑。

similar_words.description: 「desktop」指台式电脑，laptop指笔记本电脑，二者为功能相近的电子设备。

definitions.example: The improvement of students' academic performance is largely attributed to effective teaching methods.

largely.etymology: 派生词，由「large（大的）」加副词后缀「-ly」构成，表程度。

similar_words.description: 「lately」指最近、近来，largely指主要地、很大程度上，二者形近易混。

definitions.example: Laser technology plays a crucial role in modern medical surgery and precision manufacturing.

laser.etymology: 首字母缩写词，全称为「Light Amplification by Stimulated Emission of Radiation」，指受激辐射放大的光。

similar_words.description: 「razor」指剃须刀，laser指激光或激光器，二者形近易混。

definitions.example: The strong wind lashed against the windows, making a loud rattling sound.
She applied mascara to make her lashes look longer and thicker.

lash.etymology: 源自古英语「læscian（鞭打）」，后引申为风、雨的猛击，又指类似鞭状的睫毛。

similar_words.description: 「slash」指劈砍、削减，lash指猛击或睫毛，二者形近易混。

definitions.example: Lately, environmental researchers have focused on the impact of microplastics on freshwater ecosystems.

lately.etymology: 由形容词late加副词后缀-ly构成，表时间维度的近期状态

similar_words.description: 两者拼写接近，但「lately」指从过去到当前的近期时间，「later」指时间上的稍后、之后

definitions.example: The study aims to explore the latent risks of long-term social isolation among young adults.

latent.etymology: 源自拉丁语latere（隐藏），加形容词后缀-ent构成，表隐藏未显现的状态

similar_words.description: 两者拼写接近，但「latent」表隐藏的潜在属性，「lateral」表方位的侧面或横向

definitions.example: Later, the research team revised their experiment design to reduce systematic measurement errors.

later.etymology: 形容词late的比较级形式，表时间上的先后关系

similar_words.description: 两者拼写接近，但「later」指时间上的稍后或过去某节点之后，「lately」指从过去到当前的近期

definitions.meaning: 侧面的，横向的（学术/商务语境）
definitions.example: The company adopted a lateral management strategy to enhance cross-departmental communication and cooperation.

lateral.etymology: 源自拉丁语latus（侧面），加形容词后缀-al构成，表方位相关的属性

similar_words.description: 两者拼写接近，但「lateral」表方位的侧面或横向，「latent」表隐藏的潜在属性

definitions.meaning: 拉丁语；拉丁语的，拉丁（人）的
definitions.example: Many technical terms in biology, law and medicine are derived from Latin roots.

Latin.etymology: 源自古法语Latin，最终源自拉丁语Latinus，指与古罗马相关的语言或族群

similar_words.description: 两者拼写开头接近，但「Latin」指拉丁语或拉丁相关的，「latitude」指纬度或行动自由度

definitions.example: This endangered bird species is distributed across a wide range of latitude in the Northern Hemisphere.

definitions.meaning: （行动、言论的）回旋余地，自由度
definitions.example: The new education policy gives primary school teachers more latitude in designing personalized teaching plans.

latitude.etymology: 源自拉丁语latitudo（宽度），由latus（宽的）加名词后缀-tudo构成，引申为空间或权限的宽度

similar_words.description: 两者拼写接近，但「latitude」指纬度或行动自由度，「attitude」指人的态度或看法

definitions.example: Of the two data analysis methods proposed in the paper, the latter is more suitable for small-sample studies.

latter.etymology: 源自古英语lætra，为late的比较级变体，特指两者中的后一个

similar_words.description: 两者拼写接近，但「latter」特指两者中的后者，「later」指时间上的稍后或之后

definitions.example: The sound of children’s laughter from the nearby playground interrupted the quiet atmosphere of the research lab.

laughter.etymology: 源自古英语hleahtor，与动词laugh同源，表笑的动作或声音的名词形式

similar_words.description: 两者拼写接近，但「laughter」指笑声，「slaughter」指屠杀或屠宰

definitions.meaning: 推出（产品、计划）；发动（活动）
definitions.example: The university will launch a new online course on climate change mitigation for undergraduate students next semester.
The space agency plans to launch a new satellite to monitor ocean acidification by the end of the year.

launch.etymology: 源自古法语lancher（投掷），最终源自拉丁语lanceare（用矛刺），引申为推出或发射的动作

similar_words.description: 两者拼写接近，但「launch」表推出、发射，「lunch」指午餐

definitions.example: The on-campus apartment building has a shared laundry room equipped with washing machines and dryers for all residents.

laundry.etymology: 源自古法语lavanderie，源自拉丁语lavare（洗），表与洗衣相关的场所或物品

similar_words.description: 两者拼写开头接近，易拼错；「laundry」指洗衣店或要洗的衣物，「launch」表推出、发射

definitions.example: Many university dormitories are equipped with shared lavatories and laundry rooms.

lavatory.etymology: 来自拉丁语「lavare」（洗） + 后缀「-atory」（表场所），指用于洗漱的场所

similar_words.description: 「lavatory」指盥洗室，「laboratory」指实验室，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: Every citizen must abide by the laws enacted by the national legislature.
Newton’s laws of motion are fundamental to classical physics.

law.etymology: 来自古英语「lagu」，原指具有约束力的规则

similar_words.description: 「law」指国家制定的正式法律，「rule」指通用规则（含非正式）

definitions.example: The university’s central lawn is a popular spot for students to relax between classes.

lawn.etymology: 来自古法语「launde」，原指荒地，后引申为修整过的草地

similar_words.description: 「lawn」指草坪，「lane」指小巷、车道，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: Many law school graduates aim to become corporate lawyers specializing in intellectual property.

lawyer.etymology: 来自「law」（法律） + 后缀「-yer」（表从事某职业的人）

similar_words.description: 「lawyer」为通用律师称呼，「attorney」尤指被授权代理当事人的律师（多用于美式英语）

definitions.example: Workers are laying new asphalt on the main road leading to the campus.
The report was written for lay readers rather than medical experts.

lay.etymology: 来自古英语「lecgan」（放置），形容词义由「非神职的」引申为「非专业的」

similar_words.description: 「lay」为及物动词（放置）或形容词（非专业的）；「lie」为不及物动词（躺、说谎），其过去式为「lay」，二者易混淆

definitions.example: The archaeological team found a layer of ancient pottery fragments beneath the topsoil.

layer.etymology: 来自「lay」（铺设） + 后缀「-er」（表事物），指铺设形成的层级

similar_words.description: 「layer」指层，「ladder」指梯子，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The book explains complex economic theories in language accessible to the layman.

layman.etymology: 来自「lay」（非专业的） + 「man」（人），指非专业领域的人

similar_words.description: 「layman」指对某专业完全陌生的外行，「amateur」指有一定兴趣但非职业的业余爱好者

definitions.example: The tech company announced a large-scale layoff due to declining market demand.

layoff.etymology: 来自动词短语「lay off」（解雇）的名词化形式，尤指企业大规模裁员

similar_words.description: 「layoff」尤指企业因经营问题的大规模裁员，「dismissal」指一般解雇（含个人违规等原因）

definitions.example: The architect adjusted the layout of the office building to improve natural lighting.

layout.etymology: 来自动词短语「lay out」（安排、布置）的名词化形式，指事物的排布设计

similar_words.description: 「layout」指具体的布局、排版，「blueprint」指总体规划、蓝图

definitions.example: Lazy students often struggle to meet the deadlines for their research papers.

lazy.etymology: 来自中古英语「lasie」，原指松懈、无力，后引申为懒惰的

similar_words.description: 「lazy」指主观上不愿劳作的懒惰，「idle」指无事可做的空闲（含贬义时指懒散）

definitions.example: The research team’s breakthrough led to a new approach to treating diabetes, which was published in a top medical journal.
Regulatory agencies have set strict limits on lead content in children’s toys to protect their physical health.

lead.etymology: 源自古英语lǣdan（引导），无派生词缀，为基础多义词，兼具动词与名词用法

similar_words.description: 「load」为动词/名词“装载；负载”，与「lead」形近易拼错
「guide」侧重“指引方向、提供指导”，「lead」侧重“主导、带领他人或事物”

definitions.example: Strong leadership is essential for a non-profit organization to mobilize resources and fulfill its social mission.

leadership.etymology: 由「leader（领导者）」+名词后缀「-ship（表身份、能力、状态）」构成，表抽象的领导能力或具体的领导群体

similar_words.description: 「management」侧重“企业或组织的日常管理（实务层面）”，「leadership」侧重“领导能力或战略引领（能力层面）”
「governance」侧重“制度层面的治理”，「leadership」侧重“人的领导行为或能力”

definitions.example: Climate change is a leading factor contributing to the decline of biodiversity in tropical rainforests.

leading.etymology: 由「lead（领导）」+现在分词后缀「-ing（表属性、特征）」构成，作形容词表“处于领先或核心地位的”

similar_words.description: 「foremost」语气更强，表“首要的、最顶尖的”，「leading」表“领先的、主要的”
「leaden」为形容词“铅制的；沉重的”，与「leading」形近易混

definitions.example: Deciduous trees shed their leaves in winter to conserve water and energy.
The rare 18th-century book has a torn leaf, which reduces its collection value significantly.

leaf.etymology: 源自古英语lēaf（叶子），无派生词缀，为基础名词，后引申指书籍的“一张纸”

similar_words.description: 「leaves」为「leaf」的名词复数（表“叶子”），而「leafs」为动词「leaf（快速翻页）」的第三人称单数，形近易混
「page」指书籍的“单面页”，「leaf」指“一张（两面）”

definitions.example: The local health bureau distributed leaflets to residents to promote COVID-19 vaccination awareness.

leaflet.etymology: 由「leaf（纸张、页）」+指小后缀「-let（表小型事物）」构成，原指“小叶子”，后引申为“薄的传单或宣传页”

similar_words.description: 「brochure」指“较厚的、装订成册的宣传册”，「leaflet」指“薄的、单页或折叠的传单”
「leafed」为形容词“有叶子的”，与「leaflet」形近易拼错

definitions.example: The confidential government document leaked to the press, triggering a nationwide political scandal.
The pipeline leak has caused severe environmental pollution to the surrounding farmland and water sources.

leak.etymology: 源自古英语leccan（使潮湿），无派生词缀，为基础多义词，兼具动词与名词用法

similar_words.description: 「leakage」为名词“泄漏（量或行为）”，「leak」可作动词或名词
「lean」为动词/形容词“倾斜；瘦的”，与「leak」形近易拼错

definitions.example: The lean management model helps enterprises reduce unnecessary costs and improve operational efficiency.
During the lean years of the global financial crisis, many small businesses went bankrupt.

lean.etymology: 源自古英语hlǣne（瘦的），无派生词缀，为基础多义词，兼具形容词与动词用法

similar_words.description: 「thin」泛指“瘦的”，「lean」侧重“健康的瘦、精干的（人或组织）”
「leak」为动词/名词“泄漏”，与「lean」形近易混

definitions.meaning: 跳跃；（数量、水平）激增；飞跃
definitions.example: The number of electric vehicle sales leapt by 60% in 2023, driven by government subsidies and technological advances.

leap.etymology: 源自古英语hlēapan（跳跃），无派生词缀，为基础多义词，兼具动词与名词用法

similar_words.description: 「jump」泛指“普通的跳跃”，「leap」侧重“大的、有力的跳跃或（数量、水平的）大幅增长”
「lead」为动词/名词“领导；铅”，与「leap」形近易拼错

definitions.example: The learned scholar has published over 50 papers in top international academic journals in the field of linguistics.

learned.etymology: 由「learn（学习）」+过去分词后缀「-ed（表属性）」构成，作形容词时发音为/ˈlɜːnɪd/，与动词过去式「learned（/lɜːnd/，表“学习过”）」形同音义不同

similar_words.description: 「scholarly」侧重“具有学者风范的、学术的”，「learned」侧重“博学的（人）或学术性的（出版物）”
动词过去式「learned」发音为/lɜːnd/，表“学习的过去动作”；形容词「learned」发音为/ˈlɜːnɪd/，表“博学的”，形同音义不同

definitions.example: Lifelong learning is a core competence for individuals to adapt to the rapidly changing digital era.

learning.etymology: 由「learn（学习）」+动名词后缀「-ing（表行为、状态）」构成，表学习的过程或积累的学识

similar_words.description: 「earning」为名词“收入，收益”，与「learning」形近易拼错
「knowledge」侧重“已掌握的具体知识”，「learning」侧重“学习的过程或积累的系统学识”

definitions.example: The local government leases the vacant industrial factory to a high-tech startup focused on renewable energy.
The two companies signed a 10-year lease for the office space in the central business district.

lease.etymology: 源自拉丁语「laxare（放松、放宽）」，原指放宽物品的使用权，后引申为租赁、租约相关含义

similar_words.description: 拼写相近，lease表「租赁/租约」，least表「最少的（地）」

definitions.example: Among all the poverty alleviation plans proposed, this one requires the least financial input and has the highest feasibility.

least.etymology: 源自古英语「lǣst」，为形容词「little」的最高级形式

similar_words.description: 拼写相近，least表「最少的（地）」，lease表「租赁/租约」

definitions.example: The premium luggage brand selects full-grain leather as its core material to ensure durability and elegance.

leather.etymology: 源自古英语「lether」，与德语「Leder」同源，指经加工的动物皮革

similar_words.description: 拼写相近，leather表「皮革/皮革制品」，leisure表「闲暇/空闲」

definitions.example: The Nobel Prize winner delivered a 90-minute lecture on quantum computing at the university’s centennial celebration.

lecture.etymology: 源自拉丁语「legere（讲、读）」，原指公开宣读的内容，后引申为讲座、演讲

similar_words.description: 同根词（源自拉丁语「legere（讲/读）」），lecture表「公开讲座/演讲」，legend表「传说/传奇」

definitions.example: The 2008 global financial crisis left a lasting legacy of stricter financial supervision worldwide.

legacy.etymology: 源自拉丁语「legare（遗赠）」，原指遗留的财产，后引申为遗留影响、遗产

similar_words.description: 近义，legacy侧重「遗赠的遗产或遗留影响」，heritage侧重「国家/民族的文化遗产」

definitions.example: Any enterprise that violates legal provisions on environmental protection will face severe administrative penalties.

legal.etymology: 源自拉丁语「lex（法律）」，加形容词后缀「-al」，表「与法律相关的」

similar_words.description: 同根词（源自拉丁语「lex（法律）」），legal侧重「符合法律条文的」，legitimate侧重「合法且合理的/正当的」

definitions.meaning: 传说；传奇人物；传奇故事
definitions.example: The ancient Chinese legend of 'Chang'e Flying to the Moon' has been adapted into various artistic works.

legend.etymology: 源自拉丁语「legenda（需宣读的内容）」，来自「legere（讲、读）」，后引申为传说、传奇

similar_words.description: 同根词（源自拉丁语「legere（讲/读）」），legend表「传说/传奇」，lecture表「公开讲座/演讲」

definitions.example: The National People’s Congress passed a new piece of legislation to strengthen the protection of personal information online.

legislation.etymology: 源自拉丁语「lex（法律）+latio（带来）」，字面义为「带来法律」，指立法过程或制定的法规

similar_words.description: 近义，legislation侧重「立法过程或制定的法规体系」，law侧重「具体的法律条文或法律整体」

definitions.example: Only legitimate claims for intellectual property rights will be accepted by the court.
The government took measures to legitimate the informal employment of rural migrant workers.

legitimate.etymology: 源自拉丁语「legitimus（合法的）」，来自「lex（法律）」，加后缀「-imate」表「具有…性质的」

similar_words.description: 同根词（源自拉丁语「lex（法律）」），legitimate侧重「合法且合理的/正当的」，legal侧重「符合法律条文的」

definitions.example: The rise of the sharing economy has provided people with more flexible ways to spend their leisure time.

leisure.etymology: 源自拉丁语「licere（被允许）」，原指被允许的空闲时间，后引申为闲暇、悠闲

similar_words.description: 拼写相近，leisure表「闲暇/空闲」，leather表「皮革/皮革制品」

definitions.example: Many nutritionists recommend adding a slice of lemon to warm water for daily vitamin C supplementation.
The second-hand laptop he purchased online turned out to be a lemon, which crashed within three days.

lemon.etymology: 源自中古英语lemon，借自古法语limon，最终源自阿拉伯语laymūn，无考研核心可拆解词根

similar_words.description: 「lime」指酸橙，体积较小，味道更酸；「lemon」指普通柠檬，果皮多为亮黄色

definitions.example: The Great Wall has a total length of over 21,000 kilometers according to official national statistics.

length.etymology: 由形容词「long」派生，词根「long-」表“长”，后缀「-th」为名词后缀，表“性质、状态”

similar_words.description: 「width」指宽度（横向维度）；「length」指长度（纵向维度），二者均为表物理维度的核心名词

definitions.example: A high-precision lens is crucial for capturing clear microscopic images in scientific research.

lens.etymology: 源自拉丁语lens（原指扁豆，因透镜形状类似），无考研核心可拆解词根

similar_words.description: 「less」为限定词/副词，表“更少的（地）”；「lens」为名词，表“透镜、镜片”，拼写易混淆

definitions.example: Postgraduate candidates are advised to spend less time on rote memorization and more on logical thinking.

less.etymology: 源自古英语lǣssa（更少的），词根「les-」表“小、少”，为考研核心高频词根

similar_words.description: 「fewer」修饰可数名词复数；「less」修饰不可数名词、形容词或副词比较级，为考研语法高频考点

definitions.example: He double-checked the exam schedule lest he (should) miss the postgraduate entrance examination.

lest.etymology: 源自古英语短语「thǣ lǣs the」（以免）的缩合形式，无考研核心可拆解词根

similar_words.description: 「in case」后多接陈述语气；「lest」为书面语连词，后接(should)+动词原形的虚拟语气，为考研完形高频考点

definitions.example: The overall academic level of domestic postgraduate education has risen steadily in the past decade.

level.etymology: 源自拉丁语libella（水平仪），词根「lib-」表“平衡、水平”（同根词如liberty）

similar_words.description: 「rank」指具体的职位、军衔或排名等级；「level」指抽象的水平、标准或物理的平坦状态

definitions.example: Engineers used a heavy metal lever to lift the damaged bridge beam for repair.

lever.etymology: 源自拉丁语levare（举起），词根「lev-」表“举起、轻”（同根考研词如elevate、relieve）

similar_words.description: 「level」表“水平、等级”；「lever」表“杠杆、撬动工具”，二者拼写仅最后一个字母不同（lever为r，level为l），极易混淆

definitions.example: The local government plans to levy a carbon tax on high-emission enterprises to promote green development.

levy.etymology: 源自拉丁语levare（举起、筹集），词根「lev-」表“举起、筹集”（同根考研词如lever、elevate）

similar_words.description: 「tax」为普通用词，泛指“税”或“征税”；「levy」为正式书面语，侧重官方强制征收（税款、罚款等），多用于考研经济类阅读

definitions.example: The manufacturing company refused to take liability for the product defects reported by consumers.
Excessive liability has forced the start-up company to suspend its overseas expansion plan.

liability.etymology: 由形容词「liable」派生，词根「li-」表“捆绑、约束”（同根考研词如oblige、rely），后缀「-ability」为名词后缀，表“性质、状态”

similar_words.description: 「responsibility」侧重道德、职务上的一般责任；「liability」侧重法律、经济上的责任或债务，为考研法律/商务类核心词汇

definitions.example: If you violate the labor contract, you will be liable for the financial losses caused to the employer.
Elderly people with chronic diseases are liable to suffer from seasonal flu outbreaks.

liable.etymology: 词根「li-」表“捆绑、约束”，后缀「-able」为形容词后缀，表“可…的”，原义为“被约束（负责）的”

similar_words.description: 「likely」侧重中性的可能性；「liable」表“可能的”时侧重易发生负面事件，或表法律上的“有责任的”，为考研法律类高频考点

definitions.example: Many liberal educators advocate for student-centered learning approaches in higher education.

liberal.etymology: 源自拉丁语liber（自由）加形容词后缀-al，表「与自由相关的」

similar_words.description: 形近易混，'liberal'表自由的、开明的，'literal'表字面的、逐字的

definitions.example: The social movement aimed to liberate marginalized groups from systemic discrimination.

liberate.etymology: 源自拉丁语liber（自由）加动词后缀-ate，表「使…获得自由」

similar_words.description: 同根易混，'liberate'为动词表解放，'liberal'为形容词表开明的、自由的

definitions.example: The national constitution guarantees citizens the liberty of speech and peaceful assembly.

liberty.etymology: 源自拉丁语liber（自由）加名词后缀-ty，表「自由的状态或权利」

similar_words.description: 近义易混，'liberty'侧重法律或制度赋予的自由权，'freedom'侧重泛指的不受束缚的状态

definitions.example: The university librarian helped the graduate student locate rare academic journals in the archive.

librarian.etymology: 由library（图书馆）加表人的后缀-ian构成，指从事图书馆管理的人员

similar_words.description: 形近易混，'librarian'指图书馆管理员，'liberian'指利比里亚人或利比里亚的

definitions.example: Drivers must carry a valid license when operating vehicles on public roads.
Excessive artistic license may distort historical facts in biographical films.

license.etymology: 源自拉丁语licere（被允许），后演变为表许可凭证或许可行为的词汇

similar_words.description: 拼写变体，'license'为美式拼写，'licence'为英式拼写，二者语义完全相同

definitions.example: The laboratory cat licked its injured paw gently during the behavioral observation.
The wildfire licked the dry pine trees along the national park boundary.

lick.etymology: 源自古英语liccian，本义为用舌头触碰

similar_words.description: 形近易混，'lick'表舔、吞卷，'tick'表滴答声、打钩或蜱虫

definitions.example: The researcher lifted the lid of the petri dish to add the bacterial culture sample.

lid.etymology: 源自古英语hlid，指覆盖容器的物体

similar_words.description: 形近易混，'lid'表盖子、眼睑，'lip'表嘴唇

definitions.example: The Nobel Prize winner devoted her lifetime to the research of renewable energy sources.

lifetime.etymology: 复合词，由life（生命）和time（时间）构成，表生命的整个时长

similar_words.description: 近义易混，'lifetime'侧重人的终身经历或期限，'lifespan'侧重生物的寿命时长

definitions.meaning: 举起；抬起；解除（禁令、限制等）
definitions.example: The local authority announced to lift the indoor mask mandate starting next Monday.
We took the lift to the 12th floor to attend the academic seminar on climate change.

lift.etymology: 源自古英语lyft（空气），原指借助空气举起，后引申为各种举起、提升义

similar_words.description: 近义易混，'lift'为及物动词，需接宾语；'rise'为不及物动词，表自身升起

definitions.example: The sudden lightning strike disrupted the satellite communication system during the storm.

lightning.etymology: 由light（光）加后缀-ning构成，指天空中突发的强光放电现象

similar_words.description: 形近易混，'lightning'指闪电，'lighting'指照明、灯光设计

definitions.example: Researchers are analyzing the likelihood of antibiotic resistance spreading among livestock populations.

likelihood.etymology: 由形容词「likely」加名词后缀「-hood」构成，表「状态、性质」

similar_words.description: 「likeness」意为「相似、相像」，「likelihood」意为「可能性」，拼写接近易混淆

definitions.example: A likely explanation for the decline in bee populations is the overuse of pesticides in agricultural areas.

likely.etymology: 由形容词「like」加后缀「-ly」构成，原表「相似的」，后引申为「可能的」

similar_words.description: 二者均表「有…倾向的」，「liable」侧重「易遭受（不利情况）或有法律责任的」，「likely」侧重「（事件）大概率发生的」

definitions.example: Regular exercise improves cardiovascular health; likewise, a balanced diet contributes to overall well-being.

likewise.etymology: 由形容词「like」加副词后缀「-wise」构成，表「以相似的方式」

similar_words.description: 二者均表「同样地」，「likewise」更正式，多用于书面论述，符合考研阅读语体

definitions.example: The medical team developed a new prosthetic device to help patients with injured upper limbs regain mobility.
The storm damaged several large limbs of the ancient banyan tree in the university’s botanical garden.

limb.etymology: 源自古英语「lim」，原指「人体或动物的肢体」，后引申为「树枝」

similar_words.description: 「limp」作动词/形容词表「跛行；松软的」，「limb」作名词表「肢体；树枝」，拼写形近易混淆

definitions.example: The environmental agency has imposed a strict limit on the amount of industrial waste discharged into the river.
To ensure the validity of the study, researchers must limit their sample to participants aged 18 to 25.

limit.etymology: 源自拉丁语「limes」，意为「边界、界限」，后传入英语指「限制」

similar_words.description: 二者均表「限制」，「limit」侧重「设定具体的数量、范围界限」，「restrict」侧重「通过规则、法令约束」

definitions.meaning: 局限，限制（指事物本身的不足或制约因素）
definitions.example: A major limitation of this research model is its failure to consider the impact of socioeconomic factors.

limitation.etymology: 由动词「limit」加名词后缀「-ation」构成，表「限制的行为或结果」

similar_words.description: 「limit」侧重「人为设定的具体界限（如数量、时间）」，「limitation」侧重「事物固有的不足或客观制约」

definitions.example: Due to limited research funding, the team could only conduct a preliminary investigation into the phenomenon.

limited.etymology: 由动词「limit」加形容词后缀「-ed」构成，表「被限制的状态」

similar_words.description: 二者均表「受限制的」，「limited」侧重「数量、范围等客观有限」，「restricted」侧重「因规定、权限而被限制」

definitions.example: After spraining his ankle during the basketball match, the player had to limp off the court.
The wet cardboard became limp and could no longer support the weight of the books.

limp.etymology: 源自中古英语「limpen」，原指「蹒跚行走」，后引申为「松软无力的」

similar_words.description: 「limb」作名词表「肢体；树枝」，「limp」作动词/形容词表「跛行；松软的」，拼写形近易混淆

definitions.example: Students are required to write their answers on the blank line provided beneath each question.
She has accumulated rich experience in the line of digital marketing over the past decade.

line.etymology: 源自古英语「line」，原指「绳、线」，后引申出「文本行、行业」等多个义项

similar_words.description: 二者均可表「行」，「row」侧重「横向的一排（人/物）」，「line」侧重「纵向的线或连续的文本行」

definitions.example: The study revealed a linear correlation between the number of study hours and the final exam scores of graduate students.

linear.etymology: 由名词「line」加形容词后缀「-ar」构成，表「与线相关的」

similar_words.description: 「lineal」意为「直系的、嫡系的」，「linear」意为「线性的、直线的」，拼写形近易混淆

definitions.example: The eco-friendly bedding is made of organic linen sourced from northern European farms.

linen.etymology: 来自古英语「lin」（亚麻），原指亚麻纤维织成的布料

similar_words.description: 两者拼写接近，「linen」为名词表亚麻布，「liner」为名词表班轮/衬里

definitions.example: The luxury ocean liner set sail for Southampton with over 1,500 passengers aboard.

liner.etymology: 来自「line」（航线/线条）+名词后缀「-er」，表沿航线行驶的船或起衬垫作用的物品

similar_words.description: 两者拼写接近，「liner」为名词表班轮/衬里，「linen」为名词表亚麻布

definitions.example: Many art lovers linger in the museum's Renaissance gallery to admire masterpieces by Da Vinci.

linger.etymology: 来自古英语「leng」（长）+动词后缀「-er」，原指延长停留时间

similar_words.description: 两者为近义词，「linger」侧重因留恋而逗留，「dawdle」侧重因懒散而拖延

definitions.example: The research paper analyzes the linguistic features of academic writing in STEM fields.

linguistic.etymology: 来自拉丁语词根「lingu-」（舌头，引申为语言）+形容词后缀「-istic」，表与语言相关的

similar_words.description: 「linguistic」为形容词，「linguistics」为派生名词，表语言学这一学科

definitions.meaning: v. 连接，联系；n. 环节，联系
definitions.example: Epidemiologists have linked air pollution to increased rates of respiratory diseases.

link.etymology: 来自古英语「hlinc」（环），原指链条中的一环，引申为连接、联系

similar_words.description: 两者为近义词，「link」侧重事物间的逻辑或物理关联，「connect」侧重将分离的事物连为一体

definitions.example: The lion is widely regarded as a symbol of royalty and courage in European folklore.

lion.etymology: 来自拉丁语「leo」、希腊语「leon」，原指大型猫科动物狮子

similar_words.description: 两者拼写接近，「lion」为名词表狮子，「line」为名词/动词表线/排列

definitions.example: She pressed her lips together to hold back tears during the emotional speech.

lip.etymology: 来自古英语「lippa」，原指人或动物的嘴唇

similar_words.description: 两者拼写接近，「lip」为名词表嘴唇/边缘，「lap」为名词/动词表大腿部/舔食

definitions.meaning: adj. 液体的；流畅的；n. 液体
definitions.example: The spacecraft's cooling system relies on a special liquid that remains stable in extreme temperatures.

liquid.etymology: 来自拉丁语「liquere」（流动），原指具有流动性的物质

similar_words.description: 两者为近义词，「liquid」侧重物质的液态状态，「fluid」可指液体或气体，更侧重流动性

definitions.example: The city council passed a law banning the sale of liquor after 10 PM in residential areas.

liquor.etymology: 来自拉丁语「liquor」（液体），与「liquid」同根，后特指含酒精的烈性液体

similar_words.description: 两者同根，「liquor」为名词表烈性酒，「liquid」为名词/形容词表液体（的）

definitions.meaning: n. 列表，清单；v. 列出，列举
definitions.example: The professor distributed a list of required textbooks for the spring semester.
The fishing boat listed to port after a large wave crashed over its starboard side.

list.etymology: 来自古英语「liste」（边缘，条状物），原指写在纸条上的条目，后引申为列表；其「倾斜」义来自中古英语的变体用法

similar_words.description: 两者拼写接近，「list」为名词/动词表列表/倾斜，「last」为形容词/动词表最后的/持续

definitions.example: The government has launched a nationwide campaign to improve adult literacy rates.

literacy.etymology: 词根「liter」（文字、字母）+ 名词后缀「-acy」（表性质、状态），指具有读写能力的状态

similar_words.description: 「literacy」为名词，指读写能力；「literate」为形容词，指识字的、有读写能力的

definitions.example: Do not take his sarcastic remark literally, for he did not mean it seriously.

literally.etymology: 形容词「literal」（字面的）+ 副词后缀「-ly」，表按照字面的方式

similar_words.description: 「literally」为副词，表逐字地、确实地；「literary」为形容词，表文学的

definitions.example: The professor specializes in 19th-century British literary criticism.

literary.etymology: 词根「liter」（文字、字母）+ 形容词后缀「-ary」（表与…相关的），指与文字、文学相关的

similar_words.description: 「literary」为形容词，表文学的；「literally」为副词，表逐字地

definitions.example: In many developing countries, more than half the population is now literate.

literate.etymology: 词根「liter」（文字、字母）+ 形容词后缀「-ate」（表具有…性质的），指具有读写能力的

similar_words.description: 「literate」为形容词，表识字的；「literacy」为名词，表读写能力

definitions.example: Researchers reviewed existing literature to support their experimental findings.

literature.etymology: 词根「liter」（文字、字母）+ 名词后缀「-ature」（表集合、状态），指文字作品的集合或学术研究的文献集合

similar_words.description: 「literature」为名词，表文学、文献；「literary」为形容词，表文学的

definitions.example: The new fuel-efficient sedan consumes only 5 litres of gasoline per 100 kilometers.

litre.etymology: 源自法语「litre」，最初来自希腊语「litra」（一种重量单位），后演变为容量单位

similar_words.description: 「litre」为名词，表容量单位升；「litter」为名词/动词，表垃圾、乱扔（垃圾）

definitions.example: Hikers are strictly reminded not to litter or leave any litter in the national park.

litter.etymology: 源自古英语「litter」（铺床的稻草），后引申为乱扔的杂物、垃圾

similar_words.description: 「litter」为名词/动词，表垃圾、乱扔；「litre」为名词，表容量单位升

definitions.example: The keynote speaker gave a lively presentation that kept the entire audience engaged.

lively.etymology: 动词「live」（活）+ 形容词后缀「-ly」（表具有…性质的），指具有活力的、生动的

similar_words.description: 「lively」为形容词，表活泼的、生动的；「living」为形容词/名词，表活着的、生计

definitions.example: Excessive alcohol consumption can cause irreversible damage to the human liver.

liver.etymology: 源自古英语「lifer」，与德语「Leber」（肝脏）同源，是维持生命的重要器官

similar_words.description: 「liver」为名词，表肝脏；「river」为名词，表河流（拼写仅首字母不同，易混淆）

definitions.example: Earning a stable living has become a top priority for many recent college graduates.

living.etymology: 动词「live」（活）+ 后缀「-ing」（构成形容词或名词），形容词义表处于活着的状态，名词义表维持生活的方式

similar_words.description: 「living」为形容词/名词，表活着的、生计；「lively」为形容词，表活泼的、生动的

definitions.example: The cargo ship was loaded with medical supplies bound for the earthquake-hit region.
Graduate students usually shoulder a heavier academic load than undergraduate students.

load.etymology: 源自古英语lad，表“负荷、行程”，无复杂衍生词缀，核心义与“承载重物”相关

similar_words.description: 「load」侧重承载动作或负荷量，「loaf」多作名词表一条面包或动词表游荡
「load」表承载相关义，「loan」表借贷（钱/物）相关义

definitions.example: The elderly lady bought a loaf of rye bread from the corner bakery every Saturday.
Instead of loafing around, unemployed youths should participate in vocational training courses.

loaf.etymology: 源自古英语hlaf，表“面包”，动词义由“像吃面包般懒散度日”引申而来

similar_words.description: 「loaf」表面包或游荡，「load」表装载或负载
「loaf」侧重无目的懒散游荡，「wander」侧重无固定方向的漫游

definitions.example: The central bank has cut interest rates to encourage small businesses to apply for loans.
The university library loans out rare academic books only to authorized researchers.

loan.etymology: 源自古北方法语loan，表“借贷”，与英语单词lend（借出）同源

similar_words.description: 「loan」表借贷相关义，「load」表承载相关义
「loan」多作名词或正式动词表借出，「lend」侧重口语化的借出动作

definitions.example: International scholars gathered in the hotel lobby to discuss cross-cultural research projects.
Environmental organizations lobbied the European Parliament to ban single-use plastics.

lobby.etymology: 源自中古拉丁语lobbyium，表“门廊”，先引申为“建筑物入口大厅”，再因游说者常在议会大厅活动，引申出“游说”义

similar_words.description: 「lobby」表大厅或游说，「lodge」表小屋或寄宿
「lobby」侧重建筑物入口处的小型大厅，「hall」侧重较大的礼堂或走廊

definitions.example: The local government has launched a campaign to protect the city's historical buildings.
Locals often know the best hidden restaurants that tourists rarely discover.

local.etymology: 源自拉丁语localis，由词根locus（地方）加形容词后缀-al构成，表“与地方相关的”

similar_words.description: 「local」多作形容词表当地的，「locale」作名词表事件发生的特定场所
「local」侧重小范围的本地，「regional」侧重较大范围的地区

definitions.example: Archaeologists found prehistoric cave paintings in a remote locality of the Andes Mountains.

locality.etymology: 源自拉丁语localitas，由词根locus（地方）加名词后缀-ity构成，表“特定的地方或区域”

similar_words.description: 「locality」侧重某一特定区域或地带，「location」侧重具体可确定的位置或场所
「locality」是名词表地点，「local」多作形容词表当地的

definitions.example: Rescue teams used GPS technology to locate the missing climbers in the snowy mountains.
The new national museum is located in the central business district of the capital.
