locate.etymology: 源自拉丁语locare（放置），由词根locus（地方）加动词后缀-ate构成，表“放置在某位置”

similar_words.description: 「locate」表定位或位于，「allocate」表分配或划拨（资源、资金等）
「locate」侧重主动找位置或设立，「situate」侧重描述某物所处的位置状态

definitions.example: The film director selected a quiet coastal town as the main shooting location for the movie.

location.etymology: 源自拉丁语locatio，由词根locus（地方）加动词后缀-ate的名词形式-ion构成，表“放置的位置”

similar_words.description: 「location」侧重具体可确定的位置或场所，「locality」侧重某一特定区域或地带
「location」侧重物理空间的位置，「position」可表职位、姿态或抽象位置

definitions.example: Please make sure to lock the laboratory door after finishing the experiment.
The trade negotiations locked over the tariff issue, leading to a three-month suspension.

lock.etymology: 源自古英语loc，表“锁、门闩”，动词义由“用锁固定”引申出“卡住”义

similar_words.description: 「lock」表锁或卡住的动作，「locker」表带锁的储物柜
「lock」侧重用锁固定或机械性卡住，「block」侧重堵塞或阻碍

definitions.example: Students can store their sports equipment in the school gym lockers during class.

locker.etymology: 由单词lock（锁）加名词后缀-er构成，表“带锁的柜子”

similar_words.description: 「locker」是带锁的储物柜，「lock」表锁或锁的动作
「locker」侧重公共场合的带锁寄存柜，「cabinet」侧重家用或办公用的橱柜

definitions.example: Early steam locomotives revolutionized long-distance land transportation in the 19th century, laying the foundation for modern railway networks.

locomotive.etymology: 由词根「loco-（地方）」+「mot-（移动）」+名词后缀「-ive」构成，原指能在不同地方移动的机器，后特指机车、火车头

similar_words.description: 同根名词，locomotive指机车、火车头，locomotion指生物的运动、移动能力

definitions.example: Many visiting international scholars lodge at the university's designated guest house during their short-term research collaborations.
The consumer protection association lodged a formal lawsuit against the e-commerce platform for false advertising.

lodge.etymology: 源自古法语「loge（小屋、棚屋）」，引申为临时住宿，后又抽象化为正式提出申诉等含义

similar_words.description: 近义词，accommodate侧重提供长期或正式住宿，lodge侧重临时暂住

definitions.meaning: 崇高的（目标、理想）；高耸的
definitions.example: The Nobel Prize-winning scientist attributed his success to pursuing lofty research goals rather than short-term gains.

lofty.etymology: 由名词「loft（阁楼、高处）」+形容词后缀「-y」构成，原指高耸的，后引申为崇高的目标、理想

similar_words.description: 近义词，noble侧重人的品德高尚，lofty侧重目标、理想的崇高或物体的高耸

definitions.example: Workers floated the heavy logs down the river to the coastal sawmill to reduce transportation costs.

definitions.meaning: 正式记录（航行、工作时长等）
definitions.example: Commercial pilots are legally required to log every flight's duration, route, and fuel consumption accurately.

log.etymology: 源自古诺尔斯语「log（木材）」，动词义由早期航海用木牌记录航程的习俗引申为正式记录

similar_words.description: 近义词，record为通用记录，log侧重官方、系统性的航行、工作等记录

definitions.example: The editorial's flawed logic, which ignored conflicting data, drew widespread criticism from academic circles.

logic.etymology: 由词根「log-（言语、理性）」+名词后缀「-ic（表学科、性质）」构成，原指推理的学科，后引申为逻辑、推理方法

similar_words.description: 形近名词，ethic指伦理规范，logic指逻辑、推理方法

definitions.example: It is logical to infer that increased public investment in education will boost long-term national productivity.

logical.etymology: 由名词「logic（逻辑）」+形容词后缀「-al」构成，表与逻辑相关的

similar_words.description: 近义词，rational侧重人的理性思维，logical侧重符合逻辑规则

definitions.example: The reclusive poet, though surrounded by nature, often felt lonely due to the lack of intellectual companionship.

lonely.etymology: 由形容词「lone（单独的）」+后缀「-ly」构成，此处「-ly」为形容词后缀，表具有…性质的

similar_words.description: 语义易混词，alone侧重客观上单独（无情感色彩），lonely侧重主观上的孤独感

definitions.example: 18th-century sailors relied on lunar distance measurements to calculate their longitude, a breakthrough in maritime navigation.

longitude.etymology: 由词根「long-（长的）」+名词后缀「-itude（表性质、状态）」构成，因经度是沿地球纵向（长方向）的度量参数

similar_words.description: 形近地理名词，latitude指纬度，longitude指经度，均为地球坐标参数

definitions.example: Artisans in the remote mountain village still use hand-operated looms to weave traditional woolen textiles.

definitions.meaning: （危险、危机等）赫然逼近，隐约出现
definitions.example: A severe energy crisis loomed across Europe as natural gas supplies were disrupted in late 2022.

loom.etymology: 源自古英语「loma（织布机）」，动词义由织布机上下穿梭、隐约显现的特征引申为危险等赫然逼近

similar_words.description: 形近词，gloom指昏暗、忧郁，loom指织布机或危险等赫然逼近

definitions.example: The computer science student optimized the code by replacing multiple nested loops with a single efficient algorithm.
The conference organizers decided to loop the keynote speaker's presentation for late attendees.

loop.etymology: 来自中古荷兰语「loep（环、圈）」，引申为程序循环等含义

similar_words.description: 形近名词，hoop指篮球、桶的箍、环，loop侧重闭合的圈或程序循环

definitions.example: The loose soil on the hillside was washed away by the heavy rain during the flood season.

loose.etymology: 来自古诺斯语「lausr」，词根「laus-」表“松的”

similar_words.description: 「loose」为形容词表‘松的’，「lose」为动词表‘失去’，拼写接近易混淆

definitions.example: The central bank decided to loosen its monetary policy to stimulate the sluggish economy.

loosen.etymology: 由形容词「loose」加动词后缀「-en」（表使动）构成的派生词

similar_words.description: 「loosen」为动词表‘使松开’，「loose」多作形容词表‘松的’，为同根衍生词

definitions.example: The medieval lord owned vast tracts of farmland around the castle.

lord.etymology: 来自古英语「hlāford」，原义为“面包守护者”，后引申为“领主、贵族”

similar_words.description: 「lord」表‘贵族/勋爵’，「landlord」表‘房东/地主’，拼写接近易混淆

definitions.example: The lorry loaded with construction materials was stuck in the traffic jam on the suburban highway.

lorry.etymology: 来自北英格兰方言「lurry」（表“推”），原指手推车，后引申为“货运卡车”

similar_words.description: 「lorry」为英式英语表‘货运卡车’，「truck」为美式英语对应近义词

definitions.example: Many small businesses lose their competitive edge in the global market due to lack of innovation.

lose.etymology: 来自古英语「losian」，表“失去、丧失”

similar_words.description: 「lose」为动词表‘失去/输掉’，「loose」为形容词表‘松的’，拼写接近易混淆

definitions.example: The company reported a huge financial loss in the second quarter due to the supply chain disruption.

loss.etymology: 来自古英语「los」，与动词「lose」同根，为其名词形式

similar_words.description: 「loss」为名词表‘损失/亏损’，「lose」为动词表‘失去’，为同根衍生词

definitions.example: Winning the national lottery is often seen as a life-changing event for ordinary people.

lottery.etymology: 来自荷兰语「loterij」，词根「lot-」表“签、命运”

similar_words.description: 「lottery」表‘彩票/碰运气的事’，「lot」表‘签/命运’，为同根衍生词

definitions.example: The loud noise from the construction site disturbed the residents' daily life in the neighborhood.

similar_words.description: 「loud」为形容词表‘大声的’，「aloud」为副词表‘出声地’，词性与用法易混淆

definitions.example: The announcement was broadcast through the loudspeaker in the campus square to inform all students of the emergency.

loudspeaker.etymology: 合成词，由形容词「loud」（响亮的）与名词「speaker」（发声器）构成

similar_words.description: 「loudspeaker」为具体物品‘扬声器/喇叭’，「speaker」可表‘演讲者/泛指扬声器’

definitions.example: Passengers waited for their delayed flight in the comfortable airport lounge with free Wi-Fi and refreshments.

lounge.etymology: 来自法语「longue」（表“长的”），原指长沙发，后引申为“休息室”

similar_words.description: 「lounge」表‘休息室/懒洋洋地坐’，「lodge」表‘小屋/寄存’，拼写接近易混淆

definitions.example: Many literature lovers contribute insightful book reviews to academic journals.

lover.etymology: 由动词love（爱）加名词后缀-er构成，指与喜爱或情爱相关的人

similar_words.description: 「lover」意为爱好者/情人，「liver」意为肝脏，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The lower strata of society often face more barriers to accessing higher education.
To boost domestic consumption, the central bank may lower the benchmark interest rate.

lower.etymology: 由形容词low（低的）加比较级后缀-er构成，动词义由形容词义引申而来

similar_words.description: 「lower」可作形容词/动词表较低/降低，「lover」为名词表爱好者/情人，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: A loyal customer base is crucial for the long-term development of any retail enterprise.

loyal.etymology: 源自古法语loial，词根loy-同leg-，表法律、约定，原指信守约定的，后引申为忠诚的

similar_words.description: 「loyal」意为忠诚的，「royal」意为皇家的/王室的，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: Employees' loyalty to the company can be enhanced through reasonable welfare systems and career development plans.

loyalty.etymology: 由形容词loyal（忠诚的）加名词后缀-ty构成，表忠诚这一抽象性质

similar_words.description: 「loyalty」意为忠诚，「royalty」意为王室/版税，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: Workers need to lubricate the machine parts regularly to ensure smooth operation.

definitions.meaning: 促进（进程、关系等），使顺利进行
definitions.example: Mutual respect and trust can lubricate cross-cultural communication between different countries.

lubricate.etymology: 由词根lubric-（表光滑的）加动词后缀-ate构成，本义为使光滑，后引申为促进事务顺利进行

similar_words.description: 「lubricate」意为润滑/促进，「fabricate」意为编造/制造，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: Many successful entrepreneurs attribute part of their achievements to good luck rather than just hard work.

luck.etymology: 源自中古英语luck，原指机遇、命运，后泛指运气

similar_words.description: 「luck」意为运气，「lock」意为锁/锁住，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: Those who are well-prepared are more likely to seize lucky opportunities in the competitive job market.

lucky.etymology: 由名词luck（运气）加形容词后缀-y构成，表有好运的

similar_words.description: 「lucky」为普通用词，多指偶然的好运；「fortunate」为正式用词，多指符合预期的好境遇

definitions.example: Passengers are required to check their oversize luggage at the airline counter before boarding.

luggage.etymology: 由动词lug（拖拉、搬运）加名词后缀-age构成，原指需要拖拉搬运的大件物品，后专指行李

similar_words.description: 「luggage」多用于英国英语，「baggage」多用于美国英语，二者均表示行李，为不可数名词

definitions.example: The local factory imports high-quality lumber from Canada to produce solid wood furniture.

definitions.meaning: 缓慢吃力地移动；笨拙地行进
definitions.example: The elderly man lumbered across the icy road with the help of a walking stick.

lumber.etymology: 源自中古英语lumber，原指笨重的杂物，因木材笨重难搬引申出木材义，动词义由笨重的特征引申而来

similar_words.description: 「lumber」多指加工后的商用木材，「timber」多指未加工的原木或用材林

definitions.example: The doctor found a small lump in the patient's breast and recommended further medical examination.
We should not lump all social media platforms together, as each has its own unique user group and operational logic.

lump.etymology: 源自中古英语lump，原指块状固体，动词义由聚成块的动作引申为归并同类事物

similar_words.description: 「lump」多指实心的块状物或身体肿块，「bump」多指碰撞造成的隆起或撞击动作

definitions.example: Scientists have been studying the lunar surface for decades to explore the possibility of human settlement.

definitions.meaning: 阴历的，按月球运行测定的
definitions.example: The Lunar New Year is one of the most important traditional festivals in many Asian countries.

lunar.etymology: 词根lun-（表「月亮」）+ 形容词后缀-ar，表「与…有关的」

similar_words.description: 同根词，词根均为lun-；lunar指月球的、阴历的，lunatic指精神错乱的（古代认为月亮影响精神状态）

definitions.example: Prolonged exposure to air pollutants may cause severe damage to the lungs and respiratory system.

lung.etymology: 源自古英语lung，原指「可漂浮的轻器官」，后特指肺

similar_words.description: 形近词，lung为名词指肺，lunge为动词/名词指冲刺、猛扑

definitions.example: Many companies lure young graduates with high salaries and promising career prospects.
The lure of urban convenience draws thousands of rural migrants each year.

lure.etymology: 源自古法语lur（猎鹰训练用的诱饵），后引申为「诱惑」

similar_words.description: 近义动词，lure侧重以利益、诱饵引诱；allure侧重以魅力、吸引力吸引

definitions.example: In times of economic recession, consumers tend to cut back on spending on luxury goods.

luxury.etymology: 拉丁语luxus（过度、奢华）+ 名词后缀-ury

similar_words.description: 同根形近词，词根均为lux-；luxury指奢侈品、奢华，luxuriant指（植物）繁茂的或（表达）华丽的

definitions.example: The factory updated its production machinery to improve efficiency and reduce costs.
The government is reforming the administrative machinery to enhance public service quality.

machinery.etymology: machine（机器）+ 集合名词后缀-ery

similar_words.description: 同根词，machine指单个具体机器，machinery指机器总称或体制机制

definitions.example: The protagonist in the novel was driven mad by the constant pressure from his family and society.
Many young people are mad about online games, which has raised concerns among educators.

mad.etymology: 源自古英语gemǣdd（精神错乱的）

similar_words.description: 形近词，mad为形容词指疯狂的、狂热的，madam为名词指女士、夫人

definitions.meaning: 夫人，女士（正式称呼已婚女性）
definitions.example: Madame Curie is widely recognized as one of the most influential scientists in history.

madame.etymology: 法语借词，由ma（我的）+ dame（夫人）构成

similar_words.description: 形近近义词，madame为法语借词，多用于正式场合称呼已婚女性；madam为英语本土词，使用场景更宽泛

definitions.example: This academic magazine publishes papers on a wide range of topics in social sciences.
The military base's magazine was destroyed in a sudden explosion during the war.

magazine.etymology: 源自阿拉伯语makhzan（仓库），后引申为「知识仓库」即杂志

similar_words.description: 近义名词，magazine多为面向大众的通俗杂志，journal多为学术期刊或个人日志

definitions.example: In ancient myths, magic was often used by heroes to overcome supernatural obstacles.
The scientist's discovery had a magic effect on the development of modern medicine.

magic.etymology: 源自希腊语magike（巫术、魔法），原指波斯祭司的技艺

similar_words.description: 同根词，magic可作名词或形容词；magical仅作形容词，侧重表达神奇迷人的效果

definitions.example: The magistrate ruled that the defendant was not guilty due to lack of sufficient evidence.

magistrate.etymology: 拉丁语magistratus（长官职位），由magister（主人、长官）+ 名词后缀-atus构成

similar_words.description: 同根词，词根均为magist-；magistrate指地方法官、行政长官，magister为古英语词，指硕士或主人

definitions.meaning: 磁铁，磁体；有吸引力的人或事物
definitions.example: The university’s cutting-edge engineering lab acts as a magnet for top engineering graduates worldwide.

magnet.etymology: 源自拉丁语magnes（磁石），同根词有magnetic、magnitude等

similar_words.description: 「magnet」指磁铁或有吸引力的对象；「maggot」指蝇蛆，为形近易混词

definitions.meaning: 磁的，有磁性的；有吸引力的，有魅力的
definitions.example: The magnetic field of the Earth protects living organisms from harmful cosmic radiation.

magnetic.etymology: 由magnet（磁石）加形容词后缀-ic构成，表'与…有关的'

similar_words.description: 「magnetic」指有磁性的或有魅力的；「magisterial」指有权威的、专断的，为形近易混词

definitions.meaning: 壮丽的，宏伟的；极好的，出色的
definitions.example: The magnificent ancient architecture in Xi’an attracts millions of tourists every year.

magnificent.etymology: 由拉丁语词根magn-（大的）加形容词后缀-ificent（表'…性质的'）构成，表'宏大的'

similar_words.description: 二者均含词根magn-（大）；「magnificent」侧重壮丽出色，「magnanimous」侧重心胸宽大

definitions.example: The media often magnifies minor social issues to attract more public attention.

magnify.etymology: 由词根magn-（大）加动词后缀-ify（使…）构成，表'使变大'

similar_words.description: 二者均含词根magn-（大）；「magnify」为动词表放大/夸大，「magnitude」为名词表大小/重要性

definitions.example: The full magnitude of the environmental disaster was not revealed until months later.

magnitude.etymology: 由词根magn-（大）加名词后缀-itude（表状态/性质）构成，表'大的状态'

similar_words.description: 「magnitude」表大小/重要性；「multitude」表众多（人/物），为形近易混词

definitions.meaning: 女仆，女佣；少女，年轻女子
definitions.example: In the classic novel, the loyal maid played a crucial role in protecting the heroine’s secret.

maid.etymology: 源自古英语mægden（少女），后衍生出'女仆'义

similar_words.description: 二者同源；「maid」指女仆或少女，「maiden」多指未婚少女或作形容词表'首次的'

definitions.meaning: n. 未婚少女，处女；adj. 首次的，初次的
definitions.example: The young pilot completed his maiden flight successfully despite the bad weather.

maiden.etymology: 与maid同源，源自古英语mægden，作形容词时表'首次的'为考研核心考点

similar_words.description: 二者同源；「maid」侧重女仆或普通少女，「maiden」侧重未婚少女，且可作形容词表'首次的'

definitions.example: The main reason for the decline in wild bee populations is the widespread use of pesticides.
The city’s water main burst, causing widespread flooding in the downtown area.

main.etymology: 源自古英语mægen（力量），原指'力量的核心'，后衍生出'主要的'义，名词义'总管道'为熟词僻义

similar_words.description: 「main」为形容词/名词，表主要的/总管道；「mainly」为副词，表'主要地'

definitions.meaning: 大陆，本土（相对于岛屿或半岛而言）
definitions.example: Many species unique to the island cannot survive on the mainland due to different ecological conditions.

mainland.etymology: 由main（主要的）加land（土地）构成，指'主要的土地'即大陆

similar_words.description: 「mainland」侧重相对于岛屿的本土大陆；「continent」侧重地理上的七大洲等大型陆地

definitions.example: It is essential for a country to maintain a stable financial system to promote economic growth.
The scholar maintained that traditional cultural values should be integrated into modern education.

maintain.etymology: 由拉丁语词根manu-（手）加ten-（握住）构成，原指'用手握住'，后衍生出'维持、坚持'等义

similar_words.description: 二者均有'维持'义；「maintain」侧重主动维持（状态/设备/观点），「sustain」侧重承受住、持续（多为被动或客观状态）

definitions.example: Regular maintenance of public transportation systems ensures smooth urban mobility and reduces long-term operational costs.

maintenance.etymology: 源自动词「maintain」，词根main-（手）+ ten-（持有）+ 名词后缀-ance，表“用手持有并维持的状态”

similar_words.description: 「maintenance」为名词，表“维护/维持”；「maintain」为动词，表“维持/保养”

definitions.example: The grand architecture of the Forbidden City fully embodies the majesty of ancient Chinese imperial power.

majesty.etymology: 词根maj-（大，伟大）+ 最高级后缀-est + 名词后缀-y，表“伟大的状态”

similar_words.description: 「majesty」为名词，表“威严/陛下”；「magnificent」为形容词，表“壮丽的”，二者共享表“大”的词根

definitions.example: Climate change is a major challenge that requires global cooperation to address effectively.
She selected environmental science as her major to contribute to sustainable development research.

major.etymology: 词根maj-（大）+ 比较级后缀-or，表“更大的，占主导的”

similar_words.description: 「major」表“主要的/专业”；「minor」表“次要的/辅修”，二者为反义关系且形近

definitions.example: The majority of respondents in the national survey supported the policy to expand renewable energy use.

majority.etymology: 源自形容词「major」，加名词后缀-ity，表“主要的部分”

similar_words.description: 「majority」表“大多数”；「minority」表“少数”，二者为反义关系且同根

definitions.meaning: 男性的；雄性的 / 男性；雄性
definitions.example: The study analyzed the dietary habits of male and female wild pandas in the Qinling Mountains.

male.etymology: 源自拉丁语表「雄性」的词根mas，为基础性别类词汇

similar_words.description: 「male」表“男性/雄性”；「female」表“女性/雌性”，二者为反义关系且形近

definitions.meaning: 故障，失灵 / 发生故障，失灵
definitions.example: A sudden malfunction of the aircraft's landing gear forced the pilot to make an emergency landing.

malfunction.etymology: 前缀mal-（坏，异常）+ 名词/动词「function」，表“功能异常（的状态）”

similar_words.description: 「malfunction」多表“设备/系统故障”；「dysfunction」多表“人体生理功能障碍”，二者均含表“异常”的前缀

definitions.example: The malign effects of long-term sleep deprivation on cardiovascular health have been confirmed by multiple studies.
The celebrity sued the tabloid for maligning his reputation with false accusations.

malign.etymology: 前缀mal-（坏）+ 词根lign-（=gen-产生），表“产生坏的（影响/言论）”

similar_words.description: 「malign」多表“（影响/言论）有害/诽谤”；「malignant」多表“（疾病）恶性的”，二者共享表“坏”的前缀

definitions.example: The new targeted therapy has shown promising results in treating malignant breast cancer in clinical trials.

malignant.etymology: 前缀mal-（坏）+ 词根lign-（产生）+ 形容词后缀-ant，表“产生坏结果的”

similar_words.description: 「malignant」多表“（疾病）恶性的”；「malign」多表“（影响/言论）有害的”，二者共享表“坏”的前缀

definitions.example: Local welfare agencies have launched campaigns to protect elderly people from being maltreated by caregivers.

maltreat.etymology: 前缀mal-（坏）+ 动词「treat」，表“恶劣地对待”

similar_words.description: 「maltreat」和「mistreat」均表“虐待”，前者更正式，后者更常用

definitions.example: Bats, the only flying mammals, play a crucial role in pollinating night-blooming plants.

mammal.etymology: 词根mamm-（乳房，源自拉丁语mamma）+ 名词后缀-al，表“有乳房的动物”

similar_words.description: 「mammal」表“哺乳动物”；「reptile」表“爬行动物”，二者同属动物分类核心词汇

definitions.example: Most successful high-tech startups require experienced entrepreneurs to manage their daily operations and resource allocation efficiently.
Despite the shortage of research funds, the team managed to publish three papers in top-tier journals last year.

manage.etymology: 源自拉丁语manus（手）+agere（行动、做），原指「用手处理具体事务」，后引申为管理、设法完成等义

similar_words.description: 「manage」侧重日常经营或事务的统筹处理，「administer」侧重官方或机构的行政监管与执行

definitions.example: Effective project management is a key factor determining the success of postgraduate research programs.
The company’s management and labor union finally reached a consensus on the annual wage adjustment plan.

management.etymology: 动词「manage」的名词形式，加名词后缀-ment，表「行为、结果或状态」

similar_words.description: 「management」侧重企业或项目的经营管理，「administration」侧重政府或公共机构的行政管理

definitions.meaning: （尤指为达目的）巧妙操纵，策划
definitions.example: The opposition party tried to maneuver the tax reform bill through the parliamentary committee with tactical amendments.
His sudden withdrawal from the race was merely a political maneuver to gain public sympathy.

maneuver.etymology: 源自法语manœuvre，原指「体力劳动」，由拉丁语manus（手）+opera（工作）演变而来，后引申为「操纵、策略」

similar_words.description: 「maneuver」为美式英语拼写，「manoeuvre」为英式英语拼写，二者语义完全一致

definitions.example: The short-video mania among adolescents has sparked heated debates about its impact on mental health.
Patients with severe mania often exhibit extreme restlessness and impulsive spending behavior.

mania.etymology: 源自希腊语mania（疯狂），同根词有maniac（躁狂者）

similar_words.description: 「mania」侧重长期、群体性的狂热或癖好，「frenzy」侧重短暂、失控的狂乱状态

definitions.meaning: （自然地）显示，表明（情感、品质或现象）
definitions.example: Her strong sense of social responsibility manifests in her active participation in public welfare projects.
It is manifest that the current environmental policy has not achieved the expected carbon reduction targets.

manifest.etymology: 源自拉丁语manifestus，由manus（手）+festus（可触及的）构成，原指「用手可触及的」，后引申为「明显的、显示」

similar_words.description: 「manifest」侧重通过迹象或行为自然显现，「demonstrate」侧重通过证据或实验主动证明

definitions.meaning: （以不正当手段）操纵，控制（人或舆论）
definitions.example: Some social media platforms manipulate public opinion by algorithmically amplifying biased content.
The aerospace engineer can manipulate the satellite control system with extreme precision.

manipulate.etymology: 源自拉丁语manus（手）+pulare（拉、操作），原指「用手操作工具」，后引申为「操纵、控制」

similar_words.description: 「manipulate」侧重以不正当手段暗中操纵，「maneuver」侧重巧妙策划以达到合法或合理目的

definitions.meaning: 人类（集合名词，侧重整体）
definitions.example: Mankind has made unprecedented breakthroughs in genetic engineering over the past two decades.

mankind.etymology: 由man（人）+kind（种类）构成，原指「人类这一种类」

similar_words.description: 「mankind」侧重人类整体，「humanity」侧重复数时指‘人’、单数时指‘人性、人道’

definitions.meaning: 方式，方法（侧重做事的风格）
definitions.example: The manner in which a scholar presents research findings significantly affects academic communication efficiency.
His courteous and composed manner earned him the respect of all interview panel members.

manner.etymology: 源自拉丁语manus（手），原指「用手做事的方式」，后引申为「行为方式、举止」

similar_words.description: 「manner」侧重做事的风格或习惯方式，「method」侧重系统、有步骤的科学方法

definitions.meaning: （尤指为达目的）巧妙策划，移动
definitions.example: The chess champion managed to manoeuvre his opponent into a hopeless defensive position.
The supermarket’s loyalty program was a marketing manoeuvre to retain regular customers.

manoeuvre.etymology: 同「maneuver」，为英式英语拼写，源自法语manœuvre，由拉丁语manus（手）+opera（工作）演变而来

similar_words.description: 「manoeuvre」为英式英语拼写，「maneuver」为美式英语拼写，二者语义完全相同

definitions.meaning: 手工的，体力的（与机械、自动化相对）
definitions.example: Many low-skilled manual labor jobs are being replaced by automated production lines in manufacturing.
Before conducting the experiment, all students must read the laboratory safety manual thoroughly.

manual.etymology: 源自拉丁语manus（手）+alis（形容词后缀），原指「用手的」，后引申为「手工的、手册」

similar_words.description: 「manual」侧重操作或使用的详细指南，「handbook」侧重便携、内容精炼的参考手册

definitions.example: The automobile factory plans to manufacture 10,000 electric cars annually to meet the growing market demand.
The government has introduced policies to support the development of high-end manufacture in the region.

manufacture.etymology: 词根manu-（手） + fact-（做） + 后缀-ure，原指手工制作，现引申为大规模生产或制造业

similar_words.description: manufacture指制造、生产，manipulate指操纵、操控（多指暗中或熟练地控制）

definitions.example: The 18th-century literary manuscript, written in ink, was discovered in a private collection last year.

manuscript.etymology: 词根manu-（手） + script-（写），指手写的文稿或原稿

similar_words.description: manuscript指手写的原始文稿，script指剧本、脚本等正式书面稿

definitions.example: The columns of the historic museum are carved from polished white marble.
Children in the village used to play with colorful marbles on the street.

marble.etymology: 源自古法语marbre，原指大理石，后引申为弹珠（因形似大理石小球）

similar_words.description: marble指大理石或弹珠，marvel指令人惊奇的事物或奇迹

definitions.example: She wrote her critical notes in the narrow margin of the academic journal article.
The company's net profit margin dropped by 4% due to rising raw material costs.

margin.etymology: 源自拉丁语margo，指边界、边缘，后引申为页边空白或利润边际

similar_words.description: margin常指页边、利润边际等抽象或具体的边缘，border指地理或物体的明确边界

definitions.example: The marginal increase in sales failed to offset the rise in production expenses.
The study found only a marginal correlation between sleep duration and academic performance.

marginal.etymology: margin（边缘） + 后缀-al（形容词后缀），指边缘的，引申为边际的、微小的

similar_words.description: marginal侧重边际、几乎无效的，minor侧重次要的、规模较小的

definitions.example: Marine ecosystems are highly vulnerable to ocean acidification caused by climate change.

marine.etymology: 词根mar-（海） + 后缀-ine（形容词后缀），指与海洋相关的

similar_words.description: marine侧重海洋生物、生态等自然领域，maritime侧重海运、海事等人文领域

definitions.example: The research explores the impact of financial stress on marital satisfaction among young couples.

marital.etymology: 词根marit-（婚姻，源自拉丁语maritus「丈夫」） + 后缀-al（形容词后缀），指与婚姻相关的

similar_words.description: marital是形容词，指婚姻相关的（如marital problems），married指人的婚姻状态（已婚的）

definitions.example: The signing of the Paris Agreement marks a global commitment to combating climate change.
Her outstanding mark in the postgraduate entrance exam earned her a scholarship.

mark.etymology: 源自古英语mea(r)c，指标记、界限，后引申为标志、分数等含义

similar_words.description: mark可指抽象或具体的标记、分数，label侧重具体的标签、标识

definitions.example: The study examines the factors influencing the stability of marriage in modern society.

marriage.etymology: marry（结婚） + 后缀-age（名词后缀），指婚姻状态或婚姻关系

similar_words.description: marriage指婚姻状态或关系，wedding指结婚的仪式

definitions.example: According to the survey, married individuals tend to have higher life satisfaction than single ones.
She married her college sweetheart after graduating from university.

married.etymology: marry（结婚） + 后缀-ed（形容词/过去分词后缀），指已婚的或已结婚的

similar_words.description: married指人的婚姻状态（已婚的），marital指与婚姻相关的（如marital duties）

definitions.example: Many young adults choose to marry later in life due to rising living costs and career pressures.

marry.etymology: 源自古英语maerrian，与名词marriage同根，核心含义与‘婚姻、结合’相关

similar_words.description: 形近易混，marry表‘结婚、结合’，merry表‘愉快的、欢乐的’

definitions.example: The research team presented a marvelous breakthrough in the field of renewable energy.

marvelous.etymology: 由名词marvel（奇迹）加形容词后缀-ous构成，表‘充满奇迹特征的’

similar_words.description: 同根词，marvel作动词表‘感到惊奇’，作名词表‘奇迹’，marvelous为其形容词形式

definitions.example: Marxist critical theory has been widely applied in contemporary literary studies.
He was a devoted Marxist who dedicated his life to promoting social equality.

Marxist.etymology: 由专有名词Marx（马克思）加后缀-ist构成，表‘…主义者/…的’

similar_words.description: 同根词，Marxism表‘马克思主义’（理论体系名词），Marxist指人或作形容词

definitions.example: The study examines how traditional masculine norms impact men’s mental health outcomes.

masculine.etymology: 由拉丁语词根mascul-（男性）加形容词后缀-ine构成，表‘与男性相关的’

similar_words.description: 反义近形词，masculine表男性相关特征，feminine表女性相关特征

definitions.example: Medical staff wore protective masks to prevent the spread of infectious diseases.
The official’s vague remarks masked the true severity of the economic crisis.

mask.etymology: 源自古法语masque，核心含义为‘遮盖物’

similar_words.description: 形近易混，mask表‘遮盖、掩饰’，mass表‘大量、块’

definitions.example: The mass of empirical evidence supports the researcher’s core hypothesis.
Mass production techniques have significantly reduced the cost of consumer goods.

mass.etymology: 源自拉丁语massa（团、块），为massive、massacre等同根词的词根

similar_words.description: 形近易混，mass表‘大量、大规模’，mask表‘遮盖、掩饰’

definitions.example: Historical archives document the massacre of innocent civilians during the civil war.
The invading army massacred hundreds of villagers in the border region.

massacre.etymology: 由词根mass（大量）加表‘杀戮’的语素构成，指大规模的杀戮行为

similar_words.description: 同根词，mass表‘大量’，massacre特指大规模的杀戮事件

definitions.example: The country faces a massive challenge in reducing carbon emissions by 2030.

massive.etymology: 由词根mass（大量）加形容词后缀-ive构成，表‘具有大量或大规模特征的’

similar_words.description: 同根词，mass作形容词表‘大规模的’，massive更强调规模或程度的极大性

definitions.example: She managed to master three foreign languages and advanced statistical methods in college.
The oil painting was created by a renowned Italian Renaissance master.

master.etymology: 源自古英语maegester，核心含义与‘支配、精通’相关

similar_words.description: 形近易混，master表‘精通、大师’，mister为尊称‘先生’

definitions.example: Shakespeare’s 「Hamlet」 is widely regarded as a timeless literary masterpiece.

masterpiece.etymology: 由master（大师）与piece（作品）构成的合成词，指大师创作的杰出作品

similar_words.description: 同义词，二者均表‘杰作’，masterpiece使用更为广泛

definitions.example: The biologist placed the collected moss sample on a sterilized mat under the dissecting microscope.

mat.etymology: 源自古英语mætt，原指小块用于铺垫的织物或覆盖物

similar_words.description: mat指铺垫用的垫子，mate指伙伴或配偶

definitions.example: Ornithologists recorded the male penguin regurgitating food to feed its mate during the harsh Antarctic winter.

mate.etymology: 源自古英语gemæcca，原指共享空间或任务的同伴

similar_words.description: mate指伙伴或配偶，mat指铺垫用的垫子

definitions.example: Renewable biological materials are being tested as eco-friendly alternatives to plastic in packaging.
The report emphasizes the material benefits of urban green spaces for public mental health.

material.etymology: 源自拉丁语materia，原指「木料、原料」，词根mater-表「母」，引申为生产的基础原料

similar_words.description: material指具体的生产或制作材料，matter指泛指的物质或事情

definitions.example: The research team used mathematical modeling to predict the spread of the infectious disease across regions.

mathematical.etymology: 源自希腊语mathematikos，词根math-指「学习、知识」，后缀-ical为形容词后缀，表「与…相关的」

similar_words.description: mathematical是形容词，修饰名词表数学相关的，maths是名词，指数学学科

definitions.meaning: 数学（英式英语，为mathematics的缩写）
definitions.example: Undergraduate programs in data science require a solid foundation in advanced maths.

maths.etymology: 为名词mathematics的英式缩写形式，简化了原词的音节

similar_words.description: maths是名词，指数学学科，mathematical是形容词，表与数学相关的

definitions.meaning: 成熟的（心智、生理或思想层面）
definitions.example: The longitudinal study found that individuals with secure attachment tend to develop more mature emotional regulation strategies.
The 10-year government bond will mature next month, returning the principal and accumulated interest to investors.

mature.etymology: 源自拉丁语maturus，原指「适时的、成熟的」，后引申出金融领域的「到期」义

similar_words.description: mature侧重人或思想的心智成熟，ripe侧重植物果实或时机的生理成熟

definitions.example: The study established a maximum threshold of 20 micrograms per liter for the pollutant to protect aquatic life.

maximum.etymology: 源自拉丁语maximus（最大的），后缀-um为中性名词后缀，表「状态或数值」

similar_words.description: maximum指最大的数值或限度，minimum指最小的数值或限度

definitions.example: The mayor proposed a budget increase to fund the renovation of municipal libraries and community centers.

mayor.etymology: 源自古法语maire，原指城市的行政长官，后演变为现代英语的「市长」

similar_words.description: mayor指城市行政长官（名词），major指主要的（形容词）或主修科目（名词）

definitions.meaning: 草地，牧场（多指平缓、湿润的天然或人工草地）
definitions.example: The ecologists monitored the carbon sequestration capacity of the alpine meadow over a three-year period.

meadow.etymology: 源自古英语mǣdwe，原指用于放牧或割草的平缓草地

similar_words.description: meadow多指小块、平缓的牧场或草地，grassland多指大面积的天然草原

definitions.example: Researchers are exploring sustainable means of energy production to mitigate the effects of climate change.
The renowned philosopher lived a simple life despite his considerable means from book royalties.

means.etymology: 源自古英语mǣnan（处于中间），引申为达成目标的「中间媒介（手段）」，后进一步引申为「维持生活的资源（财富）」

similar_words.description: means指达成目标的任何具体或抽象手段，method指系统、有步骤的固定方法

definitions.example: Researchers analyzed the survey data; meantime, they drafted a summary of preliminary results.

definitions.meaning: 在此期间（常用搭配in the meantime）
definitions.example: The new policy will take effect next month; in the meantime, citizens must follow the old regulations.

meantime.etymology: 由mean（中间的）+ time（时间）构成，指两件事之间的中间时段

similar_words.description: 「meantime」可作副词或名词（搭配in the meantime）；「meanwhile」仅作副词，多用于连接并列分句

definitions.example: Urban areas saw a rise in employment; meanwhile, rural regions faced a shortage of labor.

meanwhile.etymology: 由mean（中间的）+ while（时段）构成，表中间的时段

similar_words.description: 「meanwhile」仅作副词，可位于句首或句中；「meantime」可作名词（搭配in the meantime）或副词

definitions.example: Economists measure the country’s economic growth by its gross domestic product.
The local government implemented strict measures to reduce air pollution in the city center.

measure.etymology: 源自拉丁语mensurare（测量），同根词有dimension（维度）、commensurate（相称的）

similar_words.description: 「measure」多指官方或正式的应对措施；「step」指一般性的行动步骤或措施

definitions.example: The experienced mechanic identified the faulty part of the car’s engine within 10 minutes.

mechanic.etymology: 由mechan（机械）+ -ic（表人的名词后缀）构成，指从事机械维修的人

similar_words.description: 「mechanic」为名词，指机械师；「mechanical」为形容词，指机械的或呆板的

definitions.example: The factory replaced manual assembly with mechanical production lines to improve efficiency.

definitions.meaning: 呆板的；无思想的（熟词僻义）
definitions.example: His mechanical repetition of the textbook content showed he did not understand the topic deeply.

mechanical.etymology: 由mechan（机械）+ -ical（形容词后缀）构成，表与机械相关的，引申为呆板的

similar_words.description: 「mechanical」为形容词，表机械的或呆板的；「mechanic」为名词，指机械师

definitions.meaning: 机制；机理（尤指生物、社会系统的运作方式）
definitions.example: Scientists are studying the mechanism by which the human body regulates blood sugar levels.

mechanism.etymology: 由mechan（机械）+ -ism（表体系、运作方式的名词后缀）构成，指类似机械运作的系统或机理

similar_words.description: 「mechanism」指具体的运作机制、机理；「mechanics」指力学学科或整体运作方法

definitions.example: The Olympic champion was awarded a gold medal for her outstanding performance in the 100-meter race.

medal.etymology: 源自拉丁语medalia（硬币、奖章），同根词有medallion（大奖章）

similar_words.description: 「medal」为奖章（荣誉物品）；「metal」为金属（物质材料），拼写仅差第二个字母

definitions.example: Many top universities offer interdisciplinary programs combining medical science and artificial intelligence.

medical.etymology: 由med（治疗，如medicine）+ -ical（形容词后缀）构成，表与医疗、医学相关的

similar_words.description: 「medical」指医学的、医疗的；「medicinal」指有药效的、药用的

definitions.example: The medieval cathedral in the city center is a popular tourist attraction for history lovers.

medieval.etymology: 由medi（中间的）+ eval（时代，源自拉丁语aevum）+ -al（形容词后缀）构成，指欧洲历史上的中间时代

similar_words.description: 「medieval」指中世纪的；「primeval」指原始的、远古的，两者均含表示「时代」的后缀-eval

definitions.example: Many psychologists recommend that people meditate for 15 minutes daily to reduce stress.

meditate.etymology: 源自拉丁语meditari（思考），同根词有meditation（冥想、沉思）

similar_words.description: 「meditate」为动词，指沉思、冥想；「mediate」为动词，指调解、斡旋，拼写仅差中间一个字母

definitions.example: Regular meditation has been shown to reduce anxiety levels in peer-reviewed clinical studies.

meditation.etymology: 词根「med-」（思考、沉思）+ 名词后缀「-ation」，源自动词「meditate」

similar_words.description: 「meditation」表“冥想、沉思”，「mediation」表“调解、斡旋”，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: Social media is often regarded as a pervasive medium for shaping public discourse in contemporary society.
The study recruited a sample of medium-sized enterprises across three European countries.

medium.etymology: 词根「medi-」（中间）+ 后缀「-um」（表事物或状态的名词/形容词后缀），本义为“中间的事物”

similar_words.description: 「medium」为单数形式，表“媒介、中等物”；「media」为其复数形式，常特指“大众传播媒介”，易被误用为单数

definitions.example: The composer integrated a traditional folk melody into the opening section of his award-winning symphony.

melody.etymology: 词根「mel-」（歌、旋律）+ 后缀「-ody」（表歌曲的名词后缀），源自希腊语「meloidia」（唱歌）

similar_words.description: 「melody」指单一的“旋律、曲调”；「medley」指“混合曲、杂集”，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The agricultural research center developed a new variety of melon with high disease resistance and sweet flavor.

melon.etymology: 源自古法语「melon」，指葫芦科的甜瓜类植物，无明显考研核心词根

similar_words.description: 「melon」为“瓜、甜瓜”，「lemon」为“柠檬”，拼写相似易混淆

definitions.meaning: 融化，熔化；（情感、态度）软化
definitions.example: Scientists warn that the Arctic sea ice will continue to melt at an accelerated rate without immediate climate action.

melt.etymology: 源自古英语「meltan」，表“使液化、融化”，无明显考研核心词根

similar_words.description: 「melt」为“融化、软化”，「malt」为“麦芽”，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.meaning: 会员身份，会员资格；全体会员
definitions.example: The international academic association requires candidates to submit relevant publications to qualify for full membership.

membership.etymology: 名词「member」（会员）+ 后缀「-ship」（表身份、状态的名词后缀）

similar_words.description: 「membership」表“会员身份”，「fellowship」表“学术奖学金、研究员职位”，均含表“身份、状态”的后缀「-ship」

definitions.example: The university’s administrative office issued a memo reminding faculty of the upcoming deadline for grant applications.

memo.etymology: 是「memorandum」的缩写，词根「memor-」（记忆）+ 后缀「-andum」（表事物的名词后缀），本义为“助记的文件”

similar_words.description: 「memo」为“备忘录（助记文件）”，「memory」为“记忆、回忆”，均含表“记忆”的词根「memor-」

definitions.example: The institution held a memorial ceremony to commemorate the 50th anniversary of its founding president’s passing.
A bronze memorial was erected in the campus square to honor alumni who sacrificed their lives in the war.

memorial.etymology: 词根「memor-」（记忆）+ 后缀「-ial」（表“与…相关的”形容词/名词后缀），表“与记忆相关的（事物）”

similar_words.description: 「memorial」作形容词表“纪念的”，作名词表“纪念碑”；「memorable」为形容词“难忘的”，均含表“记忆”的词根「memor-」

definitions.example: The archival documents preserved in the national library are invaluable for recovering lost memories of the 20th-century social movements.

memory.etymology: 词根「memor-」（记忆）+ 后缀「-y」（表状态的名词后缀）

similar_words.description: 「memory」为“记忆、回忆”，「memoir」为“回忆录、自传片段”，均含表“记忆”的词根「memor-」

definitions.example: The World Health Organization has identified antimicrobial resistance as a global menace to public health security.
The extremist group menaced the border towns with coordinated attacks, prompting a military response.

menace.etymology: 源自拉丁语「minacia」（威胁），本义为“恐吓、威胁”

similar_words.description: 「menace」为“威胁、危险物”，「menu」为“菜单”，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: Excessive use of short-video apps may impair young people's mental ability to sustain long-term focus on academic tasks.

mental.etymology: 源自拉丁语词根「mens」（思想、精神）+形容词后缀「-al」，表与精神或脑力相关的

similar_words.description: 「metal」指金属，「mental」指精神的、脑力的，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.meaning: 商品，货物（尤指批量销售的）
definitions.example: Large e-commerce platforms update their seasonal merchandise regularly to meet consumer demand.

merchandise.etymology: 源自拉丁语词根「merx」（货物）+名词后缀「-andise」，表集合性的货物

similar_words.description: 同根词，「merchant」指经营商品的商人，「merchandise」指商人经营的商品

definitions.example: In medieval Europe, Italian merchants controlled most of the Mediterranean trade routes.

merchant.etymology: 源自拉丁语词根「merx」（货物）+名词后缀「-ant」，表从事货物交易的人

similar_words.description: 同根词，「merchandise」指商品，「merchant」指经营商品的商人

definitions.example: Exposure to mercury can cause irreversible damage to the human nervous system.

mercury.etymology: 源自罗马神话中神使「墨丘利（Mercurius）」，因水银流动性强如信使得名

similar_words.description: 「mercy」指仁慈、宽恕，「mercury」指化学元素水银，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: When trapped in the wild, hikers are often at the mercy of unpredictable weather conditions.

mercy.etymology: 源自拉丁语词根「merces」（报酬、怜悯），后衍生出仁慈、宽恕义

similar_words.description: 「mercury」指水银，「mercy」指仁慈、怜悯，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The gap between the rich and the poor is not a mere difference in income but a systemic social issue.

mere.etymology: 源自拉丁语词根「merus」（纯的、纯粹的），引申为仅仅的、只不过的

similar_words.description: 同根词，「mere」为形容词修饰名词，「merely」为副词修饰动词或句子

definitions.example: Many critics argue that the new policy is merely a superficial attempt to address the housing crisis.

merely.etymology: 由形容词「mere」+副词后缀「-ly」构成，表仅仅地、只不过地

similar_words.description: 同根词，「mere」为形容词修饰名词，「merely」为副词修饰动词或句子

definitions.example: The two pharmaceutical companies decided to merge their research departments to accelerate vaccine development.

merge.etymology: 源自拉丁语词根「mergere」（浸入、融合），引申为合并、融合

similar_words.description: 同根词，「merge」为动词表合并动作，「merger」为名词表合并事件

definitions.example: The recent merger of the two media giants has raised concerns about market monopoly.

merger.etymology: 由动词「merge」+名词后缀「-er」构成，表合并的事件或结果

similar_words.description: 同根词，「merge」为动词表合并动作，「merger」为名词表合并事件

definitions.example: The university admissions committee evaluates each candidate based on their academic merit and personal qualities.
The growing threat of antibiotic resistance merits urgent attention from global health organizations.

merit.etymology: 源自拉丁语词根「merere」（值得、应得），名词义指值得称赞的优点或功绩，动词义指值得、应得

similar_words.description: 「mercy」指仁慈，「merit」指优点或值得，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The merry atmosphere of the intercollegiate academic salon relieved the participants' research-induced tension.

merry.etymology: 源自古英语merige，表「愉快的」，无明显可拆分的考研核心词根词缀

similar_words.description: 形近词，merry表「愉快的」，marry表「结婚、嫁/娶」

definitions.example: The mess of unclassified archaeological documents in the lab delayed the research team's dating work.

mess.etymology: 源自古法语mes，原指「混合的食物」，后引申为「混乱的状态」

similar_words.description: 形近词，mess表「混乱」，mass表「大量、团块；群众」

definitions.meaning: 信息，消息；（文艺作品的）主旨
definitions.example: The encrypted message from the astronomical observatory contained data about the newly discovered exoplanet.

message.etymology: 源自古法语message，来自拉丁语missus（派遣），原指「被派遣的事物」，后引申为「信息、主旨」

similar_words.description: 形近词，message表「信息、主旨」，passage表「段落；通道」

definitions.meaning: 信使，送信者；（生物）信使分子
definitions.example: The messenger RNA (mRNA) transfers genetic instructions from DNA to ribosomes for protein synthesis.

messenger.etymology: 由message（信息）+ 后缀-er（表「执行动作的人/物」）构成，指「传递信息的人/物」

similar_words.description: 同根词，message是「传递的内容（信息）」，messenger是「传递信息的载体（信使）」

definitions.example: The new composite material, reinforced with rare metals, exhibits excellent heat resistance for aerospace applications.

metal.etymology: 源自希腊语metallon，表「金属」，无复杂的考研核心词根拆分

similar_words.description: 形近词，metal表「金属」，medal表「奖牌、勋章」

definitions.example: The literary critic pointed out that the 'mirror' metaphor in the novel reflects the protagonist's fragmented self-identity.

metaphor.etymology: 源自希腊语metaphora（转移），由meta-（转移）+ phora（携带）构成，指「将意义从一个事物转移到另一个事物的修辞」

similar_words.description: 近义词，metaphor是无比喻词的暗喻，simile是用like/as等的明喻

definitions.example: The sociological study adopted a mixed-method approach to integrate quantitative survey data and qualitative interview records.

method.etymology: 源自希腊语methodos，由meta-（跟随）+ hodos（道路）构成，原指「遵循的道路」，后引申为「方法」

similar_words.description: 同根词，method是具体的操作方法，methodology是系统的方法论

definitions.example: The cross-river tunnel under construction has a designed length of 11,700 metres, ranking among the world's top five.
The romantic poet adjusted the metre of each stanza to match the melancholic tone of his love verses.

metre.etymology: 源自希腊语metron（测量），原指「长度测量单位（米）」，后引申为诗歌中「节奏的测量（格律）」

similar_words.description: 地域拼写变体，metre是英式拼写的「米/格律」，meter是美式拼写的「米/仪表」

definitions.example: All scientific research institutions in China adopt the metric system for consistent measurement standards.
The core metric for evaluating the digital economy's contribution is the share of digital industries in GDP.

metric.etymology: 源自希腊语metron（测量）+ 后缀-ic（表「与…相关的」），指「与测量相关的（公制的）」或「测量的标准（指标）」

similar_words.description: 同根词，metre是具体的测量单位或诗歌格律，metric是公制体系或衡量指标

definitions.example: The metropolitan area of Shanghai has become a global hub for finance, trade and technological innovation.

metropolitan.etymology: 源自希腊语metropolis（母城、首府）+ 后缀-an（表「与…相关的」），由metro-（主要的、母的）+ polis（城市）构成

similar_words.description: 近义词，metropolitan侧重「属于大都市的」，cosmopolitan侧重「世界性的、国际化的」

definitions.example: The researcher used a high-sensitivity microphone to record the subtle mating calls of the experimental amphibians.

microphone.etymology: 由词根「micro-（微小的）」和「phone（声音）」构成，原指传递微小声音的设备

similar_words.description: 同以「micro-」为词根，前者为传声设备（麦克风），后者为观察微小物体的仪器（显微镜）

definitions.example: Scientists examined the fossilized cell samples under a high-resolution microscope to trace evolutionary clues.

microscope.etymology: 由词根「micro-（微小的）」和「scope（观察仪器）」构成，指用于观察微小物体的光学仪器

similar_words.description: 同以「micro-」为词根，前者为观察微小物体的显微镜，后者为传声的麦克风

definitions.meaning: 中间，当中（尤指处于某群体、事件或环境中）
definitions.example: In the midst of the global public health crisis, international medical cooperation became particularly critical.

midst.etymology: 由词根「mid（中间）」加名词后缀「-st」构成，表方位的中间

similar_words.description: 两者均表中间，middle为通用口语/书面词，midst为书面语，常构成固定搭配「in the midst of」

definitions.meaning: 可能，也许（表推测或委婉请求）
definitions.example: The mysterious ancient structure might have served as a ceremonial site for local tribes 3,000 years ago.
The might of the oceanic tides has been harnessed to generate clean electricity in coastal regions.

might.etymology: 源自古英语「miht」，情态动词义表推测，名词义由「能力、力量」的原始义引申而来

similar_words.description: 两者均表推测，might语气比may更委婉，表可能性更低

definitions.example: Many African elephant populations migrate long distances each year in search of water and fresh vegetation.

migrate.etymology: 由词根「migr（迁移）」加动词后缀「-ate」构成，指人或动物的迁移行为

similar_words.description: 同根「migr（迁移）」，migrate泛指人/动物的迁移，emigrate特指从本国移居国外（移出）

definitions.meaning: 温和的；轻微的（尤指天气、疾病、性情等）
definitions.example: The mild climate in this region makes it an ideal habitat for a variety of endangered bird species.

mild.etymology: 源自古英语「milde」，原指性情温柔的，后引申为天气、疾病等的温和/轻微

similar_words.description: 两者均表温和，mild多用于天气、疾病、性情，gentle多用于人的行为、动作或态度

definitions.meaning: 英里（英制长度单位，约合1.6公里）
definitions.example: The archaeological team walked 12 miles across the desert to reach the newly discovered ancient tomb.

mile.etymology: 源自拉丁语「milia」（千），原指一千步的距离，后演变为英制长度单位

similar_words.description: 拼写接近，前者为名词（长度单位英里），后者为形容词（温和的、轻微的）

definitions.meaning: 好战的，激进的；斗志昂扬的
definitions.example: The militant environmental group organized large-scale protests to oppose the construction of the coal-fired power plant.

militant.etymology: 由词根「milit（战斗、士兵）」加形容词后缀「-ant」构成，指具有战斗性的

similar_words.description: 同根「milit（战斗）」，militant指（人/组织）好战激进的，military指与军队、战争相关的（军事的）

definitions.example: The government announced a plan to upgrade its military equipment to enhance national defense capabilities.

military.etymology: 由词根「milit（战斗、士兵）」加形容词后缀「-ary」构成，指与战斗、军队相关的

similar_words.description: 同根「milit（战斗）」，military指军事的、军队的，militant指（人/组织）好战激进的

definitions.meaning: 磨坊；（尤指纺织、面粉等的）工厂
definitions.example: During the British Industrial Revolution, thousands of rural workers migrated to cities to work in cotton mills.

mill.etymology: 源自古英语「mylen」，原指磨谷物的磨坊，工业革命后引申为纺织、面粉等专用工厂

similar_words.description: 两者均指工厂，mill原指磨坊，现尤指纺织、面粉等专用工厂，factory为通用型工厂

definitions.example: The thickness of the high-precision optical lens is only 0.8 millimeter, which requires strict manufacturing standards.

millimeter.etymology: 词根milli-（表示「千分之一」）+ meter（表示「米」），指千分之一米的长度单位

similar_words.description: 「centimeter」（厘米）与「millimeter」同为长度单位，均含词根「meter」，前者指百分之一米，后者指千分之一米

definitions.example: The self-made millionaire established a foundation to provide scholarships for outstanding students from poor families.

millionaire.etymology: 词根million（表示「百万」）+ 名词后缀-aire（表「具有某种特征的人」），指拥有百万资产的人

similar_words.description: 「billionaire」（亿万富翁）与「millionaire」同为财富量级相关的名词，前者指拥有十亿及以上资产的人，后者指拥有百万资产的人

definitions.meaning: 混合，掺杂；（人）交往，混在一起
definitions.example: At the international symposium on climate change, experts from over 50 countries mingled to discuss carbon reduction strategies.

mingle.etymology: 源自古英语mengan，原义为「混合」，后引申为「在社交场合与人交往」

similar_words.description: 「blend」侧重不同物质融合成新整体，「mingle」可指物质混合，也可指人在群体中自由交往

definitions.example: The aerospace research center developed a miniature satellite to monitor ocean currents in real time.
The small border town is widely regarded as a miniature of the cultural integration between the two countries.

miniature.etymology: 词根mini-（表示「小的」）+ 名词后缀-ature，原指「小尺寸的画作」，后引申为「微型的」「缩影」

similar_words.description: 「microscopic」指需显微镜观察的微观事物，「miniature」指尺寸远小于正常的宏观事物，二者均含表示「小」的词根

definitions.example: Enterprises should adopt green production technologies to minimize the discharge of harmful pollutants.
Some critics tried to minimize the significant impact of the research on renewable energy development.

minimize.etymology: 词根mini-（表示「小的」）+ 动词后缀-mize（表「使…化」），指使变小，引申为「使减少到最低限度」「贬低」

similar_words.description: 「maximize」指使最大化，与「minimize」（使最小化）为反义同根词，均含表示「大小」的词根

definitions.example: The university requires a minimum of two years' relevant work experience for applicants to the postgraduate program.
The local government raised the minimum monthly wage to improve the living standards of low-income groups.

minimum.etymology: 源自拉丁语minimus（表示「最小的」），进入英语后可作名词（最小值）或形容词（最小的）

similar_words.description: 「minimize」是动词，指使减少到最低；「minimum」是名词/形容词，指最低限度/最小的，二者同根

definitions.example: The Minister of Foreign Affairs held a press conference to elaborate on the country's diplomatic policy.
The volunteer ministered to the homeless people in the city center every winter.

minister.etymology: 源自拉丁语minister（表示「仆人，助手」），后引申为「政府官员（部长）」或「宗教仆人（牧师）」

similar_words.description: 「ministry」是名词，指政府部门；「minister」是名词，指部门的负责人（部长），二者同根

definitions.example: The Ministry of Education issued a regulation to reduce the academic burden of primary school students.
He chose to dedicate his life to the ministry after graduating from seminary.

ministry.etymology: 词根minister（部长/牧师）+ 名词后缀-ry（表「职责或集合体」），指「政府部门」或「牧师的职责」

similar_words.description: 「department」可指各类机构的部门，「ministry」特指中央政府的部级部门，范围更窄

definitions.example: The research paper only has a few minor grammatical errors and can be submitted after revision.
The law prohibits the sale of tobacco and alcohol to minors in all public places.

minor.etymology: 源自拉丁语minor（表示「较小的」），进入英语后可作形容词（次要的）、名词（未成年人）

similar_words.description: 「major」指主要的、主修的，与「minor」（次要的、辅修相关的）为反义同根词

definitions.example: Only a minority of scholars hold the view that artificial intelligence will replace human labor completely.
The country has formulated preferential policies to promote the economic development of ethnic minorities.

minority.etymology: 词根minor（较小的）+ 名词后缀-ity（表「性质或状态」），指「少数的状态」，引申为「少数派」「少数民族」

similar_words.description: 「majority」指多数、多数派，与「minority」（少数、少数派）为反义同根词

definitions.example: In the economic model, the net profit is calculated as total revenue minus total costs.
The experiment was conducted at a minus 20 degree Celsius environment to simulate polar conditions.

minus.etymology: 源自拉丁语，原意为「较少的」，词根「min-」表「小、少」，考研中常作介词或形容词使用

similar_words.description: 同根词，均含表「小、少」的词根「min-」，「minimize」为动词「使最小化」，「minus」为介词/形容词「减去；负的」
反义词，「plus」表「加；正的」，「minus」表「减；负的」

definitions.example: The recovery of the severely damaged coral reef is regarded as an ecological miracle by marine biologists.

miracle.etymology: 源自拉丁语「miraculum」，词根「mir-」表「惊奇」，后缀「-acle」表「事物」，意为「令人惊奇的事物」

similar_words.description: 近义词，均表「令人惊奇的事物」，「miracle」侧重超自然或罕见的奇迹，「marvel」侧重令人赞叹的事物
同根词，「miracle」为名词「奇迹」，「miraculous」为形容词「奇迹般的」

definitions.example: Archaeologists unearthed a well-preserved bronze mirror dating back to the Tang Dynasty in the imperial tomb.
The annual report mirrors the growing income inequality between urban and rural populations in the nation.

mirror.etymology: 源自拉丁语「mirari」（惊奇），原指「让人惊奇的反射物」，词根「mir-」表「惊奇」，后缀「-or」表「事物」

similar_words.description: 近义词，均表「反映」，「mirror」侧重具象或抽象的映照，「reflect」更侧重光线、声音的反射或思想的反映
形近词，「mirror」为「镜子；反映」，「mirage」为「海市蜃楼；幻想」

definitions.example: The school implemented a peer mentorship program to reduce adolescent mischief and foster positive social behavior.
The mischief caused by the oil spill has disrupted the local marine ecosystem for decades.

mischief.etymology: 源自古法语「meschief」，由「mis-」（表「坏、错误」）+「chief」（原指「状况」）构成，原意为「坏状况」

similar_words.description: 同根词，「mischief」为名词「恶作剧；损害」，「mischievous」为形容词「淘气的；有害的」
近义词，均表「不当行为」，「mischief」侧重孩童的恶作剧，「misconduct」侧重正式的违规或不当行为

definitions.example: The award-winning documentary vividly portrays the miserable living conditions of slum dwellers in the megacity.
The team's miserable performance in the championship match disappointed their loyal supporters.

miserable.etymology: 源自拉丁语「miser」（可怜的），后缀「-able」表「易于…的」，意为「易于感到可怜的」

similar_words.description: 近义词，均表「悲惨的」，「miserable」侧重内心痛苦或处境恶劣，「wretched」侧重因贫困、不幸而极度悲惨
同根词，「miserable」为形容词「痛苦的」，「miser」为名词「吝啬鬼」（原指「可怜的人」）

definitions.example: The Great Depression brought unprecedented economic misery to millions of working-class families in the United States.

misery.etymology: 源自拉丁语「miseria」，同根于「miser」（可怜的），后缀「-y」表「状态」，意为「可怜的状态」

similar_words.description: 同根词，「misery」为名词「苦难；痛苦」，「miserable」为形容词「痛苦的；悲惨的」
近义词，均表「痛苦」，「misery」侧重长期的苦难，「suffering」侧重具体的痛苦感受

definitions.example: Despite her repeated misfortunes, the renowned physicist remained optimistic and dedicated to her quantum research.

misfortune.etymology: 由「mis-」（表「坏、错误」）+「fortune」（运气）构成，意为「坏运气」

similar_words.description: 近义词，均表「不幸」，「misfortune」侧重意外的厄运，「misery」侧重长期的痛苦或苦难
同根词，「misfortune」为名词「不幸」，「fortune」为名词「运气；财富」

definitions.example: The biased media coverage misled the public into forming a false perception of the international trade dispute.
Exposure to violent online content may mislead vulnerable teenagers into engaging in aggressive behavior.

mislead.etymology: 由「mis-」（表「错误、坏」）+「lead」（引导）构成，意为「错误引导」

similar_words.description: 近义词，均表「误导」，「mislead」侧重故意或无意的错误引导，「misguide」侧重因无知或错误判断而引导
同根词，「mislead」为动词「误导」，「lead」为动词「引导；带领」

definitions.example: The international community expressed grave concern over the country's latest intercontinental ballistic missile test.
The protesters threw various missiles at the police barricade during the violent demonstration.

missile.etymology: 源自拉丁语「mittere」（发送），后缀「-ile」表「可…的」，原指「可发送的物体」，考研中常指「导弹」

similar_words.description: 同根词，均含表「发送」的词根「miss-」，「missile」为名词「导弹」，「mission」为名词「使命；任务」
近义词，均表「发射物」，「missile」侧重军事导弹，「projectile」侧重普通发射物（如炮弹、石块）

definitions.example: Rescue teams deployed drones and sniffer dogs to search for the missing hiker in the dense mountainous forest.
The research team identified several missing control variables that may have skewed the experiment's statistical results.

missing.etymology: 源自动词「miss」（错过；遗漏）的现在分词形式，作形容词表「被错过的、缺失的」

similar_words.description: 近义词，均表「失踪的；丢失的」，「missing」侧重暂时找不到的，「lost」侧重永久丢失或迷路的
同根词，「missing」为形容词「缺失的」，「miss」为动词「错过；想念」

definitions.example: Many international non-governmental organizations take protecting global biodiversity as their core mission.

mission.etymology: 来自拉丁语「mittere（派遣）」，加名词后缀「-ion」，原指被派遣执行任务的群体

similar_words.description: 「mission」指使命、任务，「omission」指省略、遗漏，二者词形相近易拼错

definitions.example: The missionary spent decades establishing schools and medical clinics in underdeveloped rural areas.

missionary.etymology: 由「mission（使命）」加形容词/名词后缀「-ary」构成，指肩负传教使命的人或相关属性

similar_words.description: 「mission」是表使命、任务的名词，「missionary」指传教士或传教的，二者同根

definitions.example: The thin morning mist obscured the view of the ancient castle on the hill.

mist.etymology: 源自古英语「mist」，与荷兰语「mist（雾）」同源，指轻薄的水汽凝结物

similar_words.description: 「mist」指能见度较高的薄雾，「fog」指能见度较低的浓雾，二者表雾的程度不同

definitions.example: During the Victorian era, having a mistress was a widely condemned moral scandal among the upper class.
Virginia Woolf is universally acknowledged as a mistress of modernist stream-of-consciousness narration.

mistress.etymology: 源自古法语「maistresse」，是「master（主人）」的阴性形式，原指家庭女主人

similar_words.description: 「master」指男主人、大师，「mistress」指女主人、情妇或女能手，二者为同根的对应阴性词

definitions.example: Without sufficient contextual information, readers are likely to misunderstand the author’s critical stance.

misunderstand.etymology: 由否定前缀「mis-（错误地）」加「understand（理解）」构成的合成动词

similar_words.description: 「misunderstand」侧重无意的信息误解，「misinterpret」侧重有意或无意的错误解读、阐释

definitions.example: The scientist mixed different chemical reagents carefully to observe the reaction phenomenon.

mix.etymology: 来自拉丁语「miscere（混合）」，与英语「muddle（弄混）」同源

similar_words.description: 「mix」侧重随意混合、打乱，「blend」侧重均匀融合、调和

definitions.example: The novel presents a complex mixture of romantic fantasy and social realism.

mixture.etymology: 由「mix（混合）」加名词后缀「-ture（表行为结果）」构成，指混合后的产物

similar_words.description: 「mixture」指物理混合的产物（各成分保留原性质），「compound」指化学化合的产物（生成新物质）

definitions.example: The wounded soldier began to moan softly when the nurse changed his bandage.

moan.etymology: 源自古英语「mānian」，与德语「möhnen（抱怨）」同源，指低声的呻吟或抱怨

similar_words.description: 「moan」侧重低声、持续的呻吟或抱怨，「groan」侧重沉重、短促的呻吟

definitions.meaning: 暴民，乌合之众；围攻，聚众闹事
definitions.example: The local government deployed additional police officers to disperse the unruly mob gathered at the square.

mob.etymology: 源自拉丁语短语「mobile vulgus（易动的民众）」的缩写，原指流动的无序人群

similar_words.description: 「mob」指无秩序、有暴力倾向的暴民，「crowd」指普通的、无特定倾向的人群

definitions.meaning: 可移动的；流动的；移动电话
definitions.example: The rapid development of mobile communication technology has greatly changed people’s way of life.

mobile.etymology: 来自拉丁语「mobilis（可移动的）」，与英语「move（移动）」同源

similar_words.description: 「mobile」侧重位置易变的、流动的（如人口、设备），「movable」侧重可被人为移动的（如家具、财产）

definitions.example: Public health agencies can mobilize sufficient medical resources to respond to sudden outbreaks.

mobilize.etymology: 由词根「mob（移动）」+ 后缀「-il-（连接成分）」+ 后缀「-ize（使动）」构成，表「使移动→动员、调动」

similar_words.description: mobilize表动员、调动；immobilize表使固定、使不能移动，为同根反义动词

definitions.example: Some critics mock the new environmental policy as unrealistic and inefficient.
Graduate applicants usually take mock interviews to practice before the official assessment.

mock.etymology: 源自古法语「mocquer」，原义为“嘲弄、讥笑”，后引申出“模拟的、假装的”含义

similar_words.description: mock侧重带戏仿或讽刺意味的嘲弄；ridicule侧重公开的、尖锐的嘲笑

definitions.example: Digital technology has transformed people's mode of daily communication.

mode.etymology: 源自拉丁语「modus」，原义为“度量、限度”，引申为“方式、模式”

similar_words.description: mode表方式、模式；model表模型、典范，为形近易混词

definitions.example: Economists develop mathematical models to analyze long-term economic growth.
Researchers model the spread of infectious diseases to predict potential risks.

model.etymology: 与mode同根，源自拉丁语「modus」，表“按度量制作的事物→模型”，后引申出“典范、榜样”及动词义

similar_words.description: model表模型、典范；module表模块、单元，为形近易混词

definitions.example: Moderate daily exercise helps reduce the risk of chronic diseases.
The central bank adjusted interest rates to moderate inflation pressure.

moderate.etymology: 源自拉丁语「modestus」（在度量范围内的），由词根「mod（度量）」+ 后缀「-estus（形容词后缀）」构成，后引申出动词义“缓和、节制”

similar_words.description: moderate侧重程度的适度、缓和；modest侧重人的谦虚或事物的朴素，为形近易混词

definitions.example: Rural modernization has improved living standards for local residents.

modernization.etymology: 由形容词「modern（现代的）」+ 名词化后缀「-ization（表过程）」构成，表“现代化的过程”

similar_words.description: modernization为名词，表现代化（过程）；modernize为动词，表使现代化

definitions.example: The Nobel Prize winner remained modest and refused to publicize his achievements.
The young scholar lives on a modest income in a quiet neighborhood.

modest.etymology: 与moderate同根，源自拉丁语「modestus」，原义为“在度量范围内的”，引申为“谦虚的、朴素的”

similar_words.description: modest侧重人的谦虚或事物的朴素；moderate侧重程度的适度、缓和，为形近易混词

definitions.example: The academic committee decided to modify the thesis submission guidelines.
Regular breaks can modify the negative effects of long hours of study.

modify.etymology: 由词根「mod（度量）」+ 后缀「-ify（使动）」构成，表“使符合既定度量→修改、修正”

similar_words.description: modify侧重对细节的小修改；revise侧重对内容的全面修订，为近义易混词

definitions.example: The graduate research program is divided into four core modules covering quantitative methods.

module.etymology: 由词根「mod（度量）」+ 后缀「-ule（小后缀）」构成，表“小的度量单位→模块、单元”

similar_words.description: module表模块、单元；model表模型、典范，为形近易混词

definitions.example: Moist climate in tropical regions supports the growth of diverse rainforest plants.

moist.etymology: 源自拉丁语「muscus」（苔藓），因苔藓多生长在潮湿环境中，引申为“潮湿的、湿润的”

similar_words.description: moist侧重轻微的、适宜的潮湿；damp侧重令人不适的潮湿，为近义易混词

definitions.example: Desert plants are adapted to retain moisture in their leaves even in extreme heat.

moisture.etymology: 由形容词「moist」（潮湿的）加名词后缀「-ure」构成，表抽象状态或属性。

similar_words.description: 「moist」为形容词，表「潮湿的」；「moisture」为名词，表「潮气、水分」，二者为同根派生词。

definitions.example: Each water molecule consists of two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom.

molecule.etymology: 词根「mole-」（意为“块、团”）加指小后缀「-cule」构成，原指“小块物质”，后专指化学分子。

similar_words.description: 「particle」泛指各类粒子、微粒；「molecule」特指化学、物理意义上的分子，二者语义范围不同。

definitions.example: Any momentary lapse of concentration in the experiment may lead to unexpected errors.

momentary.etymology: 由名词「moment」（瞬间）加形容词后缀「-ary」构成，表“具有某属性的”。

similar_words.description: 「momentary」表时间短暂；「momentous」表意义重大、关键的，二者拼写相近易混淆。

definitions.meaning: （事物发展的）势头，动力；物理动量
definitions.example: The national economy has maintained a strong growth momentum despite the global trade tensions.

momentum.etymology: 词根「moment-」（意为“移动、推动”）加名词后缀「-um」构成，原指物理中的动量，引申为事物发展的势头。

similar_words.description: 「momentum」侧重事物已经形成的持续发展的势头；「impetus」侧重推动事物启动或加速发展的动力。

definitions.example: The monarch signed the new bill into law after a long debate in the parliament.

monarch.etymology: 前缀「mon-」（意为“唯一的”）加词根「arch」（意为“统治”）构成，指“唯一的最高统治者”。

similar_words.description: 「monarch」指君主、统治者本人；「monarchy」指君主制、君主政体这一国家制度。

definitions.example: The central bank adjusted its monetary policy to curb the rising inflation rate.

monetary.etymology: 词根「monet-」（意为“钱、货币”，源自「money」）加形容词后缀「-ary」构成。

similar_words.description: 「monetary」侧重与货币发行、流通、调控相关的；「financial」侧重与财务、资金、财政相关的，适用场景有差异。

definitions.example: Researchers use satellites to monitor the melting of polar ice caps year-round.
The hospital has installed heart monitors in all intensive care units.

monitor.etymology: 词根「mon-」（意为“警告、提醒”）加后缀「-itor」（表人或物）构成，原指“负责提醒、监督的人或设备”。

similar_words.description: 「monitor」侧重持续观察事物的状况或数据变化；「supervise」侧重监督人的工作或行为，监督对象不同。

definitions.example: Researchers observed that the monkey used a small stone to crack open the hard nut shell.

monkey.etymology: 无常见可拆分的考研核心词根词缀，为基础原生词。

similar_words.description: 「monkey」指猴子；「donkey」指驴，二者拼写相近易混淆。

definitions.example: The tech giant was accused of abusing its market monopoly to suppress smaller competitors.

monopoly.etymology: 前缀「mono-」（意为“唯一的”）加词根「poly」（意为“卖”）构成，原指“市场上唯一的卖家”，后引申为垄断。

similar_words.description: 「monopoly」为名词，表垄断（状态或行为）；「monopolize」为动词，表「垄断、独占」。

definitions.example: The long and monotonous routine of factory work made many young people seek other job opportunities.

monotonous.etymology: 前缀「mono-」（意为“单一的”）加词根「ton」（意为“音调”）加形容词后缀「-ous」构成，原指“只有一种音调的”，引申为单调无变化的。

similar_words.description: 「monotonous」侧重因缺乏变化而单调；「tedious」侧重因冗长、繁琐而乏味，语义侧重不同。

definitions.meaning: 怪物；庞然大物（尤指大型机构或企业）
definitions.example: The tech giant, once a small startup, has grown into a market monster dominating the global industry.

monster.etymology: 词根monst-表示「异常、警告」，加名词后缀-er，指形态异常的生物，后引申为庞然大物

similar_words.description: 同根形容词，「monstrous」意为可怕的；巨大的，注意词性不同

definitions.example: The research institute publishes a monthly bulletin on environmental protection trends.

monthly.etymology: 名词month（月）加后缀-ly（构成形容词/副词），作名词时指月刊

similar_words.description: 同后缀时间词，「weekly」意为每周的（地）；周刊，对应时间单位不同

definitions.example: The ancient stone monument in the valley is a symbol of the local civilization dating back 2,000 years.

monument.etymology: 词根mon-表示「提醒、记忆」，加连接字母u和名词后缀-ment，指用来提醒后人的纪念物

similar_words.description: 近义名词，「memorial」侧重纪念性建筑/活动，「monument」侧重具有历史意义的纪念碑或遗迹

definitions.example: The stressful preparation for the postgraduate entrance examination put most candidates in an anxious mood.

mood.etymology: 源自古英语mod，原指「勇气、精神」，后引申为情绪、心情

similar_words.description: 同根形容词，「moody」意为喜怒无常的；情绪化的，注意词性不同

definitions.meaning: 道德的；道义上的；（故事的）寓意
definitions.example: The academic community emphasizes that moral integrity is the foundation of scientific research.

moral.etymology: 源自拉丁语moralis，意为「与习俗相关的」，后引申为道德的，作名词指故事的寓意

similar_words.description: 形近名词，「morale」意为士气；精神状态，拼写差一个字母e，极易混淆

definitions.example: The debate on artificial intelligence involves not only technical issues but also questions of morality.

morality.etymology: 形容词moral（道德的）加名词后缀-ity，构成抽象名词，指道德或道德体系

similar_words.description: 形近名词，「mortality」意为死亡率；死亡，二者词根不同（mor- vs mort-），极易混淆

definitions.example: Moreover, the study found that regular exercise can improve both physical and mental health of postgraduates.

moreover.etymology: 由more（更多）和over（在…之上）构成，指在原有基础上补充更多内容

similar_words.description: 近义连接副词，均用于正式语境表递进，可互换使用

definitions.meaning: 终有一死的；致命的；凡人
definitions.example: As mortal beings, humans should face the reality of life and death with a rational attitude.

mortal.etymology: 词根mort-表示「死亡」，加形容词后缀-al，指会死亡的，作名词指凡人

similar_words.description: 近义形容词，「mortal」侧重「终有一死的」或「导致死亡的」，「fatal」侧重「不可避免的致命的」

definitions.example: The implementation of universal healthcare has significantly reduced the mortality rate of the local population.

mortality.etymology: 词根mort-表示「死亡」，加名词后缀-ality，构成抽象名词，指死亡状态或死亡率

similar_words.description: 形近名词，「morality」意为道德；道德准则，二者词根不同（mort- vs mor-），极易混淆

definitions.example: Many young people choose to take out a 20-year mortgage to buy an apartment in first-tier cities.

mortgage.etymology: 词根mort-表示「死亡」，加名词gage（抵押品），原指「死抵押」（若无法偿还则抵押品归债主），后引申为抵押贷款

similar_words.description: 近义名词/动词，「mortgage」特指房产等的按揭贷款，「pledge」泛指抵押品或承诺

definitions.example: Archaeologists have found a well-preserved Roman mosaic on the site of the ancient villa.
The city's cultural landscape is a rich mosaic of traditional customs and modern art.

mosaic.etymology: 源自希腊语「mousa（缪斯）」，原指缪斯掌管的艺术作品，后引申为镶嵌类艺术及多元拼合的事物。

similar_words.description: 「mosque」意为清真寺，「mosaic」意为马赛克、镶嵌画，二者拼写开头相近易混淆。

definitions.example: Mosquitoes are carriers of multiple infectious diseases that pose threats to public health in tropical regions.

mosquito.etymology: 源自西班牙语，由「mo-（小）」+「mosca（蝇）」构成，原指小型飞虫，后特指蚊子。

similar_words.description: 「mosaic」意为马赛克、镶嵌画，「mosquito」意为蚊子，二者拼写开头相近易混淆。

definitions.example: The damp stone walls of the medieval castle are covered with thick green moss.

moss.etymology: 源自古英语meos，指生长在阴湿环境的苔藓类低等植物。

similar_words.description: 「mousse」意为奶油冻、慕斯，「moss」意为苔藓，二者拼写相近易混淆。

definitions.example: The researchers found that the participants' responses were mostly consistent with the conclusions of the previous study.

mostly.etymology: 由「most（大多数的）」+「-ly（副词后缀）」构成，表程度范围。

similar_words.description: 二者均为副词表“主要地”，mostly侧重数量或比例上占多数，mainly侧重事物的核心或主体部分。

definitions.meaning: 汽车旅馆（为驾车旅客提供住宿及停车场地）
definitions.example: The cross-country travelers chose an inexpensive motel near the highway for the night.

motel.etymology: 缩合词，由「motor（汽车）」+「hotel（旅馆）」拼合而成。

similar_words.description: 「hostel」意为青年旅舍、招待所，「motel」意为汽车旅馆，二者均指经济型住宿场所，拼写相近易混淆。

definitions.example: Newton's laws of motion describe the relationship between an object's mass and its acceleration under external force.
The committee member put forward a motion to revise the local environmental protection regulations.

motion.etymology: 源自拉丁语movere（移动），「-tion」为名词后缀，表动作或状态，后引申为正式场合的提议。

similar_words.description: 「motion」可指抽象的连续运动状态或正式动议；「movement」侧重具体的动作、活动或有组织的社会运动。

definitions.example: Sustainable development goals can motivate enterprises to adopt more environmentally friendly production modes.

motivate.etymology: 由「motive（动机）」+「-ate（动词后缀，使…）」构成，意为使产生动机。

similar_words.description: 「motivate」侧重激发人的内在动机、积极性；「stimulate」侧重外界因素刺激人或事物产生反应。

definitions.example: The police are still investigating the suspect's motive for committing the financial fraud.
The motive power of the ancient watermill comes from the continuously flowing river.

motive.etymology: 源自拉丁语movere（移动），原指驱动人行动的因素，后引申出形容词“驱动的、运动的”义。

similar_words.description: 「motive」作名词时指具体的做事动机、目的，多为可数；「motivation」泛指动力、积极性，多为不可数。

definitions.example: The new energy vehicle is equipped with a high-efficiency electric motor that greatly reduces energy consumption.
Regular outdoor exercise helps improve children's motor coordination and physical fitness.

motor.etymology: 源自拉丁语movere（移动），原指驱动机器的动力装置，后引申为汽车及相关形容词义。

similar_words.description: 「motor」多指小型电动机、马达，也可指代汽车；「engine」多指内燃机、引擎等大型动力装置。

definitions.example: The factory uses precision steel moulds to produce standardized plastic parts for electronic devices.
Family education and social environment jointly mould a child's character and core values.

mould.etymology: 源自古法语modle，原指制作器物的模型，后引申出动词“塑造”义，也可指形似模印纹理的霉菌。

similar_words.description: 「mound」意为土堆、小丘，「mould」意为模具、塑造，二者拼写相近易混淆。

definitions.example: Tensions between the two countries mounted as border disputes escalated.
The mountaineers planned to mount the highest peak before sunrise.

mount.etymology: 源自拉丁语「mons」（山），原指“登山”的动作，引申为“数量上升”

similar_words.description: 「amount」为名词“数量”或动词“总计”，「mount」为动词“增加；登上”

definitions.meaning: 哀悼，悲痛（因死亡、损失等）
definitions.example: Thousands of citizens gathered in the square to mourn the victims of the natural disaster.

mourn.etymology: 源自古英语「murnan」，表“悲伤、哀悼”

similar_words.description: 「morn」为名词“黎明”，「mourn」为动词“哀悼”

definitions.example: Laboratory mice are widely used in biomedical research to test new drug effects.

mouse.etymology: 源自古英语「mus」，与“鼠”的生物属性相关

similar_words.description: 「mouth」为名词“嘴”，「mouse」为名词“老鼠；鼠标”

definitions.example: The environmental movement has gained widespread public support across Europe since the 1990s.
The dancer’s fluid movements captivated the audience during the performance.

movement.etymology: 由词根「move」（移动）+ 名词后缀「-ment」构成，表“移动的行为或状态”

similar_words.description: 「move」为动词“移动”，「movement」为其名词形式，表“运动；动作”

definitions.example: The award-winning movie depicts the struggles of immigrant families in 20th-century America.

movie.etymology: 为「moving picture」（活动影像）的口语化缩写

similar_words.description: 「film」为正式用词，可指“电影；胶片”，「movie」为口语化用词，多指“电影”

definitions.example: Heavy rain turned the construction site into a field of thick mud, delaying the project schedule.

mud.etymology: 源自古英语「mudda」，指“湿软的泥土”

similar_words.description: 「mad」为形容词“疯狂的”，「mud」为名词“泥”

definitions.example: She sipped hot cocoa from a ceramic mug while preparing for the postgraduate entrance exam.
A graduate student was mugged by two strangers on his way back to the dormitory last Friday.

mug.etymology: 源自斯堪的纳维亚语，原指“脸”，后引申为“形状类似脸的杯子”，动词义为口语化的“抢劫”

similar_words.description: 「muggy」为形容词“（天气）闷热的”，「mug」为名词“马克杯”或动词“抢劫”

definitions.example: The postgraduate research project requires multiple data sources to ensure its academic reliability.
The number 18 is a common multiple of 2, 3, 6 and 9.

multiple.etymology: 由前缀「multi-」（多）+ 后缀「-ple」（倍、重）构成，源自拉丁语「multus」（多）

similar_words.description: 「multiple」为形容词“多个的”或名词“倍数”，「multiply」为动词“乘；增加”

definitions.example: Postgraduate math candidates must be able to multiply complex numbers accurately.
High-tech startups have multiplied in the national high-tech zone over the past three years.

multiply.etymology: 由前缀「multi-」（多）+ 后缀「-ply」（折叠、倍）构成，与「multiple」同根

similar_words.description: 「multiply」为动词“乘；增加”，「multiple」为同根形容词“多个的”或名词“倍数”

definitions.example: A multitude of factors contributed to the decline of the traditional print media industry.
The political reform proposal failed to gain support from the multitude of ordinary voters.

multitude.etymology: 由前缀「multi-」（多）+ 名词后缀「-tude」（表状态）构成，源自拉丁语「multitudo」（大量）

similar_words.description: 「attitude」为名词“态度”，「multitude」为名词“大量；民众”

definitions.example: The municipal government has allocated 50 million yuan to upgrade the city's public bicycle system.

municipal.etymology: muni-（表「公共的」） + -cip-（表「掌管、管理」） + -al（形容词后缀，表「与…相关的」）

similar_words.description: 二者均表「地方的」，前者侧重市一级的市政相关事务，后者侧重省一级的地方相关事务

definitions.example: The court convicted the defendant of murder based on sufficient forensic evidence.
The suspect was arrested for murdering a local entrepreneur during a robbery.

murder.etymology: 来自古英语morthor，词根mur-表「死亡」，-der为名词后缀，表「行为」

similar_words.description: 二者均与死亡相关，前者指他杀（谋杀行为），后者指自杀行为

definitions.example: The audience murmured in dissatisfaction when the keynote speaker failed to show up on time.
A murmur of approval spread through the conference room after the reform plan was introduced.

murmur.etymology: 拟声词根murm-（模拟轻柔低语的声音） + -ur（动词/名词后缀）

similar_words.description: 二者均指低声说话，murmur侧重轻柔低语或小声抱怨，mumble侧重含糊不清地嘟囔

definitions.example: Regular resistance training can effectively build muscle and improve overall physical fitness.
The journalist muscled his way through the packed press conference to ask the minister a question.

muscle.etymology: 来自拉丁语musculus，原指「小老鼠」，因肌肉收缩时形似老鼠得名，词根mus-表「老鼠」，-cle为指小后缀

similar_words.description: 二者拼写接近，前者指肌肉或强行挤入，后者指音乐相关的或音乐剧

definitions.meaning: 肌肉的；肌肉发达的，强健的
definitions.example: The swimmer's muscular build is the result of ten years of rigorous daily training.

muscular.etymology: muscle（肌肉） + -ar（形容词后缀，表「具有…性质的」）

similar_words.description: 二者拼写接近，前者指肌肉的、强健的，后者指音乐相关的或音乐剧

definitions.example: The National Museum houses a precious collection of ancient Chinese bronze wares.

museum.etymology: muse（缪斯，希腊神话中掌管艺术的女神） + -um（名词后缀，表「场所」）

similar_words.description: 二者均为展览场馆，museum侧重收藏展示各类文物的综合场馆，gallery侧重美术作品展览或画廊

definitions.example: Health experts warn that eating unidentified wild mushrooms may cause severe food poisoning.

definitions.meaning: 迅速增长，雨后春笋般涌现
definitions.example: New energy vehicle manufacturing bases have mushroomed in the central region over the past five years.

mushroom.etymology: 来自古法语mousseron，原指「苔藓上生长的菌类」，mush-表「伞状」，-room为名词后缀表「物体」

similar_words.description: 二者拼写接近，前者指蘑菇或迅速增长，后者指软糊的物质

definitions.example: The musical performance by the national symphony orchestra received a standing ovation.
The award-winning musical has been staged in more than 20 countries worldwide.

musical.etymology: music（音乐） + -al（形容词/名词后缀，表「与…相关的」或「具有…性质的事物」）

similar_words.description: 二者拼写接近，前者指音乐相关的或音乐剧，后者指肌肉的、强健的

definitions.example: The young Chinese musician won the gold medal at the International Violin Competition.

musician.etymology: music（音乐） + -ian（名词后缀，表「从事某职业的人」）

similar_words.description: 二者均为艺术从业者，musician特指音乐领域的创作者或表演者，artist泛指各类艺术领域的从业者

definitions.example: The usually outspoken student remained mute when questioned about the missing lab equipment.
All participants are required to mute their mobile phones during the academic symposium.

mute.etymology: 来自拉丁语mutus，表「沉默的」，词根mut-表「沉默」，-e为后缀

similar_words.description: 二者均有「沉默的、哑的」含义，mute侧重主观不愿说话或客观无声，dumb侧重生理缺陷导致的哑或愚笨

definitions.meaning: （声音、反应）轻柔的，压低的；（颜色）柔和的
definitions.example: The scholar offered muted criticism of the research methodology in the peer-reviewed journal.

muted.etymology: 由已考核心词「mute」（v. 使静音）的过去分词转化而来，表“被压低的、被弱化的”状态

similar_words.description: 「mute」作动词表“使静音”、作形容词表“哑的”；「muted」仅作形容词，侧重“（声音/反应）被弱化的”

definitions.meaning: 低声嘀咕，喃喃自语（常带不满）
definitions.example: Some audience members muttered complaints about the speaker's off-topic remarks during the conference.

mutter.etymology: 拟声词，模拟人低声不满说话的声音，为情绪类动词

similar_words.description: 「murmur」多指“连续轻柔的低语（如人群、流水声）”；「mutter」侧重“带不满情绪的小声嘀咕”

definitions.example: Roast mutton, once a staple of British rural cuisine, is now less common in urban restaurants.

mutton.etymology: 源自古法语「moton」，指绵羊或羊肉，为食品类基础词汇

similar_words.description: 「lamb」指“羔羊肉（幼羊的肉）”；「mutton」指“成年羊的肉”

definitions.example: The two universities established a mutual exchange program for graduate students.

mutual.etymology: 词根「mut-」表“交换”，加形容词后缀「-ual」，表“可交换的→相互的、共同的”

similar_words.description: 「common」侧重“多个主体共享的（如common interest）”；「mutual」侧重“双方之间相互的（如mutual respect）”

definitions.example: Archaeologists discovered a mysterious ancient manuscript with unreadable characters in the cave.

mysterious.etymology: 词根「myst-」表“神秘”，加形容词后缀「-erious」，表“具有神秘性质的”

similar_words.description: 「mystic」可作名词“神秘主义者”或形容词“神秘主义的”；「mysterious」仅作形容词，侧重“难以解释的神秘”

definitions.meaning: 神秘的事物，谜；侦探小说
definitions.example: The cause of the ancient civilization's collapse remains a long-standing mystery for historians.

mystery.etymology: 词根「myst-」表“神秘”，加名词后缀「-ery」，表“神秘的事物或状态”

similar_words.description: 「puzzle」侧重“需要逻辑解谜的难题（如智力题）”；「mystery」侧重“难以用科学/逻辑解释的神秘事物”

definitions.meaning: 神话；（社会上的）虚构信念，错误观念
definitions.example: The myth that 'wealth equals happiness' has been debunked by numerous psychological studies.

myth.etymology: 源自古希腊语「mythos」，原指“口头传说”，后引申为“神话、未经证实的虚构观念”

similar_words.description: 「legend」多指“基于历史人物/事件的传说”；「myth」多指“解释自然/社会现象的神话或完全虚构的观念”

definitions.example: The carpenter used a hammer and iron nails to assemble the wooden bookshelf.
The federal agents finally nailed the ringleader of the drug trafficking gang.

nail.etymology: 源自古英语「nægl」，原指“钉子”，后引申为“指甲”及动词义“钉牢、抓住”

similar_words.description: 「snail」为名词“蜗牛”，拼写仅差首字母「s」，易与「nail」混淆

definitions.meaning: 天真的，幼稚的（尤指缺乏社会经验）
definitions.example: It is naive to believe that a single policy can solve all the country's economic problems.

naive.etymology: 源自拉丁语「nativus」（天生的），原指“未受教化的”，后引申为“因缺乏经验而天真幼稚的”

similar_words.description: 「native」作形容词表“本地的、天生的”、作名词表“本地人”；「naive」仅作形容词，侧重“缺乏经验的天真”

definitions.meaning: 裸体的；无遮蔽的，赤裸裸的
definitions.example: The artist's controversial painting features a naked figure standing in a barren desert.

naked.etymology: 源自古英语「nacod」，指“未穿衣服的”，为基础描述性形容词

similar_words.description: 「bare」侧重“部分裸露的（如bare hands）”；「naked」侧重“完全裸体的、无任何遮蔽的”

definitions.example: The three core components of the research framework, namely hypothesis formulation, data analysis and conclusion drawing, were discussed in detail in the first chapter.

namely.etymology: 由名词「name」加副词后缀「-ly」构成，原指‘按名字明确指代’，引申为‘也就是，即’

similar_words.description: 「namely」用于明确列举前文提到的具体项，「that is (i.e.)」用于解释前文的抽象概念或换种说法

definitions.example: Many office workers take a 15-minute nap during lunch break to improve work efficiency in the afternoon.

nap.etymology: 源自古英语「hnappian」，原指‘打瞌睡’，引申为‘白天的短时间小睡’

similar_words.description: 「nap」多指白天的短盹（通常10-30分钟），「snooze」更口语化，也可指闹钟的‘贪睡功能’

definitions.example: The laboratory assistant used a sterile napkin to wipe the surface of the petri dish before inoculation.

napkin.etymology: 源自古法语「nappe」（桌布）加指小后缀「-kin」，原指‘小桌布’，后引申为‘餐巾’

similar_words.description: 「napkin」多指用餐或实验中用的专用餐巾/餐巾纸，「tissue」泛指各类纸巾（如面巾纸、卫生纸）

definitions.example: The award-winning documentary narrates the 40-year struggle of a group of farmers to restore the degraded grassland in northern China.

narrate.etymology: 源自拉丁语「narrare」（讲述、叙述），同根词有「narrative」等

similar_words.description: 「narrate」侧重正式、有条理地叙述事件或故事，「recount」侧重详细复述亲身经历或事件的细节

definitions.example: The literary critic pointed out that the novel’s non-linear narrative mirrors the protagonist’s fragmented memory.

narrative.etymology: 源自拉丁语「narrativus」，由「narrare」（讲述）派生，指‘与叙述相关的结构或内容’

similar_words.description: 「narrative」侧重正式文本或学术分析中的叙事结构/内容，「story」泛指各类虚构或真实的故事

definitions.example: The narrow mountain road made it impossible for the large truck to pass through.
The defending champion won the tennis match by a narrow margin of two points.

narrow.etymology: 源自古英语「nearu」，原指‘空间狭窄的’，引申为‘观点狭隘的’或‘结果勉强的’

similar_words.description: 「narrow」可指空间狭窄、观点狭隘或结果勉强，「slender」多指身材苗条或资金、数量微薄

definitions.meaning: 令人讨厌的；恶劣的；严重的
definitions.example: The nasty thunderstorm caused severe damage to the local agricultural crops.

nasty.etymology: 源自中古英语「nasty」，原指‘肮脏的’，引申为‘令人讨厌的、恶劣的或严重的’

similar_words.description: 「nasty」侧重事物令人厌恶或情况恶劣（如天气、言论），「awful」侧重程度极深或主观感觉糟糕

definitions.example: The nation has launched a nationwide campaign to promote awareness of carbon neutrality by 2060.

nation.etymology: 源自拉丁语「natio」（出生、种族），由「nascor」（出生）派生，指‘由共同血缘、文化联结的群体或国家’

similar_words.description: 「nation」侧重国家的人民或民族群体，「state」侧重国家的政权或政体

definitions.meaning: 国家的；民族的；全国性的
definitions.example: The national library will hold a large-scale exhibition of traditional Chinese handicrafts next spring.

national.etymology: 由名词「nation」加形容词后缀「-al」构成，指‘与国家或民族相关的’

similar_words.description: 「national」侧重属于整个国家的（如national park等），「domestic」侧重国内的（与国外相对）或家庭的

definitions.example: The international conference requires all participants to state their full name, occupation and nationality.

nationality.etymology: 由形容词「national」加名词后缀「-ity」构成，指‘法律上的国籍或所属民族属性’

similar_words.description: 「nationality」侧重法律上的国籍或官方认定的民族，「ethnicity」侧重文化、种族层面的族群属性

definitions.example: Many native species in the Amazon rainforest are facing severe threats from illegal logging.
The government has introduced policies to protect the rights of the natives in remote mountainous areas.

native.etymology: 源自拉丁语「natus」（出生），后缀「-ive」表形容词属性，原指「出生于某地的」

similar_words.description: 二者拼写相近，「native」指本土的、本地的，「naive」指天真的、幼稚的

definitions.example: Natural resources such as clean water and solar energy are critical for sustainable development.
The researcher found that infants have a natural ability to recognize human faces.

natural.etymology: 源自名词「nature」（自然），后缀「-al」表形容词属性，意为「与自然相关的」

similar_words.description: 二者同根于拉丁语「natus」（出生），「natural」指自然的、天生的，「native」指本土的、本地的

definitions.example: Human activities have caused irreversible damage to the balance of nature in recent decades.
The debate over whether human nature is inherently good or evil has lasted for centuries.

nature.etymology: 源自拉丁语「natura」（出生、本性），原指「事物的天生属性」，后引申为「自然」

similar_words.description: 二者拼写相近且语义易混，考研常考「nature vs nurture」（天性vs教养），「nature」指自然、天性，「nurture」指养育、培育

definitions.example: The naughty boy was reprimanded by the teacher for disturbing the class with loud jokes.
