naughty.etymology: 源自古英语「nawiht」（无用的），后语义演变为「顽皮的、淘气的」

similar_words.description: 二者拼写相近，「naughty」指顽皮的、淘气的，「naive」指天真的、幼稚的

definitions.example: The country has invested heavily in upgrading its naval fleet to safeguard maritime territorial rights.

naval.etymology: 源自拉丁语「navis」（船），后缀「-al」表形容词属性，意为「与船/海军相关的」

similar_words.description: 二者同根于拉丁语「navis」（船），「naval」为形容词（海军的），「navy」为名词（海军）

definitions.example: Satellite navigation systems have greatly reduced the risk of ship collisions in busy sea lanes.

navigation.etymology: 源自拉丁语「navis」（船）+「igare」（驾驶），后缀「-ation」表名词属性，意为「驾船航行的行为/技术」

similar_words.description: 二者同根于拉丁语「navis」（船），「navigation」为名词（导航、航行），「naval」为形容词（海军的）

definitions.example: The navy conducted a joint military exercise with neighboring countries to enhance maritime cooperation.

navy.etymology: 源自拉丁语「navis」（船），原指「船队」，后专指「海军」

similar_words.description: 二者同根于拉丁语「navis」（船），「navy」为名词（海军），「naval」为形容词（海军的）

definitions.example: A nearby library provides a quiet study environment for students preparing for the postgraduate entrance exam.

nearby.etymology: 由形容词「near」（近的）和副词「by」（在旁边）复合而成，表「在附近的/地」

similar_words.description: 二者拼写相近，「nearby」指附近的（地），「nearly」指几乎、差不多

definitions.example: The neat arrangement of research data in the paper makes it easy for readers to follow the logic.

neat.etymology: 源自拉丁语「nitere」（发光、整洁），原指「干净发亮的」，后引申为「整洁的、简洁的」

similar_words.description: 二者为近义词，「neat」侧重整洁且有条理（常指物品、排版），「tidy」侧重整齐有序（常指经过整理的空间）

definitions.example: The rapid expansion of urban population necessitates the construction of more public transportation infrastructure.

necessitate.etymology: 源自拉丁语「necessarius」（必要的），后缀「-ate」表动词属性，意为「使成为必要」

similar_words.description: 二者同根，「necessary」为形容词（必要的），「necessitate」为动词（使成为必要、需要）

definitions.example: In the context of sustainable development, clean water and sanitation are widely regarded as basic necessities for all communities.
The necessity of implementing strict environmental regulations has been emphasized by numerous scientific studies.

necessity.etymology: 来自同根动词「necessitate」，由否定前缀「ne-」+ 词根「cess（走）」+ 名词后缀「-ity」构成，原指「不能不走的事」，引申为必需品、必要性

similar_words.description: 同根词，前者为名词表必需品/必要性，后者为动词表「使成为必要」

definitions.example: The medical researcher observed abnormal swelling in the neck of the laboratory rat during the experiment.
The neck of the glass bottle was too narrow to pour the thick liquid smoothly.

neck.etymology: 源自古英语「hnecca」，原指人类或动物的脖子，后引申为物体的颈状部分

similar_words.description: 形近同根，前者指身体部位或物体颈状部分，后者指戴在脖子上的饰品项链

definitions.example: The royal necklace, embedded with rare rubies, was the centerpiece of the museum's historical jewelry exhibition.

necklace.etymology: 由「neck（脖子）」+「lace（饰带、带子）」构成，指戴在脖子上的饰带，即项链

similar_words.description: 同根词，前者为饰品项链，后者指身体颈部或物体颈状部分

definitions.example: The tailor used a fine needle to sew the delicate silk fabric without damaging it.
The compass needle stabilized to point north once the hiker moved away from the metal rock formation.

needle.etymology: 源自古英语「nǣdl」，原指缝衣用的针，后引申为仪器的指针等

similar_words.description: 近义易混，前者为有孔的缝衣/仪器针，后者为无孔的固定用别针

definitions.example: The psychological study found that negative social feedback can significantly reduce an individual's self-esteem.
In statistical analysis, the number of negatives in the dataset was far lower than that of positives.

negative.etymology: 由否定前缀「neg-」+ 形容词后缀「-ative」构成，表「否定的、负面的」

similar_words.description: 反义易混，考研态度题常考，前者表负面消极，后者表正面积极

definitions.example: Scholars warn that we should not neglect the cultural context when interpreting ancient literary works.
The child's poor health condition was attributed to the neglect of his legal guardians.

neglect.etymology: 由否定前缀「neg-」+ 词根「lect（选择、关注）」构成，指「不选择、不关注」，即忽视、疏忽

similar_words.description: 同根词，前者为动词/名词表忽视/疏忽行为，后者为形容词表疏忽的

definitions.example: The insurance company refused to compensate the victim, claiming the driver was negligent in ignoring traffic signals.

negligent.etymology: 由否定前缀「neg-」+ 词根「lig（选择、关注）」+ 形容词后缀「-ent」构成，指「不关注的」，即疏忽的

similar_words.description: 形近同根易混，前者指人疏忽的，后者指事物可忽略的

definitions.example: The difference in test scores between the two groups was statistically negligible, so the hypothesis was rejected.

negligible.etymology: 由否定前缀「neg-」+ 词根「lig（选择）」+ 形容词后缀「-ible（可...的）」构成，指「可被不选择的」，即可忽略的

similar_words.description: 形近同根易混，前者指事物无足轻重，后者指人疏忽大意

definitions.example: The labor union and the management will negotiate a new wage agreement to address the workers' demands.
The experienced driver managed to negotiate the narrow mountain road without any difficulty.

negotiate.etymology: 由否定前缀「neg-」+ 词根「oti（休闲）」+ 动词后缀「-ate」构成，原指「不休闲地处理事务」，后引申为谈判、协商

similar_words.description: 近义易混，前者侧重正式协商（如国家、企业），后者侧重日常讨价还价

definitions.meaning: 黑人（旧称，现多具冒犯性）
definitions.example: In 19th-century American historical documents, the term 'negro' was frequently used to refer to enslaved African Americans, though it is now deemed offensive.

negro.etymology: 源自西班牙语「negro（黑色的）」，原指黑人，现为旧称且具冒犯性

similar_words.description: 近义易混，前者为旧称且具冒犯性，后者为中性表述黑人

definitions.example: Residents in the suburban community often gather at the central square to chat with their neighbors after weekend meals.
The coastal province neighbors two major economic hubs with well-developed port systems.

neighbor.etymology: 由古英语词根neah（近的）+ 后缀-bor（居民）构成，原指「近处的居民」

similar_words.description: neighbor为美式拼写，neighbour为英式拼写，二者语义完全一致，考研阅读中可能出现两种拼写形式需辨识
同根词，neighbor指单个邻居，neighborhood指邻里所在的区域或街区

definitions.example: The newly launched community library serves a neighborhood with over 12,000 permanent residents.

neighborhood.etymology: 由词根neighbor（邻居）+ 名词后缀-hood（表区域、群体）构成，指「邻居所在的连片区域」

similar_words.description: 同根词，neighborhood指邻里区域，neighbor指单个邻居个体

definitions.example: My nephew, a postgraduate candidate majoring in finance, is preparing for the entrance examination of a top university.

nephew.etymology: 源自拉丁语nepos，原指「孙辈、男性晚辈亲属」，后语义缩小为「侄子、外甥」

similar_words.description: 对应女性晚辈亲属称谓，nephew指男性（侄子/外甥），niece指女性（侄女/外甥女），拼写接近易混淆

definitions.example: The research team found that certain nutrients can promote the repair of damaged nerve cells in elderly patients.
She nerved herself to deliver a 20-minute academic speech in front of hundreds of professors.

nerve.etymology: 源自拉丁语nervus，原指「肌腱、神经」，后通过隐喻引申出「胆量、勇气」的义项

similar_words.description: 同根词，nerve为名词（神经/勇气），nervous为形容词（紧张的/神经的）

definitions.example: Most postgraduate candidates feel nervous before the written examination due to intense competition.

nervous.etymology: 由词根nerve（神经）+ 形容词后缀-ous（表具有…性质的）构成，指「与神经相关的；易紧张的」

similar_words.description: 近义形容词，nervous侧重因紧张、慌乱而心神不宁，anxious侧重因担忧结果而焦虑不安

definitions.example: The ornithology student observed the sparrow building its nest in the oak tree near the research institute.
Some bird species nest in dense bushes to avoid being detected by predators.

nest.etymology: 源自古英语nest，与拉丁语nidus（巢）同源，原指「鸟类的栖息窝巢」

similar_words.description: 拼写接近易混淆，nest指「巢/筑巢」，net指「网/净的」

definitions.example: The coastal fishermen repaired their fishing net after a storm damaged it last week.
The multinational corporation reported a net profit of 35 million euros in the second quarter of 2024.

net.etymology: 源自古英语net，原指「编织的网状物」，后通过隐喻引申出「净的、纯的」（扣除成本后的剩余）的义项

similar_words.description: 拼写接近易混淆，net指「网/净的」，nest指「巢/筑巢」

definitions.meaning: 网络；网状系统；人际关系网
definitions.example: The new high-speed railway line will integrate the remote mountain area into the national transportation network.

network.etymology: 复合词，由net（网）+ work（系统）构成，原指「网状的结构系统」，后引申为「人际、通信等网络」

similar_words.description: 近义名词，network侧重结构化的功能性网状系统（如交通、通信、人际），web侧重错综复杂的非功能性网状物（如蜘蛛网、万维网）

definitions.meaning: 神经学；神经病学（医学学术义）
definitions.example: The research team in the department of neurology has made breakthroughs in treating Parkinson’s disease.

neurology.etymology: 由希腊语词根neuro-（神经的）+ 学科后缀-logy（…学）构成，指「研究神经系统的医学学科」

similar_words.description: 同根学术词，neurology侧重临床神经病学，neuroscience侧重基础神经科学研究

definitions.example: The small landlocked country remained neutral during the regional military conflict to protect its economic interests.

neutral.etymology: 源自拉丁语neutralis，由否定前缀ne-（不）+ 词根uter（两者之一）构成，原指「不偏向任何一方的」

similar_words.description: 拼写接近易混淆，neutral侧重「立场/性质中立的」，neuter侧重「语法中性的/无性别差异的」

definitions.example: Nevertheless, the cross-cultural study still has several limitations that need to be verified by larger sample sizes.

nevertheless.etymology: 由never（从不）、the、less（表否定/让步）组合而成，表转折让步逻辑

similar_words.description: 均为表转折的副词，拼写接近，考研阅读中常可通用，仅语体微差

definitions.example: Nickel is a key raw material for manufacturing lithium-ion batteries used in new energy vehicles.
He paid for the postcard with a nickel and three pennies.

nickel.etymology: 源自德语「Nickel」（原指“魔鬼”，因早期矿工认为镍矿无实用价值，戏称其为魔鬼的金属）

similar_words.description: 拼写接近，前者指镍或五分镍币，后者指昵称、绰号

definitions.example: Many renowned scientists are known to the public by their affectionate nicknames rather than formal names.

nickname.etymology: 由nick（附加的、额外的）和name（名字）组合而成，指正式名字之外的称呼

similar_words.description: 拼写接近，前者指昵称、绰号，后者指镍或五分镍币

definitions.example: She enrolled her niece in a weekend painting class to develop her artistic talent.

niece.etymology: 源自古法语「niece」，为亲属称谓中「nephew（侄子/甥男）」的阴性对应形式

similar_words.description: 同属晚辈亲属称谓，前者指女性（侄女/甥女），后者指男性（侄子/甥男），拼写接近易混

definitions.example: She woke up sweating after having a terrifying nightmare about being lost in a primeval forest.
The sudden epidemic outbreak was a nightmare for the local tourism industry.

nightmare.etymology: 由night（夜晚）和mare（古英语指“夜间压身的女妖”）组合而成，原指夜间恐怖幻象，后引申为可怕经历

similar_words.description: 均表可怕事物，但nightmare侧重亲身经历的具体困境或噩梦，horror侧重抽象恐惧情绪或恐怖事物

definitions.example: The research team selected nineteen volunteers with no prior medical history for the clinical trial.

nineteen.etymology: 由nine（九）和后缀-teen（表“十几”的数词后缀）组合而成

similar_words.description: 拼写接近，前者表基数词19（十几），后者表基数词90（几十），考研数字细节题易混

definitions.example: Nitrogen is an essential nutrient for plant growth, and most agricultural fertilizers contain high levels of it.

nitrogen.etymology: 由词根nitro-（含氮的、硝基的）和后缀-gen（表“产生…的元素”）组合而成

similar_words.description: 同属常见化学元素，均以-gen为后缀，前者为氮（符号N），后者为氧（符号O），易混淆

definitions.meaning: 高尚的，崇高的（指道德或品格）
definitions.example: His noble decision to donate all savings to the rural school moved countless people.
In medieval Europe, nobles held large amounts of land and political power.

noble.etymology: 源自古拉丁语「nobilis」（著名的、高贵的），后引申为品格高尚的或贵族阶层

similar_words.description: 拼写仅差一个字母，前者为普通词汇表高尚的/贵族，后者为专有名词指诺贝尔奖相关

definitions.meaning: 点头（表同意、打招呼或示意）
definitions.example: When the lecturer mentioned the core argument, the students nodded to show understanding.
She gave a slight nod to the waiter to order another cup of black coffee.

nod.etymology: 源自古英语「hnodian」（点头），为动作衍生词汇

similar_words.description: 拼写接近，前者为动词/名词表点头动作，后者为名词表节点、结（如网络节点）

definitions.example: The noisy construction work near the library seriously affected the students' study efficiency.

noisy.etymology: 由名词noise（噪音）加形容词后缀-y（表“具有…性质的”）组合而成

similar_words.description: 拼写接近，前者表嘈杂的，后者表恶臭的、有害健康的（低频僻义，考研易混）

definitions.example: The king is only the nominal head of the state, with real power held by the prime minister.

definitions.meaning: （金额、费用）象征性的，微不足道的
definitions.example: Academic institutions usually charge only a nominal fee for alumni to access the library.

nominal.etymology: 词根nomin-意为‘名称、名字’，后缀-al为形容词后缀，表‘与…相关的’，合指与名号相关但无实质的

similar_words.description: 二者同根nomin-（名称），nominal为形容词表‘名义上的’，nominate为动词表‘提名’

definitions.example: She was nominated as the candidate for the national academic award by her supervisor.
The board nominated Mr. Smith as the new CEO of the company.

nominate.etymology: 词根nomin-意为‘名称、名字’，后缀-ate为动词后缀，表‘使成为…’，即使某人获得提名或任命的名号

similar_words.description: 二者同根nomin-（名称），nominate为动词表‘提名、任命’，nominal为形容词表‘名义上的’

definitions.example: The new policy has some unavoidable flaws; nonetheless, it can effectively improve the efficiency of public services.

nonetheless.etymology: 由否定前缀non-、定冠词the、形容词less（更少的）组合而成，表让步转折逻辑，即不受前述情况影响

similar_words.description: 二者均为表让步转折的副词，多数语境可互换，nonetheless语气稍显正式

definitions.example: His claim that social media has completely replaced traditional print media is absolute nonsense.

nonsense.etymology: 前缀non-表否定，词根sense意为‘意义、道理’，合指没有道理、毫无意义的言论

similar_words.description: nonsense含否定前缀non-，表‘荒谬的言论’，sense为词根同时也是独立单词，表‘意义、理智’

definitions.example: In most East Asian cultures, noodles are widely regarded as a symbol of longevity and good fortune.
Some critics dismissed the inexperienced young manager as a noodle who could not handle the crisis.

noodle.etymology: 源自德语Nudel，原指一类面食，后引申出口语化的‘笨蛋’义

similar_words.description: 二者拼写相近易混淆，noodle意为‘面条；笨蛋’，needle意为‘针；指针’

definitions.example: Social norms often determine what kind of behavior is considered acceptable in a specific community.
Remote work has gradually become the norm for many knowledge workers after the global pandemic.

norm.etymology: 本身为词根，意为‘规则、标准’，是normal、normalize等词的核心构词部分

similar_words.description: 二者均可表‘标准’，norm侧重社会群体默认的行为规范，standard侧重官方或行业制定的技术/质量标准

definitions.example: After one week of rest, the patient’s blood pressure returned to a normal level.

definitions.meaning: 精神正常的，神志健全的
definitions.example: The judicial evaluation confirmed that the suspect was mentally normal when committing the crime.

normal.etymology: 词根norm-意为‘规则、标准’，后缀-al为形容词后缀，表‘符合…的’，即符合常规标准的

similar_words.description: 二者均可表‘平常的’，normal侧重符合既定标准或常规状态，ordinary侧重普通、无特殊之处

definitions.example: The two countries signed a joint statement to promote the normalization of bilateral economic and trade relations.

normalization.etymology: 由形容词normal（正常的）加名词化后缀-ization构成，表‘使成为正常状态的过程’

similar_words.description: 二者均含‘标准化’之意，normalization侧重恢复到正常状态，standardization侧重符合统一的规定标准

definitions.example: The newly built national wetland park is located in the northeast part of the province.

northeast.etymology: 由north（北）和east（东）组合而成的方位复合词，可作名词、形容词、副词使用

similar_words.description: 二者均为方位复合词，northeast指东北，northwest指西北，后半部分拼写不同易混淆

definitions.example: Many rare wild animal species are protected in the dense forests of the northern mountainous areas.

northern.etymology: 由名词north（北）加方位形容词后缀-ern构成，表‘属于北方区域的’

similar_words.description: 二者均为方位形容词，后缀相同，northern表‘北方的’，southern表‘南方的’，首部分拼写不同易混淆

definitions.example: The national park is located in the northwest of the province, covering an area of 500 square kilometers.
The northwest wind brought heavy snow to the northern region last winter.

northwest.etymology: 复合词，由词根north（北）和west（西）组合而成，表方位

similar_words.description: 二者均为方位复合词，「northwest」指西北，「northeast」指东北

definitions.example: The study revealed a notable correlation between regular exercise and improved cognitive function in elderly people.

notable.etymology: 词根not（标记、注意）+ 形容词后缀-able（可…的），意为「可被注意到的」→值得注意的

similar_words.description: 二者均含「值得注意的」义，「notable」还可作名词指「名人」，「noteworthy」仅作形容词

definitions.example: The linguist took detailed notes on dialect variations in a leather notebook during fieldwork.
Most graduate students use a lightweight notebook for online courses and data analysis.

notebook.etymology: 复合词，由note（记录）和book（本子）组合而成，原指记录用的本子，后引申为笔记本电脑

similar_words.description: 二者均与书写记录相关，「notebook」指装订成册的本子或笔记本电脑，「notepad」指便签本或电子记事本

definitions.example: The researcher's findings on climate change adaptation are noteworthy for their practical implications for policymakers.

noteworthy.etymology: 复合词，由note（注意）和worthy（值得的）组合而成，意为「值得注意的」

similar_words.description: 二者均为形容词表「值得注意的」，「noteworthy」仅作形容词，「notable」还可作名词指「名人」

definitions.example: Economists noticed a gradual decline in consumer confidence amid rising inflation.
The university posted a notice on its official website regarding the adjustment of tuition fees.

notice.etymology: 词根not（标记）+ 后缀-ice（兼作名词/动词后缀），原指「做标记使人注意」，引申为「注意到」「通知」

similar_words.description: 二者同根，「notice」可作动词表「无意注意到」或名词表「通知」，「notify」仅作动词表「正式通知」

definitions.example: There has been a noticeable reduction in plastic waste since the city implemented the ban on single-use plastics.

noticeable.etymology: 动词notice（注意）+ 形容词后缀-able（可…的），意为「可被注意到的」→显而易见的

similar_words.description: 二者均含「明显的」义，「noticeable」侧重容易被察觉的（尤指变化），「notable」侧重值得被注意的（含重要性）

definitions.example: The immigration office will notify applicants of their visa status within 15 working days.

notify.etymology: 词根not（标记）+ 动词后缀-ify（使…），意为「使…被注意到」→正式通知

similar_words.description: 二者同根，「notify」为及物动词，表「正式通知某人」，需接宾语；「notice」可作动词表「无意注意到」或名词表「通知」

definitions.example: The sociologist challenged the traditional notion that gender roles are fixed and unchangeable.

notion.etymology: 词根not（标记、认知）+ 名词后缀-ion，意为「认知到的内容」→概念、看法

similar_words.description: 二者均表「概念、观念」，「notion」侧重个人或群体的模糊、未成熟的看法，「concept」侧重系统、明确的概念

definitions.meaning: 臭名昭著的，声名狼藉的
definitions.example: The 19th-century factory was notorious for its harsh working conditions and child labor practices.

notorious.etymology: 词根not（标记）+ 形容词后缀-orious（表「具有…性质的」），原指「因恶行被广泛标记的」→臭名昭著的

similar_words.description: 二者均表「有名的」，「notorious」为贬义，指「臭名昭著的」；「famous」为褒义，指「著名的」

definitions.example: Notwithstanding the limited funding, the research team achieved significant progress in renewable energy development.

notwithstanding.etymology: 复合词，由not（不）和withstanding（抵挡、承受）组合而成，原指「不被（困难）抵挡」→尽管

similar_words.description: 二者均表「尽管如此」，「notwithstanding」可作介词、连词或副词，后可接名词短语；「nevertheless」仅作副词，多置于句首或句中

definitions.example: In academic writing, precise use of nouns helps convey complex logical relationships clearly.

noun.etymology: 源自拉丁语nomen（名字），为语法学核心术语，指代表示人、事、物的词类

similar_words.description: 「pronoun」指代词，「noun」指名词，二者均为语法词性术语，易在语法类考题中混淆

definitions.example: Scholars in humanities should nourish their critical thinking through extensive reading and cross-disciplinary research.

nourish.etymology: 词根nour-（源自拉丁语nurire，意为「滋养」）+ 动词后缀-ish，表「使…得到滋养」

similar_words.description: 「nurture」侧重「养育、培育（人或抽象事物的发展）」，「nourish」侧重「提供养分以滋养（身体或思想）」，语义侧重点易混用

definitions.example: This Nobel Prize-winning novel explores the ethical dilemmas of scientific research in the 21st century.
Researchers proposed a novel algorithm to improve the accuracy of facial recognition systems.

novel.etymology: 源自拉丁语novus（新的），名词义引申为「新创作的长篇文学作品」，形容词义直接保留「新的」引申义

similar_words.description: 「fiction」指虚构类文学（含小说、短篇故事等），「novel」特指长篇虚构小说，语义范围易混淆

definitions.example: The novelty of remote work faded quickly as employees faced challenges of blurred work-life boundaries.

novelty.etymology: 形容词novel（新奇的）+ 名词后缀-ty（表性质、状态），指「新奇的性质或事物」

similar_words.description: 「novel」可作形容词表「新奇的」或名词表「小说」，「novelty」仅作名词表「新奇（性/事物）」，词性与语义范围易混淆

definitions.example: Nowadays, an increasing number of college students choose to pursue postgraduate studies to enhance their competitiveness.

nowadays.etymology: 合成副词，由now（现在）+ a（连接成分）+ days（日子）构成，表「当前的一段时期」

similar_words.description: 「now」指「此刻、现在」（具体时间点），「nowadays」指「现今、时下」（较长的时间段），时间范围易混淆

definitions.example: Nowhere is the impact of urbanization on biodiversity more obvious than in tropical rainforest regions.

nowhere.etymology: 合成词，由no（无）+ where（哪里）构成，表「不存在任何地方」

similar_words.description: 「anywhere」用于否定/疑问句表「任何地方」，「nowhere」本身表否定含义「无处」，否定逻辑易混淆

definitions.example: The international community has reached a consensus on strengthening the supervision of nuclear power plants.

nuclear.etymology: 词根nucle-（源自拉丁语nucleus，意为「核」）+ 形容词后缀-ar，表「与核相关的」

similar_words.description: 「nucleus」是名词表「原子核；核心」，「nuclear」是形容词表「核能的；核心的」，词性易混淆

definitions.example: The nucleus of a research team usually consists of senior scholars with rich academic experience.

nucleus.etymology: 源自拉丁语nucleus（原指「坚果核」），后引申为「事物的核心部分」，为nuclear的名词形式

similar_words.description: 「nuclear」是形容词表「核能的；核心的」，「nucleus」是名词表「原子核；核心」，词性易混淆

definitions.example: The constant noise from the nearby construction site has become a serious nuisance for local residents.

nuisance.etymology: 源自古法语nuisance（伤害），源自拉丁语nocere（伤害），后引申为「造成困扰的人或事」

similar_words.description: 「annoyance」侧重「烦恼的情绪状态」，「nuisance」侧重「造成烦恼的具体人或事」，语义侧重点易混淆

definitions.example: After walking in the snow for two hours, her toes felt numb and she could barely feel them.
The repeated failures in experiments numbed his initial enthusiasm for scientific research.

numb.etymology: 源自古英语num（被剥夺感觉的），原指生理上失去知觉，后引申为心理上的麻木

similar_words.description: 「dumb」表「哑的；愚蠢的」，「numb」表「麻木的」，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The numerical data from the experiment reveal a significant correlation between two variables.

numerical.etymology: 词根为「numer-」（表「数字」），加形容词后缀「-ical」（表「与…有关的」）构成

similar_words.description: 二者均以「numer-」开头，「numerical」表「数字的」，「numerous」表「许多的」

definitions.example: Numerous studies have shown that regular exercise improves cognitive function in adults.

numerous.etymology: 词根为「numer-」（表「数字」），加形容词后缀「-ous」（表「充满…的」）构成，字面义为「充满数字的」，引申为「众多的」

similar_words.description: 二者均以「numer-」开头，「numerous」表「许多的」，「numerical」表「数字的」

definitions.example: The local government has funded 12 new nurseries to address the shortage of childcare services.

nursery.etymology: 词根为「nurse」（表「护理、养育」），加名词后缀「-ery」（表「场所」）构成，指「养育幼儿的场所」即托儿所，也指「培育植物的场所」即苗圃

similar_words.description: 二者同根（均与「养育」相关），「nursery」表「场所」，「nurture」表「养育的动作或概念」

definitions.meaning: 养育；培养（动词）；养育，培养（名词）
definitions.example: Parents should nurture their children's curiosity rather than suppress it.

nurture.etymology: 词根为「nurt-」（等于「nurse」，表「养育」），加后缀「-ure」（可作名词或动词后缀）构成

similar_words.description: 二者同根（均与「养育」相关），「nurture」表「动作或概念」，「nursery」表「场所」

definitions.example: Squirrels store nuts in tree hollows for winter food.
The mechanic tightened the nut on the engine bolt to secure the component.

nut.etymology: 源自中古英语「nute」，无考研核心词根，为基础名词

similar_words.description: 二者均与「滋养」相关，「nut」指「含营养的坚果」，「nutrient」指「营养物质」

definitions.example: Proper nutrition is essential for the healthy physical and mental development of adolescents.

nutrition.etymology: 词根为「nutri-」（等于「nourish」，表「滋养」），加名词后缀「-tion」构成

similar_words.description: 二者同根（均与「滋养」相关），「nutrition」为名词表「营养」，「nourish」为动词表「滋养」

definitions.meaning: 尼龙（聚酰胺纤维的商品名）
definitions.example: Nylon is widely used in the production of outdoor clothing and industrial ropes due to its high tensile strength.

nylon.etymology: 源自美国杜邦公司的商品名，无考研核心词根，为专业名词

similar_words.description: 二者均为合成纤维，「nylon」是尼龙，「rayon」是人造丝

definitions.example: The ancient oak tree in the campus courtyard has become a symbol of the university's long history.

oak.etymology: 源自古英语「ac」，无考研核心词根，为植物类名词

similar_words.description: 二者均为落叶乔木，「oak」是橡树，「elm」是榆树

definitions.example: The rower adjusted the angle of the oar to navigate through the narrow river channel.

oar.etymology: 源自古英语「ār」，无考研核心词根，为水上工具类名词

similar_words.description: 二者字形相近，「oar」指「桨」，「ore」指「矿石」

definitions.meaning: 誓言，誓约（尤指法律或宗教场合的正式誓言）
definitions.example: Witnesses in the federal court must take a solemn oath to tell the whole truth.

oath.etymology: 源自古英语「āth」，无考研核心词根，为法律/宗教类名词

similar_words.description: 二者均表「承诺」，「oath」侧重法律或宗教场合的正式誓言，「pledge」侧重一般的承诺或保证

definitions.example: In traditional patriarchal societies, strict obedience to the orders of male elders was considered a moral duty for women.

obedience.etymology: 来自动词obey的名词形式，词根edi-表「听」，前缀ob-表「朝向」，-ence为名词后缀，字面义为听从、服从。

similar_words.description: 「obedience」为名词，意为服从、顺从；「obeseness」为名词，意为肥胖，二者拼写接近易混淆。

definitions.example: The obedient participants strictly followed the instructions given by the lead researcher during the psychological experiment.

obedient.etymology: 词根edi-表「听」，前缀ob-表「朝向」，-ent为形容词后缀，意为听从的、顺从的。

similar_words.description: 「obedient」指服从的、顺从的；「obeisant」指恭敬的、行礼的，二者拼写相近易混淆。

definitions.example: All citizens, regardless of their social status, must obey the laws enacted by the national legislature.

obey.etymology: 词根edi-表「听」，前缀ob-表「朝向」，字面义为听从、遵守。

similar_words.description: 「obey」可作及物/不及物动词，可直接接宾语；「comply」为不及物动词，需与介词with搭配，二者均表遵守、服从。

definitions.example: Archaeologists carefully analyzed the material and texture of each small object unearthed from the Bronze Age site.
Many environmental activists object to the large-scale exploitation of fossil fuels in ecologically fragile areas.

object.etymology: 前缀ob-表「朝向、反对」，词根ject-表「投掷」；作名词时字面义为扔在面前的事物，即物体、对象；作动词时字面义为反向抛出不同意见，即反对。

similar_words.description: 「object」作名词表物体、对象，作动词表反对；「subject」作名词表主题、受试者，作动词表使服从，二者拼写与义项均易混淆。

definitions.example: Despite strong objection from the opposition party, the new immigration bill was passed by the parliament.

objection.etymology: 来自动词object（反对）的名词形式，-ion为名词后缀，表行为或状态。

similar_words.description: 「objection」多指具体的反对意见或异议；「opposition」多指抽象的反对立场或整体反对势力，二者均表反对。

definitions.example: When analyzing experimental results, researchers must remain objective and avoid being influenced by personal expectations.
The primary objective of this longitudinal study is to explore the long-term impact of early education on children's cognitive development.

objective.etymology: 来自名词object，-ive为兼类后缀，作形容词表基于事实的、客观的；作名词表追求的对象，即目标。

similar_words.description: 「objective」指客观的、以事实为依据的；「subjective」指主观的、以个人感受为基础的，二者为反义形容词，常成对考查。

definitions.example: According to the international treaty, each signatory country has the obligation to reduce carbon emissions by 30% before 2030.

obligation.etymology: 前缀ob-表「朝向」，词根lig-表「绑定、约束」，-ation为名词后缀，字面义为被绑定的责任，即义务。

similar_words.description: 「obligation」多指法律、合同或规则规定的强制性义务；「responsibility」多指分内应承担的主动责任，二者均表责任。

definitions.example: The newly revised labor law obliges all employers to provide equal pay for equal work to male and female employees.
To oblige the visiting scholars from overseas universities, the academic center arranged special shuttle buses between the hotel and the conference venue.

oblige.etymology: 前缀ob-表「朝向」，词根lig-表「绑定」，字面义为被绑定必须做某事，即迫使；引申为施恩使对方欠人情。

similar_words.description: 「oblige」为常用动词，表迫使、施恩；「obligate」为正式法律用语，多表使负有法律义务，二者为同根形近词。

definitions.meaning: 模糊的，费解的；鲜为人知的
definitions.example: The meaning of the symbols on the ancient stone tablet remains obscure to most historians even after decades of research.
The sudden heavy sandstorm obscured the drivers' vision, leading to a series of traffic accidents on the highway.

obscure.etymology: 前缀ob-表「覆盖」，词根scur-表「遮盖」，字面义为被盖住的，即模糊的；作动词表遮盖、使模糊。

similar_words.description: 「obscure」多指因信息不足、模糊或未知而难以理解；「ambiguous」多指因存在多种含义而有歧义，二者均表难以明确的。

definitions.example: The research team collected a large amount of first-hand data through 12 months of continuous observation of the polar bear population.
The professor's sharp observation on the flaws of the current economic system has triggered heated discussions in academic circles.

observation.etymology: 来自动词observe，前缀ob-表「朝向」，词根serv-表「看」，-ation为名词后缀，表观察的行为或结果，引申为观察后发表的评论。

similar_words.description: 「observation」指观察、观测或评论；「observance」指对法律、习俗等的遵守、奉行，二者为同根形近词易混淆。

definitions.meaning: 观察；观测（尤指科研、行为研究中的系统观察）
definitions.example: Researchers observed the foraging behavior of wild birds over a six-month period.
Most European countries observe Christmas as a national public holiday.

observe.etymology: ob-（表加强）+ serv（词根，意为「保持、服务」）+ 动词后缀-e；原指「持续保持关注（观察）」或「保持节日习俗（庆祝）」

similar_words.description: 形近词；「reserve」意为「保留；预订」，「observe」意为「观察；庆祝节日」

definitions.meaning: 使痴迷；使心神不宁（尤指无法控制地反复思考）
definitions.example: The pressure of college admission obsessed many high school seniors.

obsess.etymology: ob-（表加强）+ sess（词根，意为「坐」）+ 动词后缀；原指「一直坐着（反复思考某事物）」，引申为「痴迷」

similar_words.description: 同根词；「obsess」是动词，表「使某人痴迷」的动作；「obsession」是名词，表「痴迷的状态或事物」

definitions.meaning: 痴迷；执念；困扰人的念头
definitions.example: His obsession with academic excellence led to chronic sleep deprivation.

obsession.etymology: ob-（表加强）+ sess（词根，意为「坐」）+ -ion（名词后缀）；由动词obsess派生而来，表「痴迷的状态或事物」

similar_words.description: 同根词；「obsession」是名词，表「痴迷状态」；「obsess」是动词，表「使某人痴迷」的动作

definitions.meaning: 过时的；淘汰的（尤指因技术更新不再使用）
definitions.example: Traditional film cameras have become largely obsolete with the rise of digital photography.

obsolete.etymology: ob-（表离开、不再）+ solet（词根，意为「使用」）+ 形容词后缀；原指「不再被使用的」

similar_words.description: 形近词；「obsolete」意为「过时的；淘汰的」，「obstinate」意为「固执的；顽固的」

definitions.meaning: 障碍；阻碍（抽象或具体的阻挡物）
definitions.example: Lack of professional skills is a major obstacle to young people's employment.

obstacle.etymology: ob-（表相反、对面）+ st（词根，意为「站」）+ -acle（名词后缀，表事物）；原指「站在对面阻挡的事物」

similar_words.description: 近义同根词；「obstacle」多指已存在的「固定障碍事物」；「obstruction」多指主动实施的「阻碍行为」或临时「阻塞物」

definitions.meaning: 固执的；顽固的（尤指拒绝改变观点）
definitions.example: The obstinate CEO refused to adopt the new market strategy proposed by his team.

definitions.meaning: 难以去除的；顽固的（指污渍、疾病等）
definitions.example: The obstinate ink stain on the research paper could not be removed completely.

obstinate.etymology: ob-（表加强）+ stin（词根，意为「站」）+ -ate（形容词后缀）；原指「一直站着不动摇的」

similar_words.description: 形近词；「obstinate」意为「固执的；难以去除的」，「obsolete」意为「过时的；淘汰的」

definitions.meaning: 阻碍；阻塞；遮挡（尤指物理或政策上的阻挡）
definitions.example: The fallen power line obstructed the main road leading to the industrial park.

obstruct.etymology: ob-（表相反、对面）+ struct（词根，意为「建造」）；原指「在对面建造以阻挡」

similar_words.description: 形近同根词；「construct」意为「建造；构建」，「obstruct」意为「阻碍；阻塞」，二者词根均为「struct（建造）」

definitions.meaning: 阻碍（行为）；阻塞（物）（尤指主动实施的阻挡）
definitions.example: The parliamentary opposition caused an obstruction to the passage of the new tax bill.

obstruction.etymology: ob-（表相反、对面）+ struct（词根，意为「建造」）+ -ion（名词后缀）；由动词obstruct派生而来，表「阻碍的行为或事物」

similar_words.description: 近义同根词；「obstruction」多指「主动阻碍行为或临时阻塞物」；「obstacle」多指「已存在的固定障碍」

definitions.meaning: 获得；得到（尤指通过努力或正规途径）
definitions.example: She obtained a doctoral degree in environmental science from a top university.
The ancient custom of communal farming still obtains in some remote rural areas.

obtain.etymology: ob-（表加强）+ tain（词根，意为「抓住」）；原指「抓住（某物）」，引申为「获得」；僻义为「（习俗）一直抓住（存在）」

similar_words.description: 形近近义词；「obtain」多指「获得具体事物（如学位、资金）」；「attain」多指「达到抽象目标（如水平、成就）」

definitions.meaning: 明显的；显而易见的（无需推理即可看出）
definitions.example: It is obvious that global warming has posed a serious threat to biodiversity.

obvious.etymology: ob-（表加强）+ vi（词根，意为「看」）+ -ous（形容词后缀）；原指「完全能被看到的」

similar_words.description: 近义词；「obvious」强调「无需推理的明显」；「evident」强调「有证据支撑的明显」

definitions.example: Many graduates delivered inspiring speeches on the occasion of their graduation ceremony.
I have had no occasion to visit him recently since we have no academic cooperation now.

occasion.etymology: oc-（表加强）+词根cas（降落、发生）+-ion（名词后缀），原指偶然发生的事件，后引申为场合、时机

similar_words.description: occasion既可指特定正式场合，也可指合适时机；opportunity仅侧重利于做某事的机会

definitions.example: The scientist only makes occasional visits to the remote research station due to the harsh local weather.
The university hired some occasional tutors to help students prepare for the postgraduate entrance exam.

occasional.etymology: 由名词occasion加形容词后缀-al构成，表「有时发生的」

similar_words.description: occasional侧重事件发生的频率低；casual侧重态度或形式随意、非正式

definitions.example: Please state your name, age and occupation when filling in this official application form for the research project.
The occupation of too much social media time has greatly reduced students' learning efficiency.

occupation.etymology: oc-（表加强）+词根cup（拿、占有）+-ation（名词后缀），原指占有职位，后引申为职业、占领

similar_words.description: occupation泛指各类职业；profession特指需要专业技能的高端职业如医生、律师

definitions.meaning: 占据，占有（空间、时间、职位等）
definitions.example: Environmental protection issues occupy a central position in the government's annual work report.
The retired professor occupies himself with compiling a vocabulary handbook for postgraduate entrance exam candidates.

occupy.etymology: oc-（表加强）+词根cup（拿、抓），表「抓住并占有」，引申为占据、使忙碌

similar_words.description: occupy侧重占据空间、时间等抽象或具体范畴；possess侧重拥有财产、品质等事物

definitions.example: Cases of academic plagiarism occur frequently in some well-known universities according to the latest investigation.

definitions.meaning: （想法、主意）被想到，浮现在脑中
definitions.example: It never occurred to the young researcher that his paper would be published in such a prestigious international academic journal.

occur.etymology: oc-（表加强）+词根cur（跑、流动），原指「跑来、来临」，引申为发生、被想到

similar_words.description: occur为正式用词，可指计划内或意外的事件；happen为日常用词，多指意外发生的事

definitions.example: Frequent extreme weather events are now a common occurrence across the globe.
The sudden occurrence of the epidemic forced many universities to switch to online teaching temporarily.

occurrence.etymology: 由动词occur加名词后缀-ence构成，表「发生的事或发生的过程」

similar_words.description: occurrence泛指任何大小的事件；incident多指不重要的小事或突发冲突事件

definitions.example: Marine biologists are exploring the deep ocean to study unknown species of marine creatures.
We have oceans of raw data to analyze before we can draw a reliable conclusion for the research.

ocean.etymology: 源自古希腊语ōkeanos，原指环绕陆地的巨流，后引申为海洋

similar_words.description: ocean指面积广阔的大洋；sea指靠近陆地、面积较小的海

definitions.meaning: 奇怪的，怪异的，不同寻常的
definitions.example: His odd behavior during the postgraduate interview made the recruiters doubt his suitability for the major.
Students with odd student numbers were arranged to take the exam in the second teaching building.

odd.etymology: 源自古挪威语odda，原指「尖端、奇数」，后引申为奇怪的、零散的

similar_words.description: odd侧重异乎寻常、令人困惑的奇怪；strange侧重陌生、新奇的奇怪

definitions.example: The odds are that the research team will make a breakthrough in the field of artificial intelligence this year.
His experimental conclusion is at odds with the previous research findings published in the core journal.

odds.etymology: 是形容词odd的复数名词形式，原指「差额、差异」，后引申为可能性、分歧

similar_words.description: odd为形容词，表奇怪的、奇数的；odds为复数名词，表可能性、分歧
odds为常用词，多指实际情形下的可能性；probability为正式学术用词，指数学概率或理论可能性

definitions.example: The pungent odor in the chemical lab warned the researchers that there might be a toxic gas leak.
The local official is in good odor among residents after he promoted a series of livelihood improvement policies.

odor.etymology: 源自拉丁语odor，原指气味，后引申为名声的好坏如同气味一般可被感知

similar_words.description: odor为正式用词，多指难闻的气味；smell为通用词，可指任何好闻或难闻的气味

definitions.example: Driving without a license is a serious offence in most countries.

offence.etymology: 词根offend（冒犯）+名词后缀-ence，表行为或状态，为英式拼写

similar_words.description: 「offence」为英式拼写，「offense」为美式拼写，语义无差异

definitions.example: The comments made by the politician offended many voters.

offend.etymology: 来自拉丁语offendere（撞击，冒犯），of-（=ob- 反对）+fendere（击打）

similar_words.description: 「offend」为动词，表动作；「offence」为名词，表行为或结果

definitions.meaning: 冒犯的，无礼的；进攻性的
definitions.example: The company was criticized for using offensive language in its advertisement.

offensive.etymology: 词根offend（冒犯）+形容词后缀-ive，表属性

similar_words.description: 「offensive」为形容词，表性质；「offend」为动词，表动作

definitions.example: Many universities offer scholarships to international students with excellent academic records.

offer.etymology: 来自拉丁语offerre（带来，提供），of-（=ob- 向）+ferre（带来）

similar_words.description: 「offer」侧重主动提出或给予；「provide」侧重为某人提供所需物品或服务

definitions.example: The police officer directed the traffic at the busy intersection.

officer.etymology: 词根office（官职，办公处）+名词后缀-er，表人

similar_words.description: 「officer」侧重穿制服的执法或军事官员；「official」侧重文职或行政官员

definitions.meaning: 官员，行政人员；官方的，正式的
definitions.example: The official announcement of the policy change will be made next week.

official.etymology: 词根office（官职）+后缀-ial，可作名词或形容词

similar_words.description: 「official」侧重文职行政官员，可作形容词表官方的；「officer」侧重穿制服的执法或军事官员

definitions.example: The increase in production costs was offset by higher sales revenues.

offset.etymology: 源自短语set off（抵消，出发），由set（设置）+off（离开）构成

similar_words.description: 「offset」侧重用等量事物抵消损失或影响；「counteract」侧重采取行动抵消某种作用

definitions.example: The study examines the health outcomes of offspring born to mothers who smoked during pregnancy.

offspring.etymology: off（离开）+spring（出生，萌芽），原指离开母体出生的后代

similar_words.description: 「offspring」可指人或动植物的后代，也可指抽象产物；「descendant」仅指人的后代，强调血缘传承

definitions.example: The price of crude oil has a significant impact on the global economy.

oil.etymology: 来自古英语oil，原指橄榄油，后泛指各类油

similar_words.description: 「oil」特指油类（如石油、食用油）；「fuel」泛指可燃烧产生能量的燃料

definitions.example: The project manager gave her okay to proceed with the new marketing campaign.

okay.etymology: 源自19世纪美国口语，为「oll korrect（all correct）」的缩写变体

similar_words.description: 「okay」为口语化的同意或认可，可作多种词性；「alright」侧重情况尚可，多用于非正式语境

definitions.example: Researchers must not omit key data that contradicts their initial hypotheses.

omit.etymology: 由前缀o-（表‘离开’）与词根mit（表‘送、释放’，如transmit）构成，字面义为‘送走’，引申为‘省略、遗漏’

similar_words.description: 「emit」为动词，表‘发出（光、热、声音等）’；「omit」为动词，表‘省略、遗漏’，二者形近易混

definitions.example: One should always be honest with oneself when evaluating academic performance.

oneself.etymology: 由泛指代词one（一个人）与反身代词后缀self组合而成，用于指代句中主语自身

similar_words.description: 「ones」为复数代词，指代前文提到的同类人或物；「oneself」为反身代词，指代主语自身，二者形近易混

definitions.example: Onion is a common ingredient in the regional cuisine analyzed in the cultural studies passage.

onion.etymology: 无复杂可拆分考研核心词缀，词源可追溯至拉丁语unio，原指‘球状鳞茎’，后专指洋葱

similar_words.description: 「union」为抽象名词，表‘联盟、工会、联合’；「onion」为具体名词，表‘洋葱’，二者形近易混

definitions.example: The researcher carefully transferred the purified sample onto the sterile glass slide.

onto.etymology: 由介词on（在…上）与to（朝向）合成的介词，表动作指向物体表面

similar_words.description: 「into」表‘进入…之中’，侧重动作指向物体内部；「onto」表‘到…之上’，侧重动作指向物体表面，二者形近且易混淆用法

definitions.example: Opaque curtains are used in the laboratory to avoid interference from external light.
The philosopher’s opaque writing style makes his works hard for ordinary readers to comprehend.

opaque.etymology: 无明显可拆分的考研核心词缀，词源为拉丁语opacus，原指‘阴暗的、模糊的’

similar_words.description: 「optic」为形容词，表‘视觉的、光学的’，与视觉感知相关；「opaque」为形容词，表‘不透明的、难懂的’，与透明度或文本可读性相关，二者形近易混

definitions.example: The opening of the new research institute has drawn widespread attention from the academic community.
The opening paragraph of the passage clearly presents the author’s central argument.

opening.etymology: 由动词open（打开）加后缀-ing构成，可作动名词（表行为或结果）或形容词（表状态）

similar_words.description: 「openness」为名词，表‘开放、坦率’，侧重态度或状态；「opening」可作名词或形容词，表‘开端、开口、职位空缺’或‘开始的’，二者为同根衍生词

definitions.example: The historic opera house in Vienna is frequently cited in articles about European cultural legacy.

opera.etymology: 来自拉丁语opus（作品）的复数形式，原指‘音乐作品’，后专指以歌唱和音乐为核心的戏剧形式

similar_words.description: 「drama」意为‘话剧、戏剧’，侧重台词与表演；「opera」意为‘歌剧、歌剧院’，侧重歌唱与音乐结合的戏剧形式，二者为近义的戏剧类名词

definitions.example: Most high-tech manufacturing plants operate with automated systems to enhance productivity.
The medical team will operate on the patient to treat the heart condition next week.

operate.etymology: 由词根oper（表‘工作、操作’，如opus‘作品’）加动词后缀-ate构成，表‘使…工作’

similar_words.description: 「cooperate」为动词，表‘合作、协作’；「operate」为动词，表‘操作、运转、动手术、经营’，二者为同根衍生词

definitions.example: The company’s cross-border operation has faced challenges due to changing trade policies.
The patient made a quick recovery after the successful operation.

operation.etymology: 由词根oper（表‘工作’）加名词后缀-ation构成，表‘工作的行为、过程或结果’

similar_words.description: 「cooperation」为名词，表‘合作、协作’；「operation」为名词，表‘操作、运转、手术、经营活动’，二者为同根衍生词

definitions.meaning: 操作的，运转的；可使用的
definitions.example: The new data center will become operational after completing all safety inspections.

operational.etymology: 由名词operation（操作、运转）加形容词后缀-al构成，表‘与操作相关的’

similar_words.description: 「operative」为形容词，表‘生效的、起作用的’，侧重效果；「operational」为形容词，表‘操作的、运转的、可使用的’，侧重运行状态，二者形近易混

definitions.example: The factory operator has invested heavily in automated production lines to improve operational efficiency.

operator.etymology: 词根oper-意为「工作、操作」，后缀-ator表执行动作的人或机构，由动词operate派生而来

similar_words.description: 两词词形相近，cooperator表合作者，operator表经营者、操作员

definitions.example: In academic research, scholars should distinguish between factual evidence and subjective opinion when analyzing social phenomena.

opinion.etymology: 词根opin-意为「思考、判断」，后缀-ion为名词后缀，表动作或状态

similar_words.description: 两词为近义易混，opinion侧重个人主观判断的意见，view侧重对事物的系统看法

definitions.example: During the congressional hearing, the senator challenged his opponent’s proposal to cut funding for public education.

opponent.etymology: 前缀op-（=ob-，表反对）+ 词根pon-（=put，表放置）+ 后缀-ent（表人），字面义为「站在对立面的人」

similar_words.description: 两词词形相近，component表组成部分，opponent表对手、反对者

definitions.example: Many postgraduate candidates regard the entrance examination as a precious opportunity to pursue advanced studies in their favorite fields.

opportunity.etymology: 前缀op-（表朝向）+ 词根port-（=carry，表携带）+ 后缀-unity（名词后缀），原指「带到面前的事物」，引申为机会、时机

similar_words.description: 两词为近义易混，opportunity侧重有利的、适合行动的时机，chance侧重偶然的、随机的机会

definitions.example: A growing number of residents oppose the plan to build a chemical plant near the residential area due to health concerns.

oppose.etymology: 前缀op-（=ob-，表反对）+ 词根pose-（=put，表放置），字面义为「置于对立面」，引申为反对、抵制

similar_words.description: 两词词形相近，propose表提议、求婚，oppose表反对、抵制

definitions.example: The conclusion drawn by the research team is opposite to that of the previous study published in the same journal.

opposite.etymology: 前缀op-（表反对）+ 词根posit-（=put，表放置）+ 后缀-e，字面义为「放置在对面的」，引申为相反的、对立的

similar_words.description: 两词词形相近，apposite表合适的、贴切的，opposite表相反的、对立的

definitions.example: In the history of colonialism, imperial powers often oppressed indigenous peoples and exploited their natural resources.

oppress.etymology: 前缀op-（=ob-，表加强）+ 词根press-（表按压），字面义为「重压」，引申为压迫、压制

similar_words.description: 两词为近义易混，oppress侧重长期残酷地压迫（多指人），suppress侧重压制情绪、起义等

definitions.example: Facing fierce competition in the job market, many undergraduates opt to pursue postgraduate studies to enhance their competitiveness.

opt.etymology: 词根opt-意为「选择」，源自拉丁语optare，直接用作动词表选择、挑选

similar_words.description: 两词词形相近，apt表合适的、易于…的，opt表选择、挑选

definitions.example: Optical fiber communication technology has greatly improved the speed and stability of data transmission in modern information systems.

optical.etymology: 词根opt-意为「视觉」，后缀-ical为形容词后缀，表与…相关的，指光学的、视觉的

similar_words.description: 两词词形相近，optimal表最佳的、最优的，optical表光学的、视觉的

definitions.example: The central bank’s latest report indicates that most economists are optimistic about the country’s economic recovery in the next two years.

optimistic.etymology: 词根optim-意为「最好的」+ 后缀-ist（表持某种态度的人）+ 后缀-ic（形容词后缀），指持最好看法的，即乐观的

similar_words.description: 两词为反义易混，optimistic表乐观的，pessimistic表悲观的

definitions.example: Engineers optimize the algorithm to reduce data processing time by 30 percent.

optimize.etymology: 来自拉丁语词根「optimus」（最好的），加动词后缀「-ize」（使…化），表使达到最佳状态

similar_words.description: 「optimize」是动词，表优化；「optimum」是形容词/名词，表最优的/最优值，形近易混

definitions.example: The optimum temperature for the chemical reaction is between 25 and 30 degrees Celsius.
Researchers are trying to determine the optimum for the new energy storage system.

optimum.etymology: 直接源自拉丁语「optimus」（最好的），与「optimize」同根

similar_words.description: 「optimum」是形容词/名词，表最优的/最优值；「optimize」是动词，表优化，形近易混

definitions.example: Consumers have the option to cancel the subscription within seven days without any penalty.

option.etymology: 来自词根「opt-」（选择），加名词后缀「-ion」，表选择的行为或对象

similar_words.description: 「option」是名词，表选择/选项；「optional」是形容词，表可选择的，形近易混

definitions.example: The university offers optional seminars on digital marketing for senior students.

optional.etymology: 由名词「option」（选择）加形容词后缀「-al」构成

similar_words.description: 「optional」是形容词，表可选择的；「option」是名词，表选择/选项，形近易混

definitions.example: All graduate candidates must pass an oral defense of their thesis before graduation.

oral.etymology: 来自拉丁语词根「os」（嘴），加形容词后缀「-al」，表与嘴相关的

similar_words.description: 「oral」表口头的/口腔的；「aural」表听觉的，仅首字母不同，形近易混

definitions.example: The satellite has remained in a stable orbit around the moon for three years.
Scientists have detected a new planet orbiting a star 40 light-years away from Earth.

orbit.etymology: 来自拉丁语「orbis」（圆圈、圆环），原指天体运行的圆形轨迹

similar_words.description: 「orbit」表轨道/沿轨道运行；「orb」表球体/天体，形近易混

definitions.example: The local government plans to convert part of the abandoned orchard into a public park.

orchard.etymology: 源自古英语「hort-geard」，由表「花园」的词根「hort-」和表「院子」的「yard」组合而成，指种植果树的院子

similar_words.description: 「orchard」表果园；「orchestra」表管弦乐队，前缀部分形近易混

definitions.example: The national orchestra will perform a series of classical symphonies at the city's concert hall.
We booked two seats in the orchestra for the upcoming Broadway musical.

orchestra.etymology: 源自希腊语「orkhestra」，原指古希腊剧场中合唱队跳舞的区域，后引申为乐队所在区域及乐队本身

similar_words.description: 「orchestra」表管弦乐队/正厅前排；「orchard」表果园，前缀部分形近易混

definitions.meaning: 有秩序的，整齐的；守秩序的
definitions.example: The orderly arrangement of books in the library helps readers find materials efficiently.
An orderly helped the elderly patient move to the wheelchair gently.

orderly.etymology: 由名词「order」（秩序）加形容词后缀「-ly」构成，表有秩序的

similar_words.description: 「orderly」是「order」的派生词，表有秩序的；「order」可作动词/名词，表命令/秩序

definitions.example: The study aims to explore the psychological traits of ordinary people facing major life changes.

ordinary.etymology: 来自词根「ordin-」（顺序、秩序），加形容词后缀「-ary」，表符合常规秩序的，即普通的

similar_words.description: 「ordinary」强调「平凡、无特色」；「common」强调「普遍、常见」，为近义词易混

definitions.example: Geologists are investigating the distribution of high-grade copper ore in this mountainous region.

ore.etymology: 源自古英语，无显性词缀，为原生名词

similar_words.description: 「ore」指矿石、矿砂；「oar」指船桨、橹，二者为形近易混词

definitions.example: The heart is a vital organ that pumps blood throughout the human body.
This international organ is responsible for monitoring global environmental policies.

organ.etymology: 源自希腊语「organon」（工具、器官），为同根词基础词根

similar_words.description: 「organ」指器官或机构；「orgy」指纵欲的聚会，二者为形近易混词

definitions.meaning: 有机的；绿色的（指未用化学药剂的农产品）
definitions.example: Organic agriculture prioritizes ecological balance and avoids synthetic fertilizers.
The organic structure of the coral reef supports thousands of marine species.

organic.etymology: 「organ」（器官） + 形容词后缀「-ic」，表与生物/器官相关的

similar_words.description: 「organic」为形容词，表有机的、生物的；「organism」为名词，表有机体、生物，二者为同根易混词

definitions.meaning: 有机体，生物（从单细胞到多细胞的生命体）
definitions.example: Even a tiny single-celled organism has a complex metabolic system.

organism.etymology: 「organ」（器官） + 名词后缀「-ism」，表具有器官结构的生命体

similar_words.description: 「organism」指生物有机体；「organization」指人为建立的组织、机构，二者为同根易混词

definitions.example: This non-profit organization focuses on providing education for rural children.

definitions.meaning: 组织（指行为、结构或编排方式）
definitions.example: The organization of the academic symposium requires detailed scheduling and coordination.

organization.etymology: 「organize」（组织） + 名词后缀「-ation」，表组织的行为或结果

similar_words.description: 「organization」指人为的组织、机构；「organism」指生物有机体，二者为同根易混词

definitions.example: The student union will organize a charity sale to raise funds for disaster relief.
She spent two hours organizing her research notes and reference materials.

organize.etymology: 「organ」（工具、器官） + 动词后缀「-ize」，表使形成有序的结构

similar_words.description: 「organize」为美式拼写，「organise」为英式拼写，考研中均认可，美式拼写更常用

definitions.meaning: 使适应（新环境）；使熟悉；以…为导向
definitions.example: International students are oriented to campus life and academic rules during the first week.

definitions.meaning: 东方（正式/文学用语，尤指东亚、东南亚）
definitions.example: The travelogue vividly describes the exotic customs of the Orient in the 1920s.

orient.etymology: 源自拉丁语「oriri」（升起，指太阳升起的东方）

similar_words.description: 「orient」可作动词（使适应）或名词（东方）；「oriental」作形容词（东方的）或名词（东方人），二者为同根易混词

definitions.meaning: 东方的（尤指东亚、东南亚的文化、物产等）
definitions.example: The museum has a precious collection of oriental porcelain and calligraphy works.

definitions.meaning: 东方人（正式用语，略带旧义）
definitions.example: In 19th-century Western literature, the Orientals were often depicted with mysterious images.

similar_words.description: 「oriental」作形容词（东方的）或名词（东方人）；「orient」作动词（使适应）或名词（东方），二者为同根易混词

definitions.meaning: （新生、新员工的）迎新情况介绍；适应期
definitions.example: The university provides a three-day orientation for freshmen to familiarize themselves with campus facilities.
The company's new marketing strategy has a clear consumer-oriented orientation.

orientation.etymology: 「orient」（使定向） + 名词后缀「-ation」，表定向或适应的行为

similar_words.description: 「orientation」指定向、迎新；「origin」指起源、出身，二者为形近易混词

definitions.example: Scholars have long debated the origin of modern human beings and their migration routes.
The college admits students from diverse cultural and social origins.

origin.etymology: 源自拉丁语「oriri」（升起、开始），与「orient」同根

similar_words.description: 「origin」指起源、出身；「orientation」指定向、迎新，二者为形近易混词

definitions.example: Many ancient literary works have lost their original wording due to repeated transcription over centuries.

original.etymology: 由名词「origin（起源）」+形容词后缀「-al」构成，表「与起源相关的、独创的」

similar_words.description: 均含「初始的」之意，initial侧重时间上的开端，original侧重事物的原始状态或独创性

definitions.example: This theory of cognitive development originated from the long-term observational studies of child psychologists.

originate.etymology: 由名词「origin（起源）」+动词后缀「-ate」构成，表「源自、发源」

similar_words.description: 均含「起源」之意，originate侧重事物的产生源头，derive侧重由某事物衍生而来

definitions.example: The marble ornament on the cathedral’s facade was carved by a famous Renaissance sculptor.

ornament.etymology: 由词根「orn-（装饰）」+名词后缀「-ment」构成，表「用于装饰的物品」

similar_words.description: 均含「装饰」相关含义，ornament多指小巧精致的实体装饰品，decoration可指各类装饰或装饰行为

definitions.example: The novel depicts the harsh living conditions of orphans in 19th-century London.

orphan.etymology: 源自希腊语「orphanos（失去父母的）」，直接借入英语作名词，表「孤儿」

similar_words.description: orphan指孤儿个体，orphanage指孤儿院，为orphan的衍生名词

definitions.example: The economist criticized the orthodox monetary policy for failing to address the widening income gap.

orthodox.etymology: 由词根「ortho-（正的、正统的）」+词根「dox（观点、教义）」构成，表「符合正统观点的、传统的」

similar_words.description: 均含「传统的」之意，orthodox侧重符合既定学术/宗教正统标准，conventional侧重符合社会惯例

definitions.example: You need to cite all sources in your thesis; otherwise, you may be accused of academic plagiarism.

otherwise.etymology: 由形容词「other（其他的）」+副词后缀「-wise（表方式、方向）」构成，表「以其他方式；否则」

similar_words.description: 均含「否则」之意，otherwise为正式书面语，or为口语或非正式用语

definitions.example: Researchers ought to adhere strictly to ethical guidelines when conducting human subject experiments.

ought.etymology: 由古英语情态动词「ahte（应当）」演化而来，为专用情态动词，后接带to的不定式

similar_words.description: 均含「应该」之意，ought to语气稍重，侧重道德/责任上的应当，should侧重建议或推测

definitions.example: The pharmaceutical company produces vaccines in batches measured by the ounce to ensure precise dosage.

ounce.etymology: 源自拉丁语「uncia（十二分之一）」，原指罗马磅的十二分之一，后演变为英制重量单位

similar_words.description: 均为英制重量单位，ounce为盎司（约28克），pound为磅（约454克）

definitions.example: The 2009 outbreak of H1N1 influenza prompted global cooperation in pandemic prevention and control.

outbreak.etymology: 由短语动词「break out（爆发）」转化而来的合成名词，表「（突发事件的）突然发生」

similar_words.description: 均含「爆发」之意，outbreak侧重战争、疾病等集体性突发事件的发生，burst侧重情绪、能量的突然释放

definitions.example: The outcome of the Sino-US trade negotiations will have a profound impact on global economic stability.

outcome.etymology: 由短语动词「come out（显现、产生）」转化而来的合成名词，表「最终显现的结果」

similar_words.description: 均含「结果」之意，outcome侧重最终的、决定性的结果，result可指各类直接或间接的结果

definitions.example: Many top universities offer outdoor leadership programs to cultivate students' teamwork and resilience.

outdoor.etymology: 前缀out-（外）+ 词根door（门）→ 门外的→户外的

similar_words.description: 前缀相反的同根词，「indoor」指室内的，「outdoor」指室外的

definitions.meaning: 外面的，外部的；远离中心的
definitions.example: The outer layer of the space probe is coated with heat-resistant materials to withstand high temperatures.

outer.etymology: 前缀out-（外）+ 后缀-er（表方向/程度）→ 更靠外的→外面的

similar_words.description: 均为out-开头的形容词，「outer」表位置上的外部的，「outdoor」表与室外空间相关的

definitions.meaning: 全套服装（尤指特定活动用）；全套装备
definitions.example: She prepared a lightweight waterproof outdoor outfit for the week-long hiking expedition.
The rescue team was outfitted with advanced communication devices before the mission.

outfit.etymology: 前缀out-（完全）+ 词根fit（合身/合适）→ 完全合身的一套→全套服装/装备

similar_words.description: 均表成套物品，「outfit」侧重服装或装备套装，「kit」侧重工具或组件套装

definitions.meaning: 短途旅行，远足；户外活动
definitions.example: The research center organized a spring outing to the nearby wetland reserve for its staff.

outing.etymology: 前缀out-（外）+ 后缀-ing（动名词后缀）→ 外出的活动→远足

similar_words.description: 均为out-开头的名词，「outing」指短途休闲旅行，「outfit」指全套服装或装备

definitions.example: The chemical factory upgraded its waste gas outlet to meet the new environmental protection standards.
Many consumers choose to buy branded apparel at outlet stores for more affordable prices.

outlet.etymology: 前缀out-（外）+ 词根let（使流出/售出）→ 使流出的地方/售出的渠道→出口/经销店

similar_words.description: 前缀相反的同根词，「inlet」指入口，「outlet」指出口或商品经销店

definitions.example: The supervisor required each intern to submit a detailed outline of their research report within three days.
The annual report outlines the key achievements and challenges of the company in the past year.

outline.etymology: 前缀out-（外）+ 词根line（线条）→ 外部的线条→轮廓→框架性的大纲

similar_words.description: 均表概要内容，「outline」侧重框架性的大纲，「summary」侧重浓缩后的完整摘要

definitions.example: The latest economic survey presents a cautious outlook for the global trade growth in 2024.
Her positive outlook on career development inspired many young colleagues in the department.

outlook.etymology: 前缀out-（向外）+ 词根look（看）→ 向外观察的结果→前景/观点

similar_words.description: 均表未来前景，「outlook」侧重宏观趋势的整体展望，「prospect」侧重具体的发展可能性

definitions.example: The automobile plant increased its monthly output by 12% after introducing automated production lines.
The new solar panel has a higher power output than the traditional models under the same sunlight intensity.

output.etymology: 前缀out-（向外）+ 词根put（放置/产生）→ 向外产生的量→产量/输出

similar_words.description: 前缀相反的同根词，「input」指输入量或输入，「output」指输出量或输出

definitions.example: The company's illegal discharge of waste water sparked widespread outrage among local residents.
The unfair dismissal policy outraged the entire staff of the publishing house.

outrage.etymology: 前缀out-（过度）+ 词根rage（愤怒）→ 过度的愤怒→愤慨

similar_words.description: 均与愤怒情绪相关，「rage」指个人的强烈愤怒，「outrage」指公众的集体愤慨

definitions.example: At the outset of the clinical trial, the researchers clearly defined the inclusion criteria for participants.

outset.etymology: 前缀out-（出现）+ 词根set（放置）→ 放置出来的起始点→开端

similar_words.description: 均表事件的开始，「outset」指一般事件的开端，「onset」常指不好的事情的起始（如疾病、危机）

definitions.example: Many top research institutions are located on the outskirts of major cities to secure sufficient land for expansion.

outskirts.etymology: 前缀out-（表外部）+skirt（原指裙子下摆，引申为边缘地带）+复数后缀-s，指城市外围的边缘区域

similar_words.description: 「suburb」泛指城市周边的居民区，「outskirts」特指城市最外围的边缘地带

definitions.example: The committee selected three outstanding candidates for the national postgraduate scholarship program.
The company still has an outstanding debt of 2 million yuan to its raw material suppliers.

outstanding.etymology: 前缀out-（表超过）+stand（站立）+形容词后缀-ing，原指站在众人之上，引申为杰出或未处理的

similar_words.description: 「distinguished」侧重因成就、地位而受人尊敬，「outstanding」侧重突出、优于同类

definitions.example: Despite the outward calm of the stock market, underlying volatility remains a major concern for investors.

outward.etymology: 前缀out-（表外部）+后缀-ward（表方向），可作形容词或副词

similar_words.description: 「outwards」仅作副词表「向外」，「outward」可作形容词表「外表的」、作副词同「outwards」

definitions.example: Archaeologists discovered an oval pottery jar dating back to the Neolithic period in the excavation site.

oval.etymology: 源自拉丁语ovum（蛋），引申为蛋形的椭圆形

similar_words.description: 「oral」意为「口头的、口腔的」，「oval」意为「椭圆形的」，拼写仅首尾字母有差异，易混淆

definitions.example: The cookbook specifies that the bread should be baked in a preheated oven at 200 degrees Celsius for 25 minutes.

oven.etymology: 古英语来源，指用于加热烹饪的密闭器具

similar_words.description: 「stove」泛指烹饪用的炉灶（含烤箱、灶台），「oven」特指密闭的烤箱/烤炉

definitions.example: Overall, the pass rate of this year's postgraduate entrance exam has increased by 3 percent compared to last year.

overall.etymology: 前缀over-（表全部）+all（全部），指涵盖全部的

similar_words.description: 「general」侧重普遍的、一般的，「overall」侧重涵盖全部的、总体的

definitions.meaning: 大衣，外套（特指防寒厚大衣）
definitions.example: He wrapped himself in a thick woolen overcoat to fend off the biting cold wind on the way to the exam hall.

overcoat.etymology: 前缀over-（表覆盖、在…之上）+coat（外套），指覆盖在外的厚大衣

similar_words.description: 「coat」泛指各类外套，「overcoat」特指防寒的厚长大衣

definitions.example: To achieve their postgraduate admission goals, candidates must overcome psychological pressure and academic bottlenecks.

overcome.etymology: 前缀over-（表越过、战胜）+come（来），原指越过障碍而来，引申为克服

similar_words.description: 「conquer」侧重武力或强势征服，「overcome」侧重克服困难、障碍等抽象事物

definitions.example: Continuous heavy rain caused the river to overflow its banks, inundating dozens of rural households.

definitions.meaning: 挤满，充满（常用搭配overflow with）
definitions.example: The lecture hall overflowed with students eager to learn about postgraduate application strategies.

overflow.etymology: 前缀over-（表超过）+flow（流），指超过容量而流出

similar_words.description: 「outflow」指液体、资金等的流出（量），「overflow」指超过容量而溢出或挤满

definitions.example: The Ministry of Education plans to overhaul the postgraduate enrollment system to ensure fairer access for candidates from rural areas.

overhaul.etymology: 前缀over-（表完全、全部）+haul（拉、拖），原指彻底拉出检修，引申为全面改革

similar_words.description: 「renovate」侧重修复旧物使其完好，「overhaul」侧重全面检修、彻底改革制度或体系

definitions.meaning: 在头顶上方的/地；高架的
definitions.example: The overhead power lines in the rural area were upgraded to prevent power outages.
Small startups often struggle to control overheads such as office rent and software fees.

overhead.etymology: 前缀over-（在…之上）+词根head（头），原指在头上方的事物，后引申出企业间接成本（因该成本在直接生产成本之上）

similar_words.description: 「overhead」指在头顶的、高架的或企业管理费用；「overhang」指物体悬垂、突出于上方

definitions.example: He overheard his professors debating the validity of the new research method outside the lecture hall.

overhear.etymology: 前缀over-（越过，指无意间逾越倾听的边界）+词根hear（听），指无意中听到他人未公开的谈话

similar_words.description: 「overhear」是动词，表无意中听到；「overhead」多作形/副词，表头顶上方的

definitions.meaning: （使）重叠；交叠（时间、范围、内容等）
definitions.example: The study areas of the two research teams overlap, so they decided to collaborate to avoid duplication.

overlap.etymology: 前缀over-（在…之上）+词根lap（原指衣物折叠于大腿，引申为折叠、重叠），指部分覆盖重合

similar_words.description: 「overlap」指部分重叠交叠；「overlay」指在表面覆盖一层（如涂层、图片）

definitions.example: It is dangerous to overlook the side effects of this new drug during clinical trials.
The manager decided to overlook the intern's minor typo since it was her first day.

overlook.etymology: 前缀over-（越过）+词根look（看），原指越过某物看而未注意到，引申为忽略；后又引申为宽容（越过过错不追究）

similar_words.description: 「overlook」指忽略或宽容；「oversee」指监督、管理（工作、项目）

definitions.meaning: 一夜之间的/地；突然的/地
definitions.example: The unknown singer became famous overnight after her performance went viral on social media.

overnight.etymology: 前缀over-（越过）+词根night（夜晚），指越过一整夜的时间，引申为突然发生

similar_words.description: 「overnight」指一夜之间的/地；「overtime」指加班（的时间）或超时的

definitions.example: The municipal government built a 500-meter overpass to reduce traffic jams in the downtown area.
The mountaineer overpassed the 8,000-meter mark on his way to the summit.

overpass.etymology: 前缀over-（在…之上）+词根pass（通过），原指从上方通过的通道，即立交桥；动词义为从上方越过

similar_words.description: 「overpass」指地面上方的立交桥；「underpass」指地面下方的地下通道

definitions.meaning: 海外的/地；国外的/地（尤指跨洋的）
definitions.example: Many Chinese tech companies are expanding their overseas markets to increase global influence.

overseas.etymology: 前缀over-（越过）+词根sea（海洋）+后缀-s（副词后缀），指越过海洋的区域，即海外

similar_words.description: 「overseas」可作形/副词，强调跨洋的国外；「abroad」多作副词，泛指在国外（不限跨洋）

definitions.meaning: 追上；超过（车辆、运动员等）
definitions.example: The cyclist accelerated to overtake the leading rider in the final 100 meters of the race.

definitions.meaning: 突然降临；压倒（灾害、情绪等）
definitions.example: A wave of nostalgia overtook her when she visited her childhood neighborhood.

overtake.etymology: 前缀over-（越过）+词根take（取，引申为移动），原指从上方越过移动的物体，引申为追上超过；后又引申为突然降临（如情绪、灾害）

similar_words.description: 「overtake」指追上超过（移动的人/物）或突然降临；「overpass」指立交桥或静态的越过

definitions.example: The popular uprising overthrew the dictatorship and established a provisional government.

overthrow.etymology: 前缀over-（翻转）+词根throw（扔），原指扔翻某物，引申为推翻政权、制度等

similar_words.description: 「overthrow」多指推翻政权、社会制度；「overturn」多指推翻法律判决、官方决定等

definitions.example: The company's employees receive 1.5 times their regular salary for working overtime on weekdays.

overtime.etymology: 前缀over-（超过）+词根time（时间），指超过规定工作时间的时段，即加班

similar_words.description: 「overtime」指加班或超时的；「overnight」指一夜之间的/地

definitions.example: The Supreme Court overturned the lower court's controversial ruling last month.
Strong gusts of wind overturned several temporary stalls at the market.

overturn.etymology: 「over-（翻转、超过）」 + 「turn（转动）」，表翻转超过原位置

similar_words.description: 「overturn」侧重推翻裁决、制度或物体翻转，「overthrow」侧重用武力推翻政权、统治

definitions.meaning: 使不知所措，使难以承受
definitions.example: The college freshman was overwhelmed by the heavy academic workload.
The invading army was overwhelmed by the local militia's surprise attack.

overwhelm.etymology: 「over-（过度、超出）」 + 「whelm（淹没，源自古英语）」，表被过度淹没

similar_words.description: 「overwhelm」侧重心理、数量上的压倒性影响，「overpower」侧重武力、力量上的制服

definitions.example: The overwhelming majority of respondents supported the new environmental policy.

overwhelming.etymology: 动词「overwhelm」的现在分词转化的形容词，表具有压倒性质的

similar_words.description: 「overwhelming」修饰事物，表性质；「overwhelmed」修饰人，表被动承受的状态

definitions.example: He still owes his former roommate 500 dollars for the shared rent.
The Nobel Prize winner owes his breakthrough to years of dedicated research.

owe.etymology: 源自古英语「āgan」，原义为“拥有、支配”，后引申为“欠负”

similar_words.description: 「owe」表欠负或归功，「own」表拥有或自己的，词性与词义均有差异

definitions.example: Owing to the sudden outbreak of flu, the final exam was postponed for two weeks.
There is still a sum of 200 pounds owing on the purchase of the laptop.

owing.etymology: 动词「owe」的现在分词转化的形容词，表处于欠负状态的

similar_words.description: 「owing to」多放句首引导原因状语，「due to」多放系动词后作表语

definitions.example: The owl, a nocturnal bird, often appears as a symbol of wisdom in Western literature.

owl.etymology: 源自古英语「ūle」，拟声词，模仿猫头鹰的叫声

similar_words.description: 「owl」指猫头鹰，「fowl」指家禽（如鸡、鸭等），词义完全不同

definitions.example: The store owner decided to launch a promotion to attract more customers.

owner.etymology: 「own（拥有）」 + 「-er（表施动者的名词后缀）」，表拥有某物的人

similar_words.description: 「owner」指合法的所有者，「holder」指暂时持有者（不一定拥有所有权）

definitions.example: The dispute over the ownership of the ancient castle has not been resolved yet.

ownership.etymology: 「owner（所有者）」 + 「-ship（表身份、权利的名词后缀）」，表拥有的权利

similar_words.description: 「ownership」指合法的所有权，「possession」指实际占有（不一定合法）

definitions.example: In traditional agricultural societies, the ox was the main labor force for plowing.

ox.etymology: 源自古英语「oxa」，指供役用的公牛

similar_words.description: 「ox」多指阉过的役用公牛，「cow」指母牛或奶牛

definitions.example: Carbon dioxide, a common oxide, is a major contributor to global warming.

oxide.etymology: 「ox-（源自oxygen，表“氧”）」 + 「-ide（表化合物的名词后缀）」，表含氧的化合物

similar_words.description: 「oxide」是氧化物的统称，「dioxide」指含两个氧原子的二氧化物

definitions.example: Marine plants release oxygen into seawater through photosynthesis, supporting diverse aquatic life.

oxygen.etymology: 词根「oxy-」（氧）+ 后缀「-gen」（产生），早期化学认知中氧被认为是产生酸的气体

similar_words.description: 同根「oxy-」，oxide指氧化物，oxygen指氧元素或氧气

definitions.example: Ozone layer depletion increases the amount of ultraviolet radiation reaching the Earth's surface.

ozone.etymology: 源自希腊语「ozein」（嗅），因臭氧具有特殊刺激性气味得名

similar_words.description: 形近，zone指区域、地带，ozone指臭氧

definitions.example: The steady pace of technological innovation reshapes the global economic landscape.
Pace the critics, this policy has effectively reduced regional poverty rates.

pace.etymology: 源自拉丁语「passus」（步），引申为步速、节奏，介词义为礼貌反驳的委婉表达

similar_words.description: 形近，phase指阶段、时期，pace指节奏、步速

definitions.meaning: 打包；挤满；包裹；（一）群（动物）
definitions.example: The stadium was packed with football fans cheering for their favorite team.

pack.etymology: 源自古英语「pac」（包裹），引申为挤满、一群等义

similar_words.description: 同根「pack」，pack泛指包裹、挤满，packet指小包裹、小袋

definitions.example: The fragile package was carefully handled by the logistics staff.
The government announced a package of measures to boost domestic consumption.

package.etymology: 词根「pack」（包）+ 后缀「-age」（表集合或动作结果的名词后缀），引申为一揽子计划等义

similar_words.description: 同根「pack」，pack泛指包、挤满，package指较大包裹或一揽子计划

definitions.example: The sensor transmits a packet of data to the central server every five minutes.

packet.etymology: 词根「pack」（包）+ 后缀「-et」（指小的名词后缀），指小型包裹或数据包

similar_words.description: 同根「pack」，packet指小包裹/数据包，package指大包裹/一揽子计划

definitions.example: The two countries signed a non-aggression pact to maintain regional stability.

pact.etymology: 源自拉丁语「pactum」（协议），指国家或团体间的正式协定

similar_words.description: 形近，pact指协定、条约，pack指包、挤满

definitions.example: She scribbled her phone number on a pad of paper by the telephone.
The contractor was accused of padding the construction budget by 15%.

pad.etymology: 源自低地德语「pad」（平的垫子），引申为便签本、爪垫等，动词虚报义为口语转义的僻义

similar_words.description: 形近，pad指垫、虚报，paddle指桨、用桨划

definitions.meaning: 桨；（乒乓球的）球拍；用桨划；涉水
definitions.example: He grabbed a paddle and steered the small boat toward the shore.

paddle.etymology: 源自古英语「padel」（桨），引申为乒乓球拍、涉水等义

similar_words.description: 形近，pad指垫、虚报，paddle指桨、用桨划

definitions.example: She carried a pail of fresh water from the well to the farmhouse.

pail.etymology: 源自古英语「pægel」（桶），指盛液体的容器或一桶的量

similar_words.description: 形近，pail指桶、一桶的量，mail指邮件、邮政

definitions.example: Chronic pain not only affects physical health but also impairs patients' mental well-being.

pain.etymology: 源自拉丁语「poena」（惩罚、痛苦），无词缀变化

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，「pane」指窗格玻璃，「pain」指疼痛、痛苦

definitions.example: The economic recession has left painful scars on many low-income families.

painful.etymology: 由词根「pain（痛苦）」加形容词后缀「-ful（充满…的）」构成

similar_words.description: 同根反义，「painless」指无痛的、轻松的，「painful」指疼痛的、令人痛苦的

definitions.example: The author paints a vivid picture of life in the 1960s rural America in his memoir.
We need to buy some white paint to refresh the bedroom walls.

paint.etymology: 源自拉丁语「pingere（绘画）」，经中古英语「pinten」演变而来

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，「print」指打印、印刷，「paint」指绘画、（用语言）描绘

definitions.example: The impressionist painter is known for his bold use of color and loose brushwork.

painter.etymology: 由词根「paint（绘画、油漆）」加名词后缀「-er（表执行者）」构成

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，「printer」指印刷工、打印机，「painter」指画家、油漆工

definitions.example: This Renaissance painting depicts a religious scene with meticulous detail.

painting.etymology: 由词根「paint（绘画）」加名词后缀「-ing（表动作或结果）」构成

similar_words.description: 近义，「drawing」多指素描、线条画，「painting」多指油画、水彩画等带色彩的绘画作品

definitions.example: She turned pale when she heard the unexpected news of her father's accident.
The achievements of last year pale in comparison to the scale of this year's scientific breakthrough.

pale.etymology: 源自拉丁语「pallidus（苍白的）」，经古法语「pale」进入英语

similar_words.description: 近义，「faint」侧重（光、声、味等）微弱的、模糊的，「pale」侧重颜色苍白的、暗淡的

definitions.example: He held the small coin tightly in the palm of his hand.

definitions.meaning: （用欺骗手段）把…塞给（常与off连用）
definitions.example: The street vendor tried to palm off a fake watch to the unsuspecting tourist.

palm.etymology: 源自拉丁语「palma（手掌、棕榈树）」，因棕榈树叶形似手掌得名

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，「calm」指平静的、镇定的，「palm」指手掌、棕榈树

definitions.example: The environmental organization distributed a pamphlet introducing practical measures to reduce domestic carbon emissions.

pamphlet.etymology: 源自拉丁语「pamphilus（中世纪爱情诗小册子）」，后泛指各类小册子

similar_words.description: 近义，「leaflet」多指单页传单，「pamphlet」多指装订成册的小册子、宣传册

definitions.example: She heated the extra-virgin olive oil in a large cast-iron pan before adding the chopped vegetables.
Most film critics panned the new superhero movie for its formulaic plot and underdeveloped characters.

pan.etymology: 源自古英语「panne（平底锅）」，为日耳曼语族同源词

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，「can」指罐头、能够，「pan」指平底锅、严厉批评

definitions.example: The national nature reserve has launched a long-term conservation program to protect wild pandas and their fragile mountain habitats.

panda.etymology: 源自尼泊尔语「panda（小熊猫）」，后特指大熊猫

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，「pander」指迎合（不良欲望），「panda」指大熊猫

definitions.meaning: 专家小组；（建筑、设备的）面板
definitions.example: The government appointed a panel of experts to evaluate the environmental impact of the new infrastructure project.

panel.etymology: 由pan-（表“全部”）加小词后缀-el构成，原指全幅的布料，后引申为“面板”“专家小组”等义

similar_words.description: 「pane」指窗格，「panel」指专家小组或面板，二者拼写形近易混淆

definitions.example: When the fire alarm went off, the crowd in the subway station fell into a state of panic.
The sudden loud noise panicked the small children in the playground.

panic.etymology: 源自希腊神话中牧神Pan的名字，传说他的突然出现会引发人类的恐慌，后引申为“恐慌”的含义

similar_words.description: 「pan」指平底锅，「panic」指恐慌，二者拼写形近易混淆

definitions.example: The report provides a comprehensive panorama of the global climate change trends over the past decade.

panorama.etymology: 由pan-（表“全部”）加-orama（表“视野”，源自希腊语horama）构成，指“全视野”，即“全景”“概览”

similar_words.description: 二者为同根词，「panorama」是名词表全景/概览，「panoramic」是形容词表全景的

definitions.example: After running up the steep stairs, he stood there panting heavily, unable to speak for a moment.
We could hear the pant of the runner as he crossed the finish line.

pant.etymology: 源自古法语pantaisier，本义为“喘气”，后引申为“渴望”等义

similar_words.description: 「pint」指品脱（容量单位），「pant」指喘息，二者拼写形近易混淆

definitions.example: Most companies require their employees to wear formal pants and shirts for business meetings.

pants.etymology: 源自16世纪喜剧角色Pantaloon（潘塔隆）所穿的宽松长裤pantaloons，后缩写为pants，指“裤子”

similar_words.description: 「pant」作动词/单数名词表喘息，「pants」是复数名词表裤子，二者拼写差一个s易混淆

definitions.example: The best-selling novel is available in both hardback and paperback editions at the campus bookstore.

paperback.etymology: 由paper（纸）加back（封面）构成，指用纸做封面的“平装本”图书

similar_words.description: 二者为图书装帧类名词，「paperback」指平装本，「hardback」指精装本

definitions.example: The emergency rescue team parachuted into the disaster-stricken area to deliver food and medicine.
The soldiers parachuted behind enemy lines to carry out the secret mission.

parachute.etymology: 由para-（表“防御、防”）加chute（表“降落”，源自法语chute）构成，指防御降落的“降落伞”

similar_words.description: 二者均以para-开头，「parachute」指降落伞，「parade」指游行，拼写形近易混淆

definitions.example: The annual military parade on National Day showcases the country’s latest defense equipment.
The athletes paraded through the stadium during the opening ceremony of the sports meeting.

parade.etymology: 由para-（表“准备”）加名词后缀-ade构成，原指准备好的队伍，后引申为“游行”“阅兵式”等义

similar_words.description: 二者均以para-开头，「parade」指游行，「parachute」指降落伞，拼写形近易混淆

definitions.example: The discovery of DNA double helix created a new paradigm for modern biological research.

paradigm.etymology: 由para-（表“超越、旁”）加-digm（表“显示”，源自希腊语deiknunai）构成，指超越常规的“范式”“典范”

similar_words.description: 二者均以para-d开头，「paradigm」指范式，「paradox」指悖论，拼写形近易混淆

definitions.example: It is a paradox that in such a wealthy country there are still a large number of homeless people.

paradox.etymology: 由para-（表“相反、对立”）加-dox（表“观点”，源自希腊语doxa）构成，指相互对立的观点即“悖论”

similar_words.description: 二者均以para-d开头，「paradox」指悖论，「paradigm」指范式，拼写形近易混淆

definitions.meaning: 似非而是的，自相矛盾的
definitions.example: It is paradoxical that a region with rich agricultural resources faces frequent food shortages.

paradoxical.etymology: 源自名词「paradox」（悖论），加形容词后缀-ical，表「具有…性质的」

similar_words.description: 前者为形容词，指似非而是的；后者为名词，指悖论、自相矛盾的事物

definitions.example: Each paragraph in the academic paper is structured to support one central claim.

paragraph.etymology: para-（旁、侧）+ graph（写、画），原指「写在正文旁的注释」，后引申为文章的段落

similar_words.description: 前者指文章的段落，后者指对文本的改写、释义

definitions.meaning: 平行的；相似的 / 平行线；相似物
definitions.example: The study found a parallel trend in the development of urbanization and carbon emissions.

parallel.etymology: para-（旁）+ allel（彼此），原指「彼此相邻的」，引申为平行的、相似的

similar_words.description: 前者指平行的、相似的，后者指使瘫痪、使丧失作用

definitions.example: The cyber attack paralyzed the city’s traffic management system for 12 hours.

paralyze.etymology: para-（旁）+ lyze（松开、分解），原指「（肌肉）旁侧松弛无力」，引申为使瘫痪、使失效

similar_words.description: 前者指使瘫痪、使丧失作用，后者指平行的、相似的

definitions.example: The biologists adjusted the temperature parameter to observe the bacteria’s growth rate.
All activities must be carried out within the parameters of the university’s regulations.

parameter.etymology: para-（旁）+ meter（测量），原指「测量时的参照值」，引申为参数、界限

similar_words.description: 前者指参数、界限，后者指周长、周边

definitions.example: The parasite feeds on the host’s blood and weakens its immune system.

parasite.etymology: para-（旁）+ site（食物），原指「在旁取食宿主食物的生物」，引申为寄生虫、依附他人的人

similar_words.description: 前者为名词，指寄生虫或寄生者；后者为形容词，表寄生的

definitions.example: The postman delivered a parcel containing reference books for the postgraduate exam.

parcel.etymology: parc（部分）+ -el（小的），原指「小的部分」，引申为包裹、小块土地

similar_words.description: 前者指包裹、小块土地，后者指微粒、粒子

definitions.meaning: 议会，国会（尤指英国议会）
definitions.example: The new environmental bill was debated in the UK Parliament for three weeks.

parliament.etymology: parlia（说话、商议）+ -ment（名词后缀），原指「商议的场所」，引申为议会、国会

similar_words.description: 前者为名词，指议会；后者为形容词，表议会的、国会的

definitions.example: Reading classic works is an essential part of postgraduate English preparation.
The crowd parted to make way for the Nobel Prize winner.

part.etymology: 源自拉丁语pars，表「分开、部分」，为英语核心词根，构成大量相关词汇

similar_words.description: 前者使用范围广，可指任何部分；后者侧重比例、份额

definitions.example: The research team only obtained partial data due to equipment failure.
The supervisor was accused of being partial to students from his alma mater.

partial.etymology: 源自名词「part」（部分），加形容词后缀-ial，表「具有…性质的」；偏向某一部分即偏袒

similar_words.description: 前者指部分的或偏袒的，后者指公正的、不偏不倚的

definitions.example: The researchers recruited 250 participants from 10 universities to conduct the longitudinal study.

participant.etymology: 同根派生自动词「participate」，词根为「part（部分）」+「-cip（拿取）」+「-ant（表人后缀）」，意为‘参与其中的人’

similar_words.description: 同根词，participant为名词‘参与者’，participate为动词‘参与’

definitions.example: Many undergraduate students participate in academic competitions to enhance their research abilities.

participate.etymology: 词根为「part（部分）」+「-cip（拿取）」+「-ate（动词后缀）」，意为‘加入成为其中一部分’

similar_words.description: 同根词，participate为动词‘参与’，participant为名词‘参与者’

definitions.example: The new microscope can detect submicroscopic particles in the air samples.

particle.etymology: 词根为「part（部分）」+「-icle（指小后缀）」，意为‘极小的部分，微粒’

similar_words.description: 形近词，particle为名词‘粒子/微粒’，particular为形容词‘特定的’

definitions.example: The policy aims to support a particular group of low-income families in urban areas.
She is very particular about the quality of her research data.

particular.etymology: 词根为「part（部分）」+「-icular（形容词后缀）」，意为‘属于某一部分的，特定的’

similar_words.description: 形近词，particular为形容词‘特定的/挑剔的’，particle为名词‘粒子/微粒’

definitions.example: The decline in global biodiversity is partly caused by human activities such as deforestation.

partly.etymology: 派生自名词「part（部分）」，加副词后缀「-ly」，意为‘部分地’

similar_words.description: 近义词，partly侧重‘整体中的某一部分’，partially侧重‘不完全地’，考研语境中常可互换

definitions.example: The tech startup is looking for a strategic partner to expand its Asian market.

partner.etymology: 词根为「part（部分）」+「-ner（表人后缀）」，意为‘共同分担的人’

similar_words.description: 近义词，partner指‘合作/合伙的伙伴’，colleague指‘职场同事’

definitions.example: The railway company provided free meals to passengers stranded by the typhoon.

passenger.etymology: 词根为「pass（行走、通过）」+「-enger（表人后缀）」，意为‘乘坐交通工具行走的人’

similar_words.description: 形近词，passenger指‘乘坐交通工具的乘客’，passerby指‘路过的行人’

definitions.example: A passerby took photos of the rare bird and uploaded them to a wildlife protection website.

passerby.etymology: 复合词，由「pass（通过）」+「-er（表人）」+「by（经过）」构成，意为‘路过的人’

similar_words.description: 形近词，passerby指‘路人’，passenger指‘乘坐交通工具的乘客’

definitions.example: His passion for classical literature led him to pursue a PhD in comparative literature.

passion.etymology: 词根为「pass（感受、忍受）」+「-ion（名词后缀）」，原指‘强烈的情感’，后引申为激情

similar_words.description: 形近词，passion为名词‘热情’，passive为形容词‘被动的’，词义相反

definitions.example: A passive learning style may hinder students' ability to think independently.

passive.etymology: 词根为「pass（忍受）」+「-ive（形容词后缀）」，意为‘忍受的、被动的’

similar_words.description: 形近词，passive为形容词‘被动的’，passion为名词‘热情’，词义相反

definitions.example: When compiling academic research notes, scholars often paste relevant data excerpts into a unified digital document.
The librarian used rice paste to repair the torn pages of the ancient manuscript.

paste.etymology: 源自拉丁语pasta（面团、浆糊），词根为past-，与「黏合、填充」的语义相关

similar_words.description: 「paste」表粘贴/浆糊，「taste」表品尝/味道，拼写仅首字母不同，易混淆

definitions.example: In 19th-century rural England, bird-watching was a popular pastime among middle-class families.

pastime.etymology: 由「past（过去）」+「time（时间）」构成，指「打发时间的活动」

similar_words.description: 「pastime」表消遣活动，「pasture」表牧场，拼写前四个字母相同，易混淆

definitions.example: The vast alpine pasture provides sufficient forage for thousands of sheep and goats.
Local herders pasture their cattle in the valley during the summer months.

pasture.etymology: 源自拉丁语pastura（放牧），词根为past-（喂养、放牧），与「畜牧」语义相关

similar_words.description: 「pasture」表牧场/放牧，「pastime」表消遣活动，拼写前四个字母相同，易混淆

definitions.example: The coach patted the nervous athlete on the back to boost his confidence before the race.
His pat response to the interview question impressed all the panel members.

pat.etymology: 拟声词，模仿轻拍柔软物体的声音，语义延伸为「轻拍的动作」及「恰到好处的」

similar_words.description: 「pat」多指温柔的轻拍（表安抚），「tap」多指轻敲（表提醒或示意），语义相近易混淆

definitions.example: A small patch of wildflowers grew beside the abandoned railway track.
The engineer patched the damaged pipeline to prevent further oil leakage.

patch.etymology: 源自古英语pæcce（小块、碎片），语义延伸为「补丁」及「修补动作」

similar_words.description: 「patch」表补丁/小块，「batch」表一批（人或物），拼写仅首字母不同，易混淆

definitions.example: The biotech company filed a patent for its new gene-editing technology to protect intellectual property.
It is a patent lie that the factory has never discharged untreated industrial wastewater.

patent.etymology: 源自拉丁语patere（公开），因专利需公开技术内容，语义延伸为「专利」；后引申为「明显的（公然的）」

similar_words.description: 「patent」表专利/明显的，「patient」表病人/有耐心的，拼写仅中间字母不同，易混淆

definitions.example: The study found that paternal involvement in early childhood education has a long-term positive impact.

paternal.etymology: 源自拉丁语pater（父亲），词根为pater-，加形容词后缀-al，表「与父亲相关的」

similar_words.description: 「paternal」表父亲的/父系的，「maternal」表母亲的/母系的，语义相对易混淆

definitions.meaning: 小路；路径；（行动或发展的）途径
definitions.example: Following the mountain path, the researchers finally reached the remote ethnic village.

path.etymology: 源自古英语pæth（小路），语义延伸为「抽象的发展路径」

similar_words.description: 「path」多指自然形成的小路，「lane」多指人工划定的车道或小巷，语义相近易混淆

definitions.meaning: 可怜的；可悲的（含轻微贬义）
definitions.example: The pathetic sight of elderly people begging on the street sparked public concern about social welfare.

pathetic.etymology: 源自拉丁语pathet-（受苦），词根为path-（感情），加形容词后缀-etic，表「引发怜悯或悲哀感情的」

similar_words.description: 「pathetic」表可怜的/可悲的（含贬义），「sympathetic」表同情的/共鸣的，语义相关易混淆

definitions.example: Mastering academic writing requires great patience and continuous revision of drafts.
She spent the long train journey playing patience to pass the time.

patience.etymology: 源自拉丁语patientia（忍耐），词根为pat-（忍受），表「忍受的能力」；后引申为「单人纸牌戏（需耐心玩的游戏）」

similar_words.description: 「patience」是不可数名词，表耐心；「patients」是可数名词「patient（病人）」的复数形式，拼写完全相同易混淆

definitions.example: Teachers should be patient with students who struggle with complex academic concepts.
Hospital staff must ensure each patient receives timely and appropriate medical care.

patient.etymology: 来自拉丁语pati（忍受），后缀-ent构成形容词或名词，表「具有…性质的人/物」

similar_words.description: 「patience」为名词，表‘耐心’；「patient」可作形容词‘有耐心的’或名词‘病人’，二者为同根派生关系
「impatient」为形容词，表‘不耐烦的’，是「patient」加反义前缀im-的派生词，词形相近易混淆

definitions.example: Many patriotic citizens volunteered to assist in disaster relief efforts during the national crisis.

patriotic.etymology: 词根patr（父，引申为「祖国」），后缀-otic表形容词，表「与…相关的」，合义为「与祖国相关的→爱国的」

similar_words.description: 「patriot」为名词，表‘爱国者’；「patriotic」为形容词，表‘爱国的’，二者为同根派生关系
「patriotic」表‘爱国的’，「patrol」表‘巡逻’，词形相近易混淆

definitions.example: Security guards patrol the campus perimeter 24 hours a day to ensure student safety.
A patrol of police officers was dispatched to the area following reports of suspicious activity.

patrol.etymology: 来自古法语patrouille，原指「在泥泞中行进的队伍」，后引申为「巡逻」

similar_words.description: 「patrol」表‘巡逻’，「patriotic」表‘爱国的’，词形相近易混淆
「patrol」表‘巡逻’，「petrol」表‘汽油’，词形仅中间字母a/e不同，易混淆

definitions.example: The local art gallery relies on donations from a wealthy patron to fund its annual exhibitions.
The small bookstore offered discounts to its regular patrons to encourage repeat business.
