patron.etymology: 词根patr（父，引申为「保护者」），后缀-on表名词，表「具有…身份的人」，合义为「保护者→赞助人」

similar_words.description: 「patron」表‘赞助人/老主顾’，「pattern」表‘模式/图案’，词形相近易混淆
「patriot」表‘爱国者’，「patron」表‘赞助人’，均含词根patr，语义不同易混淆

definitions.example: Researchers identified a consistent pattern in the data collected over the five-year study period.
The fabric was patterned with delicate floral designs that matched the room's decor.

pattern.etymology: 来自古法语patron（模型、原型），后引申为「模式、图案」

similar_words.description: 「pattern」表‘模式/图案’，「patron」表‘赞助人/老主顾’，词形相近易混淆
「pattern」侧重「重复的模式/样式」，「model」侧重「可供模仿的模型/范例」

definitions.example: The speaker paused briefly to take a sip of water before continuing her keynote address.
There was a long pause in the conversation after the controversial statement was made.

pause.etymology: 来自拉丁语pausa（停止、停顿），直接进入英语沿用至今

similar_words.description: 「pause」表‘暂停’，「paw」表‘爪子’，词形相近易混淆
「pause」侧重「短暂暂停，可继续」，「cease」侧重「永久停止，不再进行」

definitions.meaning: 铺（路、地面等）；为…铺平道路
definitions.example: Advances in medical research have paved the way for more effective treatments for rare diseases.

pave.etymology: 来自拉丁语pavire（敲打、压实），引申为「铺设硬质路面」

similar_words.description: 「pave」表‘铺路/铺平道路’，「wave」表‘波浪/挥手’，词形相近易混淆
「pave」特指「铺硬质路面（如沥青、石板）」，「lay」泛指「放置、铺设（各种材料）」

definitions.example: Pedestrians are required to walk on the pavement to avoid accidents with moving vehicles.

pavement.etymology: 动词pave（铺路）加名词后缀-ment，表「行为的结果」，合义为「铺好的地面→人行道/路面」

similar_words.description: 「pave」为动词‘铺路’，「pavement」为名词‘人行道/路面’，二者为同根派生关系
「pavement」为英式英语‘人行道’，「sidewalk」为美式英语‘人行道’

definitions.example: The cat used its sharp paw to scratch the surface of the wooden table.
The dog pawed at the door, eager to go outside for a walk.

paw.etymology: 来自古法语poue（动物的爪子），后进入英语沿用至今

similar_words.description: 「paw」表‘爪子’，「pause」表‘暂停’，词形相近易混淆
「paw」多指猫、狗等哺乳动物的「爪子」，「claw」多指鸟类、甲壳类的「爪/螯」

definitions.example: Online payment platforms have become increasingly popular due to their convenience and security.

payment.etymology: 动词pay（支付）加名词后缀-ment，表「行为或结果」，合义为「支付的行为/款项」

similar_words.description: 「pay」为动词‘支付’，「payment」为名词‘付款/款项’，二者为同根派生关系
「repayment」表‘偿还款项’，是「payment」加前缀re-（回、再）的派生词

definitions.example: Researchers used drought-tolerant peas to conduct agricultural experiments mentioned in the science passage.

pea.etymology: 来自拉丁语pisum（豌豆），为英语单根词，无复杂词缀变化

similar_words.description: pea指豌豆，peace指和平/平静，拼写仅差末尾的字母c

definitions.example: The international summit aimed to promote lasting peace in the war-torn region, as stated in the news report.

peace.etymology: 来自拉丁语pax（和平），为英语核心名词，同根词多含peace-前缀或后缀

similar_words.description: peace指和平/平静，pea指豌豆，拼写仅差末尾的字母c

definitions.example: The peaceful rural environment helped the scientists focus on their long-term research project.

peaceful.etymology: 由名词peace（和平）加形容词后缀-ful（表「具有…性质的」）构成，为派生词

similar_words.description: peace为名词，peaceful为其形容词形式，表「和平的/平静的」

definitions.example: The nutritional value of peaches was analyzed in the food science reading material.

peach.etymology: 来自拉丁语persicum（波斯苹果，即桃子），经法语简化为英语peach，为单根词

similar_words.description: peach指桃子/桃树，peak指山峰/峰值，拼写仅差中间的字母c

definitions.meaning: 山峰；（数据、经济等的）峰值
definitions.example: The inflation rate hit its peak in the third quarter, as shown in the economic analysis passage.
Peak sales of the new product occurred during the holiday season.

peak.etymology: 来自古英语pic（尖顶），为英语核心词，可作名词或形容词

similar_words.description: peak指山峰/峰值，peach指桃子/桃树，拼写仅差中间的字母c

definitions.example: Peanuts are widely used in the production of edible oil, as mentioned in the agricultural technology passage.

definitions.meaning: 小数额；微不足道的事物
definitions.example: The startup received only peanut funding from the local government, which hindered its expansion.

peanut.etymology: 为合成词，由pea（豌豆，因花生外形类似小型带壳豌豆）加nut（坚果）构成

similar_words.description: peanut为含pea的合成词，指花生/小数额，pea单独指豌豆

definitions.example: The ripening process of pears was observed to study plant hormone regulation in the biology experiment.

pear.etymology: 来自拉丁语pirum（梨），为英语单根词，无复杂词缀

similar_words.description: pear指梨，pearl指珍珠/珍品，拼写仅差末尾的字母l

definitions.example: The ancient pearl necklace unearthed from the tomb was studied to understand medieval trade routes, as stated in the archaeology passage.

pearl.etymology: 来自拉丁语perla（珍珠），为英语核心名词，可引申指「珍品」

similar_words.description: pearl指珍珠/珍品，pear指梨，拼写仅差末尾的字母l

definitions.example: The peasant revolts in the 16th century were a key topic in the European history reading material.

peasant.etymology: 来自拉丁语pagus（村庄），经法语进入英语，后缀-ant表「人」，指村庄里的劳动者即农民

similar_words.description: peasant指旧时的农民/佃农，tenant侧重指租赁土地的佃农/租户，强调租赁关系

definitions.example: Geologists examined the riverbed pebbles to determine the ancient water flow direction, as mentioned in the geography passage.

pebble.etymology: 来自古英语papol（小圆石），为英语单根词，表小型的圆形石块

similar_words.description: pebble指细小光滑的卵石/小圆石，rubble指破碎的瓦砾/碎石，尺寸更大且多为不规则形状

definitions.meaning: 特有的，独特的；奇怪的，古怪的
definitions.example: Each indigenous tribe has its peculiar cultural traditions that have been passed down for centuries.

peculiar.etymology: 源自拉丁语pecul-（私人财产，原指家畜）加形容词后缀-iar，原指“私人的”，引申为“特有的、古怪的”

similar_words.description: 「peculiar」侧重“独特的、反常的”，「particular」侧重“特定的、具体的”

definitions.meaning: n. 踏板；v. 踩踏板（骑车）
definitions.example: The cyclist pressed the brake pedal sharply to avoid colliding with the stray dog.

pedal.etymology: 源自拉丁语ped-（脚）加形容词后缀-al，指“用脚操作的部件”

similar_words.description: 「pedal」与“脚、踏板”相关，「peddle」指“兜售、叫卖”

definitions.example: The city government has built more overpasses to ensure pedestrian safety.
The academic paper was criticized for its pedestrian arguments and lack of original thinking.

pedestrian.etymology: 源自拉丁语ped-（脚）加后缀-estrian（表人），原指“步行的人”，引申为形容词义“平庸的（像步行一样普通）”

similar_words.description: 「pedestrian」指行人或平庸的，「pediatrician」指儿科医生，语义完全不同

definitions.meaning: v. 剥皮，削皮；n. 果皮
definitions.example: She peeled the orange carefully and distributed the segments to the children in the orphanage.

peel.etymology: 源自古英语pilian，指“剥去外层覆盖物”，引申为名词义“果皮”

similar_words.description: 「peel」指“剥皮、果皮”，「peal」指“钟声、鸣响”

definitions.meaning: v. 窥视，偷看；n. 窥视
definitions.example: The journalist peeped through the crack of the door to gather evidence of the illegal transaction.

peep.etymology: 拟声词，模拟轻轻窥视的细碎声响，引申为“偷偷看”的动作

similar_words.description: 「peep」侧重“偷偷摸摸且伴随小声的窥视”，「peek」侧重“快速瞥一眼”

definitions.example: Peer review is a crucial procedure to ensure the quality of academic papers.
The archaeologist peered at the tiny inscription on the ancient bronze ware.

peer.etymology: 源自拉丁语par（平等），原指“同等地位的人”，引申为动词义“仔细看（确认是否同等）”

similar_words.description: 「peer」指同龄人或凝视，「pier」指码头

definitions.example: The company was imposed a heavy penalty for violating the national environmental protection law.

penalty.etymology: 源自拉丁语pen-（惩罚）加名词后缀-alty，指“对违规行为的惩处”

similar_words.description: 「penalty」指“外在的惩罚、罚金”，「penance」指“内在的忏悔、赎罪”

definitions.example: The pendulum of the old church clock has swung steadily for over a hundred years.
Public attitude towards the new education policy has swung like a pendulum in the past year.

pendulum.etymology: 源自拉丁语pend-（悬挂）加后缀-ulum（表工具），指“悬挂摆动的工具”，引申为“摇摆的事态”

similar_words.description: 「pendulum」指钟摆或摇摆事态，「pendant」指吊坠饰品

definitions.example: The heavy rain penetrated the leaky roof and soaked the books on the shelf.
Only a few experts can penetrate the hidden logic of the economic crisis.

penetrate.etymology: 源自拉丁语penetr-（穿过）加动词后缀-ate，原指“物理穿透”，引申为“抽象洞察”

similar_words.description: 「penetrate」侧重“主动穿透某一障碍”，「permeate」侧重“弥漫、渗透至各处”

definitions.example: The Arabian Peninsula is rich in oil reserves and has a unique desert climate.

peninsula.etymology: 源自拉丁语pen-（几乎，接近）加insul-（岛屿）加名词后缀-a，指“几乎是岛的陆地（三面环海）”

similar_words.description: 「peninsula」指“三面环海的半岛”，「island」指“四面环海的岛屿”

definitions.meaning: 便士（英国等国的小额货币单位）
definitions.example: One penny donated by each citizen can accumulate into a substantial fund for public welfare.

penny.etymology: 来自古英语penig，原指小额金属硬币

similar_words.description: 「penny」为单数便士（表单个硬币），「pence」为复数便士（表金额）

definitions.example: Many elderly rural residents rely on their monthly pension to cover basic medical expenses.

pension.etymology: 来自拉丁语pensio（支付），词根pens-表「称量、支付」，指定期发放的福利金

similar_words.description: 「pension」指养老金，「pensioner」指领养老金的人

definitions.example: A pinch of pepper can significantly enhance the umami flavor of homemade soup.

definitions.meaning: （在话语、文本中）零星插入、点缀
definitions.example: The award-winning essay was peppered with subtle references to classical Chinese poetry.

pepper.etymology: 来自拉丁语piper，原指胡椒这种香料

similar_words.description: 「paper」指纸张、论文，「pepper」指胡椒粉或点缀动作

definitions.example: People often perceive remote work as a more flexible employment model.

perceive.etymology: per-（完全）+ ceive（抓、拿），完全抓住信息→感知、理解

similar_words.description: 「perceive」为动词表感知动作，「perception」为名词表感知结果或看法

definitions.example: Nearly 40 percent of the participants reported improved sleep quality after the experiment.

percent.etymology: per（每）+ cent（百），每一百中的比例→百分之…

similar_words.description: 「percent」需与具体数字连用（如20 percent），「percentage」可单独作主语

definitions.example: The percentage of college students choosing to study abroad has declined in recent years.

percentage.etymology: per（每）+ cent（百）+ age（名词后缀），表百分之的比率

similar_words.description: 「percentage」可单独充当句子成分，「percent」必须搭配具体数字

definitions.example: Public perception of artificial intelligence has shifted from fear to cautious optimism.

perception.etymology: per（完全）+ cept（抓、拿）+ ion（名词后缀），完全抓住信息的结果→感知、看法

similar_words.description: 「perception」为名词表感知或看法，「perceive」为动词表感知动作

definitions.example: The study focuses on the perceptual differences between children and adults in facial recognition.

perceptual.etymology: per（完全）+ cept（抓、拿）+ ual（形容词后缀），与感知过程相关的

similar_words.description: 「perceptual」指与感知机制相关的（学术义），「perceptive」指人有洞察力的

definitions.example: The architect spent five years refining the design in pursuit of structural perfection.

perfection.etymology: per（完全）+ fect（做）+ ion（名词后缀），完全做好的状态→完美

similar_words.description: 「perfection」指完美（状态），「perfectionist」指追求完美的人

definitions.example: Students who engage in regular exercise often perform better in academic assessments.

perform.etymology: per（完全）+ form（形式、做），完全按照形式完成→执行、表现

similar_words.description: 「perform」为动词表执行或表演动作，「performance」为名词表表现或表演结果

definitions.example: The company’s annual financial report highlighted its strong performance in emerging markets.
This new semiconductor material significantly improves the performance of electronic devices.

performance.etymology: 由动词perform（执行、完成）加名词后缀-ance构成，表行为的结果或状态

similar_words.description: 拼写接近，均为以-per开头的名词；performance表「表现、业绩/性能」，perfection表「完美、完善」，语义差异较大易混淆

definitions.example: The faint perfume of jasmine drifted through the open window of the old university library.

perfume.etymology: 由前缀per-（完全）加词根fume（烟、气）构成，原指「弥漫的香气」，后引申为香水

similar_words.description: 同含词根fume（烟、气）；perfume表「香水、香气」，fume单独作名词表「烟雾、怒气」，易混淆词根衍生义

definitions.example: Perhaps the most striking finding of the study is its correlation between social capital and life satisfaction.

perhaps.etymology: 由前缀per-（通过）加古英语haps（机会）构成，原指「通过机会发生」，引申为可能性

similar_words.description: 均表可能性；perhaps为正式书面语（适配考研阅读/写作），maybe为口语化表达

definitions.example: The interwar period saw a surge in experimental art movements across Europe.

period.etymology: 源自希腊语periodos（周期、循环），进入英语后指具有特定特征的时间阶段

similar_words.description: 均表「阶段」；period侧重较长的、有明确起止的完整时期，phase侧重某一过程中的特定环节

definitions.example: The groundbreaking research was featured in a peer-reviewed scientific periodical.

periodical.etymology: 由period（时期）加形容词后缀-ical构成，后转指定期出版的刊物

similar_words.description: 均指学术出版物；periodical为广义的定期期刊，journal多指专业学会或高校主办的权威学术刊物

definitions.example: Many ancient Mesopotamian civilizations perished due to prolonged drought and political unrest.
The rubber hoses on the vintage car began to perish after decades of exposure to harsh weather.

perish.etymology: 由前缀per-（完全）加动词后缀-ish构成，原指「完全消失」，后引申为毁灭、变质

similar_words.description: 均表「消失」；perish侧重彻底毁灭、消亡（常指生物、文明），vanish侧重突然、神秘地消失

definitions.example: The 2010 oil spill caused permanent damage to the fragile coastal ecosystem of the Gulf of Mexico.

permanent.etymology: 由前缀per-（完全）加词根man（停留，源自拉丁语manere）加形容词后缀-ent构成，指「完全停留的」，即永久的

similar_words.description: 均表「永久的」；permanent侧重状态持久不变，perpetual侧重无间断地持续

definitions.meaning: 弥漫；渗透（尤指思想、文化的传播）
definitions.example: The concept of inclusive education has gradually permeated educational policies across many OECD countries.

permeate.etymology: 由前缀per-（通过）加词根meare（走，源自拉丁语meare）加动词后缀-ate构成，指「通过并走遍」，即渗透

similar_words.description: 均表「渗透」；permeate侧重弥漫、充满整个空间或领域，penetrate侧重刺穿、深入某一物体或组织

definitions.example: Researchers must obtain written permission from the ethics committee before conducting human subject experiments.

permission.etymology: 由动词permit（允许）加名词后缀-ion构成，表许可的行为或状态

similar_words.description: 同形衍生词；permission为不可数名词，表「许可的行为/状态」，permit作名词时为可数名词，表「许可证、通行证」

definitions.example: The new immigration policy permits highly skilled workers to apply for permanent residency within two years.
Foreign nationals must hold a valid work permit to be legally employed in Canada.

permit.etymology: 由前缀per-（通过）加词根mit（送，源自拉丁语mittere）构成，原指「允许通过」，引申为许可、许可证

similar_words.description: 同含词根mit（送）；permit表「允许通过、许可」，admit表「允许进入、承认」

definitions.example: Perpetual noise from urban construction has severely disrupted the daily life of local residents and harmed wildlife habitats.

perpetual.etymology: per-（始终）+ pet（追求）+ -ual（形容词后缀），引申为始终持续的状态

similar_words.description: 「permanent」侧重状态长期不变；「perpetual」侧重持续无间断

definitions.example: The contradictory findings in the sociological study perplexed even the most experienced researchers in the field.

perplex.etymology: per-（完全）+ plex（折叠），完全折叠理不清，引申为使困惑

similar_words.description: 「puzzle」为常用词，可指困惑的事；「perplex」为正式用词，侧重因复杂难解而困惑

definitions.example: During the totalitarian regime, many intellectuals were persecuted for expressing their independent political views.

persecute.etymology: per-（始终）+ secut（跟随）+ -e，始终追赶纠缠，引申为迫害

similar_words.description: 「prosecute」意为「起诉、检举」；「persecute」意为「迫害」，词形相近易混淆

definitions.example: The Nobel Prize winner attributed his success to decades of perseverance in basic scientific research.

perseverance.etymology: per-（始终）+ sever（坚持）+ -ance（名词后缀），表始终坚持的品质

similar_words.description: 「persistence」侧重持续状态，可含贬义；「perseverance」侧重克服困难的毅力，含褒义

definitions.example: Despite repeated failures in the experiment, the young scientist persevered and finally made a groundbreaking discovery.

persevere.etymology: per-（始终）+ sever（坚持）+ -e（动词后缀），表始终坚持的动作

similar_words.description: 「persist」可表中性或贬义的坚持；「persevere」仅表褒义的、克服困难的坚持

definitions.example: If the extreme weather persists, the government will launch an emergency relief program for affected regions.

persist.etymology: per-（始终）+ sist（站立），始终站立，引申为坚持、持续

similar_words.description: 「insist」侧重坚持主张、意见，后常接on/that从句；「persist」侧重坚持行动、状态，后常接in doing sth

definitions.example: Many graduate programs require applicants to submit a personal statement outlining their academic goals and research experience.
The department head gave a personal feedback session to each graduating student to discuss their career plans.

personal.etymology: person（人）+ -al（形容词后缀），表属于个人的

similar_words.description: 「personnel」为集合名词，指「全体职员、人事部门」；「personal」为形容词，指「个人的、私人的」，词形相近易混淆

definitions.example: In management studies, a leader’s personality traits are found to have a significant impact on team performance and employee satisfaction.

personality.etymology: person（人）+ -ality（名词后缀，表性质），表人的性格特质

similar_words.description: 「character」侧重人的品德、品格；「personality」侧重人的性格、行事风格等整体特质

definitions.meaning: 全体职员，人员；人事部门
definitions.example: The university’s personnel office is currently reviewing applications for the new tenure-track faculty positions.

personnel.etymology: person（人）+ -nel（名词后缀，表集合），表人的集合体

similar_words.description: 「personal」为形容词，指「个人的、私人的」；「personnel」为集合名词，指「全体职员、人事部门」，词形相近易混淆

definitions.example: From a cross-cultural perspective, the concept of 「success」 varies greatly across different societies.
The Renaissance artist’s use of geometric perspective revolutionized the way depth was represented in European painting.

perspective.etymology: per-（通过）+ spect（看）+ -ive（名词后缀），表通过特定角度观察的方式，引申为观点、透视法

similar_words.description: 「prospective」为形容词，指「预期的、未来的」；「perspective」为名词，指「观点、透视法」，词形相近易混淆

definitions.example: Environmental activists tried to persuade local governments to enforce stricter vehicle emission standards.

persuade.etymology: 词根词缀：per-（完全、彻底）+ 词根suad-（建议、劝说）+ 动词后缀-e，意为「彻底劝说」，引申为说服

similar_words.description: 近义词易混，persuade侧重劝说的动作（常接sb to do），convince侧重使信服的结果（常接sb of sth）

definitions.example: Her data-backed argument had strong persuasion and won the support of the research committee.

persuasion.etymology: persuade的名词形式：persuad-（=persuade，劝说）+ 名词后缀-ion，表动作或结果

similar_words.description: 近义词易混，persuasion表说服的动作/能力，conviction表坚定的信念（无劝说含义）

definitions.example: Pessimistic economic analysts warned that the global recession might last longer than expected.

pessimistic.etymology: 词根词缀：pessim-（最坏的）+ 名词后缀-ist（表人，悲观主义者）+ 形容词后缀-ic，表「属于悲观主义者的」

similar_words.description: 反义易混（态度题常考），pessimistic表悲观，optimistic表乐观，需注意拼写区分

definitions.example: The invasive agricultural pest caused a 30% drop in local wheat production last year.

pest.etymology: 源自拉丁语pestis（瘟疫、灾祸），原指传播瘟疫的生物，后引申为害虫、讨厌鬼

similar_words.description: 形近易混，pest指害虫/讨厌鬼，pestle指研磨用的杵（如研杵）

definitions.example: A growing number of elderly people keep a pet to alleviate loneliness in their daily life.

pet.etymology: 源自中古英语pet（心爱的动物或小孩），原指被宠爱者，后专指宠物

similar_words.description: 形近易混，pet指宠物/宠儿，pat指轻拍的动作或声音

definitions.example: Local residents submitted a signed petition to the city council opposing the construction of a waste incinerator.

petition.etymology: 词根词缀：pet-（寻求、请求）+ 名词后缀-ition，表「请求的文件或行为」，作动词表请愿

similar_words.description: 形近易混，petition指请愿书/请愿，potion指药水、饮剂（尤指药用或魔法药水）

definitions.example: The sharp rise in petrol prices has increased the cost of public transportation in urban areas.

petrol.etymology: 词根词缀：petr-（岩石、石油）+ 名词后缀-ol（表油类），指从石油中提炼的汽油

similar_words.description: 同根易混，petrol指提炼后的汽油，petroleum指未提炼的原油

definitions.example: Petroleum is still the primary energy source for most industrial and transportation sectors worldwide.

petroleum.etymology: 词根词缀：petr-（岩石）+ 拉丁语oleum（油），原指「岩石油」，即天然存在的原油

similar_words.description: 同根易混，petroleum指未加工的原油，petrol指加工后的汽油

definitions.example: The manager wasted too much time on petty administrative details instead of strategic planning.

petty.etymology: 词根词缀：petit-（小的）+ 形容词后缀-y，表「小的、微不足道的」，引申为琐碎的、小气的

similar_words.description: 形近易混，petty指琐碎的/小气的，pretty指漂亮的/相当（作副词）

definitions.example: The community pharmacy offers free health consultations and prescription refills for senior citizens.

pharmacy.etymology: 词根词缀：pharm-（药、药物）+ 名词后缀-acy（表场所或学科），指售药的场所或药学学科

similar_words.description: 同根易混，pharmacy指药房/药学实践，pharmacology指研究药物的药理学学科

definitions.example: Many ecological studies suggest that climate change has entered an irreversible phase worldwide.
The new carbon reduction policy will be phased in over five years to minimize its impact on small businesses.

phase.etymology: 词根phas-（表「显示、出现」），源自希腊语phasis，原指天体的星相，后引申为发展的阶段

similar_words.description: 二者拼写相近易混淆，「phase」多指事物发展的阶段，「phrase」多指语言中的短语或措辞表达

definitions.example: Glacier melting is a widely documented phenomenon driven by global temperature rise.
The teenage math prodigy was regarded as a phenomenon in the international academic circle.

phenomenon.etymology: 词根phen-（表「显示、出现」），-menon为希腊语名词后缀，字面含义为“显现出来的事物”

similar_words.description: 「phenomenon」是单数名词，「phenomena」是其对应的复数形式，考研语法与翻译题型常考查二者的单复数辨析

definitions.meaning: 爱好音乐的；爱乐团体的
definitions.example: The Shanghai Philharmonic Orchestra has launched a series of popularization activities for classical music.
The Vienna Philharmonic is famous for its annual New Year Concert broadcast globally.

philharmonic.etymology: 由词根phil-（表「爱」）与harmon-（表「和谐、音乐」）构成，字面含义为喜爱音乐的

similar_words.description: 二者共含表「爱」的词根phil-，「philharmonic」与音乐相关，「philosopher」指研究哲学的哲学家

definitions.example: Ancient Greek philosopher Socrates is known for his dialectical method of questioning to explore truth.

philosopher.etymology: 词根phil-（表「爱」）+ soph-（表「智慧」）+ -er（表人的名词后缀），字面含义为爱智慧的人

similar_words.description: 二者为同根派生词，「philosopher」指从事哲学研究的人，「philosophy」指哲学、人生理念

definitions.example: The philosophy of utilitarianism has a profound influence on modern social policy making.
The startup’s core philosophy is to make affordable clean energy accessible to rural households.

philosophy.etymology: 词根phil-（表「爱」）+ soph-（表「智慧」）+ -y（名词后缀），原指对智慧的追求，后引申为哲学、理念

similar_words.description: 二者为同根派生词，「philosophy」指哲学、理念，「philosopher」指研究哲学的哲学家

definitions.example: All attendees are required to mute their phones during the academic defense session.
In phonetics, a phone refers to the smallest distinct unit of human speech sound.

phone.etymology: 词根phon-（表「声音」），是telephone的缩写形式，原指声音，后引申为电话

similar_words.description: 二者拼写相近易混淆，「phone」多指电话或音素，「phase」多指事物发展的阶段

definitions.example: Mastering common fixed phrases can greatly improve one’s efficiency in English reading and writing.
The spokesperson phrased the announcement carefully to avoid unnecessary public controversy.

phrase.etymology: 词根phas-（表「显示、表达」），原指话语的表达方式，后引申为短语、措辞

similar_words.description: 二者拼写相近易混淆，「phrase」指短语或措辞表达，「phase」指事物发展的阶段

definitions.example: Studies show that regular physical activity can reduce the risk of many chronic diseases significantly.
The new measurement method can accurately calculate the physical properties of nanomaterials.

physical.etymology: 词根phys-（表「自然、身体」）+ -ical（形容词后缀），原指关于自然规律的，后引申为身体的、物理的

similar_words.description: 二者同含词根phys-，「physical」指身体的、物理的，「physiological」指生理的、生理学的，语义和用法易混淆

definitions.example: The primary care physician suggested that the patient should take a regular health check every year.

physician.etymology: 词根phys-（表「身体、自然」）+ -ician（表专业人员的名词后缀），原指研究人体健康规律的人，后引申为内科医生

similar_words.description: 二者拼写相近易混淆，「physician」指内科医生，「physicist」指物理学家

definitions.example: Nobel Prize-winning physicist Albert Einstein proposed the theory of relativity in the early 20th century.

physicist.etymology: 词根phys-（表「自然、物理」）+ -ic（形容词后缀）+ -ist（表专业人员的名词后缀），指从事物理学研究的专业人员

similar_words.description: 二者拼写相近易混淆，「physicist」指物理学家，「physician」指内科医生

definitions.example: Many cutting-edge researches in renewable energy rely on advanced theories of physics to optimize energy conversion efficiency.

physics.etymology: 来自希腊语词根phys-（自然、物理），加学科后缀-ics，表「研究自然规律的学科」

similar_words.description: physics指「物理学」这一学科，physicist指「物理学家」这一职业身份

definitions.example: The study explores the physiological responses of the human body to extreme high-altitude environments.

physiological.etymology: 来自词根physi-（生理、自然），加形容词后缀-ological，是名词physiology（生理学）的形容词形式

similar_words.description: physiological侧重「与生理学、生理机能相关的」，physical侧重「身体的；物理的」

definitions.example: A white pickup loaded with construction materials stopped at the entrance of the building site.
The travel agency provides complimentary airport pickup for clients who book luxury tour packages.

pickup.etymology: 由动词短语「pick up」（接载、拾起）合成的名词，表相关事物或行为

similar_words.description: pickup特指「轻型载货皮卡」，truck泛指「各类载货卡车」

definitions.example: The biology class organized a picnic in the forest to observe plant species in their natural habitat.

picnic.etymology: 源自法语pique-nique，原指参与者自带食物的聚会

similar_words.description: picnic指「野餐」，panic指「恐慌」，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The traditional apple pie is a popular dessert in Western family gatherings during holiday seasons.

pie.etymology: 源自古英语pīe，原指喜鹊（因喜鹊收集杂物，后引申为用多种食材制作的馅饼）

similar_words.description: pie指「馅饼」，lie指「躺；说谎」，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The high-power laser beam can pierce through the thick metal plate with extreme precision in industrial processing.
A sudden scream pierced the stillness of the remote mountain village at midnight.

pierce.etymology: 源自拉丁语pungere（刺），同源词包括punch（打孔）等

similar_words.description: pierce指「彻底刺穿」，prick指「轻刺、扎小窟窿」，二者语义程度有别

definitions.example: The research on pig genome provides valuable insights into human genetic diseases due to their high genetic similarity.

similar_words.description: pig指「猪」，big指「大的」，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The homing pigeon has been used as a reliable messenger for centuries due to its excellent navigation ability.

similar_words.description: pigeon指「鸽子」，penguin指「企鹅」，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: A pile of ungraded academic papers lay on the professor's desk, waiting to be reviewed.
Hundreds of job seekers piled into the career fair to seek positions in multinational corporations.

pile.etymology: 源自古英语pīl（桩、柱），后引申为「堆积的物品」

similar_words.description: pile指「有序或随意堆放的堆」，heap指「大量无序堆积的堆」，二者语义程度有别

definitions.example: Every year, thousands of pilgrims travel to the ancient sacred temple to participate in traditional religious ceremonies.
The pilgrims who sailed on the Mayflower in 1620 established the first permanent English settlement in North America.

pilgrim.etymology: 源自拉丁语peregrinus（外国人、流浪者），后引申为「前往圣地朝拜的流浪者」

similar_words.description: pilgrim指「朝圣者」，pilgrimage指「朝圣之旅」，二者为同根词（pilgrim加名词后缀-age表行为/旅程）

definitions.example: Many patients with chronic diseases rely on daily pills to control their symptoms.

pill.etymology: 源自拉丁语pilula，原指小丸状物，后特指药用药丸，为基础词根词

similar_words.description: 「pill」指药用药片，「pillow」指枕头，词形相近易混淆

definitions.example: The marble pillars of the ancient university library have witnessed generations of scholars’ efforts.
Outstanding researchers are the pillars of the national scientific innovation system.

pillar.etymology: 源自拉丁语pila（柱状支撑物），加名词后缀-ar，引申为核心力量

similar_words.description: 「pillar」指柱子或核心力量，「pill」指药用药片，词形相近易混淆

definitions.example: She placed the annotated thesis draft under her pillow to review before the defense.

pillow.etymology: 源自词根pill（小丸状物）加名词后缀-ow，因古代枕头多为圆球状得名

similar_words.description: 「pillow」指枕头，「pill」指药用药片，词形相近易混淆

definitions.example: The experienced pilot safely landed the research aircraft despite the stormy weather.
The education department will pilot a new postgraduate admissions system next year.

pilot.etymology: 源自希腊语pēlotēs（掌舵者），后引申为飞行员、领航员，动词义指「掌舵项目」即试行

similar_words.description: 「pilot」指试行具体方案或担任飞行员，「pioneer」指开创某领域或事业，语义侧重不同

definitions.example: She used a pin to fasten the literature review outline to her desk bulletin board.

definitions.meaning: （用别针等）别住；固定（想法、含义）
definitions.example: Linguists struggle to pin down the exact semantic range of the ancient term.

pin.etymology: 源自古英语pinn（细尖固定物），动词义由名词功能引申而来

similar_words.description: 「pin」指大头针或固定动作，「pill」指药用药片，词形相近易混淆

definitions.example: The rising tuition fees pinch many low-income postgraduate applicants.
Add a pinch of sodium chloride to the experimental solution as per the lab protocol.

pinch.etymology: 源自拉丁语pungere（刺），后引申为捏、掐的动作，再引申为经济拮据

similar_words.description: 「pinch」指捏、掐或使经济拮据，「punch」指打孔或拳击，词形相近易混淆

definitions.example: The pine trees surrounding the research station provide shelter for local wildlife.
She pined for her hometown while conducting field research in remote areas for her doctorate.

pine.etymology: 源自古英语pin（松树），动词义由「松树树脂滴落的状态」引申为憔悴、渴望

similar_words.description: 「pine」指松树或因思念憔悴，「vine」指藤蔓植物，词形相近易混淆

definitions.example: The research team used pink highlighters to mark key findings in the survey data.
The scholar’s pink stance on social welfare was debated in the political science seminar.

pink.etymology: 源自荷兰语pink（小鳍鱼），因鱼身呈淡粉色引申为颜色义，政治义为隐喻用法

similar_words.description: 「pink」指粉色或政治偏左的观点，「pint」指品脱容量单位，词形相近易混淆

definitions.example: The biology lab requires a pint of distilled water to prepare the cell culture medium.

pint.etymology: 源自拉丁语pincta（古罗马容量单位），后进入英语指品脱

similar_words.description: 「pint」指品脱容量单位，「pink」指粉色或政治偏左的观点，词形相近易混淆

definitions.example: The engineers replaced the rusted pipe in the university’s central heating system.
Birds piped melodiously outside the library where postgraduates prepared for their exams.

pipe.etymology: 源自古英语pīpa（管状物体），动词义由名词的功能或形态引申而来

similar_words.description: 「pipe」指管子、管道或鸟鸣叫，「ripe」指成熟的，词形相近易混淆

definitions.example: Archaeologists discovered the remains of a 17th-century pirate ship off the coast of Florida.
Some academic journals have strict policies against pirating research findings from other scholars.

pirate.etymology: 由希腊语peiran（尝试、冒险）派生，转经拉丁语pirata，后缀-ate兼具名、动属性；原指冒险出海的人，后引申为海盗，动词义指像海盗掠夺般盗用版权

similar_words.description: 二者均含「盗用他人成果」语义；pirate可指海盗或剽窃行为，plagiarist专指学术剽窃者

definitions.example: The detective drew his pistol when he spotted the suspect hiding behind the warehouse door.

pistol.etymology: 源自德语pistole，原指枪管带孔的小型火器，词根pist-表「孔、管」，后缀-ol表「小型物品」

similar_words.description: 二者拼写形近；pistol指武器手枪，piston指机械部件活塞

definitions.example: The piston moves up and down inside the cylinder to convert thermal energy into mechanical power.

piston.etymology: 源自拉丁语pinsere（压、推），词根pist-表「推、压」，后缀-on表「部件」；指内燃机中推动曲轴的核心机械部件

similar_words.description: 二者拼写形近；piston指机械活塞，pistol指武器手枪

definitions.example: The miners fell into a deep pit after the underground tunnel collapsed.
The upcoming negotiation will pit labor unions against corporate management over wage increases.

pit.etymology: 源自古英语pytt（挖成的坑），动词义由「挖坑使陷入」引申为「使双方对立竞争」，为考研熟词僻义考点

similar_words.description: 二者均与「坑」相关；pit指实体凹陷或抽象对立，pitfall指陷阱、隐患（抽象坑）

definitions.example: The debate over climate change reached a fever pitch ahead of the international summit.
The startup team pitched their renewable energy plan to a panel of venture capitalists.

pitch.etymology: 源自古英语piccian（刺、投），由「投射」引申为「音调（声音投射）」「程度（强度投射）」「推销（观点投射）」

similar_words.description: 二者拼写形近；pitch指程度、推销，patch指补丁、小块区域

definitions.example: The 14th-century Black Death was a plague that killed over 20 million people across Europe.
Rural areas in many developing countries are plagued by inadequate medical facilities.

plague.etymology: 源自拉丁语plaga（打击、灾害），由「打击人类的灾害」引申为「瘟疫」，动词义指「持续打击、困扰」

similar_words.description: 二者拼写形近；plague指瘟疫、困扰，plaque指牌匾、牙菌斑

definitions.example: The guidelines for the research paper are written in plain language to avoid ambiguity.
The Great Plains in North America are known for their vast grasslands and agricultural productivity.

plain.etymology: 源自拉丁语planus（平的），由「平坦的」引申为「清楚的（平白无修饰）」「朴素的」，名词义指「平坦的区域（平原）」

similar_words.description: 二者拼写形近；plain指清楚的、平原，plane指飞机、平面

definitions.example: Astronomers use advanced telescopes to study the atmospheric composition of distant exoplanets.

planet.etymology: 源自希腊语planētēs（漫游者），词根plan-表「漫游」，后缀-et表「天体」；原指在恒星背景中移动的天体，即行星

similar_words.description: 二者均为天体；planet绕恒星运行，satellite绕行星运行（卫星）

definitions.example: The transatlantic slave trade was closely linked to the expansion of sugar plantations in the Caribbean.

plantation.etymology: 由plant（种植）+ -ation（名词后缀，表场所）构成，指专门种植经济作物的大型农业场所

similar_words.description: 二者均为大型农业场所；plantation种植经济作物，ranch饲养牲畜（牧场）

definitions.example: The nurse applied a waterproof plaster to the patient's cut to prevent infection.
Campaign posters for the mayoral candidate were plastered on every available public surface.

plaster.etymology: 源自希腊语plastron（石膏），由「石膏类材料」引申为「医用膏药」「建筑灰泥」，动词义指「用灰泥/粘贴物固定」

similar_words.description: 二者拼写形近；plaster指膏药、灰泥，plastic指塑料、可塑的

definitions.example: Environmentalists urge consumers to reduce the use of single-use plastic products to curb ocean pollution.
Adolescent brains are still in a plastic stage and can be shaped by various environmental factors.

plastic.etymology: 源自希腊语plastikos（可塑造的），词根plast-意为「塑造、成型」

similar_words.description: 「plastic」指塑料或可塑的，「plaster」指灰泥、膏药

definitions.example: The Qinghai-Tibet Plateau is known as the 'Roof of the World' for its high altitude and unique ecosystem.
After five years of rapid expansion, the company's revenue growth hit a plateau in 2022.

plateau.etymology: 源自法语plateau（平的高地），词根plat-意为「平坦的」

similar_words.description: 「plateau」指高原或发展停滞期，「plate」指盘子或金属板

definitions.example: Social media platforms have become major channels for information dissemination and public opinion expression.
The party's election platform focuses on improving public healthcare and reducing income inequality.

platform.etymology: 合成自法语plat（平坦的）+forme（形状），意为「平坦的场地」

similar_words.description: 「platform」指平台、站台或政党纲领，「plateau」指高原或发展停滞期

definitions.meaning: 貌似可信的，貌似合理的
definitions.example: The researcher put forward a plausible hypothesis to explain the unexpected experimental results, but further verification is needed.

plausible.etymology: 源自拉丁语plausibilis（值得喝彩的），词根plaus-意为「鼓掌、认可」

similar_words.description: 「plausible」指貌似可信但未必真实，「possible」指客观上存在可能性

definitions.example: The local government allocated funds to renovate the school playground to ensure students have a safe space for physical activities.
The emerging digital economy has become a playground for innovative startups worldwide.

playground.etymology: 合成词，由play（玩耍）+ground（场地）构成

similar_words.description: 「playground」指操场、游乐场，「playfield」指（球类）运动场

definitions.example: The charity made a desperate plea for donations to support the flood victims in the disaster-stricken area.
The defendant entered a plea of not guilty during the court hearing last week.

plea.etymology: 源自拉丁语placere（取悦、请求），词根plac-意为「安抚、取悦」

similar_words.description: 「plea」是名词，表恳求或法律抗辩；「plead」是动词，表恳求或法律答辩

definitions.example: The mother pleaded with the doctor to do everything possible to save her critically ill child.
The suspect pleaded guilty to the charge of theft in exchange for a reduced sentence.

plead.etymology: 与plea同根，源自拉丁语placere（取悦、请求），动词形式表「恳求、答辩」

similar_words.description: 「plead」是动词，表恳求或法律答辩；「plea」是名词，表恳求或法律抗辩

definitions.example: The pleasant weather and scenic views made the weekend hiking trip an unforgettable experience for all participants.

pleasant.etymology: 源自动词please（取悦），后缀-ant表「具有…性质的」，意为「令人愉悦的」

similar_words.description: 「pleasant」侧重事物或环境整体令人愉快，「pleasing」侧重符合他人心意、讨人喜欢

definitions.example: The government made a solemn pledge to reduce carbon emissions by 40 percent before 2030.
All graduates pledged to abide by the school's code of conduct and contribute to social development.

pledge.etymology: 源自古法语plege（保证、抵押），核心义为「承诺」

similar_words.description: 「pledge」指誓言、保证，「plight」指困境、苦境

definitions.example: The fertile farmland in the region provides plentiful grain supplies for the entire country every year.

plentiful.etymology: 源自名词plenty（丰富），后缀-ful表「充满…的」，意为「充足的」

similar_words.description: 「plentiful」侧重数量充足且够用，「abundant」侧重数量极其丰富

definitions.example: Plenty of social science studies have examined the correlation between educational attainment and social mobility.

plenty.etymology: 源自拉丁语plenus（满的），无复杂词缀，与同根词「plentiful」语义相关

similar_words.description: 「plentiful」为形容词，表「充足的」；「plenty」为代词/名词，表「大量」

definitions.example: The plight of rural left-behind children has been a key focus of China's social policy research in recent years.

plight.etymology: 源自古英语plight（危险），无明显可拆分词根词缀

similar_words.description: 「pledge」为名词/动词，表「保证；发誓」；「plight」为名词，表「困境」

definitions.example: The plot of the award-winning science fiction film revolves around a team of astronauts trying to save Earth from an asteroid collision.

plot.etymology: 源自古英语plot（小块地；计划），无复杂词缀

similar_words.description: 「ploy」为名词，表「花招；策略」；「plot」侧重指精心策划的阴谋或故事的情节

definitions.example: Small-scale farmers in mountainous areas often rely on traditional ploughs to prepare their fields.

definitions.meaning: （船、车辆等）费力穿过；破浪前进
definitions.example: The research vessel ploughed through the stormy Atlantic to collect data on deep-sea ecosystems.

plough.etymology: 源自古英语plōh（农耕工具），无明显可拆分词根词缀

similar_words.description: 「plow」为美式拼法，「plough」为英式拼法，二者语义完全一致

definitions.example: Engineers installed a special plug to stop the leakage of crude oil from the offshore drilling platform.

plug.etymology: 源自古荷兰语plugge（塞子），无复杂词缀

similar_words.description: 「plumber」为名词，表「水管工」；「plug」为名词/动词，表「塞子；堵塞」

definitions.example: The university maintenance department called a plumber to repair the blocked sewage pipes in the dormitory.

plumber.etymology: 源自plumb（铅，古代水管常用铅制作）+ 名词后缀-er（表人），原指「制作铅管的人」，后引申为水管工

similar_words.description: 「plumb」为同根名词/形容词，表「铅锤；垂直的」；「plumber」指从事管道维修的工人

definitions.example: Many young graduates plunge into entrepreneurship despite the high risks and uncertainties.
The stock price of the tech giant plunged by 15% after the release of its disappointing quarterly earnings report.

plunge.etymology: 源自拉丁语plungere（浸入），无复杂词缀

similar_words.description: 「lunge」为动词/名词，表「猛冲；刺」；「plunge」侧重指突然投入、陷入或骤降

definitions.meaning: 复数的；多元的；复数（形式）
definitions.example: Contemporary democratic systems emphasize the protection of plural interests and diverse voices.

plural.etymology: 源自拉丁语pluralis（多的），由词根plur-（多）+ 形容词后缀-al构成

similar_words.description: 「plentiful」表「充足的」，侧重数量；「plural」表「多元的；复数的」，侧重形式或种类

definitions.example: Plus the grants from private foundations, the environmental protection program has raised enough funds to launch.
The statistical report indicated a plus 3% increase in renewable energy consumption last year.

plus.etymology: 源自拉丁语plus（更多），无复杂词缀

similar_words.description: 「minus」为介词/形容词，表「减；负面的」，与「plus」语义相反，常出现在数据统计语境

definitions.example: The clinical study found that vaccination can significantly reduce the risk of severe pneumonia in elderly patients.

pneumonia.etymology: 源自希腊语pneumon（肺）+ 名词后缀-ia（表疾病），指发生在肺部的炎症疾病

similar_words.description: 「pneumatic」为形容词，表「气动的；充气的」；「pneumonia」为名词，表「肺炎」，二者同源于希腊语表「肺/气」的词根

definitions.example: She slipped the coded note into her inner jacket pocket before the diplomatic meeting.
The local official was accused of pocketing funds allocated for rural infrastructure projects.

pocket.etymology: 源自古法语「poche」（口袋），无复杂词缀，属于基础名词转义为动词的词汇

similar_words.description: 「pocket」指衣物上的口袋或装入口袋的动作；「packet」指小包裹、封袋，如「a packet of letters」

definitions.example: The Pulitzer Prize-winning poem reflects on the trauma of post-war immigration.

poem.etymology: 源自希腊语「poēma」（创作的文学作品），核心词根为「poet-」（创作）

similar_words.description: 「poem」是可数名词，指具体的一首诗；「poetry」是不可数名词，指诗歌的总称或诗歌体裁

definitions.example: As a confessional poet, Sylvia Plath explored themes of mental illness and identity in her works.

poet.etymology: 源自希腊语「poētēs」（创作者），核心词根为「poet-」（创作），与「poem」同根

similar_words.description: 「poet」指创作诗歌的人；「poem」指诗人创作的具体诗作

definitions.example: The novelist’s lyrical prose is filled with the imagery and rhythm of traditional Chinese poetry.

poetry.etymology: 由「poet」（诗人）+ 名词后缀「-ry」构成，表「某一领域的作品总称」

similar_words.description: 「poetry」是不可数名词，指诗歌体裁或总称；「poem」是可数名词，指具体的一首诗

definitions.example: The key point of the cognitive science study is that multitasking impairs working memory.
Recent economic data points to a gradual recovery in the European manufacturing sector.

point.etymology: 源自拉丁语「punctum」（点），核心词根为「punct-」（刺，点），引申为「观点、指向」等义

similar_words.description: 「point」泛指指向、提出观点；「pinpoint」指精准确定、明确指出，如「pinpoint the cause of the error」

definitions.example: The environmental report warns that industrial waste contains a potent poison harmful to freshwater ecosystems.
False accusations on social media can poison the trust between community members.

poison.etymology: 源自古法语「poison」（药水，毒药），核心词根为「pos-」（喝），原指「饮用的药水」，后转义为「毒药」

similar_words.description: 「poison」是名词/动词，指毒药或毒害的动作；「poisonous」是形容词，指有毒的

definitions.example: Certain species of tropical frogs secrete a poisonous substance to defend themselves from predators.

poisonous.etymology: 由「poison」（毒药）+ 形容词后缀「-ous」（表「具有…性质的」）构成

similar_words.description: 「poisonous」侧重「含有毒素、可致中毒的」，适用范围广；「toxic」更正式，多用于学术、医学语境，指「有毒性的」

definitions.example: The hiker used a wooden stick to poke the underbrush for hidden snakes before proceeding.
Journalists often poke into the personal lives of public figures to find exclusive stories.

poke.etymology: 源自中古英语「poken」（戳，捅），无复杂词缀，属于动作类基础动词

similar_words.description: 「poke」指用硬物戳、捅或打探；「peek」指偷偷看、瞥，如「peek through the window」

definitions.example: Scientists are monitoring the shrinking of polar ice sheets to track climate change trends.

definitions.meaning: 截然对立的，完全相反的
definitions.example: The two political parties hold polar positions on the reform of the national healthcare system.

polar.etymology: 源自拉丁语「polus」（天极，地极），核心词根为「pol-」（极），引申为「对立的」（因南北极截然对立）

similar_words.description: 「polar」指极地的、对立的；「solar」指太阳的、太阳能的，如「solar energy」

definitions.example: The controversial reform bill has polarized the nation’s public opinion and legislative bodies.

polarize.etymology: 由「polar」（对立的）+ 动词后缀「-ize」（表「使…化」）构成，指「使处于两极对立的状态」

similar_words.description: 「polarize」是动词，指使两极分化的动作；「polar」是形容词，指对立的或极地的

definitions.example: Scientists have documented a significant rise in temperature around the North Pole.
The renowned pianist Chopin was a Pole born in the early 19th century.

Pole.etymology: 源自拉丁语polus，原指‘天极、轴’，大写时引申为‘波兰人’（与Poland同源）

similar_words.description: 二者形近，Pole指地极、波兰人，poll指民意调查、投票

definitions.example: The local police have launched a full investigation into the cyber fraud case.

police.etymology: 词根pol-（源自希腊语polis，意为‘城邦、治理’），后缀-ice（名词后缀，表机构），指‘治理城邦的执法机构’

similar_words.description: 二者形近，police指警方，policy指政策、保险单

definitions.example: The central government has introduced a new policy to boost rural revitalization.
The claimant must present a valid auto insurance policy to file for compensation.

policy.etymology: 词根pol-（源自希腊语polis，意为‘城邦、治理’），后缀-icy（名词后缀，表抽象事物），原指‘治理准则’，后引申出保险单义

similar_words.description: 二者形近，policy指政策、保险单，police指警方

definitions.example: Polish folk music has gained increasing popularity across Europe in recent years.
She has been learning Polish for two years to communicate with her relatives in Warsaw.

Polish.etymology: 源自国名Poland（波兰），形容词和名词形式表与波兰相关的属性

similar_words.description: 二者形近，Polish指波兰的、波兰语，pole指杆、地极

definitions.example: The debate over climate change has become a highly political issue in many countries.

political.etymology: 词根polit-（源自希腊语polis，意为‘城邦、治理’），后缀-ical（形容词后缀，表‘与...相关的’），指与政治活动相关的

similar_words.description: 二者同根，political指‘政治的’，politic为熟词僻义，表‘精明的、得当的’

definitions.meaning: 从政者，政治家；（贬义）政客
definitions.example: The young politician has proposed a series of bills to improve social welfare.

politician.etymology: 词根polit-（城邦、治理），后缀-ician（表‘从事...职业的人’），指从事政治活动的人

similar_words.description: 二者均指从政者，statesman为褒义的‘政治家’，politician可指中性的从政者或贬义的政客

definitions.example: Many young people are becoming more engaged in politics to advocate for social justice.

politics.etymology: 词根polit-（城邦、治理），后缀-ics（表‘学科、活动’），指政治活动或政治学学科

similar_words.description: 二者同根，politics指政治活动/学科，policy指具体的政策、方针

definitions.example: A recent national poll indicates that 68% of respondents support the carbon reduction plan.

poll.etymology: 源自古英语pol，原指‘头’，古代投票以人头计数，后引申为投票、民意调查

similar_words.description: 二者形近，poll指民意调查、投票，pole指地极、杆

definitions.example: Industrial wastewater has severely polluted the river basin and threatened aquatic ecosystems.

pollute.etymology: 词根pollut-（源自拉丁语polluere，意为‘弄脏、玷污’），动词后缀-e，指污染环境等

similar_words.description: 二者均表‘污染’，pollute多指自然环境的污染，contaminate可指食物、水源或精神层面的玷污

definitions.example: Fine particulate matter is a major component of air pollution in urban areas.

pollution.etymology: 词根pollut-（弄脏），后缀-ion（名词后缀，表行为或结果），指污染行为或污染物

similar_words.description: 二者均表‘污染’，pollution多指环境方面的污染，contamination可指各类污染或玷污

definitions.example: Wild ducks often gather on the small pond near the forest reserve to rest and forage.

pond.etymology: 源自古英语pond，无考研核心可拆分词缀

similar_words.description: 均表水域，pond多指小型自然或人工静水塘，pool可指泳池、资源汇集处

definitions.meaning: 仔细考虑，沉思（尤指权衡利弊）
definitions.example: Scholars often ponder the long-term impacts of climate change on global agricultural systems in academic papers.

ponder.etymology: 源自拉丁语pondus（重量），引申为权衡思考，无考研常见可拆分词缀

similar_words.description: 均表思考，ponder侧重反复权衡利弊，contemplate侧重长时间冥想或深思

definitions.example: The Olympic-sized swimming pool in the university is open to all students during summer vacation.
Researchers from three top universities pooled their clinical data to conduct a large-scale medical study.

pool.etymology: 源自古英语pōl，无考研核心可拆分词缀

similar_words.description: 均表水域，pool可指泳池、资源汇集处，pond多指小型静水塘

definitions.example: Pop culture elements such as short videos have reshaped young people's way of acquiring information.
A new perspective on ancient Greek philosophy popped up in the latest issue of the academic journal.

pop.etymology: 为单词popular的口语化缩写，无复杂词缀

similar_words.description: 均表流行的，popular为正式书面语，pop为口语化表达，多作定语

definitions.meaning: 教皇（罗马天主教会最高领袖）
definitions.example: The pope delivered a public speech on environmental protection at the United Nations General Assembly.

pope.etymology: 源自拉丁语papa（父亲），引申为天主教会最高领袖

similar_words.description: 均为宗教神职人员，pope是天主教会最高领袖，priest为普通基层神职人员

definitions.example: Online learning platforms have become increasingly popular among non-traditional students in recent years.
The new tax policy faced strong popular opposition from low-income groups.

popular.etymology: 源自拉丁语populus（人民），-ar为形容词后缀，意为受人民喜爱的、与民众相关的

similar_words.description: 均表流行的，popular为正式书面语，适用范围更广

definitions.example: The study examined the impact of habitat loss on the population of endangered amphibians in tropical rainforests.

population.etymology: 源自拉丁语populus（人民），-ation为名词后缀，指人口数量或生物种群

similar_words.description: 均与人相关，population指人口数量或生物种群，populace指普通民众、平民

definitions.example: The British Museum houses a large collection of ancient Chinese porcelain dating back to the Ming Dynasty.

porcelain.etymology: 源自意大利语porcellana（贝壳），因瓷器光泽酷似贝壳得名

similar_words.description: 均与陶瓷相关，porcelain特指硬质瓷器，ceramic为陶瓷制品的统称

definitions.meaning: 门廊，入口走廊（房屋入口处的附属结构）
definitions.example: She would sit on the front porch every evening, watching the sunset and listening to birds.

porch.etymology: 源自拉丁语porticus（带柱廊的走廊）

similar_words.description: 均表走廊，porch多指房屋入口的小型门廊，veranda多指环绕房屋的大型开放式走廊

definitions.example: The port of Shanghai is one of the busiest container ports in the world, handling millions of tons of cargo annually.
The remote desktop software requires a specific network port to establish a stable connection with the host computer.

port.etymology: 源自拉丁语portus（港口），无考研核心可拆分词缀

similar_words.description: 均表港口，port多指商用客运货运港口，harbor多指避风港或军用港口

definitions.example: Many researchers now use portable sensors to collect field data for ecological studies.

portable.etymology: 词根port（携带）加形容词后缀-able（可…的）构成，表‘可携带的’

similar_words.description: 「portable」指便携的、手提式的；「potable」指可饮用的，二者拼写仅差一个字母

definitions.example: The porter helped the elderly traveler carry his heavy luggage to the train compartment.

porter.etymology: 词根port（携带）加名词后缀-er（表人）构成，指‘携带物品的人’

similar_words.description: 「porter」多指受雇于公共场所的行李搬运工；「carrier」泛指运输者、承运商

definitions.example: Each participant in the nutrition study received a fixed portion of whole grains daily.

portion.etymology: 词根port（分配）加名词后缀-ion（表事物）构成，指‘分配出的部分’

similar_words.description: 「portion」指具体的一份、一部分；「proportion」指比例、比率

definitions.example: The museum recently acquired a rare portrait of a 19th-century British novelist.

portrait.etymology: 词根port（呈现、描绘）加名词后缀-rait构成，指‘呈现的人物形象’

similar_words.description: 「portrait」为名词，指肖像、描绘；「portray」为动词，指描绘、扮演

definitions.example: The documentary portrays the daily struggles of rural teachers in remote mountain areas.

portray.etymology: 词根port（呈现、描绘）加动词后缀-ray构成，指‘呈现形象的动作’

similar_words.description: 「portray」可指文字/艺术描绘或演员扮演；「depict」多指用图画/文字具体描绘

definitions.meaning: 造成（威胁、困难等）；摆姿势
definitions.example: Rising sea levels pose a severe threat to coastal communities worldwide.

pose.etymology: 词根pos（放置）加动词后缀-e构成，表‘放置姿态/威胁的动作’

similar_words.description: 「pose」作动词指摆姿势、造成；「posture」作名词指姿势、态度，作动词指故作姿态

definitions.example: She applied for a senior research position at the national academy of sciences.

position.etymology: 词根pos（放置）加名词后缀-ition（表状态/位置）构成，指‘放置的地方/身份/观点’

similar_words.description: 「position」多指正式的、有特定职责的职位；「post」可指职位或岗位，也可指邮政

definitions.example: Regular exercise has a positive effect on both physical and mental health of students.

positive.etymology: 词根pos（放置）加形容词后缀-itive（表性质）构成，指‘放置在正面的’

similar_words.description: 「positive」指积极的、肯定的；「passive」指被动的、消极的，二者拼写仅差首字母

definitions.example: Many artificial intelligence systems now possess the ability to recognize human emotions.

possess.etymology: 词根pos（持有）加动词后缀-sess（表状态）构成，指‘持有物品的动作’

similar_words.description: 「possess」为动词，指拥有、具有；「possessive」为形容词，指占有欲强的、所有格的

definitions.example: The court ordered the return of the stolen possessions to their rightful owner.

possession.etymology: 动词possess（拥有）加名词后缀-ion（表状态/事物）构成，指‘拥有的状态或事物’

similar_words.description: 「possession」指拥有、财产；「obsession」指痴迷、困扰，二者拼写前半部分相似

definitions.example: Researchers are investigating the possibility that daily moderate exercise can reduce the risk of age-related cognitive decline.

possibility.etymology: 由形容词「possible」（可能的）加名词后缀「-ibility」构成，表「可能的性质/状态」

similar_words.description: 「possibility」侧重客观存在的可能性，「probability」侧重数学或统计意义上的概率

definitions.example: Rising global temperatures could possibly disrupt the seasonal migration patterns of many avian species.

possibly.etymology: 由形容词「possible」（可能的）加副词后缀「-ly」构成

similar_words.description: 「possibly」语气较弱，表「或许」；「probably」语气较强，表「很可能」

definitions.example: Many cross-border e-commerce platforms offer free postage for orders exceeding a specified minimum value.

postage.etymology: 由词根「post」（邮政）加名词后缀「-age」（表费用）构成

similar_words.description: 「postage」指邮政服务的费用，「posture」指身体姿势或立场态度

definitions.example: Vintage postcards depicting 19th-century urban landscapes are prized collectibles among history enthusiasts.

postcard.etymology: 由词根「post」（邮政）加名词「card」（卡片）构成，指用于邮寄的卡片

similar_words.description: 「postcard」是用于邮寄的通信卡片，「poster」是用于宣传的招贴画

definitions.example: The university library put up posters to promote its upcoming academic workshop on digital humanities.

poster.etymology: 由词根「post」（张贴）加名词后缀「-er」（表物）构成，指张贴出来的宣传品

similar_words.description: 「poster」是用于宣传的招贴画，「postcard」是用于邮寄的通信卡片

definitions.example: Many postmen have to work outdoors in extreme weather to ensure timely delivery of mails and packages.

postman.etymology: 由词根「post」（邮政）加名词「man」（人）构成，指从事邮政投递的人员

similar_words.description: 「postman」负责邮政投递，「porter」负责搬运行李或货物

definitions.example: Due to the unforeseen public health emergency, the international academic conference was postponed until further notice.

postpone.etymology: 由前缀「post-」（后）加词根「pone」（放置，来自拉丁语ponere）构成，表「将…放在后面」即推迟

similar_words.description: 「postpone」侧重正式场合（如会议、活动）的主动延期，「delay」侧重意外或人为导致的被动延误

definitions.example: Maintaining an upright sitting posture can effectively alleviate chronic back pain among office workers.
The government’s rigid posture on trade tariffs has raised concerns among global economic analysts.

posture.etymology: 由词根「post」（放置）加名词后缀「-ure」（表状态）构成，指身体放置的状态（姿势），引申为立场姿态

similar_words.description: 「posture」指身体姿势或立场态度，「postage」指邮政服务的费用

definitions.example: Botanists cultivated the rare orchid species in sterilized clay pots under controlled greenhouse conditions.

pot.etymology: 源自古英语「pott」，指用于盛装或种植的容器

similar_words.description: 「pot」指有把手的深型容器（如花盆、汤锅），「pan」指浅型烹饪容器（如煎锅）

definitions.example: Renewable energy sources such as wind and solar are potential alternatives to fossil fuels.
High-quality early childhood education can help children unlock their full intellectual potential.

potential.etymology: 由词根「potent」（有力的）加形容词后缀「-ial」构成，引申为「尚未发挥力量的（潜在的）」，名词化表「潜力」

similar_words.description: 「potential」侧重尚未实现的潜力或潜在属性，「possible」侧重客观上可能发生的情况

definitions.example: In some rural areas, raising poultry has become a stable source of income for low-income families.

poultry.etymology: 源自拉丁语pullus（幼禽），为表家禽类的集合名词

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，poultry指家禽/禽肉，poetry指诗歌

definitions.example: The exchange rate between the US dollar and the British pound fluctuates slightly this month.
A mature turkey can weigh up to 20 pounds during the holiday season.

pound.etymology: 源自拉丁语pondus（重量），最初指重量单位，后衍生为英国货币单位

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，pound指英镑/磅，pond指池塘

definitions.example: She poured a cup of hot tea for the exhausted researcher who had been working all night.
The rain poured down for three consecutive days, delaying the outdoor scientific experiment.

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，pour指倾倒/倾盆而下，pore指仔细研读/毛孔

definitions.example: The government has launched a series of policies to alleviate poverty in remote mountainous areas.
His speech was criticized for its poverty of original ideas and concrete evidence.

poverty.etymology: 源自拉丁语paupertas（贫穷），词根pau-表「少的、穷的」

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，poverty指贫穷/贫乏，property指财产/属性

definitions.example: The chemist carefully measured the white powder before adding it to the reaction vessel.

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，powder指粉末，power指力量/权力/电力

definitions.example: The new policy gives local governments more power to regulate environmental protection in their jurisdictions.
The wind farm generates enough power to supply electricity to 50,000 households.

power.etymology: 源自拉丁语potere（能够），词根pot-表「有能力的」

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，power指力量/权力/电力，powder指粉末

definitions.meaning: 强大的；强有力的；有权势的
definitions.example: The powerful evidence presented by the prosecutor convinced the jury of the defendant's guilt.

powerful.etymology: 由名词power（力量）加形容词后缀-ful（充满…的）构成

similar_words.description: 同根反义，powerful指强大的，powerless指无力的

definitions.example: The course focuses on practical skills that students can apply directly to their future careers.
We need to come up with a practical solution to the problem of campus waste disposal.

practical.etymology: 源自拉丁语practicus（实践的），词根pract-表「实践」

similar_words.description: 近义易混，practical侧重「实用的、实际的」，practicable侧重「（计划等）可实施的」

definitions.example: Practically, the new regulation has not brought about any significant changes to the daily operations of small businesses.
The old library is practically empty during the summer vacation.

practically.etymology: 由形容词practical（实际的）加副词后缀-ly（…地）构成

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，practically指实际上/几乎，pragmatically指务实着地

definitions.example: To improve English writing skills, students need to practise regularly and seek feedback from teachers.
She has been practising law in Shanghai for over 15 years.

practise.etymology: 源自拉丁语practicare（实践），词根pract-表「实践」，为英式拼写

similar_words.description: 拼写变体，practise为英式拼写（主要作动词），practice为美式拼写（可作名词/动词）

definitions.meaning: （律师、医生等的）执业者，从业者
definitions.example: Many medical practitioners emphasize the importance of regular health check-ups for middle-aged people.

practitioner.etymology: 来自「practise」（v.执业，实践）+名词后缀-ion+表人的后缀-er，指从事某一专业的执业人员

similar_words.description: 二者同根，「practitioner」为名词表执业者，「practical」为形容词表实际的、实用的，易混

definitions.example: Many critics praise the author’s vivid description of rural life in his latest novel.
The new environmental policy received widespread praise from non-profit organizations.

praise.etymology: 来自古法语preisier，原指评价、看重，后引申为赞扬

similar_words.description: 二者形近，「praise」表赞扬，「pray」表祈祷、祈求，易拼混

definitions.example: Many local residents pray for the safety of rescue workers during the flood disaster.

pray.etymology: 来自拉丁语precare，原指恳求、哀求，后引申为宗教意义上的祈祷

similar_words.description: 二者形近，「pray」表祈祷，「praise」表赞扬，易拼混

definitions.example: She spent ten minutes in silent prayer before taking the postgraduate entrance examination.

prayer.etymology: 由「pray」（v.祈祷）加名词后缀-er构成，表祈祷的动作或祷文内容

similar_words.description: 二者同根，「prayer」为「pray」的名词形式，表祈祷/祷文，易混淆词性

definitions.meaning: 布道，宣讲；竭力鼓吹，宣扬
definitions.example: The professor preached the importance of interdisciplinary research to the graduate students.

preach.etymology: 来自拉丁语praedicare，原指宣布、公开说明，后引申为宗教布道或宣扬观点

similar_words.description: 二者形近，「preach」表布道/鼓吹，「peach」表桃子，易拼混

definitions.example: Taking regular backups of important files is a basic precaution against data loss.

precaution.etymology: 由前缀「pre-（提前）」+「caution（n.小心）」构成，指提前采取的防备措施

similar_words.description: 二者同根，「precaution」表提前的预防措施，「caution」表一般的小心、谨慎，易混

definitions.meaning: 在…之前发生（或出现），先于
definitions.example: The economic recession preceded the outbreak of the global public health crisis by two years.

precede.etymology: 由前缀「pre-（在前）」+词根「ced（走）」+后缀-e构成，指走在前面、先于发生

similar_words.description: 二者同含词根「ced（走）」，「precede」表在…之前，「proceed」表继续进行，易混

definitions.meaning: 先例，判例（尤指法律或社会领域）
definitions.example: The supreme court’s ruling set a new precedent for cases involving digital privacy protection.

precedent.etymology: 由「precede」（v.先于）加名词后缀-ent构成，指先于当前事件的先例

similar_words.description: 二者同根，「precedent」表先例/判例，「precedence」表优先权，易混

definitions.example: The data from the preceding three months shows a steady growth in domestic consumption.

preceding.etymology: 由「precede」（v.先于）的现在分词转化而来，作形容词表时间或顺序上在前的

similar_words.description: 二者均表先前的，「preceding」强调紧接在前的，「previous」可指任意之前的，易混

definitions.example: The ancient bronze ware is regarded as a precious cultural relic by the national museum.

precious.etymology: 来自拉丁语pretium（价值），原指有高价值的，后引申为珍贵的

similar_words.description: 二者形近，「precious」表珍贵的，「precise」表精确的，易拼混

definitions.example: The experiment requires precise measurements of temperature and pressure to ensure reliable results.

precise.etymology: 由前缀pre-（预先）和词根cis（切割）构成，指预先切割得整齐准确，引申为精确的

similar_words.description: 「precise」指精确的，「precious」指珍贵的，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The precision of the satellite's navigation system enables it to locate targets within a one-meter range.

precision.etymology: 由形容词precise（精确的）加名词后缀-ion构成，指精确的性质或状态

similar_words.description: 「precision」侧重数据、操作的精密程度，「accuracy」侧重结果与事实的符合度

definitions.example: The lack of sufficient funding precludes the research team from launching a large-scale field study.

preclude.etymology: 由前缀pre-（预先）和词根clud（关闭）构成，指预先关闭通道或可能性，引申为阻止

similar_words.description: 「preclude」侧重预先阻止可能性，「exclude」侧重把人或物排除在外，二者共享词根clud（关闭）

definitions.example: The new university library is much larger than its predecessor built in the 1980s.

predecessor.etymology: 由前缀pre-（在前）、词根de-（完全）、cess（行走）和名词后缀-or构成，指走在前面的人或事物

similar_words.description: 「predecessor」指前任或事物的前身，「successor」指继任者或事物的后继者，二者拼写相近、含义相反

definitions.example: Economists predict that inflation will remain at a stable level over the next six months.

predict.etymology: 由前缀pre-（预先）和词根dict（说）构成，指预先说出未来的情况

similar_words.description: 「predict」为通用词，可指各类预测；「forecast」侧重气象、经济等领域的官方预测

definitions.example: The predominant reason for the species' decline is the loss of its natural habitat.

predominant.etymology: 由前缀pre-（超过）和形容词dominant（统治的）构成，指超过其他而占统治地位的

similar_words.description: 「predominant」侧重在众多事物中占主导，「dominant」侧重本身具有支配性，二者共享词根domin（统治）

definitions.example: The author explains her research motivation in the preface to the academic monograph.

preface.etymology: 由前缀pre-（在前）和词根face（面）构成，指书籍或文章前面的部分

similar_words.description: 「preface」多为作者本人撰写的学术或书籍前言，「foreword」多为他人撰写的推荐序

definitions.example: Many graduate students prefer to conduct independent research rather than participate in group projects.

prefer.etymology: 由前缀pre-（在前）和词根fer（带来）构成，指把某物放在前面选择，引申为更喜欢

similar_words.description: 「prefer」侧重主观选择的偏好，「favor」侧重出于好感的偏爱

definitions.example: For environmental protection, using public transport is preferable to driving private cars.

preferable.etymology: 由动词prefer（更喜欢）加形容词后缀-able构成，指更能被偏好的，即更可取的

similar_words.description: 「preferable」侧重主观判断的更合适，「superior」侧重客观质量、等级的更高

definitions.example: The survey reveals a clear preference for online learning among young college students.

preference.etymology: 由动词prefer（更喜欢）加名词后缀-ence构成，指偏爱的状态或事物

similar_words.description: 「preference」侧重个人喜好，「priority」侧重重要性的先后顺序

definitions.example: Many urban young women delay getting pregnant to pursue postgraduate education and career advancement.

definitions.meaning: 意味深长的（如停顿、话语）
definitions.example: There was a pregnant pause after the professor raised the 'controversial ethical question'.

pregnant.etymology: pre-（在前）+ 词根gn（生育，源自拉丁语gen-）+ -ant（形容词后缀），原指「生育前的状态」，引申为意味深长的

similar_words.description: expectant指怀孕时为委婉表述，还可表期待的；pregnant为直接表述怀孕，还可表意味深长的

definitions.example: Racial prejudice remains a persistent social problem documented in numerous postgraduate sociology studies.
Biased media coverage often prejudices the public against marginalized groups.

prejudice.etymology: pre-（预先）+ 词根jud（判断，源自拉丁语iudic-）+ -ice（名词后缀），指「预先做出的不合理判断」

similar_words.description: prejudice指带有贬义的偏见；prejudgement指中性的预先判断（基于有限信息）

definitions.example: The research team conducted a preliminary survey to identify key variables before the formal study.
All preliminaries for the academic conference have been completed two weeks in advance.

preliminary.etymology: pre-（在前）+ 词根limin（门槛，源自拉丁语limen）+ -ary（形容词后缀），指「在正式门槛前的，尚未进入核心阶段的」

similar_words.description: tentative侧重试探性、不确定的；preliminary侧重为正式环节做准备的初步的

definitions.example: Tsinghua University is widely recognized as the premier institution for engineering research in China.
The premier announced a new policy to support postgraduate innovation in the annual address.

premier.etymology: pre-（在前）+ 词根mier（源自拉丁语primus，第一），指「排在最前面的」

similar_words.description: premier指首要的/总理；premise指前提/场所，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The argument that 'technology boosts education equity' is based on the premise of universal internet access.
Postgraduate students must swipe their ID cards before entering the laboratory premises.

premise.etymology: pre-（在前）+ 词根mis（放置，源自拉丁语mittere）+ -e，指「预先放置的论证基础」，引申为场所

similar_words.description: premise指前提/场所；promise指承诺/诺言，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: Investors are willing to pay a premium for stocks of companies with strong ESG performance.
The university library offers premium research databases exclusively for postgraduate students.

premium.etymology: pre-（在前）+ 词根mium（源自拉丁语emere，购买），指「预先支付的额外费用」，引申为高端的

similar_words.description: premium指溢价/高端的；premier指首要的/总理，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: Her preoccupation with linguistic research left her with little time for social activities during postgraduate study.
Climate change has become a major preoccupation for global academic researchers.

preoccupation.etymology: pre-（在前）+ 词根occup（占据，源自拉丁语occupare）+ -ation（名词后缀），指「精神预先被占据的状态」

similar_words.description: preoccupation指精神上的专注；occupation指职业/实际占据的空间

definitions.meaning: 准备，预备；（常复数）准备工作
definitions.example: Adequate preparation for the postgraduate entrance exam significantly increases admission chances.

preparation.etymology: pre-（在前）+ 词根par（准备，源自拉丁语parare）+ -ation（名词后缀），指「预先做的准备工作」

similar_words.description: preparation指为某事做的实际准备；preoccupation指精神上的专注，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: Professors require postgraduate students to prepare a detailed outline before writing research papers.

prepare.etymology: pre-（在前）+ 词根par（准备，源自拉丁语parare），指「预先做好安排」

similar_words.description: prepare指准备；preclude指阻止、排除，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: Mastering preposition usage is essential for writing accurate academic English essays.

preposition.etymology: pre-（在前）+ 词根posit（放置，源自拉丁语ponere）+ -ion（名词后缀），指「放在名词/代词前的语法词」

similar_words.description: preposition指语法中的介词；proposition指提议/命题，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: Doctors are required to prescribe generic drugs to reduce patients' medical costs.
The regulation prescribes that all vehicles must undergo annual safety inspections.

prescribe.etymology: 前缀pre-（在前，预先）+ 词根scrib（写）+ 动词后缀-e，本义为「预先写下」，引申为开处方、规定

similar_words.description: 「proscribe」意为「禁止，放逐」，与「prescribe」拼写仅字母顺序（s和r）不同，易混淆

definitions.example: The pharmacist cannot dispense the controlled medicine without a valid doctor's prescription.
The report offers a clear prescription for improving urban air quality.

prescription.etymology: 前缀pre-（在前，预先）+ 词根script（写）+ 名词后缀-ion，指「预先写下的内容」，即处方、规定

similar_words.description: 「proscription」意为「禁止，放逐（的行为）」，与「prescription」拼写接近，易混淆

definitions.example: His unexpected presence at the academic seminar surprised all the participants.
The presence of artificial intelligence has transformed many traditional industries.

presence.etymology: 前缀pre-（在前）+ 词根sent（存在）+ 名词后缀-ce，指「在前方存在的状态」，即出席、存在

similar_words.description: 「absence」意为「缺席，不存在」，是「presence」的反义词，语义对立易混淆

definitions.example: Presently, over 20 research institutions are collaborating on this climate change project.
The host will announce the award winners presently.

presently.etymology: 形容词present（现在的）+ 副词后缀-ly，表时间状态

similar_words.description: 「currently」仅表「目前，现在」，「presently」还可表「不久，很快」，语义范围更广

definitions.example: Local authorities have launched campaigns to preserve the traditional folk culture of the region.
The coastal wetland is a protected preserve for migratory birds.

preserve.etymology: 前缀pre-（在前，预先）+ 词根serv（保持，保存）+ 动词后缀-e，本义为「预先保持」，引申为保护、保存

similar_words.description: 「conserve」侧重「节约使用（资源）」，「preserve」侧重「维持原状、防止破坏」，语义有细微差异

definitions.example: The Nobel Prize laureate will preside over the closing ceremony of the international academic forum.

preside.etymology: 前缀pre-（在前）+ 词根sid（坐）+ 动词后缀-e，指「坐在前面（主持）」，即主持、主管

similar_words.description: 「reside」意为「居住；存在于」，与「preside」拼写仅首字母后字母（p和r）不同，易混淆

definitions.meaning: 总统；（大学）校长；（机构）会长/董事长
definitions.example: The president of Peking University emphasized the importance of interdisciplinary research in his speech.

president.etymology: 前缀pre-（在前）+ 词根sid（坐）+ 名词后缀-ent（表人），指「坐在前面的人」，即领导者、校长等

similar_words.description: 「principal」多指中小学或学院校长，「president」多指大学校长、国家总统或企业董事长

definitions.example: The investors pressed the startup team to present a detailed financial plan within a week.
The controversial policy drew widespread criticism from the press and the public.

press.etymology: 来自拉丁语pressare（反复按压），词根为press（压），引申为按压、催促、新闻界（因印刷需按压）

similar_words.description: 「compress」意为「压缩，压紧」，与「press」同根（表「压」）但语义更具体

definitions.meaning: 压力；压强；（心理/社会）压迫
definitions.example: Under great academic pressure, many postgraduate students choose to seek psychological counseling.

pressure.etymology: 词根press（压）+ 名词后缀-ure，指「按压的状态或力量」，即压力、压强

similar_words.description: 「stress」多指心理、精神层面的压力，「pressure」可指物理、社会或心理压力，范围更广

definitions.example: Publishing in the top journal greatly enhanced the young scholar's academic prestige worldwide.

prestige.etymology: 来自拉丁语praestigium（幻象），后引申为「令人敬畏的声望」，可拆解为前缀pre-（在前）+ 词根stig（标记），指「在前方的卓越标记」

similar_words.description: 「prestigious」是「prestige」的形容词形式，表「有声望的」，易混淆词性

definitions.example: Many top graduates aspire to secure positions in prestigious academic institutions or multinational corporations.

prestigious.etymology: 由前缀pre-（在前）、词根stige（=status，地位）和形容词后缀-ious构成，本义为「站在前列、拥有较高地位的」，引申为有威望的

similar_words.description: 「prestigious」是形容词，表「有威望的」；「prestige」是同根名词，表抽象的「威望」

definitions.example: Presumably, the sudden decline in global biodiversity was accelerated by human activities such as deforestation.

presumably.etymology: 由前缀pre-（预先）、词根sum（拿，认为）和副词后缀-ably构成，本义为「预先认定地」，引申为据推测

similar_words.description: 「presumably」表推测，「presumptuously」表「放肆地」，词根相同但语义差异极大

definitions.example: We cannot presume that all ancient civilizations followed the same social hierarchy without archaeological evidence.
I presume to suggest that you revise the methodology section of your thesis for greater rigor.

presume.etymology: 由前缀pre-（预先）和词根sume（拿，取）构成，本义为「预先拿定观点」，引申为假定；虚化后表冒昧行事

similar_words.description: 「presume」侧重基于经验的合理推测，「assume」侧重无证据的主观假设

definitions.example: Some politicians pretend to prioritize public welfare to gain voter support during electoral campaigns.

pretend.etymology: 由前缀pre-（向前）和词根tend（伸展）构成，原义为「向前伸展伪装的姿态」，引申为假装

similar_words.description: 「pretend」表假装，「protest」表抗议，词形相近但语义完全不同

definitions.example: The firm used a fake budget crisis as a pretext to lay off dozens of senior employees.

pretext.etymology: 由前缀pre-（预先）和词根text（编织）构成，本义为「预先编织的理由」，引申为借口

similar_words.description: 「pretext」表借口，「context」表上下文，均含词根「text」但语义无关

definitions.example: The pretty landscape illustrations in the environmental report enhance its readability for general readers.
The implementation of the new education policy has been pretty slow in underdeveloped rural areas.

pretty.etymology: 源自古英语prætty（机灵的，狡猾的），后引申为「漂亮的」；虚化后作副词表「相当」（考研常考熟词僻义）

similar_words.description: 「pretty」表漂亮或相当，「petty」表琐碎的、小气的，词形相近但语义差异大

definitions.example: A mindset of lifelong learning prevails among young professionals in the rapidly changing digital age.
After months of negotiation, the carbon emission reduction proposal finally prevailed over opposing views.

prevail.etymology: 由前缀pre-（在前）和词根vail（=val，力量）构成，本义为「力量在前、占上风」，引申为流行或获胜

similar_words.description: 「prevail」侧重在竞争中占上风，「triumph」侧重获得辉煌的决定性胜利

definitions.example: Mental health issues are increasingly prevalent among college students due to academic pressure.

prevalent.etymology: 由前缀pre-（在前）、词根val（力量）和形容词后缀-ent构成，本义为「力量广泛存在的」，引申为流行的

similar_words.description: 「prevalent」是形容词表状态，「prevail」是同根动词表动作（流行、获胜）

definitions.example: Strict traffic regulations and awareness campaigns can help prevent drunk driving accidents.

prevent.etymology: 由前缀pre-（在前）和词根vent（来）构成，本义为「在到来之前挡住」，引申为预防、阻止

similar_words.description: 「prevent」侧重提前阻止某事发生，「hinder」侧重阻碍正在进行的活动

definitions.example: The scholar’s previous research on ancient Chinese literature provided crucial insights for his current study.

previous.etymology: 由前缀pre-（在前）、词根vi（路）和形容词后缀-ous构成，本义为「走在前面的」，引申为先前的

similar_words.description: 「previous」表先前的，「precious」表珍贵的，词形相近但语义完全不同

definitions.example: In the Arctic food chain, lemmings are a primary prey for Arctic foxes.
The anxiety of unemployment began to prey on his mental health.

prey.etymology: 源自拉丁语praeda（战利品、猎物），无可拆分的常见词缀，属于考研核心基础词汇

similar_words.description: 「prey」表猎物/捕食，「pray」表祈祷，拼写仅差字母e的位置，极易混淆

definitions.example: The gardener wore gloves to avoid being pricked by the rose thorns.
He felt a sharp prick on his ankle when walking through the grass.

prick.etymology: 源自古英语prician（刺），属于拟声类基础动词

similar_words.description: 「prick」为动词/名词，表刺/扎；「prickly」为形容词，表多刺的、易动怒的，是同根衍生词

definitions.example: His pride prevented him from accepting financial help from others.
A pride of lions was resting under the shade of an acacia tree on the savanna.

pride.etymology: 源自古英语pryde，与古法语prud（勇敢的）相关，无明显词缀拆分

similar_words.description: 「pride」为名词，表骄傲/自尊心；「proud」为形容词，表骄傲的，是同根衍生词

definitions.example: The local priest held a memorial service for the victims of the natural disaster.

priest.etymology: 源自拉丁语presbyter（长者、牧师），由pre-（在前）+byter（年长者）构成，原指教会中的年长者

similar_words.description: 「priest」指男性基督教神职人员；「priestess」指女性神职人员，是性别衍生词

definitions.example: The primary cause of global climate change is excessive greenhouse gas emissions.
Primary education is a fundamental right for children in most countries.

primary.etymology: 源自拉丁语primarius（第一的、主要的），由prim-（第一、首要）+ -ary（形容词后缀）构成

similar_words.description: 「primary」表主要的、初级的；「prime」表首要的、最好的，同根（prim-）且语义相近易混淆

definitions.meaning: 首要的，主要的；最好的，第一流的
definitions.example: The prime reason for the company's rapid growth is its technological innovation.
She was at the prime of her acting career when she won the Academy Award.

prime.etymology: 源自拉丁语primus（第一的），同根于primary，无额外词缀

similar_words.description: 「prime」表首要的、全盛期；「primary」表主要的、初级的，同根（prim-）易混淆使用场景

definitions.example: Archaeologists discovered primitive stone tools used by early humans in the cave.

primitive.etymology: 源自拉丁语primitivus（最初的），由prim-（第一、最初）+ -itive（形容词后缀）构成

similar_words.description: 「primitive」表原始的、简陋的；「primary」表主要的、初级的，同根（prim-）语义不同易混淆

definitions.example: The young prince received strict education in politics and military affairs.

prince.etymology: 源自拉丁语princeps（第一的、首领），由prim-（第一）+ capere（拿）构成，原指首领，后引申为王室成员

similar_words.description: 「prince」指男性王室成员（王子）；「princess」指女性王室成员（公主），是性别衍生词

definitions.example: The fairy tale depicts a brave princess who saved her kingdom from a dragon.

princess.etymology: 源自拉丁语princeps的阴性形式，由prim-（第一）+ -cess（阴性后缀，表女性）构成

similar_words.description: 「princess」指女性王室成员（公主/王妃）；「prince」指男性王室成员（王子），是性别衍生词

definitions.example: The principal challenge for the new administration is to curb inflation.
When repaying a loan, borrowers must pay back both the principal and the interest.

principal.etymology: 源自拉丁语principalis（主要的），由prim-（第一）+ -al（形容词后缀）构成，作名词时引申为核心人物（校长）或核心资金（本金）

similar_words.description: 「principal」作形容词表主要的，作名词表校长/本金；「principle」作名词表原则、原理，拼写仅差最后一个字母，是考研最易混淆的形近词之一

definitions.example: Adhering to scientific principles is essential for conducting valid and replicable academic research.

principle.etymology: 词根为「prin-」（首要）+「cip-」（拿）+名词后缀「-le」，源自拉丁语principium，本义为「首要的事物」，引申为「原则；原理」

similar_words.description: 「principal」作形容词表「主要的」，作名词表「校长；本金」；「principle」仅作名词，表「原则；原理」

definitions.example: Many peer-reviewed academic journals still print hard copies for subscribers who prefer physical reading materials.
Forensic investigators found partial prints of the suspect at the burglary crime scene.

print.etymology: 源自古法语preindre（压印），词根「print-」表「压出痕迹」，原指「压印的结果」，后引申为「印刷品；痕迹」

similar_words.description: 「imprint」作名词表「印记；痕迹」，强调「主动压印的结果」；「print」作名词可表「印刷品」或广义的「痕迹」

definitions.example: Prior to submitting their graduation theses, undergraduate students must pass a rigorous academic integrity check.

prior.etymology: 源自拉丁语prior（更早的），词根「pri-」（在前）+比较级后缀「-or」，表「时间或顺序上更早的」，引申为「优先的」

similar_words.description: 「prior」常与介词「to」搭配；「previous」多作前置定语，不与「to」搭配

definitions.example: Enhancing the employability of graduates has been listed as a top priority by the Ministry of Education.

priority.etymology: 由形容词「prior」（先前的）+名词后缀「-ity」构成，表「优先的状态或事物」

similar_words.description: 「prior」是形容词，表「先前的；优先的」；「priority」是名词，表「优先事项；优先权」

definitions.example: The documentary examines the long-term psychological effects of solitary confinement in maximum-security prisons.
Many avant-garde artists view rigid artistic conventions as a prison for creative expression.

prison.etymology: 源自拉丁语prehensio（抓住），原指「抓住并关押人的地方」，引申为「监狱」，后比喻「束缚人的事物」

similar_words.description: 「prison」指长期关押已判刑犯人的大型监狱；「jail」指短期关押嫌疑人的小型看守所

definitions.example: The prison reform program aims to reduce recidivism rates by providing vocational training for prisoners.
International humanitarian organizations call for the immediate release of all prisoners of war.

prisoner.etymology: 由名词「prison」（监狱）+表人的后缀「-er」构成，指「被关押在监狱中的人」

similar_words.description: 「prisoner」特指被判刑的犯人；「inmate」可指监狱、精神病院等机构的所有居住者，范围更广

definitions.example: The widespread use of facial recognition technology has sparked intense debates over personal privacy protection.

privacy.etymology: 由拉丁语「privatus」（私人的）+名词后缀「-acy」构成，表「私人的状态或领域」

similar_words.description: 「private」是形容词，表「私人的；私有的」；「privacy」是名词，表「隐私；私密」

definitions.example: Private research institutions often collaborate with public universities to translate academic findings into practical applications.
The private was awarded a commendation for his courage during the military training exercise.

private.etymology: 源自拉丁语「privatus」（离开公共的），词根「priv-」（分开；私人）+形容词后缀「-ate」，表「非公共的」

similar_words.description: 「private」强调「非公共的、专属的」；「personal」强调「与个人相关的、涉及自身的」

definitions.example: Equal access to quality higher education should not be a privilege reserved for affluent families.

privilege.etymology: 源自拉丁语「privilegium」（为个人制定的法律），词根「priv-」（私人）+「leg-」（法律）+名词后缀「-ium」，原指「私人享有的法律特权」

similar_words.description: 「privilege」指少数人享有的特殊优待；「right」指全体公民依法享有的基本权利

definitions.example: The Nobel Prize in Literature recognizes authors who make outstanding contributions to world literary heritage.
Many leading scholars prize interdisciplinary collaboration over isolated academic research.

prize.etymology: 源自古法语「pris」（价值），原指「有价值的事物」，引申为「奖品」，后作动词表「珍视」

similar_words.description: 「prize」多指竞赛或荣誉性的奖品；「award」可指官方、机构颁发的各类奖励，范围更广

definitions.example: The probability of extreme weather events has increased significantly due to global climate change.

probability.etymology: 词根「prob」（证明、测试）+ 名词后缀「-ability」（表性质、状态），可被验证的程度→可能性、概率

similar_words.description: 均表可能性，probability侧重统计或科学层面的概率，possibility泛指客观存在的可能

definitions.example: It is highly probable that the research findings will be published in a top scientific journal.

probable.etymology: 词根「prob」（证明）+ 形容词后缀「-able」（可…的），可被证明合理的→很可能的

similar_words.description: 均表可能，probable的可能性远高于possible，possible仅指存在可能性

definitions.example: Scientists probe the neural mechanisms underlying human memory formation.

probe.etymology: 词根「prob」（测试、探查），引申为探查、探究行为或工具

similar_words.description: 同根于「prob」，probe指探查、探究，prove指证明、证实

definitions.example: The experimental procedure must be strictly followed to ensure the validity of research results.

procedure.etymology: 前缀「pro-」（向前）+ 词根「ced」（走）+ 名词后缀「-ure」（表行为、结果），向前推进的流程→程序、步骤

similar_words.description: 均表流程，procedure侧重具体的操作步骤，process侧重整体的发展过程

definitions.example: After obtaining official approval, the project team proceeded with the construction work.

proceed.etymology: 前缀「pro-」（向前）+ 词根「ceed」（走），向前走→继续进行、前进

similar_words.description: 形近易混，precede意为“在…之前发生/出现”，proceed意为“继续进行、前进”

definitions.example: The proceeding of the international climate conference was broadcast live globally.
Legal proceedings were launched against the enterprise for violating environmental regulations.

proceeding.etymology: 动词「proceed」（继续进行）+ 名词后缀「-ing」（表行为、状态），进行中的行为→进程；引申为法律层面的诉讼程序

similar_words.description: 均与流程相关，proceeding侧重正在进行的行动或诉讼，procedure侧重既定的操作步骤

definitions.example: Acquiring a second language is a gradual process that demands persistent practice.

process.etymology: 前缀「pro-」（向前）+ 词根「cess」（走），向前推进的历程→过程；引申为加工处理的动作

similar_words.description: 均表流程，process侧重整体的发展过程，procedure侧重具体的操作步骤

definitions.example: A solemn procession of veterans marched through the city center on National Memorial Day.

procession.etymology: 前缀「pro-」（向前）+ 词根「cess」（走）+ 名词后缀「-ion」（表行为、群体），向前行进的群体→行列、队伍

similar_words.description: 同根于「cess」（走），procession指具体的行列队伍，process指抽象或具体的过程

definitions.example: The head of state proclaimed a national day of mourning for the victims of the disaster.

proclaim.etymology: 前缀「pro-」（公开、向前）+ 词根「claim」（呼喊），公开呼喊→宣布、宣告

similar_words.description: 均表宣布，proclaim更正式，侧重官方公开的宣告，declare可用于正式或非正式场合

definitions.example: The new assembly line produces high-quality automotive components efficiently.
The local farmers' market offers a wide variety of fresh organic produce.

produce.etymology: 前缀「pro-」（向前）+ 词根「duce」（引导），引导出成果→生产；引申为产出的农产品总称

similar_words.description: 同根于「duce」（引导），produce作名词指农产品总称，product指具体的工业或加工产品

definitions.example: Many tech companies focus on developing sustainable products to meet growing consumer demand for eco-friendly goods.

product.etymology: 来自动词「produce」（生产），词根「duct-」表「引导、生产」，引申为生产出的物品

similar_words.description: 「produce」作动词表‘生产’，作名词表‘农产品’；「product」专指工业或商业领域的具体产品

definitions.example: The automobile factory has expanded its production line to meet the surging demand for electric vehicles.

production.etymology: 由动词「produce」加名词后缀「-tion」构成，表‘生产的行为或结果’

similar_words.description: 「product」指具体的单个产品，「production」指生产行为或总产量

definitions.example: The cross-disciplinary research team held a productive meeting that yielded three innovative research proposals.

productive.etymology: 由名词「product」加形容词后缀「-ive」构成，表‘具有生产性质的’

similar_words.description: 二者均表‘有成效的’，「productive」侧重生产或工作的效率，「fruitful」侧重成果的数量与质量

definitions.example: Investment in digital technology has significantly enhanced the productivity of small and medium-sized enterprises.

productivity.etymology: 由形容词「productive」加名词后缀「-ity」构成，表‘生产的效率状态’

similar_words.description: 「production」指生产行为或产量，「productivity」指单位时间内的生产效率

definitions.meaning: 职业（尤指需专业知识的）；行业
definitions.example: Medicine, law and engineering are widely recognized as prestigious professions requiring rigorous academic training.

profession.etymology: 由前缀「pro-（公开）」+ 词根「fess-（宣称）」+ 名词后缀「-ion」构成，原指‘公开宣称从事某职业’，后引申为专业职业

similar_words.description: 「profession」侧重需专业资质的特定职业，「career」侧重个人终身从事的职业历程

definitions.example: All candidates for the research position must have professional experience in data analysis and scientific writing.
The conference invited several top professionals in the field of artificial intelligence to deliver keynote speeches.

professional.etymology: 由名词「profession」加后缀「-al」构成，可作形容词或名词

similar_words.description: 「professional」侧重具备专业资质的，「vocational」侧重与职业技能培训相关的

definitions.example: The professor of molecular biology has led a groundbreaking research project on gene editing for the past five years.
