professor.etymology: 由前缀「pro-（公开）」+ 词根「fess-（讲授）」+ 名词后缀「-or（表人）」构成，指‘公开讲学的大学教师’

similar_words.description: 「professor」指大学正教授（高级职称），「lecturer」指大学讲师（初级职称）

definitions.meaning: 熟练；精通（尤指技能或语言）
definitions.example: Most postgraduate programs in foreign languages require applicants to demonstrate high proficiency in at least one second language.

proficiency.etymology: 由前缀「pro-（向前）」+ 词根「fici-（做）」+ 名词后缀「-ency」构成，表‘做得好的熟练状态’

similar_words.description: 「proficiency」侧重技能的熟练程度，「mastery」侧重完全精通的最高境界

definitions.meaning: 概况；人物简介；侧面轮廓
definitions.example: The annual report includes a detailed profile of the company’s market share and business strategies over the past year.

profile.etymology: 由前缀「pro-（向前）」+ 词根「file-（线条）」构成，原指‘侧面的线条轮廓’，后引申为事物的概况

similar_words.description: 「profile」侧重人物、机构或事件的整体概况，「outline」侧重内容的结构大纲或外形轮廓

definitions.example: Despite the global economic downturn, the e-commerce platform reported a 20% increase in annual profit due to cross-border sales growth.
Students can profit greatly from participating in academic seminars and collaborative research projects.

profit.etymology: 由前缀「pro-（向前）」+ 词根「fit-（做）」构成，原指‘做得好所获得的收益’

similar_words.description: 「profit」指扣除成本后的净利润，「revenue」指未扣除成本的总收入

definitions.example: The sustainable energy sector has become increasingly profitable for global institutional investors in the past decade.

profitable.etymology: 来自名词「profit（利润）」+ 形容词后缀「-able（可…的）」，意为「可产生利润的」

similar_words.description: profitable 侧重经济层面的盈利，beneficial 侧重广义的有益（如健康、社会等）

definitions.example: The anthropologist's fieldwork has made a profound contribution to the study of indigenous cultures.

profound.etymology: 前缀「pro-（表深度、向前）」+ 词根「found（来自fundus，表底部、基础）」，意为「深入基础的，深刻的」

similar_words.description: profound 指思想、影响等深刻，proficient 指技能、能力熟练

definitions.example: The UK government rolled out a nationwide training programme to enhance the digital skills of small business owners.

programme.etymology: 前缀「pro-（预先）」+ 词根「gram（写、记录）」，意为「预先写好的安排，计划」

similar_words.description: programme 为英式拼写，考研阅读中英籍作者文章常用；program 为美式拼写

definitions.example: Scientists have achieved remarkable progress in decoding the genetic mechanism of certain rare diseases.

progress.etymology: 前缀「pro-（向前）」+ 词根「gress（走）」，意为「向前走，取得进展」

similar_words.description: progress 指向前的进步、进展；process 指事物发展的过程、流程

definitions.example: The progressive policy framework adopted by the Nordic countries has been widely studied by global policymakers.

progressive.etymology: 来自名词「progress（进步）」+ 形容词后缀「-ive（表属性）」，意为「具有进步属性的」

similar_words.description: progressive 指进步、先进的；aggressive 指好斗的、激进的

definitions.example: The international treaty prohibits the commercial exploitation of endangered marine species.

prohibit.etymology: 前缀「pro-（表强调）」+ 词根「hibit（持有、阻止）」，意为「强行阻止，禁止」

similar_words.description: prohibit 多指官方、法律层面的禁止；inhibit 多指抑制情绪、行为等

definitions.example: The municipal government allocated 200 million yuan to the urban renewal project targeting old residential areas.

project.etymology: 前缀「pro-（向前）」+ 词根「ject（扔、投射）」，意为「扔到前面的计划，项目」

similar_words.description: project 指项目、工程；object 指物体、目标，也可作动词表反对

definitions.example: During the academic conference, the keynote speaker used a high-definition projector to present the experimental data.

projector.etymology: 来自动词「project（投射）」+ 名词后缀「-or（表事物）」，意为「投射影像的设备」

similar_words.description: projector 是投射影像的设备；project 是项目或投射动作

definitions.example: The research institute applied to the funding agency to prolong the project duration by six months.

prolong.etymology: 前缀「pro-（向前、延伸）」+ 词根「long（长的）」，意为「向前拉长，延长」

similar_words.description: prolong 侧重延长时间、期限；extend 可指空间或时间的延伸，也可指范围扩大

definitions.example: He is a prominent economist who has served as a consultant for the World Bank for over a decade.

prominent.etymology: 前缀「pro-（向前）」+ 词根「min（来自minere，表突出）」+ 形容词后缀「-ent」，意为「向前突出的，杰出的」

similar_words.description: prominent 侧重因显著成就或特征而知名；eminent 侧重在领域内德高望重、地位崇高

definitions.example: The research team promises to make all original experimental data accessible to the public after the study is officially published.
The novel nanomaterial developed by the laboratory holds great promise for extending the service life of new energy vehicle batteries.

promise.etymology: 前缀pro-（向前、公开）+ 词根-mis（送、约定），原指当众许下的约定，后引申为承诺、前途等义

similar_words.description: 拼写相近，「premise」意为前提、假设，与本词语义差异较大

definitions.example: Promising results from the phase I clinical trial have attracted huge investment from global pharmaceutical enterprises.

promising.etymology: 动词promise（有指望）的形容词形式，后缀-ing表属性，意为「展现出成功希望的」

similar_words.description: 拼写相近，「compromising」意为损害声誉的、妥协的，语义与本词完全不同

definitions.example: The local government has launched a series of policies to promote the integrated development of digital economy and real economy.
She was promoted to the position of associate professor for her outstanding achievements in ecological research.

promote.etymology: 前缀pro-（向前）+ 词根-mot（移动），原指向前移，引申为促进、晋升等义

similar_words.description: 同根反义，「demote」意为使降级、降职，与本词表晋升的语义相反

definitions.example: Prompt intervention from the epidemiological investigation team effectively contained the spread of the infectious disease.
The frequent extreme weather events around the world have prompted scholars to re-evaluate the impact of climate change.

prompt.etymology: 来自拉丁语promptus（准备好的），原指准备好立刻行动的，引申为迅速的、促使等义

similar_words.description: 拼写相近，「promote」意为促进、晋升，与本词语义及用法均有差异

definitions.example: Sedentary middle-aged people are more prone to cardiovascular diseases than those who exercise regularly.
The archaeologist lay prone on the ground to carefully examine the tiny pottery fragments unearthed from the site.

prone.etymology: 来自拉丁语pronus（向前倾的），原指身体向前俯的，引申为有固有倾向的、俯卧的

similar_words.description: 近义，「liable」也表易于…的，但更常用于法律语境或指客观可能的不良后果，prone侧重主观或固有的倾向

definitions.example: In formal academic writing, scholars are often advised to reduce the use of first-person pronouns such as 'I' and 'we' to maintain objectivity.

pronoun.etymology: 前缀pro-（代替）+ 词根noun（名词），意为用来代替名词的词类

similar_words.description: 拼写相近，「pronounce」是动词，意为发音、宣布，与作为语法术语的本词语义完全不同

definitions.example: Many English learners find it difficult to correctly pronounce words with silent letters at the end.
The supreme court pronounced the final ruling on the intellectual property dispute after six months of trial.

pronounce.etymology: 前缀pro-（公开、向前）+ 词根-nounce（说），原指当众说出，引申为发音、正式宣布等义

similar_words.description: 同根近义，「announce」侧重公开告知普通消息，pronounce作宣布义时更侧重权威、正式的宣告或判决

definitions.example: Standard English pronunciation assessment is an important part of the postgraduate admission interview for English majors.

pronunciation.etymology: 动词pronounce的名词形式，后缀-ation表动作或状态，指发音的动作或方式

similar_words.description: 拼写相近，「annunciation」意为宣布、通报，与本词语义无关

definitions.example: The research team failed to provide conclusive proof to support their radical hypothesis about the origin of the universe.
The newly developed building material is proof against high temperatures and strong impacts.

proof.etymology: 来自拉丁语probare（检验），原指经检验证实的真实性，引申为证据、可抵御某事物的等义

similar_words.description: 同根词，「prove」是动词，意为证明、证实，proof是其对应的名词形式

definitions.example: Readers should learn to distinguish objective news reports from biased political propaganda on social media.

propaganda.etymology: 前缀pro-（向前）+ 词根-pag（固定、传播）+ 后缀-anda，原指用于传播的宗教教义，后引申为带有误导性的宣传，多含贬义

similar_words.description: 近义，「promotion」多指中性的商业推广或职位晋升，本词常指带有误导性的政治或意识形态宣传，多含贬义

definitions.example: The advancement of digital technology has propelled the transformation of traditional manufacturing industries.

propel.etymology: 由拉丁前缀pro-（向前）+ 词根pel-（推、驱动，源自pellere）构成，表“向前推”的动作

similar_words.description: 「repel」意为“排斥、击退”，与「propel」词根相同（均含pel-“推”）、拼写相近，前缀相反导致语义相反

definitions.example: It is proper for researchers to disclose their funding sources in academic papers.
The survey covers the urban area proper, excluding the surrounding suburban districts.

proper.etymology: 源自拉丁语proprius（自己的、特有的），原指“属于自身的”，后引申为“符合身份的→适当的”，再引申出“本身的”僻义

similar_words.description: 「appropriate」侧重“符合特定场合/标准的合适”，「proper」侧重“符合常规、得体的合适”，后者更常用作基础义

definitions.meaning: 财产，资产（尤指合法所有的）
definitions.example: The new regulation strengthens the protection of intellectual property for small and medium-sized enterprises.

definitions.meaning: 特性，性质（事物的固有属性）
definitions.example: This composite material has excellent mechanical properties suitable for aerospace applications.

property.etymology: 由「proper」的词根propri-（自己的）+ 名词后缀-ty构成，原指“自己的东西→财产”，后引申“事物自身的属性→特性”

similar_words.description: 「possession」多指“个人拥有的具体物品（常复数）”，「property」侧重“合法所有的财产（含不动产、知识产权等）”

definitions.meaning: 预言家；（社会变革等的）倡导者，鼓吹者
definitions.example: Rachel Carson is regarded as a prophet of the modern environmental protection movement.

prophet.etymology: 源自希腊语prophetes（先知、代言人），由pro-（在前）+ phetes（说者）构成，原指“提前说出趋势的人”

similar_words.description: 「predictor」侧重“基于数据/规律的客观预测者”，「prophet」侧重“有远见、能引领趋势的主观倡导者/预言家”

definitions.meaning: 比例，比率（整体中部分的占比）
definitions.example: The proportion of renewable energy in China's total power generation has risen steadily in recent years.
A significant proportion of the research budget is allocated to basic scientific research.

proportion.etymology: 由拉丁前缀pro-（按照）+ portion（部分）构成，原指“按照部分划分的占比→比例”

similar_words.description: 「ratio」侧重“两个数量的精确比值（如1:4）”，「proportion」侧重“整体中某部分的相对占比”

definitions.meaning: 提议，建议（尤指正式提交的、需审议的）
definitions.example: The parliamentary committee rejected the proposal to cut funding for public healthcare.

proposal.etymology: 由动词「propose」加名词后缀-al构成，原指“提出的方案→提议”

similar_words.description: 「suggestion」侧重“非正式的、个人提出的建议”，「proposal」侧重“正式提交的、需集体审议的提议”

definitions.example: The senator proposed a new bill to regulate the collection of personal data by tech companies.
She was proposed as a candidate for the position of director of the research institute.

propose.etymology: 由拉丁前缀pro-（向前）+ 词根pos-（放置，源自ponere）构成，原指“把…放在前面→提出”

similar_words.description: 「suggest」侧重“委婉地、试探性地提出建议”，「propose」侧重“正式地、明确地提出（提议、提名等）”

definitions.meaning: 主张，观点；（需论证的）提议
definitions.example: The sociologist put forward a controversial proposition about the relationship between social media and mental health.
This mathematical proposition remained unproven for over a century until a young mathematician solved it.

proposition.etymology: 源自动词「propose」，与「proposal」同根，但更侧重“抽象的主张、观点或逻辑命题”，而非具体的方案

similar_words.description: 「proposal」侧重“具体的、可操作的正式提议”，「proposition」侧重“抽象的、需论证的主张或逻辑命题”

definitions.meaning: 散文（无严格韵律的文学体裁）
definitions.example: Virginia Woolf's prose is celebrated for its lyrical quality and deep exploration of human consciousness.

prose.etymology: 源自拉丁语prosa（直白的语言），原指“不同于诗歌的、无严格韵律的直白文体→散文”

similar_words.description: 「poetry」指“讲究韵律、意象的诗歌”，「prose」指“无严格韵律、侧重叙事/议论的散文”，二者为文学体裁的对立范畴

definitions.meaning: 起诉，检举（尤指官方提起的刑事诉讼）
definitions.example: The company was prosecuted for violating the national environmental protection regulations.

definitions.meaning: 继续从事（某项研究、事业等）
definitions.example: Despite financial hardships, he prosecuted his research on ancient Chinese bronze inscriptions for decades.

prosecute.etymology: 由拉丁前缀pro-（向前、彻底）+ 词根secut-（跟随，源自sequi）构成，原义为“跟随到底、坚持从事”，后引申为法律语境下的“起诉、检举”

similar_words.description: 「sue」侧重“个人或企业提起的民事起诉（要求赔偿）”，「prosecute」侧重“官方（如检察院）提起的刑事起诉”

definitions.example: The federal prosecutor gathered sufficient evidence to bring the high-profile corruption case to court.

prosecutor.etymology: 来自同根词prosecute（起诉、控告）加名词后缀-or，表「执行某动作的人」，即执行起诉动作的人。

similar_words.description: persecutor意为「迫害者」，拼写与prosecutor仅有前缀字母的差异，极易混淆。

definitions.example: Many new graduates hold an optimistic prospect of their career development in the digital economy sector.
The geological team was sent to the remote plateau to prospect for rare earth minerals.

prospect.etymology: 词根spect意为「看」，前缀pro-表「向前」，向前看引申为「前景」；引申为勘察义时，指向前查看矿脉等资源。

similar_words.description: perspective为名词，意为「观点；透视法」，与prospect拼写相近，容易混淆词义。

definitions.example: Prospective employers often attach great importance to candidates' internship experience during campus recruitment.

prospective.etymology: 来自名词prospect（前景）加形容词后缀-ive，表「具有…性质的」，即有前景的、未来的。

similar_words.description: perspective为名词，意为「观点；透视角度」，与prospective拼写高度相似，需注意词性与词义差异。

definitions.example: Local handicraft industries can prosper with the support of cultural conservation and e-commerce policies.

prosper.etymology: 词根sper意为「希望」，前缀pro-表「向前」，有向前的希望引申为事业兴旺、成功。

similar_words.description: proper为形容词，意为「恰当的；合适的」，与prosper拼写相近，需注意词性与词义差异。

definitions.example: The government's new opening-up policy is designed to boost regional economic prosperity and improve people's well-being.

prosperity.etymology: 来自动词prosper（兴旺）加名词后缀-ity，表抽象性质，即兴旺的状态。

similar_words.description: property为名词，意为「财产；属性」，与prosperity拼写相近，极易混淆词义。

definitions.example: Over the past four decades, China has transformed from a backward agricultural country into a prosperous industrial power.

prosperous.etymology: 来自动词prosper（兴旺）加形容词后缀-ous，表「充满…的」，即充满兴旺状态的。

similar_words.description: 二者均为形容词，prospective意为「预期的」，prosperous意为「繁荣的」，词形相近易混淆。

definitions.example: The reserve has established a series of rules to protect endangered wild animals from illegal hunting.

protect.etymology: 词根tect意为「覆盖」，前缀pro-表「在前方」，在前方覆盖住以避免受到伤害，引申为保护。

similar_words.description: protest可作动词或名词，意为「抗议；反对」，与protect拼写仅有两个字母的差异，极易混淆。

definitions.example: Nutritionists suggest that adults should consume an appropriate amount of protein every day to maintain muscle health.

protein.etymology: 来自希腊语proteios，意为「首要的」，因蛋白质是维持生命活动的首要营养物质得名。

similar_words.description: 二者均为考研科普阅读常见的营养物质名词，carbohydrate意为「碳水化合物」，与protein同属核心营养范畴。

definitions.example: Hundreds of local residents gathered outside the city hall to protest against the proposed construction of a chemical plant near the residential area.
The researcher protested that her experimental data was accurate and had not been tampered with.

protest.etymology: 词根test意为「证据」，前缀pro-表「在前方」，在前方拿出证据来表达反对立场，引申为抗议；进一步引申为拿出证据申明自己的立场。

similar_words.description: protect为动词，意为「保护」，与protest拼写相近，需注意词义差异。

definitions.example: The research team spent one year developing the first prototype of the intelligent medical device before conducting clinical trials.

prototype.etymology: 前缀proto-表「最初的、原始的」，词根type意为「类型、模型」，最初的模型即原型。

similar_words.description: 二者同属type词根词，stereotype意为「刻板印象；固定模式」，prototype意为「原型」，词形有相似性易混淆。

definitions.example: Many first-generation college students are proud of their humble family origins despite economic hardships.
The proud lighthouse stood against the raging storm on the isolated cape.

proud.etymology: 来自古英语prūd，原义为「勇敢的、有尊严的」，后引申出「骄傲的」及「高耸的」义

similar_words.description: 「pride」为名词，表「骄傲；自豪感」，是「proud」的同源名词形式
「arrogant」侧重「傲慢的、盛气凌人的」（贬义更强），「proud」可褒可贬

definitions.example: Researchers have proved that regular physical exercise can reduce the risk of chronic diseases.
The seemingly simple task proved to be far more challenging than expected.

prove.etymology: 来自拉丁语probare（检验，证明），词根prob-表「证明、测试」

similar_words.description: 「verify」侧重「核实、验证（真实性）」，「prove」侧重「通过证据证明（结论）」
「approve」表「批准、赞成」，与「prove」同词根prob-（证明合格）

definitions.example: Universities should provide students with sufficient resources for academic research.
The contract provides that the tenant must pay the rent by the 5th of each month.

provide.etymology: 来自拉丁语providere（预见，准备），由pro-（向前）+videre（看）构成，原义「提前准备」，引申为「提供」

similar_words.description: 「supply」侧重「批量供给（物资）」，「provide」侧重「为某人提供所需（物品、服务等）」

definitions.example: You will be admitted to the program provided that you submit all application materials before the deadline.
The provided data has been verified by the research team.

provided.etymology: 为动词「provide」的过去分词形式，作连词时引申为「在（满足前提）的情况下」

similar_words.description: 「providing」与「provided」作连词时同义，均表「假如」，可互换
「if」为通用连词，「provided」更正式，且常接that从句

definitions.example: The new high-speed railway will connect three major provinces in central China.
The study of ancient manuscripts is in the province of historical linguistics.

province.etymology: 来自拉丁语provincia（行省，职权范围），原指古罗马的行省，后引申为「专业领域」

similar_words.description: 「domain」侧重「（知识、活动的）领域、领地」，「province」侧重「（某人/学科的）专属范围」
「region」侧重「地理区域」，「province」为行政区划的「省」

definitions.example: The government is responsible for the provision of medical care to rural residents.
The employment contract includes a provision prohibiting employees from disclosing trade secrets.

provision.etymology: 来自拉丁语provisio（预见，准备），是动词「provide」的名词形式，词根pro-（向前）+vid-（看）

similar_words.description: 「clause」特指「合同、法律文件中的条款」，「provision」还可表「供给」

definitions.example: His rude remarks were a deliberate provocation aimed at triggering a conflict.

provocation.etymology: 来自拉丁语provocatio（召唤，挑战），是动词「provoke」的名词形式，词根pro-（向前）+voc-（呼喊）

similar_words.description: 「incitement」侧重「煽动（暴力等）」，「provocation」侧重「挑衅（引发愤怒）」

definitions.example: The politician’s provocative speech sparked widespread protests across the country.

provocative.etymology: 来自拉丁语provocativus，由「provoke」派生而来，词根pro-（向前）+voc-（呼喊）

similar_words.description: 「inflammatory」侧重「煽动性的（引发动乱）」，「provocative」侧重「挑衅的（引发愤怒）」

definitions.example: The reporter’s sharp question provoked the official to lose his temper.
The new policy has provoked heated debate among economists and the public.

provoke.etymology: 来自拉丁语provocare（向前呼喊，挑战），由pro-（向前）+vocare（呼喊）构成，词根voc-表「呼喊」

similar_words.description: 「irritate」侧重「使烦躁、激怒（程度较轻）」，「provoke」侧重「故意挑衅以激怒」
「evoke」表「唤起（记忆、情感）」，与「provoke」同词根voc-（呼喊）

definitions.meaning: 谨慎，审慎（尤指决策时的远见）
definitions.example: In times of economic uncertainty, financial prudence is essential for small businesses.

prudence.etymology: 来自拉丁语prudentia（远见，谨慎），由pro-（向前）+videre（看）构成，原义「预见」，引申为「谨慎」

similar_words.description: 「caution」侧重「小心、谨慎（避免危险）」，「prudence」侧重「审慎、有远见的谨慎（尤指决策）」

definitions.example: It is prudent for central banks to adjust monetary policies gradually amid high inflation.

prudent.etymology: 源自拉丁语prudens（有远见的），词根prud-=provid-（预见、提前准备），同根词为prudence

similar_words.description: 同根名词，意为「审慎，谨慎」，为prudent对应的名词形式

definitions.example: Psychiatry has made remarkable progress in treating anxiety disorders in the past decade.

psychiatry.etymology: 词根psych-（精神、心灵）+ 后缀-iatry（医学治疗学科后缀），指研究精神疾病诊疗的医学分支

similar_words.description: 同根形近词，psychology是研究人类心理与行为的通用学科，psychiatry是侧重精神疾病诊疗的医学学科

definitions.example: Social psychology explores how group norms influence individual decision-making.

psychology.etymology: 词根psych-（精神、心灵）+ 后缀-ology（学科后缀），指研究心理与行为的学科

similar_words.description: 同根形近词，psychology侧重普通心理研究与心理特征，psychiatry侧重精神疾病的医学诊疗

definitions.example: Traditional British pubs often serve local beer and simple homemade dishes.

pub.etymology: 短语public house的缩写形式，原指对公众开放的饮酒场所

similar_words.description: 近义名词，bar泛指各类酒吧，pub特指英式社区型小酒馆

definitions.example: The government has invested heavily in improving public transport infrastructure in rural areas.
The celebrity's apology failed to win forgiveness from the angry public.

public.etymology: 源自拉丁语publicus（属于人民的），为publish、publication等同根词的核心词根

similar_words.description: 形近词，拼写仅差一个字母'l'，pubic意为「耻骨的，阴部的」，语义完全不同

definitions.example: The publication of the Nobel laureate's memoir was scheduled for the end of 2024.
Academic publications are important carriers of cutting-edge research results.

publication.etymology: public（公开的）+ 名词后缀-ation，指将内容公之于众的行为或产物

similar_words.description: 同根形近词，publication侧重出版行为或出版物，publicity侧重宣传与公众关注度

definitions.example: The anti-smoking campaign has gained widespread publicity through national television.

publicity.etymology: public（公开的）+ 名词后缀-ity，指进入公众视野的状态或相关行为

similar_words.description: 同根形近词，publicity侧重宣传推广与公众曝光度，publication侧重出版行为或出版物

definitions.example: The environmental organization decided to publicize the water pollution data to arouse public awareness.

publicize.etymology: public（公开的）+ 动词后缀-ize（使…化），意为使…进入公众视野

similar_words.description: 同根近义词，publicize侧重主动宣传推广以提升关注度，publish侧重出版作品或官方发布信息

definitions.example: Many young scholars struggle to publish their papers in top international journals.
The National Bureau of Statistics will publish the latest CPI data tomorrow.

publish.etymology: 源自拉丁语publicare（使公开），同根于public，原指将事项公之于众

similar_words.description: 同根近义词，publish侧重出版作品或官方正式发布信息，publicize侧重主动宣传推广以扩大影响力

definitions.example: He puffed hard to blow away the dust on the old book cover.
Some e-commerce influencers deliberately puff low-quality products to earn high commissions.

puff.etymology: 源自古英语puffen，为拟声词，模仿吹气的声音，后引申出吹捧等义

similar_words.description: 近义动词，puff侧重夸大式的商业或媒体吹捧，flatter侧重为讨好他人的奉承式恭维
形近动词，huff意为「怒气冲冲，生气」，与puff语义完全不同

definitions.example: The doctor measured the patient's pulse rate before conducting the cardiovascular examination.
Fresh blood pulsed through the athlete's veins after the intense training session.

pulse.etymology: 来自拉丁语pulsus（击打、推动），词根「puls-」表“推动、击打”，为同根词如「impulse」的核心词根

similar_words.description: 「pulse」指脉搏/脉冲，「pump」指泵/抽水机，词形相近易混淆

definitions.example: The factory installed high-efficiency pumps to circulate cooling water for the heavy machinery production line.
The cyclist had to pump air into the flat tire before continuing the cross-country race.

pump.etymology: 来自中古英语pumpe，无复杂词缀，为基础借词

similar_words.description: 「pump」指泵/抽水机，「pulse」指脉搏/脉冲，词形相近易混淆

definitions.example: The worker used a steel punch to make precise holes in the metal panel for industrial assembly.
A single punch on the security alarm triggered the immediate response of the factory guards.

punch.etymology: 来自拉丁语punctum（点），词根「punct-」表“点、刺”，为同根词如「punctual」的核心词根

similar_words.description: 「punch」指猛击/打孔，「punctual」指守时的，词首「pun-」易混淆

definitions.example: Candidates for the postgraduate entrance examination are required to be punctual for the written test to avoid disqualification.

punctual.etymology: 词根「punct-」（点、对准）+ 形容词后缀「-ual」，原义“对准点的”，引申为“守时的”

similar_words.description: 「punctual」指守时的，「punch」指猛击/打孔，词首「pun-」易混淆

definitions.example: The regulatory authority will punish enterprises that discharge untreated sewage into the river in accordance with the law.

punish.etymology: 词根「pun-」（惩罚）+ 动词后缀「-ish」，同根词如「punishment」

similar_words.description: 「punish」指惩罚，「punch」指猛击/打孔，词首「pun-」易混淆

definitions.example: The international media exposed that the rebel organization was a puppet of certain foreign powers in the regional conflict.

puppet.etymology: 来自古法语poupette（玩偶），同根词如「puppy」（幼犬，原指小玩偶）

similar_words.description: 「puppet」指木偶/傀儡，「puppy」指小狗/幼犬，词形相近易混淆

definitions.example: Many university libraries purchase electronic databases to provide students with access to academic journals.
The annual report listed the total purchase of office supplies by the administrative department.

purchase.etymology: 前缀「pur-」（向前）+ 词根「chase」（追逐），原义“追逐以获取”，引申为“正式购买”

similar_words.description: 「purchase」指购买/采购，「pursue」指追求/从事，词首「pur-」易混淆

definitions.example: The scientific experiment requires a pure sample of the compound to ensure the reliability of the analytical results.

pure.etymology: 词根「pur-」（纯净）+ 形容词后缀「-e」，同根词如「purify」「purity」

similar_words.description: 「pure」指纯净的/纯粹的，「poor」指贫穷的/劣质的，词形相近易混淆

definitions.example: The water treatment plant uses multi-stage filtration systems to purify raw water from the reservoir.

purify.etymology: 词根「pur-」（纯净）+ 动词后缀「-ify」（使…），意为“使纯净”

similar_words.description: 「purify」是动词表净化/提纯，「pure」是形容词表纯净的，为同根衍生词易混淆

definitions.example: In ancient European royal courts, purple robes were reserved for monarchs to symbolize supreme power.
The research found that the purple pigment extracted from the plant has potential medicinal value.

purple.etymology: 来自古英语purpul，无复杂词缀，为基础颜色类词汇

similar_words.description: 「purple」指紫色的/紫色，「purse」指钱包/女用手提包，词首「pur-」易混淆

definitions.example: The primary purpose of this longitudinal study is to examine the long-term effects of early childhood education on career development.

purpose.etymology: pur-（向前）+ pos-（放置）+ -e，原指「提前设定的行动目标」，引申为「目的、意图」

similar_words.description: 同根词，propose为动词，表「提议、建议」；purpose为名词，表「目的、意图」

definitions.example: She carefully placed her identification card into her leather purse before leaving the office.
Local governments are required to disclose the allocation of the public purse to ensure fiscal transparency.

purse.etymology: 来自古法语pors，原指「装钱币的皮革袋」，后引申为「（公共）资金」

similar_words.description: 形近词，purpose表「目的、意图」；purse表「钱包、资金」，拼写易混淆

definitions.example: An increasing number of young people choose to pursue postgraduate studies to expand their professional knowledge.

pursue.etymology: pur-（向前）+ sue（来自拉丁语sequi，表「跟随」），原指「向前跟随」，引申为「追求、追赶」

similar_words.description: 同根词，pursue为动词，表「追求、追赶」；pursuit为名词，表「追求、寻求」

definitions.example: The pursuit of academic excellence requires consistent effort and critical thinking skills.

pursuit.etymology: pursu(e)（追求）+ -it（名词后缀，表行为或状态），表示「追求的行为或过程」

similar_words.description: 同根词，pursuit为名词，表「追求、寻求」；pursue为动词，表「追求、追赶」

definitions.example: Climate scientists are puzzled by the unexpected acceleration of ice sheet melting in Antarctica.
The origin of human language remains one of the greatest unsolved puzzles in linguistics.

puzzle.etymology: 来自古英语pyslian，原指「使陷入混乱或困惑的状态」，引申为「使困惑、谜题」

similar_words.description: 近义词，均表「使困惑」；perplex语气更正式，侧重「因复杂而难以理解」；puzzle更常用，侧重「因奇特而困惑」

definitions.meaning: 金字塔；金字塔形等级结构
definitions.example: The corporate management pyramid illustrates that senior executives account for only a tiny proportion of the workforce.

pyramid.etymology: 来自希腊语pyramis，原指「埃及金字塔」，后引申为「具有层级结构的组织或体系」

similar_words.description: 形近词，pyramid表「金字塔、层级结构」；prism表「棱镜、棱柱」

definitions.example: A master's degree in relevant fields is often a necessary qualification for senior research positions.

qualification.etymology: qualify（使合格）+ -ation（名词后缀，表行为或结果），表示「使合格的条件或资质」

similar_words.description: 形近词，qualification表「资格、资质」；quality表「质量、品质」

definitions.meaning: 使合格，取得资格；限定，修饰
definitions.example: Completing the certified training program will qualify you to work as a professional data analyst.

qualify.etymology: qual（来自拉丁语qualis，表「性质、种类」）+ -ify（动词后缀，表「使…」），原指「使具有某种合格性质」，引申为「使合格、限定」

similar_words.description: 同根词，qualify为动词，表「使合格、限定」；qualification为名词，表「资格、资质」

definitions.example: The sociological study combines qualitative interviews with quantitative surveys to collect comprehensive data.

qualitative.etymology: qual（性质）+ -it（连接成分）+ -ative（形容词后缀，表「与…相关的」），表示「与性质相关的」

similar_words.description: 形近反义词，qualitative表「定性的（关注性质）」；quantitative表「定量的（关注数量）」，常用于学术研究语境

definitions.meaning: 质量，品质；优质的（作定语）
definitions.example: The Ministry of Education has launched a series of initiatives to improve the quality of compulsory education nationwide.

quality.etymology: qual（来自拉丁语qualis，表「性质」）+ -ity（名词后缀，表「性质、状态」），表示「事物的性质或品质」

similar_words.description: 形近词，quality表「质量、品质」；qualification表「资格、资质」

definitions.example: Researchers can quantify the long-term impact of deforestation on biodiversity by analyzing satellite data.

quantify.etymology: 词根quant-（表「数量」，源自拉丁语quantus）+ 动词后缀-ify（表「使…化」）

similar_words.description: 「quantify」表量化（与数量相关），「qualify」表（使）合格、限定（与性质相关），二者词根不同、语义迥异

definitions.example: Quantitative analysis of survey responses helps scholars identify statistically significant correlations.

quantitative.etymology: 词根quant-（表「数量」）+ 连接成分-it- + 形容词后缀-ative（表「有…性质的」）

similar_words.description: 「quantitative」表定量的（侧重数量维度），「qualitative」表定性的（侧重性质维度），为研究方法中的一对对应概念

definitions.example: Consumers often prioritize quantity over quality when purchasing affordable household goods.

quantity.etymology: 词根quant-（表「数量」）+ 名词后缀-ity（表「性质、状态」）

similar_words.description: 「quantity」指数量、数额，「quality」指质量、品质，二者为常被对比的核心概念

definitions.example: The bitter quarrel between the two political parties delayed the passage of the new bill.
Some colleagues often quarrel over trivial matters in the workplace.

quarrel.etymology: 源自古法语querele，原指「抱怨、申诉」，后引申为「争吵」

similar_words.description: 「quarrel」多指因琐事引发的带情绪的激烈争吵，「argue」侧重理性的争论、辩论

definitions.meaning: 夸脱（液体或干量的计量单位，约合0.95升）
definitions.example: The experimental procedure requires adding one quart of distilled water to the solution.

quart.etymology: 源自拉丁语quartus（表「第四」），因夸脱为1加仑的四分之一而得名

similar_words.description: 「quart」是计量单位夸脱，「quarter」指四分之一、季度，语义完全不同

definitions.example: The central bank publishes quarterly economic forecasts to guide market expectations.

quarterly.etymology: 名词quarter（表「季度」）+ 后缀-ly（构成形容词或副词，表「每…的/地」）

similar_words.description: 「quarter」为名词「季度」，「quarterly」为其衍生的形容词/副词，表「每季度的/地」

definitions.meaning: 石英（一种矿物，常用于电子设备）
definitions.example: Quartz crystals are widely used in watches for their precise oscillation frequency.

quartz.etymology: 源自德语Quarz，原指「硬质矿石」

similar_words.description: 「quartz」是矿物石英，「quart」是计量单位夸脱，拼写仅末尾字母不同

definitions.example: The queen's annual Christmas speech conveys her best wishes to the nation.
Madonna was once regarded as the queen of pop music in the 1980s.

queen.etymology: 源自古英语cwen，原指「女人、妻子」，后专指「女王、王后」

similar_words.description: 「queen」指女王、王后，「king」指国王、君主，为对应的王室核心头衔

definitions.example: His queer habit of talking to himself during lectures made him stand out among classmates.

queer.etymology: 源自古德语quer（表「斜的、偏离的」），引申为「反常的、奇怪的」

similar_words.description: 「queer」侧重偏离常规的反常怪异，「weird」侧重带神秘感或超自然感的怪异

definitions.example: Drinking a bottle of iced tea can quickly quench your thirst after a long run.
Firefighters used foam to quench the fierce fire in the chemical warehouse.

quench.etymology: 源自古英语acwencan，原指「熄灭、扑灭」，后引申为「解渴」

similar_words.description: 「quench」可指解渴或灭火，「extinguish」仅指灭火、消除抽象事物（如希望）

definitions.example: Scholars have embarked on a quest to decipher ancient manuscripts unearthed in the Gobi Desert.

quest.etymology: 来自拉丁语quaerere（寻求），与「query」同根，表主动寻求的长期行为

similar_words.description: 「query」意为‘疑问、查询’，多表具体提问行为；「quest」表长期的探索、追求，侧重过程

definitions.example: The authenticity of the newly discovered historical document remains questionable due to inconsistent watermarks.

questionable.etymology: 由名词「question」（疑问）加形容词后缀「-able」（可…的）构成，表‘可被质疑的’

similar_words.description: 「doubtful」侧重主观上的怀疑；「questionable」侧重客观上存疑、存在问题

definitions.example: The research team distributed a 20-item questionnaire to 500 participants to collect data on consumer behavior.

questionnaire.etymology: 由名词「question」（问题）加法语来源名词后缀「-naire」（表物）构成，指包含问题的调查工具

similar_words.description: 「survey」范围更广，可包含问卷、访谈等多种调查形式；「questionnaire」仅指书面调查问卷

definitions.example: There was a long queue of students waiting to register for the postgraduate examination at the admissions office.

queue.etymology: 来自古法语cue（尾巴），引申为‘排成一线的长队’，为英式英语常用词

similar_words.description: 「line」为美式英语常用‘队列’；「queue」为英式英语，考研阅读中多以英式义出现指‘长队’

definitions.example: The researchers tested the thermal insulation capacity of a traditional cotton quilt in a controlled climate chamber.

quilt.etymology: 来自拉丁语culcita（垫子、褥子），后演变为指有夹层绗缝的被褥

similar_words.description: 「blanket」指无夹层的毛毯、厚毯子；「quilt」指有绗缝夹层的被子

definitions.example: One of the quirks of the renowned linguist is that he only writes academic papers with a vintage fountain pen.

quirk.etymology: 源自中古英语quirk（突然的动作），后引申为‘人或事物的独特怪癖’

similar_words.description: 「oddity」指怪事、怪人，侧重反常性；「quirk」侧重人或事物的独特、轻微的怪癖

definitions.example: Many graduates quit their entry-level jobs within six months due to mismatched career expectations.

quit.etymology: 来自古法语quitter（离开、释放），与「quiet」同根，原义为‘使平静、解脱’，后引申为‘停止、放弃’

similar_words.description: 「resign」特指正式提交辞呈的辞职；「quit」可表停止、放弃、辞职，范围更广

definitions.example: The leaves of the oak tree quivered in the cold autumn wind as the sun set behind the mountains.

quiver.etymology: 来自古英语cwifer（活跃的、敏捷的），引申为‘轻微快速的抖动’

similar_words.description: 「tremble」侧重因恐惧、寒冷产生的剧烈颤抖；「quiver」侧重轻微、快速的抖动

definitions.example: The professor administered a short quiz at the beginning of the lecture to assess students' understanding of the previous lesson.

quiz.etymology: 来源不明，18世纪初原指‘恶作剧’，后引申为‘小型测试、问答游戏’

similar_words.description: 「test」范围广泛，可指正式考试、专业测试；「quiz」特指小型、简短的课堂测验或问答比赛

definitions.example: The university set a strict quota for international postgraduate applicants to maintain a balanced student population.

quota.etymology: 来自拉丁语quota（多少），与「quantity」同根，表‘规定的数量限额’

similar_words.description: 「ration」特指物资的配给量；「quota」指人员、名额等的定额、配额，多用于学术或官方语境

definitions.meaning: 引用，援引（他人的话、研究数据等）
definitions.example: The scholar quoted a peer-reviewed study to back up her claim about climate adaptation.
The book opens with a quote from a famous environmental activist.

quote.etymology: 源自拉丁语quotare（标记页码、引用），词根为quot-（表“多少”）

similar_words.description: 「cite」侧重正式引用文献、权威观点以佐证，「quote」侧重直接引用他人原话或原文内容

definitions.example: The research observed the foraging behavior of wild rabbits in suburban grasslands.

rabbit.etymology: 源自中古英语rabet，可能来自荷兰语robbe（幼兔）

similar_words.description: 「hare」指野兔，体型较大、善奔跑；「rabbit」多指家兔，体型较小、多穴居

definitions.example: The report analyzes the disparities in healthcare access across different race groups.

definitions.meaning: 赛跑；（商业、科技等领域的）竞争
definitions.example: The global race for artificial intelligence leadership has intensified in recent years.

race.etymology: 词源分两支：表“赛跑”源自古英语rās（跑）；表“种族”源自古法语race（世系、族群）

similar_words.description: 「race」侧重基于生理特征的人种划分，「ethnicity」侧重基于文化、习俗的族群身份

definitions.example: Racial equality is a core principle advocated by many international organizations.

racial.etymology: 由名词race（种族）加形容词后缀-ial（表“与…相关的”）构成

similar_words.description: 「racial」侧重与人种生理特征相关的，「ethnic」侧重与族群文化、传统相关的

definitions.example: The laboratory stored the test tubes on a metal rack to avoid contamination.

definitions.meaning: 折磨；使（国家、机构等）深受困扰
definitions.example: The European economy was racked by high inflation and energy shortages in 2022.

rack.etymology: 源自古英语ræc（架子），动词义由“在架子上拉伸受刑”引申为“折磨、困扰”

similar_words.description: 「wrack」意为“毁坏、海草”，「rack」作名词指“架子”，作动词指“折磨、困扰”

definitions.example: The professional tennis player tested several new rackets before the Grand Slam tournament.
The financial regulatory body cracked down on a cross-border investment racket last month.

racket.etymology: 源自阿拉伯语rākāt（手掌），引申为“球拍”；“诈骗”义为19世纪俚语引申

similar_words.description: 「racquet」是「racket」的变体拼写，多用于指壁球、羽毛球拍；「racket」还可指非法诈骗活动

definitions.meaning: 雷达（无线电探测与测距系统）
definitions.example: The airport’s advanced radar system can detect small drones within a 50-kilometer radius.
The startup’s breakthrough battery technology flew under the radar of major automakers for two years.

radar.etymology: 为词组Radio Detection And Ranging的首字母缩写，属于科技术语

similar_words.description: 「lidar」指激光雷达（用于地形测绘），「radar」指无线电雷达（用于目标探测）

definitions.meaning: 辐射的；发光的，光芒四射的
definitions.example: The radiant energy emitted by the sun drives the Earth’s water cycle and climate system.
The Nobel Prize winner looked radiant as she accepted the award in Stockholm.

radiant.etymology: 源自拉丁语radiare（辐射、发光），加形容词后缀-ant（表“具有…性质的”），词根为radi-（辐射、光线）

similar_words.description: 「radiant」侧重“从中心向四周发散式发光/辐射的”，「luminous」侧重“本身主动发光的、明亮的”

definitions.example: Nuclear power plants generate electricity by using the heat radiated by nuclear fission.
The renowned scientist radiated humility and curiosity during the public lecture.

radiate.etymology: 源自拉丁语radiare（辐射、发光），词根为radi-（辐射、光线），同根词有radiant、radiation

similar_words.description: 「radiate」侧重“从中心向四周发散式发射”，「emit」侧重“主动发出（光、热、气体等）”

definitions.meaning: 辐射；放射物（尤指核辐射）
definitions.example: Long-term exposure to low-level nuclear radiation may increase the risk of certain cancers.

radiation.etymology: 由动词radiate（辐射）加名词后缀-ion（表“动作、过程或结果”）构成，词根为radi-（辐射、光线）

similar_words.description: 「radiation」指辐射的过程或辐射物，「radioactivity」指物质释放辐射的性质（即放射性）

definitions.example: The policy brought about radical changes in the country's economic structure.
Some radical groups demand immediate, uncompromising reforms in social welfare.

radical.etymology: 来自拉丁语「radix」（根），加形容词后缀「-ical」，表「与根源相关的」

similar_words.description: 「radical」侧重从根源上的彻底改变，「fundamental」侧重事物的基础、核心属性

definitions.example: The research findings have radically altered our understanding of climate change mechanisms.

radically.etymology: 由形容词「radical」加副词后缀「-ly」构成，表程度或方式

similar_words.description: 「radically」表程度（根本地），「radiantly」表状态（发光地、容光焕发地）

definitions.example: Researchers wear protective suits when handling radioactive materials in the laboratory.

radioactive.etymology: 由前缀「radio-」（表辐射、射线）加形容词「active」（活跃的）构成，表「具有辐射活性的」

similar_words.description: 「radioactive」指物质具有放射性的，「radiative」指与辐射过程相关的

definitions.example: The study surveyed all households within a 10-kilometer radius of the industrial plant.

radius.etymology: 来自拉丁语「radius」（原指射线、轮辐），后引申为圆的半径及范围义

similar_words.description: 「radius」指圆的半径或覆盖范围，「diameter」指圆的直径

definitions.example: She used an old rag to wipe the dust off the antique bookshelf.
The boys ragged their classmate for forgetting his homework again.

rag.etymology: 源自古英语「ræc」，原指破布、碎布，无复杂词缀

similar_words.description: 「rag」指破布、碎布，「rug」指小地毯、毛毯

definitions.example: He stormed out of the meeting in a fit of rage after being criticized publicly.
The influenza epidemic raged across the continent for six months.

rage.etymology: 源自古法语「rage」，与拉丁语「rabies」（狂犬病）同源，原指疯狂状态，后引申为愤怒

similar_words.description: 「rage」指失控的强烈狂怒，「anger」指普通的可控愤怒

definitions.example: The police conducted a dawn raid on the suspected drug trafficking hideout.

raid.etymology: 源自古英语「rād」（原指骑行、远征），后引申为有计划的突然突袭或搜查

similar_words.description: 「raid」指有计划的突然突袭/搜查，「attack」指广义的攻击行为

definitions.example: The maintenance crew repaired the damaged rail to ensure train safety.
The residents railed against the government's new housing policy at the town hall meeting.

rail.etymology: 来自拉丁语「regula」（原指规则、直杆），后引申为栏杆、铁轨；动词义由“用杆打”引申为抱怨

similar_words.description: 「rail」指铁轨、栏杆或抱怨，「trail」指小径、痕迹或追踪

definitions.example: The transcontinental railroad connected the eastern and western parts of the country in 1869.

definitions.meaning: （不经充分讨论）强迫通过（法案等）
definitions.example: The ruling party tried to railroad the controversial tax bill through parliament.

railroad.etymology: 合成词，由「rail」（铁轨）加「road」（道路）构成；动词义由“铁路快速运输”引申为强迫推进

similar_words.description: 「railroad」多用于美式英语，「railway」多用于英式英语

definitions.example: The rainbow appeared in the sky shortly after the summer thunderstorm.

rainbow.etymology: 合成词，由「rain」（雨）加「bow」（弓）构成，因彩虹形状如弓形得名

similar_words.description: 「rainbow」指彩虹，「rainfall」指降雨量

definitions.example: Some cross-border e-commerce platforms raked in substantial profits during the global shopping festival.

rake.etymology: 源自古英语racan，本义为「耙、刮」，后引申为「搜刮（钱财）」等义

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，rack意为「支架；折磨」，rake意为「耙；搜刮」，词义差异较大

definitions.meaning: 召集，集合（民众以支持或反对某事）
definitions.example: Environmental organizations rallied thousands of volunteers to protest ocean pollution.

definitions.meaning: （政治或社会的）集会，群众大会
definitions.example: The presidential candidate held a campaign rally to attract young voters.

rally.etymology: 源自古法语rallier，由前缀re-（再、重新）+allier（联合）构成，本义为「重新集合」

similar_words.description: 同根词，ally意为「盟友；结盟」，rally侧重「召集盟友/民众的行动」

definitions.example: The research team selected a random sample of 300 students to conduct the survey.

random.etymology: 源自古法语randir，本义为「奔跑」，引申为「无定向的、随机的」

similar_words.description: 均含「任意的」之义，random侧重无规律的随机，arbitrary侧重主观武断的任意

definitions.example: The prices of organic vegetables range from 3 yuan to 10 yuan per kilogram.
This university provides a wide range of scholarship programs for overseas students.

range.etymology: 源自古法语range，本义为「行列、排列」，后引申为「范围、幅度」等义

similar_words.description: 均含「范围」之义，range侧重可量化的变化幅度，scope侧重活动或影响的范畴

definitions.example: After 15 years of service, he was promoted to the rank of colonel.
The authoritative journal ranked this research institution top in the field of artificial intelligence.

rank.etymology: 源自古英语ranc，本义为「行列、等级」，后引申为「分级、排列」等义

similar_words.description: 均含「等级」之义，grade侧重学习或质量的层级，rank侧重社会或职位的正式等级

definitions.example: Rap music, with its strong rhythm and social critique, is popular among young people worldwide.
The public rapped the local government for its inadequate disaster relief measures.

rap.etymology: 源自拟声词，模仿敲击声，后引申为「说唱（节奏敲击）、指责（言语敲击）」等义

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，tap意为「轻敲；利用」，rap意为「说唱；指责」，词义不同

definitions.example: The criminal law stipulates heavy penalties for the crime of rape.
Illegal logging has raped the ancient forest that was once a paradise for wildlife.

rape.etymology: 源自拉丁语rapere，本义为「抢夺、夺走」，后引申为「强奸、肆意破坏」等义

similar_words.description: 均含「破坏」之义，ravage侧重大规模的毁坏，rape侧重掠夺式的肆意破坏

definitions.example: The rapid popularization of 5G technology has accelerated the digital transformation of various industries.

rapid.etymology: 源自拉丁语rapidus，词根rap-（快速移动、抢夺），本义为「快速的」

similar_words.description: 均含「快速的」之义，swift侧重动作的敏捷，rapid侧重进程的快速

definitions.example: The black rhino is one of the rarest animals on the brink of extinction.
She ordered a rare steak because she prefers tender, juicy meat.

rare.etymology: 源自拉丁语rarus，本义为「稀疏的、稀少的」，后引申为「罕见的、半熟的」等义

similar_words.description: 均含「稀少的」之义，scarce侧重因短缺而稀少，rare侧重因罕见而稀少

definitions.meaning: 很少，难得（置于句首时句子需部分倒装）
definitions.example: Rarely do academic papers gain such extensive media coverage and public attention.

rarely.etymology: 由形容词rare（稀有的）+副词后缀-ly构成，意为「稀少地、很少」

similar_words.description: 均为否定频率副词，可互换，置于句首时均需部分倒装

definitions.example: Making a rash decision without conducting sufficient data analysis may result in huge economic losses for the enterprise.
Patients with this viral infection often develop a red rash on their chest and back.

rash.etymology: 源自中古英语rasch，原指动作仓促迅速，后引申为鲁莽轻率的含义，无常见可拆分词根词缀

similar_words.description: 「rash」多作形容词，表鲁莽轻率的；「rush」多作动词，表冲、匆忙行事

definitions.example: Laboratory rats are commonly used in psychological experiments to study learning and memory mechanisms.
The former gang member was labeled a rat after testifying against his accomplices in court.

rat.etymology: 源自古英语ræt，为基础名词，指代体型较大的啮齿类动物，无常见可拆分词根词缀

similar_words.description: 「rat」多指体型较大的家鼠、田鼠；「mouse」多指体型较小的小鼠、宠物鼠

definitions.example: The annual inflation rate of the country rose to 4.2% last month, arousing widespread concern among policymakers.
Most education experts rate the new online learning platform as effective for improving students' autonomous learning ability.

rate.etymology: 源自拉丁语rata（固定的金额），后引申出比率、速率、评估等多重含义

similar_words.description: 「rate」可表动态的速率、费率或评价动作；「ratio」特指两个数量的静态对比比值

definitions.example: The conclusion drawn from the small-scale survey is rather unconvincing due to the limited sample size.
Many conservationists would rather shut down the coal-fired power plant than see local residents suffer from air pollution.

rather.etymology: 源自古英语hrathor（更快地），为古形容词的比较级形式，后演变为表程度或偏好的副词

similar_words.description: 「rather」可修饰比较级或与would搭配表偏好；「fairly」仅表程度「相当地」，语气较弱

definitions.example: The ratio of undergraduate students to postgraduate students in the university is approximately 5:1, according to the latest enrollment report.

ratio.etymology: 源自拉丁语ratio（计算、理性），与rational为同根词，专指两个数量之间的对比比值

similar_words.description: 「ratio」特指两个数量的对比关系，常用搭配「A to B ratio」；「rate」侧重表速率、比率或评价

definitions.example: When facing unexpected crises, it is crucial for managers to stay calm and make rational judgments.
In the math test, students were required to convert the recurring decimal into a rational number in fractional form.

rational.etymology: 源自拉丁语rationalis，词根为「rat-」（表计算、理性），后缀「-al」为形容词后缀，表「与…相关的」

similar_words.description: 「rational」侧重符合逻辑、有理性的，与「感性的」相对；「reasonable」侧重公平合理、不过分的

definitions.example: The public health department warns that eating raw seafood may expose people to harmful bacteria and parasites.
The research team spent six months collating and analyzing the raw data collected from 2,000 respondents.

raw.etymology: 源自古英语hrēaw，原指未煮熟的，后引申为未加工、原始的含义，为基础形容词

similar_words.description: 「raw」侧重未经过任何处理的，如生食、原始数据；「crude」侧重粗糙的、粗略的，如原油、粗略估算

definitions.example: The gamma ray emitted by the supernova can be detected by astronomical telescopes orbiting the Earth.
Marine researchers found that the population of the giant manta ray has declined by 30% over the past decade.

ray.etymology: 源自古法语rai，源自拉丁语radius（射线、半径），与radius为同根词，指代放射状的线条或光线

similar_words.description: 「ray」指细小的、单束的光线或射线；「beam」指较粗的、成束的光线或光柱

definitions.example: The study finds that the safety razor is more environmentally friendly than disposable plastic razors.

razor.etymology: 源自古法语rasor，词根「ras-」表刮、擦，后缀「-or」表工具，意为用来刮毛的工具

similar_words.description: 「razor」指剃须刀，首字母为r；「eraser」指橡皮擦，首字母为e，拼写高度相似

definitions.meaning: 到达，抵达；达到（某水平、数量）
definitions.example: By the end of this century, the number of elderly people in China is expected to reach 400 million, posing challenges to the pension system.
Advanced communication technologies have extended the reach of remote education to remote rural areas.

reach.etymology: 源自古英语rǣcan，原指伸出手够取物体，后引申为到达、范围等多重含义

similar_words.description: 「reach」侧重到达某个地点、水平或范围；「achieve」侧重通过努力实现目标、取得成就

definitions.example: Many animals react to sudden loud noises by fleeing into dense cover.

react.etymology: re-（表示反向、回应）+ act（行动），字面义为‘作出回应行动’

similar_words.description: react侧重对刺激的即时、本能反应；respond侧重更正式、有意识的回应

definitions.example: The environmental science book has attracted a wide range of readers across different academic backgrounds.
Each college entrance exam essay is graded by two independent readers to ensure scoring fairness.

reader.etymology: read（阅读）+ -er（表示人/物的名词后缀），表‘从事阅读的人’

similar_words.description: reader可指普通读者或阅卷人；proofreader特指从事文稿校对的专业人员

definitions.example: Relevant research data is readily accessible via the university's digital library.

readily.etymology: ready（准备好的）+ -ily（副词后缀），表‘处于准备好的状态地’

similar_words.description: readily侧重无阻碍地、轻易地；willingly侧重主观乐意地

definitions.example: Extensive academic reading is a core requirement for postgraduate entrance examination candidates.
Her critical reading of the novel uncovers its hidden feminist themes.

reading.etymology: read（阅读）+ -ing（名词后缀，表动作或结果）

similar_words.description: reading指阅读行为或读物；writing指写作行为或作品

definitions.example: The real obstacle to the project is the shortage of qualified technical personnel.

real.etymology: 来自拉丁语realis，词根real-表‘真实’

similar_words.description: real侧重客观存在的、非虚构的；true侧重符合事实真相的

definitions.example: Many graduates only realise the importance of career planning after entering the workforce.

realise.etymology: real（真实的）+ -ise（动词后缀，表‘使成为’），字面义为‘使成为真实’

similar_words.description: realise侧重突然意识到某种情况；recognize侧重认出、承认已知的事物

definitions.example: It is unrealistic to expect all students to master advanced mathematics in one semester.

realistic.etymology: real（真实的）+ -ist（表‘信奉…的’后缀）+ -ic（形容词后缀），表‘符合真实情况的’

similar_words.description: realistic侧重符合客观实际的、不空想的；practical侧重实用的、可操作的

definitions.example: The harsh reality is that environmental degradation has reached an alarming level.

reality.etymology: real（真实的）+ -ity（名词后缀，表性质或状态）

similar_words.description: reality侧重客观存在的现实状况；truth侧重经过验证的事实真相

definitions.example: This breakthrough has opened up a new realm of research in artificial intelligence.

realm.etymology: 来自古法语realme，词根reg-表‘统治’，原指‘国王统治的区域’，后引申为‘领域’

similar_words.description: realm可指抽象领域或具体王国；domain侧重学术、专业等特定的专属领域

definitions.example: Students who invest consistent effort in vocabulary building will reap rewards in the reading comprehension section.

reap.etymology: 来自古英语ripan，原指‘收割谷物’，后引申为‘收获成果’

similar_words.description: reap指收割作物或收获成果；heap指堆积物品或大量的东西

definitions.example: The rear section of the museum houses ancient Egyptian artifacts unearthed in the 1920s.
She reared her three children alone while working as a part-time nurse in the local clinic.

rear.etymology: re-（表‘向后’或‘强化’）+ 隐含词根（表‘位置’或‘生长’），作形容词指空间上的后部，作动词指培育使成长

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，rear表‘后部的/抚养’，rare表‘稀有的、罕见的’

definitions.example: The professor’s requirement for a 500-word analytical essay is reasonable for undergraduate students.
The price of organic dairy products in this community store is more reasonable than in downtown supermarkets.

reasonable.etymology: reason（理由、理性）+ -able（形容词后缀，表‘能…的’）→能被理由支撑的→合理的

similar_words.description: 近义，reasonable侧重‘合乎情理、符合常识’，rational侧重‘有逻辑、基于理性的’

definitions.example: The researcher’s reasoning is supported by peer-reviewed data from three top academic institutions.

reasoning.etymology: reason（推理、理性）+ -ing（动名词后缀，表‘动作/过程’）→有逻辑的推理行为或过程

similar_words.description: 近义，reasoning侧重‘有逻辑的论证推理’，thinking侧重‘一般性的思考活动’

definitions.example: The central bank’s statement provided much-needed reassurance to investors amid market volatility.

reassurance.etymology: re-（表‘再次’）+ assurance（保证、确信）→再次给予保证→消除疑虑的安慰

similar_words.description: 同根，reassurance表‘再次安慰/保证’，assurance表‘自身的保证、自信’

definitions.example: The school principal tried to reassure parents that student safety was the institution’s top priority.

reassure.etymology: re-（表‘再次’）+ assure（使确信）→再次使确信→消除对方的疑虑

similar_words.description: 同根，reassure强调‘消除已有疑虑’，assure强调‘使对方确信某事实’

definitions.example: Local residents rebelled against the government’s plan to build a chemical plant near their neighborhood.
The rebel leader was arrested by government forces after a three-month manhunt.

rebel.etymology: re-（表‘相反、反对’）+ bel（词根，表‘战争’）→发起反对的战争→反叛；引申为反叛者

similar_words.description: 同根，rebel表‘反叛动作/反叛者’，rebellion表‘集体反叛行为’

definitions.example: The 1857 rebellion in India was a major uprising against British colonial rule.

rebellion.etymology: rebel（反叛）+ -lion（名词后缀，表‘行为/状态’）→集体发起的反叛行为

similar_words.description: 近义，rebellion侧重‘有组织的大规模反叛’，revolt侧重‘突发的小规模反抗’

definitions.example: The author recalls his experiences as a war correspondent in the final chapter of his memoir.
The electronics giant issued a recall of 200,000 laptops due to a battery overheating defect.

recall.etymology: re-（表‘再次’）+ call（唤起、召集）→再次唤起记忆→回忆；再次召集产品→召回

similar_words.description: 近义，recall侧重‘主动唤起清晰记忆’，recollect侧重‘努力回忆模糊细节’

definitions.meaning: 后退，退去；（问题、影响等）减弱
definitions.example: As the global pandemic receded, international travel restrictions were gradually lifted.

recede.etymology: re-（表‘向后’）+ cede（词根，表‘走、放弃’）→向后移动→后退；引申为影响减弱

similar_words.description: 近义，recede侧重‘逐渐、被动后退/减弱’，retreat侧重‘主动、有目的的撤退’

definitions.example: Recent surveys indicate that more young people prefer remote work over traditional office jobs.

recent.etymology: re-（表‘重新’）+ cent（词根，表‘开始’）→重新开始阶段的→最近的

similar_words.description: 近义，recent侧重‘时间上不久前的’，latest侧重‘顺序上最新的’

definitions.example: The university held a formal reception to welcome visiting professors from top global institutions.
The poor radio reception in the remote mountainous area made it hard to access emergency broadcasts.

reception.etymology: re-（回、向后）+ cept（拿、取）+ -ion（名词后缀），字面义为「拿回来」，引申为接待、接收

similar_words.description: 均以「-ception」结尾，perception指「感知、看法」，reception指「接待、接收」

definitions.example: During the 2008 global recession, thousands of small and medium-sized enterprises went bankrupt.

recession.etymology: re-（回、向后）+ cess（走、行进）+ -ion（名词后缀），字面义为「往回走」，引申为经济衰退

similar_words.description: 均以「re-」开头，recession指「经济衰退」，reception指「接待、接收」

definitions.example: The management book offers a practical recipe for building high-efficiency remote work teams.

recipe.etymology: re-（再、重复）+ cipe（拿、取），字面义为「反复抓取配料」，引申为食谱、方法

similar_words.description: 均以「reci-」开头，receipt指「收据、收条」，recipe指「食谱、方法」

definitions.example: The Nobel Prize recipient delivered an inspiring speech on climate change mitigation.

recipient.etymology: re-（回）+ cip（拿、取）+ -ient（表人的名词后缀），字面义为「拿取（物品）的人」，引申为接受者

similar_words.description: 均以「reci-」开头，recipe指「食谱、方法」，recipient指「接受者」

definitions.example: The two countries signed a reciprocal trade agreement to reduce tariffs on agricultural products.

reciprocal.etymology: re-（回）+ pro（向前）+ -cal（形容词后缀），字面义为「来回交互的」，引申为相互的、互惠的

similar_words.description: 均含「相互的」之义，mutual侧重共同拥有，reciprocal侧重有来有回的互惠性

definitions.example: The graduate student was asked to recite the core arguments of the academic paper in the thesis defense.

recite.etymology: re-（再、重复）+ cite（召唤、引用），字面义为「反复引用内容」，引申为背诵、叙述

similar_words.description: 为同根词，cite指「引用、引证」，recite指「背诵、叙述」

definitions.example: Reckless investment in high-risk assets led to the collapse of the financial company.

reckless.etymology: reck（顾虑、谨慎）+ -less（无…的形容词后缀），字面义为「无顾虑的」，引申为鲁莽的

similar_words.description: 拼写相近，reckless为形容词指「鲁莽的」，regardless为副词指「不管不顾地」

definitions.example: Economists reckon that the global inflation rate will remain above 2% in the next fiscal year.
The project manager reckoned the total cost of the infrastructure construction to be around 80 million yuan.

reckon.etymology: re-（加强语气）+ ckon（计算、考量），引申为估计、计算

similar_words.description: 均含「计算」之义，calculate侧重精确计算，reckon侧重粗略估计或主观判断

definitions.example: The local government launched a project to reclaim desert land for ecological agriculture development.
The legal team helped the elderly artist reclaim his stolen paintings from the black market.

reclaim.etymology: re-（回、重新）+ claim（呼喊、要求），字面义为「要求拿回」，引申为回收、取回

similar_words.description: 为同根词，proclaim指「宣布、宣告」，reclaim指「回收、取回」

definitions.example: Facial recognition technology has been widely applied in urban public security management.
Her groundbreaking research in renewable energy earned her international recognition from academic circles.

recognition.etymology: re-（再、重新）+ cogn（知道、认知）+ -ition（名词后缀），字面义为「重新认出」，引申为识别、认可

similar_words.description: 均以「-ization」结尾，realization指「实现、认识」，recognition指「认出、认可」

definitions.example: The security system can recognize facial features to grant access to authorized personnel only.

definitions.meaning: 承认，认可（合法性、价值等）
definitions.example: Many international academic journals recognize this study as a landmark contribution to climate science.

recognize.etymology: re-（再，重新）+ 「cogn」（知道，认知）+ -ize（动词后缀）→ 重新知晓→认出；承认

similar_words.description: 同根词，recognize为动词，recognition为对应名词，表「认出；认可」
近义，recognize侧重认出熟悉的人/物，identify侧重确认身份或特征

definitions.example: She could not recollect the exact data points mentioned in the professor’s lecture on behavioral economics.

recollect.etymology: re-（再）+ 「collect」（收集）→ 重新收集记忆碎片→回忆

similar_words.description: 近义，recollect侧重主动努力回想细节，recall侧重自然唤起记忆
同根词，collect为动词「收集」，recollect由其加前缀re-派生

definitions.example: The hiring panel recommended the candidate for a senior position in the corporate strategy department.

definitions.meaning: 建议，劝告（基于经验的正式建议）
definitions.example: Nutritionists recommend limiting processed sugar intake to reduce the risk of chronic diseases.

recommend.etymology: re-（表强调）+ 「commend」（推荐，称赞）→ 强调推荐/建议

similar_words.description: 同根词，commend侧重「称赞，表彰」，recommend侧重「推荐给他人」
近义，recommend更正式，基于经验的建议；suggest较随意，表提议

definitions.example: The independent mediator helped reconcile the two parties’ conflicting demands over the infrastructure project budget.

reconcile.etymology: re-（再）+ 「concil」（会议，协商）+ -e → 再次协商→和解；调和

similar_words.description: 同根名词，表「和解；调和」，reconcile为其动词形式
近义，reconcile侧重调和双方分歧达一致，resolve侧重解决问题本身

definitions.meaning: 记录，记载（客观事实的留存）
definitions.example: The archaeological record provides critical evidence of early human settlement in the Yangtze River Basin.
Researchers record participants’ physiological reactions to the stimulus in the controlled lab experiment.

record.etymology: re-（再）+ 「cord」（心，记住）→ 再次牢记→记录

similar_words.description: 同根词，record可作名/动，表「记录」；recorder为名词，表「记录员；录音机」
近义，record侧重客观事实的记录，document侧重作为证据的正式文件

definitions.meaning: 录音机，录像机；记录员，登记员
definitions.example: The qualitative research team used a digital voice recorder to capture participants’ verbal responses during in-depth interviews.

recorder.etymology: record（记录）+ -er（表人/物的名词后缀）→ 执行记录的人/工具→记录员；录音机

similar_words.description: 同根词，record可作名/动，含义更广；recorder仅作名词，表记录的人/工具
形近词，recorder侧重「记录行为」，reporter侧重「报道新闻的记者」

definitions.example: It took the Olympic athlete nearly six months to recover from the severe knee injury sustained during the qualifying round.
The local police department helped the foreign tourist recover her stolen passport and travel documents.

recover.etymology: re-（再）+ 「cover」（覆盖，遮蔽）→ 重新覆盖（找回被遮蔽的事物）→恢复；找回

similar_words.description: 同根名词，表「恢复；追回」，recover为其动词形式
近义，recover侧重自身状态的恢复，restore侧重外力使事物恢复原状

definitions.example: The patient’s rapid recovery after the minimally invasive surgery surprised the entire medical team.

recovery.etymology: recover（恢复）+ -y（名词后缀）→ 恢复的状态或过程→康复；追回

similar_words.description: 同根动词，表「恢复；追回」，recovery为其名词形式
近义，recovery侧重从负面状态恢复，renewal侧重更新、重启

definitions.example: Urban planning policies should prioritize allocating more green spaces for residents’ outdoor recreation and physical activity.

recreation.etymology: re-（再）+ 「creation」（创造）→ 重新创造（身心活力）→娱乐，消遣

similar_words.description: 形近词，recreation指「娱乐活动」，creation指「创造的行为或产物」
近义，recreation侧重主动参与的休闲活动，entertainment侧重被动接受的娱乐内容

definitions.meaning: 招募，招收（新成员、新兵、员工）
definitions.example: The top university is actively recruiting international students to enhance the diversity of its campus community.

definitions.meaning: 新兵，新成员（刚加入的人员）
definitions.example: The new military recruit completed the 12-week basic training program with outstanding performance evaluations.

recruit.etymology: re-（再）+ 「cruit」（成长，增加）→ 再次增加（人员）→招募

similar_words.description: 同根名词，表「招募（行为）」，recruit为其动词/名词形式
近义，recruit侧重机构主动招募他人，enroll侧重个人主动报名注册

definitions.example: The architectural blueprints include a large rectangle designated as the main exhibition hall.

rectangle.etymology: 由词根「rect-（正、直）」和名词「angle（角）」构成，指四个角为直角的四边形

similar_words.description: 均为几何图形类名词，拼写均以-gle结尾；区别为前缀含义不同：tri-表“三”，rect-表“正、直”

definitions.example: The university's academic committee decided to rectify the grading errors in the final exam papers.

rectify.etymology: 由词根「rect-（正、正确）」和动词后缀「-ify（使…）」构成，意为“使变正确”

similar_words.description: 均为-ify结尾的动词；区别为：rectify侧重“纠正已有错误”，verify侧重“核实信息真实性”

definitions.example: The same traffic congestion problem recurs during the morning rush hour every weekday.

recur.etymology: 由前缀「re-（再、又）」和词根「cur-（发生、跑）」构成，意为“再次发生”

similar_words.description: 均含词根「cur-」的动词；区别为：occur指“首次发生”，recur指“再次发生、复发”

definitions.example: Regular exercise and a balanced diet can help prevent the recurrence of heart disease.

recurrence.etymology: 由前缀「re-（再）」、词根「cur-（发生）」和名词后缀「-ence」构成，是recur的名词形式

similar_words.description: 均为-ence结尾的名词；区别为：occurrence指“发生的事件”，recurrence指“再次发生的状态”

definitions.example: The recurrent natural disasters have forced the local government to upgrade its emergency response system.

recurrent.etymology: 由前缀「re-（再）」、词根「cur-（发生）」和形容词后缀「-ent」构成，是recur的形容词形式

similar_words.description: 均为-ent结尾的形容词；区别为：recurrent指“反复出现的”，current指“当前的、现行的”

definitions.example: The government has launched a campaign encouraging residents to recycle household waste such as plastic bottles.

recycle.etymology: 由前缀「re-（再）」和名词「cycle（循环）」构成，意为“再次循环利用”

similar_words.description: 均含词根「cycle（循环、轮）」；区别为：recycle指“回收利用”，bicycle指“双轮自行车”

definitions.example: The new traffic rule is designed to reduce the number of traffic accidents in urban areas.

reduce.etymology: 由前缀「re-（回、向后）」和词根「duc-（引导）」构成，意为“引导向后退→减少”

similar_words.description: 均含词根「duc-」的动词；区别为：reduce指“减少数量/程度”，deduce指“逻辑推断”

reduction.etymology: 由前缀「re-（回）」、词根「duc-（引导）」和名词后缀「-tion」构成，是reduce的名词形式

similar_words.description: 均为-tion结尾的名词；区别为：reduction指“数量/程度的减少”，deduction指“扣除金额或逻辑推断”

definitions.example: The global economic recession led to the redundancy of over 500 workers in the automobile factory.

redundancy.etymology: 由前缀「re-（再）」、词根「und-（涌出、波动）」和名词后缀「-ancy」构成，意为“过多涌出→过剩、冗余”

similar_words.description: 均为-ancy结尾的名词；区别为：redundancy指“不必要的过剩（含贬义）”，abundance指“充足的丰富（含褒义）”

The redundant sentences in the academic paper were deleted to enhance its readability.

redundant.etymology: 由前缀「re-（再）」、词根「und-（涌出）」和形容词后缀「-ant」构成，是redundancy的形容词形式

similar_words.description: 均为-ant结尾的形容词；区别为：redundant指“多余的、不必要的（含贬义）”，abundant指“充足的、丰富的（含褒义）”

definitions.example: Coastal wetland reserves are covered with dense reeds, which serve as ideal habitats for migratory waterfowl.
A damaged reed will greatly affect the tone quality of a clarinet or saxophone.

reed.etymology: 源自古英语hreod，表芦苇，属于无复杂派生的基础名词，后引申出乐器簧片的含义

similar_words.description: 「reed」指芦苇或簧片，「reel」可表眩晕或卷轴，拼写仅末尾字母不同，易形近混淆

definitions.example: When the young athlete heard that he had won the championship, he reeled with excitement and nearly lost his balance.
The museum curator carefully restored a reel of silent film footage shot in the early 20th century.

reel.etymology: 源自古英语hreol，原指旋转的圆盘，核心含义与转动相关，后引申出眩晕、卷轴等义

similar_words.description: 「reel」可表眩晕或卷轴，「reed」指芦苇或簧片，拼写仅末尾字母不同，易形近混淆

definitions.example: In his academic paper on urbanization, the professor refers to three classic cases of city planning in Western Europe.
When translating economic texts, translators often need to refer to professional glossaries to ensure accuracy.

refer.etymology: re-（回、再）+ fer（带来、携带），字面义为带回相关内容，引申为提及、参考等核心义

similar_words.description: 二者均含词根fer，「refer」表提及、参考，「infer」表推断、推论，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.meaning: （纠纷的）仲裁人；（学术论文的）审稿人

referee.etymology: refer（参考、征询）+ -ee（动作承受者后缀），原指被征询意见做裁决的人，引申为裁判、审稿人等义

definitions.meaning: 参考，查阅；参考资料，参考文献

definitions.example: Crude oil needs to be refined in professional factories before it can be processed into gasoline and diesel.

definitions.meaning: 改进，完善（方法、理论、体系等）

similar_words.description: 「refine」表精炼、改良，「define」表下定义、阐明，拼写仅首字母不同，易形近混淆

definitions.meaning: 反射（光、热等）；认真思考，反思

definitions.meaning: （对某人能力、品行的）批评，指责
definitions.example: The frequent occurrence of safety incidents is a serious reflection on the factory’s neglect of management regulations.

reflection.etymology: reflect（反射、反思）+ -ion（名词后缀），表动作或结果，引申出反映、反思、批评等义

similar_words.description: 二者为同根词，「reflect」是动词，表反映、反思；「reflection」是对应的名词，表反映、反思等义

definitions.meaning: 改革，改良（制度、体系、机构等）
definitions.example: The government has promised to reform the outdated medical insurance system to reduce the burden on residents.

definitions.example: Taking a 10-minute walk outdoors can refresh the mind after hours of concentrated study.

refreshment.etymology: 由动词refresh（使恢复精力）加名词后缀-ment构成，表恢复精力的事物或引申为茶点

refuge.etymology: re-（回）+ 词根fug（逃，源自拉丁语fugere），字面义为「逃回去的地方」，即避难所

refugee.etymology: 由名词refuge（避难）加表人的后缀-ee构成，指寻求避难的人

definitions.example: His persistent refusal to follow the safety regulations led to a serious accident.

The municipal government has introduced a new policy to classify household refuse.

refuse.etymology: re-（相反）+ 词根fus（流），字面义为「流回去」，引申为动词「拒绝」；名词义由「被丢弃的无用之物」引申而来

definitions.example: The biologist cited genetic evidence to refute the controversial hypothesis about species evolution.

refute.etymology: re-（相反）+ 词根fut（打，源自拉丁语futere），字面义为「打回去」，引申为驳斥论点

definitions.example: Stephen Hawking is regarded as one of the greatest physicists of the 20th century.
The project plan shows little regard for the potential impact on local ecosystems.

regardless.etymology: 「re-（加强意义）+ gard（注视，来自单词regard）+ -less（形容词后缀，表‘无…的’），原指‘不被重视的’，后演变为副词表‘不顾、不管’」

definitions.example: Reforestation projects help regenerate degraded forest ecosystems in tropical regions.

regenerate.etymology: 「re-（再、重新）+ generate（v. 产生），由‘重新产生’引申为‘使再生、使复兴’」

definitions.example: The new regulatory regime for internet platforms aims to protect users' privacy.

regime.etymology: 「来自拉丁语regere（统治），原指‘统治方式’，后引申为‘政权、监管制度’」

similar_words.description: regime指‘政权/监管制度’；regimen指‘养生法/疗程’

definitions.example: The Arctic region has become a focal point of international climate negotiations.

region.etymology: 「reg-（统治、规则）+ -ion（名词后缀），原指‘被统治的区域’，后泛指‘地区、领域’」

similar_words.description: region指广义‘地区/区域’（含自然、政治）；district指行政划分的‘区’

definitions.example: All international students must register with the local immigration authority within 30 days of arrival.
The university maintains a register of all alumni who have donated to the scholarship fund.

register.etymology: 「re-（再、重复）+ gist（带来，来自拉丁语gerere‘携带、执行’），原指‘再次带来（记录）’，引申为‘登记、注册’，名词义由动词义衍生」

similar_words.description: register指一般‘登记/注册’；enroll侧重‘入学/入会的注册’

definitions.example: Many job seekers regret not having participated in more internships during their undergraduate years.
To his great regret, he missed the deadline for submitting his graduate application.

regret.etymology: 「re-（再、重复）+ gret（哭，来自古法语gret‘悲伤’），原指‘再次哭泣’，引申为‘后悔、遗憾’」

similar_words.description: regret为普通用词，表‘后悔/遗憾’；repent侧重‘带有道德/宗教意味的悔悟’

definitions.example: Regular participation in academic seminars can broaden students' research horizons.

regular.etymology: 「reg-（规则、统治）+ -ular（形容词后缀，表‘有…性质的’），指‘符合规则的’，引申为‘有规律的、常规的’」

definitions.example: The government has introduced policies to regulate the overheated real estate market.

similar_words.description: regulate侧重‘按规则管理/调节’；control侧重‘直接掌控/限制’

definitions.example: Strict environmental regulations have forced many factories to upgrade their pollution control equipment.

regulation.etymology: 「regulate（v. 管理、调节）+ -ion（名词后缀），引申为‘规章、管理’」

similar_words.description: regulation指官方制定的‘规章/条例’；rule指一般‘规则/守则’

definitions.example: The research team conducted a rehearsal of the experiment to identify potential operational errors.

rehearsal.etymology: 「rehearse（v. 排练，来自古法语rehercier‘重复说’）+ -al（名词后缀），指‘重复练习的过程’，即‘排练、演习’」

similar_words.description: rehearsal侧重‘文艺表演或实验的排练/演习’；drill侧重‘军事或技能的反复练习/演习’

definitions.example: The reign of Queen Elizabeth II spanned over seven decades, witnessing profound social changes.
A sense of optimism reigned among the researchers after the successful experiment.

reign.etymology: 源自拉丁语regere（统治），同根词有regime、region，表与统治、秩序相关的含义

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，reign表统治、占主导，rein表缰绳、控制（具象或抽象）

definitions.example: The central bank keeps a tight rein on money supply to curb inflation risks.
Parents should learn to rein in their children's excessive screen time.

rein.etymology: 源自古英语rǣn（缰绳），引申为抽象的控制含义

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，rein表控制（具象或抽象），reign表统治、占主导

definitions.meaning: 加强；强化（观点、结构、信念等）
definitions.example: The latest survey data reinforces the conclusion that remote work boosts employee productivity.

reinforce.etymology: re-（再）+ in-（进入）+ force（力量），指再次注入力量以加强

similar_words.description: 近义易混，reinforce表加强（观点、结构），enforce表实施（法规、规则）

definitions.meaning: 拒绝（接受、考虑）；驳回（申请、投稿等）
definitions.example: The academic journal rejected the manuscript due to its insufficient theoretical framework.

reject.etymology: re-（回）+ ject（扔），指把东西扔回去，引申为拒绝

similar_words.description: 近义易混，reject侧重因不符合标准而拒绝（如投稿、申请），refuse侧重主观不愿做某事

definitions.meaning: 感到高兴；欣喜（正式用语）
definitions.example: Archaeologists rejoice at the discovery of a well-preserved ancient tomb in central China.

rejoice.etymology: re-（加强）+ joice（joy的古英语变体），指感到极大的喜悦

similar_words.description: 近义易混，rejoice为正式用语，表因特定好事而欣喜；enjoy为日常用语，表享受某事物

definitions.example: The research team attempts to relate climate change to the decline of polar bear populations.
International students often struggle to relate to local peers at first.

relate.etymology: re-（再）+ late（源自拉丁语latus，表携带），指把事物再次带到一起，引申为联系

similar_words.description: 近义易混，relate侧重逻辑或情感上的关联，connect侧重物理或直接的连接

definitions.example: There is a significant statistical relation between sleep duration and cognitive function.

relation.etymology: relate的名词形式，-ion为名词后缀，表动作或状态

similar_words.description: 近义易混，relation侧重事物间的客观、抽象联系（如数据、现象），relationship侧重人或群体间的主观、情感关系

definitions.example: The study examines the relationship between teacher-student interaction and learning outcomes.

relationship.etymology: relation + -ship（表状态、性质的名词后缀），指关系的状态或性质

similar_words.description: 近义易混，relationship侧重人或群体间的主观、情感关系，relation侧重事物间的客观、抽象联系

definitions.example: The concept of beauty is relative and varies across different cultural contexts.
She visited her distant relatives in the countryside during the Spring Festival.

relative.etymology: relate的形容词形式，-ive为形容词后缀，表与…相关的、相对的

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，relative表相对的、与…有关的；relevant表相关的、切题的

definitions.meaning: 相对论（物理学术语）；相关性
definitions.example: Einstein's theory of general relativity transformed our understanding of gravity and space-time.

relativity.etymology: relative的名词形式，-ity为名词后缀，表性质，特指物理领域的相对论或抽象的相关性

similar_words.description: 同根易混，relativity为名词，特指相对论或抽象相关性；relative为形容词（相对的）或名词（亲属）

definitions.example: Students are advised to relax their tight schedules appropriately to avoid burnout during the postgraduate entrance examination preparation.

relax.etymology: re-（表示‘再、重新’）+ lax（表示‘松的’），由‘重新变得松弛’引申为放松

similar_words.description: 均含‘缓解’之意，relax侧重身心状态的放松，relieve侧重解除具体的痛苦、压力或负担

definitions.example: The official media will relay the latest policy announcement regarding postgraduate enrollment to the public nationwide.

relay.etymology: re-（表示‘再、又’）+ lay（表示‘放置’），反复放置以传递→转播、传达

similar_words.description: 词形相近，delay意为‘推迟、耽搁’，relay意为‘转播、传达’

definitions.example: The Ministry of Education will release the official outline for the national postgraduate entrance examination in early September.
The court ruled to release the small enterprise from part of its tax liabilities due to its financial difficulties.

release.etymology: re-（表示‘再、回’）+ leas（=lease，表‘松弛、放开’），松开束缚→释放、发布；引申为免除

similar_words.description: 均含‘发布’之意，release侧重发布信息、产品或释放对象，issue侧重官方发布文件、声明等

definitions.example: Candidates should only cite relevant academic literature to support their arguments in the postgraduate admission interview.

relevant.etymology: re-（表示‘再、回’）+ lev（表‘举、升’）+ -ant（形容词后缀），反复被提起的→相关的、切题的

similar_words.description: 均含‘相关的’之意，relevant侧重与当前主题切题、密切相关，related仅指存在某种联系

definitions.example: Researchers need to collect reliable data from authoritative databases to ensure the validity of their postgraduate thesis.

reliable.etymology: rely（表‘依赖’）+ -able（表‘可…的’），可依赖的→可靠的、可信赖的

similar_words.description: 词形相近，reliable意为‘可靠的’，liable意为‘有责任的、易…的’

definitions.example: Excessive reliance on cramming may lead to poor long-term retention of knowledge for postgraduate examinees.

reliance.etymology: rely（表‘依赖’）+ -ance（名词后缀，表状态），依赖的状态→依赖、依靠

similar_words.description: 词形相近，reliance意为‘依赖、依靠’，reliability意为‘可靠性、可信度’

definitions.example: Getting the admission notice brought great relief to the candidate who had prepared for the postgraduate exam for two years.
The ancient stone relief unearthed in the archaeological site provides valuable clues for historical research.

relief.etymology: re-（表示‘再、回’）+ lief（=lev，表‘轻’），使变轻→缓解、宽慰；引申为浮雕（凸起的造型）

similar_words.description: 均含‘缓解’之意，relief侧重痛苦、压力的减轻或精神宽慰，ease侧重状态的轻松、缓和

definitions.example: Regular physical exercise can help relieve the mental pressure of postgraduate examinees effectively.

relieve.etymology: re-（表示‘再、回’）+ liev（=lev，表‘轻’）+ -e，使变轻→缓解、减轻

similar_words.description: 均含‘减轻’之意，relieve侧重缓解痛苦、压力，release侧重释放对象或发布信息

definitions.example: The academic paper analyzes the influence of traditional religion on the social structure of rural areas in the 19th century.

religion.etymology: re-（表示‘再、回’）+ lig（表‘绑、约束’）+ -ion（名词后缀），重新约束行为的体系→宗教、宗教信仰

similar_words.description: 词形极为相近，religion意为‘宗教、宗教信仰’，region意为‘地区、区域’

definitions.example: The research focuses on the religious practices and cultural traditions of ethnic minorities in southwest China.

religious.etymology: religion（表‘宗教’）+ -ous（形容词后缀），与宗教相关的→宗教的、虔诚的

similar_words.description: 均含‘虔诚的’之意，religious还可指‘与宗教相关的’，pious仅指人对宗教的虔诚

definitions.example: Many humanities professors relish the opportunity to engage in in-depth discussions with curious graduate students.
The chef added a spoonful of relish to the sandwich to enhance its flavor.

relish.etymology: re-（强化语气）+lish（源自古英语表示「愉悦、喜爱」的词根，核心义为享受美好事物）

similar_words.description: 「relish」表喜爱、享受或调味品；「relieve」表减轻、缓解（痛苦等）或接替

definitions.example: The reluctant witness finally agreed to testify in court after being assured of protection.

reluctant.etymology: re-（反对、抗拒）+luct（源自拉丁语luctari，表「挣扎」）+-ant（形容词后缀，表「具有…性质的」）→ 内心挣扎抗拒的→ 不情愿的

similar_words.description: 「reluctant」是形容词，修饰人或行为；「reluctance」是名词，表不情愿、勉强的态度

definitions.meaning: 依赖，依靠；信赖（常与on/upon搭配）
definitions.example: Many small businesses rely on social media platforms to promote their products and services.

rely.etymology: re-（再次、反复）+ly（源自拉丁语ligare，表「捆绑」）→ 反复捆绑在一起→ 依赖、信赖

similar_words.description: 「rely」表依赖、信赖，需接on/upon；「relay」表转播、转达（信息或信号）

definitions.example: Despite the rapid advancement of digital media, print newspapers remain an important source of information for many elderly people.
Only a few old trees remain in the area after the large-scale urban construction.

remain.etymology: re-（回、向后）+main（源自拉丁语manere，表「停留、保持」）→ 回到停留的状态→ 保持、剩余

similar_words.description: 「remain」侧重状态的自然持续或事物的遗留；「maintain」侧重主动采取措施维持（秩序、设备等）

definitions.example: The remainder of the conference will focus on the practical implementation of the new environmental regulations.
The will specifies that the remainder of the estate goes to the deceased’s grandchildren.

remainder.etymology: remain（剩余）+-der（名词后缀，表「…的事物」）→ 剩余的事物→ 剩余部分

similar_words.description: 「remainder」侧重整体被取走或使用后的剩余部分；「remains」侧重遗留的遗迹、遗体或残留物

definitions.example: The remains of the meal were thrown into the trash can after the dinner party.
Archaeologists have unearthed the remains of a 12th-century castle in the northern region.

remains.etymology: remain（剩余）+-s（名词复数后缀）→ 多件剩余的事物→ 剩余物、遗迹

similar_words.description: 「remains」可指遗迹、遗体或残留物，语义范围广；「remainder」仅指整体扣除后的剩余部分

definitions.example: The leading economist remarked that the global economic recovery would take longer than expected.
Her controversial remarks on gender equality sparked a heated debate on social media.

remark.etymology: re-（再次、反复）+mark（标记、标注）→ 反复标注（观点）→ 评论、谈论

similar_words.description: 「remark」较正式，侧重简短或随口的评论；「comment」更通用，可指书面或口头的详细评论

definitions.meaning: 显著的，非凡的，值得注意的
definitions.example: The research team has made remarkable breakthroughs in the field of cancer treatment over the past two years.

remarkable.etymology: remark（评论）+-able（形容词后缀，表「值得…的」）→ 值得被评论的→ 非凡的、显著的

similar_words.description: 「remarkable」侧重因出众、奇特而引人注意；「notable」侧重因重要、著名而值得注意

definitions.example: The government has proposed a series of measures as a remedy for the widening income gap.
The new software is designed to remedy the security flaws found in the previous version.

remedy.etymology: re-（再次、恢复）+med（源自拉丁语mederi，表「治疗」）+-y（名词/动词后缀）→ 恢复健康的方法→ 治疗法、补救

similar_words.description: 「remedy」可指药物、补救方法或纠正行为，适用范围广；「therapy」侧重专业的治疗手段（如心理、物理治疗）

definitions.example: The sound of the school bell reminds me of the happy days I spent in my junior high school.

remind.etymology: re-（再次、重新）+mind（头脑、记忆）→ 重新进入头脑→ 提醒、使想起

similar_words.description: 「remind」侧重外界因素（人/物）使人想起；「remember」侧重主动回忆、记起（过去的事）

definitions.example: Archaeologists found a small remnant of an ancient pottery jar at the excavation site.

remnant.etymology: re-（回、再）+ 「manere」（拉丁语，停留）的变体mn- + -ant（名词后缀）→ 停留下来的部分→残余

similar_words.description: remnant多指残留的小部分（如遗迹、物质碎片），remainder多指剩余的整体部分（如数量、集合）

definitions.example: The remote mountain village had no access to electricity until 2010.
There is only a remote possibility that the team will win the championship this year.

remote.etymology: re-（离开）+ 「movere」（拉丁语，移动）的变体mot- + -e → 移开的→遥远的

similar_words.description: remote强调距离极远或偏僻（如偏远村庄），distant侧重空间/时间上的一般距离

definitions.example: The removal of industrial waste from the river has significantly improved local water quality.

removal.etymology: re-（离开）+ 「movere」（拉丁语，移动）+ -al（名词后缀，表行为/结果）→ 移开的行为/结果→清除、搬迁

similar_words.description: removal是名词（指移动、清除的行为或结果），remove是动词（指移走、清除的动作）

definitions.example: The teacher asked students to remove all personal items from desks before the final exam.

remove.etymology: re-（离开）+ 「movere」（拉丁语，移动）+ -e → 移开→移走、清除

similar_words.description: remove强调「移走、去除（使其脱离原处或消失）」，move仅指「改变位置的移动」

definitions.meaning: （文学、艺术的）复兴；文艺复兴（首字母大写）
definitions.example: The Harlem Renaissance of the 1920s was a golden age for African American literature and art.

renaissance.etymology: re-（重新）+ 「nasci」（拉丁语，出生）的变体naiss- + -ance（名词后缀）→ 重新出生→复兴

similar_words.description: renaissance多指大规模、持久的文化/艺术复兴（如文艺复兴），revival多指短期的复苏或复兴

definitions.example: The court's ruling rendered the controversial policy unconstitutional.
The construction crew will render the interior walls with white plaster next week.

render.etymology: re-（回）+ 「reddere」（拉丁语，归还、给予）→ 给予回→提供、使成为

similar_words.description: render侧重「使处于某状态、提供服务/翻译」，provide侧重「提供物品、资源等实体或抽象支持」

definitions.example: The library allows readers to renew borrowed books online if no one has reserved them.

renew.etymology: re-（重新）+ new（新的）→ 重新变新→更新、恢复

similar_words.description: renew侧重「重新开始、续订或恢复活力」，restore侧重「修复破损物或恢复原状」

definitions.example: The heritage foundation allocated funds to renovate the 19th-century castle and open it to the public.

renovate.etymology: re-（重新）+ 「novus」（拉丁语，新的）的变体nov- + -ate（动词后缀）→ 重新变新→翻新、修复

similar_words.description: renovate特指修复/翻新建筑物等实体物，renew多指更新抽象事物（如合同、活力）

definitions.example: Many young graduates choose to rent small apartments near their workplaces due to high housing prices.
The hiker found a small rent in his backpack after climbing through thorny bushes.

rent.etymology: （核心义）来自古法语「rente」（收入、租金）；（僻义）来自「rend」（撕裂）的过去分词→ 撕裂的口子

similar_words.description: rent指「付费租用/出租（使用权）」，lend指「借出（物品，可无偿或有偿）」

definitions.example: Technicians spent three days repairing the damaged satellite communication equipment.
The celebrity held a press conference to repair his tarnished public image.

repair.etymology: re-（重新）+ 「parare」（拉丁语，准备）的变体pair- → 重新准备好→修理、修复

similar_words.description: repair多指修复较复杂的物品（如机器、建筑），mend多指修补简单的物品（如衣服、鞋子）

definitions.example: Many college graduates have to work part-time to repay their student loans within the specified period.

repay.etymology: re-（再、回）+ pay（支付）→ 再次支付以清偿债务或回报恩情

similar_words.description: repay表偿还或报答，replay表重放（录音/录像）或重播（节目）

definitions.example: In English learning, it is essential to repeat new words regularly to consolidate memory.

repeat.etymology: re-（再次）+ peat（来自拉丁语petere，表“践行、寻求”）→ 再次践行同一行为

similar_words.description: repeat可作动词或名词，repetition仅作名词，侧重重复的行为或状态

definitions.example: The research team repeatedly adjusted the experimental parameters to obtain reliable data.

repeatedly.etymology: re-（再次）+ peat（践行）+ -ed（形容词后缀）+ -ly（副词后缀）→ 反复践行地

similar_words.description: repeatedly侧重动作多次重复，continuously侧重动作无间断持续

definitions.example: The immune system can repel harmful pathogens to maintain the body’s internal balance.

repel.etymology: re-（回、反）+ pel（来自拉丁语pellere，表“推”）→ 推回以阻挡

similar_words.description: repel表击退、抵制或使厌恶，compel表强迫、迫使

definitions.example: Many middle-aged people repent having wasted their youth on trivial and meaningless matters.

repent.etymology: re-（再次）+ pent（来自拉丁语paenitere，表“痛苦”）→ 再次因过往行为感到痛苦

similar_words.description: repent表后悔、懊悔，repaint表重新粉刷或绘画

definitions.meaning: （表演者的）全部剧目/曲目；（人的）全部技能
definitions.example: The famous violinist’s repertoire includes masterpieces by Bach, Beethoven and Brahms.

repertoire.etymology: re-（再次）+ pert（来自拉丁语portare，表“携带”）+ -oire（法语名词后缀）→ 可反复携带表演的节目集合

similar_words.description: repertoire侧重具体的全部剧目或技能，repertory侧重储备或轮换剧目剧院

definitions.example: Excessive repetition in teaching materials may dampen students’ enthusiasm for independent thinking.

repetition.etymology: re-（再次）+ pet（来自拉丁语petere，表“践行”）+ -ition（名词后缀）→ 再次践行的行为或状态

similar_words.description: repetition仅作名词，表重复的行为或状态；repeat可作动词或名词

definitions.meaning: 替换，取代；更换；放回原处
definitions.example: Renewable energy sources are predicted to replace fossil fuels as the main energy supply by 2050.

replace.etymology: re-（再、回）+ place（放置）→ 重新放置以替代原有事物或放回原位

similar_words.description: replace侧重替换旧的或损坏的事物，displace侧重迫使某人或某物离开原位

definitions.example: The company is actively recruiting a replacement for the outgoing marketing director.

replacement.etymology: re-（再）+ place（放置）+ -ment（名词后缀）→ 用于替换的人或事物

similar_words.description: replacement是名词，表替换物或接替者；replace是动词，表替换

definitions.example: The applicant took three days to reply to the university’s admission offer with a formal letter.

reply.etymology: re-（回）+ ply（来自拉丁语plicare，表“折叠、回应”）→ 折回以回应

similar_words.description: reply侧重正式的书面或口头答复，answer为通用的回答，适用范围更广

definitions.example: According to a New York Times reporter, the new environmental regulation will take effect nationwide next month.

reporter.etymology: 词根词缀：re-（回、向后）+ port（携带、传递）+ -er（表人的名词后缀），指将新闻信息传递回来的从业者

similar_words.description: 均指新闻从业者；「reporter」侧重现场采访、即时报道的记者，「correspondent」侧重驻外或特定领域的通讯员

definitions.example: The marble sculpture represents the unyielding spirit of the working class during the industrial revolution.

represent.etymology: 词根词缀：re-（再、重新）+ present（呈现），指重新呈现某群体的意志或某事物的特征
