similar_words.description: 均有「象征」义；「represent」可指人或具体事物代表抽象概念，「symbolize」侧重以特定符号象征抽象意义

definitions.example: The international organization dispatched a representative to mediate the trade dispute between the two countries.
The research selected a representative sample of 3,000 residents to ensure the validity of the survey results.

representative.etymology: 词根词缀：re-（再）+ present（呈现）+ -ative（兼作名词、形容词的后缀），指能呈现整体特征的人或事物

similar_words.description: 均指「代表」；「representative」侧重代表群体的一般成员，「delegate」侧重被授权参加特定会议的正式代表

definitions.meaning: 抑制；压抑（感情、欲望）；镇压（叛乱）
definitions.example: Psychological studies show that people who repress their grief for a long time are more likely to develop mental health problems.

repress.etymology: 词根词缀：re-（回、向后）+ press（压），指将情绪、欲望或反抗等压回去

similar_words.description: 均有「抑制、镇压」义；「repress」侧重主观压抑自身情绪，「suppress」侧重外力强制镇压（叛乱、信息等）

definitions.example: The paper analyzes the social impacts of political repression on marginalized communities in the region.

repression.etymology: 词根词缀：re-（回）+ press（压）+ -ion（名词后缀），指压抑的行为或状态

similar_words.description: 均为「抑制、镇压」的名词形式；「repression」侧重心理或情感层面的压抑，「suppression」侧重外力的强制压制

definitions.example: The repressive government imposed strict censorship on all media outlets and banned public gatherings.

repressive.etymology: 词根词缀：re-（回）+ press（压）+ -ive（形容词后缀），表示具有压抑或镇压性质的

similar_words.description: 均指「压迫的」；「repressive」侧重政治上的专制镇压，「oppressive」侧重气候、环境或生活的压抑感

definitions.example: The project manager reproached the team for neglecting the key quality standards during the production process.
Her constant reproach for his carelessness eventually led to the breakdown of their partnership.

reproach.etymology: 词根词缀：re-（回、反）+ proach（接近），原指走近反驳对方，后引申为责备

similar_words.description: 均有「责备」义；「reproach」侧重因对方失职或过错而指责，语气较委婉；「blame」侧重归咎于对方，语气较强

definitions.meaning: 繁殖；生殖；复制；再现（实验结果等）
definitions.example: The independent lab failed to reproduce the groundbreaking findings reported in the prestigious scientific journal.

reproduce.etymology: 词根词缀：re-（再、重新）+ produce（生产），指重新生产、繁殖或复制

similar_words.description: 均有「复制」义；「reproduce」可指生物繁殖或实验结果再现，「duplicate」侧重实物的完全复制

definitions.example: Paleontologists have discovered fossil remains of a previously unknown ancient reptile in the Gobi Desert.

reptile.etymology: 词根词缀：re-（再）+ pt（爬行）+ -ile（表物的名词后缀），指爬行的脊椎动物

similar_words.description: 均为脊椎动物类别；「reptile」指爬行动物（如蛇、蜥蜴），「amphibian」指两栖动物（如青蛙、蟾蜍）

definitions.example: The nation was established as a sovereign republic after gaining independence from colonial rule in 1960.

republic.etymology: 词源：源自拉丁语「res publica」，意为「公共事务」，指由公民或选举代表治理的无君主政体

similar_words.description: 均与政体相关；「republic」指无君主的共和制国家，「democracy」指人民掌握权力的民主政体

definitions.example: Republican politicians in the U.S. Congress have proposed a series of bills related to economic stimulus.
The candidate won the support of a large number of republicans in the midterm elections.

republican.etymology: 由名词「republic」（共和国）加后缀「-an」（表与…相关的人/物的形容词/名词后缀）构成

similar_words.description: republican指共和党的/共和党人，与美国政党相关；democratic指民主党的/民主党人，二者为考研阅读中常见的政治对立类词汇

definitions.example: The prestigious university has maintained a global reputation for academic excellence over the past century.

reputation.etymology: 由前缀「re-」（反复）+ 词根「put」（认为、评价）+ 后缀「-ation」（名词后缀）构成，指「众人反复评价形成的名声」

similar_words.description: reputation侧重长期积累的、有好坏之分的名声；fame侧重短期的、轰动性的名气

definitions.example: The research team requested additional funding from the National Science Foundation to complete the project.
Her request for a leave of absence to attend the academic conference was granted by the department.

request.etymology: 由前缀「re-」（再）+ 词根「quest」（寻求、请求）构成

similar_words.description: request指礼貌、自愿的请求；require指强制的、规定的要求

definitions.example: All graduate students are required to complete a thesis defense before obtaining their degrees.

require.etymology: 由前缀「re-」（再）+ 词根「quire」（要求、寻求）构成

similar_words.description: require指强制的、规定的要求；request指礼貌、自愿的请求

definitions.example: The job posting lists a master’s degree in economics as a basic requirement for applicants.

requirement.etymology: 由动词「require」（要求）加后缀「-ment」（名词后缀）构成

similar_words.description: requirement指必须满足的硬性规定或条件；request指个人或机构提出的自愿请求

definitions.example: Coast Guard crews rescued 12 sailors from the sinking cargo ship in the Atlantic Ocean.
The international community launched a rescue mission to aid the disaster-stricken region.

rescue.etymology: 由前缀「re-」（再）+ 词根「scue」（=secur，安全）构成，指「使重新获得安全」

similar_words.description: rescue侧重从危险中紧急营救；save可指各种场景下的拯救，范围更广

definitions.example: Cutting-edge research in neuroscience has revealed new insights into how the human brain processes information.
The team has been researching the impact of social media on adolescent mental health for three years.

research.etymology: 由前缀「re-」（再）+ 动词「search」（搜索）构成，指「反复搜索、探究」

similar_words.description: research指系统的、深入的学术研究；survey指针对特定群体的问卷调查或普查

definitions.example: There is a remarkable resemblance between the ancient fossil and the modern species, which supports the theory of evolution.

resemblance.etymology: 由前缀「re-」（再）+ 词根「sembl」（相似，来自simul，意为「相同」）+ 后缀「-ance」（名词后缀）构成

similar_words.description: resemblance侧重人或事物外观、外在特征的相似；similarity可指各种维度的相似，范围更广

definitions.example: The newly discovered species closely resembles a type of dinosaur that lived 150 million years ago.

resemble.etymology: 由前缀「re-」（再）+ 词根「sembl」（相似）+ 后缀「-e」（动词后缀）构成

similar_words.description: resemble指相似；assemble指集合、组装，二者词形相近易混淆

definitions.example: Many rural residents resent the government’s new policy that restricts agricultural water usage.

resent.etymology: 由前缀「re-」（相反、反向）+ 词根「sent」（感觉）构成，指「产生相反的、负面的感觉」

similar_words.description: resent指怨恨；consent指同意、准许，二者词形相近易混淆

definitions.meaning: 预订（座位、酒店等）；保留意见
definitions.example: Many international students make flight and accommodation reservations in advance before studying abroad.

reservation.etymology: re-（回、再）+ serv（保持）+ -ation（名词后缀）→ 保持住的权益或安排，引申为「预订；保留意见」

similar_words.description: 「reserve」可作动/名词，表「保留；储备」，「reservation」为名词，侧重「预订行为或保留的意见」

definitions.example: The university reserves the right to adjust the curriculum based on students' feedback.
The central bank increased its foreign exchange reserves to stabilize the currency market.

reserve.etymology: re-（回、再）+ serv（保持）+ 无后缀 → 主动保持住，引申为「保留；预订；储备」

similar_words.description: 「preserve」侧重「保护、保存（使完好）」，「reserve」侧重「主动留存（以备使用）或预订」

definitions.meaning: 水库；（知识、人才等的）储备库
definitions.example: The large reservoir provides irrigation water for over 100,000 mu of farmland in the region.

reservoir.etymology: re-（回、再）+ serv（保持）+ -oir（表地点的名词后缀）→ 保持物资（尤指水）的场所，引申为「水库；储备库」

similar_words.description: 「reserve」作名词指「储备的物资」，「reservoir」指「储存物资的容器或场所」

definitions.example: The diplomat's official residence is located in the most secure area of the capital.

residence.etymology: re-（加强）+ sid（坐、居住）+ -ence（名词后缀）→ 坐着（居住）的地方或状态，引申为「住宅；居住」

similar_words.description: 「resident」指「居住的人（居民）」，「residence」指「居住的场所或行为」

definitions.example: Local residents have organized a volunteer team to clean the urban green belts regularly.
The hospital has a team of resident doctors on duty 24 hours a day.

resident.etymology: re-（加强）+ sid（坐、居住）+ -ent（表人或形容词的后缀）→ 居住的人或状态，引申为「居民；居住的」

similar_words.description: 「inhabitant」侧重「长期居住在某地的人」，「resident」侧重「合法居住在某区域的人（如城镇居民）」

definitions.example: After the data breach incident, the chief technology officer decided to resign from his post.

definitions.meaning: 使顺从；使接受（不快的事）
definitions.example: She had to resign herself to the reality that her research project would not be funded.

resign.etymology: re-（离开）+ sign（标记、签字）→ 签字解除职务后离开，引申为「辞职」；进一步引申为「使顺从（接受离开或无法改变的事）」

similar_words.description: 「assign」意为「分配、指派」，「resign」意为「辞职、顺从」，词形相近易混淆

definitions.example: The environmental group launched a campaign to resist the construction of the chemical plant near the river.

resist.etymology: re-（相反）+ sist（站立）→ 站在对立面，引申为「抵抗、抵制」

similar_words.description: 「persist」意为「坚持（做某事）」，「resist」意为「抵抗、抵制」，词形相近易混淆

definitions.example: The new vaccine has been proven to enhance the body's resistance to the influenza virus.

resistance.etymology: re-（相反）+ sist（站立）+ -ance（名词后缀）→ 抵抗的行为或状态，引申为「抵抗；阻力；抵抗力」

similar_words.description: 「resist」为动词表「抵抗」，「resistance」为名词表「抵抗的行为、阻力或抵抗力」

definitions.meaning: 抵抗的；有抵抗力的；耐…的
definitions.example: Drought-resistant crops have significantly increased agricultural productivity in arid areas.

resistant.etymology: re-（相反）+ sist（站立）+ -ant（形容词后缀）→ 能站在对立面的，引申为「有抵抗力的、耐…的」

similar_words.description: 「resistance」为名词表「抵抗、抵抗力」，「resistant」为形容词表「有抵抗力的、耐…的」

definitions.example: The resolute decision of the board of directors helped the company overcome the financial crisis.

resolute.etymology: re-（完全）+ solut（松开、解决）+ -e → 完全解决问题的，引申为「坚决的、果断的」

similar_words.description: 「absolute」意为「绝对的、完全的」，「resolute」意为「坚决的、果断的」，词形相近易混淆

definitions.example: The UN Security Council adopted a resolution calling for an immediate ceasefire in the conflict zone.
High-resolution satellite images are essential for monitoring deforestation in tropical rainforests.

resolution.etymology: re-（加强语气）+ solv（松开、解决）+ -ution（名词后缀，表动作结果），源自拉丁语resolutio，原义为「松开、解决」

similar_words.description: 二者同根，resolve多作动词表「解决；下决心」，resolution是其名词形式，表「决议；决心」

definitions.example: She resolved to tackle the root cause of the team's low productivity instead of making temporary adjustments.
His unshakable resolve to complete his PhD dissertation impressed his academic advisor.

resolve.etymology: re-（加强语气）+ solv（松开、解决）+ -e（动词/名词后缀），同根于resolution

similar_words.description: 二者同根，resolve多作动词，resolution是其名词形式，表「决议；决心」

definitions.example: Many developing countries resort to export subsidies to boost their domestic manufacturing sectors.
Mountain resorts have become popular destinations for urban residents seeking a break from daily stress.

resort.etymology: re-（再次）+ sort（源自拉丁语sortiri，抽签、分配，引申为「求助」），原义为「求助于抽签」，后引申为「诉诸；度假胜地」

similar_words.description: 二者拼写接近，resort表「诉诸；度假胜地」，resource表「资源；智谋」，易混淆

definitions.example: The Ministry of Education has allocated additional financial resources to improve infrastructure in rural schools.
Her quick resource in handling the unexpected equipment failure saved the entire field experiment.

resource.etymology: re-（再次）+ source（来源），原义为「可再次获取的来源」，即「资源」，后引申为「智谋」

similar_words.description: 二者拼写接近，resource表「资源；智谋」，resort表「诉诸；度假胜地」，易混淆

definitions.example: In this respect, the new carbon pricing policy has achieved better results than expected in reducing emissions.
All participants in the academic forum must respect the intellectual property rights of other researchers.

respect.etymology: re-（再次）+ spect（看），原义为「再次观看、看重」，引申为「尊重；（看待的）方面」

similar_words.description: 二者同根且拼写接近，respect可作名词/动词，respective是形容词，表「分别的」

definitions.example: After the strategy meeting, each department director returned to their respective offices to draft implementation plans.

respective.etymology: re-（再次）+ spect（看）+ -ive（形容词后缀，表「与…相关的」），原义为「各自看待的」，即「分别的」

similar_words.description: 二者同根且拼写接近，respective是形容词表「分别的」，respect可作名词/动词表「尊重；方面」

definitions.example: The pharmaceutical company responded promptly to public concerns about the side effects of its new vaccine.
Nearly 80% of the patients responded positively to the novel immunotherapy treatment.

respond.etymology: re-（回）+ spond（承诺、回应），源自拉丁语respondere，原义为「回应承诺」，引申为「回应、作出反应」

similar_words.description: 二者同根，respond是动词表「回应」，response是其名词形式表「回应；反应」

definitions.example: The professor's detailed response to the student's question clarified the core logic of game theory.
The study measured participants' physiological response to different levels of noise pollution.

response.etymology: re-（回）+ spond（回应）+ -se（名词后缀），为respond的名词形式

similar_words.description: 二者同根，response是名词表「回应」，respond是其动词形式表「回应；作出反应」

definitions.example: Corporate social responsibility has been widely recognized as a key factor in a company's sustainable development.

responsibility.etymology: re-（回）+ spons（回应）+ -ibility（名词后缀，表性质），为responsible的名词形式，原义为「可回应承诺的性质」，即「责任」

similar_words.description: 二者同根，responsibility是名词表「责任」，responsible是其形容词形式表「负责的」

definitions.example: The project manager is responsible for coordinating all cross-departmental tasks and meeting deadlines.
Poor ventilation in the laboratory is responsible for the frequent occurrence of respiratory discomfort among researchers.

responsible.etymology: re-（回）+ spons（回应）+ -ible（形容词后缀，表「可…的」），原义为「可回应承诺的」，即「负责的」

similar_words.description: 二者同根，responsible是形容词表「负责的」，responsibility是其名词形式表「责任」

definitions.meaning: 焦躁不安的；难以休息的
definitions.example: After waiting for the postgraduate admission result for a week, she grew increasingly restless and could not concentrate on her daily tasks.

restless.etymology: 由词根「rest（休息）」加否定后缀「-less」构成，表“无法休息的”，引申为“焦躁不安的”

similar_words.description: 二者均含词根「rest」，restless指人因焦虑等情绪焦躁不安，restive指人或动物难管束、躁动

definitions.example: The government has taken targeted measures to restore public confidence in the domestic education system.

restore.etymology: 由前缀「re-（再，重新）」加词根「store（安置，储存）」构成，表“重新安置至原有状态”，引申为“恢复；修复”

similar_words.description: 二者均含“恢复”义，restore侧重恢复原有状态或修复物品、体系，recover侧重人恢复健康或找回失物

definitions.example: Researchers were advised to restrain themselves from drawing hasty conclusions based on limited experimental data.

restrain.etymology: 由前缀「re-（向后）」加词根「strain（拉紧，束缚）」构成，表“向后拉紧以控制”，引申为“抑制；约束”

similar_words.description: 二者均含“限制”义，restrain侧重抑制情绪、冲动或行为，restrict侧重通过规则、条件限制范围或数量

definitions.example: The strict economic restraints imposed by the central bank have effectively curbed the excessive growth of housing prices.

restraint.etymology: 由动词「restrain（抑制）」加名词后缀「-t」构成，表“抑制的行为或措施”

similar_words.description: 二者均为表“限制”的名词，restraint侧重主动的情感克制或行为约束，restriction侧重官方或规则设定的硬性限制

definitions.example: Many top universities restrict the number of cross-disciplinary applicants to ensure the quality of postgraduate training.

restrict.etymology: 由前缀「re-（加强语气）」加词根「strict（拉紧，严格）」构成，表“严格拉紧以限定范围”，引申为“限制；约束”

similar_words.description: 二者均含“限制”义，restrict侧重通过规则限制范围或数量，restrain侧重抑制情绪或行为

definitions.example: The groundbreaking result of the genetic research has been published in the internationally renowned journal *Nature*.
Long-term exposure to air pollution has resulted in a significant increase in respiratory diseases among urban residents.

result.etymology: 由前缀「re-（回）」加词根「sult（跳）」构成，表“跳回来的事物”，引申为“结果”；动词义表“引发结果”

similar_words.description: 二者均含“结果”义，result为通用中性词，consequence侧重不良或重大的后果

definitions.meaning: 作为结果的；因而发生的
definitions.example: The resultant rise in global sea levels has posed a severe threat to coastal cities around the world.

resultant.etymology: 由名词「result（结果）」加形容词后缀「-ant（表“有…性质的”）」构成，表“由结果引发的”

similar_words.description: 二者均为result的形容词形式，resultant更正式，常用于学术或官方语境，resulting更通用

definitions.example: After a three-week winter break, the postgraduate research seminars will resume next Tuesday as scheduled.
Applicants for the PhD program are required to submit a detailed resume along with their academic transcripts.

resume.etymology: 由前缀「re-（再）」加词根「sume（拿，取）」构成，动词义表“重新拿起（工作、学习）”；名词义“简历”为借自法语的常用义

similar_words.description: 二者均含词根「sume（拿）」，resume表重新开始或简历，assume表假定、承担或呈现

definitions.example: The company’s annual revenue from online retail has increased by 22% compared with the previous fiscal year.
This organic skincare brand retails its products exclusively through official online stores.

retail.etymology: 由前缀「re-（再）」加词根「tail（切割，分割）」构成，表“将货物再分割成小份销售”，引申为“零售”

similar_words.description: 二者均为商务领域表销售方式的词，retail指面向消费者的零售，wholesale指面向商家的批发

definitions.example: Many research institutions offer competitive salaries and welfare packages to retain outstanding young scholars.

retain.etymology: 由前缀「re-（保持，持续）」加词根「tain（拿，持）」构成，表“持续拿着不失去”，引申为“保持；保留”

similar_words.description: 二者均含词根「tain（拿）」，retain侧重保留原有状态、物品或人才，sustain侧重维持生命、关系或支撑

definitions.example: Studies show that active learning strategies can significantly enhance the retention of new information.

retention.etymology: re-（回、向后）+ tent（持有，源自动词retain的词根）+ -ion（名词后缀），表示保持的状态或行为

similar_words.description: retention意为保留、保持；detention意为拘留、扣押

definitions.example: After working as a research professor for 35 years, she decided to retire and focus on her personal writing.

retire.etymology: re-（回、向后）+ tire（<拉丁语> tirare，拉），表示拉回、引退

similar_words.description: retire多指因年龄或工龄满退休；resign多指主动辞职

definitions.meaning: （作动词）反驳、回嘴；（作名词）反驳的话
definitions.example: When criticized for his flawed experiment design, the scholar retorted that critics had ignored key control variables.

retort.etymology: re-（回、反）+ tort（<拉丁语> torquere，扭曲），表示拧回去、反过来说→反驳

similar_words.description: retort意为反驳；distort意为扭曲、歪曲

definitions.meaning: （作动词）撤退、退缩；（作名词）撤退、退避
definitions.example: Facing overwhelming opposition, the government was forced to retreat from its controversial policy proposal.

retreat.etymology: re-（回、向后）+ treat（<拉丁语> trahere，拉），表示拉回去→撤退

similar_words.description: retreat多指因压力或困难撤退、退缩；retire多指退休或主动退出

definitions.example: Users can retrieve relevant academic papers from the database by entering specific keywords.

retrieve.etymology: re-（回、重新）+ trieve（<拉丁语> trovere，找到），表示重新找到→取回、检索

similar_words.description: retrieve侧重找回丢失或存储的事物（如信息、物品）；retake侧重重新夺取、重新拍摄

definitions.example: In retrospect, the decision to expand the company’s overseas business was overly optimistic.

retrospect.etymology: re-（回、向后）+ tro（看，<拉丁语> specere）+ -spect（复合看的词根），表示向后看→回顾

similar_words.description: retrospect意为回顾；respect意为尊重、方面

definitions.example: The paper emphasizes the role of retrospection in improving teaching effectiveness through self-evaluation.

retrospection.etymology: re-（回）+ tro（看）+ -spect（看）+ -ion（名词后缀），是retrospect的正式名词形式

similar_words.description: retrospection指对过去事件的回顾；introspection指对内心思想的反省

definitions.meaning: 回顾的；追溯的，常修饰研究、分析等
definitions.example: The research team conducted a retrospective analysis of 500 patient records to identify risk factors.

retrospective.etymology: re-（回）+ tro（看）+ -spect（看）+ -ive（形容词后缀），表示向后看的→回顾的

similar_words.description: retrospective意为回顾的、追溯的；prospective意为预期的、未来的，二者常成对出现在学术语境中

definitions.example: The newly published study reveals a strong correlation between sleep quality and academic performance.

reveal.etymology: re-（去除）+ veal（<拉丁语> velum，面纱），表示去掉面纱→揭示

similar_words.description: reveal意为揭示、透露；conceal意为隐藏、隐瞒，二者为反义易混词

definitions.meaning: 揭示、透露；被揭示的真相
definitions.example: The revelation of widespread corruption in the department led to a series of government reforms.

revelation.etymology: re-（去除）+ vel（面纱）+ -ation（名词后缀），是reveal的名词形式

similar_words.description: revelation多指惊人的真相被揭露；disclosure多指一般信息的公开或透露

definitions.example: Many tragic plots in classic literary works stem from the desire for revenge.

revenge.etymology: re-（表示‘回、反’）+ 词根venge（表示‘惩罚、复仇’，源自拉丁语vindicare），字面义为‘反惩’，引申为报复、复仇

similar_words.description: 「avenge」多指为他人或正义复仇，强调正当性；「revenge」多指为个人私怨报复，强调主观恶意
「revenue」指收入，「revenge」指报复，拼写仅末尾字母不同，易混淆

definitions.meaning: 财政收入，税收；（企业的）营业收入
definitions.example: Government revenue from value-added tax accounts for a large proportion of national fiscal income.

revenue.etymology: re-（表示‘回’）+ 词根ven（表示‘来’，源自拉丁语venire）+ 名词后缀-ue，字面义为‘回来的款项’，引申为收入、税收

similar_words.description: 「income」多指个人收入，「revenue」多指政府或企业的营收、税收
「revenge」指报复，「revenue」指收入，拼写仅末尾字母不同，易混淆

definitions.meaning: v. 反转，颠倒；n. 相反情况，背面；adj. 反向的
definitions.example: To reverse the decline in global biodiversity, cross-border cooperation is urgently needed.

reverse.etymology: re-（表示‘回、反’）+ 词根vers（表示‘转’，源自拉丁语vertere）+ 后缀-e，字面义为‘转回’，引申为反转、颠倒

similar_words.description: 「converse」作动词表交谈、作形容词表逻辑上的相反；「reverse」侧重动作上的反转、位置上的反向
「inverse」侧重数学或逻辑上的反向对应，「reverse」侧重动作或状态的反转

definitions.meaning: v. 复习，回顾；评审，审查；n. 复习，回顾；评审报告
definitions.example: The core academic journal will review all submitted manuscripts within two months.

review.etymology: re-（表示‘再、重新’）+ 词根view（表示‘看’），字面义为‘再看一遍’，引申为复习、回顾、评审

similar_words.description: 「review」侧重回顾、评审或复习已有内容；「revise」侧重修改、订正原有内容

definitions.meaning: 修改，修订（文稿、计划等）；复习（英式用法）
definitions.example: The author revised the research paper to address the reviewers' critical comments.

revise.etymology: re-（表示‘再、重新’）+ 词根vis（表示‘看’，源自拉丁语videre）+ 后缀-e，字面义为‘再审视’，引申为修改、修订

similar_words.description: 「revise」侧重对内容的实质性修改；「review」侧重对内容的回顾评审

definitions.meaning: 使复苏，使复兴；恢复（活力、记忆、经济等）
definitions.example: Local cultural support policies have helped revive the traditional handicraft industry in rural areas.

revive.etymology: re-（表示‘再、重新’）+ 词根viv（表示‘活、生命’，源自拉丁语vivere）+ 后缀-e，字面义为‘再活过来’，引申为复苏、复兴

similar_words.description: 「survive」指经历灾难后幸存；「revive」指使事物恢复活力、复兴

definitions.example: The oppressed factory workers revolted against the employer's harsh exploitation.
Many consumers revolt at the idea of buying genetically modified food without clear labeling.

revolt.etymology: re-（表示‘反、逆’）+ 词根volt（表示‘转’，源自拉丁语volvere），字面义为‘反方向转’，引申为反叛；进一步引申为（情感上）反感、厌恶

similar_words.description: 「revolt」多指小规模、突发性的反叛；「revolution」指彻底的社会政治或技术革命

definitions.meaning: 革命（社会政治的）；重大变革（技术、产业的）
definitions.example: The Digital Revolution has fundamentally changed people's way of communication and work.

revolution.etymology: re-（表示‘再、完全’）+ 词根volut（表示‘转’，源自拉丁语volvere）+ 名词后缀-ion，字面义为‘完整转一圈’，引申为彻底变革、革命

similar_words.description: 「evolution」指渐进的进化、演变；「revolution」指突发的、彻底的变革

definitions.meaning: adj. 革命的，革命性的；n. 革命者
definitions.example: The new medical breakthrough has brought revolutionary changes to the treatment of rare diseases.

revolutionary.etymology: 由名词「revolution（革命）」加形容词/名词后缀-ary构成，表‘与革命相关的（人或事物）’

similar_words.description: 「revolutionary」指革命的、颠覆性的；「evolutionary」指进化的、渐进式的

definitions.example: The academic discussion revolved around the ethical boundaries of artificial intelligence research.

revolve.etymology: re-（表示‘反复、环绕’）+ 词根volv（表示‘转’，源自拉丁语volvere）+ 后缀-e，字面义为‘反复转动’，引申为旋转、围绕…展开

similar_words.description: 「revolve」指物理或话题上的旋转、围绕；「evolve」指生物或事物的渐进进化、发展

definitions.example: Volunteers often get intangible rewards such as a sense of fulfillment beyond material gains.
The university will reward outstanding students with full scholarships for their academic achievements.

reward.etymology: 词根re-（回，再）+ ward（守护，引申为报酬），原指履行守护职责后获得的回报

similar_words.description: reward侧重因付出或功绩获得的回报（可物质可精神），award侧重官方或机构正式授予的奖项（如奖金、证书）

definitions.example: The rhythm of seasonal changes affects the migration patterns of many bird species.

rhythm.etymology: 来自希腊语rhythmos（流动的规律），词根rhy-（流），原指事物流动的有序规律

similar_words.description: rhythm指节奏、韵律（侧重节奏规律），rhyme指押韵（侧重语音呼应）

definitions.example: The doctor examined the patient’s chest to check for any fractured ribs after the traffic accident.

rib.etymology: 源自古英语ribb，指人体或动物的肋骨，引申为形似肋骨的长条状物

similar_words.description: rib是名词，指肋骨/肋状物；rub是动词，指摩擦、揉搓

definitions.example: The environmental scientist used a thin ribbon of biodegradable material to mark the protected wetland boundary.

ribbon.etymology: 源自古法语riban，与rib（肋骨）同源，因形似肋条的长条形态得名，原指装饰用丝带

similar_words.description: rib指肋骨/肋状物（实体结构），ribbon指细长的丝带/带状物（装饰或功能用）

definitions.meaning: 使摆脱；使去掉（常与of搭配）
definitions.example: Many local governments are launching campaigns to rid urban areas of unlicensed street vendors to maintain order.

rid.etymology: 源自古英语ryddan，指清除、清理，引申为使摆脱不利事物

similar_words.description: rid是动词，指使摆脱；rod是名词，指杆、棒

definitions.meaning: 谜语；谜团（比喻义为未解之谜）
definitions.example: The origin of the prehistoric cave paintings remains a riddle that has puzzled archaeologists for centuries.

riddle.etymology: 源自古英语rǣdels，与read（解读）同源，原指需要解读的谜语，引申为未解之谜

similar_words.description: riddle侧重需巧妙解答的谜语或带文学性的未解之谜，puzzle侧重复杂难解的智力题或实际难题

definitions.example: The agricultural expert advised farmers to plant crops along the ridge to improve water drainage in the field.

ridge.etymology: 源自古英语hrycg，与back（背部）同源，原指动物的脊背，引申为山脊、田垄等隆起的长条结构

similar_words.description: ridge指隆起的长条结构（如山脊、田垄），bridge指连接两岸的桥梁

definitions.example: The academic committee rejected the ridiculous proposal due to its lack of theoretical basis and empirical data.

ridiculous.etymology: 词根rid-（笑）+ 形容词后缀-ic + -ulous（多…的），原指引人发笑的，引申为荒谬的

similar_words.description: ridiculous侧重因滑稽可笑而荒谬，absurd侧重因违背逻辑或常理而荒谬

definitions.example: The military historian analyzed the design evolution of standard infantry rifles during the 20th century.
The investigative reporter rifled through the confidential archives to uncover evidence of political corruption.

rifle.etymology: 源自古法语rifler（刮、抢），原指枪管内的膛线（刮制而成），引申为步枪；动词义由「刮动」引申为快速翻找

similar_words.description: rifle作名词指步枪，作动词指翻找（泛指物品）；riffle作动词指迅速翻阅（书页）

definitions.meaning: 僵硬的；严格的（尤指规则、制度）
definitions.example: The rigid hierarchy of the traditional organization made it difficult for junior employees to propose innovative ideas.

rigid.etymology: 词根rig-（硬、直）+ 形容词后缀-id，原指物体僵硬的，引申为规则、制度死板不容变通的

similar_words.description: rigid侧重规则或结构的死板、不容变通，strict侧重要求或标准的严格、不放松

definitions.example: The research project was subject to rigorous scrutiny by the academic committee before publication.

rigorous.etymology: 源自词根「rig」（表「僵硬、严格」），先加名词后缀「-or」构成名词「rigour」，再加形容词后缀「-ous」转化为形容词，表「符合严格标准的」。

similar_words.description: 「rigid」侧重「僵硬的、死板的（形容规则或物体）」，「rigorous」侧重「严格的、缜密的（形容审查、标准等）」。

definitions.example: The rigour of the postgraduate entrance examination ensures the selection of qualified candidates.

rigour.etymology: 源自词根「rig」（表「僵硬、严格」），加英式名词后缀「-our」构成，美式拼写为「rigor」。

similar_words.description: 「rigour」是名词，表「严格的性质或状态」；「rigorous」是其对应的形容词，表「严格的」。

definitions.example: The rim of the ancient bronze mirror was carved with delicate floral patterns.

rim.etymology: 源自古英语「rim」，表「边缘、边界」，无复杂词缀，可具象联想各类物体的外围边界。

similar_words.description: 「rim」泛指各类物体的边缘；「brim」特指容器的上沿或满溢的边缘（如「the brim of a cup」）。

definitions.example: The peaceful protest unexpectedly escalated into a violent riot that disrupted public order.

riot.etymology: 源自古法语「rioter」，原指「争吵、打闹」，后引申为「大规模的无秩序暴力骚乱」。

similar_words.description: 「riot」侧重无明确政治诉求的无秩序骚乱；「revolt」侧重有组织、有政治诉求的反叛或起义。

definitions.example: Please handle the ancient manuscript with care to avoid ripping its fragile pages.

rip.etymology: 源自中古英语「rippen」，表「撕裂、扯开」，为拟声词（模拟撕裂的声音）。

similar_words.description: 「rip」侧重突然、用力地撕裂；「tear」为普通用词，指一般的撕扯或破裂。

definitions.example: The farmers harvested the ripe wheat before the heavy rain arrived.
By the end of the 19th century, the conditions were ripe for industrial reform in Europe.

ripe.etymology: 源自古英语「ripe」，原指「果实成熟的」，后引申为「时机、条件准备充分的」。

similar_words.description: 「ripe」侧重果实或时机的成熟；「mature」侧重人、思想或生物体的发育成熟。

definitions.example: The number of students applying for postgraduate programs has risen steadily over the past decade.
A new generation of digital payment platforms began to rise in the early 2010s.

rise.etymology: 源自古英语「risan」，表「向上移动、起身」，后引申为「数量增长、事物兴起」。

similar_words.description: 「rise」为不及物动词，无被动形式（如「sun rises」）；「raise」为及物动词，可接宾语（如「raise salary」）。

definitions.example: Financial advisors always remind investors to evaluate the potential risk before making decisions.
The journalist risked her life to report on the humanitarian crisis in the war zone.

risk.etymology: 源自意大利语「risco」，原指「航海中的危险」，后引申为「各类可评估的风险」。

similar_words.description: 「risk」侧重可预见、可评估的潜在风险；「danger」侧重具体、迫近的危险。

definitions.example: Many traditional ethnic groups still practice their ancient wedding rituals to preserve cultural heritage.
The university’s graduation ceremony includes several ritual procedures that have been followed for decades.

ritual.etymology: 源自词根「rit」（=「rite」，表「仪式」），加后缀「-ual」（表「与…相关的」），可作名词或形容词。

similar_words.description: 「rite」特指具体的宗教或正式仪式；「ritual」可指仪式的总称或日常的例行惯例。

definitions.example: The two automobile manufacturers have been fierce rivals in the global market for over 50 years.
Her proficiency in academic writing rivals that of the most experienced scholars in her field.

rival.etymology: 源自拉丁语「rivalis」，原指「同饮一河水的人」（因争夺水资源产生竞争），后引申为「竞争对手」。

similar_words.description: 「rival」侧重有长期对抗性、实力相当的对手；「competitor」泛指各类参与竞争的主体。

definitions.meaning: （大型动物）吼叫；（机器、车辆）轰鸣
definitions.example: As the wildfire spread across the savanna, elephants roared in panic and fled toward the water source.

roar.etymology: 拟声词，模拟咆哮、轰鸣的声响，无典型考研可拆分词缀

similar_words.description: 「howl」侧重狼、狗的嚎叫或人因痛苦的哭喊，「roar」侧重大型动物的吼叫或机器的轰鸣

definitions.example: The desert sun roasted the expedition team, forcing them to take shelter in a cave.
The academic committee roasted the researcher for fabricating experimental data in his paper.

roast.etymology: 来自古法语rostir，表「烤」，无典型考研可拆分词缀

similar_words.description: 「toast」侧重烤面包片或为…祝酒，「roast」侧重烤大块食材、暴晒或严厉批评

definitions.meaning: 抢劫，盗取；使丧失（常用结构rob sb of sth）
definitions.example: The hackers robbed the company of confidential customer data through a phishing attack.

rob.etymology: 来自古法语rober，表「抢劫」，无典型考研可拆分词缀

similar_words.description: 「rob」侧重以暴力、威胁夺取，后接sb/sth + of sth；「steal」侧重秘密窃取，后直接接被偷物

definitions.meaning: 长袍，礼服；（法官、牧师的）法衣，祭服
definitions.example: The Supreme Court justice entered the courtroom wearing a black wool robe with a velvet collar.

robe.etymology: 来自古法语robe，表「衣服」，无典型考研可拆分词缀

similar_words.description: 「gown」侧重女性晚礼服、学士服或手术服，「robe」侧重法官、牧师的正式法衣或宽松长袍

definitions.example: The automotive factory has replaced 30% of manual workers with industrial robots to improve production efficiency.

robot.etymology: 来自捷克语robota（劳役），由科幻作家卡雷尔·恰佩克创造，无典型考研可拆分词缀

similar_words.description: 「automaton」侧重无自主意识的固定程序自动机器，「robot」为通用机器人术语，使用范围更广

definitions.meaning: 强健的，健壮的；（体制、经济）稳固的，强劲的
definitions.example: The country's robust manufacturing sector has driven its economic growth for three consecutive quarters.

robust.etymology: 来自拉丁语robustus（坚硬的），词根robur-表「橡树、力量」，无典型考研可拆分词缀

similar_words.description: 「vigorous」侧重人精力充沛、活动有活力的，「robust」侧重身体强健或体系、经济稳固的

definitions.example: Geologists analyzed the sedimentary rock layers to determine the age of the ancient lakebed.

definitions.meaning: 使震惊，使震动（指抽象领域）
definitions.example: The sudden resignation of the CEO rocked the company's stock price by 15% in a single day.

rock.etymology: 来自古英语rocc，表「岩石」，动词义源自摇晃动作的引申，无典型考研可拆分词缀

similar_words.description: 「stone」侧重小块的普通石头，「rock」侧重大块的岩石、岩层；动词义无近义混淆

definitions.example: The space agency launched a new rocket to deploy a weather satellite into polar orbit.
The demand for electric vehicle batteries has rocketed, leading to a shortage of raw materials.

rocket.etymology: 来自意大利语rocchetto（纺锭，因火箭形状类似纺锭），无典型考研可拆分词缀

similar_words.description: 「soar」侧重高飞、价格飙升，语气更正式；「rocket」侧重快速、急剧的上升，常用语经济领域

definitions.example: The mechanical engineer replaced the worn metal rod in the engine to prevent malfunction.

rod.etymology: 来自古英语rodd，表「棒」，无典型考研可拆分词缀

similar_words.description: 「pole」侧重长而粗的杆（如电线杆、旗杆），「rod」侧重细而硬的杆（如铁棒、钓竿）

definitions.example: The United Nations plays a pivotal role in mediating international conflicts and promoting peace.

role.etymology: 来自法语rôle（原指演员的角色台词卷），无典型考研可拆分词缀

similar_words.description: 「function」侧重事物的具体功能，「role」侧重人或组织在特定场景中的作用或身份

definitions.example: When the flash flood hit, large wooden logs began to roll down the steep riverbank.
The college keeps an official roll of all graduates from the past 50 years.

roll.etymology: 源自原始日耳曼语词根*roll-，表‘转动、滚动’义，后引申出‘名单’等义

similar_words.description: roll 表滚动、名单，role 表角色、作用，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The award-winning novel explores the tender romance between a rural teacher and a wandering poet.

romance.etymology: 来自古法语romanz，原指用罗曼语创作的叙事诗，后引申为浪漫史、传奇故事义

similar_words.description: romance 是名词，表浪漫史；romantic 是形容词，表浪漫的，同根易混词性

definitions.meaning: 浪漫的；充满浪漫色彩的
definitions.example: The ancient town with cobblestone streets and river views has a deeply romantic atmosphere.
His romantic plan to solve the energy crisis overnight was dismissed by experts.

romantic.etymology: 名词romance的形容词形式，后缀-ic表示形容词属性，表‘与浪漫相关的’

similar_words.description: romantic 是形容词，表浪漫的；romance 是名词，表浪漫史，同根易混词性

definitions.example: The heavy snowfall last winter caused the roof of the old village barn to collapse.

roof.etymology: 源自古英语hrof，原指房屋的顶部覆盖结构，后引申为‘顶部’义

similar_words.description: roof 表屋顶、顶部，root 表根、根源，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: Researchers claim that the root of social inequality lies in unequal access to education.

root.etymology: 源自古英语rōt，原指植物的地下部分，后引申为‘根源、根基’等抽象义

similar_words.description: root 表根、根源，roof 表屋顶、顶部，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The professional climbers used a thick, durable rope to secure themselves to the mountain ridge.

rope.etymology: 源自古英语rāp，指由纤维、金属丝等编织而成的长条捆扎或牵引物

similar_words.description: rope 表绳索，ripe 表成熟的，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The campus garden is filled with pink and white roses that bloom every May.

definitions.meaning: （不规则动词rise的过去式）上升；起身
definitions.example: As the sun rose above the horizon, the mist over the lake gradually disappeared.

rose.etymology: 名词义源自古英语rōse（蔷薇科观赏植物），动词义为不规则动词rise的过去式

similar_words.description: rose 是rise的过去式，同时表玫瑰；rise 是动词原形，表上升，易混屈折形式

definitions.example: If stored in a damp basement, fresh fruits and vegetables will rot within a few days.

rot.etymology: 源自古英语rotian，特指有机物因霉菌、潮湿等因素而腐烂变质

similar_words.description: rot 表腐烂、腐朽，rat 表老鼠，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The new rotary engine design has significantly improved the fuel efficiency of small aircraft.

definitions.meaning: 旋转式机器（如旋转发动机）
definitions.example: The factory installed a high-speed rotary to streamline the packaging process.

rotary.etymology: 源自拉丁语rotarius，词根rot-表‘转动’，后缀-ary表形容词性，意为‘与转动相关的’

similar_words.description: rotary 是形容词，表旋转的；rotate 是动词，表（使）旋转，同根易混词性

definitions.example: The Earth rotates on its axis once every 24 hours, causing the cycle of day and night.
The hospital rotates medical staff between emergency and general wards to avoid burnout.

rotate.etymology: 源自拉丁语rotare，词根rot-表‘转动’，后缀-ate表动词性，意为‘（使）转动’，后引申出‘轮换’义

similar_words.description: rotate 是动词，表（使）旋转；rotary 是形容词，表旋转的，同根易混词性

definitions.example: Rotten food waste in municipal landfills releases methane, a greenhouse gas 25 times more potent than carbon dioxide.

rotten.etymology: 源自名词「rot」（腐烂）加形容词后缀「-en」，表「具有腐烂性质的」

similar_words.description: 「rot」为动词/名词，表‘腐烂（物）’；「rotten」为其形容词形式，表‘腐烂的；腐朽的’

definitions.example: The research team provided a rough estimate of the project’s total cost before launching detailed calculations.

rough.etymology: 源自古英语「ruh」，表「表面不平整的」

similar_words.description: 「rough」侧重表面粗糙或数据粗略；「tough」侧重物体坚韧或任务艰巨

definitions.example: Roughly 35 percent of the world’s freshwater reserves are stored in underground aquifers.

roughly.etymology: 源自形容词「rough」加副词后缀「-ly」，表「以粗略的方式」

similar_words.description: 「rough」为形容词，表‘粗糙的；粗略的’；「roughly」为其副词形式，表‘粗略地；大约’

definitions.example: Instead of giving a direct answer, the politician made a roundabout statement to avoid public scrutiny.

roundabout.etymology: 复合词，由「round」（环绕的）加「about」（围绕）构成，表「绕圈子的」

similar_words.description: 「round」表‘圆形的；环绕的’；「roundabout」侧重表达或行动迂回、不直接

definitions.example: The documentary about environmental pollution roused public awareness of climate change.

similar_words.description: 「rouse」侧重唤醒具体的人或激起明确的情绪；「arouse」更正式，常指激起抽象情感或欲望

definitions.example: The newly opened trade route has boosted cross-border e-commerce between the two countries.

route.etymology: 源自古法语「rute」，表「行进的道路」

similar_words.description: 「route」指具体的行进或运输路线；「routine」指固定的惯例、常规

definitions.example: Following a consistent daily routine helps graduate students balance study and rest effectively.

routine.etymology: 源自名词「route」（路线）加名词后缀「-ine」，表「固定的路线→日常惯例」

similar_words.description: 「routine」指固定的日常惯例或程序；「route」指具体的行进路线

definitions.example: The participants sat in the back row of the conference room to avoid being noticed.
The committee members had a heated row over the allocation of research funds.

row.etymology: 核心义（排）源自古英语「rāw」；僻义（争吵）为方言引申义，原指「大声叫嚷」

similar_words.description: 「row」表‘排；争吵’；「raw」表‘生的；未加工的’

definitions.example: The royal commission was established to investigate the corruption scandal in the government.

royal.etymology: 源自拉丁语「regalis」（国王的），与词根「reg-」（统治）同源

similar_words.description: 「royal」指与王室相关的；「loyal」指忠诚的、忠心的

definitions.example: The European royalty gathered for the international summit on cultural heritage preservation.
The renowned scholar earned a substantial royalty from the sales of his academic monograph.

royalty.etymology: 源自形容词「royal」加名词后缀「-ty」，表「王室身份」；版税义为引申义（原指王室征收的税费，后引申为作者的知识产权收益）

similar_words.description: 「royal」为形容词，表‘王室的’；「royalty」为其名词形式，表‘王室成员；版税’

definitions.example: Researchers found that rubbing the affected area can ease muscle tension slightly.

rub.etymology: 源于古英语的基础动作动词，无考研需掌握的复杂词缀

similar_words.description: rub为动词表‘擦、摩擦’，rug为名词表‘小地毯’，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: Rubber is widely used in automobile manufacturing for its elasticity and durability.

rubber.etymology: 由动词rub（擦）+-er（表物）构成，原指用来擦拭的橡皮，后引申为橡胶

similar_words.description: rubber为名词表‘橡胶、橡皮’，源于动词rub（擦），二者为同根词

definitions.meaning: 垃圾；废物；无意义的言论
definitions.example: Scholars dismissed the theory as academic rubbish with no empirical evidence to support it.

rubbish.etymology: 由rub（原指磨损的碎屑）+-ish（名词后缀）构成，表废弃的物品或无价值的言论

similar_words.description: rubbish为名词表‘垃圾、废物’，rubber为名词表‘橡胶、橡皮’，二者拼写与词根均相近易混淆

definitions.example: The crown of the royal family is inlaid with a large ruby at its center.

ruby.etymology: 源于拉丁语ruber（红色），表红色的宝石或深红色

similar_words.description: ruby为名词表‘红宝石、深红色’，rub为动词表‘擦、摩擦’，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: His rude remarks during the academic seminar offended several visiting professors.

rude.etymology: 源于古法语的基础形容词，无考研需掌握的复杂词缀

similar_words.description: rude多形容人的‘粗鲁无礼’，crude多形容事物‘粗糙、未加工’，二者拼写与含义均相近易混淆

definitions.example: The woolen rug in the study has been worn out after years of frequent use.

rug.etymology: 源于古英语的基础名词，无考研需掌握的复杂词缀

similar_words.description: rug为名词表‘小地毯’，rub为动词表‘擦、摩擦’，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The sudden flood ruined the crops in the southern region and caused huge economic losses.
The ruins of the ancient Roman amphitheater attract thousands of tourists every year.

ruin.etymology: 源于拉丁语ruina（倒塌），表因倒塌或破坏而形成的废墟或毁坏的行为

similar_words.description: ruin强调彻底毁坏至无法修复的状态，destroy强调通过外力摧毁、破坏，二者含义相近易混淆

definitions.example: The new rule requires all graduate students to submit a research proposal before the end of the semester.
The queen ruled the small kingdom with wisdom and justice for over 40 years.

rule.etymology: 源于拉丁语regula（规则），同根词根reg-表‘统治、规则’

similar_words.description: rule为通用的‘规则、统治’，regulation为正式的‘规章、条例’，二者为同根形近近义易混淆

definitions.example: The ruler of the ancient dynasty was known for his emphasis on cultural development.

ruler.etymology: 由动词rule（统治、规则）+-er（表人/物）构成，表统治的人或用来测量的尺子

similar_words.description: ruler为名词表‘统治者、尺子’，源于动词rule，二者为同根词

definitions.example: The rumor about the company’s upcoming layoffs spread quickly on social media platforms.

rumor.etymology: 源于古法语rumor（噪音、传闻），表未经证实的消息或传闻

similar_words.description: rumor为美式拼写的‘谣言、传闻’，rumour为英式拼写，二者含义完全相同，拼写略有差异易混淆

definitions.example: The government has launched a series of policies to boost economic development in rural regions.

rural.etymology: 源自拉丁语ruralis，词根rur-（意为「乡村」）加形容词后缀-al

similar_words.description: rural指乡村的，urban指城市的，为考研常考的城乡对立主题核心词，易记混

definitions.meaning: 冲，奔；匆忙行事；（人群的）蜂拥，热潮
definitions.example: When the fire alarm rang, students rushed out of the classroom immediately.

rush.etymology: 源自古英语rysan（冲，猛动），无明显复杂词缀

similar_words.description: 二者均表「冲」，rush侧重匆忙、慌乱的动作，dash侧重快速且有力的冲刺

definitions.example: The old iron gate was covered with rust after years of exposure to rain and sun.

rust.etymology: 源自古英语rūst，与德语rost同源，指金属氧化形成的锈层

similar_words.description: 拼写形近易混，rust指锈或生锈，rural指乡村的

definitions.meaning: 车辙；（生活或工作的）刻板状态，惯例（考研常考后者）
definitions.example: Many young people fear getting stuck in a rut after graduation and choose to pursue further study.

rut.etymology: 源自古英语rūt（车辙），原指车轮压出的沟，引申为重复不变的生活轨迹

similar_words.description: 拼写形近易混，rut指车辙或刻板状态，rust指锈或生锈

definitions.meaning: 无情的，残忍的；坚决的（考研常考前者）
definitions.example: The ruthless dictator suppressed all political opposition without mercy.

ruthless.etymology: 由ruth（古英语，意为「怜悯」）加否定后缀-less构成，即「无怜悯的」

similar_words.description: 二者均表「无情的」，ruthless更侧重为达目的不择手段的冷酷，merciless侧重无怜悯心

definitions.meaning: 解雇；麻袋（考研常考前者）
definitions.example: The company sacked three employees for violating the data protection regulations.

sack.etymology: 源自古英语sacc（麻袋），16世纪引申为「把（东西）装进麻袋带走」，进而引申为「解雇」

similar_words.description: 拼写形近易混，sack指麻袋或解雇，pack指包裹或打包

definitions.meaning: 神圣的，宗教的；不可侵犯的
definitions.example: In many cultures, the right to life is considered a sacred value that cannot be violated.

sacred.etymology: 源自拉丁语sacer（神圣的）加形容词后缀-ed，原指与神相关的

similar_words.description: 二者均表「神圣的」，sacred更侧重与宗教、仪式或传统相关的神圣，holy更侧重道德上的圣洁

definitions.meaning: 牺牲；献祭（考研常考前者，指为某事放弃利益）
definitions.example: Many scientists have made great personal sacrifices to pursue their research goals.

sacrifice.etymology: 源自拉丁语sacrificium，由sacer（神圣的）加facere（做）构成，原指为神做的献祭，引申为牺牲

similar_words.description: 同根词，均含词根sac-（神圣的），sacred指神圣的，sacrifice原指献祭，现指牺牲

definitions.meaning: 马鞍；使承担（责任、负担等，考研常考后者）
definitions.example: The new manager was saddled with the heavy responsibility of turning around the failing company.

saddle.etymology: 源自古英语sadol（马鞍），17世纪引申为「把负担放在（某人）身上」，像把马鞍放在马背上一样

similar_words.description: 拼写形近易混，saddle指马鞍或使承担，cradle指摇篮或发源地

definitions.meaning: 安全的；可靠的；保险箱（考研常考形容词义）
definitions.example: It is not safe to share personal information on untrusted websites.

safe.etymology: 源自拉丁语salvus（健康的，安全的），原指未受伤害的

similar_words.description: 二者均表「安全的」，safe侧重无危险、未受伤害，secure侧重有保障、无顾虑

definitions.meaning: 保护，保障（尤指权利、安全）
definitions.example: Effective institutional arrangements are needed to safeguard the legitimate rights and interests of migrant workers.
The new data protection law is an important safeguard against personal information leakage.

safeguard.etymology: 由词根「saf-」（=safe，安全）和「guard」（守护）构成，字面义为「守护安全」，引申为保障相关含义。

similar_words.description: 「safeguard」侧重系统性、制度性的保障，「protect」侧重具体的防护行为。

definitions.example: Strict safety protocols have been implemented in the nuclear power plant to prevent accidents.

safety.etymology: 由词根「saf-」（安全）加名词后缀「-ty」构成，表抽象的安全状态或具体的安全措施。

similar_words.description: 「safety」侧重具体的人身、场所安全，「security」侧重抽象的安保、国家安全。

definitions.example: The research vessel sailed to the Southern Ocean to conduct marine biodiversity surveys.
The sailor adjusted the sail to catch more wind during the transoceanic voyage.

sail.etymology: 源自古英语「segl」，本义与「漂浮、航行」相关，后衍生出动词和名词含义。

similar_words.description: 「sail」意为航行/帆，「sale」意为出售，拼写仅中间字母不同（a-i vs a-l），极易形近混淆。

definitions.example: The seasoned sailor managed to steer the ship through the unexpected hurricane in the Atlantic.

sailor.etymology: 由词根「sail」（航行）加表人的名词后缀「-or」构成，指从事航行工作或乘船出行的人。

similar_words.description: 「sailor」是同根的指人名词，「sail」是动词/名词，表航行/帆的含义。

definitions.meaning: （基督教的）圣徒；（首字母大写，用于人名前）圣…
definitions.example: Saint Augustine's 'Confessions' is regarded as a classic work in Western religious philosophy.

Saint.etymology: 源自拉丁语「sanctus」，意为「神圣的」，后演变为基督教中对圣徒的称谓，首字母需大写。

similar_words.description: 大写「Saint」为宗教头衔或圣徒专有称谓，小写「saint」指品德极其高尚的普通人。

definitions.example: For the sake of protecting the fragile ecosystem of the Qinghai-Tibet Plateau, strict tourism regulations have been issued.

sake.etymology: 源自古英语「sacu」，原义为「争执、目的」，现常构成高频短语「for the sake of」表「为了…的缘故」。

similar_words.description: 「sake」侧重出于某种目的的缘故，常搭配「for the sake of」；「cause」侧重导致结果的直接原因。

definitions.example: Nutritionists recommend eating fresh vegetable salad regularly to supplement vitamins and dietary fiber.

salad.etymology: 源自拉丁语「sal」（盐），因古代沙拉常以盐调味，后演变为指代这类混合生食菜肴。

similar_words.description: 二者同源自拉丁语「sal」（盐），「salad」因盐调味得名，「salary」源自古代罗马给士兵的盐贴（薪俸）。

definitions.meaning: （按月/年发放的固定）薪水，薪金
definitions.example: Graduates with master's degrees tend to receive a higher starting salary than those with bachelor's degrees in high-tech industries.

salary.etymology: 由词根「sal」（盐）加名词后缀「-ary」构成，源自古代罗马给士兵发放的盐津（薪水的雏形）。

similar_words.description: 「salary」通常指白领按月/年领取的固定薪水，「wage」指蓝领按周/日领取的工钱。

definitions.example: The e-commerce platform's annual sale of green household appliances reached a record high this year.

sale.etymology: 源自古英语「sellan」，原义为「给予、交付」，后引申为「出售、销售」的商业含义。

similar_words.description: 「sale」意为出售/销售，「sail」意为航行/帆，拼写仅中间字母不同（a-l vs a-i），极易形近混淆。

definitions.meaning: 售货员，推销员（通常指男性）
definitions.example: The skilled salesman used data-driven strategies to promote the new energy vehicle to potential customers.

salesman.etymology: 由「sale」（销售）加连接字母「-s-」和「man」（人）构成，指从事销售工作的男性。

similar_words.description: 「salesman」侧重主动推销商品的人，「seller」侧重泛指出售商品的人（范围更广）。

definitions.example: Saliva contains digestive enzymes that initiate the breakdown of carbohydrates in the oral cavity.

saliva.etymology: 源自拉丁语saliva，无考研常见可拆分词根词缀，为生理学术语

similar_words.description: 「saliva」指口腔分泌的唾液，「salve」指药膏或缓解不良情绪的事物，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: Numerous medical studies have linked excessive salt intake to hypertension and cardiovascular diseases.
The speaker salted his lecture with humorous anecdotes to engage the audience.

salt.etymology: 源自古英语sealt，与拉丁语sal（盐）同源，为印欧语系基础词根衍生词

similar_words.description: 「salt」指调味品盐或加盐/增趣的动作，「silt」指水体中沉积的淤泥，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: All the attendees stood up to salute the veterans who sacrificed their lives for the country.
The critics saluted the young director's bold attempt to explore social taboos in his film.

salute.etymology: 源自拉丁语salutare（问候、致意），词根salut-意为「健康、问候」，与「salvation」同根

similar_words.description: 「salute」为动词/名词，侧重动作性的敬礼或致敬；「salutation」为名词，侧重正式的问候语（如书信开头）

definitions.meaning: 拯救；（困境中的）解救办法
definitions.example: For many low-income families, affordable public housing is the only salvation from homelessness.

salvation.etymology: 源自拉丁语salvatio，词根salut-意为「健康、拯救」，与「salute」同根，为抽象名词

similar_words.description: 「salvation」指抽象的精神或困境解救，「salvage」指具体的财物、人员抢救或挽救，二者同根且拼写相近

definitions.example: The research team collected water samples from 12 rivers to test for microplastic pollution.
Tourists are encouraged to sample local cuisines to fully experience the regional culture.

sample.etymology: 源自古法语essample，后简化为sample，词根sam-意为「拿、取」，与「example」同根

similar_words.description: 「sample」指用于检验或展示的实物样本，「example」指用于说明的抽象或具体事例，二者同根且拼写相近

definitions.example: The European Union imposed strict economic sanctions on the country for violating human rights norms.
The construction project cannot proceed without the official sanction from the environmental protection agency.

sanction.etymology: 源自拉丁语sanctio（神圣的法令），词根sanct-意为「神圣的、合法的」，与「sacred」词根同源

similar_words.description: 「sanction」为名词/动词，表制裁或官方批准；「sanctify」为动词，表使神圣化或正式认可，二者同根易混淆

definitions.example: Strong winds blew fine sand from the Gobi Desert into the northern cities of China.
He carefully sanded the surface of the wooden table to remove the old paint.

sand.etymology: 源自古英语sand，为基础物质名词，无考研常见可拆分词根词缀

similar_words.description: 「sand」指沙粒或打磨动作，「sandal」指露趾的凉鞋，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: She packed a tuna salad sandwich and a bottle of orange juice for her hiking trip.
The small independent bookstore is sandwiched between a large supermarket and a cinema.

sandwich.etymology: 源自英国Sandwich伯爵，因他常吃这种夹肉面包以节省时间，后成为通用名词，无词根词缀

similar_words.description: 「sandwich」（动词义）指将某物夹在两个物体之间，「wedge」指用楔子强行挤入或插入，二者语义相近

definitions.example: It is challenging to remain sane when facing constant setbacks and uncertainty in life.
The committee finally agreed on a sane solution to the budget crisis after weeks of debate.

sane.etymology: 源自拉丁语sanus（健康的），词根san-意为「健康的」，与「sanction」词根同源

similar_words.description: 「sane」指心智或想法健全合理，「saint」指品德高尚的圣人，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: Her sarcasm about his 'perfect' plan made everyone in the room fall silent.

sarcasm.etymology: 源自希腊语sarkasmos（原意为「咬肉」，引申为尖刻挖苦），词根sarc-意为「肉」

similar_words.description: 「sarcasm」指口头或书面的尖刻个人挖苦，「satire」指通过幽默或夸张批判社会现象的文学/艺术体裁，二者语义相近

definitions.example: Her sarcastic remarks about the academic paper made the young researcher feel embarrassed.

sarcastic.etymology: 源自名词「sarcasm（讽刺）」，加形容词后缀「-atic」，表「具有…性质的」

similar_words.description: 前者为形容词，表「（言语/态度）讽刺的」；后者为名词，表「讽刺文学/作品」

definitions.example: The meteorological satellite transmits real-time atmospheric data to the ground control center.

satellite.etymology: 源自拉丁语「satelles（侍从，随从）」，加名词后缀「-ite」，原指行星的「随从」（即卫星）

similar_words.description: 前者为名词「卫星/人造卫星」；后者为名词「讽刺文学/作品」

definitions.meaning: 讽刺（文学/作品）；讽刺手法
definitions.example: The novel uses sharp satire to criticize the hypocrisy of the aristocracy in the 19th century.

satire.etymology: 源自拉丁语「satyrus（讽刺诗）」，后演变为表「讽刺文学/手法」的名词

similar_words.description: 前者多指以文学作品为载体的「讽刺（手法/作品）」；后者多指言语间的「挖苦，讽刺（情绪）」

definitions.example: To the satisfaction of the committee, the project was completed three days ahead of schedule.
The company offered financial satisfaction to the customer who received a defective product.

satisfaction.etymology: 源自动词「satisfy（使满意）」，加名词后缀「-action」，表「状态/结果」

similar_words.description: 前者为名词「满意/赔偿」；后者为形容词「令人满意的」

definitions.meaning: 令人满意的，符合要求的
definitions.example: The candidate's answer to the professional question was not satisfactory, so he failed the interview.

satisfactory.etymology: 源自词根「satisf-（足够，满意）」，加形容词后缀「-actory」，表「具有…性质的」

similar_words.description: 前者表「（事物/结果）令人满意的」；后者表「（人）感到满意的」

definitions.example: The new welfare policy failed to satisfy the basic needs of low-income families.
To apply for the scholarship, students must satisfy the academic performance requirements.

satisfy.etymology: 源自词根「satis-（足够）」，加使动词后缀「-fy」，表「使…足够/满意」

similar_words.description: 前者为动词「使满意/满足」；后者为动词「使饱和/浸透」

definitions.example: The domestic e-commerce market has been saturated, so platforms are expanding into rural areas.

saturate.etymology: 源自词根「satur-（满的）」，加使动词后缀「-ate」，表「使…满/饱和」

similar_words.description: 前者为动词「使饱和/浸透」；后者为动词「使满意/满足」

definitions.example: The chef mixed tomato sauce with basil and olive oil to enhance the dish's flavor.

sauce.etymology: 源自古法语「sauce」，原指「加盐的调味汁」，后泛指各类调味酱

similar_words.description: 前者为名词「调味汁/酱」；后者为名词「茶托，碟」

definitions.example: She placed the hot teacup on a ceramic saucer to prevent it from scalding the table.

saucer.etymology: 源自「sauce（调味汁）」，加名词后缀「-er」，原指「放调味汁的小碟」，后泛指茶托

similar_words.description: 前者为名词「茶托/碟」；后者为名词「调味汁/酱」

definitions.example: The traditional British breakfast typically includes bacon, eggs, grilled sausage and baked beans.

sausage.etymology: 源自拉丁语「salsus（盐腌的）」，因香肠需盐腌防腐而得名

similar_words.description: 前者为名词「香肠/腊肠」；后者为名词「调味汁/酱」

definitions.meaning: 野蛮的，未开化的；残暴的
definitions.example: The ancient travelers described the remote mountain tribe as a savage group with unique rituals.

savage.etymology: 源自中古英语sauvage，来自拉丁语silvaticus（属于森林的），词根silv-（森林），引申为未开化的

similar_words.description: savage指野蛮的或野蛮人，salvage指抢救（财物、生命等）

definitions.example: The medical team managed to save the elderly patient from a life-threatening heart attack.
We can save a lot of electricity by turning off unused appliances.

save.etymology: 源自拉丁语salvare（救助），词根sal-（健康、安全），引申为救助、节省

similar_words.description: save指拯救或节省，savor指品尝（味道、乐趣等）

definitions.example: Young people are advised to set aside a portion of their income as a saving for unexpected expenses.

saving.etymology: 动词save的动名词形式，后缀-ing构成名词，表动作或结果

similar_words.description: saving指储蓄或节省，saying指谚语、言论

definitions.example: She saw a rare bird perched on the oak tree during her morning walk.
The carpenter used a sharp saw to cut the wooden plank into pieces.

saw.etymology: 作为see的过去式源自古英语seon的过去式sawe；作为‘锯’的义项源自古英语sāwan（切割）

similar_words.description: saw指看见（see的过去式）或锯（子），sow指播种或母猪

definitions.example: The new industrial park will be built on a scale large enough to accommodate 500 enterprises.
The pharmacist used a precise scale to measure the medicinal herbs.

scale.etymology: 源自拉丁语scala（梯子、刻度），词根scal-（爬、梯子），引申为规模、刻度（天平的刻度）

similar_words.description: scale指规模或天平，scald指烫伤（或滚烫的液体）

definitions.example: The customs officer scanned the passenger's luggage with a portable X-ray device.

scan.etymology: 源自拉丁语scandere（爬、扫描），词根scand-（爬），引申为快速浏览、扫描

similar_words.description: scan指扫描或浏览，scam指诈骗行为

definitions.example: The corporate scandal involving embezzlement led to the resignation of several senior executives.

scandal.etymology: 源自希腊语skandalon（陷阱、耻辱），词根skand-（跳跃、陷阱），引申为使人蒙羞的事件

similar_words.description: scandal指丑闻，sandal指凉鞋

definitions.meaning: 伤疤，疤痕；（精神上的）创伤
definitions.example: The scar on her forehead was a permanent reminder of the car crash she survived.

scar.etymology: 源自希腊语eskhara（伤疤），词根schar-（切、割），引申为创伤

similar_words.description: scar指伤疤或创伤，scare指惊吓、恐慌

definitions.example: During the war, medical supplies were so scarce that many soldiers died of treatable injuries.

scarce.etymology: 源自拉丁语excarpere（摘下、筛选），词根carp-（摘、切），引申为经过筛选后剩下的稀少的

similar_words.description: scarce是形容词（缺乏的），scarcely是副词（几乎不）

definitions.example: Scarcely had the professor finished his lecture when the students raised questions eagerly.

scarcely.etymology: 形容词scarce的副词形式，后缀-ly构成，表程度或时间

similar_words.description: 两者均表‘几乎不’，scarcely侧重数量或程度的不足，hardly侧重动作或状态的困难

definitions.example: Wildlife photographers must be quiet enough not to scare the endangered animals they are observing.

definitions.meaning: 惊恐，恐慌；（人为制造的）恐慌事件
definitions.example: The false alarm about a chemical leak caused a widespread scare in the suburban area.

scare.etymology: 源自中古英语skerren，本义为「使受惊」

similar_words.description: scar作名词指伤疤、精神创伤，作动词指留下伤痕；scare侧重惊吓、恐慌的含义

definitions.example: Many students in the northern university wear thick woolen scarves to keep warm during the harsh winter.

scarf.etymology: 源自古诺斯语skarfr，原指「用于捆绑的布条」，后引申为围巾

similar_words.description: scare表惊吓、恐慌，scarf指用于保暖或装饰的围巾

definitions.example: When the police arrived, the crowd of protesters scattered in all directions.
Farmers scatter seeds over the plowed field before the rainy season comes.

scatter.etymology: 源自中古英语scateren，词根含义与「分开、散开」相关

similar_words.description: disperse语气更正式，侧重将人群或集合体彻底驱散；scatter更侧重随意、无秩序地散开或撒播

definitions.meaning: （戏剧、电影的）场景；（事件发生的）地点，现场
definitions.example: The first scene of the play is set in a small village pub during the 1950s.

definitions.meaning: 景象，情景；（当众的）吵闹，争吵
definitions.example: Rescue workers rushed to the scene of the earthquake immediately after receiving the alert.

scene.etymology: 源自希腊语skene，原指舞台上的布景棚，后引申为场景、现场等义

similar_words.description: scene侧重具体的、局部的场景或事件发生的现场，为可数名词；scenery侧重某一地区的整体自然风景，为不可数名词

definitions.example: The coastal scenery along the national highway attracts thousands of tourists every summer.

scenery.etymology: scene的派生词，后缀-ery构成集合名词，指整体的风景或布景

similar_words.description: scenery侧重某一区域的整体自然景观，为不可数名词；scene侧重具体的局部场景或事件现场，为可数名词

definitions.meaning: 香味，气味；（动物的）臭迹，踪迹
definitions.example: The hound followed the scent of the fox for miles through the forest.
Investors scented the risk of a financial crisis and sold their stocks rapidly.

scent.etymology: 源自拉丁语sentire（感知、闻），与sense为同根词

similar_words.description: sense表感觉、意识、意义；scent侧重嗅觉相关的气味、嗅出或觉察的含义

definitions.example: Many experts are sceptical about the effectiveness of the new policy aimed at reducing carbon emissions.

sceptical.etymology: 源自希腊语skeptikos（思考的、怀疑的），后引申为持怀疑态度的

similar_words.description: sceptical侧重对某种说法、理论的合理性持理性怀疑，不轻易相信；suspicious侧重对人或事的真实性感到猜疑，带有不信任的主观倾向

definitions.meaning: 计划表，进度表；日程安排
definitions.example: Most postgraduate candidates make a strict schedule to review all subjects before the exam.
The academic conference on climate change is scheduled to be held in Beijing next March.

schedule.etymology: 源自拉丁语scheda（纸条、纸片），原指写在纸条上的清单，后引申为计划表

similar_words.description: timetable侧重具体的时间安排表，如课程表、列车时刻表；schedule使用范围更广，可指各类工作、活动的计划进度，也可作动词表安排

definitions.example: The government launched a new scheme to provide financial support for low-income students.
The opposition parties are scheming to overthrow the current cabinet before the general election.

scheme.etymology: 源自希腊语schema（形状、框架），原指事物的轮廓，后引申为计划、阴谋等义

similar_words.description: plot侧重秘密策划的有害阴谋，尤指颠覆、谋杀等严重图谋；scheme可指普通的官方或公益计划，也可指带有不正当目的的密谋

definitions.example: Many well-known scholars in the field of sociology have participated in this research project.
She was a brilliant scholar at university and won the national scholarship for three consecutive years.

scholar.etymology: 源自拉丁语scholaris，原指「学校的成员」，后引申为学者、奖学金获得者

similar_words.description: academic侧重从事高等教育或学术研究的教师、研究人员；scholar侧重在某一学术领域有深入研究的专家，也可指奖学金获得者

definitions.example: Many graduate students apply for the national scholarship to cover their tuition and living expenses.
Her groundbreaking scholarship in feminist theory has influenced generations of researchers.

scholarship.etymology: 由名词scholar（学者）加后缀-ship构成，-ship表「与某身份相关的状态、资助或成果」

similar_words.description: 均指学业资助，fellowship多指研究生或研究员的专项资助，scholarship适用范围更广，可覆盖各阶段学生

definitions.meaning: 科学的；符合科学规律的
definitions.example: The research team adopted rigorous scientific methods to verify the proposed hypothesis.

scientific.etymology: 由名词science（科学）加形容词后缀-ific构成，-ific表「具有…性质的」

similar_words.description: 均与科技相关，scientific侧重科学理论、研究方法，technological侧重技术应用、工艺开发

definitions.example: The Nobel Prize-winning scientist devoted 30 years to studying the mechanism of cell regeneration.

scientist.etymology: 由名词science（科学）加名词后缀-ist构成，-ist表「从事某职业的人」

similar_words.description: 均指研究者，scientist特指从事自然或社会科学研究的人，scholar可指各学术领域的学者，含人文学科研究者

definitions.example: The editor used a pair of precision scissors to trim the margins of the submitted manuscript.

scissors.etymology: 源自古法语cisoires，原指「切割工具」，现代英语中常用复数形式表单数概念

similar_words.description: 均为切割工具，scissors指日常用的小型剪刀，shears指修剪草木、布料的大型剪刀

definitions.example: The laboratory supervisor scolded the intern for violating the experimental safety protocols.
The student received a sharp scold from his tutor for missing the deadline.

scold.etymology: 源自古诺斯语skalda，原指「辱骂」，无明显现代派生后缀

similar_words.description: 均含指责义，scold侧重当面、严厉的训斥，blame侧重对错误的归咎与问责

definitions.example: The scope of this academic paper is restricted to the analysis of 20th-century American modernist poetry.
The cross-disciplinary project provides a wide scope for young researchers to develop innovative ideas.

scope.etymology: 源自希腊语skopos（「看、观察」），原指「观察的范围」，后引申为抽象的活动、研究领域

similar_words.description: 均表范围，scope侧重抽象的研究、活动领域或权限，range侧重具体的数值、数量幅度或系列

definitions.example: His total score in the national postgraduate entrance examination ranked among the top 3% nationwide.
The judges scored the candidates' oral presentations based on clarity, logic and originality.

score.etymology: 源自古英语scoru，原指「二十」（古代计数单位），后引申为「计数、分数」

similar_words.description: 均表分数，score侧重考试、竞赛的正式得分，mark侧重教师对作业、试卷的评分或批注

definitions.example: The renowned scholar expressed her scorn for the sensationalized, unsubstantiated claims in the best-selling book.
He scorns the notion that academic success can be achieved through shortcuts rather than rigorous research.

scorn.etymology: 源自古法语escarn，原指「嘲笑、嘲弄」，无明显现代派生后缀

similar_words.description: 均含轻视义，scorn侧重内心的轻蔑态度，scoff侧重公开的嘲笑、讥讽行为

definitions.meaning: 侦察员；（人才、资源的）发掘者
definitions.example: The talent scout from the film studio discovered the young actress at a campus drama competition.
The research team scouted 12 universities across the country to find qualified PhD candidates.

scout.etymology: 源自古法语escouter（「倾听」），原指「倾听情报的人」，即侦察员，后引申为人才发掘者

similar_words.description: 均指搜集信息的人，scout多指合法的侦察员或人才发掘者，spy多指从事非法情报活动的间谍

definitions.example: The field researchers scrambled up the rocky slope to collect samples of alpine plants.
Dozens of tech companies scrambled to bid for the government's 5G infrastructure project.

scramble.etymology: 源自中古荷兰语schrapen（「抓、刮」），原指「用手脚抓着攀爬」，后引申为「争抢资源」

similar_words.description: 均表攀爬，scramble侧重手脚并用的艰难、快速攀爬，climb侧重一般的向上攀登动作

definitions.example: The archaeologists uncovered scraps of handwritten manuscripts in the medieval monastery.
The company decided to scrap the outdated production line to cut operational costs.

scrap.etymology: 源自原始日耳曼语*skrap-（表「刮、擦」），指刮下的碎屑，无复杂派生缀

similar_words.description: 「scrap」作名词表碎片、作动词表废弃；「scrape」多作动词表刮擦、勉强通过

definitions.meaning: 刮，擦；勉强通过（考试、难关）
definitions.example: She managed to scrape through the tough postgraduate qualifying exam with a marginal score.

scrape.etymology: 同scrap，源自原始日耳曼语*skrap-（表「刮、擦」），指刮擦的动作

similar_words.description: 「scrape」侧重刮擦动作或勉强过关；「scrap」侧重碎片或废弃事物

definitions.meaning: 抓，搔；刮划（物体表面）；（喻）触及表面
definitions.example: The policy report merely scratches the surface of the complex rural poverty issue.
There were deep scratches on the desk left by the previous researcher’s pen.

scratch.etymology: 源自中古英语scratchen，表「抓、搔」，无复杂派生缀

similar_words.description: 「scratch」侧重用指甲、尖物抓划；「scrape」侧重用硬物擦刮以去除表层

definitions.meaning: （因恐惧、愤怒、疼痛等）尖叫；惊呼
definitions.example: When the lab experiment caused a small explosion, the assistant screamed to alert the supervisor.
A sudden scream from the audience interrupted the academic lecture briefly.

scream.etymology: 源自古英语scriman，表「尖叫」，无复杂派生缀

similar_words.description: 「scream」多指因极端情绪的尖声叫喊；「shout」多指为引起注意的大声呼喊

definitions.example: The satellite’s weather data was displayed on the large control room screen.
The university screens all graduate applicants for academic potential and research experience.

screen.etymology: 源自古英语screne（表「遮蔽物」），后引申为「屏幕」，因筛选类似屏风过滤的功能，派生出动词义

similar_words.description: 「screen」侧重对人员、信息的初步筛选；「filter」侧重对液体、数据的过滤

definitions.example: The mechanical engineer tightened the loose screw on the experimental device to prevent malfunction.
The management tried to screw more overtime work out of the exhausted employees.

screw.etymology: 源自拉丁语scrofa（原指「母猪」，因螺丝钉的螺纹形似母猪鬃毛排列），后演变为表「螺丝钉」的词

similar_words.description: 「screw」指带螺纹的螺丝钉；「bolt」指无螺纹的螺栓或插销

definitions.meaning: 剧本，脚本；（手写的）笔迹，手迹
definitions.example: The Oscar-winning screenwriter spent a year revising the script before production.

script.etymology: 源自拉丁语scriptum（表「写的东西」），来自动词scribere（写），核心词根「script-」表「书写」

similar_words.description: 「script」侧重戏剧、影视的剧本或手写笔迹；「manuscript」侧重未出版的手稿

definitions.meaning: 仔细检查，详审（尤指批判性地）
definitions.example: Peer reviewers will scrutinize every methodological detail of the research paper.

scrutinize.etymology: 源自拉丁语scrutinari（表「搜索、检查」），核心词根「scrut-」表「搜索、审视」，-ize为动词后缀

similar_words.description: 「scrutinize」侧重极其细致、批判性的检查；「examine」侧重常规的检查或测试

definitions.example: The government’s new immigration policy has come under intense scrutiny from the media.

scrutiny.etymology: 源自拉丁语scrutinium，核心词根「scrut-」表「搜索、审视」，-y为抽象名词后缀，对应动词scrutinize

similar_words.description: 「scrutiny」侧重深入、批判性的审视；「inspection」侧重官方或正式的检查

definitions.example: The Louvre Museum houses a renowned collection of Renaissance marble sculptures.
The artist spent months sculpting the bronze statue of the historical figure.

sculpture.etymology: 源自拉丁语sculptura，来自动词sculpere（表「雕刻」），核心词根「sculp-」表「雕刻」

similar_words.description: 「sculpture」泛指各类雕刻作品；「statue」特指人物或动物的立式雕像

definitions.example: The academic agreement is valid only after it bears the official seal of the university.
Researchers decided to seal the contaminated samples to prevent cross-contamination.

seal.etymology: 源自拉丁语 sigillum「小印章」，经古法语演化后引申出「封条、密封」等义

similar_words.description: seal 指印章、密封动作；seam 指接缝、线缝

definitions.example: The old academic journal has a torn seam along its spine, making it fragile.

seam.etymology: 源自古英语 seam，原指「缝合的线」，引申为「物体的接缝、地质层缝」等义

similar_words.description: seam 指接缝、线缝；seal 指印章、密封动作

definitions.example: Researchers searched the academic database for all relevant papers on renewable energy.
The search for effective cancer treatments has lasted for several decades.

search.etymology: 源自拉丁语 circāre「环绕」，经古法语演化后原指「四处寻找」

similar_words.description: search 侧重系统、仔细的搜索（如查数据库）；seek 侧重有明确目的的寻找（如找工作）

definitions.example: Many international marine biology conferences are held at well-equipped seaside resorts.

seaside.etymology: 合成词，由 sea「海」+ side「边」构成，指「靠近海洋的休闲区域」

similar_words.description: seaside 侧重供人休闲的海滨区域；coast 侧重地理意义上的海岸带

definitions.example: In the research, socioeconomic status was a secondary variable, not the core focus.
The government has allocated more funds to improve secondary education in rural areas.

secondary.etymology: 源自拉丁语 secundarius「第二的」，由 secundus「第二的」+ 形容词后缀 -arius 构成

similar_words.description: secondary 指次要的、中等的；primary 指主要的、初等的

definitions.example: The chemist kept the formula of the new material a secret until the patent was approved.
The declassified documents reveal details of a secret government research project.

secret.etymology: 源自拉丁语 secretus「分开的、隐藏的」，由 se-「分开」+ cernere「区分」构成

similar_words.description: secret 侧重个人或小范围的秘密；confidential 侧重官方或专业领域的机密

definitions.example: The fourth section of the thesis analyzes the limitations of existing research methods.
The engineer sectioned the blueprint to display the internal structure of the bridge.

section.etymology: 源自拉丁语 sectio「切割」，由 secare「切割」+ 名词后缀 -tio 构成，引申为「部分、章节」等义

similar_words.description: section 侧重被分割出的明确部分（如论文章节）；segment 侧重事物的一段或一块（如市场 segment）

definitions.example: The service sector contributes over 60% of the total GDP in most developed countries.
The math problem requires calculating the area of a 90-degree sector of a circle.

sector.etymology: 源自拉丁语 sector「切割者、扇形」，由 secare「切割」+ 名词后缀 -tor 构成，后引申为「经济领域的部门」

similar_words.description: sector 侧重特定领域的部门（如经济 sector）；section 侧重事物的组成部分（如论文 section）

definitions.example: The university upholds secular education policies while respecting students' religious beliefs.
The report analyzes the secular decline in manufacturing employment over the past 40 years.

secular.etymology: 源自拉丁语 saecularis「属于一个时代的」，由 saeculum「时代、世纪」构成，引申为「世俗的、长期的」等义

similar_words.description: secular 侧重与宗教相对的世俗；temporal 侧重与精神相对的世俗（或时间的）

definitions.example: The research team stored all participants' personal data in a secure encrypted server.
The young linguist secured a $70,000 research grant from the National Endowment for the Humanities.

secure.etymology: 源自拉丁语 securus「无忧的」，由 se-「无」+ cura「忧虑」构成，引申为「安全的、获得」等义

similar_words.description: secure 侧重通过努力或策略获得（如科研资助）；obtain 侧重通过普通方式获得（如信息）

definitions.example: Digital security measures are essential for protecting personal information in the information age.
Government securities are considered a low-risk investment option for institutional investors.

security.etymology: 词根为「secure（安全的）」，加名词后缀-ity，表「安全的状态或性质」

similar_words.description: 「security」侧重「人为采取措施后的安全、保障」，「safety」侧重「无危险的自然状态」

definitions.example: Farmers select high-quality seeds to ensure a good harvest every year.
Her early curiosity about the universe was the seed of her later career as an astronomer.

seed.etymology: 源自古英语sed，原指「用于播种的植物繁殖体」

similar_words.description: 「seed」为「种子」，「weed」为「杂草」，拼写接近易混淆

definitions.example: Many young scholars seek opportunities to conduct research in top universities worldwide.

seek.etymology: 源自古英语secgan，表「有目的地寻找」

similar_words.description: 「seek」侧重「有明确目的的寻求」，「search」侧重「仔细搜查的动作」

definitions.example: Seemingly insignificant details can sometimes determine the outcome of a complex negotiation.

seemingly.etymology: 由「seem（似乎）」加形容词后缀-ing，再加副词后缀-ly构成，表「从表面看来的状态」

similar_words.description: 「seemingly」为副词表「似乎」，「seemly」为形容词表「得体的」，拼写接近易混淆

definitions.example: The marketing team divides the target market into several segments based on consumer preferences.
The research data is segmented into three groups for statistical analysis.

segment.etymology: 源自拉丁语segmentum，词根sec-表「切割」，指「被切割出的部分」

similar_words.description: 二者为同根近义，「segment」侧重「被分割的独立部分」，「section」侧重「整体中的自然部分」

definitions.example: Historically, some societies segregated people of different ethnic backgrounds in public facilities.

segregate.etymology: 源自拉丁语segregare，前缀se-表「分离」，词根greg表「群体」，后缀-ate为动词后缀，表「从群体中分离」

similar_words.description: 「segregate」表「隔离」，「aggregate」表「聚集」，拼写接近且语义相反易混淆

definitions.example: The military forces seized control of the strategic port during the conflict.
Customs officials have the right to seize illegal goods entering the country.

seize.etymology: 源自古法语seisir，原指「强行占有」

similar_words.description: 「seize」侧重「突然用力抓住或夺取」，「grasp」侧重「紧紧握住或理解」

definitions.example: Seldom do people realize the value of health until they lose it.

seldom.etymology: 源自古英语seldan，表「频率极低的状态」

similar_words.description: 二者均表「很少」，「seldom」更正式，常用于书面语，「rarely」适用范围更广

definitions.example: Professors will select outstanding students to participate in the national academic innovation competition.
Only a select group of researchers has access to the confidential experimental data.

select.etymology: 源自拉丁语selectus，前缀se-表「分离」，词根leg-表「选择」，表「从群体中分离出优质的」

similar_words.description: 「select」表「一般挑选」，「elect」表「选举公职人员」，拼写接近易混淆

definitions.example: The selection of appropriate research methods is a critical step in academic writing.
The library offers a wide selection of classical literary works for readers.

selection.etymology: 由「select（挑选）」加名词后缀-ion构成，表「挑选的行为或结果」

similar_words.description: 「selection」表「挑选」，「election」表「选举」，拼写接近易混淆

definitions.example: Self-awareness is a crucial factor in the process of career planning for college graduates.

self.etymology: 古英语原词，本义为「自身、本人」，是反身代词的核心词根

similar_words.description: self多作复合词前缀或单独表「自身」，oneself是完整反身代词，表「某人自己」

definitions.example: Selfish behavior in public welfare activities will undermine the trust between citizens and charitable organizations.

selfish.etymology: 由self（自身）+ 形容词后缀-ish（表「具有…性质的」）构成，表「只关注自身的」

similar_words.description: 前者表「自私的」，后者表「无私的」，易因同根词尾混淆

definitions.example: Most American universities adopt a two-semester system for undergraduate education, which is different from the trimester system in some European countries.

semester.etymology: 来自拉丁语semestris（六个月的），由sem-（六）+ mensis（月）构成，后引申为大学的学期

similar_words.description: semester多指美加等国的半年制大学学期，term可指各类学制的学期（如英国的三学期制）

definitions.example: Semiconductor chips are the core components of modern electronic devices, and their production technology affects the development of the global high-tech industry.

semiconductor.etymology: 由前缀semi-（半）+ 名词conductor（导体）构成，指导电能力介于导体和绝缘体之间的物质

similar_words.description: 前者指「半导体」，后者指「导体；指挥家」，易因同根关联混淆

definitions.example: Graduate students are required to attend at least three academic seminars per semester to expand their research horizons.

seminar.etymology: 来自拉丁语seminarium（苗圃，引申为培育思想的场所），由sem-（种子，引申为「培育」）构成，后指学术研讨班

similar_words.description: seminar多指高校内的小型专题研讨班，symposium多指大型正式的学术研讨会

definitions.meaning: （美国等国家的）参议院，上议院
definitions.example: The US Senate has the power to approve or reject presidential nominations for federal judges.
In ancient Rome, the Senate was a key political body composed of wealthy and influential nobles.

senate.etymology: 来自拉丁语senatus（元老院，由年长贵族组成的议事机构），由词根sen-（老的）构成，后指现代国家的参议院

similar_words.description: 均为美国国会组成部分，senate是参议院（上院），后者是众议院（下院），易因政治体系混淆

definitions.example: A US senator serves a six-year term, and one-third of the Senate seats are up for election every two years.

senator.etymology: 由词根sen-（老的）+ 表人的后缀-ator构成，指参议院的议员

similar_words.description: 前者为美国参议院议员，后者为众议院议员，易因国会职位混淆

definitions.example: Senior managers in multinational corporations usually need to have cross-cultural communication skills and global strategic vision.

definitions.meaning: 高年级学生；资深人士；（美国大学的）大四学生
definitions.example: College seniors are busy preparing for graduate school applications or job interviews in the final semester.

senior.etymology: 由词根sen-（老的）+ 比较级后缀-ior构成，表「较年长的、级别较高的」

similar_words.description: 前者表「年长的；高级的；大四学生」，后者表「年幼的；初级的；大三学生」，易因层级和词尾混淆

definitions.example: The sensation of pain is a protective mechanism that alerts the body to potential harm.
The discovery of the ancient tomb became a national sensation within days of being reported.

sensation.etymology: 由词根sens-（感觉）+ 名词后缀-ation构成，表「感觉的状态或结果」

similar_words.description: 前者表「具体的生理感觉或轰动事件」，后者表「抽象的意识、观念或意义」，易因同根混淆

definitions.example: It makes sense to prioritize environmental protection when formulating long-term economic development policies.
The smart sensor can sense tiny changes in temperature and humidity in the agricultural greenhouse.

sense.etymology: 来自拉丁语sentire（感觉），核心词根为sens-（感觉），可作名词或动词

similar_words.description: 前者表「话语、行为的意义或人的判断力」，后者表「词语、符号的具体含义」，易因「意义」义项混淆

definitions.example: It is sensible for policymakers to take into account both short-term and long-term effects when formulating economic policies.

sensible.etymology: 词根为「sense（感知、理性）」，后缀「-ible（表“能…的”）」，意为“能做出理性判断的”，引申为明智合理的

similar_words.description: 二者同根形近，「sensible」指明智合理的，「sensitive」指敏感易受影响的

definitions.example: Some endangered species are highly sensitive to even minor changes in their habitats.

sensitive.etymology: 词根为「sense（感知）」，后缀「-itive（表“有…性质的”）」，意为“有过度感知性质的”，引申为敏感易受影响的

similar_words.description: 二者同根形近，「sensitive」指敏感易受影响的，「sensible」指明智合理的

definitions.example: A well-organized sentence is crucial for clear expression in academic writing.
The court sentenced the former official to ten years in prison for embezzlement.

sentence.etymology: 词根为「sent-（感知、判断）」，后缀「-ence（名词后缀）」，原指法官的判决（判断结果），后引申为语言表达的完整单位“句子”

similar_words.description: 二者同根，「sentence」表句子/判决，「sentiment」表情绪观点

definitions.example: The latest poll reflects the public sentiment towards the new healthcare reform policy.

sentiment.etymology: 词根为「sent-（感知、情感）」，后缀「-ment（名词后缀）」，意为“感知到的情感/观点的集合”

similar_words.description: 二者同根形近，「sentiment」为名词表情绪观点，「sentimental」为形容词表多愁善感的

definitions.example: The old letter evoked sentimental memories of her college years with close friends.

sentimental.etymology: 由名词「sentiment（情绪）」加形容词后缀「-al」构成，意为“与情绪相关的，易动情绪的”

similar_words.description: 二者同根形近，「sentimental」为形容词表多愁善感的，「sentiment」为名词表情绪观点

definitions.example: The research team set up three separate laboratories to conduct parallel experiments.
It is difficult to separate the influence of genetic factors from that of environmental factors on personality.

separate.etymology: 前缀「se-（分开）」加词根「par（安排、排列）」加后缀「-ate（动词/形容词后缀）」，意为“分开排列的/使分开排列”

similar_words.description: 二者同根近义，「separate」指一般的分离，「segregate」指强制隔离（尤指种族、群体等）

definitions.example: The research paper clearly outlines the sequence of data collection and analysis procedures.

sequence.etymology: 词根为「sequ（跟随）」加后缀「-ence（名词后缀）」，意为“依次跟随的排列”，即序列、顺序

similar_words.description: 二者近义，「sequence」表有先后逻辑的序列，「series」表一系列同类事物（无严格顺序要求）

definitions.example: The academic journal published the serial study on artificial intelligence over four consecutive issues.

serial.etymology: 词根为「seri（连续）」加后缀「-al（形容词后缀）」，意为“连续的”，引申为连载的（分连续期发布的）

similar_words.description: 二者同根形近，「serial」多作形容词表连续的/连载的，「series」为名词表一系列

definitions.example: A series of studies have confirmed the positive correlation between sleep quality and academic performance.

series.etymology: 词根为「seri（连续）」加后缀「-es（名词复数后缀，此处单复数同形）」，意为“连续的一组事物”

similar_words.description: 二者近义，「series」表一系列同类事物，「sequence」表有先后逻辑的序列

definitions.example: The leakage of industrial wastewater has caused serious pollution to the local river system.

serious.etymology: 词根为「seri（重的、严肃的）」加后缀「-ous（形容词后缀）」，意为“程度重的/态度严肃的”

similar_words.description: 二者近义，「serious」表严重的（程度或态度），「severe」表严厉的/剧烈的（尤指天气、惩罚等）

definitions.example: The priest delivered a sermon on the importance of compassion to the congregation.

sermon.etymology: 源自拉丁语「sermo」（讲话、演说），后引申为宗教领域的布道

similar_words.description: 「seminar」指学术研讨会，「sermon」指宗教布道或说教

definitions.example: Many wealthy families in the 19th century employed several domestic servants.
As a public servant, she is committed to serving the needs of local residents.

servant.etymology: 由动词「serve」（服务）加名词后缀「-ant」构成，意为“服务他人的人”

similar_words.description: 「server」多指提供服务的人或设备，「servant」多指受雇的仆人或公职人员

definitions.example: The UN General Assembly holds its annual session in New York every September.
The spring session of the university runs from February to June.

session.etymology: 源自拉丁语「sessio」（坐下、集会），词根「sess-」表示“坐”，引申为“议事或学习的时段”

similar_words.description: 「semester」专指大学的学期，「session」可指会议、开庭期或学期，范围更广

definitions.example: The project suffered a major setback when the funding was suddenly cut off.

setback.etymology: 由短语动词「set back」（阻碍、推迟）转化而来的名词，意为“使进程倒退的因素”

similar_words.description: 「setoff」意为出发、衬托物，「setback」意为挫折、阻碍

definitions.example: The novel's setting is a small coastal town in 1950s England.
You can adjust the camera's exposure setting to take better photos in low light.

setting.etymology: 由动词「set」（设置、放置）加名词后缀「-ing」构成，可指“被设置的环境或参数”

similar_words.description: 「scene」多指具体的场景或场面，「setting」多指故事或事件发生的整体背景环境

definitions.example: The two countries finally settled their long-standing border dispute through diplomatic negotiations.
After years of traveling, they decided to settle in a quiet village in France.

settle.etymology: 源自古英语「setlan」（放置、安顿），词根「set-」表示“放置”，引申为“使稳定、解决”

similar_words.description: 「solve」多指解决具体的问题或谜题，「settle」多指解决争端、纠纷或安顿下来

definitions.example: The labor union and the company reached a settlement after three weeks of strikes.
The first permanent European settlement in North America was founded in 1607.

settlement.etymology: 由动词「settle」（解决、定居）加名词后缀「-ment」构成，表行为的结果

similar_words.description: 「agreement」泛指双方达成的协议，「settlement」多指争端或纠纷的解决协议

definitions.example: The region is facing a severe drought that has destroyed most of the crops.
The company had to make severe budget cuts to survive the economic recession.

severe.etymology: 源自拉丁语「severus」（严肃的、严厉的），引申为“程度极深的”

similar_words.description: 「serious」多指情况重要或令人担忧，「severe」多指程度极深、严厉或严峻

definitions.example: She learned to sew her own clothes when she was a teenager.

sew.etymology: 源自古英语「siowan」（缝合），为基础动词

similar_words.description: 「sow」意为播种、散布，「sew」意为缝制、缝合

definitions.example: The study examines the differences in career choices between people of different sexes.

sex.etymology: 源自拉丁语「sexus」（生理性别），后引申为“性”相关含义

similar_words.description: 「gender」多指社会文化层面的性别，「sex」多指生理层面的性别

definitions.example: The shabby tenement buildings in the old town were scheduled for renovation last year.
The workers complained about the shabby treatment they received from their employer.

shabby.etymology: 源自古英语sceabba（碎屑、残片），原指由残片拼凑而成的，引申为破旧的

similar_words.description: scabby指皮肤或表面结痂的，shabby指物品破旧的或行为卑劣的

definitions.example: Hikers often take a break in the shade of large trees to avoid sunstroke.
The interior designer chose warm shades of beige to decorate the living room.

shade.etymology: 源自古英语sceadu（阴影、黑暗），与shadow同根

similar_words.description: shade指无直射阳光的阴凉区域，shadow指物体遮挡光线投下的具体影子

definitions.example: The setting sun cast long shadows of the pedestrians on the pavement.
The private investigator shadowed the CEO for a week to collect evidence of fraud.

shadow.etymology: 源自古英语sceadu（阴影、黑暗），与shade同根

similar_words.description: shadow指物体投下的有形影子，shade指无直射光的阴凉区域

definitions.example: We rented a shady balcony apartment to escape the summer heat.
The regulatory agency launched an investigation into the company's shady financial transactions.

shady.etymology: 由shade（阴凉处）加形容词后缀-y构成，引申义由「躲在阴凉处见不得光」而来

similar_words.description: shabby指物品破旧或行为卑劣，shady指地方阴凉或行为可疑

definitions.example: The broken drive shaft of the truck caused a serious traffic accident on the highway.
A shaft of sunlight pierced through the thick clouds and lit up the valley.

shaft.etymology: 源自古英语sceaft（杆、柱），原指细长的杆状物，引申为机械轴、光线束等

similar_words.description: shift为动词/名词表转移、轮班，shaft为名词表轴、杆或光线束

definitions.example: The powerful earthquake shook the coastal city, destroying hundreds of buildings.
The unexpected resignation of the chairman shook the company's stock price.

shake.etymology: 源自古英语sceacan（摇动、颤抖），与shiver同根

similar_words.description: shake侧重外力或主动引起的摇动，shiver侧重因寒冷、恐惧的不由自主颤抖

definitions.example: The shallow river is a popular spot for children to play in summer.
The critic dismissed the author's argument as shallow and unsubstantiated.

shallow.etymology: 源自中古英语schalowe（浅的），可能与古英语sceald（浅的）有关

similar_words.description: hollow侧重物体内部空心的，shallow侧重深度或思想肤浅的

definitions.example: The customs officers seized a batch of sham luxury watches at the border.
The man who claimed to be a doctor was later exposed as a sham.

sham.etymology: 源自古诺斯语skamma（羞耻），原指见不得人的、羞耻的，引申为假冒的

similar_words.description: shame为名词/动词表羞耻、使羞愧，sham为形容词/名词表假冒的、骗子

definitions.example: He felt overwhelming shame when he realized he had cheated on the exam.
The team's humiliating defeat shamed the entire school community.

shame.etymology: 源自古英语sceamu（羞耻），与sham同根

similar_words.description: shame表羞耻或使羞愧，sham表假冒或骗子
