definitions.example: More and more consumers are choosing sulfate-free shampoos for sensitive scalps.
She shampooed her hair twice to remove the hairspray residue.

shampoo.etymology: 源自印地语champo（按摩），原指洗头时的头部按摩，后引申为洗发水

similar_words.description: sample为名词/动词表样品、抽样，shampoo为名词/动词表洗发水、洗头

definitions.meaning: 塑造，影响（观点、习惯、发展等）
definitions.example: Social media platforms significantly shape young people's consumption habits.
The ancient monument has a pyramidal shape.

shape.etymology: 源自古英语scapan，本义为「切割出的轮廓」，引申出「形状；塑造」之义

similar_words.description: 均含「形状、塑造」义，shape侧重抽象事物的塑造（如观点、习惯），form侧重具体形态或固定结构

definitions.example: Researchers are required to share their experimental data with academic peers.
Each employee will receive a small share of the company's annual profits.

share.etymology: 源自中古英语scearu，本义为「切割后的部分」，核心义为「分享；份额」

similar_words.description: 均含「分」义，share侧重共同拥有或参与分配，divide侧重分割成独立部分

definitions.example: The marine biologist studied the migration patterns of great white sharks.
Local residents were warned about loan sharks operating in the neighborhood.

shark.etymology: 源自中古英语scharke，本义为「贪婪的鱼」，因鲨鱼的贪婪隐喻引申出「诈骗者」之义

similar_words.description: 形近词，shark表「鲨鱼；诈骗者」，sharp表「锋利的；敏锐的」

definitions.example: The surgeon used a sharp scalpel to make the incision.

definitions.meaning: 敏锐的，灵敏的（思维、感官）
definitions.example: Her sharp analytical skills enabled her to spot the logical flaw in the argument.

sharp.etymology: 源自古英语scarp，本义为「可切割的」，引申出「锋利的；敏锐的」之义

similar_words.description: 均含「敏锐的」义，sharp侧重感官或思维的敏捷性，acute侧重问题的严重性或感官的敏锐度

definitions.example: The antique vase shattered into tiny fragments when it slipped from the shelf.
The sudden economic crisis shattered the government's economic recovery plans.

shatter.etymology: 源自中古英语schateren，本义为「分裂成碎片」，核心义为「粉碎；破灭」

similar_words.description: 均含「破碎」义，shatter侧重突然粉碎成细小碎片，break侧重一般性的破碎或损坏

definitions.example: He shaves his face every morning with a safety razor.
A quick shave is all he needs before the important interview.

shave.etymology: 源自古英语scafan，本义为「刮，剃」，核心义为「刮；剃须」

similar_words.description: 形近词，shave侧重刮面部等小面积毛发，shear侧重剪羊毛等大面积毛发

definitions.example: Local farmers shear their sheep annually to collect high-quality wool.
The gardener used a pair of sharp shears to trim the overgrown hedge.

shear.etymology: 源自古英语sceran，本义为「切割」，核心义为「剪；大剪刀」

similar_words.description: 形近词，shear侧重剪大面积毛发（如羊毛），shave侧重刮面部毛发

definitions.meaning: 阐明，使清楚（常与light搭配，考研核心固定搭配）
definitions.example: The latest archaeological findings shed new light on the ancient civilization.

definitions.meaning: 脱落（树叶、毛发等）；流出（眼泪、血液等）
definitions.example: Deciduous trees shed their leaves in late autumn.

shed.etymology: 源自古英语scadan，本义为「分离」，引申出「脱落；阐明」之义（「shed light on」为考研高频固定搭配）

similar_words.description: 形近词，shed表「脱落；阐明」，shade表「阴影；遮阳处」

definitions.example: The team's victory was the result of sheer dedication and consistent effort.
The hikers had to climb a sheer rock face to reach the summit.

sheer.etymology: 源自中古英语schere，本义为「透明的」，引申出「纯粹的；陡峭的」之义

similar_words.description: 形近词，sheer表「纯粹的；陡峭的」，shear表「剪；大剪刀」

definitions.meaning: 床单；（一）片，（一）张（纸、金属等）
definitions.example: She covered the mattress with a fresh cotton sheet.

definitions.meaning: （财务等的）表格，表单（如资产负债表）
definitions.example: The auditor carefully examined the company's monthly financial sheet.

sheet.etymology: 源自古英语scēat，本义为「布片」，引申出「床单；表格」之义

similar_words.description: 均表「纸张」，sheet侧重成张的材料（如纸、金属），paper侧重普通书写或印刷纸张

definitions.example: Researchers often place reference books on the shelf above their desks for easy access.

shelf.etymology: 源自古英语「scylf」，意为凸起的平台，无明显可拆分的现代词缀

similar_words.description: 形近易混，「shelf」指搁板、架子，「shell」指壳、外壳

definitions.example: Archaeologists found a well-preserved shell of a prehistoric mollusk near the riverbank.

shell.etymology: 源自古英语「scell」，指坚硬的外层结构，无明显可拆分的现代词缀

similar_words.description: 形近易混，「shell」指壳、外壳，「shelf」指搁板、架子

definitions.example: Local residents built a temporary shelter to house the homeless victims of the flood.
The cave sheltered the explorers from the sudden sandstorm.

shelter.etymology: 源自古英语「scieldtruma」（盾牌阵），由「scield（盾牌）」+「truma（队伍）」演变，引申为提供防护的场所或行为

similar_words.description: 均含防护义，「shelter」侧重提供庇护场所/长期保护，「shield」侧重用物体直接遮挡

definitions.example: The shepherd guided his flock of sheep across the grassland at dawn.
The teacher shepherded the primary students through the crowded exhibition hall.

shepherd.etymology: 源自古英语「sceaphierde」，由「sceap（羊）」+「hierde（牧人）」构成，动词义由「牧人引导羊群」引申而来

similar_words.description: 均与畜牧相关，「shepherd」指牧羊的人，「herd」指被牧的畜群

definitions.example: The ancient warrior held a bronze shield to block the enemy's arrows.
Parents often try to shield their children from negative information online.

shield.etymology: 源自古英语「scield」，直接表示「盾牌」，后引申为防护的行为

similar_words.description: 均含防护义，「shield」侧重用物体直接遮挡，「shelter」侧重提供庇护场所/长期保护

definitions.meaning: 转移，移动；改变（观点、态度等）
definitions.example: The company decided to shift its production base from coastal areas to inland cities to cut costs.
The night shift workers in the factory receive additional allowance for working late.

shift.etymology: 源自古英语「sciftan」，意为「划分、分开」，后引申为转移位置或状态

similar_words.description: 均含转换义，「shift」侧重缓慢转移，「switch」侧重快速切换

definitions.example: In the 19th century, a loaf of bread cost only a few shillings in London.

shilling.etymology: 源自古英语「scilling」，最初指划分的钱币，为英国历史法定货币单位（1英镑=20先令，1先令=12便士）

similar_words.description: 均为英国货币单位，「shilling」为旧制辅币，「pound」为现行及历史主币

definitions.example: The delay in the shipment of raw materials has caused a halt in the factory's production line.

shipment.etymology: 由「ship（运输）」+「-ment（名词后缀，表行为或结果）」构成，指运输行为或所运货物

similar_words.description: 均指货物，「shipment」侧重装运行为或一批货物，「cargo」侧重运输工具装载的货物本身

definitions.meaning: 颤抖，哆嗦（因寒冷、恐惧等）
definitions.example: When the explorer stepped out of the tent, he began to shiver violently in the minus 20-degree temperature.
A shiver ran down her spine when she heard the strange noise in the dark.

shiver.etymology: 源自古英语「sciferian」，意为颤抖，可能与古诺尔斯语「skifa（分割）」有关，引申为身体受刺激的短促抖动

similar_words.description: 均指颤抖，「shiver」侧重因寒冷、恐惧的短促抖动，「tremble」侧重因紧张、虚弱的持续抖动

definitions.example: The unexpected announcement of the company's bankruptcy came as a great shock to all employees.
The graphic images in the documentary shocked many viewers into rethinking their consumption habits.

shock.etymology: 源自法语「choc」，意为碰撞、冲击，后引申为精神上的强烈冲击

similar_words.description: 均指使惊讶，「shock」侧重因意外、可怕事件的强烈震惊，「astonish」侧重因不可思议事件的惊讶

definitions.example: The soldiers were ordered to shoot only in self-defense, and not to initiate any unprovoked attacks.
The renowned director will shoot his new historical drama in the well-preserved ancient town next spring.

shoot.etymology: 源自古英语sceotan，本义为投掷、射击，后引申出拍摄等义

similar_words.description: 「shot」是「shoot」的过去式、过去分词，也可独立作名词表示射击、镜头等义

definitions.example: The honest shopkeeper refused to sell counterfeit goods even though they promised higher profits.

shopkeeper.etymology: 合成词，由「shop（商店）」和「keeper（经营者）」构成，指经营商店的人

similar_words.description: 「storekeeper」为美式英语常用词，可指店主或仓库管理员，「shopkeeper」为英式英语常用的店主表述

definitions.example: Many endangered sea turtles return to the sandy shore of this bay to lay their eggs every summer.
The central bank has taken several measures to shore up the stability of the domestic financial market.

shore.etymology: 源自古英语scora，本义指与水体相接的陆地边缘，动词义由「用岸支撑」引申为扶持、支持

similar_words.description: 「coast」侧重沿海的大片区域，「shore」侧重与水直接接触的具体岸边

definitions.example: The prolonged power shortage has severely disrupted the production of local manufacturing enterprises.

shortage.etymology: 由「short（不足的）」加名词后缀「-age（表状态）」构成，指物资或资源不足的状态

similar_words.description: 「lack」侧重抽象事物（如能力、信心）的缺乏，「shortage」侧重具体物资（如粮食、电力）的短缺

definitions.example: The main shortcoming of the proposed environmental policy is its neglect of rural areas' practical needs.

shortcoming.etymology: 合成词，由「short（不足的）」和「coming（存在的事物）」构成，指人或事物存在的不足之处

similar_words.description: 「defect」侧重产品或系统的功能性缺陷，「shortcoming」侧重人或方案的主观或设计不足

definitions.example: The parliamentary reporter used shorthand to record every word of the prime minister's speech accurately.
「AI」 is a widely accepted shorthand for 「Artificial Intelligence」 in academic and daily contexts.

shorthand.etymology: 合成词，由「short（简短的）」和「hand（书写）」构成，原指简短的书写方式即速记，后引申为约定俗成的简略表达

similar_words.description: 「abbreviation」是通用的单词缩写，「shorthand」特指速记法或领域内的固定简略表达

definitions.example: The admissions committee will announce the final list of accepted candidates shortly after the interview process ends.

shortly.etymology: 由「short（短的）」加副词后缀「-ly」构成，指时间间隔短，即不久、很快

similar_words.description: 「soon」更口语化，可指将来或过去的不久，「shortly」更正式，多用于书面语表近期的将来

definitions.example: A sharp shot rang out from the woods, startling the group of hikers who were resting nearby.
The cinematographer spent hours adjusting the lighting to capture that iconic close-up shot of the protagonist.

shot.etymology: 由动词「shoot」的过去分词转化而来，可作名词表示射击的动作、结果或影视镜头等义

similar_words.description: 「shoot」是动词，表射击、拍摄等义，「shot」是其过去式、过去分词，也作名词使用

definitions.example: She carried her heavy laptop bag on one shoulder while rushing to attend the academic seminar.
As the lead researcher, he had to shoulder the full responsibility for the experiment's unexpected failure.

shoulder.etymology: 源自古英语sculdur，本义为人体的肩膀部位，动词义由「用肩膀扛重物」引申为承担责任、负担

similar_words.description: 「bear」侧重被动承受痛苦或压力，「shoulder」侧重主动承担责任或任务

definitions.example: During the morning rush hour, commuters had to shove their way onto the overcrowded subway train.

shove.etymology: 源自中古英语shoven，本义为用力推挤，现仍表示粗暴或用力的推挤动作

similar_words.description: 「push」是通用的推的动作，「shove」侧重粗暴、用力或在拥挤状态下的推挤

definitions.meaning: 淋浴；阵雨；（批评、赞美等的）大量
definitions.example: A shower of critical reviews poured in after the director’s latest film premiered at the international festival.

definitions.meaning: 大量给予（赞美、批评等）；倾注
definitions.example: The media showered the Nobel Prize winner with questions about her groundbreaking research in climate science.

shower.etymology: 来自古英语scūr，原指「暴雨、阵雨」，后引申为「大量涌现的事物」及「淋浴」义

similar_words.description: 「sower」意为“播种者；传播者”，与「shower」拼写仅差字母h，易因拼写疏忽混淆
「show」意为“展示；表明”，与「shower」拼写仅差后缀-er，易混淆词义

definitions.meaning: 精明的；敏锐的（尤指商业或决策能力）
definitions.example: The shrewd entrepreneur identified a gap in the sustainable packaging market long before competitors noticed the trend.

shrewd.etymology: 来自古英语scrēaw（狡猾的），后演变为中性偏褒义的「精明、有判断力」，常用于描述决策或商业能力

similar_words.description: 「shrew」为名词，意为“悍妇；鼩鼱”，是「shrewd」的同根词，易因词根关联混淆词性与词义
「shred」意为“碎片；少量”，与「shrewd」拼写仅差字母w，易拼写混淆

definitions.example: Global textile manufacturers have seen their profit margins shrink as raw material costs continue to rise.

definitions.meaning: 退缩；畏缩（尤指因恐惧或犹豫）
definitions.example: Many young people shrink from taking leadership roles in community projects due to fear of failure.

shrink.etymology: 来自古英语scrincan，原指「蜷缩、收缩身体」，后引申为「规模缩小」「畏缩」等义

similar_words.description: 「shriek」意为“尖叫；尖叫声”，与「shrink」拼写仅差字母n/e，易混淆词义
「shrug」意为“耸肩（表示不在乎）”，与「shrink」均以shr-开头，易拼写混淆

definitions.meaning: 耸肩（表示不在乎、不知道或无奈）
definitions.example: When asked about the company’s declining sales, the CEO merely shrugged and declined to comment further.

shrug.etymology: 来自中古英语shruggen，原指「抖动肩膀」，后固定为「耸肩（表达态度）」的含义

similar_words.description: 「shrub」意为“灌木”，与「shrug」拼写仅差最后一个字母，易拼写混淆
「shrink」意为“收缩；退缩”，与「shrug」均以shr-开头，易拼写混淆

definitions.example: The local library will shut temporarily for renovation starting next month, according to the official announcement.

shut.etymology: 来自古英语scyttan，原指「闩上门」，后泛指「关闭各种开口或设施」

similar_words.description: 「shout」意为“喊叫；呼喊”，与「shut」拼写仅差字母o，易混淆词义
「shutter」意为“百叶窗；快门”，是「shut」的派生词，易混淆词义与词性

definitions.example: The photographer adjusted the camera’s shutter speed to capture the fast-moving river rapids clearly.

shutter.etymology: 由动词「shut」加后缀-er构成，原指「用于关闭的装置」，后特指「百叶窗」「相机快门」

similar_words.description: 「shatter」意为“粉碎；破坏”，与「shutter」拼写仅差字母u/a，易拼写混淆
「shut」意为“关闭”，是「shutter」的词根，易混淆词义与词性

definitions.meaning: 航天飞机；穿梭班机/班车
definitions.example: The space agency announced plans to launch a new shuttle mission to study the outer atmosphere of Mars next year.
Volunteers shuttle medical supplies between the city hospital and remote rural clinics every weekend.

shuttle.etymology: 来自古英语scytel（飞镖），原指「来回穿梭的物体」，后引申为「航天飞机」「穿梭交通工具」等义

similar_words.description: 「shuffle」意为“洗牌；拖着脚走”，与「shuttle」拼写仅差字母t/f，易拼写混淆
「shovel」意为“铲子；铲起”，与「shuttle」拼写接近，易因字母t/v、l/e的差异混淆

definitions.example: The shy student hesitated before raising her hand to answer the professor’s question in front of the whole class.
The fundraising campaign ended $5,000 shy of its target, forcing the charity to scale back its community programs.

shy.etymology: 来自古英语scēoh（胆小的），原指动物「胆小易惊」，后引申为人「害羞的」，再发展出「数量不足」的僻义

similar_words.description: 「sly」意为“狡猾的；诡秘的”，与「shy」拼写仅差字母h/l，易拼写混淆

definitions.example: The cyclist swerved sideways to avoid colliding with a pedestrian who suddenly stepped into the bike lane.

sideways.etymology: 由名词「side」（侧面）加后缀「ways」（表示方向）构成，意为「向侧面的/地」

similar_words.description: 「sideway」为名词，意为“小路；旁道”，与「sideways」拼写仅差字母s，易混淆词性与词义
「sideward」意为“向侧面的/地”，与「sideways」词义接近，易混淆用法；后者更常用作副词，前者多用作形容词

definitions.meaning: 围攻；包围（尤指军事或舆论层面）
definitions.example: The controversial politician faced a siege of media questions after the leaked email scandal came to light.

siege.etymology: 来自古法语siege（座位；包围），原指「军队在被围城市外扎营」，后引申为「各种形式的围攻」

similar_words.description: 「seize」意为“抓住；夺取”，与「siege」拼写仅差字母顺序（i/e）和字母（g/z），易混淆词义
「sage」意为“圣人；鼠尾草”，与「siege」拼写仅差字母i/a，易拼写混淆

definitions.example: She sighed deeply when she saw the overwhelming list of postgraduate exam review materials.
A faint sigh escaped from the postgraduate applicant after she finished the tough written exam.

sigh.etymology: 源自中古英语syghen，为拟声词，模拟叹息时的呼吸声，无复杂词缀

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，「sigh」表叹息/叹息声，「sight」表视力/景象

definitions.example: The breathtaking alpine sight was selected as the theme of a postgraduate English reading passage.
The postgraduate student sighted her advisor in the library just before the thesis deadline.

sight.etymology: 源自原始日耳曼语*sekhwan（看），经古英语sihth演化，表视觉能力或所见事物，为核心视觉类词汇

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，「sight」表视力/景象，「sigh」表叹息/叹息声

definitions.example: Many international postgraduate applicants arrange local sightseeing after their admission interviews.

sightseeing.etymology: 合成词，由「sight（景象）」和「seeing（观看）」组合而成，表观看景观的活动

similar_words.description: 同根于「sight」，「sightseeing」为「sight」的衍生合成词，表观光游览

definitions.example: The steady growth in postgraduate application numbers is a clear sign of fierce academic competition.
All postgraduate applicants must sign the academic integrity statement before submission.

sign.etymology: 源自拉丁语signum（标记、符号），词根「sign-」表标记，为核心衍生词汇的基础

similar_words.description: 同根且拼写形近，「sign」表迹象/签署，「signal」表信号/发信号

definitions.example: The professor’s nod was a signal for the postgraduate student to begin her oral defense.
The examiner signaled the end of the postgraduate written exam with a loud bell.

signal.etymology: 源自拉丁语signalis，由词根「sign-（标记）」加形容词后缀「-al」衍生，转作名词/动词表信号/发信号

similar_words.description: 同根于「sign-」，「signal」表信号，「sign」表迹象/签署

definitions.example: The official postgraduate admission letter requires the handwritten signature of the department dean.

signature.etymology: 由词根「sign-（标记）」加名词后缀「-ature」构成，表标记身份的行为结果，即签名

similar_words.description: 同根于「sign-」，「signature」是「sign」作动词「签署」的名词形式

definitions.example: The research findings have far-reaching significance for postgraduate curriculum innovation.

significance.etymology: 由词根「sign-（标记）」、形容词后缀「-ific（使…的）」加名词后缀「-ance」构成，表具有标记价值的性质，即重要性

similar_words.description: 同根于「sign-」，「significance」是「significant」的名词形式，表意义/重要性

definitions.example: The postgraduate student made significant progress in her thesis after consulting her advisor.

significant.etymology: 由词根「sign-（标记）」、形容词后缀「-ific（使…的）」加形容词后缀「-ant」构成，表能被标记的，即重要的/显著的

similar_words.description: 同根于「sign-」，「significant」是「significance」的形容词形式，表重要的/显著的

definitions.example: The high citation rate signifies that the postgraduate’s paper has gained widespread academic recognition.

signify.etymology: 由词根「sign-（标记）」加动词后缀「-ify（使…）」构成，表使带有标记含义，即表示/意味着

similar_words.description: 同根于「sign-」，「signify」表表示/意味着，「sign」表迹象/签署

definitions.example: The awkward silence in the postgraduate interview room made the applicant’s hands tremble.
The supervisor silenced the heated discussion by posing a critical research question.

silence.etymology: 源自拉丁语silentium，词根「sil-」表安静，为基础听觉类词汇

similar_words.description: 同根于「sil-」，「silence」是「silent」的名词/动词形式，表沉默/使沉默

definitions.example: Many elderly people remain silent about their financial difficulties to avoid burdening their children.
The 'b' in the word 'doubt' is silent.

silent.etymology: 源自古英语silent，与名词「silence」同根，表“安静的、无声音的”，后衍生出“字母不发音”的僻义

similar_words.description: 「silence」为名词/动词，表“沉默；使沉默”，「silent」为形容词，涵盖“沉默的”“不发音的”义

definitions.meaning: 硅（化学元素，半导体核心材料）
definitions.example: Silicon wafers are the foundational material for manufacturing high-performance semiconductor chips.

silicon.etymology: 源自拉丁语silex（燧石），加化学元素后缀-on构成，指从燧石中提取的非金属元素

similar_words.description: 「silicon」指化学元素硅，多用于科技语境；「silver」指金属银，表物质或颜色

definitions.example: His silly remarks during the academic seminar made other scholars feel embarrassed.

silly.etymology: 源自古英语sælig（原义“幸福的”），后贬义化，表“幼稚的、愚蠢的”

similar_words.description: 「foolish」语义较重，指缺乏判断力的愚蠢；「silly」常指幼稚、无足轻重的傻

definitions.example: Silver medals are awarded to the second-place winners in international sports competitions.

silver.etymology: 源自古英语seolfor，指一种贵重金属，后衍生出颜色义

similar_words.description: 「silver」指金属银或银色，表物质/颜色；「silicon」指化学元素硅，多用于科技领域

definitions.example: The brilliance of Einstein's theory lies in its mathematical simplicity and explanatory power.

simplicity.etymology: 源自形容词simple，词根sim-（单一）+名词后缀-plicity（表状态），指“单一的状态”即简单

similar_words.description: 「simplification」指简化的动作或结果；「simplicity」指事物本身具有的简单状态

definitions.example: Professors often simplify complex academic concepts to help undergraduate students grasp them quickly.

simplify.etymology: 词根sim-（单一）+动词后缀-plify（使…），意为“使变得单一”即简化

similar_words.description: 「simplistic」为形容词，含贬义，指“过度简化的”；「simplify」为动词，指“使变简单”的动作

definitions.example: Researchers use supercomputers to simulate the spread of infectious diseases in densely populated areas.

simulate.etymology: 词根simul-（相似）+动词后缀-ate（使…），意为“使变得相似”即模拟、模仿

similar_words.description: 「simulate」指模拟、模仿；「stimulate」指刺激、激发（兴趣、活力等），二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The online platform supports simultaneous video conferencing for up to 500 authorized participants.

simultaneous.etymology: 词根simul-（同时）+形容词后缀-taneous（表“具有…性质的”），指“具有同时性的”

similar_words.description: 「simultaneous」指同时发生的；「spontaneous」指自发的、无意识的，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.meaning: （道德/宗教上的）罪；罪孽；犯过失
definitions.example: In ancient Greek tragedies, hubris was often portrayed as a sin that would lead to tragic downfall.

sin.etymology: 源自古英语synn，指违反道德或宗教准则的行为

similar_words.description: 「sin」指道德或宗教层面的罪孽；「crime」指违反法律的罪行，二者适用范畴不同

definitions.example: Her sincere apology at the press conference finally resolved the long-standing public misunderstanding.

sincere.etymology: 源自拉丁语sincerus（原义“纯净的、无杂质的”），后衍生出“情感真实的”义

similar_words.description: 「genuine」侧重事物真实、非伪造；「sincere」侧重人的情感真诚、不虚伪

definitions.example: In formal academic writing, the verb must agree with its singular subject in grammatical number.
Her singular ability to analyze complex data made her the core member of the research team.

singular.etymology: 来自拉丁语singularis，词根「sing-」表「单一、单独」，后缀「-ular」为形容词后缀，表「具有…性质的」

similar_words.description: singular为形容词，表单数的或非凡的；singularity为名词，表奇点或独特性，同根易混词性与义项

definitions.example: The cargo ship began to sink rapidly after colliding with an iceberg in the Arctic Ocean.
Consumer confidence continued to sink amid the persistent inflation pressure.

sink.etymology: 来自古英语sincan，原始日耳曼语词根，表「下沉、渗透」，无明显派生词缀

similar_words.description: sink表下沉或水槽；sync为synchronize的缩写，表同步，形近易混拼写

definitions.example: She sipped the warm herbal tea slowly while reviewing her graduate school application materials.

sip.etymology: 来自古英语sippan，表「小口喝」，为基础动词，无复杂词缀

similar_words.description: sip侧重抿饮热饮，动作轻柔；sup侧重小口饮酒或汤，更具书面性，形近近义易混

definitions.example: The siren of the fire engine echoed through the residential area near the university.
The siren of short-term high returns lured many young investors into illegal financial schemes.

siren.etymology: 源自古希腊神话中的「塞壬（Seirenes）」，原指用歌声诱惑水手的海妖，后因警报器的尖锐声音引申出「汽笛」义

similar_words.description: siren为名词，表汽笛或诱人事物；silent为形容词，表沉默的、无声的，形近易混拼写

definitions.example: The archaeological site discovered last year has yielded valuable relics from the Han Dynasty.
The new conference center is sited close to the main library for easy access.

site.etymology: 来自拉丁语situs，原始印欧语词根「*kei-」表「躺、放置」，本义为「放置的位置」

similar_words.description: site表地点、场所；sight表视力、景象，考研阅读中常因拼写相近混淆含义

definitions.example: The newly built student dormitory is situated on the hillside overlooking the campus lake.
Graduates from rural areas are often differently situated in terms of job opportunities compared to their urban peers.

situated.etymology: 来自动词situate（使坐落），词根「sit-」表「放置、坐落」，后缀「-ated」为形容词后缀，表「处于…状态的」

similar_words.description: situated更侧重所处的环境或整体境况；located更侧重具体的地理位置坐标，近义易混用法

definitions.example: The current employment situation for postgraduates requires universities to strengthen career guidance.
The situation of the ancient castle made it an ideal defensive stronghold.

situation.etymology: 来自动词situate，词根「sit-」表「放置、坐落」，后缀「-ation」为名词后缀，表「状态、性质」

similar_words.description: situation侧重整体动态的形势或处境；condition侧重具体静态的条件或状态，近义易混用法

definitions.example: Many college students go to the indoor ice rink to skate during the winter vacation.
The keynote speaker tried to skate around the controversial issues related to his research findings.

skate.etymology: 来自荷兰语schaats，原始日耳曼语词根，表「冰鞋」，引申出「滑冰」义

similar_words.description: skate指穿冰鞋滑冰；ski指穿滑雪板滑雪，形近近义易混

definitions.example: The anatomy course requires students to memorize the structure of the human skeleton in detail.
The professor asked the PhD candidates to submit a skeleton of their dissertation proposals by the end of the month.

skeleton.etymology: 来自希腊语skeletos（干的、枯瘦的），引申为「骨骼（身体的干框架）」，再引申为「提纲（内容的框架）」

similar_words.description: skeleton为名词，表骨骼或梗概；skeletal为形容词，表骨骼的、梗概的，同根易混词性

definitions.meaning: 怀疑论者，持怀疑态度的人
definitions.example: As a skeptic of the existing climate policy, he published a paper questioning its long-term effectiveness.

skeptic.etymology: 来自希腊语skeptikos（好思考的、怀疑的），词根「skept-」表「看、审视」，后缀「-ic」为表人的名词后缀

similar_words.description: skeptic侧重对未被证实的主张持理性怀疑；cynic侧重对人性或动机持悲观否定的怀疑，带贬义，近义易混

definitions.example: Many environmental scientists remain skeptical about the effectiveness of the new carbon reduction policy.

skeptical.etymology: 源自名词skeptic（怀疑论者）加形容词后缀-al，表「具有…性质的」

similar_words.description: sceptical为英式拼写，skeptical为美式拼写，二者词义完全一致

definitions.example: The urban planner presented a sketch of the pedestrian-friendly community at the public hearing.
The keynote speaker sketched the development trend of artificial intelligence in the next decade.

sketch.etymology: 源自古法语esquiss-（划线条），词根sket-表「切、划」，原指用线条勾勒的粗略轮廓

similar_words.description: sketch侧重未经细化的粗略草图或内容梗概，draft侧重经过初步整理的文稿或方案草案

definitions.example: The rental shop at the ski resort provides high-quality skis for both beginners and advanced skiers.
A growing number of young people choose to ski as their main winter leisure activity.

ski.etymology: 源自古诺尔斯语skith（雪鞋），为北欧语言借词，特指滑雪相关器具或运动

similar_words.description: skate指滑冰运动或冰鞋，对应冰面场景；ski指滑雪运动或滑雪板，对应雪面场景

definitions.example: Academic writing skill is a core requirement for postgraduate students to publish papers in international journals.

skill.etymology: 源自古诺尔斯语skil（辨别、认知），原指辨别事物的能力，后引申为经练习获得的实操技能

similar_words.description: skill侧重个人通过学习或练习获得的实操能力，technique侧重专业领域的技术方法或操作流程

definitions.example: The high-speed rail project requires a large number of skilled workers with professional technical qualifications.

skilled.etymology: 由名词skill加形容词后缀-ed构成，表「具备…属性的」

similar_words.description: skilled侧重因受过系统训练而具备专门职业技能的，常修饰人或职业；skillful侧重动作或操作的灵巧娴熟，常修饰行为或人

definitions.example: The skillful negotiation between the two companies led to a win-win cooperation agreement.

skillful.etymology: 由名词skill加形容词后缀-ful构成，表「充满…的」，即充满技能的

similar_words.description: skillful侧重动作的灵巧性与熟练度，skilled侧重具备专业训练后的职业技能

definitions.example: When dealing with long reading passages in the exam, you can skim the first and last paragraphs to grasp the main idea quickly.

skim.etymology: 源自中古英语skimmen，词根skim-表「轻掠、掠过」，原指从液体表面轻掠浮沫，后引申为快速浏览文字

similar_words.description: skim侧重快速略读以掌握文章大意，scan侧重快速搜寻文本中的特定信息

definitions.example: The research team developed a new material that can simulate the texture and function of human skin.

skin.etymology: 源自古英语scinn，原指动物的毛皮，后泛指人或动植物的外层皮肤

similar_words.description: skin可指人、小型动物或植物的外皮，hide特指大型动物的厚兽皮（如牛皮、马皮）

definitions.example: In the literature review, you can skip the outdated studies that are irrelevant to your research topic.

skip.etymology: 源自中古英语skippen，词根skip-表「跳、跃」，原指蹦跳的动作，后引申为跳过某一内容或环节

similar_words.description: skip侧重主动跳过不重要或不想处理的内容，omit侧重因疏忽或故意省略应有的内容

definitions.example: The paleontologists analyzed the structure of the fossilized skull to determine the species of the ancient mammal.

skull.etymology: 源自古诺尔斯语skulle，词根skull-表「头」，特指包裹大脑的头盖骨部分

similar_words.description: skeleton指生物的全身骨骼系统，skull仅指头盖骨（颅骨）部分

definitions.example: The gleaming skyscraper in the central business district houses the regional headquarters of three multinational corporations.

skyscraper.etymology: 合成词，由「sky（天空）」和「scraper（刮刀，源自动词scrape‘刮’）」构成，原指仿佛能刮到天空的高层建筑

similar_words.description: 「skyscraper」指摩天大楼，「skyjacker」指劫机者，二者拼写前半部分相同，易混淆

definitions.example: The supervisor warned the employees that their slack work attitude would lead to strict performance reviews.
Retailers usually launch large-scale promotional campaigns to boost sales during the slack quarter of the year.

slack.etymology: 源自古英语slæc，表‘松弛的’，同根词为slacken（动词，使松弛、减弱）

similar_words.description: 「slack」多作形容词表懈怠或萧条，「slacken」是动词表（使）松弛、减弱，词性与用法不同

definitions.example: He slammed the office door behind him after a fierce argument with his department manager.
The leading national newspaper’s editorial slammed the government’s new housing policy for ignoring low-income groups.

slam.etymology: 拟声词，模仿重物砰然闭合或撞击的声音

similar_words.description: 「slam」指砰地关或猛烈抨击，「slap」指掌掴或强制实施，动作与含义均不同，拼写易混淆

definitions.example: The passenger was fined for slapping a security guard during a dispute at the international airport.
The government decided to slap an additional 10% tax on luxury goods to curb excessive consumption.

slap.etymology: 拟声词，模仿掌掴的清脆声响

similar_words.description: 「slap」指掌掴或强制实施，「slam」指砰地关或猛烈抨击，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The animal rights organization criticized the factory for inhumanely slaughtering thousands of cows every month.
The historical documentary depicts the brutal slaughter of innocent civilians during the World War II.

slaughter.etymology: 源自古诺斯语slatr，表‘屠宰的肉’，后引申为屠宰、屠杀的动作

similar_words.description: 「slaughter」可指屠宰牲畜或屠杀人类，「massacre」仅指大规模屠杀人类，语义范围更窄

definitions.example: The novel exposes the harsh living conditions of African slaves on 19th-century American plantations.
She slaved for 12 hours a day to finish her graduate thesis before the strict deadline.

slave.etymology: 源自斯拉夫语Slav，因中世纪斯拉夫人常被掳为奴隶，后演变为表示‘奴隶’的词汇

similar_words.description: 「slave」指奴隶或苦干的动作，「slavery」指奴隶制这一制度，词性与语义不同

definitions.example: She rolled up the sleeves of her lab coat before starting the chemical experiment.
The engineer replaced the worn metal sleeve of the conveyor belt to prevent unexpected equipment breakdowns.

sleeve.etymology: 源自古英语slēfe，表‘覆盖手臂的衣物部分’

similar_words.description: 「sleeve」覆盖手臂，「glove」覆盖手部，二者覆盖的身体部位不同

definitions.example: The ballet dancer’s slender figure helped her perform difficult moves with remarkable ease.
The rural family had only a slender income from selling homemade handicrafts to cover their basic living expenses.

slender.etymology: 源自古英语slendran，表‘拉长的、纤细的’

similar_words.description: 「slender」可指身材纤细或数量微薄，「slim」多指身材苗条或机会微小，语义范围略有不同

definitions.example: She placed a slice of whole-wheat bread and a glass of skim milk on the breakfast table.
The domestic smartphone brand aims to capture a larger slice of the Southeast Asian market next year.

slice.etymology: 源自古法语esclice，表‘碎片、切片’

similar_words.description: 「slice」指切片或份额，「slide」指滑动或下跌，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The children love to slide down the snow-covered hill on their wooden sleds during the winter vacation.
The stock market index slid by 2.8% after the central bank announced a surprise interest rate hike.

slide.etymology: 源自古英语slīdan，表‘滑动、滑行’

similar_words.description: 「slide」多指在粗糙表面滑动或价格下滑，「glide」多指在空中或光滑表面平稳滑行，动作方式不同

definitions.example: Even slight differences in social norms may cause misunderstandings between cross-cultural communicators.
He felt slighted when his research proposal was rejected without any detailed explanation.

slight.etymology: 源自中古英语，无常见可拆分词根词缀，本义为「平整的、不足道的」，后引申为程度轻的，进而产生动词「轻视」的含义

similar_words.description: 二者词形相近，slight多指程度、数量上的轻微，light多指重量轻、光线明亮或颜色浅

definitions.example: Regular physical exercise helps young people keep slim and enhance their cardiorespiratory function.
There is only a slim chance that the controversial bill will be passed in the parliamentary vote.

slim.etymology: 源自拉丁语limus（细的、倾斜的），无常见可拆分词根词缀，本义为身形细长的，后引申为数量、概率小的

similar_words.description: 二者均含「纤细的」之意，slim侧重体重轻、身材匀称苗条，slender侧重身形修长优雅，也可指数量微薄

definitions.example: When rushing to the graduate entrance exam site, she slipped on the icy road and nearly missed the examination.
A tiny slip in the data calculation may lead to a huge deviation in the final research conclusion.

slip.etymology: 源自中古英语slippen，同根词为slippery，本义为滑动，后引申为动作或行为上的小失误

similar_words.description: 二者均含「滑动」之意，slip侧重意外地滑倒、滑落，slide侧重平稳地、持续地滑行或移动

definitions.example: Most star-rated hotels provide disposable slippers for guests to maintain indoor hygiene during their stay.

slipper.etymology: 同根词为slip，后缀-er表物品，指便于穿脱、可滑着穿上的便鞋

similar_words.description: 二者为同根形近词，slipper是名词表拖鞋，slippery是形容词表滑的、难捉摸的

definitions.example: The mountain path became slippery after the thunderstorm, so hikers were advised to cancel their scheduled climb.

definitions.meaning: 狡猾的，不可靠的；棘手的
definitions.example: The issue of income distribution is a slippery topic that most politicians avoid mentioning in public speeches.

slippery.etymology: 词根为slip（滑动），后缀-ery加-y构成形容词，本义为「易滑动的」，后引申为人难捉摸、事情难处理的

similar_words.description: 二者均含「滑的」之意，slippery侧重因湿、油等导致容易打滑的，smooth侧重表面平整光滑、无凸起的

definitions.example: The customs officer slit the sealed parcel open to check for prohibited items inside.
A narrow slit in the ancient castle wall allowed soldiers to observe the situation outside without being seen.

slit.etymology: 源自古英语slitan，无常见可拆分词根词缀，本义为切割，引申为切割产生的狭长缝隙

similar_words.description: 二者词形、词义相近，slit多指切开狭长的口子，split多指将整体分裂为若干部分

definitions.example: The official slogan for the 2008 Beijing Olympic Games was 「One World, One Dream」, which was widely recognized worldwide.

slogan.etymology: 源自苏格兰盖尔语，原指作战时的呐喊，后引申为政党、组织等用于宣传的简短口号

similar_words.description: 二者均含「简短准则」之意，slogan多用于宣传推广，motto多指个人或机构的座右铭、信条

definitions.example: The research team built a weather observation station on the southern slope of the mountain to collect climate data.
The design of the gallery roof slopes slightly to ensure that rainwater can drain away quickly.

slope.etymology: 与slip为同根词，本义为可滑动的斜面，后引申为倾斜的动作或状态

similar_words.description: 二者均含「斜坡、倾斜」之意，slope侧重自然形成的坡面，incline可指任何倾斜表面，也可引申为观点倾向

definitions.example: You need to insert your student ID card into the slot to unlock the door of the self-study room.
The top academic journal has only 12 slots for research papers in the field of environmental science this quarter.

slot.etymology: 源自古法语esclot，原指闩锁的槽口，后引申为预留的空位、名额

similar_words.description: 二者均含「狭长口子」之意，slot多指有特定功能的槽、孔，slit多指切割或开裂形成的裂缝

definitions.example: Many non-governmental organizations are devoted to improving the educational conditions of children in the slums of developing countries.

slum.etymology: 词源不详，原指19世纪伦敦市中心的贫民聚居区，后泛指城市中条件恶劣的贫困居民区

similar_words.description: 二者均指城市中的贫困聚居区，slum泛指因经济落后形成的贫民区，ghetto多指因种族、宗教隔离形成的聚居区

definitions.example: He was a sly politician who often avoided direct answers to sensitive public questions.
She gave him a sly smile when their teacher turned around to write on the blackboard.

sly.etymology: 源自古诺斯语slœgr，原义为‘狡猾的’，后引申出‘会意的’等含义

similar_words.description: sly为形容词，表‘狡猾的；会意的’，slay为动词，表‘杀死，杀害’，二者拼写接近易混淆
均含‘狡猾的’之意，sly侧重行为诡秘、不光明正大，cunning侧重善用计谋欺骗他人

definitions.example: Smart companies will try to encourage employees to participate in long-term professional training programs.

definitions.meaning: （身体部位）感到剧痛；（因批评、失败等）感到难受
definitions.example: His hand smarted from the deep cut he got while repairing the old wooden fence.

smart.etymology: 源自古英语smeart，原义为‘刺痛的’，后引申出‘聪明的，机灵的’等核心含义

similar_words.description: smart可作形容词/动词，表‘聪明的；感到剧痛’，smirk为动词/名词，表‘傻笑，得意地笑’，二者拼写接近易混淆
均含‘聪明的’之意，smart侧重反应快、办事机灵，clever侧重头脑灵活、善于解决问题

definitions.example: The antique vase smashed into tiny pieces when it fell off the marble display shelf.

definitions.meaning: 巨大的成功；轰动一时的事物
definitions.example: The low-budget indie film turned out to be a box-office smash, grossing over $500 million worldwide.

smash.etymology: 拟声词，模拟物体破碎的声响，后引申出‘猛撞；巨大成功’等含义

similar_words.description: 均含‘碰撞，破碎’之意，smash侧重猛烈撞击导致物体破碎，crash侧重车辆、飞机等的碰撞或坠毁
smash可作动词/名词，表‘打碎；成功’，smack可作动词/名词，表‘掴，拍；拍击声’，二者拼写接近易混淆

definitions.example: Heavy smog in northern urban areas has raised widespread concern about public respiratory health.

smog.etymology: 拼缀词，由smoke（烟）和fog（雾）混合构成，特指工业废气与雾气混合形成的污染性烟雾

similar_words.description: fog指自然形成的雾，smog指受污染的烟雾、雾霾，二者成因不同

definitions.meaning: 光滑的，平整的；顺利的，平稳的
definitions.example: The new trade agreement paved the way for a smooth transition of bilateral economic cooperation.

definitions.meaning: 使平滑；缓和（问题），消除（障碍）
definitions.example: The team leader tried to smooth over the disagreements between members of the research group.

smooth.etymology: 源自古英语smēth，原义为‘光滑的’，后引申出‘顺利的’及动词相关含义

similar_words.description: 均含‘平整的’之意，smooth侧重表面光滑无粗糙感，even侧重表面高低一致、无凹凸
smooth可作形容词/动词，表‘光滑的；使顺利’，soothe为动词，表‘安慰，减轻（疼痛）’，二者拼写接近易混淆

definitions.meaning: 走私，偷运（违禁品）；私带，夹带
definitions.example: The criminal gang was caught trying to smuggle illegal weapons across the national border.

smuggle.etymology: 源自荷兰语smokkelen，原义为‘偷偷运输’，现主要指非法走私违禁品

similar_words.description: smuggle为动词，表‘走私，偷运’，struggle为动词/名词，表‘奋斗，挣扎’，二者拼写接近易混淆
smuggle侧重秘密运输违禁品出入境，traffick侧重非法买卖、交易违禁品

definitions.example: Many college students prefer to have a quick snack between classes instead of a formal meal.
She often snacks on unsalted nuts and dried fruit while working on her academic research paper.

snack.etymology: 源自中古荷兰语snack，原义为‘咬，一口’，后引申为‘小吃’及动词含义

similar_words.description: snack为名词/动词，表‘小吃；吃零食’，snake为名词/动词，表‘蛇；蜿蜒行进’，二者拼写仅一个字母之差，极易混淆

definitions.example: The biologist discovered a new species of venomous snake in the Amazon rainforest.
The narrow country road snakes through rolling green hills to the small coastal town.

snake.etymology: 源自古英语snaca，原指蛇，后因蛇的行进形态引申出动词‘蜿蜒行进’的含义

similar_words.description: snake为名词/动词，表‘蛇；蜿蜒行进’，snack为名词/动词，表‘小吃；吃零食’，二者拼写仅一个字母之差，极易混淆

definitions.meaning: （使）咔嚓折断；厉声说话；拍快照
definitions.example: The thick tree branch snapped under the weight of the heavy snow last winter night.
Making a snap decision without sufficient data analysis may lead to severe economic losses.

snap.etymology: 拟声词，模拟物体断裂的咔嚓声响，后引申出‘折断；厉声说；突然的’等多种含义

similar_words.description: 均含‘快速动作’之意，snap侧重快速折断、拍照或说话，snatch侧重快速抢夺、抓紧
snap可作动词/形容词，表‘折断；突然的’，nap为动词/名词，表‘打盹，小睡’，二者拼写接近易混淆

definitions.example: The thief snatched her smartphone and ran into the crowded street before she could call for help.

definitions.meaning: 抽空（做某事），抓紧（时间休息等）
definitions.example: The busy nurse managed to snatch ten minutes’ rest during the midday shift break.

snatch.etymology: 源自中古英语snacchen，原义为‘抓，抢夺’，后引申出‘抓紧时间’等含义

similar_words.description: 均含‘抢夺’之意，snatch侧重突然、快速地抢夺，常含粗鲁之意，grab侧重粗暴地抓牢、夺取
snatch侧重抢夺、抓紧，snap侧重折断、突然的，二者拼写接近易混淆

definitions.example: The journalist sneaked into the closed meeting room to collect first-hand information.
The army launched a sneak attack on the enemy’s supply base at midnight.

sneak.etymology: 词源可追溯至中古英语sneken，本义为悄悄移动，无通用可拆分派生词缀

similar_words.description: 二者均有悄悄移动的含义，sneak侧重刻意躲避他人注意，creep侧重缓慢、无声响地移动
二者拼写相近，snack意为「小吃、快餐」，与sneak语义完全不同

definitions.example: People with allergies often sneeze repeatedly when exposed to pollen.

sneeze.etymology: 来自古英语fnēosan，为拟声词，模拟打喷嚏的声音

similar_words.description: 二者均为与鼻部相关的动作，sneeze指打喷嚏，sniff指吸气嗅、抽鼻子

definitions.example: The detective sniffed the cloth carefully to find clues related to the case.
You shouldn’t sniff at this opportunity, as it may bring you unexpected career progress.

sniff.etymology: 拟声词，模拟抽鼻子或吸气嗅闻的声音，无通用可拆分词缀

similar_words.description: 二者均有闻的含义，sniff侧重有意识地出声吸气嗅闻，smell为通用词，可指闻到气味的状态或动作

definitions.example: The snob looked down on those who couldn’t afford luxury brands, which made him unpopular among his colleagues.

snob.etymology: 源自18世纪英国剑桥大学俚语，原指非贵族出身的学生，后引申为势利的人

similar_words.description: 二者拼写相近，snub意为「冷落、怠慢」，与snob语义完全不同
二者均有看不起他人的含义，snob侧重因出身、财富等势利眼，elitist侧重因自认为精英而优越感十足

definitions.example: The heavy snowstorm blocked most of the main roads in the northern region for three days.

definitions.meaning: （批评、抗议等的）铺天盖地的爆发
definitions.example: The company’s new policy triggered a snowstorm of criticism from consumers and environmental groups.

snowstorm.etymology: 合成词，由「snow（雪）」+「storm（风暴）」构成，引申义指大量突发的负面事件

similar_words.description: 二者均可指暴风雪，blizzard侧重持续时间长、风力强的暴雪，snowstorm为通用说法

definitions.example: Before cooking, you should soak the beans in clean water for at least two hours.

definitions.meaning: （大量）吸收（信息、经验等）；沉浸在
definitions.example: During the international summer camp, students soaked up diverse cultural experiences from all over the world.

soak.etymology: 来自古英语sōcian，本义为浸泡，无通用可拆分词缀

similar_words.description: 二者均有吸收的含义，soak侧重液体浸泡后的吸收，absorb为通用词，可指吸收液体、光、知识等多种对象
二者拼写仅一个字母之差，sock意为「袜子」，与soak语义完全不同

definitions.example: Washing hands with soap for 20 seconds is an effective way to prevent the spread of respiratory viruses.

soap.etymology: 来自古英语sāpe，原指用于清洗的油脂混合物

similar_words.description: 二者拼写仅一个字母之差，soup意为「汤、羹」，与soap语义完全不同，为高频易混词

definitions.meaning: （鸟、飞行器）高飞，翱翔；（价格、数量等）猛增，骤升
definitions.example: According to the latest economic report, the price of crude oil has soared by 30% in the past six months.

soar.etymology: 来自拉丁语exaurare，本义为升空，后引申为数值等大幅上升，为考研经济类文本高频词

similar_words.description: 二者均可表示数值等急剧上升，soar侧重持续快速上升，surge侧重突然的、汹涌式的增长
二者拼写相近，roar意为「咆哮、轰鸣」，与soar语义完全不同

definitions.example: Soaring housing prices have become a major concern for young people in many large cities worldwide.
His soaring ambition to fight against global inequality inspired many young environmental activists.

soaring.etymology: 为动词soar的现在分词作形容词，继承soar「高飞、猛增」的核心语义

similar_words.description: 二者拼写相近，roaring意为「咆哮的、轰鸣的」，与soaring语义完全不同

definitions.example: The little girl sobbed loudly when she found she had lost her favorite stuffed toy in the community park.

sob.etymology: 拟声词，模拟抽噎哭泣的声音，无通用可拆分词缀

similar_words.description: 二者均有哭泣的含义，sob侧重出声抽噎、啜泣，weep侧重无声地流泪，多因悲伤或感动
二者拼写仅首字母不同，rob意为「抢劫、盗取」，与sob语义完全不同

definitions.example: The witness testified that he was completely sober when the traffic accident occurred.
A sober assessment of the economic data indicates a potential recession.

sober.etymology: 源自拉丁语sobrius，可拆解联想为“so-（表否定）+ ber（词根，表‘饮用’）”，即“未过量饮酒的”，后引申出“理智的”义

similar_words.description: somber 意为“忧郁的；昏暗的”，与sober拼写仅一字母之差，易在快速阅读中混淆词义

definitions.example: The national soccer team qualified for the FIFA World Cup after 12 years of persistent training.

soccer.etymology: 源自英式英语，由association football缩写演化而来，无复杂词根

similar_words.description: football 在英式英语可指足球，美式英语常指橄榄球，而soccer专指足球运动

definitions.example: Sociable employees are more likely to build effective cross-departmental collaboration in modern enterprises.

sociable.etymology: 源自拉丁语sociabilis，词根soci-意为“同伴”，-able为形容词后缀表“可…的”，即“可成为同伴的”

similar_words.description: social 侧重“与社会或社交相关的”，sociable侧重人“好交际的”性格特质

definitions.example: Social media platforms have become a major channel for political mobilization and public opinion expression.
The university's alumni association held a formal social to celebrate its centennial anniversary.

social.etymology: 源自拉丁语socialis，词根soci-意为“同伴、联合”，-al为形容词后缀，作名词时为转义用法

similar_words.description: sociable 指人“好交际的”，social 侧重“社会或社交属性”，作名词指社交活动

definitions.example: The academic paper analyzes the theoretical evolution of scientific socialism in the 19th century.

socialism.etymology: 由social（社会的）+ -ism（名词后缀，表“主义、学说”）构成，指以社会整体利益为核心的社会制度

similar_words.description: capitalism 指“资本主义”，与socialism为对立的社会制度，常同时出现在考研政治/历史阅读中

definitions.example: Digital technology has fundamentally reshaped the structure and operation of contemporary society.
The Royal Society of London published a series of groundbreaking studies on renewable energy last year.

society.etymology: 源自拉丁语societas，词根soci-意为“同伴、联合”，-ty为名词后缀表“状态、群体”

similar_words.description: community 侧重“社区；特定小型群体”，society 侧重“整体社会；正式学术/行业社团”

definitions.example: Sociology focuses on the interactions between social institutions and individual behaviors in complex societies.

sociology.etymology: 由socio-（词根变体，表“社会”，源自soci-）+ -logy（名词后缀，表“学科”）构成，即研究社会的学科

similar_words.description: psychology 指“心理学”，二者均为社会科学学科，后缀-logy易导致学科名称记混

definitions.example: She folded her cotton socks and placed them neatly in the storage box.
The journalist was socked in the face by a protester during the public demonstration.

sock.etymology: 作名词源自古英语socc，作动词为口语转义的动作用法，无复杂词根

similar_words.description: stock 意为“库存；股票；家畜”，与sock拼写仅一字母之差，易在快速阅读中看错

definitions.example: The conference hall provides complimentary soda and bottled water for registered participants.

soda.etymology: 源自拉丁语soda，原指“碳酸钠”，后引申为含碳酸或苏打的饮料

similar_words.description: sofa 意为“沙发”，与soda拼写仅一字母之差（d与f），易在快速阅读中混淆

definitions.example: After completing the 10,000-word dissertation, the graduate student collapsed onto the sofa in the study.

sofa.etymology: 源自阿拉伯语soffa，原指“铺有垫子的长凳”，后演变为现代意义的沙发

similar_words.description: soda 意为“苏打水；汽水”，与sofa拼写仅一字母之差（f与d），易在快速阅读中混淆

definitions.example: Soft materials such as recycled foam are widely applied in noise reduction equipment for industrial workshops.
The soft lighting in the university reading room helps reduce eye strain for students staying up late to review lessons.

soft.etymology: 源自古英语「sōfte」，为日耳曼语族基础词汇，无复杂词缀结构。

similar_words.description: sort作名词表种类、作动词表排序，与soft拼写仅一个字母之差，易出现拼写混淆

definitions.example: Many high-tech enterprises allocate over 30% of their annual budget to developing secure and user-friendly software.

software.etymology: 合成词，由「soft（软的）」+「ware（制品）」构成，对应hardware（硬件）的构词逻辑。

similar_words.description: hardware意为硬件，与software为信息技术领域的对应名词，常同时出现在科技类阅读中

definitions.example: Long-term excessive use of chemical pesticides will cause irreversible damage to the ecological balance of agricultural soil.
The famous lawyer refused to take the case, as he did not want to soil his reputation of being fair and just.

soil.etymology: 源自古法语「soil」，原指泥地，后引申出动词义“弄脏”。

similar_words.description: oil意为油、石油，与soil拼写仅首字母不同，易出现形近混淆

definitions.example: Against the background of carbon neutrality, solar power generation has become a key development direction of the global new energy industry.

solar.etymology: 源自拉丁语词根「sol（太阳）」+形容词后缀「-ar」，表与太阳相关的属性。

similar_words.description: lunar意为月亮的、月球的，与solar为天文领域的对应形容词，常共同出现在科普类阅读中

definitions.example: The sole purpose of this longitudinal study is to explore the long-term impact of preschool education on children's cognitive development.
After three days of hiking in the wild, the soles of his hiking boots were worn thin.

sole.etymology: 同形异义词，作形容词时源自拉丁语「solus（单独的）」，作名词时源自古英语「sol（脚底）」。

similar_words.description: soul意为灵魂、心灵，与sole拼写仅第二个字母不同，是考研中极易混淆的形近词

definitions.example: On the National Memorial Day, all people across the country stood in silent tribute in a solemn atmosphere.

solemn.etymology: 源自拉丁语「sollemnis（宗教仪式的，正式的）」，经古法语传入英语后，保留了庄重、正式的核心含义。

similar_words.description: serious为通用词，可指态度认真或情况严重；solemn多指仪式、氛围等官方、庄重的状态

definitions.example: When the external temperature drops below the freezing point, water will gradually turn into solid ice.
His solid academic foundation and rich practical experience made him the best candidate for the research project.

solid.etymology: 源自拉丁语「solidus（完整的，结实的）」，进入英语后衍生出质地坚硬、基础扎实等多重含义。

similar_words.description: firm意为牢固的、坚定的，可指具体物品或抽象态度；solid更侧重质地坚硬或基础的扎实性

definitions.example: The international community called for global solidarity to jointly fight against the cross-border epidemic.

solidarity.etymology: 源自拉丁语词根「solidus（结实的）」+名词后缀「-arity」，引申为像整体一样紧密团结的状态。

similar_words.description: unity意为统一、团结，侧重整体的一致性；solidarity侧重不同群体为共同目标而相互支持的状态

definitions.example: To avoid unnecessary social interactions, the old professor chose to live a solitary life in a suburban cottage.
The novel describes a solitary who lived in the mountain forest for more than 30 years.

solitary.etymology: 源自拉丁语词根「solus（单独的）」+形容词后缀「-ary」，表与独处相关的状态或身份。

similar_words.description: solidarity为名词，意为团结，与solitary前缀相同但语义相反，易出现拼写和语义混淆

definitions.example: Many outstanding philosophers and writers believe that proper solitude helps people think deeply and gain inspiration.

solitude.etymology: 源自拉丁语词根「solus（单独的）」+抽象名词后缀「-tude」，表独处的抽象状态。

similar_words.description: solitary为形容词，意为单独的；solitude为名词，表独处的状态，二者为同根词性转换词

definitions.example: The astronaut completed a solo spacewalk that lasted over seven hours.
Her piano solo at the graduation ceremony received a standing ovation.

solo.etymology: 词根「sol-（单独）」，源自意大利语solo，原指「独自表演的音乐家」，与单词「sole（唯一的）」同根

similar_words.description: 「sole」侧重表示「唯一的；鞋底」，「solo」侧重表示「独自的；独奏表演」，二者共享词根「sol-（单独）」

definitions.example: Salt is soluble in water, which is a basic chemical phenomenon.
Even the most challenging environmental issues are soluble with global cooperation.

soluble.etymology: 词根「solut-（解开、溶解）」，后缀「-ible（可…的）」，与单词「solve（解决）」「solution（溶液）」同根

similar_words.description: 「solvable」仅表示「可解决的」，「soluble」还可表示「可溶的」

definitions.example: The government is seeking a viable solution to the urban housing shortage crisis.
The scientists tested the PH value of the chemical solution in the laboratory.

solution.etymology: 词根「solut-（解开）」，后缀「-ion（名词后缀）」，源自拉丁语solutio，原指「解开、溶解」，后引申为「解决办法」

similar_words.description: 「resolution」侧重表示「决议；决心；正式解决」，「solution」侧重表示「具体解决办法；溶液」

definitions.example: Researchers have spent decades trying to solve the mystery of ancient climate change.

solve.etymology: 词根「solv-（解开）」，源自拉丁语solvere，原指「解开、溶解」，与单词「solution（解决办法）」「soluble（可溶的）」同根

similar_words.description: 「resolve」侧重表示「解决争端/冲突；下定决心」，「solve」侧重表示「解答谜题/解决具体问题」

definitions.meaning: 以某种方式；不知怎么地
definitions.example: Somehow, the remote mountain village has preserved its traditional folk customs for centuries.

somehow.etymology: 合成词，由「some（某种）」+「how（方式）」构成，表「以某种未知或未指明的方式」

similar_words.description: 「somewhat」表「稍微、有点」（表程度），「somehow」表「以某种方式；不知怎么地」（表方式）

definitions.meaning: 在某一时候（过去或将来）
definitions.example: Sometime next semester, the university will launch a new course on digital humanities.
The sometime mayor of the city is now a visiting professor at a top university.

sometime.etymology: 合成词，由「some（某一）」+「time（时间）」构成，表「不确定的某一时间点（过去或将来）」

similar_words.description: 「sometimes」表「有时」（表频率），「sometime」表「在某一时候（过去/将来）」（表时间点）

definitions.example: The experimental results were somewhat inconsistent with the initial theoretical hypothesis.

somewhat.etymology: 合成词，由「some（一些）」+「what（程度）」构成，表「在一定程度上，稍微」

similar_words.description: 「somehow」表「以某种方式；不知怎么地」（表方式），「somewhat」表「稍微、有点」（表程度）

definitions.example: The sophisticated weather forecasting system can predict typhoon paths with high accuracy.
The sophisticated journalist has reported from over 50 countries around the world.

sophisticated.etymology: 词根「soph-（智慧）」，后缀「-ist（表人）」+「-icated（形容词后缀）」，原指「有智慧的人」，后引申为「精密的、老练的」，与单词「sophomore（大二学生）」同根

similar_words.description: 「complicated」仅表示「结构复杂的」，「sophisticated」还可表示「老练的；尖端精密的」

definitions.example: As a sophomore majoring in sociology, he has participated in three field research projects.

sophomore.etymology: 词根「soph-（智慧）」+「moros（傻瓜）」，原指「半懂不懂的人」，后固定表示「大学二年级学生」（因大一学生刚入门，大二自认为有智慧但实则仍有不足）

similar_words.description: 「sophisticated」表「精密的；老练的」，「sophomore」表「大学二年级学生」，二者共享词根「soph-（智慧）」

definitions.example: After climbing the high mountain, the hikers had sore legs for three days.
He was still sore about being rejected by the graduate school last month.

sore.etymology: 源自古英语sar，表「疼痛的」，与德语sahr（疼痛的）同源，后引申为「恼火的」

similar_words.description: 「sour」表「酸的；发酵的」，「sore」表「疼痛的；恼火的」

definitions.example: In literary works of the Victorian era, sorrow is often depicted as a quiet, lingering emotion rather than a violent outburst.

sorrow.etymology: 来自古英语sorg，词根sorg-表“悲伤”

similar_words.description: 「sore」为形容词，表“疼痛的；恼火的”；「sorrow」为名词，表“悲伤”

definitions.example: The research paper categorizes different sorts of renewable energy technologies based on their efficiency.
Librarians spend hours each day sorting books according to the Dewey Decimal System.

sort.etymology: 来自古法语sorte，词根sort-表“种类，命运”

similar_words.description: 「kind」侧重“具体某一类事物”；「sort」侧重“大致的类别”，口语中可互换

definitions.example: The philosopher argued that art is the expression of the human soul rather than a mere imitation of reality.

soul.etymology: 来自古英语sawol，词根sawol-表“灵魂，生命”

similar_words.description: 「sole」多作形容词表“唯一的”或名词表“鞋底”；「soul」为名词表“灵魂”

definitions.example: The cafeteria serves a variety of hot soups during the winter semester to keep students warm.
Without sufficient funding, the small research team found itself in the soup halfway through the experiment.

soup.etymology: 来自古法语soupe，词根soupe-表“浸泡的食物”，后引申为“汤”

similar_words.description: 「soap」为名词表“肥皂”；「soup」为名词表“汤；困境”

definitions.meaning: 酸的；（关系、态度）尖酸的，恶化的
definitions.example: The sour taste of unripe fruits is caused by high levels of citric acid; similarly, sour relations between the two countries hindered bilateral trade.

sour.etymology: 来自古英语sur，词根sur-表“酸的”

similar_words.description: 「sore」为形容词表“疼痛的”；「sour」为形容词表“酸的；尖酸的”

definitions.example: The study traced the source of the pollution to a chemical factory located 10 kilometers upstream of the river.

source.etymology: 来自古法语sourse，词根sour-表“升起，流出”

similar_words.description: 「resource」指“可利用的资源（如人力、自然资源）”；「source」指“事物的来源、出处”

definitions.example: The southeast region of the country has witnessed rapid economic growth due to its developed port infrastructure.

southeast.etymology: 合成词，由south（南）+east（东）构成，表“东南方向”

similar_words.description: 「southwest」指“西南方向”；「southeast」指“东南方向”，均为方位合成词

definitions.example: The southern hemisphere experiences summer when the northern hemisphere is in winter, due to the tilt of the Earth’s axis.

southern.etymology: 由south（南）+形容词后缀-ern构成，表“…方向的”

similar_words.description: 「northern」指“北方的”；「southern」指“南方的”，均为方位形容词

definitions.example: The southwest part of the forest is a protected habitat for several endangered species of birds.

southwest.etymology: 合成词，由south（南）+west（西）构成，表“西南方向”

similar_words.description: 「southeast」指“东南方向”；「southwest」指“西南方向”，均为方位合成词

definitions.example: The international organization respects the right of every sovereign state to make its own domestic policies without external interference.
In medieval Europe, a sovereign held absolute power over his kingdom and its people.

sovereign.etymology: 来自古法语soverain，词根sover-表“最高的”，引申为“主权的；君主”

similar_words.description: 「reign」为动词/名词表“统治；统治时期”；「sovereign」为形容词/名词表“有主权的；君主”，同根表“最高统治”

definitions.example: Farmers sow wheat seeds in late autumn to ensure a stable harvest in the coming year.
Some unregulated social media accounts sow distrust between ethnic groups to attract more followers.

sow.etymology: 与古英语「sawan」（播种）同源，和「seed」为同根词，表“播撒、扩散”之意

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，「saw」为「see」的过去式或指‘锯子’，「sow」核心义为‘播种’

definitions.example: The university’s new library reserves a large open space for students to hold group discussions and exhibitions.
The interior designer spaced the wall paintings evenly to create a balanced visual effect.

space.etymology: 源自拉丁语「spatium」（空间、距离），为英语中表示‘空间’的核心词根

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，「spice」指‘香料、调味品’，「space」核心义为‘空间’

definitions.meaning: 航天器；宇宙飞船（泛指各类航天运载工具）
definitions.example: China’s Tianwen-1 spacecraft successfully landed on the surface of Mars in May 2021.

spacecraft.etymology: 合成词，由「space（太空）+craft（运载工具、船舶）」构成，特指用于太空探索的运载工具

similar_words.description: 近义易混，「spacecraft」泛指所有航天器（含探测器、卫星等），「spaceship」特指载人宇宙飞船

definitions.example: The three astronauts aboard the Shenzhou-15 spaceship completed their six-month space mission in June 2023.

spaceship.etymology: 合成词，由「space（太空）+ship（船）」构成，特指搭载航天员的载人航天工具

similar_words.description: 近义易混，「spaceship」特指载人航天器，「spacecraft」为各类航天器的统称

definitions.example: The spacious conference hall can accommodate up to 800 participants for international academic forums.

spacious.etymology: 派生词，由「space（空间）+形容词后缀-ious（充满…的）」构成，表‘充满空间的’

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，「spurious」意为‘虚假的、伪造的’，「spacious」意为‘宽敞的’

definitions.example: The gardener used a spade to dig a deep hole for the newly purchased cherry sapling.
In the final round of the poker game, he drew the ace of spades and won the grand prize.

spade.etymology: 源自古英语「spadu」（挖掘工具），最初指用于挖掘的铲子，后衍生纸牌花色义

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，「spay」为动词，指‘切除（雌性动物的）卵巢’，「spade」核心义为‘铲子’

definitions.meaning: 跨度；跨距；（时间的）一段，持续时间
definitions.example: The newly built cable-stayed bridge has a main span of 1,500 meters, ranking among the world’s longest.
The research project on renewable energy spanned five years and involved scholars from 12 countries.

span.etymology: 源自古英语「spannan」（伸展、拉紧），与「expand」同根，表‘延伸、覆盖’之意

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，「spin」意为‘旋转；纺纱’，「span」核心义为‘跨度、持续’

definitions.meaning: 火花；火星；（引发事件的）诱因，导火线
definitions.example: A small spark from the faulty electrical wire triggered a major fire in the warehouse.
The Nobel laureate’s speech on climate change sparked a global debate among policymakers and scholars.

spark.etymology: 源自古英语「spearca」（火花、火星），后引申为‘引发事件的微小因素’

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，「spank」为动词，指‘打（屁股）’，「spark」核心义为‘火花、引发’

definitions.example: The diamond necklace sparkled under the chandelier, attracting the attention of all guests at the banquet.
There was a sparkle of excitement in her eyes when she received the admission letter from her dream university.

sparkle.etymology: 派生词，由「spark（火花）+动词后缀-le（表反复动作）」构成，指反复发出火花般的微光

similar_words.description: 近义易混，「sparkle」侧重持续、微弱的闪烁或人的活力，「spark」侧重单次的火花或突然引发事件

definitions.example: The keynote speaker at the international education summit shared his insights on online learning.
The Speaker of the US House of Representatives announced the start of the vote on the infrastructure bill.

speaker.etymology: 派生词，由「speak（说、讲）+名词后缀-er（表执行者）」构成，核心指‘说话的人或装置’

similar_words.description: 同根易混，「speech」指‘演讲内容、讲话’，「speaker」指‘演讲者、扬声器’

definitions.example: Archaeologists found a well-preserved stone spear in the ancient burial site.
The hunter speared a wild rabbit with a sharpened stick.

spear.etymology: 源自古英语spere，表「投掷用的长柄武器」，无明显派生词缀

similar_words.description: 「spear」指「矛、标枪」，「sphere」指「球体、领域」，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: Special measures have been taken to protect the endangered species from extinction.

special.etymology: 源自拉丁语specialis，由词根「spec-（看、种类）」+ 形容词后缀「-ial」构成，表「属于某一特定种类的、特别的」

similar_words.description: 「special」侧重「与众不同的、特殊的」，「specific」侧重「具体的、明确的」，近义易混淆

definitions.example: A team of medical specialists was dispatched to the disaster area to provide emergency treatment.

specialist.etymology: 由「special（特殊的）」+ 表人的名词后缀「-ist」构成，指「专攻某一特定领域的人」

similar_words.description: 「specialist」侧重「某一专科领域的专家」，「expert」侧重「具备专业技能的行家」，近义易混淆

definitions.example: Traditional Peking duck is the speciality of this century-old restaurant.

speciality.etymology: 由「special（特殊的）」+ 名词后缀「-ity」构成，表「特殊的事物或专长领域」

similar_words.description: 「speciality」为英式拼写，「specialty」为美式拼写，均表「专长、特色」，拼写变体易混淆

definitions.example: Many biology students choose to specialize in environmental science after completing their general courses.

specialize.etymology: 由「special（特殊的）」+ 动词后缀「-ize（使…化）」构成，表「使成为某一专门领域、专攻」

similar_words.description: 「specialize」表「专攻」，「specify」表「明确说明」，同根形近易混淆

definitions.example: Climate change poses a grave threat to the survival of thousands of plant and animal species.

species.etymology: 源自拉丁语species，由词根「spec-（看、种类）」构成，原指「外貌、类别」，后专指生物学意义上的「物种」

similar_words.description: 「species」表「物种、种类」，「specimen」表「标本、样本」，同根形近易混淆

definitions.example: The research paper requires specific data to support its argument about economic inequality.
The doctor prescribed a specific for the patient's rare infection.

specific.etymology: 由词根「spec-（看、种类）」+ 形容词后缀「-ific（使…的）」构成，表「能明确被观察到的、具体的」

similar_words.description: 「specific」侧重「具体明确的」，「special」侧重「与众不同的」，近义易混淆

definitions.example: The engineering team strictly followed the design specifications to build the new bridge.

specification.etymology: 由动词「specify（明确说明）」+ 名词后缀「-ation」构成，表「被明确说明的内容、规格」

similar_words.description: 「specification」表「规格、说明书」，「specimen」表「标本、样本」，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: Please specify your preferred date of delivery when filling out the order form.

specify.etymology: 由词根「spec-（看、种类）」+ 动词后缀「-ify（使…）」构成，表「使内容明确可被观察、详细说明」

similar_words.description: 「specify」表「明确说明」，「specialize」表「专攻」，同根形近易混淆

definitions.example: Scientists collected rock specimens from the crater to study the planet's geological history.
His rude remark was a perfect specimen of poor manners.

specimen.etymology: 源自拉丁语specimen，由词根「spec-（看）」构成，表「供观察研究的样本、标本」

similar_words.description: 「specimen」表「标本、样本」，「species」表「物种、种类」，同根形近易混淆

definitions.example: The grand opening ceremony of the 2008 Beijing Olympic Games was an unforgettable spectacle.
He wore rimless spectacles when reading academic journals.

spectacle.etymology: 词根「spect-」（意为‘看’）+ 名词后缀「-acle」（表事物），原指‘被观看的事物’，后引申出‘眼镜（供观看的工具）’义

similar_words.description: 同根于「spect-」（看），spectacle指被观看的景象/眼镜，spectator指观看的观众
拼写接近，spectacle指景象/眼镜，specter指幽灵、恐惧

definitions.example: The alpine scenery in Switzerland is so spectacular that it draws tourists from all over the world.
The annual New Year's Eve fireworks display is one of the city's most popular spectaculars.

spectacular.etymology: 词根「spect-」（看）+ 形容词后缀「-acular」（表‘具有…特征的’），意为‘值得观看的’，名词义由形容词引申而来

similar_words.description: 同根于「spect-」，spectacular为形容词表壮观的，spectacle为名词表壮观景象
拼写接近，spectacular表壮观的，speculate表推测、投机

definitions.example: Thousands of spectators packed the stadium to watch the final of the national football league.

spectator.etymology: 词根「spect-」（看）+ 名词后缀「-ator」（表‘做…的人’），指‘观看活动的人’

similar_words.description: 均指观众，spectator多指体育赛事等的现场观众，audience可指戏剧、演讲等的观众/听众
同根于「spect-」，spectator指观看者，spectacle指被观看的景象

definitions.example: The research covers a broad spectrum of social problems, including income inequality and mental health.
White light can be separated into a spectrum of seven colors through a prism.

spectrum.etymology: 词根「spect-」（看）+ 名词后缀「-rum」，原指‘可见的光的范围’，后引申为抽象的‘范围、系列’

similar_words.description: 均表范围，spectrum强调连续、宽泛的系列，range侧重具体的区间或幅度
同根于「spect-」，spectrum表范围/光谱，spectacle表景象

definitions.example: Many historians speculate that the ancient Mayan civilization collapsed due to prolonged drought.
Some wealthy investors speculate in the stock market without conducting thorough research.

speculate.etymology: 词根「spect-」（看）+ 动词后缀「-ulate」，原指‘观察后进行思考推断’，后引申为‘商业投机（观察市场后下注）’

similar_words.description: 均表推测，speculate较正式，可用于学术研究，conjecture更侧重无充分依据的猜测
拼写接近，speculate为动词表推测/投机，spectacular为形容词表壮观的

definitions.example: The Nobel laureate delivered a thought-provoking speech on climate change at the international forum.
The research explores how children develop speech and language skills in early childhood.

speech.etymology: 来自古英语「spæc」，与动词「speak」同源，原指‘说话的行为’，后引申为‘演讲’义

similar_words.description: 均指公开讲话，speech多指正式演讲或日常言语，lecture侧重学术性讲座
address指正式的官方或重要场合的演讲，speech适用范围更广

definitions.example: The high-speed maglev train in Shanghai can reach a top speed of 430 kilometers per hour.
The emergency vehicle speeded through the busy street to reach the accident site.

speed.etymology: 来自古英语「spēd」，原指‘成功、快速’，后核心义固定为‘移动的速率’

similar_words.description: 均表速度，speed为常用通用词，velocity为物理学术语，强调运动的方向和速率
rate可表速率，侧重比率或变化的快慢，speed侧重移动的绝对快慢

definitions.example: Please spell your surname clearly for the hotel check-in form.

definitions.meaning: 咒语，符咒；（困境的）一段时间
definitions.example: After a long spell of economic recession, the country's economy began to recover gradually.

spell.etymology: 来自古英语「spellian」，原指‘讲述、念诵’，后引申出‘拼写（念出字母）’‘咒语（念出魔力词句）’‘一段时间（讲述的时长）’义

similar_words.description: 同根衍生，spell为多义词（拼写/咒语/时段），spelling为名词专指拼写（行为或结果）
均表有魔力的物品/词句，spell侧重书面或口头的咒语，charm侧重护身符或个人魅力

definitions.example: The teacher deducted points for incorrect spelling in the student's English composition.

spelling.etymology: 由动词「spell」（拼写）+ 名词后缀「-ing」构成，专指‘拼写的行为或结果’

similar_words.description: 同根衍生，spelling为名词专指拼写，spell为多义词，可表拼写、咒语等
均表拼写相关，spelling为常用通用词，orthography为正式学术用词，指‘正字法’

definitions.example: The new regulation will have a significant impact on the sphere of digital media regulation.
Geographers confirm that the Earth is an oblate sphere rather than a perfect round ball.

sphere.etymology: 来自希腊语「sphaira」，原指‘几何球体’，后引申为抽象的‘活动、影响的领域’

similar_words.description: 均表领域，sphere侧重抽象的活动或影响范围，domain侧重专属的、可控的专业领域
均表球形物体，globe侧重地球或地球仪，sphere侧重几何球体或抽象领域

definitions.example: Many Southeast Asian cuisines featured in food-themed reading passages are known for their spicy flavours and layered taste profiles.

definitions.meaning: （评论、话题等）尖刻的，有争议性的
definitions.example: The columnist’s spicy remarks on social inequality sparked widespread debate among readers.

spicy.etymology: 来自名词spice（香料）+形容词后缀-y，表「具有…性质的」

similar_words.description: 「spiny」为形容词，指多刺的、刺状的；「spicy」指辛辣的、尖刻的，二者拼写仅差一个字母易混淆

definitions.example: Biological research papers often mention that certain spider species can adjust their web structures to adapt to rainy weather.

definitions.meaning: （摄影、工程用的）三脚架，蜘蛛架
definitions.example: The lab technician placed the precision camera on a spider to capture stable images of the cell samples.

spider.etymology: 与动词spin（纺线）同源，因蜘蛛结网的动作类似纺线得名

similar_words.description: 「spicer」为名词，指香料商；「spider」指蜘蛛或三脚架，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: Environmental reports warn that an oil spill in the offshore area could cause irreversible damage to the coastal ecosystem.
An anonymous insider spilled details of the company’s upcoming merger plan to the financial press.

spill.etymology: 来自古英语spillan，原义为破坏、损耗，后演变为洒出、泄露

similar_words.description: 「spell」多作动词表拼写、拼读，作名词表咒语、一段时间；「spill」指洒出、泄露，二者拼写仅差一个字母易混淆

definitions.example: Physics reading materials explain that wind turbine blades spin rapidly to convert kinetic energy into electrical power.
The marketing team tried to spin the product’s minor flaw as a unique selling point to attract consumers.

spin.etymology: 来自古英语spinnan，原义为纺线，因纺线时纺锤旋转引申出旋转义，后又引申为编造话语（类似纺线编织）

similar_words.description: 「span」作名词指跨度、范围，作动词指持续；「spin」指旋转、杜撰，二者拼写仅差一个字母易混淆

definitions.example: Medical research shows that prolonged sitting with incorrect posture may lead to chronic strain on the spine and lumbar muscles.
The title of the academic monograph is printed in bold silver letters on its navy blue spine.

spine.etymology: 来自拉丁语spina，原义为刺、棘，后引申为脊柱、书脊等起支撑作用的条状结构

similar_words.description: 「spire」为名词，指（教堂等建筑的）尖顶；「spine」指脊柱、书脊、刺，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.meaning: （价格、债务、局势等）螺旋式急剧变化（常指恶化）
definitions.example: Economic analysts warn that without timely intervention, inflation could spiral out of control within six months.
Paleontologists found a fossil with a clear spiral structure, which belongs to an ancient ammonite species.

spiral.etymology: 来自拉丁语spira，原义为线圈、卷，引申为螺旋形及螺旋式变化义

similar_words.description: 「spin」指物体自身绕轴心快速旋转；「spiral」指物体沿螺旋轨迹运动，或指数额、局势呈螺旋式变化

definitions.meaning: 精神，意志；（复数）情绪，心情
definitions.example: The reformer’s pioneering spirit has been widely praised in essays discussing social transformation.
Import tariffs on foreign spirit have been adjusted to promote the development of domestic wine industry.

spirit.etymology: 来自拉丁语spiritus，原义为呼吸，古人认为呼吸是灵魂的体现，后引申为精神、灵魂，又引申为烈酒（因烈酒能刺激气血）

similar_words.description: 「soul」侧重指灵魂、心灵，带有较强的宗教或个人情感色彩；「spirit」侧重指精神、意志，也可指情绪、烈酒

definitions.example: Many people pursue artistic creation to obtain spiritual satisfaction beyond material comforts.
The institution provides both material assistance and spiritual support to people in disadvantaged communities.

spiritual.etymology: 来自名词spirit（精神）+形容词后缀-al，表「与…相关的」

similar_words.description: 「spirituous」为形容词，指含酒精的、烈性的；「spiritual」指精神的、宗教的，二者拼写极为相近易混淆

definitions.meaning: 吐（唾液等）；啐（表愤怒或鄙视）；唾液
definitions.example: Public health posters remind citizens not to spit in crowded areas to prevent the spread of respiratory diseases.

definitions.meaning: （炉火、引擎等）喷出（火花、油点等）
definitions.example: The old iron furnace began to spit sparks as the fire was stoked up in the winter morning.

spit.etymology: 来自古英语spittan，为拟声词，模拟吐痰的声音，后引申为喷出义

similar_words.description: 「spite」作名词指恶意、怨恨，作动词指故意刁难；「spit」指吐、喷出，二者拼写仅差一个字母易混淆

definitions.example: Many behavioral studies show that people may make irrational decisions out of spite when they feel treated unfairly.
He deliberately missed the deadline to spite his supervisor who had criticized him publicly.

spite.etymology: 来自古英语spiht，原义为怨恨、敌意，后演变为动词义故意刁难

similar_words.description: 「despite」为介词，直接接宾语表「尽管」；「spite」为名词，需构成短语「in spite of」表「尽管」，本身核心义为恶意、怨恨

definitions.example: The heavy rain splashed the mud onto the clean window of the university’s library building.
A loud splash echoed as the research boat dropped the sensor into the deep ocean.

splash.etymology: 拟声词，模拟液体溅泼的声音，无明显词缀派生

similar_words.description: slash为动词意为「砍，削减」，与splash拼写仅差字母p，易混淆

definitions.example: The splendid architecture of the ancient academy has become a landmark of the city’s cultural heritage.

splendid.etymology: 源自拉丁语splendere（发光），形容词后缀-id表「具有某种性质的」，本义为「发光的」，引申为「辉煌的」

similar_words.description: splendor为同根名词，意为「壮丽，辉煌」，与splendid（形容词）词性不同

definitions.example: The interdisciplinary research team split into two subgroups to conduct parallel experiments on climate change.

split.etymology: 源自古英语splittan，无明显词缀，原指「撕裂木材」，引申为「分裂」

similar_words.description: spilt为动词spill（溢出）的过去式/过去分词，与split拼写仅字母i和l的顺序有差，易混淆

definitions.example: Excessive praise from parents may spoil a child’s ability to cope with setbacks independently.
The unrefrigerated dairy products spoiled quickly in the hot summer weather.

spoil.etymology: 源自拉丁语spolium（战利品，剥下的皮），原指「剥夺」，后引申为「破坏」

similar_words.description: spool为名词意为「线轴」，与spoil拼写仅差字母i和o的位置，易混淆

definitions.example: The government spokesman issued a formal statement addressing the public’s concerns about environmental policies.

spokesman.etymology: 由表「说话」的词根变体spoke + 表「人」的man构成，指代表群体发声的男性

similar_words.description: spokesperson为中性名词，指代不分性别的「发言人」，与spokesman（特指男性）语义范围不同

definitions.example: The biologist used a sterile sponge to collect surface water samples from the wetland for microbial analysis.
He sponged off his elderly parents for three years instead of seeking full-time employment.

sponge.etymology: 源自希腊语spongos，无明显词缀派生，本义为「海绵」

similar_words.description: spouse为名词意为「配偶」，与sponge拼写仅差字母n和u的位置，易混淆

definitions.example: The international symposium on artificial intelligence was funded by several renowned corporate sponsors.
The university sponsored a series of lectures to promote interdisciplinary academic exchange.

sponsor.etymology: 源自拉丁语spondere（承诺），名词后缀-or表「人」，本义为「承诺资助的人」，引申为「赞助者」

similar_words.description: sponsorship为同根名词，意为「赞助（行为/身份）」，与sponsor（表人/动词）的词性或语义不同

definitions.meaning: 自发的，自然的；无意识的
definitions.example: Spontaneous applause broke out in the lecture hall when the Nobel laureate finished his speech.

spontaneous.etymology: 源自拉丁语spontaneus（自愿的），词根spont-表「自愿」，形容词后缀-aneous表「具有某种性质的」

similar_words.description: simultaneous为形容词意为「同时的」，与spontaneous均含-taneous后缀，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The lab technician used a small plastic spoon to transfer the powdered reagent into the Erlenmeyer flask.

spoon.etymology: 源自古英语spon（木片），原指「木勺」，无明显词缀派生

similar_words.description: spawn为动词意为「产卵」，与spoon拼写仅差字母o和a的位置，易混淆

definitions.meaning: 运动员；具有运动员精神的人
definitions.example: The retired sportsman established a foundation to support young athletes from rural areas.

sportsman.etymology: 由名词sport（运动）+ s + 名词man（人）构成，指从事体育运动的人

similar_words.description: sportswoman为同根名词，特指「女运动员」，与sportsman（原指男运动员，现可泛指但仍多偏指男）语义范围不同

definitions.meaning: 发现，认出（尤指不易察觉或令人吃惊的事物）
definitions.example: Researchers spotted a rare species of bird in the remote forest during their ecological survey.
Emergency services arrived at the spot within ten minutes after the accident was reported.

spot.etymology: 源自古英语「spott」，原指斑点、标记，后引申出地点、发现等含义

similar_words.description: 「spot」表发现、地点等含义；「spit」表吐、唾液等含义，拼写仅一个字母差异

definitions.example: Many companies provide health insurance plans that cover not only employees but also their spouses and children.

spouse.etymology: 源自古法语「spous」，源自拉丁语「sponsus（新郎）/sponsa（新娘）」，指婚姻伴侣

similar_words.description: 「spouse」指配偶；「spice」指香料、调味品，拼写接近

definitions.example: Farmers spray pesticides on crops to prevent insect damage, but excessive use may harm the local ecosystem.
A light spray of rain fell as the boat cut through the calm lake.

spray.etymology: 源自中古荷兰语「spraey」，原指飞溅的水，后引申为喷洒、喷雾等含义

similar_words.description: 「spray」侧重用工具喷射液体或细雾；「sprinkle」侧重撒细碎颗粒如盐、香草

definitions.example: The rapid spread of misinformation on social media has become a pressing issue for global policymakers.
The report analyzes the spread of income inequality across different regions of the country.

spread.etymology: 源自古英语「sprædan」，原指展开、铺开，后引申为传播等含义

similar_words.description: 「spread」侧重均匀铺开、传播，可及物或不及物；「scatter」侧重不规则驱散、散落

definitions.meaning: 撒，洒（细碎物质，如盐、香草等）
definitions.example: Professional chefs often sprinkle fresh basil leaves over pasta dishes to enhance flavor and visual appeal.

sprinkle.etymology: 源自中古英语「sprenkelen」，原指撒小颗粒，后引申为撒、洒等含义

similar_words.description: 「sprinkle」侧重撒细碎颗粒；「spray」侧重喷射液体或细雾

definitions.example: New residential communities have sprouted up along the urban riverfront in the past three years.

sprout.etymology: 源自古英语「sprutan」，原指发芽，后引申为涌现等含义

similar_words.description: 「sprout」指发芽、涌现；「spout」指喷口、滔滔不绝地说，拼写差异在字母r的位置

definitions.example: The government's new tax incentives are designed to spur private investment in renewable energy projects.

spur.etymology: 源自古英语「spura」，原指马刺，后引申为激励、促进等含义

similar_words.description: 「spur」指激励、促进；「spurn」指蔑视、拒绝，拼写多一个字母n

definitions.example: The historical novel depicts a spy who infiltrated the enemy's military intelligence agency during World War II.
Local journalists were accused of spying on the celebrity's private residence without legal permission.

spy.etymology: 源自古法语「espier」，原指窥视，后引申为间谍、监视等含义

similar_words.description: 「spy」指间谍、监视；「spay」指切除雌性动物卵巢，拼写多一个字母a

definitions.example: The environmental study found that a square meter of tropical rainforest absorbs approximately 25 kilograms of carbon dioxide annually.
Thousands of citizens gathered in the central square to demand government action on climate change.

square.etymology: 源自古法语「esquare」，源自拉丁语「quadra（正方形）」，后引申为广场、平方等含义

similar_words.description: 「square」指正方形、广场；「squad」指小队、小组，拼写差异在字母r的位置

definitions.meaning: 挤压，捏；压榨（引申为压缩开支等）
definitions.example: Many small retail businesses have to squeeze their operational budgets to survive during the economic downturn.

squeeze.etymology: 源自中古英语「squesen」，原指挤压，后引申为压榨、压缩等含义

similar_words.description: 「squeeze」指挤压、压榨；「squeak」指吱吱叫、尖声，拼写差异在字母e的数量和位置

definitions.example: Wild squirrels often store nuts in tree hollows or underground burrows to survive the harsh winter.
She squirreled away her savings in a secret bank account to avoid unnecessary extravagant expenses.

squirrel.etymology: 来自古法语esquirel，原指松鼠的毛皮，后引申指松鼠本身；动词义由松鼠贮藏坚果的习性引申而来

similar_words.description: 「squire」意为乡绅、大地主，与「squirrel」（松鼠、贮藏）拼写仅差1个字母，易混淆

definitions.example: The attacker stabbed the victim in the abdomen with a sharp dagger before escaping from the crime scene.
His betrayal of his best friend was a cruel stab in the back that hurt everyone who knew them.

stab.etymology: 来自古挪威语stabbna，为拟声词，模仿刺戳的动作声，后引申出比喻义

similar_words.description: 「stub」多指掐灭烟头、树桩残根，与「stab」（刺戳、刺痛）动作或语义指向不同

definitions.example: The central bank has launched a series of targeted monetary policies to safeguard domestic financial stability amid global market turbulence.

stability.etymology: 由拉丁语词根「stabil-」（稳定的）+名词后缀「-ity」构成，表稳定的抽象状态

similar_words.description: 「stabilization」指“稳定化的过程”（动态）；「stability」指“稳定的状态”（静态），二者语义侧重不同

definitions.example: A stable social order and sound legal system are essential for attracting foreign direct investment.
The equestrian champion built a spacious and well-equipped stable to house his top-tier racehorses.

stable.etymology: 来自拉丁语「stabilis」（能站立的），形容词义表稳固的；名词义由马站立的地方引申而来，为熟词僻义

similar_words.description: 「steadfast」侧重形容人意志坚定、不动摇；「stable」侧重形容事物状态稳定、无波动

definitions.example: The research assistant placed a neat stack of peer-reviewed journal articles on the supervisor's desk for review.
Logistics workers stacked the cartons of disaster relief supplies in an orderly manner to facilitate timely distribution.

stack.etymology: 来自古诺斯语「stakkr」，原指谷物堆，后引申为各类堆叠的物品，以及堆的动作

similar_words.description: 「stock」多指库存、股票或家畜；「stack」指堆叠成的垛、堆或堆积的动作

definitions.example: The 2022 FIFA World Cup final was held at the Lusail Stadium in Qatar, which can accommodate over 80,000 spectators.

stadium.etymology: 来自希腊语「stadion」，原指古希腊长度单位（约607英尺），后指长度为1 stadia的赛跑场地，引申为现代体育场

similar_words.description: 「gymnasium」指室内体育馆或健身房；「stadium」指室外大型体育场，多用于举办大型赛事

definitions.meaning: 全体工作人员，全体职员
definitions.example: The university's administration organized a professional training workshop to enhance the staff's teaching and research capabilities.
The elderly Nobel laureate relied on a carved wooden staff to walk to the lecture hall every day.

staff.etymology: 来自古英语「stæf」，原指棍子、拐杖；后引申为支撑机构运作的人员（即职员），为核心义

similar_words.description: 「stuff」泛指材料、东西等抽象或具体事物；「staff」指全体工作人员或拐杖

definitions.example: The project is now in the crucial stage of data validation, which will determine the accuracy of the final research findings.
The National Centre for the Performing Arts will stage a series of classic operas to celebrate its 15th anniversary.

stage.etymology: 来自古法语「estage」，原指站立的平台，后引申为舞台、发展阶段，以及上演的动作

similar_words.description: 「phase」多指自然或科学过程中的阶段（如月相、物理相位）；「stage」多指人为或社会发展的阶段（如项目阶段、人生阶段）

definitions.meaning: 蹒跚，摇晃（因疲劳、醉酒等）
definitions.example: The mountaineer, who had been stranded on the peak for two days without food, staggered into the rescue camp at dawn.
The news of the renowned scientist's sudden passing staggered the entire academic community.

stagger.etymology: 来自古挪威语「stakra」，为拟声词，模仿摇晃的动作；后引申出使震惊的比喻义

similar_words.description: 「stumble」侧重指不小心绊倒或说话结巴；「stagger」侧重指因体力不支或醉酒而摇晃行走，或指使人震惊

definitions.meaning: 令人震惊的，惊人的（尤指数量、规模）
definitions.example: The staggering amount of carbon dioxide emitted by industrial activities has accelerated global warming.
The staggering drunkard stumbled into the street and nearly collided with a passing bicycle.

staggering.etymology: 由动词「stagger」（摇晃、使震惊）+形容词后缀「-ing」构成，表具有该动作特征的

similar_words.description: 「astonishing」侧重指因出乎意料而惊讶；「staggering」侧重指因规模、数量巨大而令人震惊

definitions.example: The corporate fraud scandal stained the CEO's long-standing reputation for integrity.
A coffee stain was found on the title page of the peer-reviewed academic paper.

stain.etymology: 来自古法语「estain」，原指锡制染料，后引申为“染色、玷污”及“污渍、污点”义。

similar_words.description: 「spot」指细小的斑点或抽象的轻微污点，「stain」侧重难以去除的污渍或严重的名誉污点。

definitions.example: The emergency exit sign pointed to a narrow set of stairs leading to the ground floor of the laboratory building.

stair.etymology: 来自古英语「stǣger」，表示“阶梯”，与「steep」（陡峭的）同根。

similar_words.description: 「stair」指楼梯的台阶，「stare」指凝视、盯着看。

definitions.meaning: 楼梯间；（整体的）楼梯结构
definitions.example: The grand staircase in the university library was lined with portraits of renowned 20th-century scholars.

staircase.etymology: 由「stair」（楼梯）+「case」（容器，此处指封闭空间）构成，指包含楼梯的封闭空间或整体楼梯结构。

similar_words.description: 「stair」指单个台阶或（复数）零散的楼梯，「staircase」指整体的楼梯结构或楼梯间。

definitions.example: The future of the entire climate research project is at stake if government funding is cut next fiscal year.
The venture capital firm staked $3 million on the startup's carbon capture technology.

stake.etymology: 来自古英语「staca」（桩子），原指插在地上的标记桩，后引申为“赌注（以桩标记赌金）”“利害关系”义。

similar_words.description: 「stake」指赌注、利害关系或资助，「stack」指堆、堆叠。

definitions.example: The stale argument from the opposition failed to persuade the academic committee of its validity.

stale.etymology: 来自古英语「stalu」（偷窃，原指偷来的食物因放置过久而不新鲜），后引申为“不新鲜的、陈腐的”义。

similar_words.description: 「stale」侧重食物不新鲜或观点陈腐，「stagnant」侧重经济、水流等停滞不前。

definitions.meaning: 偷偷跟踪；蹑手蹑脚地走
definitions.example: The wildlife biologist spent weeks stalking the endangered snow leopard in the Himalayan mountains.
The tall corn stalks swayed gently in the autumn breeze across the experimental farm.

stalk.etymology: 来自古英语「stealc」（茎），后引申为“跟踪（像茎一样笔直地悄悄移动）”义。

similar_words.description: 「stalk」指植物茎或跟踪，「stock」指库存、股票或家畜。

definitions.example: A small book stall at the campus academic fair sold used graduate textbooks at 50% discount.
The trade negotiators tried to stall the meeting to gain more time for internal policy reviews.

stall.etymology: 来自古英语「steall」（位置、摊位），后引申为“发动机熄火（失去动力位置）”“拖延（停在原地）”义。

similar_words.description: 「stall」指摊位、拖延或发动机熄火，「stalk」指植物茎或跟踪。

definitions.example: The top-tier journal has strict standards for the originality and methodological rigor of submitted papers.
The standard procedure for conducting this experiment requires precise temperature control for 72 hours.

standard.etymology: 来自古法语「estandard」（旗帜，原指军队的统一旗帜作为行动标准），后引申为“标准、规范”义。

similar_words.description: 「standard」侧重公认的、通用的标准，「criterion」侧重评判的具体准则（复数为criteria）。

definitions.example: From the standpoint of behavioral economics, consumer decisions are often driven by emotional biases rather than rational analysis.

standpoint.etymology: 由「stand」（站立）+「point」（点）构成，指“站立的位置”，引申为“立场、观点”。

similar_words.description: 二者都指观点，「standpoint」侧重基于特定身份或立场的观点，「viewpoint」侧重个人的观察角度。

definitions.example: Rice and wheat are staple crops for over 60% of the world's population, especially in South Asia.
Critical thinking has become a staple skill required for graduate-level academic research in all disciplines.

staple.etymology: 来自古英语「stapol」（桩子，原指固定羊毛的桩，羊毛是中世纪英国的主要出口商品），后引申为“主要产品、主食”“主要的”义。

similar_words.description: 「staple」指主食、主要产品或主要的，「stable」指稳定的、稳固的。

definitions.example: The researchers stared at the fMRI brain scans for hours to identify subtle neural activity patterns.

stare.etymology: 源自中古英语「staren」，与古高地德语「sterren」（凝视）同源，隐含“目光固定”的核心语义

similar_words.description: 「stare」常指因惊讶、无礼或专注而盯着看，略带贬义；「gaze」指因欣赏、好奇而长时间凝视，略带褒义

definitions.example: The sudden alarm from the lab equipment startled the exhausted graduate student who was dozing off at his desk.

startle.etymology: 由中古英语「start」（跳起）加后缀「-le」（表反复动作）构成，核心逻辑为“因受惊突然跳起”

similar_words.description: 「startle」是动词表“使惊吓”；「start」作动词表“开始、启动”，词形相近易混淆

definitions.example: During the severe drought, many rural families in the region were forced to starve for days due to the blocked food supply.
Many marginalized students starve for equal access to high-quality educational resources.

starve.etymology: 源自古英语「steorfan」（死亡），后语义缩小为“因饥饿死亡”，引申出比喻义“渴望”

similar_words.description: 「starve」可表具体挨饿或比喻渴望；「famish」仅指极度饥饿，语气更正式

definitions.example: The current state of renewable energy technology still faces challenges in storage efficiency.
The research paper states that climate change has accelerated the melting of polar ice caps.

state.etymology: 源自拉丁语「status」（站立的位置→状态），与词根「stat-」（站立）同源
