similar_words.description: 「state」可作名词表“状态、国家”或动词表“陈述”；「status」仅作名词表“地位、身份”

definitions.example: The official statement from the Ministry of Education clarified the new postgraduate admission requirements.

statement.etymology: 由动词「state」（陈述）加名词后缀「-ment」（表行为或结果）构成

similar_words.description: 「statement」侧重正式的书面/口头陈述，常与官方相关；「announcement」侧重公开通知，可正式可非正式

definitions.meaning: 政治家，国务活动家（褒义）
definitions.example: The founding statesman of the country attached great importance to the construction of the legal system.

statesman.etymology: 由「state」（国家）加后缀「-sman」（表从事某职业的人）构成，指处理国家政务的杰出人士

similar_words.description: 「statesman」指有远见、受尊敬的政治家（褒义）；「politician」常指玩弄权术的政客（贬义）

definitions.example: The static growth rate of the manufacturing industry has become a major concern for the local government.

static.etymology: 源自希腊语「statikos」（静止的），与词根「stat-」（站立）同源

similar_words.description: 「static」侧重“无变化、停滞的”，可用于抽象事物（如经济）；「stationary」侧重“位置固定的”，多用于具体事物

definitions.example: The stationary monitoring camera was installed at the crossroads to record traffic violations.

stationary.etymology: 由「station」（位置）加形容词后缀「-ary」（表属性）构成，指“处于固定位置的”

similar_words.description: 「stationary」强调位置不移动，多修饰具体事物；「static」强调无变化、停滞，可修饰抽象事物

definitions.example: The university's postgraduate office provides free stationery for students participating in the thesis defense.

stationery.etymology: 源自拉丁语「stationarius」（固定摊位商人），原指固定摊位售卖的物品，后特指文具

similar_words.description: 「stationery」是名词“文具”，拼写含字母「e」；「stationary」是形容词“固定的”，拼写含字母「a」，是考研高频拼写易混词

definitions.example: The statistical analysis of the survey data shows a significant correlation between sleep quality and academic performance.

statistical.etymology: 由名词「statistics」（统计学）去词尾「-s」加形容词后缀「-al」（表属性）构成

similar_words.description: 「statistical」是形容词“统计的”；「statistic」是名词“统计数据”，或作形容词（较少用），词性用法易混

definitions.example: Official statistics indicate that rural-urban migration in China has slowed down over the past five years.
She majored in statistics at university and now works as a data analyst for a research institute.

statistics.etymology: 词根stat-（站立，引申为「状况、记录」） + 后缀-ist（从事者） + 后缀-ics（学科后缀），原指记录状况的学科

similar_words.description: 「statistical」是形容词，意为「统计的」，与「statistics」同根，易因词性混淆误用

definitions.example: The bronze statue in the central square was erected to commemorate the country's founding father.

statue.etymology: 词根stat-（站立） + 后缀-ue（名词后缀），指立在某处的人像或物像

similar_words.description: 「statue」指雕像、塑像；「status」指地位、身份，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: Many young professionals pursue advanced degrees to enhance their social status.
The project manager updated the team on the current status of the research initiative.

status.etymology: 词根stat-（站立） + 后缀-us（名词后缀），指所处的位置或状态

similar_words.description: 「status」指地位、状况；「statue」指雕像，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The new anti-discrimination statute will take effect at the beginning of next year.

statute.etymology: 词根stat-（站立，引申为「制定」） + 后缀-ute（名词后缀），指制定后生效的正式规则

similar_words.description: 「statute」是法律术语，指法令、章程；「status」指地位、状况，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: A steady increase in renewable energy investment has been observed in many European countries.
She held onto the railing to steady herself as the ship rolled in the rough sea.

steady.etymology: 词根stead-（站立，稳定） + 后缀-y（形容词后缀），引申为动作义

similar_words.description: 「steady」侧重「持续无波动的稳定」，如经济增长；「stable」侧重「结构牢固、不易变动的稳定」，如建筑物

definitions.example: The upscale restaurant offers a variety of premium steaks sourced from local organic farms.

steak.etymology: 源自古诺斯语「steik」（烤肉），指厚切的肉块

similar_words.description: 「steak」是名词，指牛排、肉排；「steal」是动词，指偷、窃取，二者拼写、发音相近易混淆

definitions.example: Cyber attackers often use phishing emails to steal sensitive personal information from users.
He stole a quick glance at his watch during the boring lecture.

steal.etymology: 源自古英语「stelan」，指非法获取物品

similar_words.description: 「steal」是动词，指偷、偷偷做；「steel」是名词，指钢、钢铁，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The old power plant used coal-fired boilers to generate steam for electricity production.
Many people choose to steam vegetables to retain their nutritional value.

similar_words.description: 「steam」指蒸汽或蒸煮动作；「steamer」指用蒸汽驱动的轮船或蒸锅，二者同根易混淆

definitions.example: Before the invention of jet aircraft, transatlantic travel was dominated by large steamers.
A bamboo steamer is commonly used in Chinese cuisine to cook dumplings and buns.

steamer.etymology: 词根「steam」（蒸汽） + 后缀「-er」（表物品/交通工具的名词后缀）

similar_words.description: 「steamer」指用蒸汽的工具或交通工具；「steam」指蒸汽本身或相关动作，二者同根易混淆

definitions.example: The construction of the high-speed railway required a massive amount of high-quality steel.
She steeled herself to deliver the bad news to her family.

steel.etymology: 源自古英语「style」，指铁碳合金

similar_words.description: 「steel」指钢、钢铁或使坚定；「steal」指偷、偷偷做，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.meaning: 陡峭的；（要求、价格等）过高的
definitions.example: The steep mountain path forced the hikers to slow down and take frequent breaks.
Herbs are often steeped in hot water to extract their medicinal properties.

steep.etymology: 源自古英语steap（高的，陡的），动词义「浸泡」源自中古荷兰语steepe，表「浸没」

similar_words.description: 拼写相近，steep可作形容词表陡峭或动词表浸泡，steer作动词表驾驶、引导

definitions.meaning: 驾驶（车船等）；引导，指导（行为、过程）
definitions.example: The coach steered the young athletes towards a healthy training routine and positive mindset.
The ranch raised steers primarily for beef production.

steer.etymology: 源自古英语steoran（控制，引导），与「把握方向」的语义相关

similar_words.description: 拼写相近，steer作动词表驾驶或引导，steep作形容词表陡峭或动词表浸泡

definitions.example: The government implemented new policies to stem the spread of misinformation on social media.
The rose’s stem was too weak to support the weight of its large bloom.

stem.etymology: 源自古英语stemn（植物的茎），动词义「阻止」源自中古英语stammen（堵塞），与「阻挡」语义相关

similar_words.description: 拼写相近，stem作动词表阻止或名词表茎，steam作名词表蒸汽或动词表蒸发

definitions.example: Taking small but consistent steps is crucial for long-term academic success in postgraduate entrance exams.
The professor stepped in to resolve the dispute between the two research teams.

step.etymology: 源自古英语stæpe（脚步），与「移动、前进」的语义相关

similar_words.description: 拼写相近，step表脚步、步骤，stop表停止、阻止

definitions.meaning: 立体声（设备）；立体声音响
definitions.example: The new stereo system in the lecture hall enhances the audio quality of academic presentations.
Stereo recordings provide a more immersive listening experience than mono ones.

stereo.etymology: 源自希腊语stereos（固体的、立体的），与「三维、固定」的语义相关

similar_words.description: 同根（希腊语stereos表「固定的」），stereo表立体声，stereotype表刻板印象

definitions.example: The study aims to challenge the stereotype that women are less competent in STEM fields.

definitions.meaning: 对…形成刻板印象，把…模式化
definitions.example: Media often stereotypes certain social groups, leading to unfair public perceptions.

stereotype.etymology: 源自法语stéréotype，由希腊语stereos（固定的）和typos（模式）构成，原指印刷用的「固定模版」，后引申为「刻板印象」

similar_words.description: 同根（希腊语stereos表「固定的」），stereotype表刻板印象，stereo表立体声

definitions.meaning: 刻板的，模式化的；老套的
definitions.example: The novel criticized the stereotyped portrayal of working-class characters in mainstream literature.

stereotyped.etymology: 由动词stereotype（使模式化）加形容词后缀-ed构成，表「被模式化的」

similar_words.description: 同根近义，stereotyped强调被模式化的结果，stereotypical强调具有刻板特征的属性

definitions.meaning: 严厉的，严格的；（形势、条件）严峻的
definitions.example: The university imposed stern measures to prevent cheating during the postgraduate entrance examination.
The captain stood at the stern of the ship, monitoring the navigation equipment.

stern.etymology: 源自古英语styrne（严厉的），名词义「船尾」源自中古荷兰语steven（船的端部），与「尾部」语义相关

similar_words.description: 近义，stern侧重态度严厉或形势严峻，strict侧重规则严格

definitions.meaning: （飞机、火车的）乘务员；（财产、机构的）管家，管理员
definitions.example: The flight steward assisted the elderly passenger with placing her luggage in the overhead compartment.

steward.etymology: 源自古英语stigweard（看门的人），由stig（房屋）和weard（守卫）构成，后引申为「管理人员、乘务员」

similar_words.description: 同根，steward指男乘务员或管理员，stewardess指女乘务员

definitions.meaning: 粘贴；坚持（与to连用）；卡住，困住
definitions.example: To achieve high scores in the English exam, candidates must stick to a regular vocabulary learning schedule.
The elderly professor used a wooden stick to point at the formulas on the blackboard.

stick.etymology: 源自古英语sticca（棍，棒），动词义「粘贴」源自中古英语stiken（刺、粘），与「粘附」语义相关

similar_words.description: 拼写相近，stick表棍、粘贴、坚持，sting表刺、叮、刺痛

definitions.example: The sticky label on the experimental sample was accidentally torn off during data collection.
The local government faced a sticky situation over the new environmental regulation.

sticky.etymology: 词根「stick（黏附）」+ 形容词后缀「-y（表具有…性质的）」，表“具有黏附性质的”，引申为“棘手的”

similar_words.description: sticky指「黏的、棘手的」，stiff指「僵硬的、生硬的」

definitions.meaning: （身体部位）僵硬的；一动就疼的
definitions.example: After sitting for 3 hours during the postgraduate qualifying exam, many candidates felt stiff in their necks and shoulders.
The stiff tone of the academic paper made it hard for junior researchers to follow.

stiff.etymology: 源自中古英语「stif」，本义为“坚硬的”，无现代可拆解的常用词根词缀

similar_words.description: stiff指「僵硬的、生硬的」，sticky指「黏的、棘手的」

definitions.meaning: 刺激；激发（积极性、兴趣等）
definitions.example: The new national scholarship policy aims to stimulate undergraduates' enthusiasm for academic research.
Adequate sunlight and water can stimulate the growth of the experimental plants.

stimulate.etymology: 词根「stimul（刺、刺激）」+ 动词后缀「-ate（使…）」，表“使受到刺激”，引申为“促进”

similar_words.description: stimulate指「刺激、促进」，simulate指「模拟、假装」

definitions.example: A bee stung the field researcher on the forearm while he was collecting wild herb samples.
The harsh comments from the thesis supervisor stung the graduate student's pride.

sting.etymology: 源自古英语「stingan」，本义为“刺、扎”，引申为“（话语）刺痛”

similar_words.description: sting作动/名词指「刺、蜇；刺痛」，stingy指「吝啬的、小气的」

definitions.meaning: 搅拌；搅动（液体、混合物等）
definitions.example: The lab assistant stirred the chemical reagent gently to avoid producing bubbles.
The documentary about marine pollution stirred widespread public concern among college students.

stir.etymology: 源自中古英语「stiren」，本义为“移动、搅动”，引申为“激起（情感）”

similar_words.description: stir指「搅拌、激起」，sting指「刺、蜇；刺痛」

definitions.example: The surgeon stitched the patient's minor surgical wound carefully to prevent infection.
After sprinting 800 meters in the physical test, he felt a sharp stitch in his right side.

stitch.etymology: 源自古英语「stice」，本义为“刺、针”，引申为“缝纫的针脚、缝合”，再延伸为“肋部刺痛（像被针扎）”

similar_words.description: stitch指「缝、缝合；肋部剧痛」，switch指「开关、转换」

definitions.example: The online retailer expanded its warehouse to increase the stock of popular study materials for postgraduate exam candidates.
Many risk-averse investors chose to buy government bonds instead of volatile stocks during the economic downturn.

stock.etymology: 源自古英语「stoc」，本义为“树干、仓库”，引申为“仓库中的存货”，后用于金融领域指“股票”

similar_words.description: stock指「库存、股票；储备」，stocking指「长筒袜」

definitions.example: In Western Christmas traditions, children hang colorful stockings by the fireplace to receive gifts from Santa Claus.

stocking.etymology: 词根「stock（原指“套子、容器”）」+ 名词后缀「-ing（表物品）」，表“套在腿上的物品”，即“长筒袜”

similar_words.description: stocking指「长筒袜」，stock指「库存、股票；储备」

definitions.example: Long-term irregular eating habits may cause chronic inflammation of the stomach.
She cannot stomach the idea of plagiarizing others' academic papers for her postgraduate thesis.

stomach.etymology: 源自希腊语「stomachos」，本义为“胃、食道”，动词义由“胃能容纳食物”引申为“忍受（不快的事）”

similar_words.description: stomach指「胃；忍受」，stoma指「（植物的）气孔、（动物的）口器」

definitions.example: The ancient city wall was constructed with massive stones quarried from the nearby mountains.
The routine physical examination revealed a small stone in the patient's left kidney.

stone.etymology: 源自古英语「stan」，本义为“石头”，引申为“像石头的硬物（比如果核、结石）”

similar_words.description: stone指「石头、结石」，stony指「多石的、冷酷的」

definitions.example: In the corner of the university library, a worn wooden stool was placed beside the bookshelf for tired readers.
The medical research team required all participants to provide a stool sample for intestinal flora analysis.

stool.etymology: 源自古英语stōl，本义为固定座位，无标准化可拆分词缀结构

similar_words.description: stool指凳子，stall指摊位、畜舍，二者拼写仅中间字母不同，易混淆

definitions.example: The archaeologist had to stoop to examine the ancient pottery fragments buried under the thin layer of soil.
The respected professor refused to stoop to falsifying experimental data to win the national research award.

stoop.etymology: 源自古英语stūpian，本义为弯腰，无明显可拆分词缀

similar_words.description: stoop表示俯身、屈尊，stool表示凳子，二者拼写仅末尾字母不同，易形近混淆

definitions.example: The research institute invested 2 million yuan to build a secure data storage system for its genomic research projects.
The rare ancient books are preserved in a constant-temperature storage to avoid damage from humidity and light.

storage.etymology: store（储存）+ age（名词后缀，表行为或场所），意为储存的行为或相关场所

similar_words.description: storage指储存、仓储，storey指楼层，二者拼写后半部分不同，易形近混淆

definitions.example: The new campus library, a 12-storey modern building, can accommodate more than 3,000 readers at the same time.

storey.etymology: 源自拉丁语historia，后演化出楼层义，英式拼写为storey，美式同形异义词story表‘故事’，美式拼写中楼层也可写作story

similar_words.description: storey特指楼房的楼层，story主要指故事、叙述，美式拼写中二者可同形，需结合语境区分

definitions.example: The sudden storm forced the ecological research team to suspend their field investigation in the mountainous area.
Hundreds of environmental activists stormed the conference venue to protest against the government's new energy policy.

storm.etymology: 源自古英语storm，本义为猛烈的天气扰动，后延伸出动词‘猛攻’义

similar_words.description: storm指风暴、猛攻，stove指炉子，二者拼写仅末尾字母不同，易形近混淆

definitions.example: In the remote village school, teachers used an old coal stove to heat the classroom during the freezing winter.

stove.etymology: 源自中古荷兰语stove，本义为加热的房间，后指取暖或烹饪用的炉子

similar_words.description: stove指炉子，stave指棍、杖或穿孔，二者拼写仅中间字母不同，易形近混淆

definitions.example: The textbook provides straightforward step-by-step instructions for conducting basic statistical analysis in social science research.
The reviewer gave straightforward comments on the thesis, pointing out the flaws in the research design without reservation.

straightforward.etymology: straight（直的）+ forward（向前的），组合表示不绕弯的、直接的

similar_words.description: straightforward是形容词，表易懂的、坦率的；straightaway是副词/名词，表立刻、直道，二者拼写后半部分不同，易混淆

definitions.example: Long-term exposure to academic pressure can cause severe mental strain among graduate students.
Scientists have developed a high-yield strain of rice that can adapt to cold climate conditions in northern China.

strain.etymology: 源自拉丁语stringere（拉紧），同根词有strict、stringent等

similar_words.description: strain多指外界因素造成的身心负担、张力，stress多指精神压力、强调，二者为近义关系，常考辨析
strain表压力、动植物品系，sprain特指关节扭伤，二者拼写相近、词义易混

definitions.example: The local tourism volunteers offer free guide services to strangers visiting the world cultural heritage site.
She is no stranger to academic writing, having published 5 papers in SSCI journals during her doctoral studies.

stranger.etymology: strange（陌生的）+ er（名词后缀，表人），意为陌生的人

similar_words.description: stranger指任何不认识的人或外地人，foreigner特指来自外国的人，二者语义范围不同
stranger指陌生人、外地人，stronger是形容词strong的比较级，表更强的，二者拼写仅中间字母不同，易形近混淆

definitions.example: The researcher adjusted the strap of his portable recording device before starting the field interview.

definitions.meaning: 用带子捆扎；系紧（安全带等）
definitions.example: All passengers are required to strap their seat belts before the high-speed train departs.

strap.etymology: 源自希腊语strappe，本义为捆扎用的带子，后延伸出动词‘捆扎’义

similar_words.description: strap指带子、捆扎，strip指条、带或剥夺，二者拼写仅末尾字母不同，易混淆
strap指带子，staple指主食、订书钉，后者拼写多一个字母p，易形近混淆

definitions.example: Many tech companies adopt a long-term strategy to invest in artificial intelligence research.

strategy.etymology: 源自希腊语「stratos（军队）」，通过拉丁语进入英语，后缀「-y」表名词，原指军事部署，后引申为策略、战略

similar_words.description: 均指行动方法，strategy侧重整体、长期的策略，tactic侧重具体、短期的战术
同根词，strategy为名词，strategic为形容词，表战略的

definitions.example: The farmer used dry straw to cover the vegetable seedlings against late frost.

straw.etymology: 源自古英语「streaw」，与动词「strew（撒播）」同源，原指撒在地上的干草

similar_words.description: 形近词，straw指稻草、麦秆，strawberry指草莓

definitions.example: Fresh strawberries are often used as a natural ingredient in dietary research studies.

strawberry.etymology: 由「straw（稻草，因草莓植株低矮，长在类似稻草的茎上）」与「berry（浆果）」组合而成

similar_words.description: 形近词，straw指稻草、麦秆，strawberry指草莓

definitions.example: A clear stream runs through the forest near the ecological research station.
Tears streamed down the researcher's face when she confirmed her hypothesis.

stream.etymology: 源自古英语「stream」，与词根「strea-（流动）」相关，原指自然流动的水体

similar_words.description: 形近词，stream指溪流/流动，steam指蒸汽

definitions.example: The university decided to streamline its admission process to reduce administrative burdens.
The new high-speed train has a streamlined design to cut down air resistance.

streamline.etymology: 由「stream（流动）」与「line（线条）」组合而成，原指让线条流畅的设计，后引申为简化流程

similar_words.description: 同根词，stream指流动，streamline指简化流程/流线型的

definitions.example: One major strength of this study is its large sample size and long follow-up period.

strength.etymology: 源自形容词「strong（强壮的）」，后缀「-th」表抽象名词，指力量、优势

similar_words.description: 同根词，strength为名词，strengthen为动词，表加强
均指力量，strength侧重内在力量或优势，power侧重外在权力或能力

definitions.example: The government has taken measures to strengthen cooperation between universities and high-tech firms.

strengthen.etymology: 由名词「strength（力量）」加动词后缀「-en（使…）」构成，指使获得力量、加强

similar_words.description: 同根词，strengthen为动词，strength为名词

definitions.example: The research team made strenuous efforts to collect first-hand data from remote rural areas.

strenuous.etymology: 源自拉丁语「strenuus（强壮的，活跃的）」，后缀「-uous」表形容词，引申为费力的、奋发的

similar_words.description: 同根词，strenuous为形容词，strength为名词
均指艰难的，strenuous侧重需付出大量努力的，arduous侧重任务本身的艰难

definitions.example: The professor stressed the significance of ethical standards in academic publishing.
Chronic work stress may contribute to the development of cardiovascular diseases.

stress.etymology: 源自拉丁语「strictus（拉紧的）」，通过古法语进入英语，原指拉紧的状态，后引申为压力、强调

similar_words.description: 均指压力，stress侧重心理或客观压力，strain侧重因过度劳累或紧张的压力

definitions.example: After sitting for hours of data analysis, she stood up to stretch her stiff shoulders.
A long stretch of low temperatures has delayed the growth of winter crops.

stretch.etymology: 源自古英语「streccan」，与词根「strea-（伸展）」相关，原指拉长、伸展

similar_words.description: 形近词，stretch指伸展，stitch指缝合
均指延长，stretch侧重物理上的伸展，extend侧重空间或时间上的延长

definitions.example: Universities usually set strict requirements for undergraduate thesis formatting and citation standards.

strict.etymology: 源自拉丁语strictus（拉紧的），由词根「string/strict（拉紧）」衍生，表「要求拉紧、不容松懈→严格的」

similar_words.description: strict多指对规则、行为的硬性约束，rigorous侧重学术、流程的严谨周密

definitions.example: The leading researcher strode to the podium to deliver his keynote speech at the academic conference.
China has made remarkable strides in renewable energy development over the past decade.

stride.etymology: 来自古英语strīdan（跨步），与strive同源，表「用力跨步→大步走；进展」

similar_words.description: strive为动词表「努力、奋斗」，stride可作动词/名词表「大步走；进展」，二者拼写仅差最后两个字母，易混淆

definitions.example: The long-standing strife between the two political factions hindered the passage of the environmental bill.

strife.etymology: 来自古法语strif（争斗），与strive同源，表「因争执而产生的冲突→纷争」

similar_words.description: strife多指长期、激烈的内部冲突，conflict范围更广，可指各种类型的冲突

definitions.example: The factory workers decided to strike for higher wages and safer working conditions.
It struck the researcher that the experimental data might have been contaminated by external factors.

strike.etymology: 来自古英语strīcan（击打），词根「stri-（击打）」，衍生义「因不满而集体行动→罢工」「（想法）猛地击中大脑→突然想到」

similar_words.description: stroke可作名词/动词表「中风；击打；轻抚」，strike核心义为「罢工；突然想到」，二者拼写仅差末尾一个字母，易混淆

definitions.example: The striking contrast between the two groups' test results attracted the attention of the academic community.

striking.etymology: 由strike（击打）+ -ing（形容词后缀）构成，表「像被击中一样显眼的→引人注目的」

similar_words.description: striking侧重因对比、差异大而显眼，prominent侧重因地位、成就高而突出

definitions.example: The archaeologists used thin string to mark the boundaries of the excavation site.
A string of unexpected findings led the team to revise their original hypothesis.

string.etymology: 来自古英语streng（绳索），词根「string（拉紧）」，衍生义「拉紧的线→细绳」「连贯的线状物→一连串」

similar_words.description: strap可作名词/动词表「带子；捆扎」，string核心义为「线；一连串」，二者拼写接近易混淆

definitions.example: Workers had to strip the old paint from the building before applying the new coating.
The court ruled to strip the company of its license for violating environmental regulations.

strip.etymology: 来自中古英语strippen（剥去），词根「strip（拉紧、撕开）」，表「用力撕下→剥去；剥夺」

similar_words.description: stripe为名词表「条纹」，strip可作动词/名词表「剥去；条带」，二者拼写仅差末尾一个字母，极易混淆

definitions.example: The research report noted that the zebra's stripes serve as a natural camouflage in grassy environments.

stripe.etymology: 来自中古荷兰语stripe（条纹），与strip同源，表「细长的条状物→条纹」

similar_words.description: strip可作动词/名词表「剥去；条带」，stripe为名词表「条纹」，二者拼写仅差末尾一个字母，极易混淆

definitions.example: Many graduate students strive to publish their research findings in top-tier academic journals.

strive.etymology: 来自古法语estriver（争斗），与stride、strife同源，表「为目标而奋力争取→努力；奋斗」

similar_words.description: stride可作动词/名词表「大步走；进展」，strive为动词表「努力；奋斗」，二者拼写仅差最后两个字母，易混淆
strive侧重长期艰苦的努力，endeavor侧重正式有目的的尝试

definitions.example: The study found that regular physical activity can reduce the risk of stroke in middle-aged and elderly people.
The researcher gently stroked the lab mouse to calm it before the experiment.

stroke.etymology: 来自古英语strāc（击打），与strike同源，衍生义「（疾病）猛地击中→中风」「轻击→轻抚」

similar_words.description: strike核心义为「罢工；突然想到」，stroke可作名词/动词表「中风；轻抚」，二者拼写接近易混淆

definitions.example: On weekends, many urban residents stroll along the tree-lined riverbank to escape the pressure of intense work.
After the academic seminar, the scholars took a leisurely stroll around the campus to continue their discussion.

stroll.etymology: 源自中古荷兰语strollen（漫游），属于str-语义场下表行走动作的基础词汇，无明显可拆分的考研常见词根词缀

similar_words.description: scroll意为「卷轴；滚动」，与stroll拼写仅相差首字母后的一个字母，形近易混
wander侧重无目的的漫游或偏离路线，stroll侧重悠闲缓慢地散步

definitions.example: The social structure of traditional rural communities has undergone dramatic changes with the advance of urbanization.
Experienced academic writers usually structure their papers to present core arguments in a clear and logical order.

structure.etymology: 源自拉丁语structura，词根struct表示「建造」，后缀-ure为名词后缀，表行为或结果；动词含义由名词活用而来，与construct、destruct为同根词

similar_words.description: stricture意为「苛评；束缚」，与structure拼写仅字母i和u的顺序不同，形近易混
framework侧重作为基础的框架或准则，structure侧重事物整体的组成结构与内部关系

definitions.example: Many first-generation college students struggle to balance academic performance and part-time work to afford their tuition.
The long struggle for equal educational opportunities for disadvantaged groups has gained wide public attention in recent years.

struggle.etymology: 源自古法语estrugle（冲突、斗争），属于核心情感动作类词汇，无明显可拆分的考研常见词根词缀

similar_words.description: strive侧重为达成目标主动付出长期努力，struggle侧重在困境中艰难挣扎、奋力拼搏
fight侧重具体的打斗或对抗，struggle更侧重抽象的、长期的努力与抗争

definitions.example: The stubborn official refused to adjust the outdated policy even when faced with widespread public criticism.
Environmental scientists are working to tackle the stubborn problem of microplastic pollution in deep oceans.

stubborn.etymology: 源自中古英语stiborn，由stub（树桩）+后缀-born构成，原义为「像树桩一样坚硬的」，后引申出「顽固的」「难处理的」等义

similar_words.description: obstinate语气更重，侧重无理地固执己见，stubborn可兼指人的性格或事物难处理
stubble意为「作物残株；胡茬」，与stubborn拼写前半部分一致，形近易混

definitions.meaning: 工作室；演播室；画室；电影制片厂
definitions.example: Many independent documentary filmmakers choose to work in small studios to maintain full control over their creative content.

studio.etymology: 源自意大利语studio，原义为「学习、研究」，来自拉丁语studium（学习），与study为同根词

similar_words.description: stadium意为「体育场」，与studio拼写结构相近，易发生混淆
study为同根词，表「学习；研究」，studio指用于创作、录制或工作的专属空间

definitions.example: Before submitting his dissertation, the graduate student sorted out all the research stuff and archived the relevant data systematically.
Many Western families stuff turkeys with breadcrumbs and herbs during the Thanksgiving holiday.

stuff.etymology: 源自古英语stoff（材料、物质），属于基础核心名词，语义宽泛，可指代各类具体或抽象事物

similar_words.description: staff意为「全体员工；手杖」，与stuff拼写仅字母a和u的区别，形近极易混淆
material侧重具体的生产或研究材料，stuff语义更宽泛，可指代各类物品、内容或素材

definitions.example: The elderly researcher stumbled over a loose stone on the way to the laboratory but was not seriously hurt.

definitions.meaning: 偶然发现（常与介词on/upon/across搭配）
definitions.example: While sorting out ancient manuscripts in the museum archive, the linguist stumbled upon a previously unknown poem by the 17th-century poet.

stumble.etymology: 源自中古英语stumblen，与古挪威语stumla（绊倒）同源，属于动作类基础动词，短语搭配为考研核心考点

similar_words.description: tumble意为「摔倒；翻滚；暴跌」，与stumble拼写仅首字母不同，形近易混
falter侧重因犹豫、虚弱而蹒跚或结巴，stumble侧重因外界阻碍或失误而绊倒、出错

definitions.example: The sudden announcement of the Nobel Prize winner stunned the entire academic circle, as the scholar had been little known before.
The wildlife researcher used a tranquilizer dart to stun the elephant temporarily for a physical examination.

stun.etymology: 源自古法语estoner（使震惊、打昏），与astonish为同源词，词根ton表「打雷」，原义为「像被雷击中般失去反应」

similar_words.description: astonish与stun同源，均表「使震惊」，但stun语气更重，侧重震惊到暂时无法思考或行动
shock为通用表震惊的词汇，stun还可指物理撞击导致的昏迷

definitions.example: The stunning landscape of the Grand Canyon has been featured in numerous documentary films and travel journals.

definitions.meaning: 令人震惊的，令人惊愕的
definitions.example: The research team published a stunning finding that challenges the long-accepted theory about the origin of the universe.

stunning.etymology: 为动词stun的现在分词转化的形容词，后缀-ing为形容词后缀，表「令人产生某种感受的」

similar_words.description: astonishing仅表「令人惊讶的」，stunning兼具「极具美感的」和「令人震惊的」两层语义
gorgeous仅修饰人或事物的美丽，stunning的语义范围更广

definitions.example: It would be stupid for the government to ignore the public’s demand for better medical services just to pursue short-term economic growth.

stupid.etymology: 源自拉丁语stupidus，词根stup表「麻木、惊愕」，后缀-id为形容词后缀，原义为「麻木的」，后引申为「愚蠢的」

similar_words.description: foolish侧重因缺乏判断力或常识而做出蠢事，stupid侧重智力低下或行为鲁莽欠考虑
sturdy意为「结实的；健壮的」，与stupid拼写开头相同，形近易混

definitions.example: The sturdy wooden table has withstood decades of daily use in the university laboratory.

sturdy.etymology: 源自拉丁语stare（站立），表示能站稳的、不易倒塌的，引申为结实的、健壮的

similar_words.description: 拼写易混，「sturdy」指物体结实或人健壮，「stubborn」指人固执、顽固

definitions.example: Her academic writing style is characterized by precise logic and rigorous citation of sources.
The media styled the young astrophysicist as a rising star in the field of space exploration.

style.etymology: 源自希腊语stylos（柱子、样式），指事物的呈现样式，引申为风格、称呼

similar_words.description: 近义易混，「style」侧重个人或群体的创作/行为风格，「genre」侧重艺术、文学的体裁类型

definitions.example: His evaluation of the social experiment was criticized for being too subjective and lacking empirical evidence.

subjective.etymology: 由sub-（在…之下）+ject（投掷）+-ive（形容词后缀）构成，原指置于个人观点之下的，引申为主观的

similar_words.description: 拼写易混，「subjective」表主观的，「subjected」是动词subject的过去分词，表遭受的、服从的

definitions.example: The naval force deployed a new type of nuclear submarine for the deep-sea archaeological mission.
Scientists have discovered a new species of fish in the submarine caves of the Pacific Ocean.

submarine.etymology: 由sub-（在…之下）+marine（海的）构成，指海下的事物或海下的

similar_words.description: 拼写与语义易混，「submarine」多指军用/大型潜水艇，「submersible」多指小型潜水器或可潜水的

definitions.example: Rising sea levels caused by climate change may submerge low-lying coastal cities within decades.
Her genuine anxiety was submerged beneath a mask of calm during the job interview.

submerge.etymology: 由sub-（在…之下）+merg（沉、浸）+-e构成，指沉到下方，引申为淹没、掩盖

similar_words.description: 近义易混，「submerge」侧重物理上的淹没/潜入水中，「immerse」侧重精神沉浸或浸入液体（范围更广）

definitions.example: All graduate candidates must submit their thesis proposals before the specified deadline.
The defense lawyer submitted that the defendant had no motive to commit the alleged crime.

submit.etymology: 由sub-（在…之下）+mit（送、派）构成，指送到下方（提交）或置于下方（屈服），正式语境引申为认为、主张

similar_words.description: 拼写易混（均含词根mit），「submit」表提交/主张，「admit」表承认/准许进入

definitions.example: In any hierarchical organization, subordinate staff must follow the instructions of their superiors.
The manager held a weekly meeting to assign tasks to his subordinates.

subordinate.etymology: 由sub-（在…之下）+ordin（顺序、等级）+-ate（形容词/名词后缀）构成，指等级在下方的

similar_words.description: 近义易混，「subordinate」侧重组织、等级上的从属关系，「inferior」侧重质量、地位上的低劣或下级

definitions.example: Many researchers subscribe to top academic journals to keep up with the latest findings in their fields.
Most macroeconomists subscribe to the view that moderate inflation stimulates economic growth.

subscribe.etymology: 由sub-（在…之下）+scrib（写）+-e构成，原指在下方签名（表示同意），后引申为订阅

similar_words.description: 拼写易混（均含词根scrib），「subscribe」表订阅/同意，「prescribe」表开处方/规定

definitions.example: Subsequent experiments confirmed the initial hypothesis put forward by the research team.

subsequent.etymology: 由sub-（在…之下）+sequ（跟随）+-ent（形容词后缀）构成，指跟在后面的

similar_words.description: 拼写易混（均含词根sequ），「subsequent」表时间上随后的，「consequent」表因果上随之发生的

definitions.example: The company established a subsidiary department to handle customer feedback and after-sales service.
The multinational corporation has 15 subsidiary companies operating across Europe and Southeast Asia.

subsidiary.etymology: 由sub-（在…之下）+sidi（坐、放置）+-ary（形容词/名词后缀）构成，指置于下方的，引申为附属的、子公司

similar_words.description: 近义易混，「subsidiary」侧重辅助性质的或公司的子机构，「subordinate」侧重等级上的从属关系

definitions.example: Many local governments subsidize vocational training programs to boost youth employment rates.

subsidize.etymology: sub-（表示支持、在下方）+ sid（拉丁语sedere，坐）+ -ize（动词后缀），字面义为坐在下方支持，引申为资助、补贴

similar_words.description: 「subsidize」为动词表资助、补贴，「subside」为动词表平息、减弱，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: Renewable energy enterprises can apply for government subsidies to cover part of their R&D costs.

subsidy.etymology: sub-（支持）+ sid（坐）+ -y（名词后缀），表用于支持的款项，即补贴、补助金

similar_words.description: 「subsidy」为名词表补贴、补助金，「subsidiary」为名词/形容词表子公司/附属的，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: Researchers have identified a new chemical substance that can effectively inhibit bacterial growth.
The substance of the academic paper focuses on the impact of digitalization on traditional manufacturing.

substance.etymology: sub-（在下方）+ st（拉丁语stare，站立）+ -ance（名词后缀），表站在下方的基础，引申为物质、实质

similar_words.description: 「substance」为名词表物质、实质，「substantial」为形容词表大量的、实质的，同根易混淆

definitions.example: Substantial academic research has confirmed the correlation between sleep quality and cognitive performance.
The two universities reached a substantial agreement on student exchange programs.

substantial.etymology: sub-（在下方）+ st（站立）+ -ant + -ial（形容词后缀），表有基础的、实在的，引申为大量的、实质的

similar_words.description: 「substantial」为形容词表大量的、实质的，「substance」为名词表物质、实质，同根易混淆

definitions.example: During the pandemic, many schools used virtual classrooms to substitute in-person teaching.
Oat milk is a popular plant-based substitute for dairy milk in many Western countries.

substitute.etymology: sub-（代替）+ stitute（拉丁语statuere，放置），表放在下方替代原有事物，引申为替换、替代品

similar_words.description: 「substitute」侧重临时或功能替代，「replace」侧重永久或位置替换，近义易混淆

definitions.meaning: 微妙的；细微的；难以察觉的
definitions.example: There is a subtle distinction between 'economic growth' and 'economic development' that is often overlooked in basic textbooks.

subtle.etymology: sub-（在下方）+ tle（拉丁语tela，编织），表织在下方不易察觉的纹理，引申为微妙的、细微的

similar_words.description: 「subtle」侧重抽象事物的细微难察，「delicate」侧重具体事物的精致脆弱，近义易混淆

definitions.example: When calculating the final grade, professors will subtract 5 points for late submission of assignments.

subtract.etymology: sub-（向下、离开）+ tract（拉丁语trahere，拉），表往下拉走一部分，引申为减去、扣除

similar_words.description: 「subtract」为动词表减去、扣除，「detract」为动词表贬低、减损，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: With the expansion of urban areas, more farmlands have been converted into residential zones in the suburb.

suburb.etymology: sub-（在…附近）+ urb（拉丁语urbs，城市），表城市附近的区域，即郊区、城外

similar_words.description: 「suburb」为名词表郊区、城外，「urban」为形容词表城市的、都市的，同根易混淆

definitions.example: The construction of the cross-city subway line is expected to ease traffic congestion in the downtown area.

subway.etymology: sub-（在地下）+ way（道路），表地下的通行道路，即地铁、地下通道

similar_words.description: 「subway」为名词表地铁、地下通道，「suburb」为名词表郊区、城外，同根易混淆

definitions.example: He succeeded in obtaining a master's degree from Peking University after two years of diligent study.
After the former prime minister stepped down, the deputy prime minister succeeded to the office.

succeed.etymology: suc-（sub-的变体，跟随、在下方）+ ceed（拉丁语cedere，走），表跟随走（接替）或走到前面（成功）

similar_words.description: 「succeed」为动词表成功、接替，「precede」为动词表在…之前、先于，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: Many young researchers attribute their academic success to years of diligent exploration.
The new energy vehicle has become a commercial success in the global market.

success.etymology: 由动词succeed（成功）派生的名词，核心词根为「ced-」（行走），前缀「sub-」表「向下→跟随→达成」，后缀为名词化标记

similar_words.description: 同根动词，前者为名词表「成功」，后者为动词表「成功；接替」，词性与用法不同

definitions.example: Successful policy makers always balance public interests and practical feasibility.

successful.etymology: 由名词success加形容词后缀「-ful」（表「充满…的」）构成

similar_words.description: 同根名词，前者为形容词修饰名词，后者为名词作主语/宾语，语法功能不同

definitions.example: The succession of extreme weather events has raised global concerns about climate change.
The royal succession was regulated by strict constitutional provisions.

succession.etymology: 由动词succeed派生的名词，后缀「-ion」表「动作或状态」，核心词根仍为「ced-」（行走）

similar_words.description: 同根词，前者表「连续/继任」，后者表「成功」，含义差异显著，易混淆

definitions.example: The research team published three successive papers in top academic journals.

successive.etymology: 由名词succession派生的形容词，后缀「-ive」表「具有…性质的」

similar_words.description: 近义形容词，前者侧重时间上的依次连续，后者侧重时空上的紧密无间隔，考研常考辨析

definitions.example: The university’s board of trustees elected a new successor to the retiring president.

successor.etymology: 由动词succeed派生的表人名词，后缀「-or」表「执行某动作的人」

similar_words.description: 反义同结构名词，前者表「继任者」，后者表「前任」，考研阅读常对比出现

definitions.example: Mangrove trees can suck saltwater from the soil through specialized root systems.
The outdated public transportation system really sucks in this small town.

suck.etymology: 源自古英语「sūcan」，核心义为「用嘴抽吸」，后引申出口语贬义用法

similar_words.description: 形近词，前者为动词/名词表「吸」，后者为名词表「短袜」，含义完全无关，易拼写错误

definitions.example: The sudden outbreak of the epidemic disrupted the original economic recovery plan.

sudden.etymology: 源自拉丁语「subitus」，由前缀「sub-」（向下）+词根「ire」（走）构成，表「突然降临的」

similar_words.description: 近义形容词，前者侧重动作发生快且无预兆，后者侧重更突兀、生硬，考研常考同义替换

definitions.example: The consumer association decided to sue the food company for selling contaminated products.
He sued the king for mercy in the ancient fable.

sue.etymology: 源自拉丁语「sequi」（跟随），原指「跟随请求」，后引申为法律层面的「起诉」

similar_words.description: 近义动词，前者侧重正式法律诉讼，后者侧重口头/书面指控（不一定走法律程序），考研易混

definitions.example: Many small businesses suffered huge financial losses during the trade war.

definitions.meaning: （因疾病、困难等）受苦；患病（常与from搭配）
definitions.example: A significant proportion of teenagers suffer from mental health issues nowadays.

suffer.etymology: 源自拉丁语「sufferre」，由前缀「sub-」（下）+词根「ferre」（带、承受）构成，表「承受（压力/痛苦）」

similar_words.description: 近义动词，前者侧重被动承受痛苦/损失，后者侧重主动长期忍受困难，考研常考辨析

definitions.example: A concise summary will suffice to convey the main findings of the research.
The remaining budget will suffice for the rest of the project.

suffice.etymology: 源自拉丁语「sufficere」，由前缀「sub-」（下）+词根「facere」（做）构成，表「做到足够」

similar_words.description: 近义动词，前者侧重数量/程度足够满足需求，后者侧重满足欲望/要求，考研易混

definitions.example: The sufficiency of public healthcare resources directly affects the well-being of low-income groups.

sufficiency.etymology: suf-（=sub- 表「使」）+ 词根「fici（做）」+ 名词后缀「-ency」，原指做到足够的状态，引申为充足

similar_words.description: 「deficiency」表「不足，缺乏」，与本词为反义形近词，共享词根与后缀，前缀语义相反

definitions.example: Sufficient empirical evidence is required to validate the theoretical assumptions proposed in the sociology study.

sufficient.etymology: suf-（=sub- 表「使」）+ 词根「fici（做）」+ 形容词后缀「-ent」，意为做到够用的

similar_words.description: 「efficient」表「有效率的」，为形近易混词，后缀相同，核心语义指向不同

definitions.example: Many economists warn that imposing excessive trade barriers would be an economic suicide for the export-oriented nation.

suicide.etymology: 词根「sui（自己）」+ 词根「cide（杀）」，原指杀害自己的行为，引申为自取灭亡的行为

similar_words.description: 「homicide」表「他杀，杀人行为」，与本词共享词根「cide（杀）」，指代的行为性质不同

definitions.example: The adjusted curriculum design suits the learning needs of students with different academic backgrounds.
The environmental protection organization filed a suit against the factory for illegal discharge of industrial sewage.

suit.etymology: 来自拉丁语「sequi（跟随）」，原指伴随的事物，后逐步引申出适合、套装、诉讼等多义

similar_words.description: 「suite」表「套房，一套物品」，为形近易混词，拼写仅差一个字母，语义差异显著

definitions.example: Teachers should select suitable teaching materials to cater to the cognitive development level of adolescent students.

suitable.etymology: 动词「suit（适合）」+ 形容词后缀「-able（可…的）」，意为可符合需求的

similar_words.description: 「appropriate」表「恰当的」，更侧重符合特定场合的规范，「suitable」更侧重契合某一目的或需求

definitions.meaning: 套房；（同类物品的）一套，一组
definitions.example: The conference center has a dedicated suite of meeting rooms that can accommodate activities of different scales.

suite.etymology: 与「suit」同源，来自法语，原指一组跟随的事物，后引申为套房、套间、成套物品等义

similar_words.description: 「suit」作动词表「适合」，作名词表「套装；诉讼」，为形近易混词，拼写仅差一个字母

definitions.example: The new environmental regulation strictly limits the emission of sulfur dioxide from coal-fired power plants.

sulfur.etymology: 源自拉丁语「sulfur」，为化学元素专有名词，英式拼写为「sulphur」

similar_words.description: 「suffer」表「遭受，忍受」，为形近易混词，语义与所属领域完全不同

definitions.example: The local government has invested a huge sum of money in the renovation of ancient cultural relics.
At the end of the panel discussion, the host summed the core viewpoints of all guests in three concise sentences.

sum.etymology: 源自拉丁语「summa（总和，顶端）」，由「总和」引申出款项、总结、共计等义

similar_words.description: 「some」作限定词表「一些，某个」，与本词为同音形近易混词，词性与语义完全不同

definitions.example: When completing a literature review, students need to accurately summarize the core findings of relevant academic studies.

summarize.etymology: 词根「sum（总和）」+ 连接成分「-mar-」+ 动词后缀「-ize（使…）」，意为使内容整合为核心总和，即总结

similar_words.description: 「generalize」表「概括，归纳」，侧重从具体案例推导普遍规律，「summarize」侧重对已有内容进行提炼汇总

definitions.example: The abstract of a research paper is a concise summary of the entire study's content and conclusions.
The reporter gave a summary account of the government's new policy on pension adjustment at the press conference.

summary.etymology: 词根「sum（总和）」+ 连接成分「-mar-」+ 后缀「-y（表名词或形容词）」，表示核心总和的（内容），即总结、总结性的

similar_words.description: 「abstract」多指学术论文的摘要，「summary」适用范围更广，可指各类内容的总结概要

definitions.meaning: 峰会，最高级会议；（山的）顶峰，顶点
definitions.example: The G20 summit held in 2023 focused on coordinating global policies to address cross-border inflation.

summit.etymology: 来自拉丁语summus（最高的），词干为summ-，表「最高」义

similar_words.description: 形近易混，summit为名词，表峰会、顶峰；summon为动词，表召唤、鼓起

definitions.example: The judicial committee summoned the key witness to testify at the public hearing.
She summoned up the courage to question the leading scholar's controversial research conclusion.

summon.etymology: 来自拉丁语summonere（传唤），由sum-（=super-，在上方）+ monere（警告、告知）构成，与summit同根

similar_words.description: 形近易混，summon为动词，表召唤、鼓起；summit为名词，表峰会、顶峰

definitions.example: The ecological survey team set off for the coastal wetland at sunrise to observe migratory birds.

sunrise.etymology: 合成词，由sun（太阳）+ rise（升起）构成，表太阳升起的时刻或景象

similar_words.description: 形近且反义，sunrise表日出，sunset表日落

definitions.example: The contemporary novelist often drew inspiration from the stunning sunset over the Mediterranean Sea.

sunset.etymology: 合成词，由sun（太阳）+ set（落下）构成，表太阳落下的时刻或景象

similar_words.description: 形近且反义，sunset表日落，sunrise表日出

definitions.example: Sufficient sunshine is a core prerequisite for the growth of most tropical flowering plants.

sunshine.etymology: 合成词，由sun（太阳）+ shine（光亮）构成，表太阳发出的光或温暖感

similar_words.description: 近义，sunshine侧重温暖的日照或喻义，sunlight侧重太阳光本身

definitions.example: His super ability to process big data helped the research team complete the project ahead of schedule.

super.etymology: 来自拉丁语super（在…之上、超过），原义为「超出一般水平的」

similar_words.description: 形近且近义，super偏口语化，superb更正式，表卓越的

definitions.example: The historian's superb mastery of primary sources made his monograph highly influential in academic circles.

superb.etymology: 来自拉丁语superbus（卓越的、骄傲的），由super-（超过）+ 形容词后缀构成

similar_words.description: 形近且近义，superb更正式，多用于书面语；super偏口语化

definitions.example: The mainstream media's superficial coverage of the policy ignored its long-term social equity impacts.

superficial.etymology: 由super-（在…之上）+ fic-（做）+ -ial（形容词后缀）构成，原义为「在表面完成的」，引申为肤浅的

similar_words.description: 形近易混，superficial表表面的、肤浅的；superfluous表多余的、过剩的

definitions.meaning: 多余的，过剩的，不必要的
definitions.example: The academic editor removed the superfluous paragraphs to make the research paper more concise and targeted.

superfluous.etymology: 由super-（超过）+ flu-（流）+ -ous（形容词后缀）构成，原义为「流出的、溢出的」，引申为多余的

similar_words.description: 形近易混，superfluous表多余的；superficial表表面的、肤浅的

definitions.meaning: adj. 优秀的，出众的；上级的；n. 上级，上司
definitions.example: This new renewable energy technology is superior to traditional fossil fuel methods in reducing carbon emissions.

superior.etymology: 来自拉丁语superior（在上方的），由super-（超过）+ 比较级后缀-ior构成，核心义为「优于…的」

similar_words.description: 形近且反义，superior表优于、上级的；inferior表劣于、下级的

definitions.example: Research has shown that children from affluent families often have a sense of intellectual superiority over their peers from low-income backgrounds.

superiority.etymology: 前缀super-（超过）+词根superior（优越的）+名词后缀-ity，表「超过的状态」

similar_words.description: superiority表「优势」，inferiority表「劣势」，为同根反义词，拼写仅前缀不同

definitions.example: The supersonic aircraft, once hailed as the future of air travel, faced strict noise regulations in most countries.

supersonic.etymology: 前缀super-（超过）+词根son（声音）+形容词后缀-ic，表「超过声速的」

similar_words.description: supersonic指「超音速的（速度）」，ultrasonic指「超声波的（声波频率）」，均含表「声音」的词根son

definitions.example: In many traditional societies, black cats are still associated with superstition and bad luck.

superstition.etymology: 前缀super-（超过）+词根stit（站立）+名词后缀-ion，原指「超出常规认知的信仰」

similar_words.description: superstition表「迷信」，supervision表「监督」，均含前缀super-，易因形近混淆

definitions.example: Many elderly people in rural areas hold superstitious beliefs about natural phenomena.

superstitious.etymology: 前缀super-（超过）+词根stit（站立）+形容词后缀-ious，对应名词superstition

similar_words.description: superstitious表「迷信的」，supercilious表「傲慢的」，均含前缀super-，易因形近混淆

definitions.example: The professor supervised a team of graduate students conducting research on climate change.

supervise.etymology: 前缀super-（在上方）+词根vis（看）+动词后缀-e，原指「在上方查看」即监督

similar_words.description: supervise侧重「上级对下级的针对性监督指导」，oversee侧重「对全局的总体把控监督」，为近义动词

definitions.example: The newspaper publishes a weekly supplement on cultural events and art exhibitions.
Many students supplement their classroom learning with online courses to broaden their horizons.

supplement.etymology: 前缀sup-（=sub-，辅助）+词根ple（填满）+名词后缀-ment，原指「辅助填满的事物」

similar_words.description: supplement侧重「对已有事物的非必需额外补充」，complement侧重「使事物更完整的必需补充」

definitions.example: The local government promised to supply sufficient medical equipment to remote rural hospitals.
The country has a limited supply of fresh water in its arid northwestern regions.

supply.etymology: 前缀sup-（=sub-，向下）+词根ply（填满），原指「向下填满容器」即供应

similar_words.description: supply侧重「提供具体物品或资源」，常与with搭配；provide侧重「提供所需（抽象或具体）」，常与for搭配

definitions.example: Most environmental activists support the strict policy of reducing industrial carbon emissions.
The groundbreaking medical research received substantial financial support from a global charity.

support.etymology: 前缀sup-（=sub-，在下方）+词根port（承载），原指「在下方托住承载」即支撑

similar_words.description: support侧重「给予精神或物质的直接支持」，sustain侧重「维持生命、状态等的持续存在」

definitions.example: Suppose we fail to meet the deadline, what alternative solutions can we propose to the committee?

definitions.meaning: [用于被动结构「be supposed to」]应该；被期望
definitions.example: College students are supposed to complete at least 120 credit hours to obtain a bachelor's degree.

suppose.etymology: 前缀sup-（=sub-，在下方）+词根pose（放置），原指「放在下方作为前提」即假定

similar_words.description: suppose侧重「基于可能性的合理假设」，assume侧重「未经证实的主观假设」

definitions.example: The authoritarian regime tried to suppress the large-scale public protest against the unfair election results.
The pharmaceutical company attempted to suppress the negative clinical trial reports about its new drug.

suppress.etymology: 前缀sup-（=sub-，向下）+词根press（按压），原指「向下按压」即镇压

similar_words.description: suppress侧重「用武力短期压制行动或信息」，oppress侧重「长期的专制压迫（针对群体）」

definitions.example: The Supreme Court is the highest judicial body in the United States.

supreme.etymology: 由前缀super-（在…之上）加表最高级的后缀-eme构成，源自拉丁语，表「最高层级的」

similar_words.description: superior表「上级的、优秀的」，有比较级；supreme表「最高的、至高的」，无比较级

definitions.example: Global oil prices surged by 15% after the geopolitical conflict broke out.

surge.etymology: 源自拉丁语surgere（升起），由前缀sur-（=sub-，在下）加词根regere（引导）构成，表「从下往上涌起」

similar_words.description: surge为动词/名词表「激增、汹涌」；surgent为形容词表「汹涌的、急剧上升的」

definitions.example: The experienced surgeon successfully performed a complex heart transplant operation.

surgeon.etymology: 由前缀sur-（=super-，在…之上）加表「工作」的词根构成，指「在病人身上实施操作的医护人员」

similar_words.description: surgeon指「外科医生」；surgery指「外科手术、外科学科」

definitions.example: Minimally invasive surgery has become a popular option for treating certain chronic diseases.

surgery.etymology: 由前缀sur-（=super-，在…之上）加表「工作」的后缀构成，指「外科领域的操作工作」

similar_words.description: surgery指「外科手术、外科学科」；surgeon指「施行手术的外科医生」

definitions.example: Li is one of the most common surnames among Chinese people.

surname.etymology: 由前缀sur-（=super-，附加的）加单词name构成，指「附加在名字前/后的家族姓氏」

similar_words.description: surname指「姓」；forename指「名」

definitions.example: The company's annual revenue surpassed $10 billion for the first time in 2023.

surpass.etymology: 由前缀sur-（=super-，超过）加单词pass（通过）构成，表「超出既定范围或标准」

similar_words.description: surpass为动词表「超过、胜过」；surge为动词/名词表「激增、汹涌」

definitions.example: The country has maintained a trade surplus with its major economic partners for five consecutive years.

surplus.etymology: 由前缀sur-（=super-，超过）加单词plus（加）构成，表「超出需求的部分」

similar_words.description: surplus表「盈余、过剩」；deficit表「赤字、短缺」

definitions.example: The enemy troops were forced to surrender after a three-month military siege.

surrender.etymology: 由前缀sur-（=super-，在…之上）加单词render（给予）构成，表「将自身控制权交予对方」

similar_words.description: surrender表「投降、放弃」；render表「给予、致使」

definitions.example: The remote village is surrounded by lush green mountains and crystal-clear rivers.

surround.etymology: 由前缀sur-（=super-，周围）加单词round（圆的）构成，表「围成圆形环绕目标」

similar_words.description: surround为及物动词表「围绕、包围」；round可作介词/形容词表「围绕、圆的」

definitions.example: Adolescents' values are greatly influenced by their family and social surroundings.

surroundings.etymology: 由动词surround（围绕）加复数名词后缀-ings构成，指「围绕在主体周边的具体事物」

similar_words.description: surroundings指「具体的周围环境、事物」；environment可指「抽象或具体的整体环境」

definitions.example: A nationwide survey of college graduates reveals that 68% of them prioritize job stability over high salary.
The research team surveyed 200 rural households to collect data on agricultural production.

survey.etymology: sur-（=super-在上方）+vey（=videre看，词根表‘看’），从上往下审视→调查、勘测

similar_words.description: 均表调查，「survey」侧重系统性、大规模的民意或实地调查，「investigate」侧重对事件、问题的深入细致调查
拼写形近，「survey」表调查/勘测，「surveillance」表监视、监控

definitions.example: In harsh desert environments, water is the key factor for the survival of all living organisms.

survival.etymology: sur-（表‘超过’）+viv（词根表‘生命’）+al（名词后缀），生命超过（死亡/困境）→生存

similar_words.description: 均表存在，「survival」侧重在困境中的存活状态，「existence」侧重普遍的存在状态

definitions.example: Only 12 of the 100 passengers survived the severe plane crash.

survive.etymology: sur-（表‘超过’）+viv（词根表‘生命’）+e，生命超过（灾难/死亡）→幸存；比…活得长

similar_words.description: 均表比…活得长，「survive」可接灾难、人等宾语，「outlive」仅接人或时间宾语

definitions.example: Elderly people are more susceptible to respiratory infections during winter.

susceptible.etymology: sus-（=sub-表‘在下方’）+cept（词根表‘拿、抓’）+ible（形容词后缀，表‘能…的’），能被从下方抓住的→易受影响的

similar_words.description: 均表易受伤害的，「susceptible」侧重易受外界因素（如疾病、影响）的影响，「vulnerable」侧重因自身脆弱而易受攻击

definitions.example: Researchers suspect that long-term exposure to blue light may damage retinal cells.
The police have arrested three suspects in connection with the bank robbery.

suspect.etymology: sus-（=sub-表‘在下方’）+spect（词根表‘看’），在下方偷偷观察→怀疑；嫌疑犯

similar_words.description: 均表怀疑，「suspect」侧重怀疑某情况属实（如怀疑某人作案），「doubt」侧重怀疑某事不属实（如怀疑消息真假）

definitions.example: The university suspended the student for cheating in the postgraduate entrance examination.

suspend.etymology: sus-（=sub-表‘在下方’）+pend（词根表‘悬挂’），挂在下方→悬挂；引申为暂停、中止

similar_words.description: 均表推迟，「suspend」侧重暂停（可能后续恢复），「postpone」侧重将时间节点往后推延

definitions.example: Her constant absence from work aroused the suspicion of her supervisor.

suspicion.etymology: sus-（=sub-表‘在下方’）+spic（=spect词根表‘看’）+ion（名词后缀），在下方观察的状态→怀疑；嫌疑

similar_words.description: 均表怀疑，「suspicion」侧重对人或事的疑心、嫌疑，「doubt」侧重对事物真实性的不确定

definitions.example: The security guard noticed a suspicious package left near the entrance of the library.

suspicious.etymology: suspicion（怀疑）+ous（形容词后缀，表‘充满…的’），充满怀疑的→可疑的；怀疑的

similar_words.description: 均表怀疑的，「suspicious」侧重对人或事觉得可疑，「doubtful」侧重对事物的可能性或真实性不确定

definitions.example: The local government has taken measures to sustain the growth of small and medium-sized enterprises.
The company sustained a huge financial loss due to the global supply chain disruption.

sustain.etymology: sus-（=sub-表‘在下方’）+tain（词根表‘拿住、保持’），在下方拿住支撑→维持；支撑；遭受

similar_words.description: 均表维持，「sustain」侧重持续支撑（生命、经济增长等），「maintain」侧重保持现有状态（如秩序、设备）

definitions.example: She swallowed the bitter medicine without making a face.
He swallowed the false advertisement claims and bought the overpriced health supplement.

swallow.etymology: 源自古英语swelgan，本义为‘吞、咽’，引申为‘忍受；轻信’等义

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，「swallow」表吞、咽或忍受，「swell」表肿胀、膨胀

definitions.example: The ecologists conducted a long-term study on the rare flora growing in the remote swamp.

definitions.meaning: 使淹没；使忙得不可开交
definitions.example: The sudden influx of applications swamped the admissions office within a week.

swamp.etymology: 源自中古英语swampe，原指潮湿的低洼地带，无明显可拆分的常用词根

similar_words.description: swarm指人或昆虫群集、蜂拥，swamp指沼泽或使忙得不可开交

definitions.example: A flock of white swans glided gracefully across the calm lake in the national nature reserve.
After retiring, he swanned around Southern Europe for three months without a fixed plan.

swan.etymology: 源自古英语swan，与拉丁语cygnus（天鹅）同源，属于印欧语系基础词汇

similar_words.description: swarm指群集，swan指天鹅或悠然漫步

definitions.example: When the celebrity appeared, fans swarmed around the entrance of the theater to take photos.
A swarm of locusts destroyed most of the crops in the rural area last summer.

swarm.etymology: 源自古英语swearm，原指蜂群，可联想为「一群发出嗡嗡声的昆虫」

similar_words.description: swamp指沼泽或淹没，swarm指群集

definitions.example: The expert’s empirical evidence swayed the jury to reach a verdict in favor of the plaintiff.
Under the sway of public opinion, the company revised its controversial marketing strategy.

sway.etymology: 源自中古英语sweien，原指摇摆，可联想为「身体左右摇摆移动」

similar_words.description: swear指发誓、咒骂，sway指摇摆、影响

definitions.example: Before the trial, all witnesses must swear to tell the truth, the whole truth, and nothing but the truth.
He swore under his breath when he realized he had forgotten his passport for the international conference.

swear.etymology: 源自古英语swerian，原指发誓，属于印欧语系基础词汇

similar_words.description: sway指摇摆、影响，swear指发誓、咒骂

definitions.example: Beads of sweat rolled down his face as he defended his doctoral thesis in front of the committee.
The research team sweated over the experiment for six months to obtain reliable data.

sweat.etymology: 源自古英语swāt，原指汗水，无明显可拆分的常用词根

similar_words.description: sweet指甜的、亲切的，sweat指汗水或辛苦工作

definitions.example: She wore a hand-knitted woolen sweater to the outdoor graduation ceremony on the chilly morning.

sweater.etymology: 由sweat（汗水）+ 名词后缀-er（表物品）构成，原指运动时穿的吸汗衫，现引申为毛衣

similar_words.description: sweat为词根（表汗水），sweater是其派生词（表毛衣）

definitions.example: A wave of artificial intelligence innovation swept through the global tech industry in the 2020s.
The police launched a sweep of the neighborhood to find the missing elderly resident.

sweep.etymology: 源自古英语swāpan，原指扫，可联想为「用工具扫过一片区域」

similar_words.description: swell指肿胀、增长，sweep指扫、席卷

definitions.example: The number of international students applying to Chinese universities swelled by 15% last year.

definitions.meaning: 很棒的，一流的（口语僻义）
definitions.example: We had a swell time at the academic exchange conference last weekend.

swell.etymology: 源自古英语swellan，原指膨胀，属于印欧语系基础词汇

similar_words.description: sweep指扫、席卷，swell指肿胀、增长

definitions.example: The government took swift measures to stabilize the housing market after the policy adjustment.
Swifts are known for their long-distance migratory flights between continents.

swift.etymology: 源自古英语swift，原指快的，可联想为「动作快如飞行的鸟类」

similar_words.description: switch指开关、转换，swift指迅速的

definitions.meaning: （使）摇摆，（使）摆动；（观点、情绪等）转变
definitions.example: Public opinion swung in favor of the strict environmental policy after the smog crisis was widely reported.

swing.etymology: 源自中古英语swingan，本义为「鞭打、摆动」，无明显可拆分考研常用词根

similar_words.description: swing侧重快速或有规律的摆动/转变；sway侧重缓慢、无规律或受外力影响的摇摆/影响

definitions.meaning: 转换，切换（观点、设备、焦点等）
definitions.example: The research team switched their experimental method to obtain more accurate data about cell growth.
A sudden switch in government policy led to widespread confusion among small business owners.

switch.etymology: 源自荷兰语swits，本义为「快速移动」，无明显可拆分考研常用词根

similar_words.description: swap侧重「交换（物品、位置）」；switch侧重「切换（状态、对象）」

definitions.example: The medieval knight drew his sword to protect the villagers from the invading army.
The authoritarian regime relied on the sword rather than democratic dialogue to suppress dissent.

sword.etymology: 源自古英语sweord，无明显可拆分考研常用词根

similar_words.description: sword意为「剑、武力」；word意为「单词、话语」，拼写仅首字母不同

definitions.example: The red rose is a universal symbol of love and romance in Western literary works.
Chemical symbols such as H₂O and CO₂ are fundamental to understanding basic chemistry.

symbol.etymology: sym-（共同）+ bol（投掷），本义为「共同投掷的标记」，引申为「象征」

similar_words.description: symbol侧重具有特定文化或抽象意义的「象征」；sign侧重传递具体信息的「标志、迹象」

definitions.example: The architectural design of the Forbidden City emphasizes strict symmetry in its overall layout.

symmetry.etymology: sym-（共同）+ metry（测量），本义为「共同测量的比例」，即「对称」

similar_words.description: symmetry意为「对称」，来自词根metry（测量）；sympathy意为「同情」，来自词根path（情感）

definitions.example: The sympathetic teacher stayed after school to help the student who had lost his textbooks.
Most members of the city council were sympathetic to the proposal for building more public parks.

sympathetic.etymology: sym-（共同）+ path（情感）+ -etic（形容词后缀），本义为「有共同情感的」

similar_words.description: sympathetic侧重「出于对他人不幸的同情的」；empathic侧重「能深入共情他人感受的」

definitions.example: I deeply sympathize with the families affected by the earthquake, having survived a similar disaster.
Many local residents sympathize with the activists’ demand for stricter gun control laws.

sympathize.etymology: sym-（共同）+ path（情感）+ -ize（动词后缀），本义为「产生共同情感」

similar_words.description: sympathize侧重「对他人表示同情」；empathize侧重「深入共情他人的感受」

definitions.example: The public expressed overwhelming sympathy for the homeless man who had saved the child from drowning.
The new labor law gained sympathy from trade unions across the nation.

sympathy.etymology: sym-（共同）+ path（情感）+ -y（名词后缀），本义为「共同的情感」

similar_words.description: sympathy侧重「对他人不幸的同情、怜悯」；empathy侧重「对他人感受的共情、共鸣」

definitions.example: Beethoven’s Ninth Symphony is widely regarded as a masterpiece of Western classical music.
The autumn leaves, blue sky and gentle breeze created a symphony of natural beauty.

symphony.etymology: sym-（共同）+ phony（声音），本义为「共同演奏的声音」，即「交响乐」

similar_words.description: symphony意为「交响乐、和谐」，来自词根phony（声音）；symposium意为「研讨会」，来自词根pos（放置）

definitions.example: The university held a symposium on the ethical implications of gene editing technology.
The symposium on global warming includes peer-reviewed papers from leading climate scientists.

symposium.etymology: sym-（共同）+ pos（放置）+ -ium（名词后缀），本义为「共同放置（观点）的场合」，即「研讨会」

similar_words.description: symposium意为「研讨会、专题论文集」，来自词根pos（放置）；symphony意为「交响乐、和谐」，来自词根phony（声音）

definitions.example: The persistent decline in employment rate is a clear symptom of the country's economic recession, which has been analyzed in many economic journals.

symptom.etymology: 词根词缀拆分：sym-（共同） + ptom（落下），指共同显现的异常状态，引申为症状、征兆

similar_words.description: 「symptom」指单一症状或问题征兆，「syndrome」指综合症状或典型症候群

definitions.example: To ensure the accuracy of the experiment, the research team synchronized all data-recording devices before starting the test.

synchronize.etymology: 词根词缀拆分：syn-（共同） + chron（时间） + -ize（动词后缀），指使处于同一时间维度，即同步、协调

similar_words.description: 「synchronize」指使同步、协调，「sympathize」指同情、赞同

definitions.meaning: 综合征；（社会问题的）典型表现
definitions.example: The 'burnout syndrome' among young office workers has become a widely discussed issue in recent sociological studies.

syndrome.etymology: 词根词缀拆分：syn-（共同） + drome（跑），指一起显现的一系列症状，引申为综合征、典型表现

similar_words.description: 「syndrome」指综合症状或典型症候群，「symptom」指单一症状或问题征兆

definitions.example: The scholar's latest work offers a synthesis of classical and modern theories to explain the evolution of social structures.

synthesis.etymology: 词根词缀拆分：syn-（共同） + thesis（放置），指将不同内容放在一起整合，引申为综合、合成

similar_words.description: 「synthesis」指综合、合成，「hypothesis」指假说、假设

definitions.example: Synthetic fibers are often used in clothing manufacturing because they are more durable than natural fibers.

synthetic.etymology: 词根词缀拆分：syn-（共同） + thet（放置） + -ic（形容词后缀），指人工整合制成的，即合成的、人造的

similar_words.description: 「synthetic」指合成的、人造的，「sympathetic」指同情的、赞同的

definitions.example: The reform of the public healthcare system is one of the core policies proposed by the government this year.

system.etymology: 词根词缀拆分：sys-（syn-的变体，共同） + st（站立） + -em，指共同构成的整体结构，引申为系统、体系、制度

similar_words.description: 「system」是名词，指系统、制度；「systematic」是形容词，指系统的、有条理的

definitions.example: The research team conducted a systematic investigation of the environmental pollution in the river basin over five years.

systematic.etymology: 词根词缀拆分：system（系统） + -atic（形容词后缀），指具有系统特征的，即系统的、有条理的

similar_words.description: 「systematic」是形容词，指系统的；「system」是名词，指系统、制度

definitions.example: The doctor advised the patient to take one anti-inflammatory tablet twice a day for a week.

tablet.etymology: 源自古法语，原指小块平板状物体，后引申为药片、平板电脑

similar_words.description: 「tablet」指药片或平板电脑，「table」指桌子或表格

definitions.example: In some traditional communities, discussing premarital pregnancy is still a strong social taboo that people dare not mention.

taboo.etymology: 源自波利尼西亚语，无明确可拆分的词根词缀，指宗教或社会层面禁止提及或触碰的事物

similar_words.description: 「taboo」指禁忌，「tackle」指处理问题或用具

definitions.meaning: v.处理，解决（问题）；n.用具，装备
definitions.example: Many international organizations are working together to tackle the global food shortage crisis.

tackle.etymology: 源自荷兰语，原指“抓住、固定”，后引申为处理问题、装备用具

similar_words.description: 「tackle」指处理问题或用具，「taboo」指禁忌

definitions.example: Researchers adopted a rigorous data-collection tactic to analyze the long-term impact of Arctic warming on polar bear habitats.

tactic.etymology: 源自希腊语「taktikos」，词根「takt-」表示「安排、部署」，原指军事战术部署，后引申为通用策略。

similar_words.description: 「tact」指待人接物的机智圆滑（抽象处事能力），「tactic」指具体的行动策略或战术方案。

definitions.meaning: n. 标签；标牌 vt. 给…贴标签；把…归类
definitions.example: All imported food products must carry a Chinese-language tag indicating their ingredients and production date.

tag.etymology: 源自中古英语「tagge」，原指小型附着片，后引申为各类标签或归类动作。

similar_words.description: 「label」可指各类标识（含文件、产品等），「tag」多指小型纸质/塑料标签，也可引申为对人的归类。

definitions.example: The rocket's tail emitted a bright orange flame as it lifted off from the launch pad.
Undercover detectives tailed the suspect for five days to gather evidence of smuggling.

tail.etymology: 源自古英语「tægl」，原指动物尾巴，后引申为物体尾部及跟踪动作。

similar_words.description: 「trail」指拖痕或林间小径，「tail」指尾巴或跟踪动作，二者拼写及语义接近易混。

definitions.example: The skilled tailor altered the wedding dress to fit the bride perfectly.

definitions.meaning: 专门制作；使适应（特定需求）
definitions.example: Universities tailor their online courses to meet the diverse learning needs of part-time students.

tailor.etymology: 源自古法语「taillier」（切割），原指裁剪布料的裁缝，后引申为按需求定制事物。

similar_words.description: 「alter」指局部修改（衣物或事物），「tailor」指针对特定需求量身定制，针对性更强。

definitions.example: The ancient tale of the Silk Road reflects cultural exchanges between East and West over two thousand years.

tale.etymology: 源自古英语「talu」，原指叙述、计数，后引申为故事或传闻。

similar_words.description: 「tail」指尾巴或尾部，「tale」指故事，二者拼写接近易混淆。

definitions.example: Her talent for statistical analysis was recognized by the journal editor when she submitted her first paper.
The tech giant launched a global talent recruitment program to expand its AI research team.

talent.etymology: 源自希腊语「talanton」（重量单位），原指上帝赋予的「天赋重量」，后引申为才能或人才。

similar_words.description: 「gift」多指天生的天赋，「talent」可指天生或后天培养的才能，也可指人才群体。

definitions.example: The once feral cat has become a tame companion for the elderly widow.
The central bank raised interest rates by 0.5% to tame the surging inflation rate.

tame.etymology: 源自古英语「tam」，原指驯服的动物，后引申为控制抽象事物。

similar_words.description: 「domesticate」特指驯化动植物，「tame」可指驯化动物或控制抽象事物（如通胀）。

definitions.meaning: n. 棕褐色；晒黑的皮肤 vt. 晒黑；鞣（革）
definitions.example: She returned from her tropical vacation with a deep tan on her shoulders and legs.

tan.etymology: 源自古英语「tannian」（鞣革），后引申为晒黑的棕褐色皮肤。

similar_words.description: 「tank」指坦克或水箱，「tan」指棕褐色或晒黑，二者拼写接近易混淆。

definitions.meaning: n. 缠结；混乱局面 vt. 使缠结；使陷入困境
definitions.example: The investigation into the corporate scandal revealed a tangle of illegal transactions spanning three continents.

tangle.etymology: 源自中古英语「tanglen」，原指线绳缠结，后引申为混乱局面。

similar_words.description: 「twist」指扭转或情节转折，「tangle」指缠结成团的混乱（如事务、线绳）。

definitions.example: The country's strategic petroleum reserve is stored in large underground tanks along its coastal provinces.

tank.etymology: 源自葡萄牙语「tanque」（容器），原指储水容器，后引申为坦克或储罐。

similar_words.description: 「tan」指棕褐色或晒黑，「tank」指坦克或水箱，二者拼写接近易混淆。

definitions.example: The oil tanker ran aground on the rocky coast, triggering a severe marine pollution incident.

tanker.etymology: 由词根「tank（油箱、坦克）」加名词后缀「-er（表运载工具）」构成，指用于运输油料的车船

similar_words.description: 同根词，tank指容器、坦克，tanker指运载油料的车船

definitions.example: Researchers are trying to tap into the vast potential of ocean energy to reduce carbon emissions.
She turned on the tap to fill the bucket with clean water.

tap.etymology: 源自古英语tappa，原指塞子、龙头，后引申为「轻拍」，考研常考引申义「利用资源」

similar_words.description: 形近词，tap侧重利用、轻拍，tape指胶带、录制

definitions.example: The journalist used a tape recorder to document the entire interview process.
The academic conference was taped for later broadcast to a global audience.

tape.etymology: 源自古英语tæppe，原指窄布条，后引申为胶带、录音带，再转化为动词义

similar_words.description: 形近词，tape指胶带、录制，tap侧重利用、轻拍

definitions.example: The road construction crew spread hot tar to repair the cracked pavement.
The unsubstantiated rumors were meant to tar the scholar's academic reputation.

tar.etymology: 源自古英语teoru，原指树脂，后指焦油、柏油，引申为动词「涂柏油」，再引申为「玷污名声」

similar_words.description: 形近词，tar指焦油、玷污，tart指酸的、尖刻的

definitions.example: The university set a target of increasing international student enrollment by 15% this year.
The new educational program is targeted at improving students' critical thinking skills.

target.etymology: 源自古法语target，原指小盾牌，后因盾牌是攻击对象引申为「目标」

similar_words.description: 近义词，target侧重具体可量化的目标，goal侧重长期抽象的目标

definitions.example: The two countries reached an agreement to reduce tariffs on agricultural products.
The hotel's tariff includes free breakfast and access to the fitness center.

tariff.etymology: 源自意大利语tariffa，原指税则，后引申为「关税」「价目表」

similar_words.description: 近义词，tariff特指国际贸易中的关税，tax泛指各类税收

definitions.example: Completing the literature review is a fundamental task for postgraduate research.
The supervisor tasked the intern with organizing the conference materials.

task.etymology: 源自古法语tasque，原指税赋，后因税赋是必须完成的事引申为「任务」

similar_words.description: 近义词，task侧重具体分配的任务，duty侧重道德或法律上的责任

definitions.example: The traditional dish has a unique taste that appeals to both locals and tourists.
The summer internship gave her a taste of professional life in the tech industry.

taste.etymology: 源自古法语taster，原指触摸、尝，后引申为「品味」「短暂体验」

similar_words.description: 近义词，taste侧重人对味道的感受或审美品味，flavor侧重食物本身的味道或特色

definitions.example: The government introduced a new tax on luxury goods to reduce income inequality.
The long-term research project taxed the team's patience and resources.

tax.etymology: 源自古法语taxer，原指评估、征税，后引申为「使负重担」

similar_words.description: 近义词，tax泛指各类税收，tariff特指国际贸易中的关税

definitions.example: The interdisciplinary research team published their breakthrough findings in 《Nature》.
The two universities teamed up to conduct a large-scale climate study.

team.etymology: 源自古英语team，原指一队牲畜，后引申为「有共同目标的团队」

similar_words.description: 近义词，team侧重协作性强的固定团队，group侧重随意或临时组成的群体

definitions.example: The fierce storm tore the roof off the small village clinic, exposing patients to the cold rain.
The emergency medical worker tore through the crowded street to reach the accident scene within minutes.

tear.etymology: 源自古英语teran，表「撕、扯」，名词义「眼泪」为同源异体义

similar_words.description: tear表「撕/猛冲」，teem表「大量出现、充满」，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: In some campus mental health surveys, students report that being teased about their appearance can trigger long-term psychological distress.

tease.etymology: 源自古英语tæsan，表「梳理、扯」，引申为「戏弄」

similar_words.description: tease侧重善意或恶意的戏弄，mock侧重带有讽刺意味的嘲笑

definitions.example: The academic paper contains too many technical terms that are inaccessible to non-specialist readers.
The defense lawyer argued that the case should be dismissed based on a technical legal detail.

technical.etymology: 源自希腊语tekhnikos（技艺的），由techn-（技艺）+ -ical（形容词后缀）构成

similar_words.description: technical侧重「专业的、技术细节的」，technological侧重「科技的、工艺体系的」

definitions.example: The lab technician carefully calibrated the spectrometer to ensure accurate experimental data.

technician.etymology: 由technic（技术）+ -ian（表「从事某职业的人」的名词后缀）构成

similar_words.description: technician侧重操作技术的技师，technologist侧重研究技术体系的专家

definitions.example: The renowned pianist’s unique playing technique has been studied by music majors across the globe.

technique.etymology: 源自希腊语tekhnike（技艺），经法语传入英语

similar_words.description: technique侧重具体的「操作技巧、方法」，technology侧重宏观的「技术体系、科技」

definitions.example: Advances in medical technology have significantly improved the survival rate of critically ill patients.

technology.etymology: 由techn-（技艺）+ -o-（连接元音）+ -logy（表「学科、体系」的后缀）构成

similar_words.description: technology侧重宏观的科技体系，technique侧重具体的操作技巧

definitions.example: The tedious process of sorting through thousands of old archival documents delayed the progress of the historical research project.

tedious.etymology: 源自拉丁语taedium（厌烦），由ted-（厌烦）+ -ious（形容词后缀）构成

similar_words.description: tedious侧重因冗长、重复而乏味，boring侧重因无趣味而无聊

definitions.example: Many social science studies focus on the behavioral and emotional changes of teenagers during their transition to adulthood.

teenager.etymology: 由teen（十，指13-19岁的年龄后缀）+ -ager（表「处于某阶段的人」的后缀）构成

similar_words.description: teenager为日常通用词，adolescent为学术用词，均指青少年群体

definitions.example: During World War II, families relied on telegrams to receive updates about their relatives serving in the military.

telegram.etymology: 由tele-（远的）+ -gram（表「书写、记录内容」的后缀）构成，指远距离传递的书面信息

similar_words.description: telegram指电报的内容，telegraph指电报机或电报系统

definitions.example: The invention of the electric telegraph revolutionized long-distance communication in the 19th century.
The war correspondent telegraphed the frontline updates to the newspaper editorial office without delay.

telegraph.etymology: 由tele-（远的）+ -graph（表「书写、记录工具」的后缀）构成

similar_words.description: telegraph指电报设备或系统，telegram指电报的内容

definitions.example: Astronomers used a high-powered telescope to observe the newly discovered exoplanet in the outer solar system.

telescope.etymology: 前缀tele-（意为「远」）+ 词根scope（意为「看」），指可远距离观测的光学仪器

similar_words.description: 二者同根scope（看），前缀不同：micro-意为「微」，指显微镜；tele-意为「远」，指望远镜

definitions.example: His short temper often led to conflicts with his teammates during the research project.
The blacksmith adjusted the steel's temper by heating it to a specific temperature and then quenching it rapidly.

temper.etymology: 源自拉丁语temperare（意为「调和、控制」），原指对事物的调和状态，后引申指人的情绪状态

similar_words.description: 二者同根，temper侧重临时的情绪或脾气，temperament侧重天生或长期的性情、气质

definitions.example: The psychologist emphasized that a child's temperament is largely influenced by genetic factors.

temperament.etymology: temper（调和）+ 名词后缀-ment，指人的性情的调和状态，即气质

similar_words.description: 二者同根，temperament指长期稳定的性情，temper指临时的情绪或脾气

definitions.meaning: 喜怒无常的，性情不定的
definitions.example: The temperamental equipment in the laboratory often broke down unexpectedly, delaying the experiment.

temperamental.etymology: temperament（气质）+ 形容词后缀-al，指与性情相关的，尤指情绪不稳定的

similar_words.description: 二者拼写相近，temperamental指性情不定的，temporary指暂时的，词义完全不同，注意区分拼写

definitions.example: Researchers found that a slight increase in ocean temperature has a significant impact on coral reef ecosystems.

temperature.etymology: temper（调和）+ 名词后缀-ature，指冷热调和的程度，即温度

similar_words.description: 二者同根，temperature指温度，temper指脾气，同根异义，注意区分

definitions.example: The ancient Buddhist temple, dating back to the 7th century, is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
She pressed her fingers against her temple to ease the headache caused by long hours of reading.

temple.etymology: 源自拉丁语templum（意为「神圣的空间」），原指宗教祭祀的场所，后引申指头骨两侧的「太阳穴」（被认为是头部的敏感区域）

similar_words.description: 二者拼写相近，temple指寺庙或太阳穴，tempo指节奏、速度，注意区分

definitions.meaning: （音乐的）节奏；（生活、工作的）速度
definitions.example: The slow tempo of rural life appeals to many people who are tired of the fast pace of cities.

tempo.etymology: 源自意大利语tempo（意为「时间」），与英语time同源，原指音乐的时间节奏，后引申为生活、工作的速度

similar_words.description: 二者拼写相近，tempo指节奏、速度，temple指寺庙或太阳穴，注意区分

definitions.example: The company hired temporary workers to handle the surge in orders during the holiday season.

temporary.etymology: 词根tempor（意为「时间」）+ 形容词后缀-ary，指时间短暂的，即临时的

similar_words.description: 二者同根tempor（时间），temporary指暂时的，contemporary指当代的、同时代的

definitions.example: The promising career prospects tempted many overseas scholars to return to China for development.

tempt.etymology: 源自拉丁语temptare（意为「尝试、引诱」），原指尝试做某事，后引申为引诱他人做某事

similar_words.description: 二者同根temptare，tempt指引诱、吸引，attempt指尝试、努力

definitions.example: Many students find it hard to resist the temptation of playing video games when preparing for postgraduate exams.

temptation.etymology: tempt（引诱）+ 名词后缀-ation，指引诱的行为或事物，即诱惑

similar_words.description: 二者同根，tempt是动词「引诱」，temptation是名词「诱惑（物）」

definitions.example: Many urban tenants struggle to afford rising rent in densely populated metropolitan areas.

tenant.etymology: 词根为「ten-」（表持有、拥有），加名词后缀「-ant」（表人），指持有房屋使用权的人

similar_words.description: 「tenant」指租户、房客，「tenet」指信条、原则，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: Undergraduates tend to prioritize academic performance over social activities in their first year.
Community volunteers tend elderly residents living alone during the pandemic.

tend.etymology: 词根为「ten-」（表延伸、伸展），引申为「趋于、倾向」（向某方向延伸）或「照料」（延伸关怀）

similar_words.description: 「tend」为动词表倾向、趋于，「trend」为名词表趋势、潮流，二者拼写及含义易混淆

definitions.example: There is a noticeable tendency for young scholars to publish papers in open-access journals.

tendency.etymology: 由动词「tend」（倾向）加名词后缀「-ency」（表状态、性质）构成，指倾向的状态

similar_words.description: 「tendency」侧重固有或主观倾向，「trend」侧重客观发展的趋势，二者为近义易混词

definitions.example: The tender shoots of the crop were damaged by the unexpected late frost.
The legal representative tendered a formal apology to the plaintiff on behalf of the company.

tender.etymology: 词根为「ten-」（表伸展、拉伸），原指「拉伸后柔软的」（嫩的），引申为「温柔的」，再引申为动词义「正式提出（伸出去）」

similar_words.description: 「tender」可作形容词表温柔的或动词表正式提出，「tendon」为解剖学术语指肌腱，二者拼写相近

definitions.example: The tense atmosphere in the boardroom reflected the severity of the company's financial crisis.
Mastering the correct use of each tense is crucial for accurate English translation in the exam.

tense.etymology: 词根为「ten-」（表绷紧、拉伸），加形容词后缀「-se」，指绷紧的、紧张的，语法义引申为「时态」（时间的界定）

similar_words.description: 「tense」指紧张的或语法时态，「tensile」为物理学术语指可拉伸的，二者拼写相近

definitions.example: The economic tension between major economies has cast a shadow over global trade.

tension.etymology: 词根为「ten-」（表绷紧），加名词后缀「-ion」（表状态），指绷紧的状态，即紧张或张力

similar_words.description: 「tension」侧重多方间的紧张关系或物理张力，「pressure」侧重外界施加的压力，二者为近义易混词

definitions.example: Environmental activists set up tents near the construction site to protest deforestation.

tent.etymology: 词根为「ten-」（表伸展、撑开），指撑开的布制遮蔽物，即帐篷

similar_words.description: 「tent」指帐篷，「tenet」指信条、原则，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The research team put forward a tentative hypothesis about the cause of the climate anomaly.

tentative.etymology: 词根为「tent-」（表伸展、尝试），加形容词后缀「-ative」（表具有…性质的），指尝试性的、试探性的

similar_words.description: 「tentative」侧重试探性、未确定的，「temporary」侧重时间短暂的，二者为近义易混词

definitions.example: The technical term 'carbon neutrality' has become a core concept in environmental policy.
The phenomenon of remote work is often termed 'telecommuting' in academic literature.

term.etymology: 词根为「termin-」（表边界、终点），原指「期限（时间的边界）」，引申为「术语（有定义边界的词）」或动词义「称为（给…定边界）」

similar_words.description: 二者同根（词根termin-表边界、终点），「term」为名词/动词表术语或称为，「terminate」为动词表终止

definitions.example: The study focuses on the psychological support for patients with terminal illnesses.
The high-speed rail terminal integrates multiple transportation modes to facilitate passenger transfer.

terminal.etymology: 词根为「termin-」（表边界、终点），加形容词/名词后缀「-al」，指终点的、晚期的或终端、终点站

similar_words.description: 「terminal」可作形容词表晚期的或名词表终端，「terminus」仅作名词表（铁路等的）终点，二者拼写相近

definitions.example: The employment contract will terminate automatically if the employee fails to meet the performance standard for three consecutive months.

terminate.etymology: 词根「term」（边界、期限）+动词后缀「-inate」，原指“到期限就结束”，后引申为“终止、结束”

similar_words.description: 同根形近词，「terminal」作形容词指“终点的、晚期的”，作名词指“终端；终点站”；「terminate」是动词，表“终止、结束”

definitions.example: The sudden economic recession brought terrible impacts on small and medium-sized enterprises.

terrible.etymology: 词根「terr」（恐惧）+形容词后缀「-ible」（可…的），原义“令人恐惧的”，后引申为“糟糕的”

similar_words.description: 形近易混词，「terrible」含贬义，表“糟糕的、可怕的”；「terrific」含褒义，表“极好的、极棒的”

definitions.example: The research team achieved a terrific breakthrough in the field of renewable energy after two years of hard work.

terrific.etymology: 词根「terr」（恐惧）+形容词后缀「-ific」（使…的），原义“令人敬畏的”，后演变为褒义“极好的”

similar_words.description: 形近易混词，「terrific」含褒义，表“极好的、极棒的”；「terrible」含贬义，表“糟糕的、可怕的”

definitions.example: The unexpected earthquake terrified the local residents who had never experienced such a disaster before.

terrify.etymology: 词根「terr」（恐惧）+动词后缀「-ify」（使…），意为“使产生恐惧”

similar_words.description: 同根词，「terrify」是动词，表“使恐惧”；「terror」是名词，表“恐怖；恐惧”

definitions.example: The two countries reached an agreement on the delimitation of their border territory after years of negotiation.

territory.etymology: 词根「terr」（土地）+名词后缀「-itory」（表场所、范围），指“土地的范围”，即“领土、领域”

similar_words.description: 近义词，「territory」侧重“地理/政治意义的领土、领地”或“活动领域”；「domain」侧重“学术、专业领域”或“势力范围”

definitions.example: The spread of extremism has brought widespread terror to the peaceful region.

terror.etymology: 词根「terr」（恐惧）+名词后缀「-or」（表状态或事物），指“恐惧的状态”

similar_words.description: 近义词，「terror」侧重“由暴力、威胁引发的强烈恐惧”；「horror」侧重“由令人厌恶、震惊的事物引发的恐惧”

definitions.example: Scientists test the effectiveness of the new drug on mice before conducting clinical trials on humans.
The final exam is a comprehensive test of students' mastery of the course content.

test.etymology: 源自古法语「test」，原指“用于检验金属纯度的陶碗”，后引申为“检验、测试”

similar_words.description: 近义词，「test」侧重“通过实践、实验检验性能或能力”；「examine」侧重“仔细检查、审查（如疾病、证据）”

definitions.example: The witness testified in court that she had seen the suspect at the crime scene on the night of the murder.

testify.etymology: 词根「test」（证据、检验）+动词后缀「-ify」（使…），意为“提供证据证明”

similar_words.description: 近义词，「testify」侧重“在法庭等正式场合作证”；「verify」侧重“通过证据验证真实性”

definitions.example: The expert's testimony provided crucial evidence for the court to reach a fair judgment.

testimony.etymology: 词根「test」（证据）+名词后缀「-imony」（表状态或事物），指“作为证据的陈述”，即“证词、证据”

similar_words.description: 同根词，「testimony」是名词，表“证词、证据”；「testify」是动词，表“作证、证明”

definitions.example: The professor recommended several authoritative textbooks for students to deepen their understanding of the theoretical framework.

textbook.etymology: 复合词，由「text」（课文、文本）+「book」（书籍）构成，指“包含教学内容的书籍”

similar_words.description: 近义词，「textbook」侧重“学校教学用的教科书”；「manual」侧重“操作手册、指南”

definitions.example: The textile industry has long been a cornerstone of the country’s export-oriented economy.

textile.etymology: 来自拉丁词根text-（编织）+ 后缀-ile（表事物），指编织而成的物品

similar_words.description: 「textile」指纺织品或纺织原料，「texture」指物体的质地或文本的结构，二者同根形近易混淆

definitions.example: The archaeologists examined the texture of the ancient pottery to determine its production technique.
The novel’s dense texture reflects the author’s complex narrative strategy.

texture.etymology: 来自拉丁词根text-（编织）+ 后缀-ure（表状态/性质），指编织的状态或质感

similar_words.description: 「texture」指物体质地或文本结构，「textile」指纺织品，二者同根形近易混淆

definitions.meaning: 感恩节（美国、加拿大的传统节日）
definitions.example: Thanksgiving, a traditional holiday in North America, is closely associated with themes of gratitude and family reunion.

thanksgiving.etymology: 合成词，由thanks（感谢）+ giving（给予）构成，原指感谢上帝赐予丰收的宗教仪式

similar_words.description: 「thanksgiving」特指北美感恩节，「thankfulness」指抽象的感激之情

definitions.example: The emergence of commercial theater in the 19th century reshaped the landscape of performing arts.
The general deployed most troops to the northern theater of the war.

theater.etymology: 来自希腊语theatron（观看场所），词根thea-（看）+ 后缀-tron（表场所），原指观看戏剧的场所，引申为军事行动区域

similar_words.description: 「theater」为美式拼写，「theatre」为英式拼写，二者含义完全一致

definitions.example: The police are investigating the theft of confidential documents from the company’s office.

theft.etymology: 来自古英语theof（小偷）+ 后缀-th（表行为/状态），指小偷实施的行为

similar_words.description: 「theft」指盗窃行为或罪名，「thief」指实施盗窃的人（小偷），二者同根形近易混淆

definitions.meaning: 主题，主旨；（音乐的）主旋律
definitions.example: The overarching theme of the academic paper is the impact of digitalization on rural education.
In functional grammar, the theme of a sentence is the element that serves as the point of departure.

theme.etymology: 来自希腊语thema（放置的事物），词根the-（放置），指放置在中心的核心内容

similar_words.description: 「theme」指贯穿全文/作品的核心主旨，「topic」指讨论或写作的具体话题

definitions.example: The scholar published a paper analyzing the theological implications of the medieval manuscript.

theological.etymology: 来自词根theo-（神）+ 词根-log-（学说）+ 后缀-ical（形容词后缀），指与神学相关的

similar_words.description: 「theological」指与神学相关的，「theoretical」指与理论相关的，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.meaning: 理论的，理论上的；假设的
definitions.example: The study provides a theoretical framework for understanding the mechanism of climate change.

theoretical.etymology: 来自词根theoret-（观察、思考）+ 后缀-ical（形容词后缀），指与观察思考所得的理论相关的

similar_words.description: 「theoretical」指与理论相关的，「theological」指与神学相关的，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The theory of evolution proposed by Darwin has profoundly influenced modern biological research.

theory.etymology: 来自希腊语theoria（观察、思考），指经系统观察思考形成的学说或观点

similar_words.description: 「theory」指经过验证的系统理论，「hypothesis」指未经证实的假设

definitions.example: Cognitive behavioral therapy is widely used to treat anxiety disorders in clinical practice.

therapy.etymology: 来自希腊语therapeia（治疗、护理），词根therap-（治疗）+ 后缀-y（名词后缀）

similar_words.description: 「therapy」侧重非药物的治疗方法（如心理、物理疗法），「treatment」侧重包括药物在内的整体治疗方案

definitions.example: After the bankruptcy filing, the firm suspended all commercial activities thereafter.

thereafter.etymology: 由「there-」（表时间/地点指代）与「after」（在…之后）构成，表‘在那之后’

similar_words.description: 均为「there-」构成的副词，thereafter指时间上的此后，therein指在其中（内容/地点）

definitions.meaning: 因此，由此（表方式带来的结果）
definitions.example: The government introduced tax incentives, thereby encouraging small businesses to expand.
