thereby.etymology: 由「there-」（指代前文内容）与「by」（通过…方式）构成，表‘通过那种方式’

similar_words.description: 均为因果类副词，thereby强调通过某方式产生结果，therefore侧重逻辑推导的结论

definitions.example: The experiment results are inconsistent with the hypothesis; therefore, the theory needs revision.

therefore.etymology: 由「there-」（指代前文逻辑）与「fore」（表承接）构成，表‘基于前文因此’

similar_words.description: 均为因果类副词，therefore侧重逻辑推导的结论，thereby强调方式带来的结果

definitions.example: Thermal power plants are major sources of carbon emissions in many countries.

thermal.etymology: 词根「therm-」（热）加形容词后缀「-al」，表‘与热相关的’

similar_words.description: 同根（「therm-」表热），thermal为形容词‘热的’，thermometer为名词‘温度计’

definitions.example: The researcher used a precision thermometer to measure the temperature of the chemical solution.

thermometer.etymology: 词根「therm-」（热）加连接字母「-o-」与「meter」（测量仪器），表‘测量热的仪器’

similar_words.description: 同根（「therm-」表热），thermometer为名词‘温度计’，thermal为形容词‘热的’

definitions.example: Her master's thesis analyzes the narrative strategies of 20th-century British novels.
The core thesis of the book is that social inequality stems from institutional bias.

thesis.etymology: 词根「thes-」（放置，设定）加名词后缀「-is」，表‘设定的观点或文稿’

similar_words.description: 同根（「thes-」表设定），thesis为学位论文或核心论点，hypothesis为未经证实的假说

definitions.example: The thick layer of snow blocked the main road leading to the mountain village.

thick.etymology: 源自古英语「thicce」，表‘密集的、厚重的’

similar_words.description: 形近易混，thick表‘厚的、浓的’，sick表‘生病的、恶心的’

definitions.example: The witness described the thief as a tall man wearing a black jacket.

thief.etymology: 源自古英语「theof」，表‘盗取他人财物的人’

similar_words.description: 同根易混，thief为名词‘小偷’，thieve为动词‘偷窃’

definitions.example: The soccer player underwent surgery to repair a torn ligament in his right thigh.

thigh.etymology: 源自古英语「theoh」，表‘人体大腿部位’

similar_words.description: 形近易混，thigh为身体部位‘大腿’，high为形容词‘高的’

definitions.example: After running the 10-kilometer race, he quenched his thirst with a bottle of mineral water.
The university encourages students' thirst for interdisciplinary research.

thirst.etymology: 源自古英语「thurst」，原指生理上的口渴，后引申为心理渴求

similar_words.description: 近义易混，thirst侧重口渴或对知识/真理的渴求，hunger侧重饥饿或对权力/财富的渴求

definitions.example: Thorns on the mountain shrub hinder hikers from reaching the hidden alpine lake.
The persistent equipment failure has long been a thorn in the side of the aerospace research team.

thorn.etymology: 源自古英语thorn，原指带刺的植物器官，后通过比喻引申为令人困扰的事物

similar_words.description: 二者形近，thorn指刺或苦恼事，throne指王座或君主地位

definitions.example: The research institute conducted a thorough investigation into the ecological impact of industrial waste discharge.

thorough.etymology: 源自中古英语thoruh，与介词through同源，表「贯穿、完全」，引申为彻底的

similar_words.description: 二者形近，thorough是形容词表彻底的，though是连词表虽然、尽管

definitions.example: The 18th-century Enlightenment thought laid the foundation for modern democratic systems.

definitions.meaning: （think的过去式/过去分词）思考；认为
definitions.example: It was widely thought that the new trade agreement would stimulate global economic growth.

thought.etymology: 源自动词think的过去分词形式，由动作引申为动作的结果（思想、想法）

similar_words.description: 二者形近，thought可作名词或动词过去分词，though仅作连词表虽然

definitions.example: The thoughtful arrangement of the academic seminar facilitated cross-disciplinary communication among scholars.
The professor wore a thoughtful expression while analyzing the complex statistical data.

thoughtful.etymology: 由名词thought加形容词后缀-ful构成，表「充满思考的」，引申为体贴的

similar_words.description: 二者同根反义，thoughtful表体贴的，thoughtless表粗心的、欠考虑的

definitions.example: The thread of the author’s critique of utilitarianism runs through the entire philosophical treatise.
The archivist used a fine cotton thread to repair the torn pages of the 17th-century manuscript.

thread.etymology: 源自古英语thræd，原指纺织用的线，后比喻为论述的线索

similar_words.description: 二者形近，thread指线或线索，threat指威胁或隐患

definitions.example: The increasing frequency of extreme weather events poses a serious threat to global food security.
The witness reported receiving a direct threat from the defendant’s associates before the trial.

threat.etymology: 源自古英语threat，原指压迫、逼迫，后引申为威胁、恐吓

similar_words.description: 二者形近，threat指威胁，thread指线或线索

definitions.meaning: 预示（不良后果）；构成威胁
definitions.example: The ongoing drought threatens to cut crop yields by 25% in the main grain-producing regions.
The extremist group threatened to launch attacks on urban infrastructure if their demands were rejected.

threaten.etymology: 由名词threat加动词后缀-en构成，表「使成为威胁」，引申为预示不良后果

similar_words.description: 二者近义，threaten可表预示不良后果，endanger仅表使处于危险中

definitions.example: The revised regulation lowers the threshold for small and medium-sized enterprises to apply for government subsidies.
She paused on the threshold of the rare book library before stepping inside.

threshold.etymology: 源自古英语threscold，由thresh（打谷、踩踏）和hold（处所）构成，原指踩踏的门槛，引申为入门界限

similar_words.description: 二者近义，threshold指入门的界限或起点，limit指一般的数量或范围限制

definitions.example: The government advocates thrift in public administration to reduce unnecessary fiscal expenditure.
Vibrant pink thrift blooms cover the coastal cliffs during the early summer months.

thrift.etymology: 源自古英语thrift，原指繁荣、兴旺，后因节俭可带来繁荣，引申为节俭

similar_words.description: 二者同根，thrift是名词表节俭，thrive是动词表繁荣、兴旺

definitions.example: The thriftless consumption patterns of the youth have sparked discussions about financial literacy education.
His thriftless attempts to reverse the company’s declining market share ended in disappointment.

thriftless.etymology: 由名词thrift加否定后缀-less构成，表「无节俭意识的」，引申为无用的

similar_words.description: 二者形近，thriftless表不节俭的，thoughtless表粗心的、欠考虑的

definitions.example: Many thrifty households in the national economic survey chose to cut non-essential spending amid persistent inflation.

thrifty.etymology: 来自名词thrift（节俭）+ 形容词后缀-y，thrift源自古英语thrifan（繁荣、节俭）

similar_words.description: 同根词，thriftless意为「浪费的、挥霍的」，是thrifty的反义词
均指「节俭的」，thrifty侧重善于理财，frugal侧重生活朴素

definitions.example: The announcement of the 2023 Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine thrilled the global biomedical research community.
She still recalls the thrill of publishing her first academic paper in a top journal.

thrill.etymology: 来自中古英语thrillen（刺穿），原指物理刺穿，后引申为情绪上的强烈冲击

similar_words.description: 形近词，thrill表情绪兴奋，thrive表事物繁荣兴旺
均表「使兴奋」，excite为通用词，thrill侧重强烈且短暂的兴奋感

definitions.example: Native plant species thrive in the undisturbed, nutrient-rich wetland ecosystem of the national reserve.

thrive.etymology: 源自古英语thrifan（繁荣、茁壮成长），与thrift为同根词

similar_words.description: 均指「繁荣」，thrive侧重适宜环境下的生长，flourish侧重达到鼎盛状态
形近词，thrill表情绪兴奋，thrive表事物繁荣

definitions.example: The clinical trial participants reported mild throat irritation as a common side effect of the new mRNA vaccine.
The narrow throat of the ancient canal restricted the passage of large cargo ships in the 18th century.

throat.etymology: 源自古英语throte（喉咙），与古诺斯语throt（喉咙）同源

similar_words.description: 形近词，throat指喉咙/入口，throne指王位/宝座

definitions.example: The constitutional amendment clarified the line of succession to the British throne for female heirs.

throne.etymology: 源自古英语thron，借自拉丁语thronus，最终源自希腊语thronos（宝座）

similar_words.description: crown指王冠（实物），throne指王位（权力象征）
形近词，throat指喉咙，throne指王位

definitions.example: Throughout the 19th-century industrial revolution, urban populations in Western Europe grew exponentially.

throughout.etymology: 由介词through（穿过）+ 副词out（向外）复合而成，表「遍及全部范围」

similar_words.description: through侧重「穿过某一空间/时间点」，throughout侧重「遍及全部范围/整个时间段」

definitions.example: The investigative journalist thrust a microphone toward the politician, demanding a response to the corruption allegations.
The thrust of the UN climate report was the urgent need for global carbon emission cuts by 2030.

thrust.etymology: 源自古诺斯语thrysta（推），与古英语thystan（推）同源

similar_words.description: 均表「推」，push为通用词，thrust侧重突然、用力的猛推

definitions.example: The Renaissance art historian noted that da Vinci often used his thumb to create soft, blended textures in his portraits.
A widely accepted rule of thumb for academic writing is to cite at least 10 peer-reviewed sources for a term paper.

thumb.etymology: 源自古英语thuma（拇指），与古高地德语dumo（拇指）同源

similar_words.description: 形近词，thumb指拇指，thunder指雷/雷声

definitions.example: The thunder of distant artillery fire echoed across the battlefield during the historical reenactment of the 1815 battle.
The audience thundered its applause when the Nobel laureate finished his keynote speech.

thunder.etymology: 源自古英语thunor（雷），与古诺斯语Thorr（雷神托尔）同源

similar_words.description: lightning指闪电，thunder指雷声，二者常伴随出现
形近词，thumb指拇指，thunder指雷

definitions.example: The study found a significant inverse correlation between screen time and adolescent cognitive function, thus emphasizing the need for digital detox programs.

thus.etymology: 源自古英语thus（如此、这样），与古高地德语dus（如此）同源

similar_words.description: 均为逻辑连接词，therefore侧重正式逻辑推理，thus可表结果或方式，位置更灵活

definitions.meaning: （钟表等）发出滴答声；（在清单、试卷上）打钩
definitions.example: Please tick the correct option on the answer sheet before submitting it.
The tick of the clock became more noticeable in the silent reading room.

tick.etymology: 拟声词，源自古英语「ticken」，模拟时钟滴答的声响

similar_words.description: 「tick」指打钩、滴答声，「trick」指诡计、技巧，为形近易混词

definitions.meaning: 潮汐；（舆论、思想等的）潮流，趋势
definitions.example: The tide of public opinion has shifted in favor of renewable energy policies in recent years.

tide.etymology: 源自古英语「tid」，原指时间、季节，后引申为随时间周期性变化的潮汐，再抽象为潮流

similar_words.description: 「tide」指潮汐、潮流，「tied」是「tie」的过去式/过去分词，表系住的、绑定的，为形近易混词

definitions.example: The research lab must be kept tidy to ensure the accuracy of experimental data.
She tidied up the desk before starting her graduate thesis writing.

tidy.etymology: 源自中古英语「tidi」，原指适时的，后引申为适时整理过的、整洁的

similar_words.description: 「tidy」指整洁的、整理，「tardy」指迟到的、拖延的，为形近易混词

definitions.example: The researcher tied the sample bags with waterproof strings to avoid contamination.
Cultural exchanges can strengthen the ties between different countries.

similar_words.description: 「tie」指系、纽带，「lie」指躺、撒谎，为形近易混词

definitions.example: The South China tiger is listed as a critically endangered species in the IUCN Red List.

tiger.etymology: 源自波斯语「tigra」，原指箭，后引申为速度如箭的老虎

similar_words.description: 「tiger」指老虎，「timer」指计时器、定时器，为形近易混词

definitions.meaning: 紧的；紧身的；（时间、预算等）紧张的
definitions.example: The graduate students faced a tight schedule to complete their dissertations before the deadline.

tight.etymology: 源自古英语「thight」，原指拉紧的

similar_words.description: 「tight」指紧的、紧张的，「light」指轻的、光亮的，为形近易混词

definitions.example: The roof tiles of the old library were damaged by the heavy rainstorm last winter.

tile.etymology: 源自拉丁语「tegula」，原指覆盖物，后引申为用于覆盖屋顶或地面的瓦片、瓷砖

similar_words.description: 「tile」指瓷砖、瓦片，「till」指直到、收银台，为形近易混词

definitions.example: The astronomer tilted the telescope slightly to observe the distant galaxy more clearly.

tilt.etymology: 源自中古英语「tilten」，原指倾斜

similar_words.description: 「tilt」指倾斜，「till」指直到、收银台，为形近易混词

definitions.example: The construction company imported high-quality timber from Northern Europe for the apartment project.

timber.etymology: 源自古英语「timber」，原指建筑材料，后特指木材、木料

similar_words.description: 「timber」指木材，「timer」指计时器、定时器，为形近易混词

definitions.example: Timely intervention from the academic advisor helped the graduate student correct the research direction.

timely.etymology: 由名词「time」（时间）加形容词后缀「-ly」构成，表符合时间节点的、及时的

similar_words.description: 「timely」指及时的，「timeless」指永恒的、不受时间影响的，为同根易混词

definitions.example: The timid student hesitated to raise his hand even when he knew the correct answer.

timid.etymology: 来自拉丁语timidus（害怕的），词根tim-表「害怕」

similar_words.description: 均表胆怯的，timorous更侧重因恐惧而畏缩，多用于书面；timid侧重天性羞怯

definitions.example: The old shelf held several rusted tins of preserved fruit.

tin.etymology: 古英语原生词，原指金属锡，后引申为锡制容器（罐头）

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，tin指锡/罐头，pin指别针、大头针

definitions.example: The scientist observed a tiny crack on the surface of the ancient fossil under the microscope.

tiny.etymology: 来自古英语tynne（薄的、细的），引申为尺寸极小的

similar_words.description: 均表微小的，minute侧重尺寸或程度极小，需注意其作形容词时发音不同；tiny侧重直观上的细小

definitions.example: The tip of the pen was broken, so it could not write smoothly.
The professor gave students a useful tip for tackling complex reading comprehension questions.

tip.etymology: 来自古英语typpa（尖端），后通过语义引申出小费、提示等义

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，tip指尖端/小费/提示，top指顶部、顶端

definitions.example: Long hours of reading without rest will tire even the most diligent students.
The driver stopped to check the tire pressure before continuing the long journey.

tire.etymology: 动词义来自古英语teorian（疲劳）；名词义（轮胎）为英式tyre的美式拼写变体

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，tire指疲劳/轮胎，tier指等级、层级

definitions.example: The tiresome lecture on administrative procedures made most audience members doze off.

tiresome.etymology: 由动词tire（使厌倦）加形容词后缀-some（表「令人…的」）构成

similar_words.description: 同根词，tired表（人）感到疲倦的；tiresome表（事物）令人厌烦的

definitions.example: The research team analyzed the brain tissue samples to study neurodegenerative diseases.
She reached for a tissue to wipe her tears during the emotional movie scene.

tissue.etymology: 来自拉丁语tela（织物），因生物组织的结构类似织物引申出「（生物）组织」义，后又指薄纸、纸巾

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，tissue指生物组织/纸巾，issue指问题、发行、流出

definitions.example: The title of the academic paper clearly indicates its research focus on climate change.
After years of dedicated service, she was awarded the title of 'Outstanding Educator'.

title.etymology: 来自古法语titre，原指铭文、标题，后引申为「头衔、称号」义

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，title指标题/头衔，tittle指微量、小点，多用于固定搭配

definitions.example: At the wedding reception, the best man raised his glass to propose a toast to the newlyweds.
She toasted two slices of bread for breakfast before heading to the exam site.

toast.etymology: 来自拉丁语tostus（烤过的），原指烤面包，因古代祝酒时常搭配烤面包的习俗引申出「祝酒、干杯」义

similar_words.description: 均表烘烤，roast侧重烤肉类等大块食物，温度较高；toast侧重烤面包片等薄食物，或指祝酒

definitions.example: The government has implemented strict regulations to ban tobacco advertising in public places.

tobacco.etymology: 来自西班牙语tabaco，原指烟草植物，后泛指烟草、烟叶

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，tobacco指烟草、烟叶；taboo指禁忌、忌讳

definitions.example: The hiker’s big toe was badly bruised after trekking on the rocky mountain trail for 10 hours.

toe.etymology: 源自古英语「tā」，是表示「足趾」的基础原生词汇，无复杂词缀拆分

similar_words.description: toe为名词表脚趾，tow为动词/名词表拖曳、拖缆，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The newly renovated public toilet in the scenic area provides accessible facilities for disabled visitors.

toilet.etymology: 源自法语「toilette」，原指「梳妆台」，后通过委婉引申指「洗手间、厕所」

similar_words.description: toilet表厕所，toile为名词表印花织物、画布，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: In Western cultures, white doves are universally regarded as a token of peace.
The university’s library uses plastic tokens to manage the borrowing of reference books.

token.etymology: 源自古英语「tācen」，原指「标记、符号」，后逐步引申为象征、代币等义

similar_words.description: token侧重作为具体信物或凭证的象征，symbol侧重抽象的、具有普遍意义的象征符号

definitions.example: Tolerance of diverse academic viewpoints is a core value of modern higher education.
Cacti have evolved a high tolerance to extreme drought and high temperatures.

tolerance.etymology: 源自拉丁语「tolerantia」，由词根「toler-（忍受）」加名词后缀「-ance」构成

similar_words.description: tolerance侧重主观的宽容态度或客观的耐受力，toleration侧重官方对异见、异教的正式容忍政策

definitions.example: A tolerant campus atmosphere encourages students to express their unique ideas freely.
Some new energy crops are highly tolerant of poor soil and harsh weather conditions.

tolerant.etymology: 由动词「tolerate（忍受）」加形容词后缀「-ant」构成，表「具有…性质的」

similar_words.description: tolerant表（人/事物）宽容的、耐受力强的，tolerable表（情况、痛苦等）可忍受的、尚好的

definitions.example: Professors will not tolerate any form of plagiarism in students’ graduation theses.
The local government does not tolerate illegal parking in the downtown business district.

tolerate.etymology: 源自拉丁语「tolerare」，意为「忍受、支撑」，是「tolerance」「tolerant」的核心词根

similar_words.description: tolerate侧重勉强忍受不喜欢的人或行为，bear侧重承受痛苦、压力等较重的生理或心理负担

definitions.example: Drivers must pay a toll of 15 yuan to cross the Yangtze River Bridge in this city.
The continuous heavy rain has taken a heavy toll on the rural areas, destroying hundreds of houses.

toll.etymology: 源自古英语「tol」，原指「税、费」，后通过引申获得「灾难造成的损失、伤亡」这一僻义

similar_words.description: toll特指道路、桥梁的通行费或灾难造成的损失，tax泛指政府向民众、企业征收的各类税款

definitions.example: Tomato is a key ingredient in many traditional Italian dishes such as pizza and pasta sauce.

tomato.etymology: 源自西班牙语「tomate」，原指美洲原住民种植的一种果实，后传入欧洲并普及

similar_words.description: tomato表番茄，potato表土豆，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: Archaeologists discovered a well-preserved ancient tomb dating back to the Han Dynasty last year.

tomb.etymology: 源自希腊语「tumbos」，原指「土堆、坟丘」，后引申为「坟墓、墓穴」

similar_words.description: tomb表坟墓，tome表大部头书籍、学术巨著，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The port handles over 10 million tons of cargo every year, making it a regional logistics hub.
The environmental protection organization has received tons of donations from the public since its launch.

ton.etymology: 源自古英语「tunne」，原指「大桶」，因大桶可容纳大量物品，后引申为重量单位「吨」，再进一步引申为「大量、许多」

similar_words.description: ton表吨或大量，tan表棕褐色、晒黑的皮肤，二者拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: According to the passage, the author's tone towards the new environmental policy is cautiously supportive.

tone.etymology: 源自拉丁语tonus，词根「ton-」表「声音、音调」，同根词如「intonation」（语调）

similar_words.description: 二者为形近易混词，tone表语气、音调；tongue表舌头、语言

definitions.example: Many indigenous tribes are making efforts to pass down their native tongue to younger generations.

tongue.etymology: 源自古英语tunge，原始日耳曼语根，表「舌头」，引申指「语言」

similar_words.description: 二者为形近易混词，tongue表舌头、语言；tone表语气、音调

definitions.example: Big data has become a critical tool for social scientists to analyze public opinion trends.

tool.etymology: 源自古英语tōl，原始日耳曼语根，表「实用器具」

similar_words.description: 二者为形近易混词，tool表工具、手段；toll表通行费、伤亡人数

definitions.example: Only applicants with top academic performance will be shortlisted for the graduate program interview.
The research team finally reached the top of the mountain after a 12-hour climb.

top.etymology: 源自古英语top，原始日耳曼语根，表「物体的最高部分」

similar_words.description: 二者近义，top侧重物体的整体顶部或最高位，可作形容词；tip侧重物体的尖端、末端

definitions.example: The 2023 Global Climate Summit will focus on the topic of carbon neutrality in developing countries.

topic.etymology: 源自希腊语topikos，词根「top-」表「地方、主题」，原指「与某地相关的讨论话题」

similar_words.description: 二者近义，topic指具体的讨论、写作话题；theme指更宏观的主旨、核心主题

definitions.example: Rescue workers used torches to navigate through the dark, debris-filled tunnel after the earthquake.

torch.etymology: 源自古法语torche，源自拉丁语torquere（扭曲），原指「浸油的扭曲布制火把」

similar_words.description: 二者为形近易混词，torch表手电筒、火炬；torture表折磨、酷刑

definitions.meaning: 折磨，痛苦；令人痛苦的事物
definitions.example: For many elderly people living alone, chronic loneliness is a constant torment in their later years.
Reporters tormented the award-winning scientist with endless questions about his personal life.

torment.etymology: 源自拉丁语tormentum，词根「tort-」表「扭曲、折磨」，同根词如「torture」（酷刑）

similar_words.description: 二者同根「tort-」（表扭曲、折磨），torment侧重精神上的持续折磨或纠缠；torture侧重蓄意的肉体或精神酷刑

definitions.meaning: 激流，洪流；（话语、批评等的）爆发，连发
definitions.example: The newly appointed minister faced a torrent of criticism from the opposition for his controversial tax reform.

torrent.etymology: 源自拉丁语torrens，词根「tort-」表「扭曲、奔流」，原指「湍急扭曲的水流」，引申指「话语的爆发」

similar_words.description: 二者为形近易混词，torrent表激流、话语爆发；torment表折磨、痛苦

definitions.example: International human rights organizations have documented numerous cases of torture in illegal detention centers.
The suspect claimed he was tortured by police during the interrogation to force a confession.

torture.etymology: 源自拉丁语tortura，词根「tort-」表「扭曲、折磨」，原指「扭曲身体的酷刑」

similar_words.description: 二者同根「tort-」（表扭曲、折磨），torture侧重蓄意的酷刑或剧烈折磨；torment侧重精神上的持续纠缠或痛苦

definitions.example: The cargo ship was tossed violently by huge waves during the severe storm in the North Atlantic.
The referee decided the starting team of the football match by a toss of a coin.

toss.etymology: 源自中古英语tossen，斯堪的纳维亚语源，表「随意抛扔」

similar_words.description: 二者近义，toss侧重随意、轻柔地抛扔，或物体的摇摆颠簸；throw侧重用力、有目标地扔

definitions.example: The total number of wild giant pandas in national reserves has increased by 18% according to the latest official survey.
The annual budget for rural revitalization totals 300 billion yuan this year.

total.etymology: 词根tot-（全部）+形容词后缀-al，表与全部相关的，引申为「总的」，作动词时表「总计达」

similar_words.description: 「total」多作形容词或动词，表总的、总计，「towel」是名词，表毛巾，二者拼写接近易混淆

definitions.example: Visitors to the museum are strictly forbidden to touch the fragile ancient ceramics on display.
The selfless deeds of the rural teachers deeply touched the audience at the award ceremony.

touch.etymology: 来自古法语toucher，词根touch-表接触，引申为触摸、感动等义

similar_words.description: 「touch」意为触摸、感动，多作动词，「tough」意为困难的、坚强的，是形容词，二者仅中间字母有差异

definitions.example: College graduates are facing tough employment pressure due to the expanding labor supply.
Researchers need tough willpower to overcome repeated setbacks in scientific experiments.

tough.etymology: 来自中古英语toghen，原指皮革坚韧耐拉，引申为困难的、坚强的等义

similar_words.description: 「tough」侧重事情难度大或人意志坚韧，「rough」侧重表面粗糙或行为粗鲁，二者拼写、含义均接近易混淆

definitions.example: The department of history organized a 10-day field tour to ancient Silk Road sites for its postgraduate students.
The world-famous symphony orchestra will tour 12 major Asian cities next season.

tour.etymology: 来自古法语tourner（转动），词根tour-表转，原指巡回出行，引申为旅行、游览

similar_words.description: 「tour」意为旅行、游览，「tower」意为塔、高耸，二者拼写末尾字母不同易混淆

definitions.example: The annual national college basketball tournament attracts more than 100 teams from universities across the country.

tournament.etymology: 词根tour-（巡回）+名词后缀-nament，原指中世纪骑士的巡回比武，现引申为锦标赛、联赛

similar_words.description: 「tournament」指正式的锦标赛、联赛，多为体育赛事，「competition」泛指各类竞争、比赛，范围更广

definitions.example: The faulty vehicle was towed to the nearest maintenance point after the breakdown on the highway.

tow.etymology: 来自古英语togian，词根tow-表牵引，引申为拖、拉、牵引等义

similar_words.description: 「tow」是动词/名词，表拖、牵引，「toward」是介词，表朝向、对于，二者拼写前三个字母相同易混淆

definitions.example: The research team is making steady progress toward finding an effective treatment for the genetic disease.
Public attitudes toward artificial intelligence have become more rational in the past five years.

toward.etymology: 介词to（向）+表方向的后缀-ward，构成介词，表朝向、对于

similar_words.description: 「toward」强调朝向某个目标的动态趋势，「to」侧重到达具体地点或对象的结果

definitions.example: Lab researchers are required to wipe the experimental equipment with a sterile towel before each operation.

towel.etymology: 来自古法语toaille，原指亚麻布巾，后引申为毛巾、抹布

similar_words.description: 「towel」是名词，表毛巾，「total」多作形容词或动词，表总的、总计，二者拼写接近易混淆

definitions.example: The 700-year-old wooden tower on the mountain is listed as a key cultural relic under national protection.
The new skyscraper in the central business district towers over all the surrounding buildings.

tower.etymology: 来自古英语torr，原指高耸的石制建筑，引申为名词「塔」，作动词时表「高耸、屹立」

similar_words.description: 「tower」意为塔、高耸，「tour」意为旅行、游览，二者拼写末尾字母不同易混淆

definitions.example: The local government has launched a special campaign to tackle the pollution caused by toxic industrial waste.

toxic.etymology: 词根tox-（毒，源自希腊语toxon，原指涂抹在箭上的毒药）+形容词后缀-ic，表「有毒的、有害的」

similar_words.description: 「toxic」多用于正式、学术语境，指工业、化学类有毒物质，「poisonous」多用于日常语境，指可导致中毒的事物

definitions.example: In the psychological experiment, children were provided with colorful toys to assess their attention span.

definitions.meaning: 漫不经心地考虑；摆弄（与with连用）
definitions.example: He toyed with the idea of quitting his job to pursue a master's degree for months but finally gave up.

toy.etymology: 源自中古英语toye，原指小饰物，无明确可拆解的考研核心词根词缀

similar_words.description: 「toy」指玩具或漫不经心地考虑，「boy」指男孩，二者为形近词

definitions.example: Researchers found tiny traces of ancient pollen in the sediment samples from the lake.
The study traces the development of feminist literary criticism from the 1960s to the present.

trace.etymology: 源自拉丁语trahere（拉，拖），核心词根「tract-」表拉、引，同根词有tractor、attract等

similar_words.description: 「trace」侧重细微痕迹或追溯起源，「track」侧重有形轨迹或持续追踪

definitions.example: The biologists analyzed the tracks of wild wolves to study their migration patterns.
The weather satellite tracks the formation and movement of tropical cyclones around the clock.

track.etymology: 源自古英语traec，与trace同源，核心词根「tract-」表拉、引

similar_words.description: 「track」侧重有形轨迹或持续追踪，「trace」侧重细微痕迹或追溯起源

definitions.example: The agricultural cooperative purchased 15 new tractors to improve farming efficiency.

tractor.etymology: 源自拉丁语trahere（拉），核心词根「tract-」（拉、引）+ 名词后缀「-or」（表工具），意为拉运货物的机器

similar_words.description: 「tractor」指拖拉机，「traitor」指叛徒，二者为形近词

definitions.example: The WTO aims to promote fair and free international trade among member countries.
In rural areas, many young people still learn a traditional trade such as blacksmithing or weaving.

trade.etymology: 源自古英语trada，源自拉丁语tradere（交付，移交），核心词根「trad-」表交付、传承

similar_words.description: 「trade」指贸易或手艺，「tread」指踩、行走，二者为形近词

definitions.example: The company has registered its distinctive logo as a trademark to prevent unauthorized use.

trademark.etymology: 合成词，由「trade」（贸易）+「mark」（标记）构成，原指贸易中用于识别商品的标记

similar_words.description: 「trademark」是受法律保护的注册标识，「logo」是品牌视觉标识（不一定注册）

definitions.example: The university has a long-standing tradition of holding a graduation ceremony on the lawn every June.

tradition.etymology: 源自拉丁语traditio（交付，传承），核心词根「trad-」（交付、传承）+ 名词后缀「-ition」（表行为结果）

similar_words.description: 「tradition」侧重长期传承的文化传统，「custom」侧重社会群体的日常习俗

definitions.example: The expansion of the subway system has significantly reduced traffic congestion in the city center.
The international police force launched a joint operation to combat human trafficking and illegal arms traffic.

traffic.etymology: 源自意大利语traffico（贸易），原指贸易往来的人流车流，后引申为交通，词根「traff-」表交易

similar_words.description: 「traffic」侧重路面交通或非法交易，「transit」侧重货物/人员的运输过境

definitions.meaning: 悲剧（文学体裁）；灾难，不幸事件
definitions.example: The sudden outbreak of the pandemic was a tragedy that affected millions of people worldwide.

tragedy.etymology: 源自希腊语tragoidia（山羊歌，古希腊悲剧的起源），核心词根「trag-」表山羊，后引申为悲剧

similar_words.description: 「tragedy」可指文学悲剧或个人不幸，「calamity」侧重重大自然灾害或公共灾祸

definitions.example: The tragic story of the refugee family touched the hearts of readers across the country.

tragic.etymology: 源自tragedy的形容词形式，核心词根「trag-」（山羊，源自悲剧起源）+ 形容词后缀「-ic」

similar_words.description: 「tragic」是形容词，表悲剧的；「tragedy」是名词，表悲剧或不幸事件

definitions.example: Wildlife researchers trail the endangered gray wolf to document its migration patterns.
A moss-covered trail leads to the hidden waterfall in the forest reserve.

trail.etymology: 来自拉丁语trahere（拉、拖），引申为「拖出的痕迹」或「尾随追踪」

similar_words.description: 「trace」侧重寻找已存在的静态痕迹，「trail」侧重尾随目标的动态追踪

definitions.example: Medical students receive hands-on training in clinical settings before graduation.

training.etymology: 由动词train（训练）加动名词后缀-ing构成，表「训练的行为或过程」

similar_words.description: 「drill」侧重反复机械的操练，「training」侧重系统全面的技能培训

definitions.example: Empathy is a core trait of effective leaders in modern organizations.

trait.etymology: 来自拉丁语tractus（拉、牵引），引申为「个体身上凸显的特征」

similar_words.description: 「feature」侧重外在可见的特征，「trait」侧重内在性格或生物遗传的特质

definitions.example: The historical drama portrays the traitor who leaked state secrets to hostile forces.

traitor.etymology: 来自拉丁语traditor（交付者、背叛者），由trad-（交付、移交）加名词后缀-or构成，引申为「背叛国家的人」

similar_words.description: 「betrayer」泛指所有背叛者，「traitor」侧重背叛国家或核心组织的叛徒

definitions.example: In Berlin, trams provide affordable and eco-friendly public transport for residents.

tram.etymology: 来自荷兰语tram（轨道、车轨），后指沿轨道行驶的电车

similar_words.description: 「trolley」英美义不同，英国指手推车、美国指无轨电车，「tram」统一指有轨电车

definitions.example: The explorers tramp through the snow-covered tundra to reach the research station.
The kind old woman offered food and shelter to the tramp on the cold night.

tramp.etymology: 来自古英语trampian（踩、踏），引申为「重步走」或「无家可归的流浪者」

similar_words.description: 「hike」侧重有计划的休闲徒步，「tramp」侧重艰难无计划的跋涉

definitions.example: The e-commerce platform records every transaction to ensure consumer rights protection.

transaction.etymology: 由trans-（跨越、转移）+ act（行动、做）+ -ion（名词后缀）构成，指「跨双方的业务行动」

similar_words.description: 「deal」侧重非正式的交易或协议，「transaction」侧重正式的金融或商务交易记录

definitions.example: Great literature can transcend time and space to resonate with readers across generations.

transcend.etymology: 由trans-（越过）+ scend（爬、攀登）构成，字面义「爬过」，引申为「超越抽象界限」

similar_words.description: 「surpass」侧重数量或质量上的超过，「transcend」侧重文化、精神等抽象层面的超越

definitions.example: Her transcendent vocal performance at the concert left the audience deeply moved.

transcendent.etymology: 由动词transcend（超越）加形容词后缀-ent构成，表「具有超越性的」

similar_words.description: 「transcendental」侧重哲学上的超验，「transcendent」指实际的卓越或超凡

definitions.meaning: 超验的，先验的（哲学术语）
definitions.example: Kant’s transcendental idealism argues that human knowledge has non-empirical foundational conditions.

transcendental.etymology: 由trans-（越过）+ scend（爬）+ -ental（形容词后缀）构成，指「超出经验范畴的」

similar_words.description: 「transcendent」指实际的卓越超凡，「transcendental」特指哲学上超出经验的超验、先验

definitions.meaning: v. 转移；调动；转学 n. 转移；换乘
definitions.example: Many undergraduate students apply to transfer to a more prestigious university after their first year of study.

transfer.etymology: trans-（穿过、越过）+ fer（带来、携带）→ 带着事物穿过空间/领域，引申为转移、调动

similar_words.description: 「transfer」侧重有形事物或身份的转移（如转学、调动），「transmit」侧重无形事物（如信号、疾病）的传输传播

definitions.meaning: 使变形；使改变性质/外观
definitions.example: Digital technology has transformed the way people access and share academic resources worldwide.

transform.etymology: trans-（改变）+ form（形状）→ 改变事物的形状或性质，引申为变形、转变

similar_words.description: 「transform」侧重事物性质、外观的根本改变，「transfer」侧重位置、身份的转移

definitions.example: Transient fluctuations in global temperature are often overlooked in long-term climate research.

transient.etymology: trans-（穿过）+ -ient（形容词后缀）→ 快速穿过的，引申为短暂的、转瞬即逝的

similar_words.description: 「transient」侧重「转瞬即逝、不留痕迹」的短暂，「temporary」侧重「有固定期限」的暂时

definitions.example: The invention of the transistor in the 1940s revolutionized the development of modern electronics.

transistor.etymology: 由「transfer（传输）」和「resistor（电阻器）」缩合而成，指可传输电信号的半导体器件

similar_words.description: 「transistor」是半导体晶体管（末尾为-istor），「transformer」是电力变压器（末尾为-ormer）

definitions.example: The transition from traditional education to online learning requires adaptive teaching strategies.

transition.etymology: trans-（穿过）+ it（走）+ -ion（名词后缀）→ 从一个阶段走到另一个阶段，引申为过渡、转变

similar_words.description: 「transition」侧重状态、阶段的渐变过渡，「transfer」侧重实体或身份的直接转移

definitions.meaning: 翻译；（理论成果）转化为实践
definitions.example: Scientists strive to translate laboratory discoveries into accessible medical treatments for the public.

translate.etymology: trans-（穿过）+ late（携带、搬运）→ 把语言/成果从一个领域搬到另一个领域，引申为翻译、转化

similar_words.description: 「translate」侧重语言翻译或成果转化，「transform」侧重事物性质的根本质变

definitions.example: The accuracy of academic translation directly affects the quality of cross-cultural academic exchanges.

translation.etymology: translate（翻译）+ -ion（名词后缀）→ 翻译的行为或结果（译文）

similar_words.description: 「translation」指翻译行为或译文，「transformation」指事物的变形或质变

definitions.example: Strict hygiene protocols are essential to reduce the transmission of respiratory infectious diseases.

transmission.etymology: trans-（穿过）+ miss（发送）+ -ion（名词后缀）→ 发送出去穿过空间，引申为传输、传播

similar_words.description: 「transmission」侧重无形事物（信号、疾病）的传播，「transfer」侧重有形事物的转移

definitions.example: Fiber-optic cables can transmit large amounts of data at extremely high speeds.

transmit.etymology: trans-（穿过）+ mit（发送）→ 发送信号/病菌穿过空间，引申为传输、传播

similar_words.description: 「transmit」侧重无形事物（信号、疾病）的传播，「transfer」侧重有形事物或身份的转移

definitions.example: A transparent decision-making process is a key indicator of a responsible corporate governance system.

transparent.etymology: trans-（穿过）+ parent（出现）→ 光线能穿过并看到内部的，引申为透明的、显而易见的

similar_words.description: 「transparent」指完全透明（可清晰看到内部），「translucent」指半透明（仅能透光）

definitions.example: Doctors successfully transplanted a healthy liver from a young donor to the elderly patient last week.

definitions.meaning: 移植手术；移植的植物/器官
definitions.example: The heart transplant performed by the medical team was recorded in a leading medical journal.

transplant.etymology: 前缀trans-（表「转移、跨越」）+ 词根plant（表「种植」），原指移种植物，后扩展至医学器官移植等领域

similar_words.description: 同含前缀trans-，transfer指「转移（人员、资金、文件等）」，transplant指「移植（器官、植物等）」

definitions.example: The logistics company uses refrigerated trucks to transport fresh vegetables across provinces.
Public transport in this city has been greatly improved to reduce traffic congestion.

transport.etymology: 前缀trans-（表「转移、跨越」）+ 词根port（表「搬运」），原指跨越空间搬运物品或人员

similar_words.description: 同含前缀trans-，transport指「实体运输」，transmit指「传输（信号、信息、疾病等）」

definitions.example: Wildlife conservationists set harmless traps to capture and study rare forest animals.

definitions.meaning: 使陷入困境；设陷阱捕捉
definitions.example: Many low-income families are trapped in cycles of poverty due to limited educational resources.

trap.etymology: 源自古英语trap，原指捕捉动物的装置，后引申为「圈套、困境」

similar_words.description: 拼写均以t-r-a开头，trap指「陷阱/使陷入」，trash指「垃圾/丢弃」

definitions.example: Students are reminded to sort their trash into recyclable and non-recyclable categories.
Critics trashed the new policy for its lack of practical feasibility.

trash.etymology: 源自中古英语tras，原指碎屑、残渣，后引申为「无用的事物或人」

similar_words.description: 拼写均以t-r-a开头，trash指「垃圾/丢弃」，trap指「陷阱/使陷入」

definitions.example: The research assistant carried a tray of laboratory samples to the testing room.

tray.etymology: 源自古英语træg，原指木板，后引申为「盛放物品的平板状容器」

similar_words.description: 拼写相近，tray指「托盘」，trash指「垃圾」

definitions.example: The former intelligence officer was convicted of treason for leaking 'classified information' to foreign powers.

treason.etymology: 源自古法语traison，意为「背叛」，与考研词traitor（叛徒）同根

similar_words.description: 拼写均以t-r-e-a开头，treason指「叛国罪/背叛」，treasure指「珍宝/珍视」

definitions.example: Archaeologists discovered a hoard of ancient gold treasure buried under the medieval castle.
She treasures the handwritten letters from her grandmother as priceless memories.

treasure.etymology: 源自古法语tresor，意为「财富、珍宝」，后引申为动词「珍视」义

similar_words.description: 拼写均以t-r-e-a开头，treasure指「珍宝/珍视」，treason指「叛国罪/背叛」

definitions.example: Medical staff are trained to treat all patients with respect and professionalism.
A hot bath after a long day of exam preparation is a wonderful treat for me.

treat.etymology: 源自古法语traiter，意为「处理、对待」，后扩展出「治疗、款待」等义

similar_words.description: 拼写均以t-r-e-a开头，treat指「对待/治疗/款待」，treaty指「条约、协定」

definitions.example: The two countries signed a bilateral treaty to promote cultural and economic exchanges.

treaty.etymology: 源自古法语traité，意为「协议、约定」，与考研词treat（对待）同根

similar_words.description: 同根，treaty指「国际条约/协定」，treat指「对待/治疗/款待」

definitions.example: The young interviewee began to tremble when facing the panel of professors.
A faint tremble ran through her voice as she spoke about the tragic incident.

tremble.etymology: 源自古法语trembler，意为「颤抖」，与考研词tremor（震颤）同根

similar_words.description: 同根，tremble指「因恐惧、寒冷等的颤抖动作」，tremor指「（医学、地震等的）震颤现象」

definitions.example: The tremendous growth of digital media has reshaped people's information consumption habits.

tremendous.etymology: 由词根trem（颤抖）加形容词后缀-endous构成，原指「让人颤抖的」，引申为「巨大的」

similar_words.description: 同根trem（颤抖），tremble为动词表「颤抖」，tremendous为形容词表「巨大的」

definitions.example: Soldiers dug deep trenches to protect themselves from enemy fire during the First World War.

trench.etymology: 源自古法语trenchier（切割），原指「切割出的沟」，引申为「战壕、沟渠」

similar_words.description: 同根trench（切割），trench为名词表「战壕/沟渠」，trenchant为形容词表「（言辞）尖锐的」

definitions.meaning: n.趋势；倾向 v.趋向；流行
definitions.example: The global trend of aging population poses severe challenges to national social security systems.

trend.etymology: 源自古英语trendan（旋转、转向），引申为「事物发展的趋势」

similar_words.description: 形近且近义，trend侧重事物发展的**总体趋势**，tend侧重个体或事物的**倾向、照料**

definitions.example: The new cancer drug has entered the final stage of clinical trial before official approval.

trial.etymology: 源自古法语trier（尝试），引申为「试验、审判」

similar_words.description: 形近易混，trial为名词表「审判/试验」，trail为名词/动词表「踪迹/追踪」

definitions.example: The three major powers formed a strategic triangle to maintain regional military balance.

triangle.etymology: 由前缀tri-（三）加词根angle（角）构成的合成词，意为「三角形」，引申为「三角关系」

similar_words.description: 同含词根angle（角），均为几何图形，triangle为「三角形」，rectangle为「矩形」

definitions.example: The indigenous tribe in the Amazon rainforest has preserved its unique cultural traditions for millennia.

tribe.etymology: 源自拉丁语tribus（部落），指「具有共同文化、血缘的群体」

similar_words.description: 形近易混，tribe为名词表「部落」，tribute为名词表「贡品/致敬」

definitions.example: The city held a grand concert as a tribute to the legendary musician's 80th birthday.

tribute.etymology: 源自拉丁语tributum（贡品），原指「分配给统治者的物品」，引申为「对他人的致敬」

similar_words.description: 形近易混，tribute为名词表「贡品/致敬」，tribe为名词表「部落」

definitions.example: The marketing team used a clever trick to attract more young consumers to the brand.

trick.etymology: 源自古法语trikier（欺骗），引申为「诡计、技巧」

similar_words.description: 形近易混，trick为名词/动词表「诡计/欺骗」，trickle为动词/名词表「滴流/细流」

definitions.example: The experienced manager advised his team not to waste time on mere trifles.
One should never trifle with the trust and sincerity of close friends.

trifle.etymology: 源自古法语trufle（小事），由词根trif（琐碎）加小后缀-le构成，引申为动词义「轻视」

similar_words.description: 同根表「琐碎」，trifle为名词/动词表「琐事/轻视」，trivial为形容词表「琐碎的」

definitions.meaning: n.扳机；诱因 v.触发；引发
definitions.example: The unexpected bankruptcy of the major bank triggered a global financial crisis in 2008.

trigger.etymology: 源自荷兰语trekker（拉的东西），原指「枪的扳机」，引申为「诱因、触发」

similar_words.description: 形近易混，trigger为名词/动词表「扳机/触发」，tricker为名词表「骗子」

definitions.meaning: 修剪（植物、毛发等）；削减（开支、预算等）
definitions.example: The company’s board decided to trim the annual advertising budget by 15% to improve profitability.
The management report praised the branch for maintaining a trim and efficient operational structure.

trim.etymology: 来自古英语trymman（使整齐、加固），后衍生出「修剪至整齐状态」及「削减冗余部分」的核心动词义，形容词义为「整洁的」是熟词僻义。

similar_words.description: 拼写相近，均为t-r开头的单音节词，trim可作动词/形容词，tram仅作名词指有轨电车。

definitions.meaning: adj. 三倍的；三重的；v. （使）增至三倍
definitions.example: The revenue of the biotech firm tripled in three years owing to its breakthrough in gene therapy.

triple.etymology: 来自拉丁语triplus（三倍的），由前缀tri-（表示「三」）+ 词根-ple（表示「倍、层」）构成，是表示数量倍数的常用词。

similar_words.description: 均含「三倍的」含义，triple为通用用法，可作形容词、动词或名词；treble多用于音乐领域（指高音部）或英式英语中指金额的三倍。

definitions.meaning: n. 胜利；巨大成功；v. 获胜；战胜
definitions.example: Despite repeated failures, the research team finally triumphed in developing a low-cost diagnostic tool.

triumph.etymology: 来自拉丁语triumphus（古罗马将军的凯旋式），后泛指「重大胜利或成功」，是表示积极成果的常用词。

similar_words.description: 同根派生词，triumph作名词/动词指胜利、获胜；triumphal为形容词，意为「胜利的、凯旋的」。

definitions.meaning: 琐碎的；不重要的；无足轻重的
definitions.example: In academic writing, scholars should avoid devoting excessive space to trivial details irrelevant to the core argument.

trivial.etymology: 来自拉丁语trivialis（三岔路口的，引申为「随处可见的、无足轻重的」），由前缀tri-（三）+ 词根via（路）+ 形容词后缀-al构成，是考研阅读表否定评价的高频词。

similar_words.description: 均含「琐碎」含义，trivial为形容词，指事物无足轻重；trifle作名词指小事，作动词指轻视。

definitions.example: Shoppers pushed fully loaded trolleys through the crowded aisles of the downtown supermarket.

trolley.etymology: 来自古法语trollier（带滚轮的运输工具），后特指「购物手推车」或「有轨电车」，是生活类阅读的常用物品词。

similar_words.description: 拼写相近，trolley作名词指手推车、有轨电车；troll作名词指巨魔，作动词指拖曳、恶意挑衅。

definitions.meaning: n. 军队；部队；一群（人/动物）；v. 成群结队地走
definitions.example: A troop of conservationists headed into the Amazon rainforest to monitor the habitat of jaguars.

troop.etymology: 来自古法语troupe（群体），原指一群人，后引申为「军队、部队」及「成群行进」的动作义，是社会/环境类阅读的常用词。

similar_words.description: 拼写仅差一个字母（troop以o结尾，troupe以e结尾），troop指军队、群体或成群行进；troupe指表演类专业团体（剧团、乐团）。

definitions.example: The tropic of Capricorn is the southernmost latitude where the sun can be directly overhead.

tropic.etymology: 来自希腊语tropikos（太阳转向的），由词根tropos（转向）构成，原指太阳直射点转向的纬线（回归线），后泛指「热带地区」，是环境类阅读的地理术语。

similar_words.description: 同根派生词，tropic作名词指回归线、热带地区；tropical为形容词，指热带的、炎热的。

definitions.meaning: 热带的；（气候）炎热潮湿的
definitions.example: The tropical rainforest ecosystem plays a crucial role in regulating the global carbon cycle.

tropical.etymology: 由名词tropic（热带地区、回归线）加形容词后缀-al（表示「与…相关的」）构成，是环境类阅读的高频形容词。

similar_words.description: 均含「炎热的」含义，tropical特指热带地区的气候；torrid指极度炎热的（可用于非热带地区）。

definitions.meaning: 令人烦恼的；麻烦的；棘手的
definitions.example: The troublesome technical glitches delayed the launch of the space probe by two months.

troublesome.etymology: 由名词trouble（麻烦）加形容词后缀-some（表示「具有…性质的」）构成，是考研阅读表负面评价的高频形容词。

similar_words.description: 同根派生词，troublesome指事物令人烦恼、棘手；troubled指人感到困扰的、处境艰难的。

definitions.example: The award-winning theatre troupe will tour six European cities with their latest production this year.

troupe.etymology: 与troop同根，来自古法语troupe（群体），特指「表演类的专业团体（剧团、乐团等）」，是文化类阅读的常用词。

similar_words.description: 拼写仅差一个字母，troupe指表演类专业团体（剧团、乐团）；troop指军队、普通群体或成群行进。

definitions.example: Many interviewees are required to wear formal trousers when applying for management positions in multinational corporations.

trousers.etymology: 源自古法语trouse，原指紧身裤，现代英语中通常以复数形式使用，指覆盖双腿的正式长裤

similar_words.description: 「trousers」为英式英语正式场合常用的长裤，「pants」为美式英语日常裤装或内裤的统称

definitions.example: The relief organization deployed dozens of trucks to transport medical supplies to the flood-stricken region.

truck.etymology: 原指轮轴，后引申为用于载货的轮式车辆，美式英语常用

similar_words.description: 「truck」为美式英语通用货运车辆，「lorry」为英式英语特指的大型货运车辆

definitions.example: The orchestra's trumpet player performed a solemn melody at the national day ceremony.
The tech company trumpeted its new energy-efficient technology in a global press conference.

trumpet.etymology: 由trump（喇叭声）加指小后缀-et构成，原指小型铜管乐器

similar_words.description: 「trumpet」特指铜管类的小号/喇叭，「horn」可指各类喇叭、号角或动物的角

definitions.example: The ancient banyan tree has a thick trunk that requires five adults to encircle.
The telecommunications company laid new trunk lines to improve internet connectivity in rural areas.

trunk.etymology: 源自古英语trunc，原指被砍削的树干，后引申为各类粗壮物体或干线

similar_words.description: 「trunk」指大型硬质行李箱，「suitcase」为通用便携行李箱

definitions.example: A successful research team is built on members trusting each other's professional abilities.
The billionaire established a charitable trust to fund rural education projects.

trust.etymology: 源自古诺斯语traust，原指坚实的信任，后引申为信托相关商务义

similar_words.description: 「trust」强调基于长期了解的信任关系，「believe」侧重对某事真实性的主观相信

definitions.example: The academic community values the pursuit of truth above personal interests.

truth.etymology: 由形容词true（真实的）加名词后缀-th构成，表真实的状态或性质

similar_words.description: 「truth」强调与谎言、谬误相对的真相或真理，「fact」指客观存在的具体事实

definitions.example: After a long day of field research, the scientist soaked in a tub of warm water to relieve fatigue.
Miners loaded iron ore into the tub for transportation to the processing plant.

tub.etymology: 源自古英语tubba，原指木制容器，后引申为各类宽口的盆桶类容器

similar_words.description: 「tub」指宽口、无提手的盆或桶，「bucket」指带提手的桶

definitions.example: The biologist transferred the cell sample into a sterile glass tube for microscopic observation.
The old cathode-ray tube television has been widely replaced by LED screens.

tube.etymology: 源自拉丁语tubus，原指管子，后引申为各类管状物体

similar_words.description: 「tube」多指细的硬质管状物体，「pipe」多指粗的管道或烟斗

definitions.example: The mother tucked the child's quilt around the mattress to keep him warm during the cold night.
The tailor added a small tuck to the dress to adjust its length.

tuck.etymology: 源自古英语tucan，原指拉、拽，后引申为将某物塞进或掖入的动作

similar_words.description: 「tuck」指将物体塞进或掖入的动作，「tug」指用力拉、拽的动作

definitions.example: The rescue worker tugged at the trapped hiker's arm to pull him out of the crevice.
A powerful tug guided the massive oil tanker into the narrow harbor channel.

tug.etymology: 源自古英语tuggan，原指用力拉，后引申为拖船等牵引工具

similar_words.description: 「tug」强调用力、短促的拉拽动作，「pull」为通用的拉动作

definitions.example: Many undergraduate students apply for scholarships to reduce their tuition burden.

tuition.etymology: 来自拉丁语「tuitus」（教导、照料），由表示「教导」的词根「tuit-」加名词后缀「-ition」构成，本义为「教学活动」，后引申为考研常考的「学费」义。

similar_words.description: 二者同根（表「教导」），tuition指学费或教学活动，tutor指导师或辅导教师。

definitions.example: Global oil prices tumbled by nearly 30% amid the outbreak of the energy crisis.

tumble.etymology: 来自古英语「tumbian」（翻滚），词根「tumb-」表「转动、翻滚」，引申为「暴跌」等考研常考的经济、动作义。

similar_words.description: 二者形近，tumble指翻滚或价格暴跌，tremble指因恐惧、寒冷等颤抖。

definitions.example: The research team is developing a new therapy to target malignant tumour cells.

tumour.etymology: 来自拉丁语「tumor」（肿胀），由表示「肿胀」的词根「tum-」加英式名词后缀「-our」构成，为医学科普类考研阅读常见词汇。

similar_words.description: 二者形近，tumour指医学上的肿瘤，humour指幽默或生理体液。

definitions.meaning: v. 调整，调节；n. 曲调，旋律
definitions.example: The government needs to tune its fiscal policy to stimulate domestic consumption.

tune.etymology: 来自古英语「tynnian」（调整音调），原指音乐领域的「调弦、定调」，后引申为考研常考的「调节政策、参数」等义。

similar_words.description: 二者形近，tune作动词表调节，tone指语气、色调或音调的强弱。

definitions.example: The construction of the high-speed railway tunnel was completed two months ahead of schedule.

tunnel.etymology: 来自中古法语「tonel」（管道），原指「桶状的中空通道」，为交通、工程类考研阅读常见词汇。

similar_words.description: 二者形近，tunnel指地下或水下的人工隧道，channel指频道、海峡或流通渠道。

definitions.example: Offshore wind turbines are widely used to generate renewable electricity in coastal areas.

turbine.etymology: 来自拉丁语「turbare」（搅动、旋转），由表示「搅动」的词根「turb-」加表「器械」的名词后缀「-ine」构成，为科普类考研低频专业词汇。

similar_words.description: 二者同根（表「搅动」），turbine指旋转的涡轮机，turbulent指局势或流体动荡的。

definitions.meaning: 动荡的，混乱的；（流体）湍流的
definitions.example: The region has witnessed a turbulent decade of political transitions and economic fluctuations.

turbulent.etymology: 来自拉丁语「turbulentus」（混乱的），由表示「搅动」的词根「turb-」加表「充满…的」的形容词后缀「-ulent」构成，为社会、经济类考研阅读常见词汇。

similar_words.description: 二者同根（表「搅动」），turbulent形容局势或流体混乱，turbine指旋转的涡轮机。

definitions.example: Roast turkey is an indispensable dish for Thanksgiving celebrations in North America.
The company's high-budget film turned out to be a turkey, grossing only $1 million worldwide.

turkey.etymology: 源自土耳其国名「Turkey」，因欧洲人最初误以为火鸡产自土耳其而得名，考研中常考其熟词僻义「失败之作」。

similar_words.description: 二者形近，turkey指火鸡或失败之作，turk指土耳其人。

definitions.meaning: 营业额，成交量；人员流动率
definitions.example: The retail giant's annual turnover exceeded $100 billion for the first time last year.

turnover.etymology: 由动词短语「turn over」（周转、翻转）复合而成，原指「货物周转量」，后引申为考研常考的「营业额」「人员流动率」等商务义。

similar_words.description: 二者形近，turnover指营业额或人员流动率，turnout指集会出席人数或产量。

definitions.meaning: n. 导师，家庭教师；v. 辅导，指导
definitions.example: Each postgraduate student is required to meet with their academic tutor at least once a month.

tutor.etymology: 来自拉丁语「tutor」（监护人、教师），由表示「教导」的词根「tut-」加表「人」的名词后缀「-or」构成，为教育类考研阅读常见词汇。

similar_words.description: 二者同根（表「教导」），tutor指导师或辅导教师，tuition指学费或教学活动。

definitions.example: Many elderly people still obtain daily information mainly from tv rather than short-video platforms.

tv.etymology: 为「television」的通用缩写形式，无传统词根词缀

similar_words.description: 前者是后者的口语及书面常用缩写，后者为全称，更正式

definitions.example: Psychologists often use twin studies to explore the interaction between heredity and environment.
The twin towers of the office building have become a landmark of the city.

twin.etymology: 源自古英语twinn，词根tw-表「二、成双」，原指两个出生时间极近的孩子

similar_words.description: 前者指双胞胎或成双的事物，后者指扭曲、转动或情节转折

definitions.example: On a cloudless night, thousands of stars twinkle brightly across the celestial sphere.

twinkle.etymology: 源自古英语twincan，词根tw-表「快速交替」，指光线快速明灭交替

similar_words.description: 前者指（光、眼睛）闪烁，后者指（金属、铃铛）发出叮当声

definitions.example: Some tabloids intentionally twist public figures’ words to gain sensational effects.
The historical study revealed an unexpected twist in the emperor’s abdication process.

twist.etymology: 源自古英语twist，词根tw-表「二、缠绕」，原指两股线缠绕在一起，后引申为扭曲、转折

similar_words.description: 前者指扭曲、转折，后者指双胞胎或成双的事物

definitions.example: This type of low-carbon technology has been widely applied in European manufacturing.
Graduate students often need to type their dissertations in strict academic formats.

type.etymology: 源自希腊语typos（模型、印记），后引申为「类型」，19世纪后衍生出「打字」义

similar_words.description: 前者指类型或打字动作，后者指打字、印刷中的小错误

definitions.example: Before the popularization of personal computers, typewriters were indispensable office tools.

typewriter.etymology: 由type（打字）+ writer（书写工具）构成的合成名词，指用于打字的工具

similar_words.description: 前者指打字工具（打字机），后者指从事打字工作的人（打字员）

definitions.example: The coastal province initiated emergency response plans as the super typhoon approached.

typhoon.etymology: 源自粤语「大风」的音译，后经英语规范为typhoon，特指西太平洋的热带气旋

similar_words.description: 前者指西太平洋的热带气旋（台风），后者指大西洋或东太平洋的热带气旋（飓风）

definitions.example: It is typical of traditional Chinese villages to have an ancestral hall at the center.

typical.etymology: 由type（类型）+ 形容词后缀-ical构成，表「属于某一典型类型的」

similar_words.description: 前者侧重指某类事物的代表性样本，后者侧重指流传久远、具有典范性的事物

definitions.example: The law firm recruited a proficient typist to transcribe large volumes of legal documents.

typist.etymology: 由type（打字）+ 表人的后缀-ist构成，指以打字为职业的人

similar_words.description: 前者指从事打字工作的人（打字员），后者指打字工具（打字机）

definitions.example: The dilapidated factory’s ugly facade was renovated into a creative industrial park.

definitions.meaning: （局势、行为）令人不快的；险恶的
definitions.example: The border dispute escalated into an ugly standoff between the two countries.

ugly.etymology: 源自古英语uglic（可怕的、令人厌恶的），后引申为「外观丑陋的」，进而衍生出「局势险恶的」僻义

similar_words.description: 前者语义较轻，指外观难看或局势不佳，后者语义较重，指极其丑陋或恐怖的

definitions.example: The ultimate goal of this interdisciplinary research is to decode the molecular mechanism of aging.

ultimate.etymology: 由前缀「ultra-」（表「极端、最终」）加形容词后缀「-ate」构成

similar_words.description: 同含前缀「ultra-」，「ultimate」表「最终的、根本的」，「ultraviolet」表「紫外线的」

definitions.example: Long-term exposure to ultraviolet rays can accelerate skin aging and trigger certain skin diseases.

ultraviolet.etymology: 由前缀「ultra-」（表「超出」）加名词「violet」（紫色）构成，指超出紫色光谱的光线

similar_words.description: 同含前缀「ultra-」，「ultraviolet」表「紫外线的」，「ultimate」表「最终的、根本的」

definitions.example: The academic committee made a unanimous decision to award the annual research grant to the young scholar.

unanimous.etymology: 由前缀「un-」（表「一」）加词根「anim」（表「心灵、精神」）加形容词后缀「-ous」构成，指众人心灵一致的

similar_words.description: 同根词，「unanimous」为形容词表「全体一致的」，「unanimity」为名词表「一致同意」

definitions.example: The investigative team uncovered crucial evidence that overturned the previous conclusion of the case study.

uncover.etymology: 由前缀「un-」（表「移除、否定」）加动词「cover」（覆盖）构成，指移除覆盖物以揭露真相

similar_words.description: 近义，「uncover」侧重「揭露隐藏的秘密/事实」，「discover」侧重「发现未知的事物」

definitions.example: Many policymakers underestimate the long-term impact of digital divide on regional economic development.

underestimate.etymology: 由前缀「under-」（表「不足、低于」）加动词「estimate」（估计）构成，指估计不足

similar_words.description: 反义形近词，「underestimate」表「低估」，「overestimate」表「高估」

definitions.example: The traditional manufacturing industry has undergone profound transformation driven by technological innovation.

undergo.etymology: 由前缀「under-」（表「在…之下」）加动词「go」（走）构成，指在某种状态下走过，引申为经历

similar_words.description: 同含前缀「under-」，「undergo」表「经历」，「undertake」表「承担、从事」

definitions.meaning: 本科生（大学在读未获学位者）
definitions.example: The university provides free academic counseling services for all undergraduate students during the semester.

undergraduate.etymology: 由前缀「under-」（表「未达到」）加名词「graduate」（毕业生）构成，指尚未毕业的大学生

similar_words.description: 同根近义，「undergraduate」指「本科生」，「postgraduate」指「研究生」

definitions.meaning: 构成…的基础；是…的根本原因
definitions.example: A deep understanding of cultural context underlies successful cross-cultural communication in international business.

underlie.etymology: 由前缀「under-」（表「在…之下」）加动词「lie」（躺）构成，指躺在下方，引申为构成基础

similar_words.description: 同含前缀「under-」，「underlie」表「构成基础」，「underline」表「强调、在…下划线」

definitions.example: The latest research report underlines the urgent need to strengthen biodiversity conservation in tropical rainforests.

underline.etymology: 由前缀「under-」（表「在…之下」）加名词「line」（线）构成，指在下方划线，引申为强调

similar_words.description: 近义，「underline」侧重「通过实例或数据突出强调」，「emphasize」为通用「强调」

definitions.example: The underlying cause of the frequent extreme weather events is the continuous rise in global average temperature.

underlying.etymology: 由动词「underlie」（构成基础）的现在分词转化而来，指处于基础位置的

similar_words.description: 同根词，「underlie」为动词表「构成基础」，「underlying」为形容词表「根本的、潜在的」

definitions.example: Constant criticism from the supervisor can undermine a young researcher's confidence in conducting independent studies.

undermine.etymology: 由前缀under（在…下）+ 词根mine（挖；采矿）构成，原指在地下挖洞破坏地基，引申为暗中破坏事物的基础

similar_words.description: 「undermine」意为暗中破坏、削弱，「undertake」意为承担、从事，二者拼写相近但语义完全不同

definitions.example: Archaeologists found ancient pottery fragments underneath a thick layer of volcanic ash in the excavation site.

underneath.etymology: 由前缀under（在…下）+ 古英语词根neath（下方）构成的复合方位词，强调正下方或内部深处

similar_words.description: 「underneath」强调在某物正下方或内部深处，「under」仅表一般意义的下方

definitions.meaning: n. 理解，理解力；adj. 善解人意的
definitions.example: A deep understanding of cultural contexts is essential for translating academic papers accurately.

understanding.etymology: 由动词understand（理解）加后缀-ing构成的派生词，兼具名词和形容词属性

similar_words.description: 「understanding」侧重主观的理解、认知，「comprehension」侧重对文本等的客观领会（如考研阅读理解题型）

definitions.example: The research team will undertake a three-year study on the impact of climate change on coastal ecosystems.

undertake.etymology: 由前缀under（在…下）+ 动词take（拿）构成，原指接手责任，引申为承担任务、从事活动

similar_words.description: 「undertake」意为承担、从事，「undermine」意为暗中破坏，二者拼写相近但语义完全不同

definitions.example: Excessive use of social media may lead to undesirable effects on teenagers' mental health.

undesirable.etymology: 由否定前缀un- + 形容词desirable（合意的）构成，表「不合意的」

similar_words.description: 「undesirable」侧重因不合意而被排斥，「unfavorable」侧重对某事不利的

definitions.example: The new administration vowed to undo the previous government's controversial environmental regulations.

undo.etymology: 由否定前缀un- + 动词do（做）构成，表「撤销已完成的行为」

similar_words.description: 「undo」可表抽象的取消或具体的解开，「untie」仅表具体的解开（绳结等）

definitions.example: Undoubtedly, the development of artificial intelligence will reshape the global employment landscape in the next decade.

undoubtedly.etymology: 由否定前缀un- + 过去分词doubted（被怀疑的）+ 副词后缀-ly构成，表「不被怀疑地」

similar_words.description: 「undoubtedly」语气更正式，常用于学术或正式文本，「doubtless」语气稍随意

definitions.meaning: 不安的，担心的；不稳定的
definitions.example: The public felt uneasy about the government's vague response to the public health crisis.

uneasy.etymology: 由否定前缀un- + 形容词easy（安心的）构成，表「不安心的」

similar_words.description: 「uneasy」侧重因不确定而产生的不安，「anxious」侧重因担忧未来而产生的焦虑

definitions.example: The government launched a series of training programs to reduce the unemployment rate among young graduates.

unemployment.etymology: 由否定前缀un- + 名词employment（就业）构成，表「无就业状态」

similar_words.description: 「unemployment」指完全失业，「underemployment」指有工作但未充分发挥能力或工时不足

definitions.example: The unexpected discovery of ancient fossils provided new insights into the evolution of early mammals.

unexpected.etymology: 由否定前缀un- + 过去分词expected（预期的）构成，表「超出预期的」

similar_words.description: 「unexpected」侧重超出预期的，「unforeseen」侧重未被事先预见的（语气更正式）

definitions.example: As the plot unfolds, the author reveals the hidden conflict between social classes and personal ideals.

unfold.etymology: un-（表相反动作）+ fold（折叠），反折叠即展开

similar_words.description: 形近易混，「uphold」意为支持、维持，「unfold」意为展开、呈现

definitions.example: Unfortunately, the existing data cannot fully support the researcher’s hypothesis about climate change.

unfortunately.etymology: un-（否定）+ fortunate（幸运的）+ -ly（副词后缀），表否定的幸运状态

similar_words.description: 同根反义，「fortunately」意为幸运地，「unfortunately」意为不幸地

definitions.example: The university has adopted a uniform grading system to ensure fairness in academic assessment.
Students in this military academy are required to wear uniforms on formal occasions.

uniform.etymology: uni-（单一）+ form（形式），单一形式即统一的；引申为统一着装的制服

similar_words.description: 同根易混，「unify」为动词，意为统一、使成一体，「uniform」为形容词/名词，意为统一的/制服

definitions.example: The new policy aims to unify the fragmented regulations of different regions into a single coherent system.

unify.etymology: uni-（单一）+ -fy（动词后缀，表使…），使成为单一整体

similar_words.description: 近义易混，「unite」侧重联合、团结（保留个体独立性），「unify」侧重统一、使成一体（消除个体差异）

definitions.example: The trade union negotiated with the management to improve workers’ working conditions and welfare benefits.

union.etymology: uni-（单一）+ -on（名词后缀），单一的团体即工会或联盟

similar_words.description: 同根易混，「unity」指抽象的团结或一致性，「union」指具体的工会或联盟组织

definitions.example: Each species has its unique genetic characteristics that distinguish it from other organisms.

unique.etymology: uni-（单一）+ -que（形容词后缀），表仅有一个的

similar_words.description: 近义易混，「unusual」侧重不寻常的，「unique」侧重独一无二的，语义更强且表唯一性

definitions.example: The meter is the basic unit of length in the International System of Units (SI).

unit.etymology: uni-（单一）+ -t（名词后缀），单一的个体或计量标准即单位

similar_words.description: 同根易混，「union」侧重联合的组织，「unit」侧重单个的单位或单元

definitions.example: The environmental crisis has united people from all walks of life to take action to protect the planet.

unite.etymology: uni-（单一）+ -te（动词后缀），使成为单一的群体即联合

similar_words.description: 近义易混，「unify」侧重使成一体（消除差异），「unite」侧重联合、团结（保留个体独立性）

definitions.example: The unity of the research team is crucial for the successful completion of this long-term project.

unity.etymology: uni-（单一）+ -ty（名词后缀，表性质），表单一、一致的性质即团结

similar_words.description: 同根易混，「union」指具体的工会或联盟组织，「unity」指抽象的团结或一致性

definitions.example: The right to education is recognized as a universal human right by the United Nations.

universal.etymology: uni-（单一）+ vers（转）+ -al（形容词后缀），转向所有范围即普遍的

similar_words.description: 近义易混，「general」侧重普遍的、一般的，「universal」侧重全体的、无例外的，语义覆盖范围更广

definitions.example: Astronomers have long explored the origins of the universe through observational data supporting the 'Big Bang' theory.

universe.etymology: 由前缀「uni-（一、单一）」+词根「vers（转）」+后缀「-e」构成，原指'旋转的整体'，后引申为'宇宙'

similar_words.description: 「universe」指宇宙、天地万物，「university」指综合性大学，二者词形相近易混淆拼写

definitions.example: Many top universities now require applicants to submit evidence of extracurricular engagement alongside academic transcripts.

university.etymology: 由前缀「uni-（一、单一）」+词根「vers（转）」+后缀「-ity（表名词）」构成，原指'学者组成的统一团体'，后引申为'（综合性）大学'

similar_words.description: 「university」指综合性大学，「universe」指宇宙、天地万物，二者词形相近易混淆拼写

definitions.example: The research paper will not be accepted for publication unless it undergoes rigorous peer review.

unless.etymology: 由前缀「un-（不）」+单词「less（更少）」构成，原指'除...之外都不'，后引申为'除非、如果不'

similar_words.description: 「unless」引导条件状语从句，表'除非、如果不'；「until」引导时间状语从句，表'直到'，二者均以un-开头易混淆用法

definitions.example: Unlike traditional fossil fuels, renewable energy sources produce little to no greenhouse gas emissions.

unlike.etymology: 由前缀「un-（不）」+单词「like（像）」构成，表'不像、与...不同'

similar_words.description: 「unlike」为介词/形容词，表'不像、与...不同'；「dislike」为动词/名词，表'不喜欢'，二者词形相近易混淆词性与语义

definitions.example: It is unlikely that the central bank will cut interest rates in the near future amid persistent inflation pressures.

unlikely.etymology: 由前缀「un-（不）」+单词「likely（可能的）」构成，表'不大可能的、未必的'

similar_words.description: 「unlikely」为形容词，表'不大可能的'；「unlike」为介词/形容词，表'不像的'，二者词形相近易混淆语义

definitions.example: Port authorities have deployed additional workers to unload cargo ships carrying emergency supplies for the disaster-stricken area.

unload.etymology: 由前缀「un-（去除、相反动作）」+单词「load（装载）」构成，表'卸货、卸载'

similar_words.description: 「unload」指卸货、卸载（物品/数据）；「upload」指上传（数据），二者均以-load结尾，前缀不同语义相反易混淆

definitions.example: The unusual frost in late spring has caused severe damage to fruit crops across the northern provinces.

unusual.etymology: 由前缀「un-（不）」+单词「usual（寻常的）」构成，表'不寻常的、罕见的'

similar_words.description: 「unusual」侧重异于常规的；「uncommon」侧重罕见、不常见的，二者语义接近易混淆侧重

definitions.meaning: 更新，使现代化；最新消息，更新内容
definitions.example: The World Health Organization regularly updates its guidelines to reflect the latest scientific findings on infectious diseases.

update.etymology: 由前缀「up-（向上、加强）」+单词「date（日期）」构成，原指'更新日期'，后引申为'更新信息、使现代化'

similar_words.description: 「update」侧重更新信息、数据或内容；「upgrade」侧重提升等级、品质或设备性能，二者均以up-开头易混淆语义侧重

definitions.meaning: 升级，提升（级别、质量）；升级，晋升
definitions.example: The government plans to upgrade the country's railway infrastructure to improve transportation efficiency and reduce carbon emissions.

upgrade.etymology: 由前缀「up-（向上、提升）」+单词「grade（等级）」构成，表'升级、提升（级别、质量）'

similar_words.description: 「upgrade」侧重提升等级、品质或设备性能；「update」侧重更新信息、数据或内容，二者均以up-开头易混淆语义侧重

definitions.example: The Supreme Court ruled to uphold the constitutional right to free speech in the landmark civil liberties case.

uphold.etymology: 由前缀「up-（向上、保持）」+单词「hold（持有、支撑）」构成，原指'保持向上'，后引申为'支持、维护（原则、法律等）'

similar_words.description: 「uphold」指支持、维护（原则、法律等）；「withhold」指扣留、拒绝给予，二者均以-hold结尾，前缀不同语义相反易混淆

definitions.meaning: 在…之上（正式书面体，常替代on）；一…就（表时间）
definitions.example: Upon arriving at the research center, the scholars immediately started their cross-disciplinary discussion.

upon.etymology: 由古英语「up」（向上）与「on」（在…上）合成，是on的正式书面变体，考研阅读中常用于正式语境替代on。

similar_words.description: on是口语及普通书面体，upon是正式书面体，考研阅读中upon常替换on表同一含义

definitions.meaning: 上面的；上层的（指层级、位置的上部）
definitions.example: The upper stratum of the ancient tomb was found to contain well-preserved ceramic artifacts by the archaeologists.

upper.etymology: 由古英语「up」（向上）加比较级后缀「-er」构成，原指位置较高的，引申为层级、地位较高的。

similar_words.description: upper侧重层级、位置的上部（如upper floor），top侧重物体的顶端（如top of the mountain）

definitions.meaning: 正直的（指品行）；垂直的（指物体）
definitions.example: An upright academic will never plagiarize others' research results to gain academic honors.

upright.etymology: 由「up」（向上）与「right」（直的）合成，原指垂直向上的，引申为人品行端正、正直。

similar_words.description: upright侧重品行符合道德规范、正直合规，honest侧重言行诚实不欺

definitions.meaning: 喧嚣；骚动（指群体的嘈杂或抗议）
definitions.example: The proposed tuition increase sparked an uproar among undergraduate students across the country.

uproar.etymology: 由「up」（向上）与「roar」（吼叫）合成，指群体向上的高声吼叫，引申为喧嚣、骚动。

similar_words.description: uproar是名词表喧嚣、骚动，uproarious是形容词表喧嚣的、骚动的

definitions.meaning: 使心烦意乱；打乱（计划、安排等）
definitions.example: The sudden cancellation of the academic conference upset many researchers who had traveled long distances to attend.
She felt upset when she learned that her paper had been rejected by the core journal.

upset.etymology: 由「up」（向上）与「set」（放置）合成，原指打翻、推翻物体，引申为打乱计划、使情绪失衡。

similar_words.description: upset侧重情绪上的心烦或计划被打乱，disturb侧重打扰、妨碍他人的正常状态

definitions.meaning: 在楼上；向楼上（副）；楼上的（形）
definitions.example: The professor went upstairs to retrieve the manuscript he had left in his study.

upstairs.etymology: 由「up」（向上）与「stairs」（楼梯）合成，表朝向或位于楼梯上方的位置。

similar_words.description: upstairs表楼上方向或位置，downstairs表楼下方向或位置，考研阅读中常成对考查空间位置

definitions.meaning: 向上的（形）；向上（副）（常表趋势）
definitions.example: The upward growth of the digital economy has become a key driving force for global economic recovery.

upward.etymology: 由「up」（向上）加表方向的后缀「-ward」构成，表朝向或处于向上的状态/趋势。

similar_words.description: upward可作形容词和副词，upwards仅作副词（英式英语常用，美式英语常用upward作副词）

definitions.example: Urban planning must balance the needs of economic development and ecological protection in modern metropolises.

urban.etymology: 来自拉丁语「urbanus」，词根「urb-」表「城市」，考研中常与派生词「urbanization（城市化）」关联考查。

similar_words.description: urban表城市的、都市的，rural表乡村的、农村的，考研阅读中常成对考查城乡对比话题

definitions.meaning: 催促；强烈要求（动）；强烈的欲望（名）
definitions.example: The World Health Organization urges all countries to strengthen their public health surveillance systems.

urge.etymology: 来自拉丁语「urgere」（推、压），引申为催促、敦促，名词义指内心强烈的推动性欲望。

similar_words.description: urge侧重带有强制性的强烈催促、敦促，persuade侧重通过说理说服他人

definitions.meaning: 紧急的；急迫的（需立即处理的）
definitions.example: The local government issued an urgent notice to evacuate residents in the flood-affected areas.

urgent.etymology: 来自拉丁语「urgentem」（urge的现在分词），词根「urg-」表「推、压」，指被推动着需立即处理的，即紧急的。

similar_words.description: urgent表需要立即处理的紧急（如urgent need），emergent表刚出现的、突发的紧急情况（如emergent disaster）

definitions.example: The usage of 「AI」 tools in academic research has sparked widespread debates among scholars.

usage.etymology: 来自动词「use」+名词后缀「-age」，表示使用的行为或具体方式

similar_words.description: usage侧重具体的「用法、惯用法」，use侧重抽象的「使用行为、用途」

definitions.example: Many consumers prefer used electronic devices to reduce electronic waste.
After three months of campus life, the freshmen have become used to the strict academic schedule.

used.etymology: 来自动词「use」+形容词后缀「-ed」，表示被使用过的或处于习惯状态的

similar_words.description: used指「用过的、习惯的」，useful指「有用的」，拼写接近易混淆

definitions.example: The official guide provides useful strategies for solving complex reading comprehension questions in the 「postgraduate entrance exam」.

useful.etymology: 来自动词「use」+形容词后缀「-ful」，表示具有使用价值的

similar_words.description: useful为「有用的」，useless为其同根反义词，表「无用的」

definitions.example: Contrary to usual practice, the professor allowed students to submit their dissertations via email this semester.

usual.etymology: 来自拉丁语「usus（使用）」+形容词后缀「-al」，表示惯常使用的

similar_words.description: usual为「通常的」，unusual为其同根反义词，表「不寻常的」

definitions.example: Researchers should fully utilize existing database resources to conduct more efficient empirical studies.

utilize.etymology: 来自拉丁语「utilis（有用的）」+动词后缀「-ize」，表示使变得有用进而利用

similar_words.description: utilize为正式书面语，侧重充分利用现有资源；use为通用词，使用范围更广

definitions.example: It is of utmost importance that candidates strictly follow the rules of the 「postgraduate entrance examination」.
The research team did their utmost to complete the project before the submission deadline.

utmost.etymology: 来自古英语「ut（出）」+最高级后缀「-most」，原指最外面的，引申为极度的、极限的

similar_words.description: utmost表「极度的、极限」，utter作adj表「完全的」、作v表「说」，拼写接近易混淆

definitions.meaning: 说，讲（尤指郑重或正式地）
definitions.example: The spokesperson refused to utter any comment on the controversial academic fraud case.
The sudden outbreak of the epidemic brought utter chaos to the local higher education system.

utter.etymology: 来自古英语「utian（说出）」，形容词义项由动词义「完全说出→毫无保留的」引申而来

similar_words.description: utter作adj表「完全的」、作v表「说」，utmost表「极度的、极限」，拼写接近易混淆

definitions.meaning: 空的，未被占用的（指职位、房间、座位等）
definitions.example: The university has announced three vacant positions for postdoctoral researchers in the field of 「artificial intelligence」.

vacant.etymology: 来自拉丁语「vacare（空）」+形容词后缀「-ant」，表示处于空的、未被占用的状态

similar_words.description: vacant指「未被占用的」，vacuum指「真空、空白」，拼写接近易混淆

definitions.example: Many postgraduate candidates choose to spend their summer vacation preparing for the exam in the university library.

vacation.etymology: 来自拉丁语「vacare（空）」+名词后缀「-ation」，表示空闲的时间，即假期

similar_words.description: vacation指「假期、休假」，vocation指「职业、使命」，拼写仅差一个字母，为考研经典易混词

definitions.example: The sudden resignation of the CEO created a power vacuum in the company's top management.

vacuum.etymology: 来自拉丁语「vacuus（空的）」+名词后缀「-um」，表示空的空间，引申为空白

similar_words.description: vacuum指「真空、空白」，vacant指「未被占用的」，拼写接近易混淆

definitions.example: The study’s findings remained vague due to insufficient sample size.

vague.etymology: 来自拉丁语vagari（漫游、徘徊），词根vag-表示「漫游、无定」，加形容词后缀-ue构成

similar_words.description: vague指抽象事物模糊、不明确；vain指努力徒劳或人自负、虚荣

definitions.meaning: 徒劳的，无效的（常与in连用构成in vain短语）
definitions.example: All attempts to rescue the trapped miners were in vain as the tunnel collapsed completely.
She was vain about her academic achievements and refused to accept criticism.

vain.etymology: 来自拉丁语vanus（空的），词根van-表示「空、无」，加形容词后缀-ain构成

similar_words.description: vain侧重努力无效或人虚荣；vague侧重事物模糊不清
vain多修饰人的努力或状态；futile侧重客观上不可能成功的尝试

definitions.meaning: 有效的，有根据的（多用于法律、学术语境）
definitions.example: The research proposal was rejected because its arguments were not logically valid.

valid.etymology: 来自拉丁语validus（强壮的、有效的），词根valid-表示「强壮、有效」，加形容词后缀-id构成

similar_words.description: valid指合法、有效的；invalid指无效的、病弱的

definitions.example: The ancient settlement was located in a fertile valley surrounded by towering mountains.

valley.etymology: 来自古法语valee，词根vall-表示「山谷」，加名词后缀-ey构成

similar_words.description: valley指地理上的山谷；valve指机械或生物的阀门、瓣膜

definitions.example: The archaeologist uncovered several valuable bronze artifacts dating back to the Han Dynasty.
She locked all her valuables in the hotel safe before going sightseeing.

valuable.etymology: 来自拉丁语valere（有价值），由名词value加形容词后缀-able（表「可…的、具有…性质的」）构成

similar_words.description: valuable侧重物品本身具有较高价值；valued侧重被人重视、珍视的

definitions.meaning: 价值，重要性；价值观（常用复数）
definitions.example: Educators emphasize the value of critical thinking in fostering students’ independent learning abilities.
Most employers value employees with strong communication skills.

value.etymology: 来自拉丁语valere（有力量、有价值），词根val-表示「价值、力量」

similar_words.description: value可作名词或动词，侧重抽象价值或主观重视；worth多作形容词或名词，侧重某物值得的客观价值或金额

definitions.meaning: 阀门，瓣膜（多用于工程、医学科普语境）
definitions.example: The heart’s mitral valve controls the flow of blood between the left atrium and left ventricle.

valve.etymology: 来自拉丁语valva（折叠的门），词根valv-表示「门、阀」

similar_words.description: valve指机械或生物的阀类结构；valley指地理上的山谷

definitions.example: The delivery van was loaded with medical supplies bound for the remote village.

definitions.meaning: 先锋，先驱（为vanguard的缩写，常与in the van of搭配）
definitions.example: The university has been in the van of artificial intelligence research for decades.

van.etymology: 来自拉丁语vanna（铲车），后引申为厢式货车；「先锋」义为vanguard的缩写形式

similar_words.description: van作「先锋」是vanguard的缩写，vanguard指先锋队伍或前沿领域

definitions.meaning: 消失，突然不见（侧重彻底、快速消失）
definitions.example: As the climate warms, many Arctic ice sheets are vanishing at an alarming rate.

vanish.etymology: 来自拉丁语vanescere（消失），词根van-表示「空」，加动词后缀-ish构成

similar_words.description: vanish侧重突然、彻底的消失（常带神秘或不可逆转性）；disappear侧重逐渐或暂时的消失

definitions.meaning: 虚荣心，自负；空虚，无价值
definitions.example: Her vanity prevented her from admitting her mistakes in the academic debate.

vanity.etymology: 来自拉丁语vanitas（空虚、虚荣），由词根van-（空）加名词后缀-ity（表性质、状态）构成

similar_words.description: vanity是vain的名词形式，vain为形容词，表徒劳的、虚荣的

definitions.example: Water vapour is a key component of the Earth's atmosphere and affects global climate regulation.

vapour.etymology: 来自拉丁语「vapor」，核心词根vapor-表‘蒸汽、水汽’，为英式拼写形式

similar_words.description: vapour为英式拼写，vapor为美式拼写，语义完全一致

definitions.example: The variable rainfall in arid regions poses a great challenge to local agricultural production.
In social science research, gender is often treated as an independent variable.

variable.etymology: 来自拉丁语「variabilis」，核心词根vari-表‘变化’，后缀-able表‘可…的’，兼具形容词与名词词性

similar_words.description: variable侧重可变性或学术变量，variant侧重同一事物的不同变体、变种

definitions.meaning: 变化，变动（尤指程度或数量）
definitions.example: The annual variation in solar radiation affects the productivity of terrestrial ecosystems.

variation.etymology: 来自拉丁语「variatio」，核心词根vari-表‘变化’，后缀-ation为名词后缀，表‘行为、状态或结果’

similar_words.description: variation强调变化的过程或程度，variant强调同一类事物的具体不同形式

definitions.example: A variety of interdisciplinary approaches have been adopted to study the impact of climate change on human society.

variety.etymology: 来自拉丁语「varietas」，核心词根vari-表‘变化’，后缀-ety为名词后缀，表‘性质、状态’

similar_words.description: variety为名词，常构成a variety of短语；various为形容词，直接修饰名词表‘各种各样的’

definitions.example: Various international organizations have launched initiatives to address the global food security crisis.

various.etymology: 来自拉丁语「varius」，核心词根vari-表‘变化’，后缀-ous为形容词后缀，表‘充满…的、具有…性质的’

similar_words.description: various为形容词，修饰名词；variety为名词，常构成a variety of短语

definitions.example: The effectiveness of this policy will vary depending on the specific economic conditions of different regions.

vary.etymology: 来自拉丁语「variare」，核心词根vari-表‘变化’，不及物动词时多与from/to搭配

similar_words.description: vary为动词表‘变化’，very为副词表‘非常’，拼写形近易混淆

definitions.example: The porcelain vase unearthed from the archaeological site dates back to the Southern Song Dynasty.

vase.etymology: 来自拉丁语「vas」，原义为‘容器’，后特指‘花瓶’

similar_words.description: vase指‘花瓶’，base指‘基础、底部’，拼写形近易混淆

definitions.example: The vast majority of scientific studies confirm that human activities are the main cause of global warming.

vast.etymology: 来自拉丁语「vastus」，原义为‘空旷的、荒芜的’，引申为‘广阔的、巨大的’

similar_words.description: vast指‘广阔的、巨大的’，fast指‘快速的’，拼写形近易混淆

definitions.example: The campus dining hall provides a range of vegetarian options to meet the dietary needs of students.
More consumers are choosing to be vegetarians to reduce their carbon footprint and improve health.

vegetarian.etymology: 来自拉丁语「vegetare」（表‘生长、使有生气’），核心词根veget-表‘植物’，后缀-arian表‘具有…特征的人或事物’

similar_words.description: vegetarian指‘素食者’或‘素食的’，vegetable指‘具体的蔬菜’

definitions.example: The destruction of native vegetation in the Amazon basin has led to a significant loss of biodiversity.

vegetation.etymology: 来自拉丁语「vegetatio」，核心词根veget-表‘植物’，后缀-ation为名词后缀，表‘集合、总称’

similar_words.description: vegetation指‘植物的总称（植被）’，vegetable指‘具体的蔬菜’

definitions.example: Electric vehicles are increasingly promoted by governments worldwide as a solution to urban air pollution.
Art serves as a vehicle for cross-cultural communication between different nations.

vehicle.etymology: 源自拉丁语vehiculum，由词根veh（意为「携带」）加名词后缀-iculum（表「工具」）构成，原指「携带的工具」，后引申为交通工具、传播媒介等义

similar_words.description: 「vessel」指船舶、血管或容器；「vehicle」指交通工具或传播媒介

definitions.example: The bride wore a delicate white veil during the royal wedding ceremony.
The company's annual report veiled its declining profitability with vague operational data.

veil.etymology: 源自拉丁语velum（意为「帆、幕」），后引申为面纱、遮蔽物及掩饰的动作

similar_words.description: 「veil」指面纱或掩饰；「vein」指静脉、叶脉或行文风格

definitions.example: The doctor used a tourniquet to locate a suitable vein for the patient's blood test.
The author writes in a humorous vein that makes academic essays more accessible to young readers.

vein.etymology: 源自拉丁语vena（意为「静脉」），后引申为叶脉、岩脉及行文风格等义

similar_words.description: 「vein」指静脉、叶脉或行文风格；「veil」指面纱或掩饰

definitions.example: The study measured the velocity of ocean currents to predict long-term climate change trends.

velocity.etymology: 源自拉丁语velocitas，由词根veloc（来自velox，意为「快的」）加名词后缀-ity（表「性质」）构成，为物理学术语

similar_words.description: 「velocity」是物理学术语，指带方向的速率；「speed」是通用词，泛指速度

definitions.meaning: 丝绒，天鹅绒；丝绒制的，柔软光滑的
definitions.example: The museum displayed a 17th-century royal coat adorned with velvet and gold thread embroidery.

velvet.etymology: 源自古法语velu（意为「多毛的」），因丝绒表面的绒毛状质感得名

similar_words.description: 「velvet」指丝绒或柔软的；「veil」指面纱或掩饰

definitions.example: We need to open all windows to ventilate the classroom after the chemical experiment.
The academic conference provided a platform to ventilate ethical issues in artificial intelligence research.

ventilate.etymology: 源自拉丁语ventilare，由词根vent（意为「风」）加动词后缀-ate（表「使动」）构成，原指「使通风」，后引申为「公开讨论」

similar_words.description: 「ventilate」指使通风或公开讨论；「venture」指冒险或冒险事业

definitions.example: The young researcher ventured to challenge the established theory in her doctoral thesis.
The joint venture between the two tech giants aims to develop 5G infrastructure in rural areas.

venture.etymology: 源自拉丁语venire（意为「来」），原指「冒险前来」，后引申为冒险行为、投机事业等义

similar_words.description: 「venture」指冒险或冒险事业；「ventilate」指使通风或公开讨论

definitions.example: In English academic writing, precise transitive verbs are preferred to vague expressions like 'do' or 'make'.

verb.etymology: 源自拉丁语verbum（意为「词、动词」），为语法学术语

similar_words.description: 「verb」为名词，指「动词」；「verbal」为形容词，指「口头的、动词的」

definitions.example: The witness gave a detailed verbal account of the accident to the police investigator.
The verbal form of the noun 'success' is the irregular verb 'succeed'.

verbal.etymology: 由词根verb（意为「动词」）加形容词后缀-al（表「与…相关的」）构成，引申为「口头的」「动词的」等义

similar_words.description: 「verbal」为形容词，指「口头的、动词的」；「verb」为名词，指「动词」

definitions.example: The Supreme Court's verdict on the environmental case will set a precedent for future lawsuits.
The public's verdict on the new education policy is mostly negative among parents.

verdict.etymology: 源自拉丁语verdictum，由词根ver（意为「真实的」）加词根dict（意为「说」）构成，原指「说出真实判断」，后引申为法律裁决及定论等义

similar_words.description: 「verdict」尤指陪审团的裁决；「judgment」泛指任何判断或判决

definitions.example: Many endangered species are on the verge of extinction due to habitat destruction.
His absurd remarks verge on insult to the academic community.

verge.etymology: 词根「verg-」（倾斜，源自拉丁语「vergere」），引申为事物倾斜即将突破的界限

similar_words.description: 「edge」指具体物体的边缘或抽象优势，「verge」侧重濒临极限的临界状态

definitions.example: The research team must verify the reliability of survey data before submitting the paper.
The lawyer asked the witness to verify the signature on the contract.

verify.etymology: 词根「ver-」（真实，源自拉丁语「verus」）+ 动词后缀「-ify」（使…），使内容真实→核实

similar_words.description: 「certify」侧重官方证明合格，「verify」侧重通过证据核实真实性

definitions.example: As a versatile designer, she is skilled in both graphic design and interior decoration.

versatile.etymology: 词根「vers-」（转动，源自拉丁语「vertere」）+ 形容词后缀「-atile」（能…的），能转向不同领域→多才多艺的

similar_words.description: 「adaptable」侧重适应环境的能力，「versatile」侧重自身具备多种技能或用途

definitions.example: The Nobel Prize winner’s latest poetry collection contains verses about urban loneliness.
The speaker quoted a verse from the Bible to illustrate his point.

verse.etymology: 源自拉丁语「versus」（转动），原指诗行的换行，引申为诗句

similar_words.description: 「poem」指完整的诗篇，「verse」指单句诗句或诗节

definitions.example: The simplified Chinese version of the classic novel was published last month.
The two suspects gave entirely different versions of the crime scene.

version.etymology: 词根「vers-」（转）+ 名词后缀「-ion」，转译后的文本→版本

similar_words.description: 「edition」侧重出版的版次，「version」侧重内容的变体或译本

definitions.example: The study compares the efficiency of renewable energy versus fossil fuels.
The case of Roe versus Wade is a landmark in American legal history.

versus.etymology: 源自拉丁语「versus」（朝向，反对），原指转向对方

similar_words.description: 「against」侧重反对，「versus」侧重对比或对阵关系

definitions.example: The vertical axis of the chart shows the annual growth rate of GDP.
The construction worker checked if the wall was perfectly on the vertical.

vertical.etymology: 源自拉丁语「vertex」（顶点，旋转中心），引申为垂直于地面的

similar_words.description: 「horizontal」指水平的，与「vertical」（垂直的）为反义关系

definitions.example: The cargo vessel was detained at the port due to customs inspection.
Long-term smoking can damage the inner walls of blood vessels.

vessel.etymology: 源自拉丁语「vas」（容器），引申为盛装物质的物体（船、血管等）

similar_words.description: 「ship」指大型远洋船，「vessel」可指各类船或容器、血管

definitions.example: He put on a down vest to keep warm in the freezing weather.
The national constitution vests the parliament with legislative power.

vest.etymology: 源自拉丁语「vestire」（穿衣），原指穿衣，引申为授予（如同穿上权力的外衣）

similar_words.description: 「invest」指投资或授予荣誉，「vest」侧重授予法定权力

definitions.example: The local government held a ceremony to honor military veterans on Memorial Day.
She is a veteran editor who has worked in the publishing industry for 30 years.

veteran.etymology: 源自拉丁语「veteranus」（年老的，有经验的），原指服役多年的士兵

similar_words.description: 「expert」指专业领域的专家，「veteran」侧重因长期从业而经验丰富的人

definitions.example: The president has the power to veto any bill passed by Congress.

veto.etymology: 源自拉丁语veto「我禁止」，引申为否决权或禁止行为

similar_words.description: 「veto」指否决、禁止，「vote」指投票、选举，形近易混

definitions.example: The package was shipped via air freight to reduce delivery time.

via.etymology: 源自拉丁语via「道路」，引申为经由某路径

similar_words.description: 「via」为介词表经由，「vial」为名词表小药瓶，形近易混

definitions.example: The strings of the violin vibrate to produce sound when played.

vibrate.etymology: 源自拉丁语vibrare「振动」，同根词为vibration（振动）

similar_words.description: 「vibrate」为动词表振动，「vibrant」为形容词表充满活力的，同根易混

definitions.example: Greed is often regarded as a destructive vice in moral essays.

vice.etymology: 源自拉丁语vitium「缺陷、恶习」，可引申为副职位前缀（如vice president）

similar_words.description: 「vice」指恶习或副职位，「vise」为名词表老虎钳，形近易混

definitions.example: Several witnesses reported seeing a suspicious vehicle in the vicinity of the crime scene.

vicinity.etymology: 源自拉丁语vicinus「邻居」，引申为邻近的区域

similar_words.description: 「vicinity」指附近区域，「velocity」指速度，形近易混

definitions.example: The newspaper launched a vicious attack on the politician's personal life.

vicious.etymology: 由名词vice（恶习）加形容词后缀-ous构成，表「有恶习的、恶毒的」

similar_words.description: 「vicious」指恶毒的，「viscous」指粘稠的，形近易混

definitions.example: Many elderly people become victims of financial fraud each year.

victim.etymology: 源自拉丁语victima「祭品、受害者」，引申为遭受伤害的人

similar_words.description: 「victim」指受害者，「victor」指胜利者，形近反义易混

definitions.example: The team celebrated their hard-won victory in the national championship.

victory.etymology: 源自拉丁语victoria「胜利」，词根为vincere「征服」

similar_words.description: 「victory」指胜利，「Victoria」为人名或地名，形近易混

definitions.example: The professor uploaded a video lecture for students who missed the class.
