video.etymology: 源自拉丁语videre「看」加名词后缀-o，表可观看的视听内容

similar_words.description: 「video」指视频，「audio」指音频，形近对应易混

definitions.example: From a historical view, the reform has far-reaching implications.
The report views climate change as a global crisis requiring immediate action.

view.etymology: 源自拉丁语videre「看」，引申为观察所得的观点或景色

similar_words.description: 「view」指普通观点或景色，「vista」指远景、展望，形近义近易混

definitions.meaning: （尤指受个人经历或信仰影响的）观点，看法
definitions.example: From a sociological viewpoint, peer influence plays a crucial role in adolescent behavior formation.

viewpoint.etymology: 合成词，由「view（观察）」和「point（立足点）」构成，指观察事物的特定角度

similar_words.description: 二者均表观点，viewpoint侧重个人或特定领域的观察视角，standpoint侧重基于立场的判断

definitions.example: The reform policy was implemented with great vigor to address the aging population issue.

vigor.etymology: 来自拉丁语vigor，词根「vig-」表“有活力的”，同根词有vigorous

similar_words.description: 二者均表活力，vigor侧重精神或行动的充沛力度，energy侧重可转化的能量或干劲

definitions.meaning: 充满活力的；强劲的，有力的
definitions.example: The government launched a vigorous anti-corruption campaign to improve governance efficiency.

vigorous.etymology: 名词vigor（活力）加形容词后缀「-ous」（表“具有…性质的”）构成

similar_words.description: 二者均表有活力的，vigorous侧重行动或措施的强劲有力，energetic侧重人的精力充沛

definitions.example: In the 19th-century food preservation research, vinegar was found effective in inhibiting bacterial growth.

vinegar.etymology: 来自古法语vinaigre，由「vin（酒）」和「aigre（酸的）」构成，原指“变酸的酒”

similar_words.description: 二者为形近词，vine指葡萄藤，vinegar指醋，注意拼写和词义的差异

definitions.meaning: 违反（规则、法律）；侵犯（隐私、权利）
definitions.example: Any enterprise that violates the labor law will be subject to administrative penalties.

violate.etymology: 来自拉丁语violare（伤害、侵犯），加动词后缀「-ate」构成

similar_words.description: 二者均表违反或侵犯，violate侧重公然违反正式规则，infringe侧重轻度侵犯或超出界限

definitions.meaning: 暴力行为；（自然力的）狂暴
definitions.example: The criminological study explores the root causes of youth violence in underprivileged communities.

violence.etymology: 来自拉丁语violentia，词根「viol-」（侵犯）加名词后缀「-ence」（表状态）构成

similar_words.description: 二者均表力量，violence侧重带伤害性的暴力行为，force侧重物理力量或强制力

definitions.meaning: 暴力的；（自然现象）剧烈的
definitions.example: The violent tornado last month caused severe damage to the rural infrastructure in the Midwest.

violent.etymology: 来自拉丁语violentus，词根「viol-」（侵犯）加形容词后缀「-ent」构成

similar_words.description: 二者均表剧烈的，violent侧重带破坏性的暴力或剧烈，fierce侧重凶猛的或激烈的竞争

definitions.example: The botanical survey documented 12 species of wild violet growing in the mountainous region.
The Renaissance painting features a noblewoman wearing a violet silk gown.

violet.etymology: 来自拉丁语viola（紫罗兰），引申指紫罗兰色

similar_words.description: 二者为形近词，violet指紫罗兰或紫罗兰色，violent指暴力的，注意发音和词义差异

definitions.example: The musicologist analyzed the structural innovations in Bach’s late violin sonatas.

violin.etymology: 来自意大利语violino，是viola（中提琴）的指小词，指尺寸较小的弦乐器

similar_words.description: 二者为同根形近词，viola指中提琴，violin指小提琴，尺寸和音域存在差异

definitions.example: The environmental group calls for global cooperation to protect the remaining virgin rainforests in the Amazon.
The ancient Greek myth tells of a virgin goddess who protected wild animals.

virgin.etymology: 来自拉丁语virgo（处女），引申为“未被触碰的”，进而指“未开发的、原始的”

similar_words.description: 二者为形近词，virgin指未开发的或处女，virile指有男子气概的，注意词义差异

definitions.example: The company's virtual monopoly in the semiconductor market has drawn intense regulatory scrutiny.
Virtual reality technology is increasingly applied in cognitive psychology experiments.

virtual.etymology: 词根「virt-」（源自拉丁语，表「真实、美德」）+ 形容词后缀「-ual」

similar_words.description: 「virtual」表实质上的或虚拟的，「visual」表与视觉相关的

definitions.example: Virtually all the leading scholars in the field attended the annual academic symposium.

similar_words.description: 「virtually」强调与事实几乎无差，「practically」强调实操层面的几乎

definitions.example: Traditional educational theories attach great importance to cultivating virtues such as integrity and empathy.
The core virtue of this research method is its ability to analyze qualitative data systematically.

virtue.etymology: 词根「virt-」（表「美德、力量」）+ 名词后缀「-ue」

similar_words.description: 「virtue」侧重道德层面的美德或事物的内在优势，「merit」侧重值得嘉奖的优点或功绩

definitions.meaning: （艺术领域的）精湛技艺，鉴赏力
definitions.example: The violinist's virtuosity in performing Baroque compositions earned her a standing ovation.

virtuosity.etymology: 名词「virtuoso（艺术大师）」+ 名词后缀「-ity」（表性质、状态）

similar_words.description: 「virtuosity」特指艺术领域的精湛技艺，「mastery」可泛指各领域的精通

definitions.example: The rapid mutation of the virus has posed great challenges to the development of effective vaccines.

virus.etymology: 源自拉丁语「virus」，原义为「毒液、毒物」，后引申为医学领域的病毒

similar_words.description: 「virus」指无细胞结构的寄生性病毒，「bacteria」指有细胞结构的细菌

definitions.example: International postgraduate students must obtain a valid student visa before entering the host country.

visa.etymology: 源自拉丁语短语「charta visa」，原义为「已验证的文件」，后简化为「visa」

similar_words.description: 「visa」是入境许可文件，「passport」是国际旅行的身份证件

definitions.example: The outline of the ancient castle is barely visible through the thick mist.
There has been a visible improvement in the country's employment rate over the past six months.

visible.etymology: 词根「vis-」（表「看」）+ 形容词后缀「-ible」（表「可…的」）

similar_words.description: 「visible」侧重「可被看见的」状态，「visual」侧重「与视觉相关的」属性

definitions.example: Regular eye check-ups are essential for maintaining healthy vision and detecting ocular diseases early.
A successful political leader must have a clear vision for the nation's long-term development.

vision.etymology: 词根「vis-」（表「看」）+ 名词后缀「-ion」（表「行为、状态」）

similar_words.description: 「vision」侧重生理视力或抽象远见，「sight」侧重实际看见的景象或视力范围

definitions.example: The national museum has launched a reservation system to control the number of daily visitors.

visitor.etymology: 动词「visit（参观、访问）」+ 名词后缀「-or」（表「人」）

similar_words.description: 「visitor」泛指各类来访者（含公务、参观等），「tourist」特指以旅游为目的的游客

definitions.example: The research team used visual aids such as infographics and animations to present their findings.

visual.etymology: 词根「vis-」（表「看」）+ 形容词后缀「-ual」（表「与…相关的」）

similar_words.description: 「visual」表与视觉相关的，「virtual」表实质上的或虚拟的

definitions.example: Adequate sleep is vital for maintaining cognitive function and emotional stability during intense postgraduate exam preparation.

vital.etymology: 由词根「vit-」（表生命，源自拉丁语vita）加形容词后缀「-al」构成，表「与生命相关的→至关重要的」

similar_words.description: 「vital」为形容词表至关重要的，「virtual」为形容词表虚拟的/实际上的

definitions.example: Many plant-based foods are rich in vitamin C, which helps boost the human immune system.

vitamin.etymology: 由词根「vit-」（表生命，源自拉丁语vita）加「amin」（早期误以为该物质为胺类）构成，指维持生命必需的微量营养素

similar_words.description: 「vitamin」为名词表维生素，「vital」为形容词表至关重要的

definitions.example: The historian's vivid account of 19th-century urban slums provides valuable insights for sociological research.

vivid.etymology: 由词根「viv-」（表活的，源自拉丁语vivus）加形容词后缀「-id」构成，表「有生命力的→生动逼真的」

similar_words.description: 「vivid」为形容词表生动的，「visual」为形容词表视觉的

definitions.example: Mastering a wide range of academic vocabulary is critical for succeeding in the reading comprehension section of the postgraduate English exam.

vocabulary.etymology: 由词根「voc-」（表呼喊、言说，源自拉丁语vocare）加后缀「-abul」（表能力）加「-ary」（名词后缀）构成，指可供言说的词语集合，即词汇或词汇量

similar_words.description: 「vocabulary」为名词表词汇（量），「vocal」为形容词表口头的/直言的

definitions.example: The research findings were presented through both written reports and vocal presentations at the academic conference.
Environmental activists have been vocal in criticizing the government's slow response to marine plastic pollution.

vocal.etymology: 由词根「voc-」（表呼喊、言说，源自拉丁语vocare）加形容词后缀「-al」构成，表「与言说相关的→口头的；直言的」

similar_words.description: 「vocal」为形容词表口头的/直言的，「vocation」为名词表职业/使命

definitions.example: For many dedicated rural teachers, education is not just a job but a lifelong vocation.

vocation.etymology: 由词根「voc-」（表召唤，源自拉丁语vocare）加名词后缀「-ation」构成，指「被召唤从事的事业→职业；使命」

similar_words.description: 「vocation」为名词表职业/使命，「vacation」为名词表假期

definitions.example: The forum provided a platform for marginalized groups to make their voices heard by policymakers.
The 2023 government work report voices strong support for the development of digital economy in rural areas.

voice.etymology: 源自古法语vois，与拉丁语vocare（呼喊）同源，表「发出的声音；表达的意见」，引申为动词「表达」

similar_words.description: 「voice」可作名词表声音/意见或动词表表达，「vocal」为形容词表口头的/直言的

definitions.meaning: 空的；无效的（法律/学术义）
definitions.example: The court ruled that the commercial contract was void because it violated national anti-monopoly regulations.
The sudden death of the renowned scientist left a void in the field of quantum physics research.

void.etymology: 源自拉丁语vacare（空），与「vacant」（空的）同源，表「空无的；无法律效力的」，引申为名词「空虚；空白」

similar_words.description: 「void」为形容词/名词表空的/无效的/空虚，「avoid」为动词表避免

definitions.example: The study analyzes the long-term impact of volcano eruptions on global atmospheric temperature and precipitation patterns.

volcano.etymology: 源自罗马神话中的火神「Vulcan」（伏尔甘），因其作坊被认为位于火山之下，故引申为火山

similar_words.description: 「volcano」为名词表火山，「volleyball」为名词表排球

definitions.example: The national college volleyball championship has become a highly anticipated annual event in China's higher education sector.

volleyball.etymology: 由「volley」（凌空截击）加「ball」（球）构成，指需凌空击球的球类运动

similar_words.description: 「volleyball」为名词表排球，「volcano」为名词表火山

definitions.example: The battery supplies a constant 1.5 volts to power the portable environmental monitoring device.

volt.etymology: 源自意大利物理学家亚历山德罗·伏打（Alessandro Volta）的姓氏，为纪念其发明伏打电堆而命名

similar_words.description: 同属电学相关词汇，volt为电压计量单位（伏特），voltage为物理量（电压）

definitions.example: The researchers adjusted the voltage of the experimental circuit to observe changes in the nanomaterial's conductivity.

voltage.etymology: 由词根「volt（伏特）」加名词后缀「-age（表物理量或状态）」构成，指电学中的电压物理量

similar_words.description: 同属电学相关词汇，voltage为物理量（电压），volt为该物理量的计量单位（伏特）

definitions.example: The volume of Arctic sea ice has declined by approximately 13% per decade since satellite records began in 1979.
The third volume of the academic series focuses on the social history of medieval Europe.

volume.etymology: 源自拉丁语动词「volvere（转动）」的派生名词「volumen（卷轴）」，原指卷成筒状的书籍，后引申出体积、容积等义

similar_words.description: 同词根「volv（转动）」，volume原指转动卷成的卷轴，revolve指物体绕轴转动

definitions.example: The university encourages undergraduate students to participate in voluntary community service to develop social awareness.

voluntary.etymology: 由词根「volunt（意志、意愿）」加形容词后缀「-ary（表属性）」构成，指出于自身意愿而非被迫的

similar_words.description: 同词根「volunt（意愿）」，voluntary为形容词（自愿的），volunteer为名词（志愿者）或动词（自愿做）

definitions.example: Volunteers from local medical schools assisted in organizing the free health check-up campaign for rural residents.
She volunteered to translate the latest climate research report for the international environmental summit.

volunteer.etymology: 由词根「volunt（意愿）」加名词后缀「-eer（表从事某活动的人）」构成，原指自愿从事公益的人，后引申出动词义（自愿做）

similar_words.description: 同词根「volunt（意愿）」，volunteer为名词/动词（志愿者/自愿做），voluntary为形容词（自愿的）

definitions.example: Citizens aged 18 and above are eligible to vote in the national parliamentary elections.
The environmental protection bill was passed by a narrow majority of votes in the lower house.

vote.etymology: 源自拉丁语「votum（誓言、许愿）」，原指宣誓表达个人意愿，后引申为投票、选举的政治行为

similar_words.description: 二者均为政治类词汇，vote表投票/选票，veto表否决权

definitions.example: In English phonetics, a vowel is produced without audible obstruction of the airflow in the vocal tract.

vowel.etymology: 源自拉丁语「vocalis（与声音相关的）」，由词根「voc（声音）」派生，指发音时无气流阻碍的语音单位

similar_words.description: 同词根「voc（声音）」，vowel为名词（元音），vocal为形容词（嗓音的、口头的）

definitions.example: The historic voyage of the HMS Beagle shaped Charles Darwin's theory of evolution.
Early European explorers voyaged across the Indian Ocean to establish trade links with Asian kingdoms.

voyage.etymology: 源自古法语「voiage（旅行）」，最终来自拉丁语「viaticum（旅行供给）」，指长距离的航海或太空航行

similar_words.description: 二者拼写相近，voyage表航行，vogue表时尚、流行

definitions.example: The literary critic condemned the novel for its vulgar language and stereotypical portrayal of working-class people.
Linguists study vulgar Latin to trace the evolution of Romance languages such as Spanish and French.

vulgar.etymology: 源自拉丁语「vulgaris（平民的）」，由词根「vulg（大众、平民）」派生，原指平民阶层的，后引申为粗俗的，学术语境下指通俗的

similar_words.description: 二者拼写相近，vulgar表粗俗的/通俗的，vulnerable表易受伤害的

definitions.meaning: 易受伤害的；脆弱的；易受攻击的
definitions.example: Elderly people living alone are particularly vulnerable to financial fraud and accidental falls.

vulnerable.etymology: 由词根「vulner（伤害）」加形容词后缀「-able（表可被...的）」构成，指容易受到伤害或攻击的

similar_words.description: 二者拼写相近，vulnerable表易受伤害的，vulgar表粗俗的/通俗的

definitions.meaning: 工资（尤指按周或按日发放的体力劳动报酬）
definitions.example: According to the latest labor statistics, the average hourly wage of manufacturing workers has increased by 2.8% this year.
The environmental organization decided to wage a campaign against plastic pollution.

wage.etymology: 源自古英语wæge，原指「抵押、约定报酬」，后引申出「薪资」「发动（战事）」等核心义项

similar_words.description: 「wage」多指按周/日发放的体力劳动薪资，「salary」多指按月发放的脑力劳动薪资

definitions.example: In the 19th century, railway wagons were the main means of transporting bulk goods across the United States.

wagon.etymology: 源自荷兰语wagen，原指「四轮运货马车」，后泛指各类大型货运车辆

similar_words.description: 「wagon」多指大型货运车辆（如铁路敞篷车），「van」多指中小型封闭货运车或厢式客车

definitions.example: Medical studies have shown that excessive fat accumulation around the waist is closely related to the risk of type 2 diabetes.

waist.etymology: 源自古英语wæst，原指「腰腹部位」，现代义保留「腰部」的核心语义

similar_words.description: 「waist」指腰部，「wrist」指手腕，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: A sociological study on the service industry investigated the working conditions and job satisfaction of 120 waiters in urban restaurants.

waiter.etymology: 由动词wait（等候、服务）加后缀-er（表施动者）构成，指提供服务的男性人员

similar_words.description: 「waiter」指男服务员，「waitress」指女服务员，为对应性别名词

definitions.example: The research interviewed 80 waitresses in chain cafes to explore gender discrimination in the catering industry.

waitress.etymology: 由动词wait（等候、服务）加后缀-ess（表女性）构成，为waiter的对应女性形式

similar_words.description: 「waitress」指女服务员，「waiter」指男服务员，为对应性别名词

definitions.example: The loud thunderstorm at midnight wakened most residents living near the construction site.

waken.etymology: 由古英语wæcnan演变而来，与wake（醒来）为同根词，表「唤醒、醒来」

similar_words.description: 「waken」多作及物动词表「唤醒」，「wake」多作不及物动词表「醒来」或名词表「航迹」

definitions.example: According to a consumer behavior survey, the widespread use of mobile payments has significantly reduced the demand for traditional physical wallets.

wallet.etymology: 源自古诺斯语valet，原指「行李袋」，后缩小语义指「随身装钱的皮夹」

similar_words.description: 「wallet」多指男士钱包，「purse」多指女士钱包或女用手提包

definitions.example: The travel writer wandered through the ancient villages in southern China, recording local folk customs.
During the three-hour academic lecture, many students’ minds began to wander and missed the key research findings presented by the professor.

wander.etymology: 源自古英语wandrian，原指「徘徊、漫游」，后引申出「思想走神」的义项

similar_words.description: 「wander」指漫游、走神，「wonder」指想知道、奇迹，拼写相近易混淆

definitions.example: The historical monograph analyzed the social and economic impacts of the war on European agricultural development.

war.etymology: 源自古英语werre，原指「冲突、战斗」，后泛指「国家间的战争」或「领域内的斗争」

similar_words.description: 「war」指具体的战争（可数），「warfare」指战争状态、作战方式（不可数）

definitions.example: The local hospital expanded its pediatric ward to accommodate more children with seasonal flu.
The candidate won a landslide victory in the suburban ward by promising to improve public transportation.

ward.etymology: 源自古英语weard，原指「守卫、保护」，后引申出「病房（受医护保护的区域）」「选区（受代表保护的区域）」等义项

similar_words.description: 「ward」指病房、选区，「warden」指看守人、管理员，为同根形近词

definitions.example: Many college students store their seasonal wardrobe in rented storage units near campus to save dorm space.

wardrobe.etymology: 由ward（保护、看管）+ robe（长袍、衣物）构成，原指看管衣物的家具，后引申为个人全部衣物

similar_words.description: 形近词，ward意为「病房；保护」，wardrobe意为「衣柜；全部衣物」

definitions.example: E-commerce platforms often build large automated warehouses in suburban areas to cut logistics costs.

warehouse.etymology: 由ware（商品、货物）+ house（房屋）构成，指存放商品的专用房屋

similar_words.description: 近义词，warehouse指大型专业仓库，stockroom指商店/工厂的小型储藏室

definitions.meaning: 战争；（群体间的）斗争，冲突
definitions.example: Cyber warfare has emerged as a critical threat to global national security in the digital age.

warfare.etymology: 由war（战争）+ fare（进行、从事）构成，指进行战争的状态或作战方式

similar_words.description: 近义词，war指具体的战争事件，warfare侧重战争的形态或对抗方式

definitions.example: The tutor's genuine warmth and patience helped the struggling student regain confidence in English learning.

warmth.etymology: 由warm（温暖的）+ 名词后缀-th构成，表温暖的物理状态或情感特质

similar_words.description: 近义词，warmth指温和的热或情感上的温暖，heat指高温或物理热量

definitions.example: The local government warned residents in flood-prone areas to evacuate immediately as the river level rose sharply.

warn.etymology: 源自古英语warnian，词根warn-意为「注意、警惕」，表提前告知潜在危险

similar_words.description: 近义词，warn侧重提前告知危险信息，alarm侧重使某人因危险而警觉

definitions.example: The police obtained a search warrant to collect digital evidence from the suspect's computer.
The significant decline in air quality warrants urgent measures to reduce vehicle emissions in the city.

warrant.etymology: 由词根war-（保护、保障）+ 后缀-rant构成，原指保障权益的凭证，后引申为动词义

similar_words.description: 近义词，warrant侧重「有正当理由做某事」，guarantee侧重「承诺兑现结果」

definitions.meaning: n. 浪费；废物  v. 浪费  adj. 废弃的
definitions.example: Improper disposal of industrial waste has led to irreversible damage to the local ecosystem.

waste.etymology: 词根waste-意为「空的、无用的」，引申为浪费、废物等核心义

similar_words.description: 近义词，waste泛指各类废物（工业/生活），trash特指生活垃圾

definitions.example: The national park attracts millions of tourists annually for its spectacular waterfall and ancient forest.

waterfall.etymology: 由water（水）+ fall（落下）构成，指水从高处落下形成的自然景观

similar_words.description: 近义词，waterfall指大型自然瀑布，cascade指小型阶梯状瀑布

definitions.meaning: adj. 防水的，不透水的  v. 使防水
definitions.example: Professional mountaineers rely on waterproof gear to withstand harsh rainy and snowy conditions.

waterproof.etymology: 由water（水）+ proof（防…的）构成，表能完全阻挡水的

similar_words.description: 近义词，waterproof指完全不透水，water-resistant指仅能抵御少量水

definitions.example: The energy-saving LED bulb consumes only 5 watts but delivers brightness equivalent to a 40-watt traditional bulb.

watt.etymology: 源自英国发明家詹姆斯·瓦特（James Watt）的姓氏，为纪念其对蒸汽机的贡献而命名

similar_words.description: 同属电学单位，watt是功率单位，volt是电压单位，易混淆

definitions.example: She waved to the academic audience after finishing her keynote speech at the international conference.
A wave of public concern over climate change swept across European countries last year.

wave.etymology: 源自古英语「wafian」，原指「摆动、摇晃」，后引申出挥手、波浪等义

similar_words.description: waive为动词，表「放弃（权利、要求）」，与wave拼写仅一个字母差异，易混淆

definitions.example: Archaeologists found a well-preserved wax tablet with ancient Latin inscriptions in the Roman tomb.
As the moon waxed, the nocturnal field researchers could observe animal behavior more clearly.

wax.etymology: 源自古英语「weax」，原指「蜂蜡」，动词义由蜡的「涂覆增厚」引申而来

similar_words.description: wane为动词，表「（月亮）渐亏；（力量）减弱」，与wax表「渐盈、增强」为反义动词，常同时出现于语境描写

definitions.example: The weak elderly patient was advised to stay in the hospital for further observation.

definitions.meaning: （论据、体系等）薄弱的；无说服力的
definitions.example: The scholar’s weak argument failed to convince the peer review panel of his hypothesis.

weak.etymology: 源自古英语「wác」，原指「虚弱的、无力的」

similar_words.description: week为名词，表「星期、周」，与weak拼写仅一个字母差异，易混淆

definitions.example: The study found that the distribution of national wealth remains highly unequal in many developing countries.

definitions.meaning: 大量；丰富（常搭配「a wealth of」）
definitions.example: The university library provides a wealth of academic resources for graduate students.

wealth.etymology: 源自古英语「wela」，原指「福利、幸福」，后引申为「物质财富」，与形容词「well（健康的、好的）」同源

similar_words.description: health为名词，表「健康」，与wealth拼写首字母不同，其余字母一致，易混淆

definitions.example: Wealthy philanthropists donated a large sum of money to build the new medical research center.
The region is wealthy in natural gas reserves, attracting many energy companies.

wealthy.etymology: 由名词「wealth（财富）」加形容词后缀「-y」构成，表「具有财富的」

similar_words.description: healthy为形容词，表「健康的」，与wealthy拼写首字母不同，其余字母一致，易混淆

definitions.example: The international agreement strictly prohibits the use of chemical weapons in any form of warfare.
Critical thinking is a powerful weapon against the spread of false information on social media.

weapon.etymology: 源自古英语「wǣpen」，原指「用于战斗的工具」，后引申出喻义

similar_words.description: armament为名词，多指「军备、武装力量的总称」，weapon可指具体武器或抽象喻义的手段

definitions.example: After three months of continuous fieldwork in the remote mountain area, the weary research team returned to the campus.
The endless bureaucratic procedures wearied the entire project team, delaying the progress.

weary.etymology: 源自古英语「wērig」，原指「因劳作而疲倦的」，与动词「wear（磨损）」同根，由「精神磨损」引申出厌倦义

similar_words.description: wear为动词，表「穿戴、磨损」，weary由wear的「磨损（精神）」义引申而来，为同根词

definitions.meaning: 编织；（喻）编排（故事、计划等）
definitions.example: The award-winning author wove several real historical events into a touching coming-of-age story.
The traditional handmade silk scarf has a delicate and intricate weave that takes months to complete.

weave.etymology: 源自古英语「wefan」，原指「用线编织成布」，后引申出抽象的「编排」义

similar_words.description: wave为动词/名词，表「挥手、波浪」，与weave拼写相似，易混淆

definitions.example: The World Wide Web has fundamentally changed the way people access and share academic information worldwide.
The spider webbed a fine trap between the branches of the old oak tree to catch insects.

web.etymology: 源自古英语「web(b)」，原指「蜘蛛网、编织物」，后引申为「网络」义

similar_words.description: wed为动词，表「结婚、娶/嫁」，与web拼写仅一个字母差异，易混淆

definitions.example: The royal wedding of the British prince attracted extensive media coverage from around the globe.
The new design represents the wedding of traditional Chinese art and modern digital technology.

wedding.etymology: 由动词「wed（结婚）」加名词后缀「-ing」构成，源自古英语「weddung」，原指「结婚的誓言或仪式」

similar_words.description: wadding为名词，表「填料、软衬垫」，与wedding拼写相似，易混淆

definitions.example: The carpenter inserted a wooden wedge to keep the heavy door from slamming shut.
She wedged herself into the narrow gap between the two bookshelves to reach the top shelf.

wedge.etymology: 源自古英语wecg，原指用于固定或劈开物体的楔形工具，后引申出动词义

similar_words.description: wedge指楔子或挤入，wage指工资或发动（战争等），二者拼写形近易混

definitions.example: The gardener spent three hours pulling weeds from the vegetable garden.
The company plans to weed out underperforming employees to improve efficiency.

weed.etymology: 源自中古英语wede，原指无实用价值的草本植物，后引申出动词剔除义

similar_words.description: weed指杂草或剔除，seed指种子或结籽，二者拼写形近易混

definitions.meaning: 工作日（一周中除周末的日子）
definitions.example: Most public transportation runs more frequently on weekdays than on weekends.

weekday.etymology: 由名词week（周）+ day（日）复合而成，特指非周末的工作日

similar_words.description: 二者均由week构成复合词，weekday指工作日，weekend指周末

definitions.meaning: 每周的（地）；一周一次的（地）
definitions.example: The research team submits a weekly progress report to the supervisor every Friday.

weekly.etymology: 由名词week（周）+ 形容词/副词后缀-ly构成，表与周相关的频率

similar_words.description: 二者均由时间名词+后缀-ly构成，daily指每日的（地），weekly指每周的（地）

definitions.example: She wept quietly in the corner after receiving the rejection letter from the graduate school.
The deep cut on his leg began to weep after he removed the tight bandage.

weep.etymology: 源自古英语wepan，原指流泪哭泣，后引申出伤口渗液义

similar_words.description: weep侧重无声或小声的持续哭泣，sob侧重带喘息声的抽泣、呜咽，情绪强度更高

definitions.example: The customs officer weighed the luggage to check if it exceeded the weight limit.
Before applying for the program, you need to weigh the academic benefits against the financial cost.

weigh.etymology: 源自古英语wegan，原指抬、举，后引申为称重量，再进一步引申为权衡利弊

similar_words.description: weigh是动词，表称重量或权衡；weight是名词，表重量或重要性

definitions.example: There was a weird smell coming from the abandoned house at the end of the street.

weird.etymology: 源自古英语wyrd，原指命运、超自然力量，后引申为离奇怪异的含义

similar_words.description: weird侧重离奇、超现实的怪异感，odd侧重反常、与众不同的奇怪状态

definitions.example: The skilled worker welded the two steel plates together to build the frame of the bridge.
Shared experiences of adversity welded the group of volunteers into a loyal team.

weld.etymology: 源自中古英语weld，原指熔合金属，后引申为使事物紧密结合的抽象义

similar_words.description: weld指焊接或结合，wield指使用（权力、工具等），二者拼写形近易混

definitions.example: The new policy aims to improve the welfare of elderly people living in rural areas.

welfare.etymology: 由副词well（良好地）+ 名词fare（生活状况）复合而成，原指良好的生活状态，后特指社会福利

similar_words.description: welfare侧重官方或机构提供的福利保障，well-being侧重个人的身心幸福状态

definitions.example: The blue whale is recognized as the largest living animal on the planet.
He whaled the intruder with a baseball bat until the neighbors called the police.

whale.etymology: 源自古英语hwæl，原指大型海洋哺乳动物鲸，后引申出动词猛烈攻击义

similar_words.description: whale指鲸或猛烈攻击，whole指完整的、全部的，二者拼写形近易混

definitions.example: Whatever research methods you adopt, you must ensure the objectivity of your data analysis.

whatever.etymology: 合成代词/限定词，由「what」（什么）加「ever」（表「无论、任何」的后缀）构成

similar_words.description: 为whatever的强调形式，仅用于否定、疑问或条件句以加强语气

definitions.meaning: 任何（用于加强否定、疑问或条件语气）
definitions.example: There is no credible evidence whatsoever to support this controversial academic claim.

whatsoever.etymology: 合成强调代词/限定词，由「what」加「so」（加强语气）加「ever」构成，为whatever的强势形式

similar_words.description: 为whatsoever的非强调形式，可用于多种语境，语气更平缓

definitions.example: Wheat is a staple crop for over one-third of the global population, especially in temperate regions.

wheat.etymology: 源自古英语「hwæte」，指禾本科谷类作物小麦

similar_words.description: 拼写接近，wheat指可食用的小麦作物，weed指有害的杂草

definitions.example: The steering wheel of the new energy vehicle is equipped with a touch-sensitive control panel.
The engineers wheeled the new prototype into the exhibition hall for public display.

wheel.etymology: 源自古英语「hweowol」，指圆形旋转部件

similar_words.description: 均与旋转相关，wheel指旋转的物体（轮子），whirl指旋转的动作

definitions.example: Whenever the international academic conference is held, scholars from 50+ countries will participate.

whenever.etymology: 合成连词，由「when」（何时）加「ever」构成

similar_words.description: 同属-ever系列连词，whenever表时间条件，wherever表地点条件

definitions.example: Whereas traditional classrooms focus on face-to-face interaction, online courses offer flexible learning schedules.

definitions.meaning: 鉴于（法律文书开头用语）
definitions.example: Whereas the two parties have negotiated for three months, they hereby sign this formal agreement.

whereas.etymology: 复合连词，由「where」加「as」构成，古英语用于引导对比或条件分句

similar_words.description: 均表对比，whereas多用于正式书面语（如考研阅读），while可表时间或对比，适用范围更广

definitions.meaning: 无论在哪里；在任何地方
definitions.example: Wherever you conduct field research, you must comply with local environmental protection regulations.

wherever.etymology: 合成连词，由「where」（何地）加「ever」构成

similar_words.description: 同属-ever系列连词，wherever表地点条件，whenever表时间条件

definitions.meaning: 无论哪个；任何一个（特定范围内）
definitions.example: Whichever topic you choose for your graduation thesis, you must consult your supervisor first.

whichever.etymology: 合成代词/限定词，由「which」（哪个）加「ever」构成

similar_words.description: 同属-ever系列代词，whichever指特定范围内的任一事物，whatever指无范围限制的任何事物

definitions.example: The shepherd used a leather whip to guide the sheep flock across the mountain path.
The mainstream media whipped the municipal government for its inefficient flood prevention measures.

whip.etymology: 源自中古英语「wippe」，为拟声词，模拟鞭打时的声响

similar_words.description: 拼写接近，whip指鞭打或严厉抨击，wipe指擦拭或消除

definitions.example: The dry leaves began to whirl in the gusty wind as the cold front moved toward the northern plain.
After the emergency announcement, the office was in a whirl of activity as staff prepared for the inspection.

whirl.etymology: 源自中古英语「whirlen」，为拟声词，模拟物体旋转时的呼啸声

similar_words.description: 均与旋转相关，whirl指旋转的动作，wheel指旋转的物体（轮子）

definitions.example: Most of the whisky produced in the Scottish highlands is exported to European and North American markets.

whisky.etymology: 源自苏格兰盖尔语「uisge beatha」（生命之水）的缩略转写，进入英语后特指威士忌酒

similar_words.description: 拼写相近，whisky主要指苏格兰、加拿大出产的威士忌，whiskey多指爱尔兰、美国出产的威士忌

definitions.example: During the academic conference, two researchers sitting beside me began to whisper about the loopholes in the keynote speech’s data analysis.
A whisper about the university’s upcoming tuition increase spread quickly among students at the beginning of the semester.

whisper.etymology: 拟声词，源自古英语「hwisprian」，模仿小声低语的声响，后引申为私下传言义

similar_words.description: 均有小声说话的含义，whisper侧重凑近他人耳边压低声音说，murmur侧重连续不清的小声嘟囔或抱怨

definitions.meaning: 吹口哨；（汽笛、火车等）鸣响
definitions.example: When the rescue team found the trapped hiker in the valley, one member whistled loudly to signal their location to the base camp.
The referee blew his whistle to stop the match when a player fell and got seriously injured on the field.

whistle.etymology: 拟声词，源自古英语「hwistlian」，模仿口哨、汽笛的尖锐声响

similar_words.description: 拼写形近，whisper指低语、耳语，whistle指吹口哨、鸣汽笛等发出尖锐声响

definitions.example: Independent journalists accused the official report of trying to whitewash the government’s negligence in the public health crisis.
Local volunteers helped whitewash the walls of the old community library to create a more comfortable reading environment.

whitewash.etymology: 合成词，由「white（白色的）+wash（洗刷）」构成，原指用白色涂料粉刷墙面，后引申为抽象的掩盖污点、粉饰错误义

similar_words.description: 均有粉饰、美化的含义，whitewash侧重掩盖负面事实或丑闻，sugarcoat侧重将不好的事物包装得更易接受

definitions.meaning: 无论谁，不管是谁；……的任何人
definitions.example: Whoever wants to participate in the national postgraduate entrance exam must register online before the specified deadline.

whoever.etymology: 合成词，由「who（谁）+ever（无论）」构成，可引导让步状语从句或名词性从句，是考研语法常考连接代词

similar_words.description: 均为连接代词，whoever在从句中作主语，whomever在从句中作宾语

definitions.example: The whole research team spent three years collecting data from remote rural areas to complete the report.
Educators emphasize that children’s mental health should be viewed as a whole, not just judged by their academic performance.

whole.etymology: 源自古英语「hāl」（健康的、未受损伤的），与「health」为同根词，后引申为全部的、整体的含义

similar_words.description: 均有全部的含义，whole侧重事物作为一个整体不可分割，entire侧重范围上覆盖所有部分，没有任何遗漏

definitions.meaning: 有益健康的；（道德上）健康的，正派的
definitions.example: Many urban residents choose to spend their weekends in the countryside to enjoy fresh air and wholesome farm food.

wholesome.etymology: 派生形容词，由「whole（健康的、完整的）+形容词后缀-some（表具有…性质的）」构成，指对身心或道德有益的

similar_words.description: 均有有益健康的含义，wholesome可修饰食物、活动、人等，兼顾身心与道德层面的益处；healthful仅修饰食物、环境等，仅指对身体健康有益

definitions.example: The success of the carbon reduction policy is not wholly attributable to government regulations, but also to active public participation.

wholly.etymology: 派生副词，由「whole（全部的）+副词后缀-ly」构成，用于修饰形容词、动词或整个句子

similar_words.description: 拼写高度形近，wholly是副词表完全地，holy是形容词表神圣的、圣洁的

definitions.meaning: 邪恶的，道德败坏的；恶劣的，有害的
definitions.example: The documentary exposed how the wicked factory owner dumped industrial waste into the river to cut production costs.

definitions.meaning: 淘气的，调皮的（尤指儿童）
definitions.example: The grandmother smiled fondly at her wicked grandson who had hidden her glasses behind the sofa.

wicked.etymology: 源自古英语「wicca」（巫师、巫婆），原指与巫术相关的、邪异的，后引申为邪恶的、调皮的含义

similar_words.description: 均有邪恶的含义，wicked侧重指行为违背道德，常带有恶作剧或故意作恶的意味；evil侧重本质上邪恶、有害，程度更深

definitions.meaning: 分布广的，普遍的，广泛的
definitions.example: The outbreak of the infectious disease caused widespread concern among the public and prompted the government to take urgent measures.

widespread.etymology: 合成词，由「wide（宽的、广的）+spread（传播、分布）」构成，常用于描述现象、问题、应用等的覆盖范围

similar_words.description: 均有普遍的、广泛的含义，widespread侧重覆盖的地域或人群范围广，prevalent侧重在特定时间或领域内流行、盛行

definitions.example: The elderly widow donated all her savings to the rural compulsory education project.

widow.etymology: 来自古英语widuwe，原指丧失配偶的女性，后专指丧夫的女性

similar_words.description: 形近词，window意为窗户，widow意为寡妇，拼写仅差末尾一个字母n

definitions.example: The architect measured the width of the corridor to ensure the wheelchair could pass through smoothly.

width.etymology: 由形容词wide（宽的）加名词后缀-th构成，表抽象属性

similar_words.description: 近义词，breadth也表宽度，但更侧重抽象的广度或幅度，width侧重具体的宽度数值

definitions.meaning: 野生的；未开化的，荒凉的
definitions.example: The expedition team found a large area of wild forest that had never been disturbed by human activities.

wild.etymology: 来自古英语wilde，原指未被驯养的，后引申为未开发的、失控的

similar_words.description: 形近词，mild意为温和的、轻微的，wild意为野生的、荒凉的，首字母不同

definitions.example: Most volunteers are willing to spend their weekends helping the elderly in the community nursing home.

willing.etymology: 由动词will（愿意）加形容词后缀-ing构成，表主动的意愿状态

similar_words.description: 同根词，willed意为有意志的、决心的，willing表主动愿意的状态

definitions.example: The research team won the national scientific award for their breakthrough in renewable energy technology.

win.etymology: 来自古英语winnan，原指奋斗、斗争，后引申为获胜、赢得

similar_words.description: 形近词，wing意为翅膀，win意为获胜、赢得，拼写仅差一个字母g

definitions.example: The passage mentioned that moderate consumption of red wine may have certain benefits for cardiovascular health.

wine.etymology: 来自古英语win，源自拉丁语vinum（葡萄树、葡萄酒）

similar_words.description: 形近词，vine意为葡萄藤、藤蔓，wine意为葡萄酒，拼写仅差一个字母w

definitions.example: The bird spread its wings and flew towards the distant mountain peak, as described in the travelogue.

wing.etymology: 来自古英语weng，原指鸟的翅膀，后引申为各种翼状结构

similar_words.description: 形近词，win意为获胜、赢得，wing意为翅膀，拼写仅差一个字母g

definitions.example: The professor gave a subtle wink to the student who had just answered the tricky question correctly.

wink.etymology: 来自古英语wincian，原指闭眼，后引申为眨眼示意、闪烁

similar_words.description: 近义词，blink指无意识的频繁眨眼，wink指有意识的眨眼示意或短暂闪烁

definitions.example: The librarian wiped the dust off the ancient books carefully before storing them in the climate-controlled cabinet.

wipe.etymology: 来自古英语wipian，原指摩擦、擦拭，后引申为消除痕迹或记忆

similar_words.description: 形近词，swipe意为猛击、刷卡，wipe意为擦拭、消除，首字母不同

definitions.meaning: 金属丝；电线；给…装电线；发电报
definitions.example: The engineers checked the underground wires to find the cause of the power outage in the residential area.

wire.etymology: 来自古英语wir，原指金属细丝，后引申为电线、电报等通讯工具

similar_words.description: 形近词，fire意为火、解雇，wire意为金属丝、电线，首字母不同

definitions.example: Traditional Chinese philosophy emphasizes that wisdom comes from both systematic learning and deep reflection.

wisdom.etymology: 由形容词「wise」加名词后缀「-dom」（表抽象状态、属性）构成，表“智慧的状态”。

similar_words.description: 「wisdom」侧重长期积累的深沉智慧、判断力；「wit」侧重反应敏捷的机智、风趣。

definitions.example: It is wise to conduct a comprehensive literature review before starting a research project.

wise.etymology: 源自古英语「wīs」，表“有见识、有判断力的”，同源词包括「wisdom」「wit」。

similar_words.description: 「wise」侧重有远见、有判断力的明智；「clever」侧重头脑灵活、反应快的聪明。

definitions.example: The historian’s speech was full of wit and rigorous analysis, impressing all the audience.
The ancient scholar was admired for his native wit and extensive knowledge.

wit.etymology: 与「wise」「wisdom」同根，源自古英语「witt」，原指“智力、见识”，后衍生出“机智、风趣”义。

similar_words.description: 「wit」侧重反应敏捷的机智、风趣；「wisdom」侧重长期积累的深沉智慧。

definitions.example: The medieval folk tale depicted the witch as a figure with mysterious herbal knowledge.

witch.etymology: 源自古英语「wicce」，原指“从事巫术的女性”，即“女巫”。

similar_words.description: 「witch」指女性巫师；「wizard」指男性巫师。

definitions.example: The multinational corporation decided to withdraw its investment from the region due to regulatory uncertainties.

withdraw.etymology: 由前缀「with-」（表“向后、反向”）加词根「draw」（拉）构成，意为“向后拉→撤回、撤出”。

similar_words.description: 「withdraw」指主动撤回、撤出（资源、人员等）；「withhold」指保留、拒绝给予（信息、资金等）。

definitions.example: The judicial authority refused to withhold the key evidence from the defense lawyer.

withhold.etymology: 由前缀「with-」（表“保留、阻止”）加词根「hold」（持有）构成，意为“持有不放→保留、拒绝给予”。

similar_words.description: 「withhold」指保留、拒绝给予；「withdraw」指主动撤回、撤出。

definitions.example: All research manuscripts must be submitted within six weeks as required by the academic journal.

within.etymology: 由介词「with」（表“在、伴随”）加介词「in」（表“在内部”）构成，表“在…之内、在内部”。

similar_words.description: 「within」指在范围、时间、空间内部；「without」指在外部、没有。

definitions.example: The new bridge structure is designed to withstand strong earthquakes and heavy floods.

withstand.etymology: 由前缀「with-」（表“对抗、面对”）加词根「stand」（站立）构成，意为“站在对抗位置→承受、经受住”。

similar_words.description: 「withstand」侧重被动承受住压力、考验；「resist」侧重主动抵抗、抵制（诱惑、攻击等）。

definitions.example: The witness provided detailed testimony about the environmental pollution incident.
The past twenty years has witnessed remarkable growth in China’s renewable energy sector.

witness.etymology: 由词根「wit」（表“知道、见证”）加后缀「-ness」（表人）构成，原指“知道事件真相的人→目击者、证人”。

similar_words.description: 「witness」作动词指目击、见证（事件发展）；「testify」指（在法庭等正式场合）作证、证明。

definitions.example: The ecological study analyzed the social structure of wolf packs in the northern forest.

wolf.etymology: 源自古英语「wulf」，为原始日耳曼语族表“狼”的核心词汇。

similar_words.description: 「wolf」指狼（群居性野兽）；「fox」指狐狸（独居性野兽）。

definitions.example: The Great Barrier Reef is widely regarded as one of the greatest natural wonders of the world.
Many historians wonder how ancient civilizations built such massive stone structures without modern engineering tools.

wonder.etymology: 来自古英语wundor，本义为「令人惊奇的事物」，后衍生出动词「感到惊奇、想知道」的含义

similar_words.description: 形近易混，wonder表「惊奇、想知道」，wander表「漫游、闲逛」

definitions.example: The research team made a wonderful breakthrough in the treatment of rare genetic diseases.

wonderful.etymology: 由词根wonder（奇迹、惊奇）加形容词后缀-ful（充满…的）构成，表「充满惊奇的、极好的」

similar_words.description: 近义易混，wonderful侧重口语化的「好」，marvelous更正式，表「令人惊叹的」

definitions.example: Traditional Chinese furniture is often crafted from high-quality solid wood.

wood.etymology: 来自古英语wudu，本义为「树木、木材」，后衍生出「小树林」的含义

similar_words.description: 形近易混，wood表「木材、树林」，wool表「羊毛、毛线」

definitions.example: The old wooden library on campus is a popular spot for students to study.
His wooden performance in the interview left a bad impression on the recruiters.

wooden.etymology: 由名词wood（木材）加形容词后缀-en（由…制成的）构成，核心义为「木制的」，后引申出「僵硬的」僻义

similar_words.description: 同根易混，wood是名词表「木材」，wooden是形容词表「木制的、僵硬的」

definitions.example: Merino wool is prized for its softness and ability to regulate body temperature.

wool.etymology: 来自古英语wull，本义为「羊毛」，后衍生出「毛线」的含义

similar_words.description: 形近易混，wool表「羊毛、毛线」，wood表「木材、树林」

definitions.example: The factory expanded its metal workshop to produce more auto components.
The university will host a workshop on academic writing for first-year graduate students.

workshop.etymology: 由名词work（工作）加名词shop（场所）构成，本义为「工作场所（车间）」，后引申出学术领域的「研讨会」含义

similar_words.description: 近义易混，workshop侧重动手操作或学术研讨的场所，studio侧重艺术创作的工作室

definitions.example: The COVID-19 pandemic had a worldwide impact on global supply chains and public health systems.

worldwide.etymology: 由名词world（世界）加形容词wide（广泛的）构成，可作形容词或副词，表「全球范围的（地）」

similar_words.description: 近义易混，worldwide可作adj/adv，global仅作adj，二者均表「全球的」

definitions.example: Some types of parasitic worm can cause serious health problems in humans and livestock.

worm.etymology: 来自古英语wyrm，本义为「蛇、蠕虫」，后特指「无脊椎的蠕虫类生物」

similar_words.description: 形近易混，worm表「蠕虫、寄生虫」，warm表「温暖的、热情的」

definitions.meaning: 更坏的（地）；更差的（地）（bad/badly的比较级）
definitions.example: The economic situation in the developing country became worse after the severe drought.

worse.etymology: 来自古英语wyrsa，是形容词bad和副词badly的比较级形式

similar_words.description: 同根易混，worse是bad/badly的比较级，worst是其最高级

definitions.meaning: 崇拜，敬奉（神、偶像等）；礼拜（仪式）
definitions.example: In ancient Egyptian civilization, people would worship the sun god Ra as the source of all life.

worship.etymology: 来自古英语weorþscipe，由weorþ（值得、尊敬）加后缀-scipe（状态、性质）构成，本义为「尊敬」，后衍生出「宗教崇拜、礼拜」的含义

similar_words.description: 形近易混，worship表「崇拜、礼拜」，warship表「军舰、战舰」

definitions.example: The worst economic downturn in a decade has forced thousands of small businesses to shut down permanently.

worst.etymology: 是形容词bad/ill的最高级形式，源自古英语wyrsa的最高级wyrrest，与比较级worse同根

similar_words.description: 「worse」是bad/ill的比较级，意为更差的；「worst」是最高级，意为最差的

definitions.meaning: 值得做的；值得花时间/精力的
definitions.example: It is worthwhile to conduct in-depth research on the impact of climate change on terrestrial ecosystems.

worthwhile.etymology: 由worth（值得）+while（时间）构成的复合形容词，意为值得花费时间的

similar_words.description: 「worthwhile」多作表语或定语，强调某事值得投入时间/精力；「worthy」多接of短语，强调人/事配得上或值得尊敬

definitions.example: His lifelong dedication to poverty alleviation is worthy of national recognition.
She is a worthy winner of the Nobel Prize in Literature.

worthy.etymology: 由名词worth（价值）加形容词后缀-y构成，意为具有…价值的

similar_words.description: 「worthy」后常接of短语，强调配得上；「worthwhile」强调某事值得投入资源

definitions.example: The mental wound left by the childhood trauma took decades to heal.
The harsh public criticism wounded his professional pride deeply.

wound.etymology: 源自古英语wund，与拉丁语vulnus（伤口）同源，指身体或精神上的损伤

similar_words.description: 「wound」多指武器或利器造成的身体伤口，也指精神创伤；「injure」泛指各种意外或故意伤害

definitions.example: The researchers wrapped the ancient pottery in bubble wrap to protect it during transport.

wrap.etymology: 源自古英语wræppan，意为缠绕、包裹

similar_words.description: 「wrap」强调用软材料包裹单个物品；「pack」强调将物品装入容器或打包成整体

definitions.meaning: 花环；花圈（用于纪念或装饰）
definitions.example: A wreath of white lilies was placed at the memorial to honor the victims of the plane crash.

wreath.etymology: 源自古英语writha，意为缠绕的东西，与wrap同根，指由花、叶等缠绕而成的环形物

similar_words.description: 「wrap」是动词，意为包、裹；「wreath」是名词，意为缠绕而成的花环、花圈

definitions.meaning: （船、飞机等的）残骸；失事交通工具
definitions.example: Marine archaeologists discovered the wreck of a 17th-century warship in the Atlantic Ocean.
The severe hurricane wrecked hundreds of coastal homes and infrastructure.

wreck.etymology: 源自古英语wrecan，意为驱赶、破坏，引申为交通工具失事或事物被毁坏

similar_words.description: 「wreck」多指交通工具失事或结构被彻底破坏；「ruin」多指建筑物、希望等的彻底毁灭

definitions.example: He wrenched his right knee while playing basketball in the campus tournament.

definitions.meaning: 痛苦的离别；难熬的分离
definitions.example: Saying goodbye to her sick mother was a terrible wrench for the young graduate.

wrench.etymology: 源自古英语wrencan，意为扭曲、猛拧，引申为身体扭伤或情感上的痛苦分离

similar_words.description: 「wrench」意为猛拧、扭伤或痛苦分离；「wreck」意为残骸、破坏

definitions.meaning: 可怜的；悲惨的；处境恶劣的
definitions.example: The wretched refugees had no access to clean water or medical care during the conflict.

wretched.etymology: 源自古英语wrecca（流浪者、不幸的人）加形容词后缀-ed，意为处境悲惨的

similar_words.description: 「wretched」侧重因外部处境艰难而显得可怜、悲惨；「miserable」侧重内心的痛苦或处境的糟糕

definitions.example: Long-term exposure to ultraviolet rays can accelerate the formation of wrinkles on the skin.
She wrinkled her forehead in confusion when analyzing the complex statistical data.

wrinkle.etymology: 源自古英语wrincan，意为扭曲、起皱，指皮肤或布料上的细小褶皱

similar_words.description: 「wrinkle」多指皮肤或布料的细小褶皱；「fold」多指较大的折叠或折痕

definitions.example: The doctor bandaged his injured wrist after the chemistry lab accident.

wrist.etymology: 源自古英语「wrist」，指人体腕部结构，无复杂词缀变化。

similar_words.description: wrist指腕关节，fist指握紧的拳头，均为手部相关身体部位词。

definitions.example: Academic writing requires logical reasoning and precise wording.

writing.etymology: 动词「write」的动名词形式，词根为「write」（书写），后缀「-ing」构成名词，表动作或产物。

similar_words.description: writing是write的动名词/名词形式，表写作行为或作品；write为动词，表书写动作。

definitions.meaning: 复印；影印；（施乐牌）复印件/复印机
definitions.example: Please xerox four copies of the research proposal for the committee meeting.

xerox.etymology: 源自施乐公司（Xerox）的商标名，因该公司首创静电复印技术，后转化为通用的复印相关词汇。

similar_words.description: xerox特指用施乐机复印，copy为通用的复制、复印或复制件。

definitions.example: The retired professor grows vegetables in his small backyard yard.
The surveyor measured the plot to be 30 yards wide.

yard.etymology: 源自古英语「geard」，原指围起的空间，后延伸出长度单位（原指一臂之长）的含义。

similar_words.description: yard多指住宅的私人庭院，court多指公共庭院、宫廷或法庭。

definitions.example: The student yawned repeatedly during the three-hour philosophy lecture.

yawn.etymology: 源自古英语「ginian」，指张口的动作，后特指因困倦或无聊打哈欠。

similar_words.description: yawn是因困倦打哈欠，sigh是因情绪（如无奈、疲惫）叹气。

definitions.meaning: 每年的；一年一度的；每年
definitions.example: The university publishes its yearly academic report in December.

yearly.etymology: 由名词「year」（年）加后缀「-ly」构成，「-ly」可作形容词或副词后缀，表时间频率。

similar_words.description: yearly可作形容词和副词，annual仅作形容词（副词形式为annually）。

definitions.example: The crowd yelled loudly when the Nobel Prize winner took the stage.

yell.etymology: 源自古英语「giellan」，指大声喊叫的动作，后特指因兴奋、愤怒等的激烈叫喊。

similar_words.description: yell多指情绪激动时的刺耳大喊，shout为通用的大声喊叫。

definitions.example: The irrigated farmland yields 1000 kilograms of rice per mu annually.
The negotiating team refused to yield to the unreasonable demands of the other side.

yield.etymology: 源自古英语「gieldan」，原指支付、回报，后延伸出「产出收益」「屈服妥协」的含义。

similar_words.description: yield侧重产出作物、收益等无形或农业产物，produce侧重制造工业产品或具体物品。

definitions.example: The local library organizes reading camps for youngsters during summer vacation.

youngster.etymology: 由形容词「young」（年轻的）加后缀「-ster」构成，「-ster」为名词后缀，指具有某特征的人。

similar_words.description: youngster特指单个年轻个体（多为未成年或刚成年），youth可指青春时期或青年群体。

definitions.example: He devoted his youth to studying ancient Chinese literature.
The youth of the nation are playing an active role in rural revitalization.

youth.etymology: 源自古英语「geoguth」，原指年轻的状态，后延伸出「青年群体」的含义。

similar_words.description: youth可指青春时期或青年群体，youngster特指单个年轻个体。

definitions.example: His zeal for interdisciplinary academic research earned him a prestigious research grant from the national education committee.

zeal.etymology: 源自希腊语「zelos」（意为热情），与形容词「zealous」为同根派生词

similar_words.description: 「zeal」为名词，表抽象的热情、热忱；「zealous」为形容词，表充满热情的

definitions.example: Zebras’ distinctive black-and-white striped patterns serve as an effective camouflage against natural predators in the savanna.

zebra.etymology: 源自葡萄牙语「zebra」，原指野驴，后引申为斑马

similar_words.description: 「zebra」为名词，指斑马；「zebrine」为形容词，指似斑马的、有斑马纹的

definitions.example: The narrow mountain trail took a zigzag course through the dense alpine forest.
The glacial meltwater zigzags across the tundra before merging into the Arctic Ocean.

zigzag.etymology: 由拟声词根「zig」和「zag」组合而成，模拟物体连续转向的形态

similar_words.description: 「zigzag」表连续的之字形或曲折行进；「zig」仅指单次的急转、转向动作

definitions.example: Zinc, an essential trace element for human health, is commonly added to dietary supplements to boost immune function.

zinc.etymology: 源自德语「Zink」，原指锌这种灰白色金属元素

similar_words.description: 「zinc」指化学元素锌；「tin」指化学元素锡，二者均为常用工业金属

definitions.example: Please secure the zip of your carry-on luggage before passing through the security checkpoint.
The rookie intern demonstrated remarkable zip and efficiency in handling administrative tasks.

zip.etymology: 源自拟声词，模拟拉链开合时发出的清脆声响

similar_words.description: 「zip」作名词指拉链时与「zipper」同义，但「zip」还可作动词或表「活力」的僻义

definitions.meaning: 区域，地带（指具有特定属性或功能的区域）
definitions.example: The polar zone is experiencing unprecedented ice melt due to anthropogenic climate change.

zone.etymology: 源自拉丁语「zona」（意为腰带），后引申为具有特定边界的区域、地带

similar_words.description: 「zone」侧重有特定属性或功能的小范围区域（如时区、开发区）；「region」侧重较大的地理或行政区域

definitions.example: Online retail sales in the region have zoomed by 35% year-on-year amid the pandemic.
The high-speed train zoomed past the rural villages without stopping.

zoom.etymology: 源自拟声词，模拟物体快速移动时发出的呼啸声

similar_words.description: 「zoom」侧重快速、急剧的增长或移动；「boom」侧重经济、行业的繁荣性增长
